PT P
PN JP2019041644-A; JP6787856-B2
TI Carbon-dioxide application apparatus has control unit that performs shutdown of apparatus and cooling of liquid by cooling mechanism.
AU SAITO T
AE FUTABA SANGYO KK (FUTS-C)
GA 201926185W
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a liquid storage tank (3) that is comprised to store liquid (L) and pass the combustion exhaust gas through the liquid. An adsorption tank (6) is provided with an adsorbent to adsorb the carbon dioxide in the combustion exhaust gas. A supply flow path (15) is comprised to supply the combustion exhaust gas passed through the liquid supplied to the adsorption tank. An apparatus is provided in the supply flow path to guide the combustion exhaust gas passed through the liquid to the downstream side of the liquid storage tank. A cooling mechanism is provided to cool the liquid in the first liquid storage tank. A control unit (7) is provided to control the apparatus and the cooling mechanism. The shutdown of the apparatus and the cooling of the liquid by the cooling mechanism are performed, when the first temperature difference becomes more than the threshold voltage.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide application apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The generation of dew condensation in the absorption tank is suppressed more reliably and the adsorption process is restarted rapidly after cooling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first temperature difference obtained by subtracting the atmospheric temperature of the adsorption tank from the temperature of the liquid in the liquid storage tank or the second temperature difference obtained by subtracting the atmospheric temperature from the temperature of the combustion exhaust gas in an inlet port of the apparatus becomes equal or more than a threshold value. The control unit performs both of shutdown of the apparatus and the cooling of the liquid by the cooling mechanism, when the first temperature difference or the second temperature difference becomes more than the threshold value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the structure of carbon-dioxide application apparatus.
   Carbon-dioxide application apparatus (1)
   Liquid storage tank (3)
   Adsorption tank (6)
   Control unit (7)
   Supply flow path (15)
   Liquid (L)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN JP2022060025-A
TI Carbonated aerosol skin external preparation used for applying to skin of limbs, consists of aerosol stock solution containing heparin-like substance, pH regulator and water, and propellant containing carbon dioxide.
AU HARADA R
   NAOE A
   SAITO T
AE KAO CORP (KAOS-C)
GA 2022595888
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonated aerosol skin external preparation consists of an aerosol stock solution containing (A) heparin-like substance, (B) pH regulator and (C) water, and a propellant containing (D) carbon dioxide, where the pH of the mixed solution of the aerosol stock solution and the propellant is 4.5-7.
   USE - The preparation is used for applying to the skin of the whole body excluding the scalp, such as any one of the face, body, and limbs, preferably the skin of limbs.
   ADVANTAGE - The external preparation utilizes a heparin-like substance in a stable manner and having less irritation to the skin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of the component (B) in the aerosol stock solution is 0.05-1 %mass. Preferred Components: The component (B) is one or more compounds chosen from organic acids, organic acid salts, amino acids, amino acid salts, amines, and inorganic bases.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The external preparation further comprises a water-soluble polymer as the component (E) in the aerosol stock solution. Preferred Method: The preparation is discharged in the form of foam.
   EXAMPLE - Typical composition comprised of: 0.3 %mass heparinoid; 0.4 %mass sodium citrate hydrate; water (remaining); 0.45 %mass Metolose 90SH-15000 (RTM: Hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose); 0.45 %mass phenoxyethanol; 0.05 %mass sodium edetate hydrate; 10 %mass glycerin; and 10 %mass dipropylene glycol.

PN CN114262523-A
TI Use of aluminum hydroxide-loaded peanut shell powder for reducing asphalt carbon emission, where peanut shell powder loaded with aluminum hydroxide is added to asphalt, so that carbon dioxide emission is greatly reduced after asphalt is heated.
AU WANG Y
   SHEN J
   SHI P
   WANG S
   ZHU X
   YUAN J
   TIAN C
   YUAN F
AE UNIV SUZHOU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSO-C)
GA 202251352M
AB    NOVELTY - Use of aluminum hydroxide-loaded peanut shell powder is claimed for reducing asphalt carbon emission, where peanut shell powder loaded with aluminum hydroxide is added to the asphalt, so that the carbon dioxide emission is greatly reduced after the asphalt is heated. In an oven at 80degrees C., after the base asphalt is melted, the mixture is uniformly stirred to obtain a carbon emission reduction pitch.
   USE - Aluminum hydroxide-loaded peanut shell powder used for reducing asphalt carbon emission (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation process is simple, the emission reduction effect is obvious, and has important application value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The aluminum hydroxide-loaded peanut shell powder is preferably loaded in an amount of 2 wt.% of the peanut shell powder, and the peanut shell powder is selected from 80-100 meshes; the addition amount of the peanut shell powder loaded with the aluminum hydroxide in the asphalt is 1 wt.% of the asphalt.

PT P
PN CN114058309-A
TI New bio-based MS glue base resins useful in field of chemical material, and prepared by e.g. carrying out polycondensation of dibasic acid compound and dibasic alcohol compound, reacting with diglycidyl ether compound, and reacting with carbon dioxide and aminopropylene trialkoxysilane.
AU ZHAO X
   HUANG J
   MA C
   LI Z
   SUN J
   GUO K
AE NANJING NANLI NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202231672T
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-based MS glue base resins (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in field of chemical material
   ADVANTAGE - The base resin is biodegradable and environmentally-friendly. The method: can reduce carbon dioxide emission; and is beneficial to carbon neutralization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bio-based MS glue base resins of formula (I) are new.
   R = -CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2OCH2CH2-, or -CH2CH2OCH2CH2OCH2CH2-;
   R1 = -CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2CH2-, -CH2CH2OCH2CH2-, or -CH2CH2OCH2CH2OCH2CH2-;
   R2 = -CH3, or -CH2CH3;
   m = 1-10; and
   n = 10-40.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) carrying out polycondensation of dibasic acid compound of formula (II) and dibasic alcohol compound of formula (III) to obtain carboxy-terminated polyester of formula (IV), (ii) reacting (IV) with diglycidyl ether compound of formula (V) to obtain epoxy-terminated polyester of formula (VI), (iii) reacting (VI) with carbon dioxide to obtain cyclic carbonate-terminated polyester of formula (VII), and (iv) reacting (VII) with aminopropylene trialkoxysilane of formula (VIII) to obtain final product.Preferred Method: In the third step reaction, the pressure is 10-80 atmospheres, the temperature is 80-120degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 12-24 hours.Preferred Components: A catalyst is used in the third step reaction, and the catalyst is 1,3-disubstituted phenylimidazole iodonium salt. The 1,3-disubstituted phenylimidazole iodonium salt is 1,3-bis-(2,6-dimethylphenyl)imidazolium iodonium salt, 1,3-bis-(2,6-diisopropylphenyl)imidazolium iodonium salt, 1,3-bis-(2,4,6-trimethylphenyl)imidazolium iodide salt. In the fourth step of the reaction, the aminopropylene trialkoxysilane is aminopropyltrimethoxysilane and/or aminopropyltriethoxysilane.
DC A28 (Condensation polymers in general.); A81 (Adhesives and binders - including chipboard.); G03 (Adhesives - excluding dispensers. Polymeric adhesives are also classified in Section A (C09H, J).)
MC A05-E02A; A05-A01E3; A10-E; A10-E22A; A10-E23; A10-E07C; A10-D05; A12-A05C; A12-A05E; A10-E17A; G03-B02E3

TI Portable air decontamination device for facial            breathing masks, has two different chambers that is            provided with check valves, to separate inspired air            from expired air charged with carbon dioxide.

   USE - Portable air decontamination device for facial                breathing masks.
   ADVANTAGE - The device has no inconvenience to our health,                serves to an end of decontamination of an air                breathed by the people of bacteria, germs and                viruses, existing in suspension in the air,                essentially prevention of COVID 19.

PT P
PN KR2021048305-A
TI Preparing high S-allyl-L-cysteine content garlic for treating Alzheimer disease comprises freezing garlic and vacuum pre-treating, treating with supercritical carbon dioxide and performing vacuum freeze-drying.
AU HYUN S
AE HYUN S (HYUN-Individual)
GA 2021482492
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high S-allyl-L-cysteine content garlic comprises (i) freezing the garlic at -80 degrees C for 12 hours and vacuum pre-treating at 60 degrees C with a pressure of 10-4 Torr, (ii) treating with supercritical carbon dioxide at 60 degrees C with a pressure of 250 bar for 3 hours, and (iii) performing vacuum freeze-drying.
   USE - The high S-allyl-L-cysteine content garlic is useful in medicinal composition and healthcare functional food for treating Alzheimer's disease (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of difficulty in ingesting raw garlic and ensures consumption of active components of garlic.
TF ACTIVITY - Neuroprotective; Nootropic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112569192-A; WO2022105726-A1
TI Production method of lycopene-containing particles with high-efficiency antioxidant effect comprises extracting Ganoderma lucidum spore oil through carbon dioxide supercritical extraction process and mixing with lycopene oil resin.
AU WANG L
   FENG P
   WANG Y
   ZHOU Y
   FENG M
   QIAN Y
AE ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
   NANJING ZHONGKE PHARM CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP JIANGSU PHARM (ZHON-Non-standard)
   ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP CORP LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
   NANJING ZHONGKE PHARM CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   ZHONGKE HEALTH IND GROUP JIANGSU PHARM (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 202137205S
AB    NOVELTY - Production method of lycopene-containing particles with high-efficiency antioxidant effect comprises extracting Ganoderma lucidum spore oil through carbon dioxide supercritical extraction process to obtain degreased Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and mixing the degreased Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and lycopene oil resin by weight ratio of 1-2:1-2 for spray granulation, where the wall-breaking rate ofGanoderma lucidum spore powder is 90-92%.
   USE - The method is useful for producing lycopene-containing particles with high-efficiency antioxidant effect.
   ADVANTAGE - The particles has lycopene content greater than 6%, polysaccharide content greater than 0.8% and good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the degreased Ganoderma lucidum spore powder and lycopene oil resin is 1:1. The Ganoderma lucidum spore powder Ganoderma lucidum spore powder erupted by Red Ganoderma lucidum. Preferred Method: The spray granulation comprises mixing lycopene oil resin with small amount of absolute ethanol to obtain lycopene oil resin alcoholate, using a fluidized bed granulator, atomizing lycopene oil resin alcoholate by the nozzle by compressed air and spraying to the wall-broken Ganoderma lucidumsporepowder in fluidized state on the fluidized bed layer by compressed air, wetting and fully mixing, flowing and granulating. The nozzle atomization is carried out at a speed rate of 40-100 ml/minute, preferably 10 ml/minute, the atomizing grain diameter is 10-12 mu m, preferably 10 kg and the fluidized bed has a sample volume of 8-10 kg, preferably 8 kg at a time. The carbon dioxide supercritical extraction process is carried out at 35-36 degrees C, preferably 35 degrees C under extraction pressure 25-28 Mpa, preferably 25 Mpa for 6-7 hours, preferably 7 hours at carbon dioxide flow rate of 400-600 l/hour preferably 500 l/hour.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.)

PN JP2020049469-A; JP7151333-B2
TI Carbon-film module for fluid separation used for            gas separation and carbon dioxide separation from            original gas of natural gas comprises carbon-film            module fixed with potting resin obtained by hardening            composition containing curing agent.
AU HORIGUCHI T
   MIHARA M
   TANAKA K
AE TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
   TORAY IND INC (TORA-C)
GA 202025087D
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-film module for fluid separation                comprises carbon-film module fixed with potting                resin in which carbon-film module has carbon atom                ratios of 63-90 at.% and the potting resin is a                resin obtained by hardening composition containing                (a) bisphenol-type epoxy compound and (b) curing                agent selected from alicyclic amine and aromatic                amine.
   USE - A carbon-film module for fluid separation used                for gas separation and carbon dioxide separation                from original gas of natural gas (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-film module exhibits sufficient                durability in separation of natural gas in                environment with organic compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Curing agent is                  alicyclic amine having cyclohexyl group. Curing                  agent is bis(4-aminocyclohexyl)methane. Ratio of                  oxygen atom with respect to all atoms in outer                  surface of carbon film for fluid separation is                  0.1-25 at.%. Ratio of nitrogen atom with respect                  to all atoms in cross section of carbon film is                  0.1-30 at.%. The carbon film is fibrous film in                  which dense layer is formed in outer periphery of                  porous portion which has porous structure. The                  porous structure is three-dimensional meshwork                  structure. The three-dimensional meshwork                  structure is co-continuous porous structure.                  Preferred Parameters: The structure period of                  co-continuous porous structure is 50-10000                  nm.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a composite material comprising a polymer and a fibrous carbon nanostructure involves contacting the fibrous carbon nanostructure with an organic solvent to obtain a mixture containing the fibrous carbon nanostructure and the organic solvent and kneading a composition containing a polymer and the mixture under a carbon dioxide atmosphere in a subcritical state or a supercritical state.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a composite material used for manufacturing a molded article.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the composite material in which fibrous carbon nanostructures are well dispersed in a polymer matrix. The composite material produces molded article having excellent conductivity, thermal conductivity and strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves contacting the fibrous carbon nanostructure and the organic solvent with each other for greater than or equal to 1 week. The method involves contacting the fibrous carbon nanostructure and the organic solvent in an amount of 10-20 times in mass ratio. Preferred Components: The fibrous carbon nanostructure comprises single-walled carbon nanotubes. Preferred Property: The fibrous carbon nanostructure has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific surface area of greater than or equal to 600 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is at least one chosen from tetrahydrofuran, methyl ethyl ketone, cyclohexane, isopropyl alcohol and toluene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer is at least one chosen from fluororubber, nitrile rubber, hydrogenated nitrile rubber, fluororesin, acrylic resin, polystyrene and polycarbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Preparing 1,2-dichlorobenzene waste removal catalyst comprises e.g. adding chromium-containing waste liquid in sealed container, aerating carbon dioxide, adding ferrous carbonate, and performing thermal decomposition treatment.
AU HUANG T
   SONG D
   ZHANG S
   LIU L
   LIU W
   TAO J
   ZHOU L
   XU J
AE CHANGSHU INST TECHNOLOGY (CHGS-C)
GA 201986891M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 1,2-dichlorobenzene waste removal catalyst comprises adding chromium-containing waste liquid in a sealed container, aerating carbon dioxide into the container to obtain carbonated chromium-containing waste liquid, adding ferrous carbonate into carbonation chromium-containing waste liquid, and stirring under seal to obtain reduced carbonation chromium-containing waste liquid, adding calcium hydroxide into reduced carbonation chromium-containing waste liquid, stirring under a sealed condition to form precipitate, which is separated by solid and liquid, and placing the solid in an atmosphere furnace for thermal decomposition treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1,2-dichlorobenzene waste removal catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of carbon dioxide to chromium-containing waste liquid is 0.5-3:1, preferably 1-2:1. The molar ratio of ferrous ion in ferrous carbonate to hexavalent chromium ion in chromium-containing waste liquid is 4-8:1, preferably 5-7:1. The molar ratio of ferrous ion in ferrous carbonate to calcium ion in calcium hydroxide is 1:2-6, preferably 1:3-5. Preferred Method: The thermal decomposition treatment is performed at 900-1000 degrees C for 1-2 hours.

PN CN110152693-A
TI Preparing catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises dispersing bismuth oxychloride nanosheet in water adding copper source to dispersion and heat treating.
AU HE T
   WANG H
AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 201977318G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst, comprises (i) preparing a bismuth oxychloride i nanosheet; (ii) dispersing bismuth oxychloride nanosheet in water to obtain bismuth oxychloride nanosheet dispersion, adding copper source to dispersion and heat treating.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has small particle size and good dispersion uniformity, providing more activity for the catalyst without affecting the absorption of light by the bismuth oxychloride nanosheet site and . The method increases activity of catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for catalyst prepared by above method comprising a bismuth oxychloride nanosheet and a copper(II) oxide cluster dispersed on the bismuth oxychloride nanosheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a) the surfactant is a nonionic surfactant. The nonionic surfactant comprises mannitol and/or polyvinylpyrrolidone; preferably. The molecular weight of the polyvinylpyrrolidone is 6000-10000. The concentration of the surfactant in the mixed solution B is 0.01-0.03 g/ml. In the step (a) the cerium source is cerium nitrate, cerium chloride and/or cerium bromide. In the step (a) the solvent is mixture of water and ethylene glycol; preferably , where volume ratio of water and ethylene glycol is 0.6-1.2. In the step (b) the chlorine source solution is an aqueous solution containing saturated chlorine ions and the chlorine source. The step (b) comprises an aqueous solution of sodium chloride and/or an aqueous solution of potassium chloride. In the step (b) the volume ratio chlorine source solution and mixed solution A is 0.1-0.4. In the step (ii) the copper source comprises copper nitrate, copper chloride and/or copper sulfate and addition amount of copper source according to copper ion dispersion concentration of 0.1-1.5 mmol/l. The concentration of the Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet in the Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet dispersion is 5-10 g/l. Preferred Method: The preparation of bismuth oxychloride nanosheet comprises (a) mixing cerium source, surfactant, and solvent to obtain a mixed solution A; (b) adding a chlorine source solution to mixed solution A to obtain a mixed solution B; (c) subjecting mixed solution B to solvothermal reaction to obtain Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet In the step (a) the concentration of bismut(III)-ionin the mixed solution A is 0.01-0.08 mol/l; preferably. In the step (ii) the temperature of the heat treatment is 80-100 degrees C. The manner of the heat treatment comprises water bath heating. The heat treatment time in is 40-90 minutes and The heat treatment comprises washing product with water and drying. The method specifically comprises (i) mixing cerium source, surfactant, and solvent to obtain a mixed solution A; mixing concentration of 0.01-0.08 mol/l bismut(III)-ion in solution A where surfactant comprises mannitol and/or polyvinylpyrrolidone, the solvent is a mixture of water and ethylene glycol in ratio of 0.6-1.2, (ii ) adding saturated chlorine -containing aqueous solution to mixed solution to obtain a mixed solution B; the volume of the saturated chlorine containing aqueous solution and the mixed solution A and ratio of the surfactant in mixed solution B is 0.01-0.03 g/ml, (iii) solvothermal reaction of mixed solution B at 150-180 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours , perfroming separation solid-liquid, washing and drying, to obtain Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet, (iv) dispersing Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet in water to obtain a Bismuth oxychloride nanosheet dispersion, adding a copper source, heat trating at 80-100 degrees C for 40-90 minute and obtaining catalyst, where nanosheet in bismuth oxychloride nanosheet dispersion degree of 5-10g / L, copper source is added in an amount concentration of copper ions in the dispersion liquid of 0.1-1.5 mmol/l.

TI Extracting Prinsepia utilis Royle oil composition, by mixing sieved Prinsepia utilis kernel powder and red raspberry leaf powder, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and mixing with e.g. ceramide, nicotinamide and glycerin.
AU FAN J
   FU Q
AE FEIYAO BIOTECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (FEIY-Non-standard)
GA 201911511B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting Prinsepia utilis Royle oil composition, involves (a) crushing P.utilis kernel, sieving using a 30-70 mesh sieve, crushing red raspberry leaves, sieving using 30-70 mesh sieve, and mixing P.utilis kernel powder and red raspberry leaf powder uniformly to obtain a mixture, (b) subjecting the mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and separating to obtain a mixed extract, and (c) mixing the mixed extract uniformly with ( beta )-cyclodextrin, ceramide, nicotinamide, glycerin, lecithin and deionized water.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting P.utilis oil composition used in a skin care product (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of P.utilis kernel powder and red raspberry leaf powder is (6-9):(1-2), preferably 8:1.5. Preferred Properties: The step (a) involves sieving P.utilis kernel powder using a 40 mesh sieve and sieving red raspberry leaf powder using a 45 mesh sieve. Preferred Conditions: The step (b) involves carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 62-65 degrees C under a pressure of 45-50 MPa and separating at 20-25 degrees C under a pressure of 13-14 MPa.

PN CN109261705-A; CN109261705-B
TI Processing arsenic-contaminated soil involves placing arsenic-contaminated soil in high-temperature and high-pressure reactor, adding metal binder, introducing carbon dioxide gas, heating, adding water and oxidizing agent and reacting.
AU TIAN S
   ZHANG Y
   LI Y
   HUANG J
   HU X
   NING P
   GU J
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2019114731
AB    NOVELTY - The processing method of arsenic-contaminated soil involves placing arsenic-contaminated soil in high-temperature and high-pressure reactor, adding metal binder, introducing carbon dioxide gas at 40-80 degrees C under pressure of 10-45 MPa and then heating, pressurizing and then extracting for 15-120 minutes, adding water and oxidizing agent, uniformly mixing, heating at 380-650 degrees C, reacting at 460-650 degrees C for 5-45 minutes, performing solid-liquid separation at 380-460 degrees C, cooling to below 100 degrees C and separating.
   USE - The method is useful for processing arsenic-contaminated soil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidizing agent is hydrogen peroxide, oxygen or ozone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further involves using modifier including methanol, ethanol and acetone. Preferred Components: The metal binder is phase transfer catalyst including ammonium quaternary ammonium bromide salt comprising tetramethylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrapropylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetrapentylammonium bromide, tetrahexylammonium bromide, tetraheptylammonium bromide and tetraoctylammonium bromide.

PT P
PN CN108606289-A
TI Jelly, prepared by adding sodium bicarbonate and citric acid into jelly raw materials, and preparing carbon dioxide gas-containing jelly.
AU MENG W
   XU Y
AE FUJIAN QINQIN CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
   QUANZHOU QINQIN FOODS CO LTD (QUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018805018
AB    NOVELTY - Jelly, prepared by adding sodium bicarbonate and citric acid into jelly raw materials, and preparing carbon dioxide gas-containing jelly, is claimed.
   USE - The jelly is useful for quenching thirst (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The jelly is refreshing and cooling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing jelly comprises (i) adding sodium bicarbonate into water to obtain sodium bicarbonate solution; (ii) adding citric acid into water to obtain citric acid solution; (iii) dissolving jelly powder, white granulated sugar, potassium sorbate and remaining water, heating to 82-88 degrees C, adding sodium bicarbonate solution to obtain jelly liquid; (iv) uniformly mixing jelly liquid and citric acid solution; (v) sealing and filling the liquid into semi-finished product; and (vi) inverting, sterilizing and cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The jelly comprises 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. jelly powder, 10-15 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate, 1-2.5 pts. wt. citric acid, 0.03-0.04 pts. wt. potassium sorbate and 75-100 pts. wt. water. The jelly powder comprises carrageenan, konjac powder and potassium chloride in a ratio of 1-2:1-1.5:1-2. The pH of the water is 6-7 and hardness is 10 mg/l calcium carbonate. Preferred Method: In the step (iv) before the filling, the jelly liquid is passed into the filler through the first conveying pipe, then through a second conveying pipe and pump, uniformly mixing the citric acid solution with the jelly material liquid into the first conveying pipe. The jelly liquid and citric acid solution are uniformly mixed at a ratio of 10:0.4-1. The pH of the jelly solution after acid addition was adjusted to 3.8-4.2. The jelly material liquid filling temperature is controlled at 80 plus minus 2 degrees C. The semi-finished product inverted insulation sterilization is carried out for 10-12 minutes, then rapidly cooling to 25-20 degrees C by a cooling tank.

PN KR1897134-B1; WO2019199015-A1; CN111937220-A; EP3767737-A1; US2021028515-A1; JP2021521580-W; EP3767737-A4
TI Lithium precursor regeneration used lithium            secondary battery, involves preparing waste            lithium-containing mixture, and then subjecting spent            lithium-containing mixture to hydrogen reduction            treatment to give preliminary precursor mixture.
AU NA J Y
   KOO M S
   LA YEON H
   SON S R
   LA Y H
   NA J
   JU M
   LUO L
   SUN C
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 2018714115
AB    NOVELTY - Lithium precursor regeneration involves                preparing waste lithium-containing mixture, and                then subjecting the spent lithium-containing                mixture to a hydrogen reduction treatment to                prepare preliminary precursor mixture containing                lithium hydroxide and lithium carbonate. The                preliminary precursor mixture is washed with water                to convert all the lithium carbonate to lithium                hydroxide. The flushing process is carried out in a                carbon dioxide-free atmosphere by purging with a                gas lacking carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for regenerating lithium precursor from                used lithium secondary battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method regenerates the lithium precursor                in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner                with high purity and improved selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The cathode active                  material mixture is prepared by separating                  positive electrode including positive electrode                  collector, positive electrode active material,                  binder and conductive material from the waste                  lithium secondary battery, and then removing the                  positive electrode current collector by                  pulverizing or treating the separated positive                  electrode with an organic solvent. The hydrogen                  reduction process is performed through a                  fluidized bed reactor utilizing hydrogen gas. The                  precursor precursor mixture comprises a lithium                  precursor comprising lithium hydroxide and                  lithium carbonate, and a transition metal                  containing reactant. The preliminary lithium                  precursor also comprises lithium oxide. The water                  washing treatment is performed by dissolving                  lithium oxide contained in the preliminary                  lithium precursor in water to convert to lithium                  hydroxide. The aqueous solution of lithium                  hydroxide is produced through water washing                  treatment, where the transition metal-containing                  reactant is precipitated. The purging comprises                  nitrogen purge. The lithium precursor comprises                  lithium hydroxide.

PN CN107968039-A
TI Method for improving wafer surface stress, involves sequentially depositing carbon dioxide silicon and silicon mononitride on front and back of silicon wafer, followed by depositing layer of silicon dioxide on silicon mononitride surface.
AU XIA Y
   JIANG Y
   WANG Y
   LI J
   ZHANG J
AE YANGTZE MEMORY TECHNOLOGIES CO LTD (UNCL-C)
GA 201834375J
AB    NOVELTY - A wafer surface stress improving method involves sequentially depositing carbon dioxide silicon and silicon mononitride on the front and back of the silicon wafer, depositing a layer of silicon dioxide on the silicon mononitride surface to protect the silicon mononitride. The silicon dioxide on the front and back of the wafer is removed, removed the front silicon mononitride of the wafer and removed the back of the wafer.
   USE - Method for improving wafer surface stress.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the wafer surface stress with effectively reaching the needed degree, reducing the wafer center and edge coverage and the critical dimension difference and generation of edge peeling.

PN CN107827152-A; CN107827152-B
TI Preparation of blue titanium dioxide for use in photocatalytic synthesis of methane with carbon dioxide, involves mixing titanium dioxide, alkali metal, and ethylenediamine, solvothermally reacting, washing, and drying.
AU HUANG F
   BI Q
   YIN G
   HUANG X
AE SHANGHAI INST CERAMICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGU-C)
GA 2018243586
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of blue titanium dioxide, involves mixing titanium dioxide, an alkali metal, and ethylenediamine, solvothermally reacting at 100-200 degrees C for 10-48 hours, washing, and drying at -10 degrees C to 150 degrees C for 10-30 hours.
   USE - Preparation of blue titanium dioxide for use in photocatalytic synthesis of methane with carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of blue titanium dioxide having excellent photocatalytic activity, under mild and controllable conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for blue titanium dioxide comprising an amorphous titanium oxide shell layer including oxygen vacancies and hydrogen atom, coated on titanium dioxide crystal nucleus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Titanium dioxide is anatase titanium dioxide, rutile titanium dioxide, and/or plate titanium dioxide, preferably anatase titanium dioxide and rutile titanium dioxide in a mass ratio of (0.1-9):1. The alkali metal is lithium, sodium and/or potassium. The ratio of titanium dioxide and ethylenediamine is (100-900) mg:(10-60) ml. The mass ratio of alkali metal and titanium dioxide is 1:(1-10), preferably 1:(3-8). Washing is carried out using 0.01-0.5 mol/L, and then with water and alcohol.

PT P
PN EP3536823-A1; WO2019172750-A1; WO2019172750-A8; EP3762522-A1; US2021047743-A1; JP2021516290-W
TI Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide into            reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture and            in-situ extracting the reduced carbondioxide product or            product mixture from an electro-chemical cell involves            introducing absorbent.
AU GOETHEER E L V
   LATSUZBAIA R
   ANASTASOPOL A
   ROELANDS C P M
   BISSELINK R J M
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 201978454X
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide into                a reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture                and in-situ extracting the reduced carbondioxide                product or product mixture from an electro-chemical                cell involves introducing carbon dioxide-rich                absorbent in-to a cathode compartment of an                electrochemical cell. The electrical potential is                applied between an anode and a cathode in the                electrochemical cell sufficient for the cathode to                reduced carbon dioxide into a reduced carbon                dioxide product or product mixture in the carbon                dioxide-rich absorbent, hence provided a carbon                dioxide-poor absorbent. The reduced carbon dioxide                product or product mixture is collected through                in-situ extraction, where the anode is separated                from the cathode by more than one separator.
   USE - Method for electrochemically reducing carbon                dioxide into a reduced carbon dioxide                product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to electrochemically reduce                carbon dioxide into a reduced carbon dioxide                product, by providing a cost and energy efficient                method for electrochemically reducing carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for                  electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide into a                  reduced carbon dioxide product further involves                  contacting a carbon dioxide-containing gas stream                  with an absorbent, hence absorbing carbon dioxide                  from the carbon dioxide-containing gas stream to                  form the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent, and                  further comprises optionally recirculating a                  portion of the carbon dioxide-poor absorbent to                  absorb carbon dioxide from the carbon                  dioxide-containing gas stream. Preferred                  Conditions: The electrochemical cell is a                  three-compartment electrochemical cell comprises                  a cathode compartment, an anode compartment, a                  cation exchange membrane, an anion exchange                  membrane, and a product compartment, where the                  method further comprises a step of adding one                  salt to the cathode compartment and/or anodic                  compartment to improve electrical conductivity.                  The product compartment of the three-compartment                  electro-chemical cell is the compartment where                  from reduced carbon dioxide product or product                  mixture is in-situ extracted. The carbon dioxide                  is selectively absorbed by the absorbent, and the                  absorbent comprises one physical solvent and/or                  one chemical solvent. The carbon                  dioxide-containing gas stream with the absorbent                  is performed at a temperature of 5 degrees C or                  higher. The absolute pressure is 1 bar or more                  and 200 bar or less, and preferably 1 bar or more                  and 140 bar or less and/or the temperature is -10                  degrees C or more. The product compartment                  comprises an acidic environment where from                  reduced carbon dioxide product or product mixture                  is extracted, and where the acidic environment                  has a pH range of 0-7. The anode and cathode in                  the electrochemical cell are heated. The                  three-compartment electrochemical cell is used                  for in-situ extraction of organic carboxylic                  acids such asformic acid, acetic acid, oxalic                  acid, glycolic acid, gly-oxylic acid and/or                  salts. Preferred Components: The physical solvent                  comprises one or more selected fromthe group                  consisting of dimethyl ethers of polyethyl-ene                  glycol, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, methanol,                  ammonia, and alkylene carbonates such as                  propylenecarbonate. The chemical solvent                  comprises one amine, one alkanolamine, or a                  mixture, preferably the chemical solvent                  comprises monoeth-anolamine, diethanolamine,                  N-methyldieth-anolamine, dimethylethanolamine,                  diisopropylamine, aminomethyl propanol, ammonia,                  and/or diglycolamine. The reduced carbon dioxide                  product or product mixture comprises one or more                  selected from the group consisting of carbon                  monoxide, alkanes, alkenes, alcohols, carboxylic                  acids and/or salts of aldehydes, or                  ketones.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)
MC A05-H03; A12-E09; A12-W11D; E10-C02D1; E10-C04D1; E10-C04D4; E10-C04J1P; E10-C04J2P; E10-C04K; E10-D01C; E10-E04E; E10-F02B; E10-J02C3; E10-J02D; E11-D02; E11-F03; E11-N; E31-N05B1; J03-A; J03-B03

   LIU Y
   WANG X
   XIE J
   WANG W
   NIU N
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
   LIAOHE OILFIELD CONSTR CO LTD (LIAO-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 202062117N
AB    NOVELTY - Processing carbon dioxide automatic welding of                the tank flip below 50000 m3 comprises carrying out                carbon dioxide transverse welding operation, carbon                dioxide transverse welding current, where 120-150a                for bottom welding, 125-155a for bottom welding,                120-145a for upper welding, and 125-160a for cover                welding, carbon dioxide welding voltage, where                bottom welding is 21-23.5v, bottom filling welding                is 22-24.5v, upper filling welding is 21-23.5v, and                cover welding is 23-25.5v, the carbon dioxide gas                flow rate of carbon dioxide horizontal welding is                30-80 l/minutes, using carbon dioxide vertical                welding operation, using carbon dioxide vertical                welding current, where 105-130a for base welding,                110-140a for filling welding, and 120-155a for                cover welding, carbon dioxide vertical welding                voltage, 20-22v for bottom welding, 21-22.5v for                filling welding, and 19-22v for cover welding, and                carbon dioxide gas flow rate for carbon dioxide                vertical welding is 30-80 l/minutes.

PN US2021399328-A1
TI Carbon dioxide redox flow battery for small-scale            electrode screening experiments, comprises negative            electrode assembly comprising porous base layer,            bifunctional catalyst layer for electrochemical            reduction of either carbon dioxide.
AU GYENGE E
   GYENGE C
   HOSSEINI-BENHANGI P
AE AGORA ENERGY TECHNOLOGIES LTD (AGOR-Non-standard)
GA 2021E8404F
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide redox flow battery or CRB 10                comprises a negative electrode assembly 12                comprising a porous base layer, a bifunctional                catalyst layer for electrochemical reduction of                either carbon dioxide (CO2) or carbonate to formate                during battery charging and for formate oxidation                to either carbonate or CO2 during battery                discharge, and an intermediate support layer                supporting the bifunctional catalyst layer and                comprising a metal oxide. The metal oxide                comprises: a perovskite structure with the formula                AxByO3, where A is one or a mixture of Lanthanum                (La), strontium (Sr), and barium (Ba) and B is one                of cobalt (Co), titanium (Ti), iron (Fe), nickel                (Ni), gallium (Ga), magnesium (Mg), indium (In),                manganese (Mn), tantalum (Ta), or cerium (Ce), or a                fluorite structure with the formula AxByO7, where A                is neodymium (Nd), and B is iridium (Ir), a                positive electrode, and a cation exchange or                bipolar membrane in between the negative and                positive electrodes.
   USE - CRB configuration is used for small-scale                electrode screening experiments.
   ADVANTAGE - The negative electrode of a carbon dioxide                redox flow battery that directly utilizes CO2 as an                active species instead of metals. The CO2 batteries                could address simultaneously and in a flexible                manner the storage of intermittent renewable energy                sources and the utilization of CO2 captured from                industrial emission sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method for electrochemically activating                a negative electrode of a redox flow battery that                comprises the negative electrode, a positive                electrode, and a cation exchange or bipolar                membrane in between the negative and positive                electrodes, the negative electrode comprising a                porous base layer, a bifunctional catalyst layer                for electrochemical reduction of either CO2 or                carbonate to formate during battery charging and                for formate oxidation to either carbonate or CO2                during battery discharge, and an intermediate                support layer supporting the bifunctional catalyst                layer and comprising a metal oxide;
   (2) a method for manufacturing a bifunctional                negative electrode for a redox flow battery, which                involves
   (a) providing a porous carbon base                layer;
   (b) providing a deposition mixture for an                intermediate support layer comprising a metal oxide                material
   (c) providing a deposition mixture for a                bifunctional porous catalyst layer comprising a                bifunctional catalyst;
   (d) depositing by electro deposition or                mechanical spraying the intermediate support layer                deposition mixture onto the carbon base layer                followed by depositing by electro deposition or                mechanical spraying the catalyst layer onto the                intermediate support layer creating a metal oxide                supported catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                schematic views of a non-metal CO2 redox flow                battery.
   Redox flow battery or CRB (10)
   Negative electrode assembly (12)

AU ZHANG H
   ZHAO J
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202077214E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polyol compound (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing aqueous polyurethane dispersion for preparing aqueous polyurethane adhesive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has alkali-resistance and oxidation-resistance; and realizes recycling of polyethylene terephthalate; and is safe and environmentally friendly. The aqueous polyurethane adhesive has excellent bonding performance, alkali resistance and oxidation resistance, T-type peeling strength of not less than 80 N/50 mm, T-type peeling strength (alkali resistance 360 hours) of more than 70 N/50 mm, T-type peeling strength (alkali resistance 360 hours) retention rate of not less than 90%, T-type peeling strength (oxidation resistance 360 hours) of more than 70 N/50 mm and T-type peeling strength (oxidation resistance 360 hours) retention rate of more than 89 N/50 mm.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide-based polyol compound of formula (I) is new.
   m = 3-60.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) aqueous polyurethane dispersion comprising 85-140 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polyol, 23-45 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 3.5-16.5 pts. wt. chain extender 1, 2.1-3.5 pts. wt. chain extender 2, 0.01-0.18 pt. wt. catalyst 2, 48-75 pts. wt. solvent, 1.8-10.2 pts. wt. neutralizing agent and 110-160 pts. wt. deionized water, where the chain extender 1 is carboxylic acid and/or amine-type compound, and the chain extender 2 is alcohol-type compound;
   (3) preparing aqueous polyurethane dispersion, comprising (1) reacting carbon dioxide-based polyol and diisocyanate in a part of solvent under condition of protective gas to obtain first intermediate, (2) reacting first intermediate, chain extender 1, chain extender 2 and catalyst 2 in the remaining solvent to obtain second intermediate, and (3) mixing second intermediate and neutralizing agent, mixing with water to emulsify and distilling under reduced pressure to obtain final product;
   (4) aqueous polyurethane adhesive comprising 100-240 pts. wt. aqueous polyurethane dispersion, 20-85 pts. wt. heavy calcium carbonate, 0.08-0.55 pt. wt. gas phase white carbon black, 0.03-0.58 pts. wt. aqueous dispersant, 0.11-0.32 pts. wt. aqueous antifoaming agent and 0.35-1.28 pts. wt. aqueous thickening agent; and
   (5) preparing aqueous polyurethane adhesive, comprising (a) mixing aqueous polyurethane dispersion, heavy calcium carbonate, gas phase white carbon black and aqueous antifoaming agent, and grinding to obtain first component, and (b) stirring first component, aqueous dispersant and aqueous thickening agent at a rotating speed of 1800-2200 revolutions/minute for 2-4 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The diisocyanate is toluene diisocyanate, xylene diisocyanate, xylylene diisocyanate, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate and/or dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, the chain extender 1 is dimethylolpropionic acid, dimethylolbutyric acid, N-methyldiethanolamine, N-butyldiethanolamine, 2,2'-iminodiethanol and/or trihydroxyethylamine, the chain extender 2 is ethylene glycol, butylene glycol and/or diethylene glycol, the catalyst 2 is N,N-dimethylhexadecylamine, N-methylmorpholine, stannous octoate, dibutyltin dilaurate, zinc isooctanoate, nickel isooctanoate or zinc naphthenate, the neutralizing agent is triethylamine, aqueous ammonia, dimethylethanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine, trifluoroacetic acid and/or concentrated hydrochloric acid, and the solvent is butanone, cyclohexanone or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) mixing polyethylene terephthalate material, poly(carbonate-ether) diol and catalyst 1, and reacting by stirring at 180-220 degrees C for 2-10 hours, and (ii) cooling step (i) product to 130-150 degrees C and vacuumizing for 1 hour to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The mass ratio between polyethylene terephthalate material, poly(carbonate-ether) diol and catalyst 1 is 150-230:380-1200:0.28-0.96, the catalyst 1 is zinc acetate, manganese(II) acetate, manganese(II) phosphate, zinc phosphate, calcium phosphate, 3A molecular sieve, stannous chloride or tetrabutyl titanate, the polyethylene terephthalate material is waste polyethylene terephthalate beverage bottle sheet and/or waste polyethylene terephthalate transparent packaging box sheet, the number-average molecular weight of poly(carbonate-ether) diol is 2000-7000, and the content of carbonate in poly(carbonate-ether) diol is 30-70 wt.%. Preferred Method: In step (1), the reaction temperature is 85-90 degrees C and the reaction time is 2-4 hours. In step (2), the reaction temperature is 60-65 degrees C and the reaction time is 1.5- 3 hours.

TI Preparation of aminobenzoic acid or aminobenzoic            acid derivative product involves fermenting specific            raw material, converting aminobenzoate to water-soluble            form, introducing carbon dioxide, precipitating and            separating.
AU KLOECKNER W
   BEGGEL F
   JAEGER G
   KLAFFL S
   OLF G
   BERGER F
   KLAFF S
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY GMBH & CO (CVST-C)
GA 2019A3784L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aminobenzoic acid or                aminobenzoic acid derivative product involves                fermenting a raw material containing a fermentable                carbon-containing compound (AA), and a                nitrogen-containing compound (BB), in a reactor                using microorganisms and a calcium salt to obtain a                mixture comprising undissolved microorganisms and                precipitated calcium aminobenzoate, separating the                reaction product from fermentation solution,                converting aminobenzoate to a water-soluble form to                form a water-insoluble calcium salt other than                calcium aminobenzoate, producing a suspension                containing precipitated calcium salt comprising                undissolved microorganisms and the water-insoluble                calcium salt, separating aqueous solution of                aminobenzoate, introducing carbon dioxide at                pressure of 1.5 bar or more, separating deposited                aminobenzoic acid, and an aqueous solution depleted                in carbon dioxide and free of deposited                aminobenzoic acid, and optionally converting                aminobenzoic acid to an aminobenzoic acid                derivative product.
   USE - Preparation of aminobenzoic acid or                aminobenzoic acid derivative product.
   ADVANTAGE - The aminobenzoic acid can be prepared                economically and industrially by simple                method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of aminobenzoic acid or                aminobenzoic acid derivative product involves                fermenting (DD) a raw material containing a                fermentable carbon-containing compound, preferably                starch hydrolyzate, sugar cane juice, sugar beet                juice, hydrolyzates from raw materials containing                lignocellulose or its mixtures, and a                nitrogen-containing compound, preferably ammonia                gas, aqueous ammonia, ammonium salts and/or urea,                in a fermentation reactor using microorganisms (GG)                and a calcium salt (CC) to obtain a mixture                comprising undissolved microorganisms and                precipitated calcium aminobenzoate, separating (FF)                the reaction product from aqueous fermentation                solution, converting aminobenzoate to a                water-soluble form to form a water-insoluble                calcium salt other than calcium aminobenzoate by                adding an aqueous phase containing water-soluble                aminobenzoate salt-forming cations and                water-insoluble calcium salt-forming anions,                producing a suspension containing the precipitated                water-insoluble calcium salt or a mixture                comprising undissolved microorganisms and the                water-insoluble calcium salt, separating aqueous                solution of aminobenzoate, introducing carbon                dioxide at pressure of 1.5 bar or more, separating                deposited aminobenzoic acid, and an aqueous                solution depleted in carbon dioxide and free of                deposited aminobenzoic acid, and optionally                converting aminobenzoic acid to an aminobenzoic                acid derivative product. The derivative product is                produced by decarboxylation of aminobenzoic acid to                aniline, or acid catalyzed reaction of the aniline                with formaldehyde to form di- and polyamines of the                diphenylmethane compounds, or reaction of                diphenylmethane compounds with phosgene to form di-                and polyisocyanates of diphenylmethane compounds,                or conversion of aniline to an azo compound, or                conversion of aminobenzoic acid to an amide, or                conversion of the aminobenzoic acid into conductive                polymers, preferably polyanthranilic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram explaining                the preparation of aminobenzoic acid.
   Carbon compound (AA)
   Nitrogen compound (BB)
   Calcium salt (CC)
   Fermentation (DD)
   Separation (FF)
   Microorganisms (GG)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The microorganisms                  contain Escherichia coli, Pseudomonas putida,                  Corynebacterium glutamicum, Ashbya gossypii,                  Pichia pastoris, Hansenula polymorpha, Yarrowia                  lipolytica, Zygosaccharomyces bailii or                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The calcium salt is                  calcium carbonate, calcium hydrogen carbonate,                  calcium hydroxide and/or calcium oxide. The                  aqueous phase contains lithium, sodium, potassium                  and/or ammonium cations, preferably ammonium                  cations, and carbonate and/or bicarbonate                  anions.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The fermentation is                  carried out discontinuously in fermentation                  cycles. The aqueous fermentation solution is                  discharged from the fermentation reactor, and                  aqueous phase is added to the fermentation                  reactor to produce a suspension containing                  mixture comprising undissolved microorganisms and                  the water-insoluble calcium salt. The aqueous                  solution of aminobenzoate, and mixture comprising                  undissolved microorganisms and the                  water-insoluble calcium salt are subjected to                  separation, and recycled for next fermentation                  cycle. The processes are repeated until the                  desired amount of aminobenzoic acid is obtained.                  Acid is added to aqueous fermentation solution,                  and pH is adjusted to 3-4. The crystallized                  aminobenzoic acid is separated, and mother liquor                  depleted in aminobenzoic acid is separated. The                  aqueous fermentation solution is returned to the                  fermentation. The deposited aminobenzoic acid and                  the aqueous solution are separated at pressure                  more than or equal to 1.5 bar.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.)

TI Nasal head for use in nasal oxygen tube device, has carbon dioxide discharge port for connecting monitoring tube, and carbon dioxide collecting opening for introducing carbon dioxide exhaled by patient into collecting cavity.
AU QIU B
   HU D
AE NINGBO SHENGYURUI MEDICAL APPLIANCES CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202202773W
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a nose, comprising an oxygen output cavity, a nose main body of carbon dioxide collecting cavity; the nose main body is provided with an oxygen input port respectively with the oxygen output cavity, an oxygen output port, carbon dioxide collecting opening carbon dioxide discharge port respectively with the carbon dioxide collecting cavity; the oxygen input port is used for connecting the oxygen inlet pipe; the oxygen output port is used for discharging the oxygen in the oxygen output cavity; the carbon dioxide discharge port is used for connecting the monitoring tube; carbon dioxide is collecting opening for introducing the carbon dioxide exhaled by the patient into the carbon dioxide collecting cavity. the nasal head main body is provided with a carbon dioxide collecting cavity and carbon dioxide collecting opening cavity connected with carbon dioxide, capable of collecting the patient exhaled carbon dioxide and conveying to the monitoring tube through the carbon dioxide collecting cavity, convenient for detecting carbon dioxide concentration. The utility model further claims a nasal oxygen tube device using the nose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a nasal oxygen tube device.

   TANG J
   TANG G
AE NANJING OURGREEN ENERGY SAVING & ENVIRON (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202252220J
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model model claims an XPS extruded sheet carbon dioxide pressurizing system, comprising orderly connected carbon dioxide storage tank, carbon dioxide pump, carbon dioxide buffer tank and carbon dioxide constant temperature tank, the carbon dioxide buffer tank is further connected with a pressure transducer and a frequency converter, the frequency converter is connected to the carbon dioxide pump; the carbon dioxide constant temperature tank is further connected with a temperature control device. The utility model model uses frequency conversion control, temperature control, which greatly improves the stability of carbon dioxide pressure, saves energy, and the safety of the device operation.

PT P
PN US2021069632-A1
TI Carbon dioxide capture system of mobile carbon dioxide capture system for reducing carbon dioxide in exhaust stream, has carbon dioxide membrane unit that is configured to channel lean carbon dioxide effluent stream to first heat exchanger.
AU HOFER D C
   DIPIETRO J P
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 202127247L
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a first heat exchanger (110) configured to exchange heat between an exhaust stream and a lean carbon dioxide effluent stream. A turboexpander (112) is provided with a compressor (116) coupled in flow communication with said first heat exchanger and configured to compress the exhaust stream and a turbine (118) coupled in flow communication with the first heat exchanger. The compressor is driven by the turbine. The turbine is configured to expand the lean carbon dioxide effluent stream. A carbon dioxide membrane unit (114) is coupled in flow communication with the compressor. The carbon dioxide membrane unit is configured to separate the exhaust stream into the lean carbon dioxide effluent stream and a rich carbon dioxide effluent stream. The carbon dioxide membrane unit is configured to channel the lean carbon dioxide effluent stream to the first heat exchanger.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture system of mobile carbon dioxide capture system (claimed) for reducing carbon dioxide in exhaust stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovered energy is used to power compressor that saves energy and reduces the energy consumption per unit of carbon dioxide recovered by recovery system. The exhaust gas stream is reduced to a safe operating temperature, and third heat exchanger cools first cooled exhaust gas stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method of reducing carbon dioxide in an exhaust stream; and
   (2) a mobile carbon dioxide capture system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system for use in recovering carbon dioxide from an exhaust gas stream.
   Carbon dioxide capture system (100)
   First heat exchanger (110)
   Turboexpander (112)
   Carbon dioxide membrane unit (114)

Without heating, the reaction of dissolution and interaction of the components with each other takes much longer. After complete dissolution of bulk materials, water was added to the resulting solution to a final volume of 1 l to give dink.

TI Preparing in vitro human testicular spermatogenesis model, by separating human testis tissue into small blocks and placing tissue on agar block, adding complete testicular medium to agar block and incubating product.
AU SHA J
   ZHOU Q
   GUO X
   YUAN Y
   LI L
AE UNIV NANJING MEDICAL (UYNM-C)
GA 201965119N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing in vitro human testicular spermatogenesis model, involves separating human testis tissue into small blocks after ex vivo process, placing tissue blocks on a 0.25-1.5% agar block replaced with complete testicular medium for at least 8 hours, adding complete testicular medium to one-third to two-thirds of the height of the agar block and incubating at 32-37 degrees C and carbon dioxide concentration of 5% for 10-100 days.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing in vitro human testicular spermatogenesis model.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The human testicular tissue includes human testes of various ages. The concentration of agar block is 0.25%. The culture temperature is 34 degrees C. The complete testicular medium comprises 90 volume/volume% alpha minimum essential medium (MEM), 10 volume/volume% serum replacement (knockout serum replacement (KOSR)), 10-50 ng/ml glial cell-derived neurotrophic factor (GDNF), 10-50 ng/ml basic fibroblast growth factor (BFGF), 10-50 ng/ml epidermal growth factor (EGF), 50-100 ng/ml transforming growth factor beta family activin A, 10-50 ng/ml stem cell growth factor (SCF), 10-50 ng/ml bone morphogenetic protein (BMP 4/7), 10-50 mM testosterone, 100-200 ng/ml follicle stimulating hormone (FSH) and 50-100 mg/ml bovine pituitary extract (BPE).

PT P
PN CN109666038-A; CN109666038-B
TI Producing trimethylchlorosilane involves adding hexamethyldisiloxane, bis(trichloromethyl)carbonate and catalyst to solvent to obtain reaction mixture, and adding initiator to reaction mixture to carry out reaction.
AU MI Z
   XUE T
   LIU M
   SONG B
   CUI L
   WANG J
   FENG L
AE NORTH CHINA PHARM CO LTD (NCHP-Non-standard)
GA 201939966E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing trimethylchlorosilane involves adding hexamethyldisiloxane, bis(trichloromethyl)carbonate and a catalyst to a solvent to obtain a reaction mixture, and adding initiator to the reaction mixture to carry out a reaction, and post-treating the reaction to obtain trimethylchlorosilane.
   USE - Method for producing trimethylchlorosilane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce trimethylchlorosilane, which is safe, controllable, easy to operate, environmental friendly, obtained product has high purity, and by-product is only carbon dioxide gas, which eliminates hydrogen chloride which is a major corrosion source in industrial production, greatly improves working environment in the production process, and has broad industrial application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is selected from one of tetrahexylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetrabutylammonium bromide, tetra-n-octyl ammonium bromide, benzyltriethylammonium bromide, or mixture obtained by mixing in any ratio. The ratio of the catalyst to hexamethyldisiloxane is from 1:50-1:175 g/ml. The initiator is N,N-dimethylformamide, and its volume ratio to hexamethyldisiloxane is from 8:10-8:35. The solvent is dichloromethane and its volume ratio to hexamethyldisiloxane is from 1:0.5-1:1.5. The temperature at which bis(trichloromethyl) carbonate added to the solvent is 20 plus minus 5 degrees C. The temperature at which the initiator added to the reaction mixture is 0-10 degrees C. The reaction temperature is 30-45 degrees C. The post-treatment is vacuum distillation, and the temperature at which the vacuum distillation is carried out is 30-35 degrees C. The post-treatment is carried out to obtain trimethylchlorosilane, the solvent and the remaining reaction solution. The solvent, hexamethylsiloxane and bis(trichloromethyl) carbonate are added to the remaining reaction solution for the next batch of reaction processes.
DC E11 (Containing P and/or Si.)
MC E05-E02B; E11-F08; E11-K03; E11-W; N05-D; N05-E03; N07-D09; N07-K01

PT P
PN US2018371180-A1; TW201905035-A; TW666231-B1; US10508177-B2
TI Purifying polybutylene terephthalate involves providing or receiving initial polybutylene terephthalate, where initial polybutylene terephthalate comprises oligomers and dissolving initial polybutylene terephthalate in hexafluoroisopropanol.
AU TAN C
   TAN C S
AE UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
   CHANGCHUN PLASTICS CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
   CHANGCHUN PETROCHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4166V
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying polybutylene terephthalate involves providing or receiving initial polybutylene terephthalate, where initial polybutylene terephthalate comprises oligomers, dissolving initial polybutylene terephthalate in hexafluoroisopropanol to form a polybutylene terephthalate/hexafluoroisopropanol solution. The oligomers are dissolved in hexafluoroisopropanol, and contacted the polybutylene terephthalate/hexafluoroisopropanol solution with compressed carbon dioxide (CO2) at a temperature and a pressure, and precipitated purified polybutylene terephthalate. The portion of the oligomers are still dissolved in hexafluoroisopropanol, and the temperature is in a range of 20-35 degrees C, and the pressure is in a range of 900-1400 pounds per square inch.
   USE - Method form purifying polybutylene terephthalate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The polybutylene terephthalate/hexafluoroisopropanol solution is treated with compressed carbon dioxide by allowing the polybutylene terephthalate/ hexafluoroisopropanol solution to form multiple droplets in the compressed carbon dioxide. The temperature is in a range of 22-26 degrees C. The pressure is in a range of 1100 -1300 pounds per square inch. Preferred Compositions: The weight of the initial polybutylene terephthalate in the polybutylene terephthalate/ hexafluoroisopropanol solution is in a range of 3-6 wt.%. The weight of the initial polybutylene terephthalate in the polybutylene terephthalate/hexafluoroisopropanol solution is 1-8 wt.%. Preferred Process: The polybutylene terephthalate/ hexafluoroisopropanol solution is contacted with compressed carbon dioxide involves precipitating a polybutylene terephthalate particles, where each of the polybutylene terephthalate particles having an average particle diameter in a range of about 0.1-0.4 microns, and further involves introducing the polybutylene terephthalate/ hexafluoroisopropanol solution into a high-pressure precipitation tank containing the compressed carbon dioxide. The method further involves drying the purified polybutylene terephthalate using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)

PN CN108690335-A
TI Sterile sampling swab comprises swab rod made of            polylactic acid, and sampling head connected to one end            of swab rod.
AU YE C
   HUANG J
AE HUIZHOU FALUYUAN GENE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201886259X
AB    NOVELTY - Sterile sampling swab comprises swab rod made                of polylactic acid, and sampling head connected to                one end of swab rod.
   USE - Sterile sampling swab.
   ADVANTAGE - The sterile sampling swab is                environment-friendly and biodegradable, preferably                it can be completely degraded by microorganisms in                nature under certain conditions and produces carbon                dioxide and water, which does not pollute                environment. It has simple preparation                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of sterile sampling swab by mixing                polylactic acid, toughening agent and plasticizer;                injection molding to obtain swab rod; applying                medical aqueous glue to one end of swab rod; and                flocking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The toughening agent is                  calcium carbonate, preferably precipitated                  calcium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polylactic acid has                  melting point of 155-185 degrees C. The swab rod                  preferably comprises 70 wt.% polylactic acid, 20                  wt.% toughening agent and 10 wt.% plasticizer.                  The plasticizer is polyethylene glycol with                  number average molecular weight of 6000-12000.                  The sampling head comprises flock hair, aqueous                  glue and curing agent, in which aqueous glue is                  aqueous polyurethane flocking glue used as                  800-1200 ml of per 5000-6000 g flock hair, and                  curing agent is diethylene triamine and propylene                  oxide butyl ether additive used at 1-3% per 1000                  ml aqueous glue. Preferred Method: The injection                  molding is performed under feed temperature at                  140-160 degrees C, compression section                  temperature at 150-170 degrees C, injection mold                  temperature at 30-60 degrees C, injection molding                  pressure at 60-100 MPa, molding time of 3-5                  seconds, and molding dwell time of 1-2 s.

TI Producing cement clinker, comprises providing raw materials, mixing and optionally grinding all raw materials, and heating the raw materials, in which cement clinkers are formed from the raw materials.
AU VIETEN J
AE DEUT ZENT LUFT & RAUMFAHRT EV (DELF-C)
GA 201879237G
AB    NOVELTY - Producing cement clinker, comprises: (a) providing raw materials, (b) mixing and optionally grinding all raw materials, and (c) heating the raw materials to a temperature of greater than or equal to 1000 degrees C, where cement clinkers are formed from the raw materials. At least one raw material is at least partially replaced by a cement clinker precursor including intermetallic compounds and alloys.
   USE - The method is useful for producing cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes renewable energies, e.g. concentrated solar energy, and prevents the release of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises a step of producing a cement clinker precursor, where the energy required for the production of the cement clinker precursor is provided by means of regenerative energies, preferably by solar energy. In step (a) raw materials comprising limestone, clay, oxides of silicon, oxides of aluminum, oxides of iron, granulated slag, pozzolan, fly ash and mixtures thereof are provided. In step (a) at least limestone is provided and the cement clinker precursor comprises calcium. The cement clinker precursor is an intermetallic compound and, besides calcium, further comprises iron, aluminum, silicon, titanium, tungsten and/or carbon. The intermetallic compound is a silicide, preferably calcium-silicon. The cement clinker precursor is stable in air. The raw material, in preferably limestone, is completely replaced by a cement clinker precursor. The raw material, preferably limestone, is partly replaced by a cement clinker precursor, where the proportion of the cement clinker precursor is selected such that the heating in step (c) takes place at least partially by the energy released in the formation of the cement clinker. The energy required for step (c) is provided by regenerative energies, preferably by solar energy. The step (c) takes place in the presence of oxygen. The oxygen is present in elemental (O2) or bound, preferably in the form of water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI New temperature-sensitive block copolymer i.e. 4,4-difluoro-N-isopropyl-2-methylhexanamide polymer, prepared by e.g. reacting initiator, chain transfer agent, polyvinylidene fluoride, carbon dioxide, vacuumizing, dialyzing and drying.
AU ZHANG L
   CAO Y
   ZHANG S
   ZHU Z
   XU A
   YANG T
   LIU Q
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
GA 201849620F
AB    NOVELTY - Temperature-sensitive block copolymer i.e. 4,4-difluoro-N-isopropyl-2-methylhexanamide polymer (I) are new.
   USE - Used as temperature-sensitive block copolymer i.e. 4,4-difluoro-N-isopropyl-2-methylhexanamide polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has simple technique and operation; is environmentally friendly, safe and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Temperature-sensitive block copolymer i.e. 4,4-difluoro-N-isopropyl-2-methylhexanamide polymer of formula (I) are new.
   n = 20-150; and
   m = 20-150.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) adding initiator and chain transfer agent in a high pressure reaction kettle, vacuumizing, adding polyvinylidene fluoride, carbon dioxide under nitrogen atmosphere, reacting at certain temperature to obtain macromolecular chain transfer agent, (ii) dissolving obtained macromolecular chain transfer agent and temperature sensitive monomer in solvent, then adding initiator, vacuumizing and reacting under nitrogen atmosphere, and (iii) dialyzing with distilled water, and drying to obtain (I). Preferred Components: The average molecular weight of the temperature-sensitive block copolymer is 9000-20000, and the degree of polymerization is 20-103. In step (i), and step (ii), the initiator is tert-butyl peroxypivalate, tert-butyl peroxyneodecanoate, tert-butyl peroxy-2-ethylhexanoate or tert-butyl peroxy-3,5,5-trimethylhexanoate. In step (i), the chain transfer agent is perfluorobutane, perfluorohexyl iodide and/or perfluorooctyl iodide; the mass ratio of initiator to polyvinylidene fluoride is 0.1-0.5:20-100, the mass ratio of chain transfer agent to polyvinylidene fluoride is 1-5:20-100, and the mass ratio of carbon dioxide to polyvinylidene fluoride is 5-25:2-10. In step (ii), the temperature-sensitive monomer is N-isopropyl acrylamide, and the solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methylpyrrolidone and dimethylsulfoxide; mass ratio of macromolecular chain transfer agent to temperature sensitive monomer is 1-5:2-12.5, the mass ratio of macromolecular chain transfer agent to solvent is 1-5:50-150, and the mass ratio of the macromolecular chain transfer agent to initiator is 2-10:0.1-0.5. Preferred Method: In steps (i) or (ii), the pressure of the reaction kettle is 20-40 MPa, it is replaced by nitrogen, the oxygen content in the reaction kettle is less than 10 parts per million, the reaction temperature is 50-80 degrees C, and the reaction time is 8-24 hours. In step (iii), the dialysis is carried out in 9000-20000 molecular weight of dialysis bag, dialyzing time is 3 times, and the drying condition is 60 degrees C and drying time is 12 hours.
