   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system for treating radioactive carbon-containing carbon dioxide, comprising a carbon dioxide gas cylinder for supplying carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon, a carbon dioxide capture reactor for liquefying the supplied carbon dioxide and converting it into carbonate, and a carbon dioxide detector for detecting the generation of the uncollected carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide collection reactor includes silica-based collector; and
   (2) treating carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon comprising liquefying carbon dioxide containing radioactive carbon to the carbon dioxide capture reactor, converting the supplied carbon dioxide into a carbonate by reacting the supplied carbon dioxide with the silica-based collector and separating the silica-based collector agent in which the carbonate is collected.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica-based collector is dispersed in water, is in a gel state. The silica-based collector further comprises as an additive at least one of boron, sodium and potassium is further impregnated. The silica-based collector comprises silica, boron oxide, sodium oxide, potassium oxide or a mixture and alkaline earth metal oxide in a molar ratio of 40:30:30-30:20:50. The alkaline earth metal forms an ionic bond with oxygen in the silica structure. The alkaline earth metal comprises at least one of strontium, calcium, barium, radium, magnesium and beryllium. The carbon dioxide capture reactor comprises 1-10 g silica-based trapping agent based on 100 ml water. The silica-based collector agent is dispersed in the carbon dioxide collection reactor, and the silica-based collector agent is dispersed under conditions of 100-1000 revolutions/minute. The carbon dioxide capture reactor maintains a temperature of 20-40 degrees C and a pressure condition of 0.5-1.5 atm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises a centrifuge for separating carbonates converted in the carbon dioxide capture reactor.

PT P
PN CN111101146-A; CN111101146-B
TI Hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide hydrogenation self-coupling comprises e.g. dissociating hydrogen gas in hydrogen-containing carbon dioxide gas in electrochemical hydrogen pump at normal temperature and pressure.
AU WU X
   JIN R
   HE G
   RUAN X
   XIAO W
   LI X
   JIANG X
   FAN S
   CHENG H
   FANG Y
   BAO Z
   YAN X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202040352C
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide hydrogenation self-coupling comprises dissociating hydrogen gas in hydrogen-containing carbon dioxide gas at the anode in an electrochemical hydrogen pump, at normal temperature, normal pressure, and external electrical energy, sequentially passing through the solid polymer electrolyte membrane and the buffer solution, transferring to the cathode catalytic layer and undergoes hydrogenation reaction with carbon dioxide in the cathode feed gas to generate formic acid, separating hydrogen-containing carbon dioxide gas from the anode part hydrogen, the concentration of carbon dioxide components in the carbon dioxide-rich gas at the anode outlet increases, and adding into the cathode as a hydrogenation raw material to realize the efficient use of the same hydrogen-containing carbon dioxide raw material gas.
   USE - The method is useful for hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation and carbon dioxide hydrogenation self-coupling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the synchronous progress of the same hydrogen/carbon dioxide feed gas and the carbon dioxide hydrogenation in the same reactor; and exhibits synergistic efficiency, which simplifies the process and device; and improves the utilization rate of the hydrogen/carbon dioxide feed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation comprises dissociating hydrogen in the anode feed hydrogen/carbon dioxide mixed gas to generate hydrogen protons under the action of external electrical energy and anode catalyst, then sequentially passing through the solid polymer electrolyte membrane and the buffer solution and transferring to the cathode and separating from the carbon dioxide in the feed gas. The hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises subjecting carbon dioxide in the cathode feed hydrogen/carbon dioxide mixed gas to hydrogenation under the action of external power and cathode catalyst to obtain formic acid. The self-coupling comprises (i) adding feed gas of the cathode and cathode originates from the same hydrogen/carbon dioxide mixed gas, and (ii) coupling of anode hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation and cathode carbon dioxide hydrogenation in the electrochemical hydrogen pump. The electrochemical hydrogen pump comprises an anode, a solid polymer electrolyte membrane, a buffer layer and a cathode are sequentially assembled between two graphite electrode plates engraved with serpentine flow channels, the anode is a gas diffusion electrode sprayed on carbon paper with a loading of 0.2-0.5 mg/cm2 platinum/carbon catalyst, the solid polymer electrolyte membrane is Nafion115 (RTM: Perfluorinated ion-exchange membrane) membrane, adding potassium bicarbonate solution with a concentration of 0.5-2.5 mol/l into the buffer layer as a buffer solution, which is forcedly circulated by a peristaltic pump with a flow rate of 10-15 ml/minute and placing the silver/silver chloride reference electrode to adjusting the cathode potential, and the cathode is a gas diffusion electrode spray-coated with 1.5-3.0 mg cm-2 nm of tin catalyst on carbon paper. The normal temperature and normal pressure is 25 degrees C and 1 atm. The externally-applied electric energy to constant current operation mode with current density of 3-30 mA/cm2. The hydrogen-containing carbon dioxide gas is mixed gas of hydrogen and carbon dioxide in which the volume fraction of hydrogen accounts for 20-75%, and the feed flow rate at the anode is 5-25 sccm. The anode outlet is rich in carbon dioxide gas, in which the concentration of carbon dioxide components increases, part of which is discharged through the branch pipe, and the other part is used as cathode feed gas; the flow rate into the cathode is controlled by the valve of the discharge pipe to 1-20 sccm.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN WO2022077112-A1
TI Extracting values from mineralized silicate material by e.g. heating material in aqueous buffered extraction medium in presence of carbon dioxide supply and selective nickel/cobalt ligand, leaching nickel and cobalt, and separating solid.
AU DREISINGER D
   WANG F
AE UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
GA 202252754M
AB    NOVELTY - Values are extracted from a mineralized silicate material by (a) heating mineralized silicate material in an aqueous H2CO3(a)/HCO3- buffered extraction medium in the presence of a gaseous carbon dioxide supply and a selective nickel/cobalt (Ni/Co) ligand; (b) providing extraction conditions to: (i) selectively leach nickel and cobalt from silicate material into extraction medium in the form of complexes with the selective Ni/Co ligand, to form solubilized Ni complexes and solubilized Co complexes and (ii) react magnesium and iron in silicate material with dissolved carbon dioxide provided by carbon dioxide supply to form solid carbonate product comprising solid magnesium carbonate and solid iron carbonate; and, (c) in a solid liquid separation, separating solid magnesium carbonate and solid iron carbonate from the solubilized Ni complexes and solubilized Co complexes, to form Ni/Co pregnant leach solution.
   USE - The process is useful for extracting values from a mineralized silicate material, where the mineralized material is a laterite material, preferably a lateritic nickel ore.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is capable of selective nickel and cobalt production from a variety of laterite materials. It combines carbon mineralization with the extraction of metal values from abundant ores to offer opportunities to extract industrially necessary materials while reducing the environmental impact of that extraction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Values are extracted from a mineralized silicate material by (a) heating mineralized silicate material in an aqueous H2CO3(a)/HCO3- buffered extraction medium in the presence of a gaseous carbon dioxide supply and a selective nickel/cobalt (Ni/Co) ligand; (b) providing extraction conditions to: (i) selectively leach nickel and cobalt from silicate material into extraction medium in the form of complexes with the selective Ni/Co ligand, to form solubilized Ni complexes and solubilized Co complexes and (ii) react magnesium and iron in silicate material with dissolved carbon dioxide provided by carbon dioxide supply to form solid carbonate product comprising solid magnesium carbonate and solid iron carbonate; and, (c) in a solid liquid separation, separating solid magnesium carbonate and solid iron carbonate from the solubilized Ni complexes and solubilized Co complexes, to form Ni/Co pregnant leach solution. Providing extraction conditions comprises: providing the Ni/Co ligand in a molar ratio to the total amount of mineralized Ni in the mineralized silicate material of greater than 1:1, to extract at least 50%, 60%, 70%, 80%, or 90% of the total amount of mineralized Ni and Co in the mineralized silicate material into the Ni/Co pregnant leach solution, and so that less than 20%, 15%, or 10% of the total amount of the mineralized Fe and Mg in the mineralized silicate material is leached into the Ni/Co pregnant leach solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Compositions: Mineralized silicate material comprises a non-hydrated nickel-containing olivine material. Hydrated mineral feedstock is a laterite material, optionally a lateritic nickel ore. Laterite material comprises a saprolite-type ore and/or a limonite-type ore. Hydrated mineral feedstock comprises a serpentine material of formula (Fe,Mg,Ni,Co)3Si2O5(OH)4 and/or a montmorillonite material of formula (Na,Ca)(Fe,Mg,Ni,Co)2(Si,Al)4O10(OH)2nH2O. Solid magnesium carbonate comprises magnesite, and/or solid iron carbonate comprises siderite. Mineralized silicate material is present in the aqueous H2CO3(a)/HCO3- buffered extraction medium at a pulp density of 0.1-50 wt.%, preferably 0.5-5 wt.%. Preferred Processes: The method further comprises calcining a hydrated mineral feedstock to provide the mineralized silicate material. Dissolved CO2 from the CO2 supply is reacted to form the solid carbonate product so that at least 50, 100, 150, 200, or 250 kg CO2 are consumed per ton of mineralized silicate material. The method further comprises recovering Ni and Co values from the Ni/Co pregnant leach solution to form a Ni/Co depleted barren solution, and recirculating the Ni/Co depleted barren solution to the aqueous buffered extraction medium. Preferred Conditions: Calcining is carried out at a calcining temperature of 600-800 degrees C. Calcining is in the presence of a calcining atmosphere comprising a carbon monoxide-carbon dioxide (CO-CO2) gas, CO-nitrogen (N2) gas, or a CO-CO2-N2 gas. Hydrated mineral feedstock comprises goethite and/or hematite and/or magnetite, and calcination is carried in a reductive calcining atmosphere to convert at least a portion of the goethite and/or hematite and/or magnetite materials to wustite materials. Reductive calcining atmosphere comprises 3-15 vol.% CO in a CO-N2 calcining gas or 10-40 vol.% in a CO-CO2 calcining gas mixtures. Preferred Properties: Mineralized silicate material has a P80=5-150 mu m, or P80=10-100 mu m, or P80=15-50 mu m, or P80=20-30 mu m. Mineral carbonation (MC) efficiency is greater than 10%, 20%, 30%, 40%, 50%, 60%, or 70%, where MC efficiency is the amount of CO2 in the CO2 gas supply that forms the carbonate product as a percentage of the theoretical maximum amount of CO2 that the silicate material could react with to form carbonates of iron, magnesium, and calcium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Selective Ni/Co ligand is Ni and Co chelating agent. Selective Ni/Co ligand is trisodium nitrilotriacetate (NTA) or EDTA. Molar ratio of selective Ni/Co ligand to total amount of mineralized Ni in the mineralized silicate material is less than or equal to 50:1, preferably less than or equal to 12:1.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

PN CN114352250-A; CN114352250-B
TI Oil extraction for optimizing steam drive based on            flue gas component, involves adjusting proportion of            nitrogen and carbon dioxide, replacing second stage,            injecting steam to formation, controlling mixture of            flue gas to second proportion.
AU ZHANG M
   ZHU D
   ZHANG C
   LI S
   LI Z
   LI B
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 2022592164
AB    NOVELTY - Oil extraction involves adjusting the                proportion of nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the                flue gas, making the nitrogen in the flue gas more                than carbon dioxide the proportion is one, the                first stage injecting steam is displaced into the                formation and the mixture of the flue gas is                controlled, a stable temperature field is formed                under the well, after the temperature field formed                is stable, injection of steam and mixture is                maintained and the lathering solution agent is                injected into the formation, the proportion of                nitrogen and carbon dioxide in the flue gas is                adjusted so that the carbon dioxide in the flue gas                is more than nitrogen, and this proportion is two                proportion, replacing the second stage, injecting                steam into the formation and controlling the                mixture of the flue gas to the second                proportion.
   USE - Method for oil extraction used for optimizing                steam drive based on flue gas component                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables dividing the displacement                process into the second stage of the first stage of                expanding the heat wave and enhancing the oil                washing effect, mixing the flue gas and the steam                with the proportion of nitrogen as main, using                nitrogen not easy to compress, the heat insulation                performance is good, supplementing the formation                energy, promoting the steam seepage, and injecting                foaming agent block the reservoir high permeability                channel, improving the flow rate ratio after                injecting gas in the primary stage, after injecting                the gas, and improving the recovery rate of the                thick oil, the second stage adjusting the carbon                dioxide is more than nitrogen, using carbon dioxide                easy to react with the crude oil, strengthening the                oil-washing capability of the displacing                medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for implementing the oil extraction method which                comprising a gas injection module and a bubble                injection module agent, where the gas injection                module comprises a steam injection boiler generator                and a flue gas condensation filter device connected                in turn, a flue gas storage device and a first                pressurizing device, between the flue gas                condensing and filtering device and the flue gas                storage device is further connected with a film                separating device through a three-way valve, the                injection foaming agent comprises a foaming agent                storage device and a second pressurizing device                connected in turn, the steam injection boiler                generator, a flue gas pressurizing device, the                first pressurizing device and the second                pressurizing device are connected to the injection                well of the reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view showing                the system of an oil extraction method for                optimizing steam drive based on flue gas                component.
   Injection well (2)
   Flue gas (7)
   Film separation device (10)
   First pressurizing device (12)
   Foaming solution agent device (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oil reservoir is a                  thickened oil reservoir or an extra ultra thick                  oil reservoir.

PN CN113648805-A; CN215916934-U
TI Double-circulation flue gas carbon dioxide absorption tower for use in coal-fired power plant, has main body divided into upper absorption and lower absorption tower bodies, where lower absorption tower body is provided with filler layer.
AU LIU Y
   YANG R
   WANG L
   WENG J
   GAO Q
   LIU F
   LI C
   SHEN Z
   QI Z
AE ZHEJIANG ZHENENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5858K
AB    NOVELTY - The tower has an absorption tower main body divided into an upper absorption tower body and a lower absorption tower body. The lower absorption tower body is provided with a lower absorption filler layer (3). The lower absorption filler layer is provided with a lower spraying device (5). An upper absorption tower is provided with an upper absorption filler layer (4) that is provided with an upper layer spraying device. The upper absorption filler layer is provided with an upper layer absorption liquid collector (8). An upper portion of the upper layer spraying device is provided with an absorption tower demister.
   USE - Double-area double-circulation flue gas carbon dioxide absorption tower for use in a coal-fired coal-fired power plant or thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The tower improves a liquid-gas ratio, and reduces height under premise for ensuring a lower carbon dioxide capture rate, by improving upper absorption tower absorbent concentration. The tower increases absorption reaction intensity, ensures upper carbon dioxide collection rate, and effectively reduces height of the tower while realizing the higher collection rate of carbon dioxide in the tower. The tower automatically controls absorption liquid discharging and supplementing process by a control system comprising a liquid level automatic alarm function and an emergency pump stopping function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a double-area double-circulation flue gas carbon dioxide absorption tower utilizing method.

AU WU Z
   WU J
   WU N
AE ZHUHAI GONGTONG LOW CARBON TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
   ZHUHAI GONGTONG LOW CARBON TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 202114505U
AB    (CN212416731-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a coal gangue carbon dioxide flame retardant device, comprising a CO2 storage tank, a drainage pipeline; an injection bus pipeline; the CO2 storage tank is connected with the injection bus pipeline through the drainage pipeline; the injection bus pipeline is connected with a plurality of injection branches; each injection branch is provided with a plurality of injection holes; the injection branch extends into the coal gangue stack; the device further comprises multiple groups of monitoring components for monitoring the temperature and oxygen content in the coal gangue stack. The utility model has simple structure, strong practicability; it can prevent coal gangue from self-igniting; accurately monitoring the temperature and oxygen content in the coal gangue stack by the monitoring component; when the temperature and oxygen content are higher than the preset value, automatically spraying liquid CO2 by the control device, killing the fire before the fire, preventing from being affected; the pipeline is set in the coal gangue pile; and the pump is matched with the nozzle; the injection range of the liquid CO2 is ensured; at the same time, the control cost is reduced.
EA    NOVELTY - The device comprises a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank, a drainage pipeline, and an injection bus pipeline. The CO2 storage tank is filled with liquid CO2, and is connected to the injection bus pipeline through the drainage pipeline. An injection bus pipeline is connected with multiple injection branches. Each of the injection branches is provided with multiple injection holes. Each injection hole is equipped with a corresponding nozzle. The injection branch extends into coal gangue stack. The device further comprises multiple groups of monitoring components for monitoring the temperature and oxygen content in the coal gangue stack.
   USE - The device is useful in mining area.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has simple structure and strong practicability; prevents self-igniting of coal gangue; accurately monitors the temperature and oxygen content in the coal gangue pile through the monitoring components; utilizes control device to automatically spray water and liquid CO2 to eliminate the fire before it catches fire, so as to prevent the occurrence of trouble when the temperature and oxygen content are higher than the preset value; ensures the spray range of liquid CO2 through the cooperation of the pump and the nozzle by installing the pipeline in the coal gangue pile; and reduces prevention and control cost.

TI Laser-carbon dioxide arc-based composite root-free root welding comprises e.g. selecting test material made of carbon steel, processing single V-shaped groove, placing wing plate on the welding work platform, and setting welding parameters.
AU XUE R
   WANG Z
   ZHOU F
   LIU J
   BI Y
   ZHENG T
AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 202019486R
AB    NOVELTY - Laser-carbon dioxide arc-based composite root-free root welding comprises (i) selecting test material made of carbon steel, where the thickness of the wing is 10-50 mm, and the thickness of the web is 10-50 mm, and the welding wire is a solid wire of ordinary carbon steel; (ii) processing single V-shaped groove with a single side of 40-45 degrees on both sides of the web, where a blunt edge of 0-1 mm is reserved before welding, sand the groove near the groove to remove impurities remaining on the surface of the welding plate, using acetone to wipe and clean welding plates to remove residual oil stains; (iii) placing the wing plate on the welding work platform, placing the web in the center of the wing plate and perpendicular to the wing plate, leaving a gap of 0-1 mm, using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding at both ends for spot welding and clamping install anti-deformation support plate T-joint and fixing by welding fixture; and (iv) setting the welding parameters.
   USE - The method is useful for laser-carbon dioxide arc-based composite root-free root welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly; and eliminates the need for root cleaning on the back, improves welding productivity, and protects the health of welding personnel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Laser-carbon dioxide arc-based composite root-free root welding comprises (i) selecting test material made of carbon steel, where the thickness of the wing is 10-50 mm, and the thickness of the web is 10-50 mm, and the welding wire is a solid wire of ordinary carbon steel; (ii) processing single V-shaped groove with a single side of 40-45 degrees on both sides of the web, where a blunt edge of 0-1 mm is reserved before welding, sand the groove near the groove to remove impurities remaining on the surface of the welding plate, using acetone to wipe and clean welding plates to remove residual oil stains; (iii) placing the wing plate on the welding work platform, placing the web in the center of the wing plate and perpendicular to the wing plate, leaving a gap of 0-1 mm, using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding at both ends for spot welding and clamping install anti-deformation support plate T-joint and fixing by welding fixture; and (iv) setting the welding parameters, and performing the composite welding under the laser and carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, where the whole is a range-axis composite method in which the arc guides the laser in front.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: A continuous fiber laser is incident at an included angle of 10-15 degrees with the wing plate, the defocus amount is -3-0 mm, and the fiber spacing is 0-3 mm. The laser is generated by a low power continuous fiber laser with a maximum power of 500 W. The welding parameters are: 150-180 A welding current, 20-28 V welding voltage, 20-25 l/minute gas flow, 10-15 mm dry elongation, 1-2 m/minute welding speed, 45-75 degrees welding gun angle, and 70-80 degrees walking angle. The carbon steel is Q235B, Q275B or NO: 25steel. The welding wire is ER49-1, ER50-3 or ER50-6.

PT P
PN WO2019030191-A1; TW201920114-A; CN110997638-A; EP3665157-A1; US2020199077-A1
TI Manufacturing cyclic urea adduct of ethylene amine compound, comprises e.g. contacting liquid medium having ethylene amine compound with carbon dioxide having gas stream to form liquid medium into which carbon dioxide has been absorbed.
AU VENEMAN R
   TEN KATE A J B
   LAKE K F
   ADRIAN MEREDITH J V
   RAAIJMAKERS M J T
   JOVIC S
   EDVINSSON R K
   VAN DAM H
   KANTZER E N
   EHLERS I
   ADRIAN M J V
   ADRIAN M J V T
AE AKZO NOBEL CHEM INT BV (ALKU-C)
   NOURYON CHEM INT BV (NOUR-Non-standard)
GA 201915326H
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing (P1) a cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine compound having a linear ethane-1,2-diylbis(azanediyl) moeities (-NH-CH2-CH2-NH-) (I), comprises: in an absorption step, contacting a liquid medium comprising an ethylene amine compound having (I) with a carbon dioxide containing gas stream at a pressure of 1-20 bara, resulting in the formation of a liquid medium into which carbon dioxide has been absorbed; and bringing the liquid medium to cyclic urea formation conditions, and in an urea formation step forming cyclic urea adduct of the ethylene amine compound, urea formation conditions including a temperature of at least 120 degrees C, where the total pressure at the end of the urea formation step is at most 20 bara and the temperature in the absorption step is lower than the temperature in the urea formation step.
   USE - The process (P1) is useful for manufacturing cyclic urea adduct of ethylene amine compound having (I).
   ADVANTAGE - The process (P1): does not require the presence of metal-containing catalysts, while performed under relatively mild conditions, preferably at low pressure and obtaining good conversion; and separates the absorption step from the urea formation step and absorption step can be carried out at relatively low temperatures and pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) manufacturing (P2) a cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine compound having (I), comprising in an absorption step contacting a liquid medium comprising an ethylene amine compound having (I) with a carbon dioxide containing gas stream at a pressure of 1-10 (preferably 1-3) bara, at a temperature of 20-110 degrees C, preferably 50-110 degrees C, where the carbon dioxide containing gas stream is added in a continuous manner or in a fed-batch manner, preferably a continuous manner, resulting in the formation of a liquid medium into which carbon dioxide has been absorbed, and bringing the liquid medium to cyclic urea formation conditions at 170-220 degrees C and a pressure of 1-10 (preferably 1-3) bara in a closed vessel, resulting in the formation of cyclic ethylene ureas; and
   (2) preparing (P3) ethyleneamines or their urea derivatives, comprising (a) manufacturing the cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine compound, where the cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine compound is a cyclic urea adduct of ethylene amine, reacting the cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine with an ethanolamine-functional compound, or their urea- or carbamate additives, or the cyclic urea adduct of an ethylene amine compound is a cyclic urea adduct of a hydroxyethyl ethylene amine, reacting the cyclic urea adduct of a hydroxyethyl ethylene amine with a ethylene mine compound or their urea- or carbamate additives.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (P1): contacting the liquid medium with the carbon dioxide containing gas steam in the absorption step is carried out at 0-200 degrees C, preferably at least 40 degrees C; the maximum total pressure in the absorption step is 1-15 (preferably 1-3) bara; the temperature in the urea formation step comprises at least 140 degrees C, preferably at most 220 degrees C; the urea formation step is carried out in a closed vessel; urea formation step is carried out in a vessel, where the volume of the liquid medium in the vessel makes up at least 50 (preferably at least 85) vol.% of the total volume of the vessel (including head space); and the pressure at the end of the cyclic urea formation step is below 15 (preferably even below 3) bara. The process (P3) comprises in an absorption step contacting a liquid medium comprising an ethylene amine having a linear (I) and an ethanolamine with a carbon dioxide containing gas stream at a pressure of 1-20 bara, resulting in the formation of a liquid medium into which carbon dioxide has been absorbed, bringing the liquid medium to cyclic urea formation conditions, and in an urea formation step forming cyclic urea adduct of the ethylene amine and cyclic urea adduct or carbamate adduct of ethanolamine, with urea formation conditions including a temperature of at least 110 degrees C, where the total pressure at the end of the urea formation step is at most 20 bara, the temperature in the absorption step is lower than that in the urea formation step, and reacting the cyclic urea adduct of the ethylene amine with the ethanolamine or their urea or carbamate derivatives. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide containing gas stream comprises at least 95 vol.% of carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide containing gas stream comprises at most 70 (preferably 4-60) vol.% of carbon dioxide. The ethylene amine compound comprises ethyleneamines comprising at least one linear (I) moiety e.g. ethyleneamines of amine moieties (H2N-(CH2-CH2-NH)p-H), where p is at least 1, preferably 1-10, preferably ethylenediamine (p is 1), diethylenetriamine (p is 2), triethylenetetramine (p is 3) and tetraethylenepentamine (p is 4), where the compounds may comprise piperazine rings or tertiary amine groups. The ethylene amine compound comprises hydroxyethyl ethyleneamines e.g. linear hydroxyethyl ethylene amine compounds having at least one amine moieties (-NH-CH2-CH2-NH-) e.g. compounds of the amine compounds (HO-CH2-CH2-NH-(CH2-CH2-NH)q-H), where q is at least 1, preferably 1-10, preferably aminoethylethanolamine (q is 1) and 2-((2-((2-aminoethyl)amino)ethyl)amino)-ethanol also indicated as hydroxyethyldiethylenetriamine (q is 2), where the compounds may comprise piperazine rings or tertiary amine groups. The reaction medium comprises ethylenediamine or monoethanolamine and an ethylene amine compound having a linear (I).
   EXAMPLE - Mixtures of ethylenediamine (EDA) and aminoethylethanolamine (AEEA) were combined with carbon dioxide in a two-step process. In the first step, the carbon dioxide was loaded into the amine mixture at temperatures above 50 degrees C. In the second step of the process, the loaded mixture was heated to temperatures above 150 degrees C for 2 hours in a closed reactor vessel to obtain a mixture of amines and their cyclic urea adducts. The pressure at the end of the second step (at reaction temperature) was below 15 bara for all experiments.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E07-D09D; E07-D11; E10-B01E; E11-A01; E11-F07A; J04-X

PN CN110100616-A; CN210157748-U
TI Chinese herbal medicine planting system, comprises multiple closed planting boxes, carbon dioxide preparation device and carbon dioxide delivery main pipeline, where carbon dioxide preparation device comprising pure carbon dioxide generator.
AU ZHENG Y
   ZHAN Z
   WANG Y
   ZHANG J
   MA J
   WANG J
   GAO Z
   PEI B
AE FUJIAN SINO-SCI BIOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 201970785U
AB    NOVELTY - A Chinese herbal medicine planting system comprises multiple closed planting boxes, a carbon dioxide preparation device and a carbon dioxide delivery main pipeline, where the carbon dioxide preparation device comprising a pure carbon dioxide generator and a carbon dioxide concentration dilution container that the pure carbon dioxide generator is connected to a carbon dioxide concentration dilution container and the container is connected to the closed planting box through a carbon dioxide delivery main pipeline.
   USE - Chinese herbal medicine planting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The planting system maximizes the demand for carbon dioxide in plant photosynthesis, controls the loss of water and improves water use efficiency, product yield and optimizes quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for planting a Chinese medicinal material, which involves determining a carbon dioxide-photosynthetic curve of the plant to determine the optimal carbon dioxide concentration range of the plant, planting plant seedlings into a closed planting box in the planting system, adjusting the air pressure balance in the planting box and the carbon dioxide conveying main pipeline by adjusting the opening and closing of the vent hole, diluting the carbon dioxide concentration by adjusting the aeration flow rate that the carbon dioxide concentration in the closed planting box is kept in an optimal range when the photosynthesis of the plant is strengthened.

GA 202233615Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide                adsorption rotating wheel system, mainly used for                carbon dioxide processing system, and provided with                a pre-processing device, a first carbon dioxide                adsorption rotating wheel, a first heating device,                a second carbon dioxide adsorption rotating wheel,                a second heating device and a chimney, two carbon                dioxide adsorption rotating wheels are connected in                series, and the gas after desorption and                concentration of the carbon dioxide desorbed by the                desorption area of the first carbon dioxide                adsorption rotating wheel is transported to the                adsorption area of the second carbon dioxide                adsorption rotating wheel for secondary adsorption,                then the desorption area of the second carbon                dioxide adsorption rotating wheel to generate                secondary desorption of carbon dioxide desorption                and concentration of the gas, which can increase                the carbon dioxide concentration efficiency, and                has the efficiency of concentrating and recycling                carbon dioxide.

PN WO2019164081-A1; KR2019101052-A; KR2037878-B1; US2021039040-A1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent for separating carbon dioxide from exhaust gas and natural gas of chemical plants, power plants, and large boilers, comprises triamine compound and ether compound.
AU LEE K S
   KIM H S
   KIM H Y
   KIM J H
   HWANG S J

AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
   UNIV KYUNGHEE IND COOP GROUP (UYKY-C)
   KOREA CARBON CAPTURE & SEQUESTRATION (KOCA-Non-standard)
   KOREA ENERGY RES INST (KOER-C)
   UNIV SOGANG RES & BUSINESS DEV FOUND (UYSO-Non-standard)
   UNIV KYUNGHEE IND COOP (UYKY-C)
   KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
   UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
   KOREA CARBON CAPTURE & SEQUESTRATION (KOCA-Non-standard)
   KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
   UNIV KYUNGHEE IND COOP (UYKY-C)
GA 2019747626
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a triamine compound (I) and an ether compound (II).
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbent for separating carbon dioxide (claimed) from exhaust gas and natural gas of chemical plants, power plants, and large boilers.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorbent can reduce the energy consumption of a carbon dioxide absorption rate, and a high temperature in a state in which carbon dioxide is absorbed, and improve the carbon dioxide absorption rate due to the low latent heat required to regenerate the absorbent, and reduce thermal denaturation at a high temperature in a carbon dioxide absorption state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide absorbent comprises a triamine compound of formula (I) and an ether compound of formula (II).
   R1-R4 = hydrogen or 1-4C alkyl;
   R5 = hydrogen or 1-4C alkyl;
   m = 2 or 3;
   R6, R7 = 1-4C alkyl;
   R8 = hydrogen or a methyl group; and
   p = 4.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for separating carbon dioxide, which involves absorbing carbon dioxide from a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide using the carbon dioxide absorbent, and removing the carbon dioxide absorbed from the carbon dioxide absorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The triamine is 2,2'-iminobis (N, N-dimethylethylamine), 2,2'-iminobis (N, N-diethylethylamine), 3,3'-iminobis (N, N-dimethylpropylamine), 3,3'-iminobis (N, N-diethylpropylamine), 2,2'-iminobis (N, N'-dimethylethyl amine), N,N-bis (2-(methylamino) ethyl) methylamine, N, N-bis (3-(methylamino) propyl) methylamine, N, N'-(iminobisethylene) bismethanamine, and N, N'-(iminobispropylene) bismethanamine. The ether is tetraalkylene glycol dialkyl ether, tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether, tetraethylene glycol diethyl ether, tetraethylene glycol dipropyl ether, and tetraethylene glycol dibutyl ether. The absorption temperature of the first step is 20-60 degrees C. The absorption pressure of the first step is atmospheric pressure to 30 atm. The stripping temperature of the second step is 70-150 degrees C, and the stripping pressure in the second step is atmospheric pressure to 10 atm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN CN107987995-A
TI Producing organic grape wine, by harvesting fresh green fruits in organic orchards, crushing, adding enzyme, performing cold impregnation, adding clarifying agent, inoculating non-Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and fermenting product.
AU WANG Y
   LIANG H
AE YANTAI ZHIQI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 201839329W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing organic grape wine, involves (a) determining picking period after change of fruit color, determining sugar content of the grape fruit, (b) harvesting fresh, healthy, disease-free and green fruits in organic orchards, (c) removing stems, adding sulfur dioxide, crushing, and adding enzyme, (d) performing cold impregnation, (e) pressing the cold-impregnated grape vines to extract juice, (f) controlling grape turbidity to 150-250 NTU at 5-8 degrees C, adding clarifying agent, standing for 24-48 hours, (g) heating the grape juice, inoculating non-Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and fermenting, (h) heating the fermented grape juice, inoculating S.cerevisiae, and fermenting, (i) inoculating Oenococcus oeni and Lactobacillus into wine obtained in step (a), and fermenting at 20-25 degrees C for 24-480 hours, (k) storing malolactic fermented wine at 10-15 degrees C for 3-6 months, and (l) adding clarifying agent to the wine obtained in step (j).
   USE - The method is useful for producing organic grape wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product which is rich in nutrients, and has no pesticidal residues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing organic grape wine, involves planting plants having ability to produce wine with insecticidal effects in orchards, managing orchards based on organic cultivation, picking ripened fruit from orchard to prepare juice, picking ripened grapes in the orchard for brewing wine, and blending fresh juice and wine in a mass ratio of 1-75:25-99. The plant having insecticidal effect is chosen from garlic, lavender, Geranium, pitcher plant, Cordyceps militaris and/or Artemisia. The fresh juice is prepared by (a) harvesting fresh, healthy, disease-free fruit in organic orchards, (b) reducing the temperature of harvested fruit to 0-5 degrees C, (c) adding 60-120 mg/kg sulfur dioxide to fruit, crushing, and filling the juice into container with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to protect oxidation, (d) adding 20-50 mg/l enzyme to juice at 5-8 degrees C, adding clarifying agent, allowing to settle for 24-48 hours, and removing oxygen using nitrogen or carbon dioxide, (e) performing low-temperature storage at 0-5 degrees C and capping with nitrogen or carbon dioxide to prevent oxidation. The method involves (a) determining picking period after the change of fruit color, determining sugar content of the grape fruit, and harvesting when the acidity reaches 6-6.5 g/l, (b) harvesting fresh, healthy, disease-free and green fruits in organic orchards with a sugar content of 180-200 g/l, (c) reducing the temperature of harvested grapes to 5-8 degrees C, removing the stems and breaking softly, adding 60-120 mg/kg sulfur dioxide, crushing, and adding 20-60 mg/l enzyme while crushing, filling the container with nitrogen or carbon dioxide before feeding to prevent oxidation, (d) performing cold impregnation by reducing temperature of grape vines to 5-8 degrees C, impregnating for 12-96 hours, and spraying 1/2-1/3 times for 1 or 2 times/day, (e) pressing the cold-impregnated grape vines to extract juice, and using nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen, (f) controlling grape turbidity to 150-250 NTU at 5-8 degrees C, adding clarifying agent, standing for 24-48 hours, and using nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen, (g) heating the grape juice at 10-12 degrees C, inoculating not less than 106 cfu/ml non-Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and fermenting at 10-12 degrees C for 24-72 hours, (h) heating the fermented grape juice at 15-25 degrees C, inoculating not less than 106 cfu/ml S.cerevisiae, and fermenting at 16-20 degrees C for 24-72 hours, (i) monitoring temperature of grape bran during alcoholic fermentation, when the specific gravity drops to 0.993, cooling to 5-10 degrees C to separate wine lees, and filling the container with nitrogen or carbon dioxide before feeding, (j) inoculating 20-60 mg/l Oenococcus oeni and 20-60 mg/l Lactobacillus into wine obtained in step (a), and fermenting at 20-25 degrees C for 24-480 hours to complete malolactic fermentation, (k) storing the malolactic fermented wine at 10-15 degrees C for 3-6 months, and controlling free sulfur dioxide concentration to 20-30 ml/l, and (l) adding clarifying agent to the wine obtained in step (j). The aerated fruity organic grape wine is prepared by (a) mixing the grape juice obtained in step (e) with the grape wine obtained in step (j) to obtain grape wine with alcohol content of 2-8 vol.%, sugar content of 50-80 g/l, acidity of 5-6 g/l, and dry extract content of more than 17 g/l, (b) freezing the grape wine at -5 to 0 degrees C for 5-15 days, clarifying by filtration, and during the period, using nitrogen or carbon dioxide to remove oxygen, (c) sterilizing the cold stable grape wine using 0.45 and 0.2 micron membrane filter, (d) cooling the sterilized grape wine, and filling with food grade carbon dioxide, so that the pressure generated reaches 0.3-0.5 MPa, and (e) canning the juice after the wine is tested or qualified. The grape variety is Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, Cabernet Gernischt, Cabernet Franc, Syrah, Pinot noir, Chardonnay, Ningxia riesling and/or Riesling. The enzyme is pectinase, cellulase, papaya proteinase, alginase and/or amylase. The clarifying agent is gelatin, bentonite, polyvinylpolypyrrolidone (PVPP) and/or egg white. The fertilizer is organic fertilizer, organic-inorganic fertilizer and bioorganic fertilizer with an ecological seaweed bio-stimulating hormone or seaweed residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The ecological seaweed bio-stimulating hormone is prepared by (i) crushing washed seaweed, (ii) adding the crushed seaweed and water into a stirred tank and sterilizing at 60-120 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, cooling to 27-45 degrees C, and performing enzymolysis for 30-60 minutes, where the mass ratio of crushed seaweed material, water and enzyme is 5-30:70-95:0.02-1, (iii) separating the seaweed by enzymolysis, and filtering to obtain seaweed filtrate and residues, (iv) adding seaweed residues, urea, magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and glucose into fermenter in a mass ratio of 95.5:1:0.5:1:1:1, and adjusting moisture content to 50-70%, sterilizing at 70-150 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, cooling at 27-36 degrees C to obtain a mixed culture medium, inoculating Rhodotorula glutinis and R.mucilaginosa in a mass ratio of 0.1-2:98-99.9 at 27-36 degrees C, controlling the oxygen concentration in fermenter to 8-21%, and fermenting for 6-144 hours, sterilizing at 70-150 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, and cooling to 20-40 degrees C, (v) adding seaweed filtrate obtained in step (iii) to the seaweed fermented material obtained in step (iv), stirring, inoculating Corynebacterium ammoniagenes prepared by melting-frozen method in a mass ratio 95-99.8:0.2-5, fermenting at 25-35 degrees C with oxygen content of 1-8 mg/l for 1-6 days, and filtering through pressure filtration, and (vi) concentrating and spray-drying product, where the seaweed is Enteromorpha, gulfweed, Undaria pinnatifida and/or kelp. The insecticide used for orchard includes lime-sulfur mixture, Bordeaux mixture, mineral oil, Trichoderma harzianum, quinoline suspension, ethanol, soda, rotenone, pyrethrum, and matrine. The C.ammoniagenes is prepared by (a1) using cells in the logarithmic growth phase as seed culturing, inoculating 10% inoculum into liquid medium, performing shaking culture at 30 degrees C and 160 revolutions per minute for 48 hours, and centrifugation at 8000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, performing expanding culture in a refrigerator at -20 degrees C for 4 hours, and dissolving at room temperature, where the liquid medium is prepared by using 1 l beef extract, 10 g peptone, 5 g sodium chloride, and 20-25 g agar. The beef extract is prepared by washing lean beef, chopping, weighing 550 g beef, soaking in 1375 ml water for overnight, filtering, and sterilizing the filtrate at 0.6 kg/cm2 for 40 minutes. The enzyme is a mixture of lysozyme and pectinase in a mass ratio of 5:1. The lysozyme is prepared by adding 5 times water to egg white or whole egg, homogenizing, adding into homogenizer and homogenizing at 30-60 degrees C and 2-40 MPa for 10-40 minutes, filtering homogenized solution and diluting filtrate, ultra-filtering diluted solution using ultrafiltration membrane having a molecular weight cut-off of 15000-30000 Da, adding resin to the diluted solution for adsorption in a weight ratio of 1:3-8, eluting the adsorbed resin with 0.001-0.5 mol/l aqueous solution of sodium chloride to obtain an eluate, ultra-filtering eluate using ultrafiltration membrane with the molecular weight cut-off of 2000-10000 Da to obtain an ultrafiltrate, and drying ultrafiltrate, where the resin is a cation exchange resin chosen from 001x 7, 732, AmberliteIR-120, Dowex-50, Lewatit-100, Diaion SK-1, Allassioncs, Duolite C-20, and SDB-3, or macroporous adsorption resin chosen from AB-8, D101, D3520, X-5, NKA-II, NKA-9, S-8, XDA-1, H-20, H-30, and H-40. The separation process in step (iii) is pressure filtration, or centrifugation. The step (iv) involves adjusting moisture content to 60%, inoculating R.glutinis and R.mucilaginosa in a mass ratio of 0.5:99.5 at 30 degrees C, controlling the oxygen concentration in fermenter at 12%, and fermenting for 120 hours, sterilizing at 120 degrees C for 20 minutes, and cooling at 20-40 degrees C. The step (v) involves inoculating C.ammoniagenes prepared by melting-frozen method into mixture in a mass ratio of 98:2, fermenting at 28 degrees C with the oxygen content of 3 mg/l for 5 days. The step (a) involves homogenizing mixture at 40 degrees C and 25 MPa and 4 MPa for 15 minutes. The ecological seaweed bio-stimulator is added to the fertilizer by blending with fertilizer raw materials, and spraying to the surface of fertilizer particles.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)
MC C04-A08G2; C04-A10K; C04-B04N1; C04-C03A; C04-F09C; C04-F10B; C04-F10B1; C04-L05B; C04-L05C; C04-N02; C05-A01B; C05-B02C; C05-C05; C11-A01A; C11-A01C; C12-M07; C14-E11; C14-S18; D03-H01G1; D05-E; A03-C01; A04-D05A; A12-W04B; A12-W04C; A12-W09; P13-A04; P13-E01; P13-E02; P13-E06

PT P
PN CN109771980-A; CN109771980-B
TI Heterogeneous azeotropic distilling for separating a mixture of 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. separating 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide from raw material inlet.
AU LI D
   XIA M
   JIA L
   HOU B
   XIAO Y
   LU H
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201949185S
AB    NOVELTY - Heterogeneous azeotropic distilling for separating a mixture of 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide, comprises (i) separating the 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide raw materials from the raw material inlet (16) of the partition column into the left half of the partition column (1), and the reboiler is left on the partition wall (3), where the reboiler provides a gas-liquid separation by reboiler, reboiling to provide heat, and the gas phase exits the gas phase outlet (22) of the dividing wall column into the condenser (2), feeding the obtained condensate to the vapor-liquid-liquid separator, performing adiabatic and constant pressure operation, and after a sufficient vapor-liquid-liquid equilibrium for a residence time of 10-30 minutes, separating the carbon dioxide-rich vapor phase and the ketone-rich phase, where the water-rich phase, the ketone-rich phase flows back into the left half of the dividing wall column.
   USE - The heterogeneous azeotropic distilling is useful for separating a mixture of 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good separation effect, saves equipment investment cost and installation space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heterogeneous azeotropic distilling for separating a mixture of 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide, comprises (i) separating the 2-pentanone, 4-heptanone, water and carbon dioxide raw materials from the raw material inlet (16) of the partition column into the left half of the partition column (1), and the reboiler is left on the partition wall (3), where the reboiler provides a gas-liquid separation by reboiler, reboiling to provide heat, and the gas phase exits the gas phase outlet (22) of the dividing wall column into the condenser (2), feeding the obtained condensate to the vapor-liquid-liquid separator, performing adiabatic and constant pressure operation, and after a sufficient vapor-liquid-liquid equilibrium for a residence time of 10-30 minutes, separating the carbon dioxide-rich vapor phase and the ketone-rich phase, where the water-rich phase, the ketone-rich phase flows back into the left half of the dividing wall column, and the water-rich phase flows back into the right half of the dividing wall column, producing a carbon dioxide-rich gas for relaxation, and the ketone-rich phase enters the subsequent rectification column, (ii) the left half of the dividing wall column, the ketone-rich phase obtained in the column reactor enters the rectification column, reboiling by the rectification column reboiler, and is obtained in the rectification column-heptanone product, condensing the gas phase from the top of the rectification column through the rectification column condenser, where the condensate enters the rectification column reflux tank, and a portion is refluxed to the rectification column another part of the product obtained 2-pentanone, and (iii) refluxing the water-rich phase into the right half of the partition wall, re-boiling under the action of reboiler on the right side of the partition wall to re-separate the water-rich phase, and the obtained gas phase is discharged from the gas phase, it comes out into the condenser together with the gas phase coming out from the left half of the dividing tower. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dividing wall tower, comprising a casing, a lower head, an upper head having a rectangular partition between the casings in the axial direction of the casing. The casing is divided into a left side of the partition wall and a right side of the partition wall. The bottom of the rectangular partition is directly connected to the lower head. The top of the rectangular partition is directly connected head, or there is a part of space between the upper head, a ketone-rich phase inlet in the upper part of the left side of the dividing tower, a raw material inlet to be separated in the middle, and a ketone-rich phase reboiler in the lower part with a mixed ketone outlet at the bottom of the lower head on the left half of the next tower. In the upper part of the right side of the partition there is a water-rich phase inlet. The lower part has a water-rich phase reboiler inlet, a aqueous phase outlet at the bottom of the lower head on the right side of the partition, and a gas phase outlet at the top of the upper head.

TI Industrial high temperature solid bulk waste heat recycling system comprises e.g. a sintering system, storage tank, direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system and carbon dioxide separation/recycle system.
AU WANG W
   DING J
   LU J
   CHEN X
   LI S
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   JIANGXI RUIFENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2018589785
AB    NOVELTY - Industrial high temperature solid bulk waste heat recycling system comprises e.g. a sintering system, storage tank, direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system, carbon dioxide separation/recycle system, air separating system, pure oxygen blast furnace, where the direct contact type heat/chemical reaction heat recovery system is provided with a gas outlet and a solid material inlet, the bottom is provided with a gas inlet, a gas inlet through the pipeline is respectively connected with raw material natural gas storage tank and gas separation/recycle system; material inlet through pipeline connected with the sintering system. The gas outlet is connected with the full-oxygen blast furnace. The pure oxygen blast furnace is provided with a gas inlet and a gas outlet, where the gas inlet of pure oxygen blast furnace connected with the directly contact the gas outlet of heat exchanger/chemical reaction heat recovery system and air separating system through pipes.
   USE - Used as industrial high temperature solid bulk waste heat recycling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system combines the high-temperature heat-dissipation and recycling of the industrial process, the direct online utilization of the produced products, and the reuse of the flue gas carbon dioxide, which is a potential process route for energy conservation and emission reduction, and is in line with the national key strategy for energy conservation and emission reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Industrial high temperature solid bulk waste heat recycling system comprises a (1) sintering system, (2) storage tank, (3) direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system, (4) carbon dioxide separation/recycle system, (5) air separating system, pure oxygen blast furnace, where the direct contact type heat/chemical reaction heat recovery system is provided with a gas outlet and a solid material inlet, the bottom is provided with a gas inlet, a gas inlet through the pipeline is respectively connected with raw material natural gas storage tank and gas separation/recycle system; material inlet through pipeline connected with the sintering system. The gas outlet is connected with the full-oxygen blast furnace. The pure oxygen blast furnace is provided with a gas inlet and a gas outlet, where the gas inlet of pure oxygen blast furnace connected with the directly contact the gas outlet of heat exchanger/chemical reaction heat recovery system, air separating system through pipes, respectively the gas outlet of the total oxygen blast furnace is connected with the carbon dioxide separation/recycle system, carbon dioxide separation/circulating system is provided with a gas inlet and a gas outlet, gas outlet, connected by pipeline and the outlet of the full-oxygen blast furnace gas inlet, the gas outlet is connected with the direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recycling system of the gas inlet, gas outlet is connected with the full-oxygen blast furnace gas inlet. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for recovering waste heat from an industrial high-temperature solid bulk material by using waste heat recovery system, comprising (i) separating carbon dioxide from methane and 4-carbon dioxide separation/circulation system in the storage tank is passed through the gas inlet of the 3-direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system to the 3-direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system, simultaneously the solid bulk material in the sintering system is fed into the direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system through the solid feed inlet of the 3-direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system, and the gas from the gas, subjecting the imported mixed gas to heat exchange and a high temperature methane reforming reaction occurs; (ii) passing the gas mixture obtained in the step (i) through a gas outlet of the direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system and all oxygen blast furnace, and carrying out the combustion reaction with oxygen separated from the air separation system; (iii) introducing the gas generated in the all-oxygen blast furnace into the 4-carbon dioxide separation/circulation system for gas separation, and passing the separated carbon dioxide into the as a raw material in the direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system, continuously passing the remaining gas to the all oxygen blast furnace for further combustion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of industrial high temperature solid bulk waste heat recycling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Sintering system (1)
   Storage tank (2)
   Direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system (3)
   Carbon dioxide separation/recycle system (4)
   Air separating system (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The sintering device is a high temperature heating device of simple, and temperature of the solid bulk material after sintering is 700-1200 degrees C. The direct contact heat exchange/chemical reaction heat recovery system is actually a exchanger and a reforming reactor, solid bulk material moving direction is from up to down, airflow movement direction is from bottom to top, and the two are connected through the direct contact type to exchange heat. The carbon dioxide separation/recycle system is actually using cryogenic method existed in the prior art gas separation device for separating tail gas, blast furnace tail gas by liquefaction and different boiling points between the gas, separating the mixed gas so as to obtain carbon dioxide. The actual air separation system, impurity purification by the air compression system and a heat exchange system, a refrigeration system and a liquefying and rectifying comprises main system by means of air liquefying, rectifying, final separation into oxygen and gas separation device of nitrogen and other useful gases.

particles to fine crusher, finely                crushing to obtain walnut peel fine particles, and                conveying walnut peel fine particles to buffer                tank, conveying straw to straw coarse crusher,                heating water for coarse crushing to obtain                coarsely crushed straw particles, and conveying                coarsely crushed straw particles to fine crusher                for fine crushing to obtain finely crushed straw                particles, (ii) transporting walnut peel fine                particles in buffer tank to tubular enzyme                inactivator through screw pump, adding hot water to                inactivate enzyme to obtain enzyme inactivation                product, (iii) conveying enzyme-inactivated product                to extraction tank for extraction, and solid-liquid                separating to obtain extraction liquid and solid                residue, (iv) conveying solid residue into finely                divided straw particles through screw conveyor,                mixing, conveying into tubular preheater for                preheating, conveying mixture to special                enzymolysis tank for enzymolysis to obtain                enzymolysis mixture, (v) filtering enzymatic                hydrolysis mixture through filter to obtain                filtrate, subjecting filtrate to membrane                separation technology to obtain separation and                separation liquid, conveying separated matter into                conditioning tank, adding separated liquid and                filtrate to mixture of finely divided straw                particles and solid residue, and reapating steps                (iv-v), (vi) conveying isolate to acidification                tank for acidification, carrying out anaerobic                fermentation to obtain aged bacteria sludge, biogas                and fermentation broth, concentrating fermented                liquid through membrane to obtain concentrate and                filtrate, anaerobic fermenting concentrate, pumping                out filtrate by effluent pump, (vii) obtaining                unpurified biological natural gas and carbon                dioxide are from biogas through gas membrane                separation technology, and (viii) purifying                unpurified biological natural gas to obtain                biological natural gas, cooling and pressurizing                carbon dioxide to obtain dry ice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, organic                  special fertilizer technology specifically                  comprises (a) washing aged bacteria mud with                  water to obtain cleaning solution and aged                  bacteria mud, adding cleaning solution to                  conditioning tank, and repeating above steps, and                  (b) drying aged bacteria mud to obtain organic                  special fertilizer. In the method, green skin                  pigment, polyphenol technology specifically                  comprises (1) filter extract obtained in step                  (iii) through a filter to obtain filtrate and                  filtrate, add filtrate obtained in step (iv),                  separate filtrate to obtain pigment extract,                  polyphenol extract and filter extract, and add                  filter extract obtained in step (v), obtain                  pigment extracting solution through membrane                  separation technology to pigment filtrate and                  pigment concentrate, and place filtrate into                  buffer tank, (2) subject pigment concentrate to                  membrane concentration to obtain filtered water                  and pigment, convey filtered water to filtered                  water tank, successively purify and concentrate                  polyphenol extract by membrane to polyphenol                  concentrate and filtered water, and convey                  filtered water into filtered water tank, (3)                  evaporate polyphenol concentrate and pigment by                  vacuum falling film evaporator, dry by a pressure                  spray drying tower, and convey to the green                  leather pigment suspension tank and polyphenol                  suspension tank after drying, and (4) package. In                  the step (i), set short-term straw stacking place                  in or near biogas station for straw raw                  materials, and determine size of straw stacking                  yard based on amount of straw purchased and                  consumed. In the step (i), pulverize dry straw in                  pulverizing room, where the particle size after                  pulverization is &#8804;10 mm, the fresh                  straw is crushed and stored in silage tank, and                  the particle size after crushing is 20-30 mm. In                  the step (ii), inactivation of the enzyme is                  performed by denaturing the protein at high                  temperature to inactivate the enzyme. In the step                  (iv), the temperature during enzymatic hydrolysis                  is 45-65&#8451;, and the pH is 4.5-5.5. In                  the step (vi), the carbon-nitrogen ratio of                  anaerobic fermentation is 20-30, and the                  temperature is 33-37&#8451;. In the method,                  the temperature and solid concentration is                  adjusted uniformly after many raw materials is                  pretreated, and remove no suspended solids with a                  diameter or length of &#8805;40 mm.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating alkali salt in Hou's                combined alkali production by chemical                sublimation-change temperature crystallization                involves preparing sodium chloride aqueous solution                of supersaturated state, passing to ammoniation                tower, feeding ammonia gas to carry out the                ammoniation process and absorbing to obtain high                ammonium salt solution, carbonizing to obtain mixed                solution containing bicarbonate, filtering to                obtain alkali salt crude product containing sodium                bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, washing,                decomposing sodium bicarbonate to obtain sodium                carbonate product and carbon dioxide tail gas,                washing, pressurizing and returning to                carbonization tower, subjecting ammonium chloride                to chemical sublimation, drying to obtain ammonium                chloride product, collecting ammonia gas released                after the chemical sublimation, returning to                ammoniation tower, and separating mother liquor                obtained after filtration to obtain sodium chloride                crude product through high temperature                crystallization.
   USE - Method for separating alkali salt in Hou's                combined alkali production by chemical                sublimation-change temperature                crystallization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and                environmentally-friendly, increases single-pass                conversion rate of sodium chloride by more than                90%, effectively separates alkali salt such as                sodium chloride with total conversion rate of more                than 95% and total ammonia conversion rate of more                than 98%, sodium carbonate with yield of more than                90% and purity of more than 99%, ammonium chloride                with yield of more than 90% and purity of more than                99%, reduces impurity content to less than 50 ppm,                reduces circulating material by more than 80%,                reduces energy consumption, and does not generate                waste liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating alkali salt in Hou's                combined alkali production by chemical                sublimation-change temperature crystallization                involves preparing sodium chloride aqueous solution                of supersaturated state with high-purity water                through ultrasonic dissolution process, passing to                ammoniation tower through the metering pump,                continuously feeding ammonia gas to carry out the                ammoniation process and absorbing to obtain high                ammonium salt solution, passing to the                carbonization tower through metering pump,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas to                carry out the carbonization process and processing                to obtain mixed solution containing bicarbonate,                filtering the mixed solution containing bicarbonate                to obtain alkali salt crude product containing                sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, washing,                decomposing sodium bicarbonate by calcining at high                temperature to obtain sodium carbonate product and                carbon dioxide tail gas, washing, pressurizing and                returning to carbonization tower, subjecting                obtained ammonium chloride crude product to                chemical sublimation, drying to obtain ammonium                chloride product, collecting the ammonia gas                released after the chemical sublimation, returning                to ammoniation tower, separating the mother liquor                obtained after filtration, which is the aqueous                solution containing ammonium chloride and sodium                chloride to obtain sodium chloride crude product                through high temperature crystallization, further                refining into sodium chloride product through                recrystallization, decolorizing and drying,                performing low-temperature crystallization on                ammonium chloride crude product, refining into                ammonium chloride product through                recrystallization, decolorizing and drying, so that                the sodium chloride impurity content in it is                reduced to below 50 ppm, collecting ammonia gas                released in the high-temperature crystallization                process and returning to the ammoniation tower, and                recycling residual liquid obtained after the                low-temperature crystallization and                recrystallization, which is an aqueous solution                containing sodium chloride and ammonium chloride                and returning to carbonization tower.

   2. preparing foamed material comprising                molding the powdery polymer into a plate on a hot                press at 300&#8451;-380&#8451; under a                pressure of 5-10 MPa for 30-60 minutes, then                placing the obtained plate in an autoclave, sealing                the kettle body, introducing carbon dioxide,                keeping the pressure 5-20 MPa and temperature of                200-350&#8451;, quickly reduce the pressure in                the kettle to normal pressure through the pressure                relief valve after 1-5 hours, open the kettle body,                taking out the sample and cool it to room                temperature to obtain foam board, the thickness of                the obtained plate is greater than 0.5-10 mm,                processing powdered polymer into pellets of uniform                size with a diameter of 0.5-3 mm by a single-screw                extruder, and then placing the obtained pellets in                an autoclave, sealing the body of the autoclave,                introducing carbon dioxide under the condition of                mechanical stirring, keeping the pressure at 5-20                MPa and the temperature at 200-300&#8451;,                quickly reducing the pressure in the kettle is                quickly reduced to normal pressure through the                pressure relief valve after 0.5-2 hours, and                spraying the foam beads from the pressure relief                port, cooling and collecting to obtain the target                foam beads, molding the powdery polymer into a                sheet on a hot press at 300-380&#8451; under a                pressure of 5-10 MPa for 30-60 minutes, and the                sheet thickness is 0.1-0.5 mm, then placing the                obtained sheet in the autoclave, sealing the                autoclave body, passing carbon dioxide, maintaining                the pressure at 5-20 MPa, and keeping the                temperature at 35-80&#8451;, taking out the                polymer sheet and quickly placing into a high                temperature oil bath or other high temperature                environment (150-250&#8451;) for foaming, take                out and cool after 10-120 seconds to obtain the                target foam sheet.
