PN CN113120859-A
TI Water vapor conversion reaction method, by using platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst comprising titanium dioxide carrier, cerium oxide, and platinum nanoparticles anchored on carrier to catalyze reaction of carbon monoxide and water vapor to generate carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU ZHOU J
   ZHANG B
   HUANG Y
   HUANG C
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202183878Q
AB    NOVELTY - Water vapor conversion reaction method, involves using a platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst to catalyze reaction of carbon monoxide and water vapor to generate carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the reaction pressure is normal pressure, and the reaction temperature is 150-300 degrees C, the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst comprises a titanium dioxide carrier, cerium oxide supported on the titanium dioxide carrier in a single-layer or multi-layer dispersion form, and forming a composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide with the titanium dioxide carrier, and platinum nanoparticles anchored on the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, there is a strong interaction between the metal oxide cerium oxide and titanium dioxide of the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, and the platinum nanoparticles used as an active component have a synergistic effect with the semiconductor film formed by cerium oxide.
   USE - Water vapor conversion reaction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The water vapor conversion reaction has mild conditions, and the used catalyst has excellent catalytic activity and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Water vapor conversion reaction method, involves using a platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst to catalyze reaction of carbon monoxide and water vapor to generate carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the reaction pressure is normal pressure, and the reaction temperature is 150-300 degrees C, the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst comprises a titanium dioxide carrier, cerium oxide supported on the titanium dioxide carrier in a single-layer or multi-layer dispersion form, and forming a composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide with the titanium dioxide carrier, and platinum nanoparticles anchored on the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, there is a strong interaction between the metal oxide cerium oxide and titanium dioxide of the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, and the platinum nanoparticles used as an active component have a synergistic effect with the semiconductor film formed by cerium oxide, the content of platinum in the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst is 0.25-5 %mass, and the content of cerium oxide in the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide is 6-30 %mass. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a hybrid nano-structured platinum catalyst for water vapor coversion reaction comprising a platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst comprising a titanium dioxide carrier, cerium oxide supported on the titanium dioxide carrier in a single-layer or multi-layer dispersion form and forming a composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide with the titanium dioxide carrier, and platinum nanoparticles anchored on the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, where there is a strong interaction between the metal oxide cerium oxide and titanium dioxide of the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, and the platinum nanoparticles used as an active component have a synergistic effect with the semiconductor film formed by cerium oxide, the content of platinum in the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst is 0.25-5 %mass, and the content of cerium oxide in the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide is 6-30 %mass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of platinum is 0.3-1 %mass. The content of cerium oxide is 15-20 %mass. Preferred Method: The platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst is prepared by (i) using an impregnation method to load cerium oxide on a titanium dioxide carrier in a single layer or multiple layers to form a composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide, and (ii) using a photocatalytic reduction method to anchor platinum nanoparticles on the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide to obtain the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst. The impregnation method involves stirring the pretreated titanium dioxide, cerium nitrate and deionized water at 80 degrees C for 2-4 hours, adding alkaline solution to adjust the pH to 8-9, continuously stirring at 80 degrees C for 4-6 hours, ultrasonically processing for 1-2 hours, leaving still and aging the mixture, filtering, washing, vacuum-drying, and calcining to obtain the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide. The photocatalytic reduction method involves adding the composite carrier cerium oxide/titanium dioxide prepared in step (i) to water and anhydrous methanol for uniform ultrasonic dispersion, adding chloroplatinic acid solution, continuously performing ultrasonic vibration for a preset time, stirring under UV light conditions, filtering the stirred solution, washing, and vacuum-drying to obtain the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst. The preparation method of the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst further involves pretreating the catalyst after step (ii) by filling the platinum-loaded-cerium oxide/titanium dioxide catalyst prepared in step (ii) in a micro fixed bed reactor, and pretreating the mixed gas of hydrogen and argon at 300-400 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, where the volume flow rate ratio of hydrogen and argon gas is 1:(1-19). Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is maintained at 250-300 degrees C. In the impregnation method, the drying is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 8-12 hours, and the calcination is carried out at 450-600 degrees C for 2-4 hours. In the impregnation method, the method of pretreating the titanium dioxide is carried out to calcinate the titanium dioxide at 500 degrees C for 2 hours. In the photocatalytic reduction method, the preset time of ultrasonic vibration is 10-20 minutes, the stirring under UV light is carried out for 12 hours, and the vacuum-drying is carried out at 75-85 degrees C for 8-12 hours.

PN CN111299839-A
TI Improved laser impact printing device based on constrained layer comprises e.g. laser, sealed cavity, cooling cavity, and liquid carbon dioxide storage tank, sealed cavity and cooling cavity separated by partition in middle.
AU CHENG W
   DAI F
   LU J
   LUO K
   FANG Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 202058162U
AB    NOVELTY - Improved laser impact printing device based on constrained layer comprises laser, sealed cavity, cooling cavity, and liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The sealed cavity and the cooling cavity is next to each other, and separated by partition in the middle. The top of closed cavity is the cavity cover. The heater and temperature sensor is set on the inner wall on the right side. The control display is located on the outer wall on the right side of the closed cavity to connect and control the temperature sensor and heater. The worktable is located in closed cavity and at the bottom. The telescopic fixture is installed above the worktable. The hand wheel is set on outer wall of the bottom end of the closed cavity to connect and control the telescopic fixture. The liquid carbon oxide storage tank is located on the left side of closed cavity, and the infusion valve connects the liquid carbon oxide storage tank and cooling cavity. The laser is located on the right side of closed cavity.
   USE - Used as improved laser impact printing device based on constrained layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses water as rigid constraint layer by transforming into ice form, and solves the shortcomings that the ordinary rigid constraining layer and absorbing layer do not fit and the rigid constraining layer is not durable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for implementing laser impact imprinting based on the improvement of the constraining layer using the device, comprising (i) grinding the light alloy work piece until there is no scratches on the surface, cleaning, drying, adding into the telescopic fixture on the workbench, (ii) polishing and cleaning the high-strength metal film, drying, etching the corresponding micro-nano patterns by the surface of the picosecond laser, placing film as mold on top of the light alloy work piece to closely fit, closely attaching the patterned surface to the light alloy work piece, attaching the absorbent layer to the side without the pattern, (iii) contracting the telescopic fixture by the hand wheel, equipping the inside of the telescopic fixture with silicone rubber film to ensure seamless gap between the high-strength metal film mold and side wall of the telescopic fixture, (iv) adding layer of water above the absorption layer, closing the lid, opening the infusion valve to slowly input liquid carbon dioxide into the cooling chamber, sensing the temperature in the closed cavity by temperature sensor, when the temperature reaches certain temperature, closing the infusion valve, and the water layer solidifies into ice layer, (v) opening the cavity cover and the laser, adjusting the process parameters of the laser, and performing laser shock to obtain nano-level surface micro texture pattern on the surface of the light alloy work piece, and (v) turning off the laser and turning on the heater for heating the sealed cavity so that the water inside it evaporates.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the improved laser impact printing device based on constrained layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the light alloy is aluminum alloy or magnesium alloy.

AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)

AB    NOVELTY - A nanocomposite cathode has an electrolyte                layer of a semi-electrolytic cell sheet covered                with a yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) framework                provided with micropores prepared using poly(methyl                methacrylate) (PMMA) as a pore-forming agent, and                composite nanoparticles prepared by co-impregnation                method, which covers the YSZ framework. The                composite nanoparticles are electrode catalyst of                solid oxide electrolytic cell (SOEC) nanocomposite                cathode. The composite nanoparticles comprise                lanthanum-iron oxide, or strontium or                manganese/copper/nickel-doped lanthanum-iron oxide                (LSFM), and gadolinium-cerium oxide (GDC). The                composite nanoparticles are co-synthesized powder                of cerium oxide dopes with LSFM, and cerium oxide                or gadolinium.
   USE - Nanocomposite cathode is used in                high-temperature solid oxide electrolytic cell                (claimed), and high-temperature carbon dioxide                reduction, clean carbon fuel preparation, and                energy and fuel cell applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocomposite cathode has excellent                electrochemical performance for high-temperature                carbon dioxide electrolysis and carbon                dioxide-water co-electrolysis, and improves                performance and operating stability of solid oxide                electrolytic cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A nanocomposite cathode has an electrolyte                layer of a semi-electrolytic cell sheet covered                with a yttria-stabilized zirconia (YSZ) framework                provided with micropores prepared using poly(methyl                methacrylate) (PMMA) as a pore-forming agent, and                composite nanoparticles prepared by co-impregnation                method, which covers the YSZ framework. The                composite nanoparticles are electrode catalyst of                solid oxide electrolytic cell (SOEC) nanocomposite                cathode. The composite nanoparticles comprise                lanthanum-iron oxide, or strontium/metal (M, where                M is manganese, copper or nickel)-doped                lanthanum-iron oxide (LaxSr1-xFeyM1-yO3, where x                and y are 0-1) (LSFM), and gadolinium-cerium oxide                (GdxCe1-xO2) (GDC). The composite nanoparticles are                co-synthesized powder of cerium oxide with LSFM,                and cerium oxide or gadolinium. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for preparation of nanocomposite                cathode, which involves preparing nitrate solutions                of lanthanum ion, strontium ion, ferric ion,                manganese ion, gadolinium ion and cerium ion,                respectively, mixing, adding ammonium citrate to                the mixed nitrate solution, adjusting pH to 1-2                using 2 mol/L nitric acid, obtaining LSFM-GDC                precursor solution, mixing YSZ powder and PMMA in a                mass ratio of 50:50, adding a predetermined amount                n-butanol as a solvent, fish oil as a dispersant,                polyvinyl butyral as a binder and dioctyl phthalate                as a plasticizer, uniformly mixing, ultrasonically                dispersing until the materials are is dispersed,                baking, adding an organic binder, stirring,                obtaining a white viscous YSZ slurry, uniformly                coating YSZ slurry on both sides of the YSZ                electrolyte support by screen printing method,                paste coating method or spin coating method, baking                in air atmosphere, obtaining YSZ framework with                microporous structure on the YSZ electrolyte                support, impregnating LSFM-GDC precursor solution                into the surface and micropores of YSZ framework by                vacuum impregnation, sintering obtained                electrolytic cell sheet with LSFM-GDC precursor                deposited in air atmosphere, and obtaining LSFM-GDC                composite nanoactive catalytic layer covered on the                surface of YSZ framework. The molar ratio of                ammonium citrate and total metal ions is                1.5:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Composition: The semi-electrolytic                  cell sheet is an electrolyte-supporting                  semi-electrolytic cell sheet. The solid oxide                  electrolytic cell is a symmetrical battery                  including LSFM-GDC nanocomposite electrodes as                  cathode and anode, with thickness of 20-100                  microns. Preferred Properties: The                  electrolyte-supporting semi-electrolytic cell                  sheet has thickness of 100-1000 microns. The                  thickness of the LSFM-GDC nanocatalyst layer of                  the SOEC nanocomposite cathode is 20-100                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The material of                  electrolyte-supporting semi-electrolytic cell                  sheet is YSZ, which is prepared by a casting                  method. The total metal ion molar concentration                  in the LSFM-GDC precursor solution is 1-2 mol/L.                  Preferred Property: The particle size of LSFM-GDC                  composite nanopowder is 10-100 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The organic binder is                  prepared by dissolving 6 wt.% ethyl cellulose in                  94 wt.% terpineol.

PN KR2113597-B1
TI Apparatus for recovering effective resources such as nitrogen in organic waste, has nitrogen recovery tank that recovers nitrogen components from ammonia degassed in exhalation tank, and separation tank separates solid and liquid components.
AU RHU D H
   CHOI J
   HWANG H
   YOUNG K
AE BKT CO LTD (BKTB-Non-standard)
GA 2020437969
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a first solid-liquid separation tank (130) that separates the digestive waste liquid into solid components and liquid components. A second solid-liquid separation tank (150) separates the eluted component and the remaining solid component through the elution tank and transfers the separated solid component to the anaerobic digester. An aerobic tank (170) injects a predetermined amount of air to dealuminate the ammonium ions in the liquid component separated from the first solid-liquid separation tank with ammonia, and generates phosphorus remaining in the liquid component as a solid. A nitrogen recovery tank recovers nitrogen components from ammonia degassed in the exhalation tank. A third solid-liquid separation tank (180) separates the solid and liquid components generated in the aerobic tank. The carbon dioxide from the gas is separated and discharged from the separation device flows into the elution tank.
   USE - Apparatus for recovering effective resources such as nitrogen and phosphorus in organic waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The various effective resources including nitrogen and phosphorus from organic waste can be efficiently recovered while minimizing the use of chemicals by appropriate pretreatment and effective use of by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The third solid-liquid separation tank transfers the separated solids to the first solid-liquid separation tank. The nitrogen recovery tank provides a predetermined environment to recover the ammonia degassed in the aerobic tank in a liquid state and supplies carbon dioxide to recover the ammonia degassed in the aerobic tank into a solid composition. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recovering resources contained in organic waste, involves performing the anaerobic digestion to decompose the organic waste into digestive waste and gas. The component contained in the gas is separated using a difference in solubility of the gas in the dissolved water. The digestive waste liquid is separated into solid components and liquid components. The low pH environment is provided to elute phosphorus from the introduced solid components. The eluted component and the remaining solid component are separated in the elution process and the separated solid component is transferred to the decomposition process. The predetermined amount of air is injected into the separated liquid component in the first solid-liquid separation process to degas some ammonium ions with ammonia and generate phosphorus remaining in the liquid component as a solid material. The solids are separated in the third solid-liquid separation process separated into solid and liquid components again by the first solid-liquid separation process. The carbon dioxide is supplied to recover the ammonia degassed in the degassing process in a liquid state. The nitrogen components are recovered from ammonia degassed in the degassing process by maintaining a predetermined temperature range by cooling water injected from the outside.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the configuration of an effective resource recovery device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Apparatus for recovering effective resources (100)
   First solid-liquid separation tank (130)
   Second solid-liquid separation tank (150)
   Aerobic tank (170)
   Third solid-liquid separation tank (180)

TI Extracting clam oil and protein comprises taking meat of clam type, homogenizing and vacuum freezing-drying, pulverizing and sieving, carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the oil in the powder and centrifuging.
AU ZHOU D
   LIU Z
   LI D
   ZHAO M
   ZHANG J
   SUN S
   ZHAO Q
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 201934991N
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting clam oil and protein comprises e.g. (i) taking meat of clam type, homogenizing and vacuum freezing-drying to obtain product having moisture content is less than 10 wt.%, (ii) taking the product obtained in the step (i), pulverizing and sieving through 40-80 meshes sieve to obtain glutinous powder, (iii) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the oil in the powder obtained in the step (ii), where the specific parameters are fluid pressure is 20-30 Mpa, extracting at 30-50 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain grease and residual powder separately, (iv) mixing the residual powder obtained in the step (iii) with distilled water in a weight ratio of 1 kg/(20-30) l, adjusting the pH value to 10-13, soaking at 2-6 degrees C for 2-3 hours and centrifuging to obtain supernatant A and precipitate A, and(v) taking the precipitate A obtained in the step (iv), mixing with distilled water in a weight-to-volume ratio of 1 kg/(3-4) l, and adjusting the pH value to 11-13.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting clam oil and protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product is clear and transparent; has stable quality; avoids oxidative deterioration and the loss of the heat sensitive active ingredient; and achieves high value utilization of low value clam.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting clam oil and protein comprises (i) taking meat of clam type, homogenizing and vacuum freezing-drying to obtain product having moisture content is less than 10 wt.%, (ii) taking the product obtained in the step (i), pulverizing and sieving through 40-80 meshes sieve to obtain glutinous powder, (iii) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of the oil in the powder obtained in the step (ii), where the specific parameters are fluid pressure is 20-30 Mpa, extracting at 30-50 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain grease and residual powder separately, (iv) mixing the residual powder obtained in the step (iii) with distilled water in a weight ratio of 1 kg/(20-30) l, adjusting the pH value to 10-13, soaking at 2-6 degrees C for 2-3 hours and centrifuging to obtain supernatant A and precipitate A, (v) taking the precipitate A obtained in the step (iv), mixing with distilled water in a weight-to-volume ratio of 1 kg/(3-4) l, adjusting the pH value to 11-13, stirring 2-6 degrees C at speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 0.5-2 hours, and centrifuging to obtain supernatant B and precipitate B, (vi) taking supernatant A obtained in the step (iv) and the supernatant B obtained in the step (v) and mixing to obtain supernatant C, adjusting the supernatant C having pH to 3.5-5, allowing to stand at 2-6 degrees C for 25-40 minutes to obtain supernatant D, centrifuging the obtained supernatant D to obtain precipitate C, and(vii) taking the precipitate C obtained in the step (vi) and vacuum-drying to obtain clam protein.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the calm is clam, green clam in Philippines and Ruditapes philippinarum. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the homogenate specifically includes rotational speed is 6000-8000 revolutions/minute (rpm), and homogenization time of 3-5 minutes. In the step (i), the vacuum freeze-drying specifically includes -50 degrees C for 2-4 hours, -40 degrees C for 3-5 hours, -30 degrees C for 3-6 hours, -20 degrees C for 5-10 hours, -10 degrees C for 5-15 hours, 0 degrees C for 10-15 hours, 5 degrees C for 10-15 hours, and 10 degrees C for 10-15 hours. In the step (v), centrifugation specifically includes 4 degrees C, 5000-8000x g, and centrifuging for 10-20 minutes. In the step (vi), centrifugation specifically includes 4 degrees C, 5000-8000x g, and centrifuging for 10-20 minutes. In the step (vii), the vacuum freeze drying specifically includes -50 degrees C for 2-4 hours, -40 degrees C for 3-5 hours, -30 degrees C for 3-6 hours, -20 degrees C for 5-10 hours, -10 degrees C for 5-15 hours, 0 degrees C for 10-15 hours, 5 degrees C for 10-15 hours, 10 degrees C for 10-15 hours. The method specifically comprises (i) taking meat of clam type, homogenizing and vacuum freezing-drying to obtain product having moisture content is less than 10 wt.%, where vacuum freeze-drying    (D) monitoring the algae growth conditions inside the reactor main body in real time by probe monitors, and transmitting the data to the data storage pool through the monitoring line to store the data to analyze the algae growth condition; and
   (E) adjusting the internal pressure of the reactor main body by gas valve, directly sampling the sampling port for measurement to analyze algal growth status.

AE DAI L (DAIL-Individual)
   YANG Y (YANG-Individual)
   WANG W (WANG-Individual)
   HOU J (HOUJ-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing liquified natural gas (LNG) by                coke oven gas and converter and/or blast furnace                gas synthesis of ethylene glycol involves                dust-removing tar coke oven coal gas dust and tar,                compressing coke oven gas, coarse desulfurizing,                removing impurity, secondary compressing coke oven                gas, and crude decarburizing and dephosphorizing                converter and/or blast furnace gas by pressure                swing adsorption; and filtering coke oven gas and                converter and/or blast furnace gas,                methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) lean liquid                pressurizing, separately filtering coke oven gas                and converter and/or blast furnace gas, eliminating                impurities, pressurizing MDEA solution, flowing                backward coke oven gas and converter and/or blast                furnace gas with pressurized MDEA lean liquid, mass                transfer heat transferring, MDEA lean liquid                absorbing coke oven gas and carbon dioxide in                converter and/or blast furnace gas to form MDEA                rich liquid, and purifying coke oven gas and                converter and/or blast furnace gas.
   USE - Method for co-producing liquified natural gas                by coke oven gas and converter and/or blast furnace                gas synthesis of ethylene glycol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing liquified natural gas (LNG) by                coke oven gas and converter and/or blast furnace                gas synthesis of ethylene glycol involves:
   (A) dust-removing tar coke oven coal gas dust                and tar to dust and tar amount in coke oven gas of                3 mg/Nm3, compressing coke oven gas to 0.58-0.62                MPa, coarse desulfurizing, removing impurity,                secondary compressing coke oven gas to 4-4.2 MPa,                and crude decarburizing and dephosphorizing                converter and/or blast furnace gas by pressure                swing adsorption;
   (B) filtering coke oven gas and converter                and/or blast furnace gas, methyldiethanolamine                (MDEA) lean liquid pressurizing, separately                filtering coke oven gas and converter and/or blast                furnace gas, eliminating impurities, pressurizing                MDEA solution, flowing backward coke oven gas and                converter and/or blast furnace gas with pressurized                MDEA lean liquid, mass transfer heat transferring,                MDEA lean liquid absorbing coke oven gas and carbon                dioxide in converter and/or blast furnace gas to                form MDEA rich liquid, and purifying coke oven gas                and converter and/or blast furnace gas;
   (C) cooling separated coke oven gas,                gas-liquid separating, filtering, separating                mechanical impurities and free liquids, complete                decarburizing of coke oven gas and converter and/or                blast furnace gas, mixing liquid-liquid separated                liquid with mechanical impurities and separated                free liquid, and stepping down MDEA rich                liquid;
   (D) flashing liquid with mechanical impurities                and free liquid mixture and depressurized MDEA rich                liquid, sending flashed gas to dispersing system                for dissipation, filtering to remove mechanical                impurities to form MDEA rich liquid, subsequent                processing, mass transfer heat transferring,                resolving acid gas in MDEA rich liquid by stripping                steam, and analyzing acid gas of MDEA rich liquid;                and
   (E) forming MDEA lean liquid, cooling gas                stripping steam, gas-liquid separating, discharging                separated gas into atmosphere, pressurizing                separated liquid, cooling MDEA lean liquid,                cooling, cryogenic separating coke oven gas of LNG                at purity of 95-97%, hydrogen pressure of 3.3-3.5                MPA, purifying hydrogen to obtain hydrogen having                99% purity, and mixing.
   The pressure swing adsorption converter and/or                blast furnace gas has volume fraction of carbon                dioxide in is 5.8-6.2% and phosphine content of 1-5                ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The coke oven gas has                  flow rate of 80000-85000 Nm3/hour, pressure of                  0.004-0.006 MPa and temperature of 18-22 degrees                  C. The coke oven gas active ingredient comprises                  20-25 vol.% methane, 60-65 vol.% hydrogen, 8-12                  vol.% carbon monoxide, 0.5-0.9 vol.% oxygen, 2-4                  vol.% carbon dioxide, 0.14-0.16 g/Nm3 tar and                  dust content, 50-150 mg/Nm3 hydrogen sulfide                  content and 150-160 mg/Nm3 other sulfide content.                  The converter and/or blast furnace gas has flow                  rate of 28000-30000 Nm3/hour, pressure of                  0.003-0.005 MPa and temperature of 18-22 degrees                  C. The converter and/or blast furnace gas has                  active ingredient comprising 40-60 vol.% carbon                  monoxide, 20-26 vol.% carbon dioxide, 20-28 vol.%                  nitrogen, 1-4 vol.% hydrogen and 0.6-1% oxygen,                  and has 100-250 mg/kg phosphine content,                  0.01-0.02 g/Nm3 tar and dust content and 16-18                  mg/Nm3 sulfide content.

PN WO2018154607-A1; IN201711006780-A; US2020010376-A1; PH12019501059-A1; BR112019013541-A2; US10793480-B2; IN373298-B
TI Preparing multi-nutrient potassic fertilizer i.e. monopotassium phosphate by recovering potassium from spent wash, comprises e.g. lime treatment of spent wash to generate carbon-rich sludge, and reacting formed filtrate with carbon dioxide.
AU MAITI P
   HALDAR S
   MAITI S
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
   COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 201867623T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing multi-nutrient potassic fertilizer, preferably monopotassium phosphate, by recovering potassium from spent wash, comprises e.g. (i) lime treatment of wash to generate carbon-rich sludge having pH greater than 12, (ii) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (i) with carbon dioxide till the pH is 8-9 to reduce calcium content in the solution, (iii) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (ii) with sulfuric acid to achieve a pH of less than 2.5 so as to minimize calcium content in solution, (iv) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (iii) with tartaric acid and sodium hydroxide to effect precipitation of potassium bitartrate while maintaining the pH of 2.5-3.1, (v) treating the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (iv) with calcium oxide/carbonate till the pH comes to 5-6, and (vi) discharging the supernatant/effluent obtained from step (v) as process effluent for subsequent environmental remediation/water recovery/salt reclamation.
   USE - The process is useful for preparation of multi-nutrient potassic fertilizer, preferably monopotassium phosphate, by recovering potassium from spent wash.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes spent wash for recovery of value-added product (i.e. potash fertilizer of greater than 99% purity); and improves ease of zero liquid discharge compliance by subjecting the relatively benign process effluent to industrially practiced techniques for water recovery and salt reclamation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing multi-nutrient potassic fertilizer, preferably monopotassium phosphate, by recovering potassium from spent wash, comprises (i) lime treatment of spent wash to generate carbon-rich sludge having pH greater than 12, (ii) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (i) with carbon dioxide till the pH is 8-9 to reduce calcium content in the solution, (iii) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (ii) with sulfuric acid to achieve a pH of less than 2.5 so as to minimize calcium content in solution, (iv) reacting the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (iii) with tartaric acid and sodium hydroxide to effect precipitation of potassium bitartrate while maintaining the pH of 2.5-3.1, (v) treating the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (iv) with calcium oxide/carbonate till the pH comes to 5-6 to recover residual tartrate as calcium tartrate, (vi) discharging the supernatant/effluent obtained from step (v) as process effluent for subsequent environmental remediation/water recovery/salt reclamation, (vii) reacting the potassium bitartrate obtained from step (iv) with calcium oxide/hydroxide/carbonate and phosphoric acid to produce solution of monopotassium phosphate, while precipitating calcium tartrate, (viii) crystallizing white crystalline monopotassium phosphate with purity of 99% through cooling/evaporating the supernatant/filtrate obtained from step (vii), (ix) treating the calcium tartrate precipitate obtained from step (v) and step (vii) with aqueous solution of sodium carbonate at 50-80 degrees C to generate disodium tartrate solution, while precipitating calcium carbonate, (x) using the disodium tartrate solution obtained from step (ix) for further process cycles in the reaction in step (iv) with additional quantity of sulfuric acid for in-situ generation of bitartrate ions, and (xi) using the calcium carbonate obtained from step (ix) for further process cycles in the reactions in step (v) and step (vii).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, lime treatment results in clarification of spent wash, potassium bitartrate precipitation is effected by maintaining mole ratio of tartaric acid and potassium content in treated spent wash to 1-1.1, potassium bitartrate is reacted with equimolar quantity of lime and phosphoric acid to produce monopotassium phosphate, insoluble tartrate salt is converted into soluble tartrate salt by reacting calcium tartrate with sodium carbonate, tartaric acid is recycled as disodium tartrate, and purging of carbon dioxide in step (ii) is done till the pH reaches 8.5. The pH of the reaction mixture in step (i) is maintained to 12.5-13.5. The pH in step (iv) is maintained to 2.8-2.9.
   EXAMPLE - 40 g Potassium bitartrate was dried (17.32 wt/wt.% potassium ion and 73.37 wt/wt.% tartaric acid) and added into 0.15 l water under stirring. 14.5 g Hydrated lime (available lime was 90%) and 20.5 g phosphoric acid (purity of 85%) were added into the potassium bitartrate slurry under stirring. Stirring was continued for 3 hours. The pH of the reaction mixture was 3.37. Upon filtration of the resultant slurry, 0.13 l filtrate (pale yellow, transparent, 62 wt/vol.% potassium ion, 0.02 wt/vol.% calcium ion and tartaric acid (nil)) was obtained, which upon evaporation yielded white crystals of monopotassium phosphate. The wet cake was dried to obtain 55 g calcium tartrate.

Chemical aspects of compost production.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.)

TI Chabazite molecular sieve useful for separation of mixed gas of carbon dioxide-nitrogen-oxygen comprises e.g. inorganic porous material having CHA topology formed by self-assembly of silicon oxytetrahedron and aluminoxy tetrahedron.
AU WANG S
   GUO Y
   SUN T
   ZHAO S
   WU R
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   DALIAN WANLIN IMPORT & EXPORT CO LTD (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 201847105J
AB    NOVELTY - Chabazite molecular sieve comprises e.g. an inorganic porous material having a CHA topology formed by self-assembly of a silicon oxytetrahedron and an aluminoxy tetrahedron, and the mol ratio of silicon/aluminum is 4-8, and adopts the BET method, the specific surface area is 400-800 m2/g, the crystal grains are 0.8-20 mu m, and different metal cations used for ion exchange modification, and the binary composite template agent is prepared in the preparation process, and where the preparation specifically comprises e.g. (1) adding suitable amount of sodium hydroxide and aluminum source dissolved in a specific amount of water, cooling to the room temperature, (2) slowly adding suitable amount of a mixture of template R1 and template R2 as double template agent under the stirring; and continuously stirring for 0.5-1 hour to make each component to fully mix, and (3) slowly adding suitable amount of the silicon source under vigorous stirring, and continuously stirring and aging.
   USE - The molecular sieve is useful for separation of mixed gas of carbon dioxide/nitrogen/oxygen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve has a large specific surface area and pore volume, and the eight-membered ring small pore window is favorable for improving the separation performance of carbon dioxide/nitrogen, and the equilibrium cation outside the skeleton is favorable for improving the separation performance of nitrogen/oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chabazite molecular sieve comprises an inorganic porous material having a CHA topology formed by self-assembly of a silicon oxytetrahedron and an aluminoxy tetrahedron, and the mol ratio of silicon/aluminum is 4-8, and adopts the BET method, the specific surface area is 400-800 m2/g, the crystal grains are 0.8-20 mu m, and different metal cations are used for ion exchange modification, and the binary composite template agent is prepared in the preparation process, and where the preparation specifically comprises (1) adding suitable amount of sodium hydroxide and aluminum source dissolved in a specific amount of water, cooling to the room temperature, (2) slowly adding suitable amount of a mixture of first template (R1) and second template (R2) as double template agent under the stirring; and continuously stirring for 0.5-1 hour to make each component to fully mix, (3) slowly adding suitable amount of the silicon source under vigorous stirring, and continuously stirring and aging for 5-24 hours at room temperature, then dynamically crystallizing for 2-5 days under the condition of 120-200 degrees C, (4) after finishing reaction, vacuum filtering and separating out solid product, washing until pH is not greater than 8, drying at 100 degrees C for 6-12 hours, roasting at 500-600 degrees C for 4-10 hours, and the roasting atmosphere is air; (5) using the obtained molecular sieve raw powder with different metal ions to be exchanged, the exchange temperature is 40-100 degrees C, and (6) activating the ion exchange molecular sieve powder by vacuum or inert atmosphere to obtain chabazite molecular sieve with excellent adsorption performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the first templating agent is one of tetramethylammonium hydroxide, tetraethylammonium hydroxide, tetrapropylammonium hydroxide and/or tetrabutylammonium hydroxide; the second templating agent is the group consisting of adamantane, amantadine, 1,3-adamantane diamine, 2-methyladamantane, and/or N,N,N-trimethyl-1-adamantyl ammonium hydroxide. In the step (2), the mol ratio of the first templating agent and second templating agent is 0.5:1-2:1. The silicon source used is one of a silica sol having a mass fraction of 30% and sodium silicate hydrate; the aluminum source is a mixture of aluminum hydroxide or aluminum hydroxide with sodium aluminate, aluminum sol, aluminum sulfate octahydrate or aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, and the weight ratio of aluminum hydroxide in the aluminum source is not less than 10%. The metal ion used for ion exchange is one of silver cation, lithium cation, calcium cation or its combination. The carbon dioxide adsorption amount at room temperature and normal pressure is 2-8 mmol/g, the nitrogen adsorption amount is 0.5-1.2 mmol/g, and oxygen adsorption amount is 0.2-0.5 mmol/g. Preferred Method: In the preparation method, each raw material satisfies: the mol ratio of silicon dioxide/aluminum oxide is 40:1-120:1, the mol ratio of sodium oxide/aluminum oxide is 2:1-12:1, the mol ratio of (R1+R2)/aluminum oxide is 2:1-20:1, and the mol ratio of water/aluminum oxide is 1000:1-5000:1. In the step (5), the specific conditions for the exchange with different metal ions comprises the metal salt used is one of the nitrate, acetate and chloride corresponding to the corresponding metal ion, and the metal ion used, the concentration is 0.1-2 mol/l, the target cation exceeds 2-8 times, and the exchange temperature is 40-100 degrees C; the exchange time is 2-5 hours, and the repeating for 2-4 times. The degree of cation exchange is not less than 95%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN RU2643256-C1
TI Installation and method for chlorella plancton strains cultivation.
AU BOGDANOV N I
   GRABARNIK V E
AE BOGDANOV N I (BOGD-Individual)
   GRABARNIK V E (GRAB-Individual)
GA 201813087S
AB    NOVELTY - Installation contains aquariums of transparent material arranged on frame, interconnected by a pipeline including drain holes connected to the finished suspension container and the nutrient solution container, and luminaires placed between the aquariums. There are three aquariums for suspension on the frame, the total volume of the two side aquariums is equal to the volume of the central one, the lamps are located vertically between the side aquariums and horizontally above the central container. All of them are united in a luminaire, consisting of a single frame fixed to the stand frame, with a possibility of its transfer to a vertical position when maintaining the aquariums and switching to sunlight. Containers for nutrient solution preparation are exposed at a level above the aquariums, and each aquarium has a separate container with the corresponding volume. In the bottom wall of each container has an opening to which pipelines for nutrient solution draining are connected. Each aquarium has three openings, one of which serves to balance the suspension volumes, the second - to drain the finished suspension, the third is located in the lower plane and is designed to remove bottom sediments and washing water while washing the aquarium, and a container for the nutrient solution. Aquariums and the container for collection and storage of finished products are interconnected by a pipeline. In the method, chlorella strains are grown using a light source, optimal nutrient medium and temperature conditions. Optimal lighting in the initial period of cultivation is achieved by vertically located lamps between the aquariums. During the growth of cells and increase of the optical density of the culture, horizontal lamps located above the central aquarium are connected. The culture which has reached the normative optical density, is poured alternately, once from the central aquarium, next time from two side containers. The original culture flows into the empty aquarium after draining the culture through the connecting pipeline, after which a nutrient solution is added. Stock culture production is integrated into a single biotechnological process, which is carried out alternately in the central or side containers. Cultivation is carried out in different regimes using separate plankton strains: Chlorella vulgaris IGF C-111, Chlorella vulgaris BIN, Chlorella kessleri VKPM Al-12 and nutrient solution optimal for each of them in terms of 1 l of tap water: Mode I: Culture of Chlorella vulgaris IGF C111 strain, block 1: Nitric-phosphorus solution 0.3 ml, copper-cobalt solution 0.5 ml; block 2: Solution of carbon dioxide obtained using a material containing fiber (straw or wheat germ), pH 6.0-7.0, 10.0 ml. Mode II: Culture of Chlorella vulgaris BIN strain, block 1: Nitric-phosphorus solution 0.6 ml, copper-cobalt solution 1.0 ml, block 2: Solution of carbon dioxide obtained using a grain material (wheat, holer oats), pH 5.0-6.0, 5.0-10.0 ml. Mode III: Culture of Chlorella vulgaris VKPM Al-12 strain, block I: Nitric-phosphoric solution 0.9 ml, Copper-cobalt solution 1.5 ml, Potassium sulphate solution 1.0 ml, block 2; Solution of carbon dioxide obtained with using grain material (holer oats), pH 5.0-6.0, 30.0 ml, intended for obtaining: according to Mode I - fodder for farm animals, according to Mode II - reservoir algolizant, according to Mode III - chlorella biomass.
   USE - Biotechnology.
   ADVANTAGE - Inventions allow to optimize cultivated crops lighting.
2 cl, 3 dwg, 3 ex

PT P
PN WO2018017693-A1; EP3845695-A1; US2021361789-A1
TI Disinfecting and optionally, sterilizing non-woven materials, comprises subjecting non-woven materials to high temperature steam, and subjecting steam-treated non-woven materials to disinfecting treatment e.g. UV light and ethylene oxide.
AU NUNN K J
   BROWN S H
AE PSIL HOLDINGS LLC (PSIL-Non-standard)
   NUNN K J (NUNN-Individual)
   PSIL HOLDINGS LLOC (PSIL-Non-standard)
GA 2018074074
AB    NOVELTY - Disinfecting (P1) and optionally, sterilizing non-woven materials, comprises: (a) subjecting non-woven materials to high temperature steam in manner in which water content of non-woven materials does not exceed 25 wt.%, where high temperature steam can be saturated steam, slightly superheated steam or superheated steam; and (b) subjecting steam-treated non-woven materials to disinfecting treatment, where disinfecting treatment is UV light, ethylene oxide, methyl bromide, and supercritical or subcritical carbon dioxide, or ozone under conditions to remove microbial contaminants that is present.
   USE - The processes are useful for disinfecting and optionally, sterilizing non-woven materials or fibers, where the non-woven material is useful in personal care, baby care, cosmetic applications, household cleaning, automotive applications or industrial cleaning applications (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: convert fibers into disinfected and/or sterilized non-woven materials; utilize the deconstructing post-consumer and/or post-industrial waste e.g. used textiles and/or leather materials, wood waste and recycled paper; provide the spunlaced non-woven materials that are treated to remove microbial contaminants; and provide fabric integrity, softness, drape, conformability for non-woven materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Disinfecting (P1) and optionally, sterilizing non-woven materials, comprises: (a) subjecting non-woven materials to high temperature steam in a manner in which the water content of the non-woven materials does not exceed around 25 wt.%, where the high temperature steam can be saturated steam, slightly superheated steam or superheated steam; and (b) subjecting the steam-treated non-woven materials to a disinfecting treatment, where the disinfecting treatment is UV light, ethylene oxide, methyl bromide, and supercritical or subcritical carbon dioxide, or ozone under conditions to remove microbial contaminants that is present. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) disinfecting (P2) and optionally, sterilizing fibers, comprising (a1) subjecting fibers to high temperature steam in a manner in which the water content of the fibers does not exceed around 25 wt.%, where the high temperature steam can be saturated steam, slightly superheated steam or superheated steam, and (b) subjecting the steam-treated fibers to a disinfecting treatment, where the disinfecting treatment is UV light, ethylene oxide, methyl bromide, and supercritical or subcritical carbon dioxide, or ozone under conditions to remove microbial contaminants that is present; and
   (2) a device for preparing disinfected non-woven materials from fibers, comprising a blend line for blending fibers, a blend line storage box for storing the blended fibers, a first conveyor belt to convey the blended fibers, a non-woven card, to align the fibers, a hydroentanglement or needle punching system comprising a dewatering belt, an optional chemical dosing system to apply a chemical treatment to the non-woven material, a drier, a calender, a module that applies high temperature steam to the non-woven material which can be saturated steam, slightly superheated steam, or superheated steam, which module comprises at least one nozzle through which the high temperature steam can be applied, a module which applies UV light in a quantity for duration of time to effectively destroy any residual contamination on the non-woven material, and at least conveyor belt to convey the fibers and/or non-woven materials through the apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises: an airlaid card to receive the blended fibers from the storage box; and a winder for winding up the non- woven material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antimicrobials are quaternary ammonium salts.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: In the method (P1): the water content of the non-woven materials does not exceed around 15 wt.%; the disinfecting treatment is UV light; the UV light is UV-C radiation at a wavelength of 100-280 nm; the disinfecting treatment is an ozone treatment; before or after being subjected to high-temperature steam, at least one chemical is applied to the non-woven materials, where the chemical comprises pesticides, insecticides, fertilizers, antimicrobials, antivirals, antimycotics, antibacterials, anti-rickettsials, antibiotics, biocides and/or biostats; the non-woven materials are prepared from virgin fibers, fibers obtained by deconstructing post-consumer and/or post-industrial waste; the combinations of fibers are used and the combinations of fibers are subjected to an intimate mixing step to provide homogeneity to the fibers, so that the composition of the blend is fairly uniform, where fairly uniform means that the composition varies by no more than 20% in any portion of the blended fibers; the non-woven materials are prepared by hydroentanglement or needle punching prior to being subjected to the high-temperature steam and disinfecting treatment; the non-woven materials are prepared by bonding fibers using thermal, chemical or adhesive bonding processes; the non-woven materials are calendered after the hydroentanglement or needle punching step, and prior to being subjected to the high-temperature steam and disinfecting treatment; and the high-temperature steam is saturated steam or slightly superheated steam. In the method (P2): the water content of the fibers does not exceed around 15 wt.%; the disinfecting treatment is UV light; the UV light is UV-C radiation at a wavelength of 100-280 nm; the disinfecting treatment is an ozone treatment; before or after being subjected to high-temperature steam and at least one chemical is applied to the non-woven materials, where the chemical comprises pesticides, insecticides, fertilizers, antimicrobials, antivirals, antimycotics, antibacterials, anti-rickettsials, antibiotics, biocides and/or biostats; the fibers are prepared from virgin fibers, fibers obtained by deconstructing post-consumer and/or post-industrial waste; the combinations of fibers are used and the combinations of fibers are subjected to an intimate mixing step to provide homogeneity to the fibers, so that the composition of the blend is fairly uniform, where fairly uniform means that the composition varies by no more than 20% in any portion of the blended fibers; the fibers are applied to an air card; the fibers are subjected to at least one carding step; the carded fibers are subjected to hydroentanglement or needle punching steps to form a non-woven material; 31. the non-woven material is subjected to a calendaring step; and the fibers are subjected to thermal, chemical or adhesive bonding processes. The process (P2) further comprises: subjecting the non-woven materials to high-temperature steam and a disinfecting treatment, where the high temperature steam can be saturated steam, slightly superheated steam, or superheated steam, and the disinfecting treatment is UV light, ethylene oxide, methyl bromide, and supercritical or subcritical carbon dioxide, under conditions to remove any microbial contaminants that may be present; and subjecting the non-woven materials to a disinfecting process comprising treatment with ozone. Preferred Components: The post-consumer and/or post-industrial waste comprises used textiles and/or leather materials. The fibers are cellulosic fibers. The cellulosic fibers comprise wood fibers.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN WO2022147560-A1; US2022212141-A1
TI Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide from large point source e.g. cement factory, has conveyor belt that is configured to rotate within housing such that portion of carbon dioxide sorbent cycles through zones repeatedly in sequential order.
AU YOUNES M
   JAMAL A
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202287942H
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100a) has conveyor belt (1300) rotates within the housing (1001), such that a portion of the carbon dioxide sorbent cycles through the set of zones repeatedly in sequential order. The carbon dioxide sorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-containing fluid as the carbon dioxide-containing fluid flows through the porous medium to produce the carbon dioxide-depleted fluid, within the first zone at a first temperature. The carbon dioxide sorbent desorb the carbon dioxide captured from the carbon dioxide-containing fluid to produce the carbon dioxide rich fluid (1021) within the third zone at a second temperature, thus regenerates the carbon dioxide sorbent. The liquid heating medium in the second zone is configured to heat the carbon dioxide sorbent from the first temperature toward the second temperature, and the liquid cooling medium in the fourth zone is configured to cool the carbon dioxide sorbent from the second temperature to the first temperature.
   USE - Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide capture apparatus comprises a housing that defines a first zone for carbon dioxide adsorption, a second zone for sorbent heating, a third zone for the carbon dioxide desorption and a fourth zone for a sorbent cooling, where second zone is filled with a liquid heating medium, and thus enables to reduce the operating pressure of the third zone to a pressure that is equal to or less than an operating pressure in the first zone, and also reduces the operating temperature of the liquid cooling medium in the fourth zone.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide.
   Apparatus (1000a)
   Housing (1001)
   Carbon dioxide rich fluid (1021)
   Conveyor belt (1300)
   Electric heater (1500)

TI Carbon dioxide recovery device used in carbon            dioxide recovery system, has regeneration unit that is            provided with anode, which generates complex compound            of amine by desorbing carbon dioxide, and cathode,            which is electrically connected to anode and            regenerates amine from complex compound.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide recovery device (1) has an                absorption unit that generates a compound of carbon                dioxide and an amine contained in an absorption                liquid, and a regeneration unit comprising an anode                that generates a complex compound of the amine by                desorbing carbon dioxide from compound, and a                cathode that is electrically connected to anode and                regenerates the amine from complex compound. The                absorption unit generates compound of carbon                dioxide contained in biogas and amine contained in                absorption liquid. The regeneration portion has a                separator for partitioning anode chamber (9), where                anode is provided, and cathode chamber (14), the                cathode (13) is provided. The regeneration unit has                a gas-liquid separation unit which is sent from the                anode chamber and separates carbon dioxide desorbed                at anode and absorption liquid containing amine                complex compound. The absorption liquid containing                amine complex compound is sent from gas-liquid                separation unit to cathode chamber.
   USE - The carbon dioxide recovery device is useful                in carbon dioxide recovery system for recovering                carbon dioxide (all claimed), and used in renewable                energy, such as sunlight, wind power, and hydraulic                energy as electrical energy for power supply.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery device recovers                carbon dioxide with low energy, reduces the carbon                dioxide emission of entire system, recovers the                carbon dioxide into valuable resources, effectively                reduces carbon dioxide, electrochemically                regenerates the absorbed liquid, is easy to match                the load of carbon dioxide recovery and                co-electrolysis, and improves the controllability                of entire carbon dioxide recovery system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide recovery system;                and
   (2) a method for recovering carbon                dioxide.

TI New p-terphenyl compound used as catalyst for carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction reaction.
AU WANG P
   LI Z
   LIU Q
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2020C1307A
AB    NOVELTY - P-terphenyl compound (I) is new.
   USE - The p-terphenyl compound is used as catalyst for carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound has good solubility, stability and good catalytic performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - P-terphenyl compound of formula (I) is new.
   M=divalent transition metal ion;
   m=1-10; and
   n=30-50.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Compound (I) is prepared by performing substitution on 4,5-dibromo-catechol with brominated alkene of formula (2) to obtain brominated aromatic hydrocarbons containing terminal alkenyl of formula (3), performing Suzuki coupling reaction of brominated aromatic hydrocarbon containing terminal alkenyl of formula (3) with 4-(2,2 ',6',2''-terpyridyl)-phenylboronic acid to obtain V-shaped bistripyridine of formula (4), performing self-assembly reaction of V-shaped bis (terpyridine) with divalent transition metal ions forms supramolecular metal complexes and performing cross-linking reaction on supramolecular metal complex by light..
   Preferred Definitions:
   M=Fe2+, Co26+, Cd2+, Zn2+ or Ni2+.

PT P
PN WO2019238548-A1; CA3102882-A1; IN202147000559-A; AU2019284962-A1; KR2021018443-A; BR112020024893-A2; EP3802509-A1; CN112566903-A; TW202014414-A; US2021163439-A1; SG11202012234-A1; JP2021527078-W; US2022056005-A9; RU2774316-C1
TI Preparing cyclic organic carbonates involve            charging an epoxide in the presence of carbon dioxide            and then adding a catalyst, where cyclic organic            carbonate is glycerol carbonate (meth)acrylate and the            epoxide is glycidyl (meth)acrylate.
AU TRESKOW M
   GRAEFF G
   CASPARI M
   SCHUETZ T
   KRILL S
   BLEITH T
   GRAFF G
   SCHUTZ T
   SCHITZ T
AE EVONIK ROEHM GMBH (ROHG-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK OPERATIONS GMBH (EVON-C)
   EVONIK ROEHM GMBH (ROHG-C)
GA 2019A6157E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic organic carbonates involve                charging an epoxide in the presence of carbon                dioxide and then adding a catalyst and in that the                reaction scale is greater than 5 mol.
   USE - Method for preparing cyclic organic                carbonates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cyclic organic                  carbonate is glycerol carbonate (meth)acrylate                  and the epoxide is glycidyl (meth)acrylate. The                  catalyst is selected from the group of the                  trialkylhydroxyalkylphosphonium bromides and                  trialkylhydroxyalkylammonium halides, preferably                  trialkylhydroxyalkylammonium bromide, more                  preferably tributylhydroxyethylphosphonium                  bromide. The catalyst is isolated from the                  reaction mixture, and optionally in that the                  catalyst is supplied to at least one further                  reaction. The polarity of the product solution is                  lowered by adding a solvent to such a degree that                  the catalyst salt is absorbed by filtering                  through a polar stationary phase, and hence the                  product is freed continuously from the catalyst.                  The catalyst is reactivated by adding bromide                  salts selected from the group of ammonium                  bromide, alkylphosphonium bromides,                  hydroxyalkylammonium bromides,                  hydroxyalkylphosphonium bromides, alkylsulfonium                  bromides. At east one stabilizer selected from                  the group consisting of substituted phenol                  derivatives, preferably hydroquinone monomethyl                  ether (HQME), 3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxytoluene                  (BHT), 4- methoxyphenol (HQ) and mixtures are                  used. Preferred Conditions: The reaction                  temperature is below 90 degrees C. The                  temperature is increased stepwise. The carbon                  dioxide insertion is effected at pressures                  between 1 and 10 bar, preferably between 2 and 8                  bar and more preferably between 3 and 7 bar. The                  catalyst content of the reaction mixture is                  between 0.05 mol.% and 25 mol.%, preferably                  between 0.5 mol.% and 10 mol%, and is more                  preferably 2 mol.%. The stabilizer content is                  between 20 ppm and 700 ppm, preferably between                  100 ppm and 300 ppm.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.)
MC E07-A04; E11-B01; E11-F02B; N05-D; N05-E01; N06-E02; N07-D02B; N07-F04

AB    NOVELTY - The system has an infrared sensor (1) that is                arranged in the suffocation pit, and detects the                carbon dioxide concentration in the suffocation                pit. A gas filling pump (2) is arranged at the                inlet end of the carbon dioxide delivery pipe, and                is used to pump carbon dioxide for suffocation into                the carbon dioxide delivery pipe. A solenoid valve                (3) is arranged at the gas outlet end of the carbon                dioxide conveying pipe, and supplies the                suffocating carbon dioxide through the carbon                dioxide conveying pipe into the suffocating pit. A                pressure reducing valve (4) is arranged on the                carbon dioxide delivery pipe at the upstream end of                the solenoid valve, so as to adjust the working                pressure of the solenoid valve to supply air to the                suffocation pit. A carbon dioxide detector (5) is                arranged on the carbon dioxide transportation                pipeline at the upstream end of the pressure                reducing valve, and detects the carbon dioxide                concentration in the carbon dioxide transportation                pipeline.
   USE - Carbon dioxide monitoring system for                suffocating case used for slaughtering live                pigs.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide monitoring system of the                suffocation cabinet can automatically control the                carbon dioxide concentration in the suffocation                pit, and keep the carbon dioxide concentration in                the suffocation pit stable, while improving the                slaughter efficiency of live pig and ensuring the                quality of meat after slaughter.

A                crude carbon dioxide gas (17) is added to                regenerator to be preheated by adding a small                amount of oxygen (18). The first portion of liquid                carbon dioxide is made to enter the bottom of                second stripper bottom of bottom heater to                evaporate into a gas. The second                portion of liquid carbon dioxide is made to enter                the high purity carbon dioxide storage tank.

   ADVANTAGE - The production apparatus is suitable for                large-scale industrial production, safe and                reliable. TI Extraction of carbon dioxide from hydrosphere in seawater desalination, involves performing electrochemical carbon dioxide recovery after extracting chemically and/or physically dissolved carbon dioxide from climate-relevant water source.
AU UNGERER M
   HANEBUTH M
   KRAUSE R
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2020A61578
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of carbon dioxide (53) from hydrosphere involves performing electrochemical carbon dioxide recovery after extracting chemically and/or physically dissolved carbon dioxide from climate-relevant water source.
   USE - Extraction of carbon dioxide from hydrosphere e.g. seawater in seawater desalination.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and large-scale extraction of carbon dioxide from hydrosphere with reduced need for chemicals to reduce scaling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for decarbonization reactor, which comprises a connection line for a climate-relevant carbonate-rich water source in which chemically and/or physically dissolved carbon dioxide is present, a feed line for a chemisorbent and/or a physisorbent which binds to carbon dioxide, a reagent regeneration reactor for the release of carbon dioxide, and a carbon dioxide removal line which is connected to a carbon dioxide electrolyzer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the decarbonization of seawater in desalination plant.
   Seawater (50)
   Concentrate stream (52)
   Carbon dioxide (53)
   Decarbonization (501)
   Desalination (502)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The decarbonization reactor is in communication with a seawater desalination plant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The chemically dissolved carbon dioxide is extracted from water source, preferably seawater (50) using a chemisorbent. The physically dissolved carbon dioxide is extracted from water source, using a physisorbent, preferably silica gel. The temperature of the water source is increased, such that carbon dioxide is outgassed and removed from the gas phase above the liquid level. Water is withdrawn from the water source and subjected to decarbonization, where (solid) carbonate precipitates, and thermal regeneration is performed to produce carbon dioxide. The thermal regeneration is followed by exothermic hydrolysis. The carbon dioxide extraction is combined with seawater desalination (502). The carbon dioxide extraction is performed by performing decarbonization (501) of the seawater, and subjecting decarbonized seawater to desalination, preferably reverse osmosis process. Waste heat from hydrolysis process is used to heat water before the reverse osmosis process. Seawater is subjected to the desalination process, before the performing carbon dioxide extraction by decarbonization of the concentrate stream (52).

PT P
PN WO2021244728-A1
TI Preparing precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. adding solid calcium bearing material to stirred extraction solution, separating solid residual material, precipitating calcium carbonate by carbonizing, and recirculating to extract.
AU MOHAMED H A
   SALUSTE A
   UIBU M
   TRIKKEL A
   KUUSIK R
   GREGOR A
   MOTLEP R
   KIRSIMAEE K
AE R-S OSA SERVICE OUE (RSOS-Non-standard)
GA 2021E0395T
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing precipitated calcium carbonate comprises e.g. (1) adding solid calcium bearing material into a continuously stirred extraction solution (R1), (2) separating the solid residual material from the calcium rich solution, (3) subjecting the calcium rich solution to carbonation (CR1) for precipitating calcium carbonate, (4) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, recovering the extraction solution and directing it to the extraction solution (R1), (5) subjecting residual material to a second calcium extraction (R2), (6) separating residual material from the aqueous calcium rich solution while still under pressure, (7) subjecting the aqueous calcium rich solution to precipitation (CR2) by releasing carbon dioxide pressure to precipitate calcium carbonate, and (8) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from the solution, recirculating the recovered solution back to the second calcium extraction (R2) in step (5).
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing precipitated calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces high-grade calcium carbonate precipitate; reduces chemical consumption per unit mass of calcium carbonate precipitate produced, operational expenditure, water treatment costs and carbon footprint, and use of chemicals; increases extraction efficiency in materials that contain significant contents of reactive and free calcium compounds e.g. free lime, portlandite and acetylene sulfide; produces commercially viable product i.e. high purity precipitated calcium carbonate, from industrial waste or by-products to reduce virgin material mining, extraction and use; captures and utilizes carbon dioxide originating from industries; reduces alkalinity, heavy metal and salt concentration in the solid residual material left from the process; and improves the calcium solubility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing precipitated calcium carbonate comprises (1) adding solid calcium bearing material into a continuously stirred extraction solution (R1), where the solid calcium bearing material is oil shale ash from energy or oil and gas generation or iron and steelmaking slags or coal ash or waste cement, (2) separating the solid residual material from the calcium rich solution, (3) subjecting the calcium rich solution from step (2) to carbonation (CR1) for precipitating calcium carbonate, where carbonation is induced by introducing carbon dioxide gas directly into the calcium containing aqueous solution (CR1), (4) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, recovering the extraction solution and directing it to the extraction solution (R1), for use in step (1), (5) subjecting residual material from step (2) to a second calcium extraction (R2), where carbonic acid is used as calcium extraction agent, the carbonic acid is produced by increasing carbon dioxide partial pressure within the extraction reactor for dissolving carbon dioxide in water, (6) separating residual material from the aqueous calcium rich solution while still under pressure, (7) subjecting the aqueous calcium rich solution to precipitation (CR2) by releasing carbon dioxide pressure to precipitate calcium carbonate, and (8) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from the solution, recirculating the recovered solution back to the second calcium extraction (R2) in step (5).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (9) introducing the calcium carbonate precipitate from step (4) and/or step (8) into a continuously stirred redissolution solution (R3), where carbonic acid is used as calcium carbonate dissolving agent, and the carbonic acid is produced by increasing carbon dioxide partial pressure within the redissolution solution reactor (R3) for dissolving carbon dioxide in water, (10) separating the undissolved calcium carbonate solids from aqueous calcium rich solution while still under carbon dioxide pressure, transferring the undissolved solids back to the redissolution solution (R3) for redissolution, (11) subjecting the aqueous calcium rich solution to precipitation (CR3) by releasing carbon dioxide pressure to precipitate calcium carbonate, (12) separating the calcium carbonate precipitate from solution, recirculating the recovered solution back into-the redissolution solution (R3) in step (9), and (13) processing the precipitated calcium carbonate further by washing with water and then de-watering to obtain dry product. In the method: in step (3), the carbon dioxide is introduced into calcium containing solution by bubbling or spraying and the temperature in the solution is 3-80 degrees C, preferably 25-45 degrees C, and the pH is greater than 7.5, preferably greater than 8; in step (5), the residual material from step (2) is transferred to the second calcium extraction (R2) containing water to achieve solid to liquid ratio of 1:1-1:20, preferably 1:2-1:5, carbon dioxide gas is introduced into solution until a carbon dioxide partial pressure of 1-20 MPa, preferably 5-15 MPa for producing carbonic acid in solution, the preferred temperature in the reactor is 3-150 degrees C, preferably 30-80 degrees C; in step (7) during the precipitation (CR2), the pressure is reduced to 0.1-7 MPa, preferably 0.1-2 MPa, and the temperature is 20-180 degrees C, preferably 20-200 degrees C; the precipitate of calcium carbonate and the residual material is washed with water and de-watered, the ammonium salt in the washed solution is concentrated by distillation or by membrane separation and re-used in the calcium extraction process in step (1) in the continuously stirred extraction solution (R1); the ferromagnetic material in residue from step (2) and/or step (6) is isolated using aqueous chemical agent and/or magnetic method and then processed; in step (9), the solid to liquid ratio is 1:1-1:20, preferably 1:2-1:5, the carbon dioxide partial pressure is 2-17 MPa, preferably 5-17 MPa, and the temperature is 3-150 degrees C, preferably 30-80 degrees C; in step (5) and step (9), carbon dioxide is present in content of greater than 50 vol.%, preferably greater than 90 vol.%, and gas is originates from industrial waste gases, preferably the carbon dioxide in the industrial waste gases is separated, purified and concentrated before use; in steps (3), (7) and (11), seed crystals are introduced into the reactor for initiating and influencing the crystal formation process; and in steps (2), (4), (6), (8), (10) and (12), separation technique is sedimentation, centrifugation, decanting, filtration and/or reverse osmosis. Preferred Components: In the method: the extraction solution is either water or ammonium-based aqueous solution e.g. ammonium chloride, preferably ammonium acetate; the solid calcium bearing material has nominal grain size of less than 1000 mu m, preferably less than 200 mu m, materials are mechanically sieved or mechanically comminuted in a dry environment; in step (1), the solid to liquid ratio is 1:1-1:20, preferably 1:1-1:5, the molarity of extraction solvent is 0.1-3 M, preferably 1.5-2 M, the temperature is 3-100 degrees C, preferably 25-70 degrees C, and the pH of solution at the end of calcium extraction is 8-13; in step (3), carbon dioxide is present in content of greater than 5 vol.% gas, where gas is originates from industrial waste gases, preferably carbon dioxide in the industrial waste gases is separated, purified and concentrated before use; the precipitated calcium carbonate produced is characterized by a calcium carbonate content is greater than 95 wt.%, an average particle diameter of 0.05-10 mu m, ferric oxide concentration is less than 0.2 wt.% and Hunter Whiteness Index of greater than 85%; and in the residual material from step (6), the silica oxide and magnesium oxide concentration is greater than 45 wt.% and greater than 15 wt.% respectively.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
