TI Electrochemical synthesizing green urea in            electrochemical cell having electrolyte and            three-electrode system of e.g. carbon paper loaded with            copper phthalocyanine catalyst ink, comprises            subjecting nitrogen source and carbon dioxide gas.
AU GHORAI U K
   MUKHERJEE J
   PAUL S
   ADALDER A
AE RAMAKRISHNA MISSION VIDYAMANDIRA (RAMA-Non-standard)
   GHORAI U K (GHOR-Individual)
GA 202297532S
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical synthesizing (P1) green urea                in an electrochemical cell including an electrolyte                and a three-electrode system comprising a working                electrode (1) comprising carbon paper loaded with                copper phthalocyanine catalyst ink, a reference                electrode (3) of silver/silver chloride saturated                with potassium chloride and an auxiliary or counter                electrode which is a platinum wire, comprises                subjecting a nitrogen source and carbon dioxide gas                to produce green urea by electrocatalytic                reduction.
   USE - The processes are useful for electrochemical                synthesizing green urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides electrochemical synthesis                of green urea which: is simple and fast process;                has much less energy consumption compared to                industrial processes; can be carried out under room                temperature and pressure, applying very low                reduction potential; uses natural eco-friendly,                easily available raw materials and the process is                environment friendly; leads to green urea in liquid                form which is easy to use; does not use expensive                noble metals and hence a cost-effective process;                and uses greenhouse gases and is a long-term                sustainable process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   electrochemical synthesizing (P2) green urea                in the electrochemical cell, comprising purging                nitrogen and carbon dioxide gases into the                electrolyte, followed by electro-reduction of the                gases and combination of the electrocatalytic                reduced products to produce green urea;
   electrochemical synthesizing (P3) urea in the                electrochemical cell, comprising providing the                nitrite ion and nitrate ion containing nitrogen                source as the electrolyte, purging carbon dioxide                gas into the electrolyte solution, followed by                electrocatalytic reduction of the ions and carbon                dioxide gas, and combination of the                electrocatalytic reduced products to produce green                urea; and
   electrochemical cell for the electrochemical                synthesis of green urea, comprising anodic chamber                and a cathodic chamber in fluid connectivity with                each other through a tubular structure configured                to hold a membrane separating the anodic chamber                and cathodic chamber, where the anodic chamber and                cathodic chamber is configured to include an                electrolyte and the three-electrode system, the                cathodic chamber includes at least two inlets for                the entry of gases and at least one outlet for the                exit of gases from its.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic diagram of H-type                electrochemical setup.
   1Working electrode
   2Gas outlet
   3Reference electrode
   4First inlet
   5Second inlet
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When the nitrogen source                  is nitrogen, nitrogen gas is diffused into the                  electrolyte for electrocatalytic reduction.                  During the reduction process, the rate of entry                  of nitrogen gas is 2-5 ml/minutes and carbon                  dioxide gas is 1-3 ml/minutes. The                  electro-reduction is effected for 1-3.5 hours.                  Preferred Components: The reference electrode is                  saturated with 3.5 M potassium chloride. The                  nitrogen source is nitrogen gas and ionic forms                  of nitrogen oxides (nitrite ion and nitrate ion).                  The nitrogen source is ionic forms of nitrogen                  oxides, the ionic forms of nitrogen oxides                  (nitrite ion and nitrate ion) are present as the                  electrolyte. The urea is green urea which is                  obtained from the electrolyte in liquid                  form.
   EXAMPLE - Nitrogen and carbon dioxide gases were                continuously purged into the 0.1 M potassium                bicarbonate electrolyte solution for 30 minutes.                During the dual gases reduction process, the rate                of entry of nitrogen gas was 4 ml/minutes and                carbon dioxide gas was 2 ml/minutes. All the                electrochemical processes were carried out in an                electrochemical work station using a                three-electrode system, where the platinum wire was                an auxiliary or counter electrode, silver/silver                chloride (saturated in 3.5 M potassium chloride)                was the reference electrode and carbon paper loaded                with copper phthalocyanine catalyst ink was the                working electrode. All potential values were                changed to the reversible hydrogen electrode. A                hydrogen ion type cell was used for all                electrochemical reduction processes. In an hydrogen                ion type cell, two cell chambers (anodic and                cathodic chambers) were separated by a Nafion(RTM:                Sulfonated tetrafluroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer) 117 membrane. Before using                the Nafion(RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer) 117 membrane, it was first                treated in 5 wt% hydrogen peroxide at 80&#176;                C for 1 hour and 0.1 M sulfuric acid at                80&#176; C for 1 hour and then in distilled                water for 3 hours at 80&#176; C. 70 ml                Electrolyte solution was taken in both anode as                well as cathode chambers. Then, the working                electrode and reference electrode were kept in                cathode chamber and counter electrode was kept in                anode chamber.

PN CN114451528-A
TI Producing cumin flavor salted egg involves            extracting cumin extract-oleoresin or essential oil by            traditional solvent method, supercritical carbon            dioxide method, subcritical method and other extraction            technologies.
AU HUANG Q
AE GUANGZHOU CHILLIHOT FOOD INGREDIENTS CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202268627P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing cumin flavor salted egg involves                extracting cumin extract-oleoresin (or essential                oil) by the traditional solvent method,                supercritical carbon dioxide method, subcritical                method and other extraction technologies. The                salted egg with the cumin flavor is particularly                produced by combining the traditional salted egg                pickling process on the basis of quantitative                analysis. The specific industrial process involves                carrying out quantitative analysis on cumin oil                resin (essential oil) which is the cumin extract                obtained by the extraction technologies of the                traditional solvent method, supercritical carbon                dioxide method, or subcritical method and product A                is obtained. The cumin extract (product A) is                changed into water-soluble product B by using                proper emulsifier (such as Tween (RTM:                Polysorbates) series, sucrose ester, monoglyceride,                etc.) and emulsification process.
   USE - Method for producing cumin flavor salted egg                used in food processing technology for people to                pursue more different tastes.
   ADVANTAGE - The salted egg not only keeps the traditional                flavor characteristic, but also has obvious cumin                flavor characteristic. The cumin-specific fragrance                and taste are increased, so as to further enrich                the dining table of people, satisfy the taste                requirement of our country consumer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing cumin flavor salted egg involves                extracting cumin extract-oleoresin (or essential                oil) by the traditional solvent method,                supercritical carbon dioxide method, subcritical                method and other extraction technologies. The                salted egg with the cumin flavor is particularly                produced by combining the traditional salted egg                pickling process on the basis of quantitative                analysis. The specific industrial process involves                carrying out quantitative analysis on cumin oil                resin (essential oil) which is the cumin extract                obtained by the extraction technologies of the                traditional solvent method, supercritical carbon                dioxide method, or subcritical method and product A                is obtained. The cumin extract (product A) is                changed into water-soluble product B by using                proper emulsifier (such as Tween (RTM:                Polysorbates) series, sucrose ester, monoglyceride,                etc.) and emulsification process. The product B                (including other water-soluble flavoring substances                and essence and perfume) is uniformly dispersed in                10 wt.% saline water to obtain product C. The                cleaned eggs are put into product C, and pickled                for 1 month to obtain the cumin-flavor salted eggs.                The cumin flavor refers to the flavor of both the                traditional salted egg and the characteristic                flavor of cumin.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbonization reactor (1) includes a gas                storage tank (8), a reaction tower (7), a                carbonization kettle (9), a first circulation                pipeline (11) and a second circulation pipeline                (12). The top of the reaction tower is provided                with an atomizing nozzle (6), and the bottom of the                reaction tower is provided with an air inlet pipe.                The gas storage tank is connected to the reaction                tower via air inlet pipe. The mixed solution can                enter the reaction tower in the form of droplets                from the nozzle. The top of the reaction tower is                also provided with an air outlet connected to the                kettle via the pipeline. The unreacted gas in the                reaction tower can enter the carbonization kettle                via the pipeline. The bottom of the reaction tower                is provided with a discharge port which is                connected to the reactor. The air inlet of the                circulation pipeline is connected to the kettle,                and the gas outlet of the circulation pipeline                extends below the reaction liquid in the                kettle.
   USE - Reactor used for carbonizing waste lead paste                to recover lead used in lead-acid battery in                electric moped, electric tricycle, and electric                vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The unreacted carbon dioxide in the                carbonization reactor continues to enter the                carbonization reactor through the second                circulation pipeline and continues to react, thus                the full utilization of carbon dioxide is realized,                and the cost is saved. There are several inclined                plates in the reaction tower, so that the reaction                residence time of carbon dioxide and complex liquid                in the reaction tower is prolonged, thus the                reaction efficiency is improved. The unreacted                carbon dioxide in the reaction tower and the                carbonization reactor is recycled, the                carbonization conversion time is short, and the                reaction efficiency is high, so that the carbon                dioxide utilization rate during the reaction is                high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                carbonization reaction method for waste lead                paste.,
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbonization reactor.

PN CN111001292-A; CN111001292-B
TI Coal chemical chain combustion mercury removal device, has oxidized oxygen carrier that passes through air cyclone, and wet flue gas desulfurizer that is connected to fuel reactor, and is configured to deliver carbon dioxide to fuel reactor.
AU LIU D
   LIU Z
   JIN J
   XIONG Z
   WANG Q

AE UNIV SHANGHAI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USHS-C)
GA 202032372G
AB    NOVELTY - The removal device (100) has a fuel reactor (1) that is configured for putting fuel into combustion. A fuel reactor U-phase valve (2) is connected to the fuel reactor. An air reactor (3) is used to provide an oxygen carrier. An air cyclone separator (4) is used to separate the oxygen carrier from the air. An air reactor U-phase valve (6) is connected to the air cyclone separator, a flue gas cyclone separator (5) and the fuel reactor. A condenser (7) is used to condense water in the flue gas. A selective catalytic reduction (SCR) denitration device (8) is used to remove nitrogen oxides in the flue gas. A carbon dioxide trap (11) is configured for collecting carbon dioxide in the flue gas. The oxidized oxygen carrier passes through the air cyclone, and enters the U phase valve of the air reactor and the fuel reactor to perform a cyclic reaction. A wet flue gas desulfurizer (10) is also connected to the fuel reactor, and is used to deliver carbon dioxide to the fuel reactor.
   USE - Coal chemical chain combustion mercury removal device.
   ADVANTAGE - The flue gas is effectively separated from the fly ash through the significant difference in density, own gravity and flow rate of the flue gas and the fly ash. The oxygen carrier is easy to obtain and low-cost general coal ash, which effectively reduces cost. The oxygen carrier is easily re-oxidized by entering the air reactor and the combustion reactor. The removal device is ensured that the oxygen carrier maintain the oxidizability under the high temperature condition of the combustion reactor to oxidize the elemental mercury in the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a coal chemical chain combustion mercury removal method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of coal chemical chain combustion mercury removal device.
   Fuel reactor (1)
   Fuel reactor U-phase valve (2)
   Air reactor (3)
   Air cyclone separator (4)

   Air reactor U-phase valve (6)
   Condenser (7)
   SCR denitration device (8)
   Wet flue gas desulfurizer (10)
   Carbon dioxide trap (11)
   Coal chemical chain combustion mercury removal device (100)

PN US2019113488-A1; WO2019073440-A1; US10955394-B2
TI Method for identifying the failure of a restrictor            used within the fluid flow path of a chromatography            system, involves measuring a first flow rate of a            carbon dioxide based mobile phase through a component            of the chromatography system.
AU FOGWILL M O
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 2019345541
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring (102) a first                flow rate of a carbon dioxide based mobile phase                through a component of the chromatography system.                The measured first flow rate is compared (104) to a                specified threshold flow rate to determine a                condition of the restrictor, the threshold flow                rate being computed based on a restrictor flow rate                that is associated with a failure of the                restrictor. The signal is generated (106) using a                processing unit based on the condition of the                restrictor, where the signal includes instructions                to replace the restrictor in response to the                condition of the restrictor being indicated as a                failure.
   USE - Method for identifying the failure of a                restrictor used within the fluid flow path of a                chromatography system. Uses include but are not                limited to carbon dioxide-based chromatography                system, supercritical fluid chromatography system,                high performance liquid chromatography system,                ultra-high performance liquid chromatography, gas                chromatography system, dense gas chromatography                system, solvating gas chromatography separation                system, and supercritical fluid extraction                system.
   ADVANTAGE - Employing an additional pressure regulation                device or pressure control element in a                chromatography system allows for efficient                full-flow introduction of the mobile phase stream                to a low-pressure detector when using a back                pressure regulator. The secondary pressure control                device ensures mobile phase density all the way                into the detector, thus preventing phase separation                and analyte precipitation, which may occur without                a secondary pressure control device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a kit for                diagnosing the condition of a restrictor element in                a chromatography system, such as a carbon                dioxide-based chromatography system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a method for                identifying the failure of a restrictor used within                the fluid flow path of a chromatography                system.
   Measuring a first flow rate of a carbon                dioxide based mobile phase through a component of                the chromatography system (102)
   Comparing measured first flow rate to a                specified threshold flow rate to determine a                condition of the restrictor, the threshold flow                rate being computed based on a restrictor flow rate                that is associated with a failure of the restrictor                (104)
   Generating signal using a processing unit                based on the condition of the restrictor, where the                signal includes instructions to replace the                restrictor in response to the condition of the                restrictor being indicated as a failure                (106)

AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to a method for producing biodiesel fuel and can be used in fat and oil, fuel and other industries. The method for producing biodiesel fuel includes interesterifying vegetable oil with supercritical alcohol in a volume ratio of 1:10 to 1:15 at a temperature of 250 to 280C, a pressure of 15 MPa, condensing vapours of excess alcohol at a temperature of 60 to 80C, extracting the resulting reaction mixture with carbon dioxide in supercritical conditions at a temperature of 240 to 260C, a pressure of 15 MPa, cooling the resulting biodiesel mixture to a temperature of 20 to 30C, separating glycerin from the resulting biodiesel mixture in the field of centrifugal forces, separating carbon dioxide vapours from the biodiesel mixture by the gas-liquid separation method, compressing the carbon dioxide vapuors to a pressure of 15 MPa and condensing at a temperature of minus 40C by regenerative heat exchange with boiling ammonia produced in an absorption water-ammonia refrigeration unit, boiling the water-ammonia solution at a temperature of 130C, condensing ammonia vapours at a temperature of 40C, throttling the condensed ammonia and boiling at a temperature of minus 45C, absorbing vapours of the boiling ammonia with a weak water-ammonia solution at a temperature of 35C, heating the water before the vapour generator with spent superheated steam after the boiler to a temperature of 90C. The invention is aimed at increasing the energy efficiency and environmental safety of the method for producing biodiesel fuel from vegetable oil with maximum regeneration and utilisation of secondary energy resources in closed thermodynamic cycles through material and heat flows using a heat pump.
   USE - Fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduced energy consumption for cooling and condensing carbon dioxide vapours per unit mass of the resulting biodiesel fuel and increased environmental safety of the method for producing biodiesel fuel in supercritical conditions.

The                liquid metal droplets in the catalysts or catalytic                systems are advantageously self-cleaning during                catalysis as a new catalytic surface is continually                generated by agitation of the droplets and the new                catalytic surface is continually presented to the                reactant(s). In this way, particularly with                reference to the reduction of carbon dioxide to                solid carbon and molecular oxygen, the catalysts or                catalytic systems are resistant to coking.                Acidifying the solvent may advantageously reduce                any oxidation of the liquid metal surface by                dissolving any metal oxides that may form on the                liquid metal droplets before or during catalysis.                An advantage of the method for catalyzing the                chemical reaction using the catalysts or catalytic                systems is that the reaction proceeds without the                application of electrical current. Optional                addition of electrical current to the catalyst or                catalytic systems may increase the conversion                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) producing catalyst or catalytic system                which involves: (a) combining a liquid metal with a                solvent; and (b) applying energy to the combination                of step (a) so as to form and disperse liquid metal                droplets in the solvent, thus forming the catalyst                or catalytic system; and
   (2) catalyzing a chemical reaction, which                involves: (a) providing the catalyst or catalytic                system; and (b) contacting the catalyst or                catalytic system with a reactant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The liquid metal                  comprises one or more metals selected from                  mercury, gallium (preferred), indium, bismuth,                  lead, cadmium and tin. It further comprises a                  catalytic element selected from silver, nickel,                  palladium, platinum, gold, silver, ruthenium,                  rhodium, iridium and cerium or salt. The liquid                  metal comprises an alloy of gallium, indium and                  tin. It consists of an alloy of gallium and                  silver. The catalyst or catalytic system further                  comprises a co-contributor. Preferred                  Composition: The liquid metal comprises galinstan                  having a composition of 60-95 wt.% gallium, 5-25                  wt.% indium and 0.01-16 wt.% tin. It comprises                  70-99.9% mercury or gallium, and 0.1-30% of one                  or more further metals selected from silver,                  nickel, palladium, platinum, gold, silver,                  ruthenium, rhodium, iridium and cerium or salts.                  The liquid metal comprises 70-99.9% base alloy                  comprising two or more of gallium, indium,                  bismuth, lead, cadmium and tin, in combination                  with 0.1-30% of one or more further metals                  selected from silver, nickel, palladium,                  platinum, gold, silver, ruthenium, rhodium,                  iridium and cerium. The liquid metal comprises                  70-99.9% gallium and 0.1-30% silver. The                  acidifying agent is an inorganic acid selected                  from phosphoric acid, sulfuric acid, nitric acid,                  and hydrochloric acid, and combination. The                  acidifying agent is present in the catalyst or                  catalytic system in an amount of 0.01-10 M. The                  catalyst or catalytic system comprises 10-80                  vol.% liquid metal. Preferred Parameters: The                  liquid metal has a melting point of 0-300 degrees                  C. The liquid metal droplets have an average                  diameter of 0.1-100 mu m. The ultrasonic energy                  has a frequency of 20-100 kHz. Energy in step (b)                  is ultrasound energy. The ultrasound energy has a                  frequency of 20 kHz to 2 MHz. Preferred Method:                  The liquid metal droplets are dispersed in the                  solvent by application of ultrasonic energy.                  Energy in step (b) is applied in the form of                  mechanical agitation selected from rapid                  stirring, mixing, blending, or through use of a                  high-pressure homogenizer. Energy is applied in                  step (b) for 1-20 minutes. The reactant is carbon                  dioxide and the chemical reaction is reduction of                  carbon dioxide to yield solid carbon and oxygen                  gas; or the reactant is methane and the chemical                  reaction is reduction of methane to yield solid                  carbon and hydrogen gas. The catalyst or                  catalytic system of step (a) is produced by                  applying ultrasound energy to a combination of                  liquid metal in solvent. Contacting of step (b)                  is performed in the presence of ultrasound energy                  and at 0-300 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  selected from dimethylformamide, acetonitrile and                  water. The solvent is acidified by addition of an                  acidifying agent. The catalyst or catalytic                  system further comprises a surfactant. Preferred                  Parameters: The solvent has a boiling point of                  25-300 degrees C, or 80-180 degrees C. The                  solvent has a carbon dioxide solubility of 20-250                  g/L at 25 degrees C.

TI Heat extinguishing device for heat engineering, has stirring shaft in shell and welded with second stirring blade distributed with equal distance, where outer wall of side of shell far away from connecting pipe connected with spraying pipe.
AU LUO J
AE LUO J (LUOJ-Individual)
GA 2020C8966E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a mixing box (1) whose bottom four corners are fixed with universal wheels (2). An outer wall of a side of a bottom part of the mixing box is connected with an air inlet pipe (3). An outer wall of a top part of the mixing box is connected with a water inlet pipe (4). The air inlet pipe is connected with a gas pipe (5) that is connected with a carbon dioxide gas bottle. The water inlet pipe is connected with a water pipe that is connected with a heat hydrant. The outer wall of the stirring shaft located in the shell is welded with a second stirring blade distributed with equal distance. An outer wall of a side of the shell far away from a connecting pipe is connected with a spraying pipe.
   USE - Heat extinguishing device for use in a heat engineering.
   ADVANTAGE - The motor drives the stirring mechanism to primarily mix the carbon dioxide and water to form carbonic acid. The water and the carbonic acid mixture are conveyed to the spray gun through the conveying pipe. The carbon dioxide and water are mixed, and after being sprayed by the spraying pipe to form bubble water. The carbonic acid is not stable. The carbonic acid is quickly decomposed into water and carbon dioxide after heating by the heat source, so that the device can be used for extinguishing heat, so as to reduce the consumption of water resource and save water resource.

PN CN111687519-A
TI Method for welding ear plate of boiler header, involves cleaning welding surface of header barrel and ear plate, assembling header barrel and ear plate, and using carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to weld header barrel and ear plate.
AU SHU Y
   XU X
   LIU H
   YANG B
   HUANG P
   WANG L
   CUI Q
   ZAN B
   SUN P
AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 202095417L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cleaning the welding surface of the header barrel (2) and the ear plate (1) before welding. The header barrel and ear plate are assembled and position welded. The carbon dioxide gas shielded welding is used to weld the header barrel and ear plate. The workbench is connected with the ground wire, and the carbon dioxide gas cylinder is opened. The welding machine is turned on. The power supply of the welding machine is 200-220 amps, and the voltage is 22-26 volts. The workpiece is placed in the flat fillet welding position, multi-layer and multi-pass welding is used, and the workpiece is placed in the vertical welding position during the cover welding. The welding wire moves at the upper groove of the header barrel and the ear plate, and pauses a little when moving to complete the welding process of the header ear plate.
   USE - Method for welding ear plate of boiler header.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to weld the ear plate of the boiler header, the welding time is short, the work efficiency is increased by 2 to 3 times and the cost is reduced. The spatter is small and the penetration depth is large, avoids the deformation of the product after welding and ensures the welding quality. The process performance and mechanical performance of the product meet the standard requirements, and the weld seam is beautifully formed. The method is simple to operate and convenient to use.

PN EP3670701-A1; WO2020127821-A1; CA3123971-A1; US2022056602-A1; JP2022515169-W; EP3670701-B1
TI Electrode for converting carbon dioxide into            carbon monoxide, comprises an electrically conductive            support of which at least a portion of the surface is            covered by a metal deposit of zinc and silver.
AU FONTECAVE M
   MOUGEL V
   WAKERLEY D
   LAMAISON S
AE PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES (PARS-Non-standard)
   PARIS SCI & LETTRES-QUARTIER LATIN (PARS-Non-standard)
   COLLEGE FRANCE (COLL-Non-standard)
GA 2020561407
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode comprises an electrically conductive                support of which at least a portion of the surface                is covered by a metal deposit of zinc and silver,                where metal deposit has a specific surface area                greater than or equal to 0.1 m 2.g-1, greater than                or equal to 0.5 m 2.g-1, for example greater than                or equal to 0.7 m 2.g -1, such as greater than or                equal to 1 m 2.g-1.
   USE - Electrode for converting carbon dioxide into                carbon monoxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing an electrode, which                involves providing an electrically conductive                support, immersing electrically conductive support                at least partially in an acidic aqueous solution                containing ions of zinc and ions of silver and                applying a current or a potential between the                electrically conductive support and a second                electrode in order to form a metal deposit of zinc                and silver on the electrically conductive support,                so as to have a current density equal to or less                than -0.1 A.cm-2, between -10 A.cm-2 and -0.1                A.cm-2, preferably between -5 A.cm-2 and -0.1                A.cm-2 between the electrically conductive support                and a second electrode;
   (2) an electrolysis device, which comprises an                electrode; and
   (3) a method for converting carbon dioxide                (CO2) into carbon monoxide, which involves                providing an electrolysis device comprising an                anode and a cathode, where cathode is an electrode,                exposing the cathode of electrolysis device to a                gaseous or liquid CO2-containing composition,                applying an electrical current or a potential                between the anode and the cathode in order to                reduce the carbon dioxide into carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Condition: The electrically                  conductive support comprises an electrically                  conductive material selected from a metal such as                  copper, steel, aluminum, zinc, silver, gold,                  iron, nickel or titanium; a metal oxide such as                  fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO), antimony-doped                  tin oxide (ATO) or indium-doped tin oxide (ITO);                  a metal sulfide such as copper indium gallium                  sulfide, cadmium sulfide or zinc sulfide; carbon                  in particular in the form of carbon felt,                  graphite, vitreous carbon, carbon nanofibers,                  carbon nanotubes, carbon black, boron-doped                  diamond, any form of gas diffusion layer (GDL)                  with or without microporous layer; a polymer                  intrinsically electrically conductive or made                  conductive by a coating with a film of conductive                  material such as metal, semi-conductor or                  conductive polymer; a semiconductor such as                  silicon which can be for example amorphous                  silicon or crystalline silicon, lead halide                  perovskite or tin halide perovskite; and a                  mixture; and optionally where the electrode has                  been submitted to one or multiple additional                  treatments, in particular to modify its                  conductivity such as treatment with carbon-based                  materials such as carbon nanofibers, carbon                  nanotubes, carbon black, graphite, boron-doped                  diamond powder or a combination; its                  hydrophobicity such as treatment with                  polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE); and/or its                  ionophilicity such as treatment with an ionomer                  such as an anion exchange polymer, a polyaromatic                  polymer, polybenzimidazole (PBI) or a mixture.                  The metal deposit has a specific surface area                  between 0.1 and 500 m 2.g-1, between 0.5 and 200                  m 2.g-1, in particular between 1 and 100 m 2.g-1,                  preferably between 1 and 50 m 2.g-1, for example                  between 1 and 30 m 2.g-1, such as between 1 and                  25 m 2.g-1. The metal deposit comprises at least                  1 wt.%, such as at least 5 wt.%, at least 10                  wt.%, for example at least 20 wt.%, such as at                  least 30 wt.% of one or multiple phases of an                  alloy of zinc and silver, such as a phase Ag                  0.13Zn 0.87. The metal deposit has a thickness                  comprised between 1 mu m and 500 mu m, for                  example between 1 mu m and 300 mu m, such as                  between 1 mu m and 280 mu m, preferably between 5                  mu m and 270 mu m. The metal deposit has a porous                  structure with an average pore size of between 1                  mu m and 500 mu m, in particular between 1 mu m                  and 200 mu m, preferably between 20 mu m and 100                  mu m. The acidic aqueous solution containing ions                  of zinc and ions of silver is an acidic aqueous                  solution containing a salt of zinc such as zinc                  sulfate, zinc chloride, zincperchlorate, zinc                  nitrate, zinc bromide, or zinc phosphate; an                  oxidized zinc species such as Zinc oxide; a Zinc                  hydroxide- -based salt; a zincate -based salt; a                  ZnO2 2- -based salt; or a mixture; in particular                  it can be Zinc sulfate, a salt of silver such as                  silver chloride, silver nitrate, silver chlorate,                  silver carbonate, silver phosphate, silver                  perchlorate, silver sulfate, agf, silver nitrite;                  an oxidized species of silver such as silver                  oxide, AgOH; or a mixture; in particular it can                  be silver nitrate. The metal deposit of zinc and                  silver is removed from the electrically                  conductive support and applied on a second                  electrically conductive support. The electrolysis                  device is coupled to a source of an electrical                  energy, in particular an intermittent source of                  renewable energy such as a photovoltaic panel or                  a wind turbine. The method is performed under a                  CO2 pressure of from 100 to 100000 kPa, from 100                  to 50000 kPa, such as from 100 to 20000 kPa, for                  example from 100 to 10000 kPa, from 100 to 8000                  kPa, such as from 100 to 6000 kPa, for example                  from 100 to 5000 kPa, for example from 100 to                  1000 kPa. The method is performed at a                  temperature from 10 to 100 degrees C, from 20 to                  100 degrees C, such as from 50 to 80 degrees C.                  The gaseous or liquid CO2-containing composition                  is a CO2-containing aqueous catholyte solution or                  a gaseous CO2-containing composition.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for producing electricity in a molten            carbonate fuel cell, by operating molten carbonate fuel            cell at prescribed transference to generate            electricity, anode exhaust, and cathode exhaust.
AU ROSEN J
   BARCKHOLTZ T A
   ELSEN H A
   KISS G
   HAN L
   SMITH T M
   DAS S K
   YUH C
   WILLMAN C A
   GEARY T C
   DAVIS K E
   HILMI A
   NOVACCO L J
   SMITHMASM
   PANCAKE D S
   MOSHI G
   KISSY D
   ABDELKADER H M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202048921Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an anode input                stream comprising hydrogen and/or a reformable fuel                into an anode gas collection zone defined by an                anode surface, a first separator plate, and an                anode collector in-between. A cathode input stream                comprising oxygen and carbon dioxide is introduced                into a cathode gas collection zone defined by a                cathode surface (730), a second separator plate,                and a cathode collector in-between. The molten                carbonate fuel cell is operated at a transference                of 0.97 or less and an average current density of                60 mA/cm2 or more to generate electricity, an anode                exhaust comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and                carbon dioxide, and a cathode exhaust comprising                2.0 vol.% or less carbon dioxide, 1.0 vol.% or more                oxygen, and 1.0 vol.% or more water. An average                cathode gas lateral diffusion length is 0.40 mm or                less, and/or an open area of the cathode surface                comprises 45% or more of a total surface area of                the cathode surface.
   USE - Method for producing electricity in a molten                carbonate fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - When operating a molten carbonate fuel cell at                conditions that result in substantial transference,                the transference can be increased so that the                amount of alternative ion transport that occurs                within the fuel cell is reduced or minimized.                Allows elevated transfer of carbon dioxide to occur                from cathode to anode while reducing or minimizing                the amount of fuel cell degradation due to the                transport of alternative ions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                molten carbonate fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view which                shows a cathode collector configuration with the                loop structures making contact with the cathode                surface.
   Cathode collector surface (110)
   Loop structures (120)
   Bottom edge of loop structures (122)
   Cathode surface (730)
   Open mesh screen (750)

TI Bunker coal plant carbon dioxide online monitoring system, has measuring instrument, non-dispersive infrared absorption process tester, electromagnetic valve and alarm electrically connected with controller by signal line.
AU XUE F
   CHEN F
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 2019A5734U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a sampling device (1) fixedly connected to a dust filtering device (3) through a sample transfer line (2). The dust filtering device is fixedly connected with a dehumidifying device (4) through the sample transfer line. The dehumidifying device is fixedly connected with a first non-dispersive infrared absorption process tester (5) through the sample transfer line. The dehumidifying device and the first non-dispersive infrared absorption process tester between the sample transfer lines is fixedly connected with a sample transfer line branch pipe. The sampling device, the dust filtering device, the dehumidifying device, the first non-dispersive infrared absorption process tester, a flow meter (6), a data processor (7), a zero calibrator (8), a measuring instrument (9), a second non-dispersive infrared absorption process tester (10), an electromagnetic valve (12) and alarm are electrically connected with a controller i.e. PLC controller by a signal line.
   USE - Bunker coal plant carbon dioxide online monitoring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is scientific and reasonable in structure, and simple and convenient in operation, and has high detecting precision, and wide range of applications, and performs online sampling monitoring flue gas in power plant in real-time manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a bunker coal plant carbon dioxide online monitoring system working method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a bunker coal plant carbon dioxide online monitoring system.

AB    NOVELTY - Carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid/MIL-101 composite catalyst, prepared by using a metal organic framework material MIL-101 as carrier and carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid 1-propionic acid-3-methylimidazolium chloride as an active component, at the same time as the MIL-101 carrier, in situ immobilizing the carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid in the skeleton structure of the MIL-101 carrier, where using 0.15-1.5 mmol of a carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid is immobilized per 1 g of the composite catalyst, is claimed.
   USE - The composite catalyst is useful for reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxy compound to form a cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst: can efficiently convert and convert carbon dioxide into a cyclic carbonate under mild conditions including low temperature and low pressure and without a co-catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for
   (1) preparing carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid/MIL-101 composite catalyst comprising firstly heating by N-methylimidazole and 3-chloropropionic acid in an anhydrous toluene solvent system under nitrogen protection, preparing carboxy functionalized imidazolium ionic liquid 1-propionic acid-3-methylimidazolium chloride by refluxing reaction, and then dissolving the 1-propionic acid-3-methylimidazolium chloride and chromium nitrate nonahydrate in deionized water, and adding terephthalic acid, in closed hydrothermal reaction to prepare the carboxy functional imidazole ionic liquid /MIL-101 composite catalyst; and
   (2) synthesizing a cyclic carbonate by using carbon dioxide and epoxy compound comprising using epoxy compound, and carbon dioxide is introducing in the presence of the composite, at reaction pressure of normal pressure of 0.7 MPa, and a reaction temperature at 25-50 degrees C, The cyclo addition reaction was carried out to synthesize cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mol ratio of N-methylimidazole to the 3-chloropropionic acid is 1-1.5:1. The mol ratio of ninehydrated chromium nitrate and terephthalic acid is 1-3:1. The mol ratio of carboxy functional imidazole ionic liquid and chromium nitrate nonahydrate is 1.25-8:1. Preferred Method: The N-methyl imidazole and 3-chloropropoonic in anhydrous toluene solvent system at 80-110 degrees C for refluxing reacting for 5-24 hours, separating the lower white oily matter, washing with ethyl acetate, vacuum drying to obtain carboxy functional imidazole ionic liquid 1-propionic acid-3-methyl imidazole chloride. The hydrothermal reaction is performing under the condition of 180-220 degrees C, for 8-12 hours. The cycloaddition time period is 12-40 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves charging an energy storage                with the liquid air, which is produced through the                consumption of the excessive power from the                co-located renewable energy sources from a grid.                The energy storage is discharged with an on-demand                for producing and delivering the power to the grid                through the pumping and the re-gasifying of the                stored air. The energy storage is recovered in a                multi-stage expander train (300), where the                combustion air is provided for a fueled                supercharged internal combustion engine. The cold                thermal energy of the discharged liquid air is                recovered, where the discharged liquid air is                regasified for the cryogenic cooling of a liquid                air energy storage facility exhaust. The unit of                carbon dioxide emissions formed by the combustion                of the fuel is liquefied in the liquid air energy                storage facility.
   USE - Method for the liquid air energy storage with                a semi-closed carbon dioxide bottoming cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy storage with the liquid air, which                is produced through the consumption of the                excessive power from the co-located renewable                energy sources from a grid, and hence ensures                providing the simple and efficient recovery of the                engine exhaust energy and maximizes the power                output and the round-trip efficiency of the energy                storage, and also significantly suppresses the                formation of the thermal nitrogen oxide                emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a power                block of a liquid air energy storage facility for                implementing a discharge operation mode with a                semi-closed carbon dioxide bottoming cycle.

   Hot recuperator (301)
   High-pressure expander (302)
   Electric generator (303)
   Bottoming cycle package (500)

An internal air of the living space and the reversible absorbing the carbon dioxide contained among the air circulating within space are circulated. The water and oxygen are made to react with the hydrogen peroxide at a water flooding injury. The absorbed carbon dioxide is collected and ejected. The generated oxygen is mixed to the sterilized air. The air circulated by using the hydrogen peroxide and water flooding injury reaction is sterilized to produce the scavenging air.
   USE - Air condition method for tourist underwater life space, limited space, closed work space, atmospheric environment facility purification, earthquake, land, or marine accident, carbon dioxide depositing and application technology, submarine, military shelter shunt equipment, nucleus or chemical warfare blockage facility, and space alive facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purification is possible with the presence of the peroxide which is stabilized in moisture. The air purification, oxygen and fresh-water supply are simultaneously possible with the presence of the peroxide which is stabilized in moisture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing the scavenging air at the air interception space;
   (2) an air cleaner;
   (3) a system for producing the scavenging air at the air interception space; and
   (4) a submarine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the sytem for producing the scavenging air at the air interception space.
   Living space (100)
   Source supplying unit (200)
   Water flooding injury reactive unit (300)
   Carbon dioxide unit (500)
   Cooling unit (700)

PN CN108330253-A
TI Manufacture of ultra-low carbon steel involves            vacuum-treating steel containing preset carbon content,            closing vacuum valve of vacuum system, switching bottom            gas of ladle from carbon dioxide gas to argon gas, and            processing.

   ZHOU H
   ZHANG D
AE BEIJING SHOUGANG INT ENG TECHNOLOGY CO (SHGG-C)
GA 201861191M
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of ultra-low carbon steel involves                vacuumizing, automatically switching bottom gas of                ladle from argon to carbon dioxide gas, vacuum                refining, carrying out natural decarburization and                forced decarburization of molten steel, performing                dehydrogenation, deoxidation, desulfurization and                de-intercalation of molten steel, controlling                bottom blowing gas flow rate in pre-refining period                at 3-4 L/t.minute, maintaining for 10-15 minutes,                automatically adjusting bottom blowing flow rate to                5-6 L/t.minute during the refining period,                re-refining for 15-20 minutes, vacuum-treating                steel containing carbon content of less than                0.005%, closing vacuum valve of vacuum system,                automatically switching bottom gas of ladle from                carbon dioxide gas to argon gas, controlling bottom                blowing gas flow rate as 3-4 L/tBminute, opening                vacuum tank cover, performing feeding operation,                disassembling connecting hose on ladle, and lifting                treated molten steel to continuous casting                machine.
   USE - Manufacture of ultra-low carbon steel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon content is controlled at                0.04-0.06%. The bottom blowing flow rate is                controlled at 3-4 L/t minute. The vacuum refining                is carried out at 66.7 Pa. The carbon dioxide gas                flow rate during vacuum refining adopts                bottom-blowing gas supply system with low first and                high height. The vacuumizing process is carried out                by tapping converter, controlling carbon content,                shifting ladle into vacuum tank of vacuum degasser,                manually connecting ladle bottom blowing hose in                vacuum tank to bottom blowing pipe on ladle,                opening bottom blow control valve, introducing                argon at bottom of ladle, controlling bottom                blowing flow rate, covering vacuum tank cover and                opening vacuum valve.

PT P
PN US2022134282-A1
TI Capturing and mineralizing hybrid carbon comprises reacting flue gas with amine solution to produce carbamate, processing carbamate to regenerate amine, treating reject brine to provide ready-made brine, and processing carbamate with salt.
AU SINOPOLI A
   ABOTALEB A
AE QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 202261485K
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing and mineralizing hybrid carbon comprises providing flue gas to react with an amine solution to produce carbamate, processing the carbamate to regenerate amine and to produce carbonates, treating reject brine to provide ready-made brine for carbonation reaction, and then processing the carbamate with salt from treating reject brine to produce a carbonate.
   USE - Method for capturing and mineralizing hybrid carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the effects of global warming to slow the increase in atmospheric carbon dioxide levels as a result of fossil fuel combustion for energy production and transportation by using both flue gas from power plants and rejected brine from the desalination process in a fast reaction time and increased kinetics for carbon dioxide absorption without using an alkaline source for precipitation and a stripper column in the carbon dioxide capture process to lower capex and shorter payback period, chemically regenerates the reusable amine solution, and eliminates any thermal energy requirements for amine solvent regeneration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of the method for capturing and mineralizing hybrid carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A makeup amine is provided to make up any amine loss during the process. The solid carbonate is precipitated from the liquid phase or amine solution. The solid carbonate is washed with an amine solution and dried the washed solid carbonate to produce an anhydride carbonate. The carbonate product comprises an alkaline-earth metal of group #2 (calcium, magnesium, barium, beryllium, strontium, and radium) and a carbonate ion. The carbonate comprises an alkali metal of group #1 (lithium, sodium, potassium, rubidium, cesium, and francium) and a carbonate ion. The carbonate comprises a transition metal (lead, iron, copper and cadmium) and a carbonate ion. The carbonate is calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, sodium carbonate, and barium carbonate. The purging is done with purity 4N carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The entrainer is an organic family such as acetone, ethanol, methanol, and isopropanol.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2022040300-A1
TI Preparing container with zero headspace comprising            aqueous solution by filling container via open end with            degassed aqueous solution, purging container with            carbon dioxide, and inserting stopper into open end of            container.
AU MANNING M
   PRADO I
   BEGLEY P D
   JI W
   LIU J
   OGEZ J
AE COHERUS BIOSCIENCES INC (COHE-Non-standard)
GA 202221976F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a container with zero headspace                comprising an aqueous solution, involves: (a)                providing a container with an open end; (b)                providing a degassed aqueous solution that is free                of a polyol, a sugar, and a surfactant; (c) filling                the container via the open end with the degassed                aqueous solution; (d) purging the container with                carbon dioxide; (e) inserting a stopper into the                open end of the container creating a headspace of                carbon dioxide; and (f) storing the container at                about less than 10degrees Celsius for at least                about 18 hours. The stopper can move within the                container while maintaining an airtight seal; and                the storing step results in the container having                zero headspace.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a container                with zero headspace comprising aqueous solution                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods can reduce or eliminate the                headspace in containers for pharmaceutical use such                as pre-filled syringes and cartridges. The methods                enable the manufacture of drug products in                containers without a headspace. The result is a                stable drug product where the use of surfactants                and other stabilizing excipients is reduced or                eliminated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: Prior to the purging step                  or prior to inserting the stopper, the container                  is subjected to a vacuum. The vacuum applies a                  pressure to the container of at least 27.5 inHg.                  Purging the container with carbon dioxide                  involves providing carbon dioxide to the                  container at a flow rate of about 10                  milliliters/minute for 0.5 minute. The container                  is stored for at least about 2 days; or the                  container is stored at about less than 10degrees                  Celsius for at least about 3 days; or the                  container is stored at about less than 10degrees                  Celsius for at least about 4 days. The container                  is stored at about 4degrees Celsius. The stopper                  is coated with silicone oil. It is coated with                  about 0.4 mg of silicone oil.Preferred Composition: Following the storing                  step, the aqueous adalimumab solution comprises                  less than about 6000 subvisible particles having                  a size of at least 10 mum per 0.8 ml of the                  aqueous adalimumab solution and less than about                  600 subvisible particles having a size of at                  least 25 mum per 0.8 ml of the aqueous adalimumab                  solution.Preferred Components: The container is a                  syringe or a cartridge. The aqueous solution is                  further free or substantially free of a buffer, a                  salt, a stabilizer, or any combination.

PN GB2597880-A; GB2597880-B
TI Method for enhanced oil recovery from oil reservoir by carbon dioxide flooding, involves producing well stream from reservoir, and separating well stream into liquid phase and gas phase with first gas/liquid separator.
AU KARSTAD P I
   NEERAAS B O
   MARAK K A
   LEVY T
   KRISTIANSEN O
   FIVELAND T
   DRESCHER M
   MARaK K A
AE EQUINOR ENERGY AS (DENO-C)
GA 202221863D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves producing a well stream (4) from a reservoir. The well stream is separated into a liquid phase and a gas phase with a first gas/liquid separator. The gas phase comprises both carbon dioxide (CO2) gas and hydrocarbon gas. The gas phase is cooled with a first cooler. The gas phase is compressed by using a first compressor into a compressed stream. The compressed stream is mixed with an external source of CO2 to form an injection stream. The injection stream is cooled with a second cooler. The injection stream is injected into the reservoir.
   USE - Method for Enhanced Oil Recovery (EOR) from an oil reservoir by carbon dioxide (CO2) flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - An ejector is powered by motive gas flow from downstream the first compressor to simplify the process, and removes the need for the third cooler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an enhanced oil recovery system with a producer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an EOR system.
   Production piping (3)
   Well stream (4)
   Pressure (5)
   Compressed gas (11)
   EOR system (40)

PN CN113979986-A
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate involves reacting            epoxide compound and carbon dioxide under catalysis of            sulfonamide bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt            catalyst compound to obtain cyclic carbonate.
AU LIU B
   GAO L
   HU Y
   SUN J
   LI Z
   LV Z
   HE J
   ZHANG B
   GUO K
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2022236096
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate involves reacting                epoxide compound (I) and carbon dioxide under                catalysis of sulfonamide bifunctional quaternary                ammonium salt catalyst compound (II) to obtain                cyclic carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cyclic carbonate                by using sulfonamide bifunctional quaternary                ammonium salt catalyst compound, which has simple                catalytic system, higher catalytic activity, good                product selectivity, mild and safe catalytic                reaction conditions, yield of more than 90% under                the atmospheric pressure and room temperature                conditions, low reaction condition requirement, no                metal residue in the product obtained after the                catalysis, and reusability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate involves reacting                epoxide compound of formula (I) and carbon dioxide                under catalysis of sulfonamide bifunctional                quaternary ammonium salt catalyst compound of                formula (II) to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   R1=H, methyl, methoxy, trifluoromethyl;
   R2=methyl, isopropyl, phenyl, benzyl;
   R3=phenyl, p-tolyl, ethyl, phenylnitro,                benzyloxy; X=chlorine, bromine or iodine; and
   R4, R5=H, alkyl with 1-4C vinyl, phenyl,                halogen, hydroxyl, phenol, vinyl, allyloxy or alkyl                having 1-4C substituted by alkoxy having 1-4C,                tert-butyl, allyloxymethyl, halophenyl, where R4                and R5 are same or different substituent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfonamide                  bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt catalyst                  compound (II) is selected from compounds of                  formulae (III-VII). The epoxide compound (I) is                  selected from compounds of formulae (VIII-XII).                  The acid binding agent is selected from                  triethylamine, sodium carbonate or potassium                  carbonate. The organic solvent is selected from                  acetonitrile, dichloromethane or                  N,N-dimethylformamide. Preferred Process: The                  preparation method of cyclic carbonate                  specifically involves adding sulfonamide                  bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt catalyst                  compound (II) and epoxide compound (I) to                  reaction vessel, using carbon dioxide to replace                  the air in reaction vessel, taking in the                  reaction vessel, filling carbon dioxide to                  initial pressure to be 0.1-2.0 megapascal,                  warming up to 20-140degrees Celsius, reacting for                  1-24 hours, and cooling to obtain cyclic                  carbonate. The preparation method of the                  sulfonamide bifunctional quaternary ammonium salt                  catalyst compound (II) involves adding acid                  binding agent and amino alcohol compound (XIII)                  and sulfonyl chloride compound (XIV) in organic                  solvent to obtain sulfonyl aziridine crude                  product, when the catalyst is DMAP                  (4-dimethylaminopyridine) or the acid binding                  agent is potassium carbonate, the catalyst is not                  used, when R2 is methyl or isopropyl, after the                  reaction is finished, adding saturated brine to                  wash, using ethyl acetate to extract, where R2 is                  phenyl or benzyl, adding toluene, performing                  suction filtration to obtain the crude product,                  obtaining product after purification by column                  chromatography, performing in the organic                  solvent, adding the obtained sulfonylaziridine,                  the catalyst Yb(OTF)3 (ytterbium triflate) after                  the addition, adding pyridine or a pyridine                  derivative to carry out ring-opening reaction to                  obtain the product, adding hydrogen halide to the                  obtained product to carry out salt-forming                  reaction, and washing with acetone/ether to                  obtain the sulfonamide bifunctional quaternary                  ammonium salt catalyst compound (II).R6=methyl, isopropyl, phenyl, benzyl;                  andR7=phenyl, p-tolyl, ethyl, phenylnitro,                  benzyloxy.
   Preferred Definitions: R1=H;
   R2=benzyl;
   R3=p-tolyl; and
   R4, R5=H, alkyl group with 1-4C carbon atom,                vinyl group, phenyl group, is replaced by halogen,                phenol group, allyloxy or alkoxy with 1-4C                substituted alkyl with 1-4C, and halophenyl.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide thickening agent for medium and high permeability oil reservoir prepared by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of first monomer, second monomer and third monomer is 5-20:10-50:2-10. The weight ratio of first intermediate and propane sultone is 10-15:3-8. The weight ratio of fourth monomer and acid chloride is 7-15:3-7. The weight ratio of second intermediate and third intermediate is 30-35:25-30. The first solvent is water, methanol and/or ethanol. The second solvent is ethanol, isopropanol and/or acetone. The third solvent is dichloromethane, tetrahydrofuran and/or pyridine. The third solvent is hexane, benzene, toluene, xylene, ether and/or acetone. The first monomer is ethyl 4-pentenoate. The second monomer is dibutylamine. The third monomer is formaldehyde. The fourth monomer is 3-phenyl-4-penten-1-amine.Preferred Method: The method further comprises adding first monomer, second monomer and third monomer to the first solvent, then adding the catalyst, adjusting the pH to 4-6, heating to 70-100degrees Celsius, and reacting for 5-7 hours to obtain the first intermediate. The method further comprises adding first intermediate and propane sultone into second solvent, and reacting at 70-120degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours to obtain the second intermediate, and adding fourth monomer and the acid chloride into the third solvent, and reacting at 0-25degrees Celsius for 3-10 hours to obtain third intermediate, adding second intermediate, third intermediate and initiator into the third solvent, heating to 50-90degrees Celsius and reacting for 1-5 hours to obtain thickening agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is zinc fluoroborate, zirconium oxide, aluminum chloride, niobium(V) chloride and/or indium(III) chloride. The acid chloride is propionyl chloride, butyryl chloride, valeryl chloride and/or hexanoyl chloride. The permeability of medium and high permeability oil reservoir is not less than 100 microns2.

PT P
PN CN113145141-A
TI Cesium lead bromide quantum dot/nano copper cobaltite composite photocatalyst useful for carbon dioxide reduction, comprises cesium lead bromide quantum dots and nano copper cobaltite.
AU LIU Y
   MIAO G
   CHEN W
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 202191551M
AB    NOVELTY - Cesium lead bromide quantum dot/nano copper cobaltite composite photocatalyst, comprises cesium lead bromide quantum dots and nano copper cobaltite with a ratio of 0.2-2:4-40 (mmol:mg).
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst realizes the bonding of the surface atom layer. The method has simple technique, good repeatability, low cost and meets the environment requirement.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing cesium lead bromide quantum dot/nano copper cobaltite composite photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, comprising (a) mixing cesium lead bromide quantum dot and nano copper cobaltite dispersed in 0.5-5 ml normal hexane in a ratio of 0.2-2:4-40 (mmol:mg), ultrasonically dispersing until mixing uniform, continuously performing continuous ultrasonic treatment, and maintaining the temperature not greater than 30 degrees C: (b) centrifugally separating the product, leaving the precipitate, washing the precipitate with 0.5-5 ml normal hexane, and repeatedly washing the precipitate for multiple times: and (c) adding the product to 0.5-5 ml normal hexane or anhydrous ethanol, ultrasonically dispersing, and fully drying under vacuum condition to obtain photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is a composite material formed by modifying zero-dimensional spherical particles on the surface of a two-dimensional sheet material. The cesium lead bromide quantum dot is the zero-dimensional nano-particle. The nano copper cobaltite is the two-dimensional nano-sheet. Preferred Method: The preparation of the nano copper cobaltite comprises (i) dissolving 0.1-1 mmol cupric nitrate hexahydrate, 0.2-2 mmol cobalt (II) nitrate hexahydrate, 0.5-5 mmol urea and 0.2-2 mmol ammonium fluoride in 30-60 ml ethanol, uniformly stirring at room temperature for 10-60 minutes and drying at 140-200 degrees C for 8-16 hours; (ii) alternately washing product with distilled water and ethanol for multiple times and drying at 60-100 degrees C for 8-12 hours; and (iii) fully calcining the product at 300-450 degrees C at a heating rate of 2-5 degrees C/minute for 2-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, fully grinding for 10-60 minutes, adding 1-10 ml normal hexane, and ultrasonically dispersing at ultrasonic power of 500-2500 W for 5-30 minutes. The preparation of the cesium lead bromide quantum dot comprises (1) adding lead bromide into 10-30 ml octadecene, introducing argon protective gas, raising the temperature to 90-120 degrees C, adding oleic acid and oleylamine separately, and completely dissolving metal salt to obtain complex solution of metal salt, where the ratio of lead bromide, cesium acetate, oleic acid and oleylamine is 1:0.1-1:1-10:1-10 (mmol:mmol:ml:ml); and (2) heating the complex solution of the metal salt to 150-180 degrees C, adding oleic acid solution of cesium acetate, completely reacting, cooling to room temperature, and cleaning by adding to tertiary butanol and normal hexane to obtain cesium lead bromide quantum dot. In the step (a), the ultrasonic dispersion is carried out at the ultrasonic power of 500-2500 W for 5-30 minutes. In the step (b), the centrifugation is carried out at a rotating speed of 2000-4000 revolutions/minute for 1-5 minutes. In the step (c), the ultrasonic dispersion is carried out at the ultrasonic power of 500-2500 W for 5-30 minutes, and drying at 60-100 degrees C for 8-15 hours. In the step (b), the washing the precipitate comprises adding 0.5-5 ml normal hexane into the precipitate, ultrasonically processing with power 500-2500 W for 5-30 minutes, fully dispersing the precipitate, and centrifuging a rotating speed of 2000-4000 revolutions/minute for 3 minutes to separate the precipitate. In the step (b), the washing the precipitate is repeated for 1-3 times.

PT P
PN CN112551484-A
TI Artificial photosynthesis processing method by supercapacitors as registers, comprises catalyzing decomposition of water, imitating electron transfer and photosynthetic phosphorylation and reducing carbon dioxide reduction.
AU ZHANG L
   ZHANG Y
   SONG Q
   JI Y
   MA Z
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 2021342199
AB    NOVELTY - Artificial photosynthesis processing method by supercapacitors as registers, comprises (a) performing absorption, transmission and conversion of light energy, taking titania/ferric oxide composite film catalyst, and catalyzing decomposition of water, light radiates on the semiconductor, when the radiated energy is more than or equal to the band gap of the semiconductor in the semiconductor, the electrons are excited to transition from the valence band to the conduction band, while the holes stay in the valence band, seperating the electrons and holes, and reducing water to hydrogen at different positions of the semiconductor, combining with the reduction of carbon dioxide, oxidize water to oxygen, and (b) forming a micro supercapacitor device that can store electrons and light conversion energy for short time as interface medium for coordinating the catalytic reaction of water splitting and reduction of carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as artificial photosynthesis processing method using supercapacitors as registers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method establishes biosynthetic system that comprises energy collection, conversion, and transportation has been established to facilitate the innovation and development of green energy-related fields e.g. solar energy, materials science, nanotechnology, and photonics.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method further comprises, (c) migrating the photogenerated electrons and holes to the surface, driving different half-reactions respectively, reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid, methanol or other small molecular organics, and converting active chemical energy into stable chemical energy, and (d) going back to step (a), and oxidizing water to oxygen.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
