   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202043485G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving production of oil and                fat, involves (i) combining microalgae culture                medium with Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 and/or                Scenedesmus obliqnus FSH-Y2 seed solution, adding                to a photoreactor, adjusting pH to 10-12 with a                continuous light intensity of 8000-20000 Lux, and                passing gas with carbon dioxide content of 1-5                vol.% to incubate for a certain time, and (ii)                inserting seed solution of Monoraphidium sp. SHJ-02                and/or Scenedesmus sp. HCS-02, introducing gas with                a carbon dioxide content of 5-45 vol.%, controlling                the pH value to 8-10, reducing the light intensity                to 1000-5000 Lux, changing to light-dark                alternating reaction, where the light-dark cycle is                0.5-5 seconds and the light-dark time ratio is                1:(1-5), culturing to stable period and harvesting                microalgae cells.
   USE - The method is useful for improving production                of oil and fat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the tolerance and                solubility of the microalgae culture system to                high-concentration carbon dioxide, and increases                the biomass and oil content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae culture                  medium adopts liquid culture medium for culturing                  microalgae with BG11 (blue-green medium) , SE and                  Bold's basal medium (BBM). Preferred Composition:                  The volume ratio of P.kessleri FSH-Y3 and/or                  S.obliqnus FSH-Y2 and microalgae culture medium                  added in the reactor is 1:20-1:10, and when two                  kinds of microalgae are contained at the same                  time, the volume ratio of P.kessleri FSH-Y3 and                  S.obliqnus FSH-Y2 seed solution is 1:1-1:5. The                  volume ratio of the seed solution of                  Monoraphidium sp. SHJ-02 and/or seed solution of                  Scenedesmus sp. HCS-02 and the microalgae culture                  medium added in the reactor is 1:20-1:10, and                  when two kinds of microalgae are contained at the                  same time, the volume ratio of Monoraphidium sp.                  SHJ-02 seed solution and Scenedesmus sp. HCS-02                  seed solution is 1:1-1:5. In step (ii), the                  Chlorella SF-B1 seed solution is connected, and                  the volume ratio of the Chlorella SF-B1 seed                  solution and the microalgae medium is 1:20-1:100.                  Preferred Method: The seed solution of P.kessleri                  FSH-Y3 and S.obliqnus FSH-Y2 is prepared by                  adjusting the pH of the culture medium to 10-12                  at 20-30 degrees C for a light period of 24 hours                  in a light-to-dark time ratio of 14:10-10:14, and                  with a light intensity of 2000-2000 Lux, and                  shaking culture to logarithmic growth period. The                  seed solution of Monoraphidium sp. SHJ-02 and                  Scenedesmus sp. HCS-02 is prepared by adjusting                  the pH of the culture medium to 7-9 at 10-35                  degrees C for a light cycle of 24 hours in a                  light-to-dark time ratio of 14:10 and with a                  light intensity of 1500-15000 Lux, and shaking to                  the logarithmic growth phase. The Chlorella SF-B1                  seed solution is prepared by adjusting the pH of                  the medium to 7-9 at 10-30 degrees C for a light                  period of 24 hours in a light-to-dark time ratio                  of 14:10 with a light intensity of 2000-20000                  Lux, and shaking to the logarithmic growth                  period. Preferred Conditions: The step (ii)                  involves culturing in a step wise cooling manner                  on the basis of the reaction temperature in step                  (i), and reducing the temperature to 0-20 degrees                  C, where the temperature is reduced by 0.1-5                  degrees C every 0.5-2 hours. The gas with a                  carbon dioxide volume content of 1-45 vol.% is                  self-made or adopts process smoke, the process                  flue gas comes from the incineration tail gas of                  the sulfur recovery unit, catalytic cracking                  regeneration tail gas or S-zorb regeneration tail                  gas, and the carbon dioxide content is 1-45                  vol.%, where the NOx content does not exceed 800x                  10-6 (vol/vol).

   UNANNOUNCED I
AE CHANGSHA KAIZE ENG DESIGN CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A0491P
AB    NOVELTY - A soil repairing method involves taking 800-1000 pts. wt. soil, 8-10 pts. wt. anion surfactant and 800-1200 pts. wt. water, mixing, dispersing by ultrasound, freezing and thawing to obtain thawed slurry, taking 800-1,000 pts. wt. thawing slurry, 8-10 pts. wt. N-dodecyl acrylamide, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol phosphate, and 8-10 pts. wt. adhesive, 40-60 pts. wt. polyethylene oxide, mixing, kneading with high viscosity and discharging to obtain a kneaded material, after processing the kneaded material under the action of an electric field for 2-4 hours, transferring it to a mixer, and quickly introducing dry carbon dioxide gas under stirring until the moisture content of the material in the mixer falls below 5%, separating the materials in the mixer by a cyclone and removing fine particles to complete the soil repair, the rapid introduction of dry carbon dioxide gas into the mixer at a rate of 600-800 L/minutes.

TI Metal organic frame and ionic liquid complex material useful as carbon dioxide adsorbent, obtained by e.g. dissolving ionic liquid, adding 2-methylimidazole, stirring, pouring cobalt nitrate solution, centrifuging, washing and drying.
AU ZHANG H
   YU J
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2019563665
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic frame and ionic liquid complex material, obtained by (1) dissolving 1,2-dibromoethane and N-(3-aminopropyl)imidazole in acetone, stirring the reaction at room temperature for 18-24 hours to obtain dark brown viscous liquid, which is separated by centrifugation and washing to obtain ionic liquid, and (2) dissolving the ionic liquid obtained in the step (1) in methanol, followed by the addition of 2-methylimidazole, stirring at normal temperature for 12 hours to obtain ionic liquid solution, pouring the resulting ionic liquid solution into solution of cobalt nitrate in methanol, stirring for 10 minutes, allowing to stand for 24 hours to obtain purple precipitate, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain a metal organic framework and an ionic liquid complex material.
   USE - The metal organic framework and ionic liquid complex material is useful as carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The complex material: has high adsorption value for carbon dioxide; is non-toxic and environmentally friendly.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the molar ratio of 1,2-dibromoethane to N-(3-aminopropyl)imidazole is 1.01-1.30:1, and the volume ratio of the 1,2-dibromoethane to acetone is 3-5:10. In the step (2), the concentration of the ionic liquid in the ionic liquid solution is 2-5 mg/ml, the mass ratio of the ionic liquid to 2-methylimidazole is 1:10-15. In the step (2), the methanol solution of cobalt nitrate is prepared by dissolving cobalt nitrate hexahydrate in methanol, and the concentration of cobalt nitrate is 0.1-0.15 mol/l, and the molar ratio of 2-methylimidazole to cobalt nitrate hexahydrate is 4:1.

PT P
PN CN109876826-A; CN109876826-B
TI Preparing sulfur-rich vacancy photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction for preparing carbon monoxide and methane comprises e.g. adding indium nitrate pentahydrate and cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, and sonicating.
AU ZHU X
   XU H
   YANG J
   LI H
   ZHOU G
   LI Q
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2019550240
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sulfur-rich vacancy photocatalyst comprises (i) adding indium (III) nitrate pentahydrate and cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, sonicating in deionized water to obtain a mixture, taking L-cysteine and transferring to the mixture, stirring and uniformly mixing, transferring to a high pressure reactor, performing hydrothermal reaction, naturally cooling, washing, and drying under vacuum to obtain cadmium indium sulfide nanosphere powder, and (ii) taking the nanosphere powder, tiling in a plasma-treated quartz reactor, passing a gas at a constant rate, performing plasma treatment at a certain time and power, and stopping the treatment to obtain sulfur-rich vacancy cadmium indium sulfide, i.e., VS- cadmium indium sulfide.
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction for preparing carbon monoxide and methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: prevents the low visible light utilization rate and poor relative stability of the traditional cadmium indium sulfide photocatalytic material; utilizes low temperature plasma to treat the photocatalyst under the argon atmosphere; expands the photocatalyst light absorption range suppresses the recombination of photogenerated carriers; improves the stable growth of the catalyst; and improves the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of dium (III) nitrate pentahydrate, cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, deionized water and L-cysteine is 0.1528-0.3056:0.0617-0.1234:20-40:0.097-0.194. In the step (ii), the amount of the nanosphere powder is 50 mg. In the step (i), the washing reagent is distilled water and ethanol. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 140-180 degrees C for 10-15 hours. In the step (ii), the plasma treatment is carried out at an electric power of 150 W in argon atmosphere for 5-15 minutes, where the gas flow rate is 300 ml/minute. The treatment is carried out for 8 minutes. In the step (i), the washing is carried out for 3-5 times, and the vacuum drying is carried out at 70 degrees C for 12 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Quenching a polymerization process comprising reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide in the presence of a bimetallic metal complex catalyst, comprises deactivation of the catalyst by contacting the catalyst with an acid effective to deactivate the catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for quenching a polymerization which is useful in various electronic components, construction materials, data storage products, automotive and aircraft products, security components, medical applications, mobile phones, packaging (including bottles), and optical applications (such as safety glass, windscreens).
   ADVANTAGE - The polymerization process can be carried out in a batch reactor or a continuous reactor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for purifying a polymer product formed from a polymerization process comprising the reaction of carbon dioxide with an epoxide in the presence of a bimetallic metal complex catalyst, comprising quenching the polymerization process by contacting the catalyst with an acid effective to deactivate the catalyst, removing the deactivated catalyst from the polymer product by contacting the deactivated catalyst and polymer product with a solid phase and/or by precipitation and optionally reactivating the catalyst by contacting the deactivated catalyst with an anion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The acid contains an anion effective to initiate the polymerization process. The deactivated catalyst is caused to precipitate from the crude polymer product by the addition of one or more precipitant or precipitates automatically. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of acid to catalyst in the deactivation step is less than or equal to 20:1 of the acid to catalyst mole ratio for the deactivation reaction. The molar ratio of acid to catalyst in the deactivation step is less than or equal to 10:1 of the acid to catalyst mole ratio for the deactivation reaction. The acid is a carboxylic acid. The acid is a functionalized carboxylic acid comprising at least one other functional group in addition to the acid group effective to form a stable bond or interaction with one or more of the metal centers of the catalyst, where the functional group is e.g. hydroxy or amine N(R9)2 , where R9 is hydrogen, an aliphatic, heteroaliphatic, alicyclic, heteroalicyclic, aryl or heteroaryl group. The functionalized carboxylic acid is a dicarboxylic, tricarboxylic or hydroxy carboxylic acid. The acid contains an anion e.g. methyl ester (preferred) or ethyl acetate. The acid has a pKa of at least 2.5. PN JP2018174910-A
TI Producing foamable refined sake by pouring raw spirit containing pure sake into sake production tank, generating carbon dioxide gas, cooling interior of tank for sedimenting residue, injecting inert gas and taking out supernatant liquid.
AU SATO J
AE TATENOKAWA INC (TATE-Non-standard)
GA 201890137M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing foamable refined sake involves (a) pouring a raw spirit containing at least a pure sake into a sake production tank, (b) generating (S12) carbon dioxide gas by re-fermentation of the sake in the sealed sake tank, (c) cooling (S13) the interior of the sake production tank to predetermined temperature and sedimenting the residue, and (d) injecting (S14) an inert gas into the sake production tank and taking out a supernatant liquid on the upper layer of the sediment in the liquid with the sake extraction pipe which has an inlet in the center side in the sake production tank.
   USE - The method is useful for producing foamable refined sake.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables mass production of fine foamable refined sake with reduced astringent taste and bitterness, and enhanced flavor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the production method of the foamable refined sake.
   Generating carbon dioxide gas (S12)
   Cooling the interior of the sake production tank (S13)
   Injecting an inert gas into the sake production tank (S14)
   Filling the container with sake (S15)
   Storing the container (S16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further involves (e) cooling the raw spirit and adjusting the temperature for performing re-fermentation, before step (a). The method involves (f) adjusting the alcohol content of the sake between the starch making process and the press-filtration process by adding water, and (g) adjusting the glucose content to 2% or more, before step (a). The step (d) is performed with a gas injection unit. The method involves (h) pouring gas into the sake production tank, and discharging the sake cake remaining on the bottom side of the sake production tank by using discharge pipe, after step (d).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The sake production tank is equipped with the closed tank main unit, a raw spirit injection unit, sake extraction pipe, an inlet, an outlet, gas injection unit, and residue discharge pipe. The gas injection unit is located such that it is above the liquid level of the sake retained in the tank main unit.

PT P
PN JP2018075510-A
TI Fixed complex catalyst used in e.g. hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises carrier and catalyst including ligand having proton-responsive functional group which can attach and detach proton with acid or base, bonded via ionic bond.
AU SAKURAI H
   KAMI T
   KIUCHI M
   HIMETA Y
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 2018382457
AB    NOVELTY - A fixed complex catalyst comprises a porous carrier and a catalyst including a ligand having a functional group exhibiting proton responsiveness, bonded via an ionic bond. The functional group can attach and detach a proton with an acid or a base.
   USE - Fixed complex catalyst is used as liquid-phase or gas-phase heterogeneous catalyst for hydrogenating carbon dioxide to produce formic acid, formaldehyde, or methanol, and decomposing formic acid, formaldehyde, or methanol to produce hydrogen (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fixed complex catalyst has excellent catalyst activity even at normal temperature and pressure. The catalyst is rigidly coupled with the porous carrier surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of fixed complex catalyst, which involves coupling the porous carrier and complex catalyst by ionic bond;
   (2) hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, which involves performing hydrogenation reaction under coexistence of a photocatalyst having hydrogen generation activity, and the fixed complex catalyst; and
   (3) hydrogenation apparatus, which comprises the photocatalyst, and the fixed complex catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The porous carrier comprises a silane coupling agent having a functional group capable of ionically bonding with the complex catalyst. The catalyst and functional group of the silane coupling agent form the coupling via the ionic bond. The functional group is chosen from a hydroxyl group, an amino group, an imino group, an amide group, a carboxyl group, and a mercapto group. The bond between the porous carrier and the complex catalyst is of formula: G-O-Si(R1R2)-Z-E(power(m))(MkLl)(power(n)). The silane coupling agent is of formula: X-O-Si(R1R2)-Z-E.E = -NH2, NRH, -NR2, -COOH, -SH or epoxy group;X = halo or alkoxy;G = porous carrier;MkLl = complex catalyst;R1,R2 = alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl or alkoxy;Z = optionally substituted aryl or optionally substituted aliphatic group;E(power(m)) = -NH3+, NRH2+, -NR2H+, -CR2CR2O-, -CR(O-)CR3 or -COO-;R = H, optionally substituted aryl or optionally substituted aliphatic group;m = 1 or -1;M = metal;k,l,n = 1-4;andL = organic ligand having at least 1 or more hydroxy, amino, imino, amido, carboxy or mercapto as functional group.

PN CN107937233-A
TI Processing chestnut involves peeling chestnut and squeezing chestnut pulp, supercritical carbon dioxide extracting chestnut peel powder, enzymolyzing, fermenting and filtering through filter membrane to obtain chestnut vinegar.
AU XU S
AE GUILIN GUONONG ECOLOGICAL AGRIC CO LTD (GUIL-Non-standard)
GA 2018327382
AB    NOVELTY - Method of processing chestnut involves peeling chestnut and squeezing the chestnut pulp to obtain a chestnut juice, drying the chestnut peel, crushing, sieving through 200 mesh sieve, then placing the chestnut peel powder in a supercritical extraction vessel, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using ethyl acetate as an entrainer at 30 degrees C and 25 MPa with a carbon dioxide flow rate of 30 l/minute for 10-30 minutes to obtain chestnut peel extract, adding pectinase, cellulase, hemicellulase, xylanase, and chestnut peel extract to the chestnut juice, enzymolyzing, adjusting the soluble solid content of the chestnut juice to 22-25%, then adding 2-6 mg/l Saccharomyces cerevisiae and 5-10 mg/l Bifidobacterium and fermenting for the first time to obtain primary fermented solution, adding 5 mg/l Acetobacter to the primary fermented solution, and then fermenting for the second time and filtering through the filter membrane to obtain a chestnut vinegar.
   USE - The method is useful for processing chestnut.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that the prepared chestnut vinegar has high dietary fiber content and transparency, healthcare and refreshing effect and excellent color, aroma and mouthfeel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The chestnut peel extract contains not less than 123 mg/100 g flavonoid, not less than 24 mg/100 g polyphenol, not less than 58 mg/100 g volatile oil and less than 21 mg/100 g brown pigment. Preferred Method: The method involves adjusting the soluble solids content of the chestnut juice using Jerusalem artichoke fructooligosaccharide and schizophyllan, fermenting for the first time at 25 degrees C and 20 %mass oxygen in the air for 2-5 days, fermenting for the second time at 30 degrees C under anaerobic condition for 5 days, where the mass ratio of ethyl acetate and chestnut peel is 2-5:138, the added amount of the pectinase is 0.1-0.5 g/l, the cellulase is 0.1-0.5 g/l, the hemicellulase is 0.1-0.5 g/l, the xylanase is 0.1-0.5 g/l and the chestnut peel extract is 1-5 g/l based on the volume of chestnut juice, and the pore size of the filter membrane is 0.25 mu m.

TI Automatically filling carbon dioxide snow block            into container within automatic dispensing station            comprises e.g. receiving first signal corresponding to            pressure of liquid carbon dioxide into controller and            inputting volume of block.
AU ZHOU Y
   BURSAC R
   SEVER R
AE ZHOU Y (ZHOU-Individual)
   BURSAC R (BURS-Individual)
   SEVER R (SEVE-Individual)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
   PRAXAIR TECHNOLOGY INC (PRAX-C)
GA 201951683B
AB    NOVELTY - Automatically filling carbon dioxide snow                block into a container within an automatic                dispensing station comprises: receiving a first                signal corresponding to a pressure of liquid carbon                dioxide into a controller as a first set point;                inputting into the controller a volume of the                carbon dioxide snow block; flowing a gaseous carbon                dioxide into a fill conduit to pressurize the fill                conduit at or above a pressure sufficient to                prevent a phase change of the liquid carbon                dioxide; at least a portion of the liquid carbon                dioxide undergoing a phase change to transform into                the carbon dioxide snow block and off-gas carbon                dioxide within the container; withdrawing the                off-gas carbon dioxide from the container through a                plate permanently fixed or removably fixed to the                container; measuring an elapsed time of filling the                container with the liquid carbon dioxide and                generating a second signal corresponding to the                elapsed time; and transmitting the second                signal.
   USE - The method is useful for automatically filling                carbon dioxide snow block into a container within                an automatic dispensing station.
   ADVANTAGE - The method automatically generates carbon                dioxide snow block within a selected container                inside a dispensing station or generates carbon                dioxide snow block by auto charging into a                container that can be situated within a charging                station.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Automatically filling carbon dioxide snow                block into a container within an automatic                dispensing station, comprises: receiving a first                signal corresponding to a pressure of liquid carbon                dioxide into a controller as a first set point;                inputting into the controller a volume of the                carbon dioxide snow block to be generated as a                second set point, where the controller determines a                fill time to generate the volume of the carbon                dioxide snow block based on the first set point and                the second set point, the controller selects the                container with an interior volume corresponding to                the second set point, the container is located                within the automatic dispensing station, and the                automatic dispensing station comprising at least                two containers of different interior volumes;                flowing a gaseous carbon dioxide into a fill                conduit to pressurize the fill conduit at or above                a pressure sufficient to prevent a phase change of                the liquid carbon dioxide, where the controller                causes a liquid carbon dioxide control valve to                allow the liquid carbon dioxide to flow along the                fill conduit and into the container when the                controller determines the pressure in the fill                conduit is at or above the pressure sufficient to                prevent the phase change of the liquid carbon                dioxide; introducing the liquid carbon dioxide into                the container; at least a portion of the liquid                carbon dioxide undergoing a phase change to                transform into the carbon dioxide snow block and                off-gas carbon dioxide within the container;                withdrawing the off-gas carbon dioxide from the                container through a plate permanently fixed or                removably fixed to the container, the plate                permeable to the off-gas carbon dioxide and at                least partially impermeable to a solid phase carbon                dioxide; measuring an elapsed time of filling the                container with the liquid carbon dioxide and                generating a second signal corresponding to the                elapsed time; transmitting the second signal                corresponding to the elapsed time to the                controller, where the controller allows the liquid                carbon dioxide to continue to flow into the                container, in the absence of an override, when the                elapsed time is less than the fill time, and                preventing the liquid carbon dioxide to flow in the                container when the elapsed time has reached the                fill time. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a method (m2) of automatically filling                carbon dioxide snow block into a container within                an automatic dispensing station and vending the                carbon dioxide snow block from the selected                container;
   (2) a method (m3) of charging carbon dioxide                snow block into a container;
   (3) a method (m4) of preparing a container for                automated charging carbon dioxide snow block into a                single container;
   (4) a method (m5) of configuring a container                into a filling orientation for receiving carbon                dioxide liquid; and
   (5) a method (m6) of selecting a container                within an automatic dispensing station to                automatically fill carbon dioxide snow block into                the container.

PT P
PN KR2020143863-A; KR2253079-B1
TI Device for supplying carbon dioxide using dry ice comprises suction unit that sucks external air including carbon dioxide from outside and supplies to mixing unit, storage unit that stores dry ice and another carbon dioxide, and mixing unit.
AU PAEK Y
   KANG S W
   KWON J K
   JANG J K
AE RURAL DEV ADMINISTRATION (RURA-C)
   KOREA PUBLIC (KOPU-Non-standard)
GA 2021011926
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide supply device (10) using dry ice comprises suction unit (110) configured to suck external air including first carbon dioxide from outside and supply it to the mixing unit; storage unit (120) for storing dry ice (1211) and second carbon dioxide generated by sublimating the dry ice; and mixing unit (130) connected to be opened and closed with suction unit and storage unit and configured to generate mixed carbon dioxide by mixing first carbon dioxide supplied from suction unit and second carbon dioxide supplied from storage unit.
   USE - A device for supplying carbon dioxide using dry ice (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device promotes crop growth and increases yield by activating photosynthesis of crops by supplying carbon dioxide gas into a greenhouse in which crops are grown. Carbon dioxide gas production cost is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for supplying carbon dioxide using dry ice (1211) by suction step in which suction unit (110) sucks external air including first carbon dioxide from the outside; storage step of storing dry ice and second carbon dioxide generated by sublimating the dry ice; circulation step of circulating the cold water between the cooler and storage unit through a circulation line connecting the storage unit (120) and a cooler for generating cold water by a circulation unit; mixing step in which a mixing unit (130) mixes the first carbon dioxide supplied from suction unit and second carbon dioxide supplied from storage unit to generate mixed carbon dioxide; supply step of supplying the mixed carbon dioxide into the greenhouse through a supply pipe in which the mixing unit communicates the interior of the mixing unit and the interior of the greenhouse; monitoring step in which control unit monitors internal conditions such as carbon dioxide concentration, temperature and humidity inside the suction unit, the storage unit, and mixing unit; and controlling step using control unit that controls the suction amount of first carbon dioxide sucked into suction unit, the dry ice weight and second carbon dioxide concentration of storage unit, and opening/closing between the suction unit or storage unit and the mixing unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide supplying apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide supply device (10)
   Suction unit (110)
   Storage unit (120)
   Mixing unit (130)
   Dry ice (1211)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Devices: The suction unit comprises an inlet through which the external air is introduced; and control plate that is rotatably disposed within the inlet and controls the opening and closing of the inlet and controls the suction amount of the external air. The storage unit comprises a storage tank in which the dry ice is stored; weight sensor disposed below the storage tank to measure the weight of the dry ice; and a gas sensor measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide generated from the dry ice. The mixing unit is disposed at one side of mixing unit so that the mixed carbon dioxide is supplied to greenhouse, and includes a supply pipe communicating the interior of mixing unit and the inside of greenhouse. The supply pipe communicates the inside of supply pipe located inside the greenhouse and inside of the greenhouse so that mixed carbon dioxide is distributed inside the greenhouse, and is predetermined on the side of the supply pipe. The mixing unit includes a blower disposed to face the supply pipe on the other side of mixing unit so that the mixed carbon dioxide flows into the supply pipe, and generates air in one direction. The carbon dioxide supplying apparatus further comprises a driving unit that generates a driving force required for operation of the blower and supplies driving force to the blower. The apparatus includes cooler for generating cold water; circulation line connecting the cooler and storage unit; and circulation unit disposed on one side of circulation line to generate hydraulic pressure so that cold water circulates between the cooler and storage unit along the circulation line. The apparatus further comprises a control unit for controlling the opening and closing between unit and mixing unit through monitoring of carbon dioxide supply device, suction amount of first carbon dioxide sucked into the suction unit, dry ice weight and second carbon dioxide concentration of storage unit, and suction unit or storage unit.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN209801789-U; CN110887230-A
TI Carbon dioxide heat pump COP value improving system, has power supply interface electrically connected to power supply interface of trans-critical carbon dioxide compressor, and expansion valve and evaporator connected to inlet of trans-critical carbon dioxide compressor.
AU TAN Y
   NING Y
AE TAN Y (TANY-Individual)
GA 2020006300
AB    (CN209801789-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a method for improving carbon dioxide heat pump COP value of the system, comprising: a trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump device and supercritical carbon dioxide to generate electricity device; supercritical carbon dioxide output by trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump device as heat source to drive said supercritical carbon dioxide generate electricity device to generate electricity, the generated electric energy and then supplying the trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump compressor, the output of dead steam afterheat continuously for condenser heat, part of the heat energy 1:1 into electrical energy and then using; thereby improving COP value of the whole heat pump system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a system for increasing coefficient of performance value of carbon dioxide heat pump. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
EA    NOVELTY - The system has a trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump device comprising a trans-critical carbon dioxide compressor, a condenser, an expansion valve and an evaporator. The trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump device and the trans-critical carbon dioxide power generation device are connected together through a circulation pipeline to realize trans-critical cycle. The critical carbon dioxide compressor is provided with a compression chamber for compressing carbon dioxide. A circulation line and an outlet are orderly connected with each other through a condenser. An expansion valve and the evaporator are connected to the trans-critical carbon dioxide compressor.
   USE - System for increasing a coefficient of performance value of a carbon dioxide heat pump.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide power generation device generates electricity and supplies the electricity to the trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump compressor. The system can output exhaust steam waste heat to convert thermal energy into electrical energy for reuse so as to improve working performance.
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PN CN113617179-A
TI Device for producing carbon dioxide based on carbon dioxide generation in carbon dioxide recycling technology field, has gas treatment system removing secondary dust from exhaust gas generated by carbon dioxide generating system.
AU ZHU G
   ZHU Y
AE HEFEI ZHONGFU IND TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 202207498A
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a carbon dioxide generating system whose exhaust end is connected with a first gas treatment system (A). An air outlet end of a second gas treatment system (B) is connected to a third gas treatment systems (C). The third gas treatment system removes secondary dust from exhaust gas generated by the carbon dioxide generating system, where a nitrogen compound contained in the exhaust gas, sulfur dioxide and oxygen is removed by a fourth gas treatment system to obtain high-purity carbon dioxide.
   USE - Device for producing carbon dioxide based on carbon dioxide generation in a carbon dioxide recycling technology field. Uses include but are not limited to natural gas exploitation and combustion, synthetic ammonia production, petroleum refining industry, steelmaking industry, boiler combustion, coke and heavy oil combustion.
   ADVANTAGE - The working efficiency and purification effect of a carbon dioxide pressure swing adsorption purification system can be improved. The carbon emission in flue gas can be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a device for producing carbon dioxide based on carbon dioxide generation in a carbon dioxide recycling technology field.
   Gas treatment systems (1,2,3,4)
   Desulphurization adsorption tower (41)
   Carbon dioxide adsorption tower (42)
   Carbon dioxide compression purification system (43)
   Rectification storage system (44)

AB    NOVELTY - Method of capturing carbon dioxide and converting the captured carbon dioxide into useful byproducts, involves providing a material including a material matrix holding an ionic liquid, exposing the material to a source of thermal energy to capture carbon dioxide within the material, removing the material from exposure to the source of thermal energy, and washing the material with a solution (120) to convert the captured carbon dioxide and wash the converted, captured carbon dioxide from the material as filtrate.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:

PN CN110511869-A
TI Cell culture device capable of generating variable pulsed electric field comprises e.g. a box body provided with heating pipe and ozone access hole, the cooling pipe and carbon dioxide access hole provided on the box body.
AU LIN T
   WANG Q
   XU A
   HU Z
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 2019A1455Q
AB    NOVELTY - Cell culture device capable of generating variable pulsed electric field comprises a box body is provided with heating pipe and an ozone access hole on one side wall of the box body. A cooling pipe and carbon dioxide access hole are provided on the other side of the box corresponding to the heating pipe. A temperature sensor is installed on the rear wall of the box. A carbon dioxide sensor and ozone sensor are used to detect temperature, carbon dioxide gas concentration and ozone gas concentration, respectively. The top of the box is equipped with an electrode device, stepper motor, and light emitting diode lamp for lighting. The electrode device is an array electrode with multiple electrode needles. The control electrode is spacing by selecting different electrode needles, adjusting the position of the electrode device through the stepping motor. The stage capable of fixing petri dish is provided below the electrode device in the cabinet. A position sensor is installed on the stage.
   USE - Used as cell culture device capable of generating variable pulsed electric field.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can adjust electric field intensity, electric field frequency, electric field polarity, and action time of the pulse electric field, which makes up for the limitation that the traditional device can only perform external cell processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for cell culturing comprising sending a signal to the main control module through the host computer, setting the ozone concentration to 15-30 ppm, the sterilization time is 20-30 minutes, turning on the ozone concentration adjustment module to sterilize the stage, electrode device and other spaces in the cabinet, through the ozone sensor real-time detection of the ozone concentration in the cabinet, comparing with the set ozone concentration value, controlling the ozone concentration in the cabinet by controlling the ozone valve in the ozone concentration adjustment module, so that the ozone concentration in the cabinet is different from the set value which is less than 1 ppm until the end of sterilization time, sending a signal to the main control module through the host computer, setting the temperature inside the box to 36-37 degrees C, turning on the temperature control module, using the temperature sensor to detect the temperature inside the box in real time, comparing with the set temperature value, the two-way switch of the module starts the heating tube or the cooling tube so that the error between the temperature of the cabinet and the set value does not exceed plus minus 0.5 degrees CC, and maintaining the cell culture environment, sending signal to the main control module through the host computer, setting the carbon dioxide concentration in the box to 5%, turning on the carbon dioxide concentration adjustment module, using the carbon dioxide sensor to detect the carbon dioxide concentration in the box in real time, comparing with the set concentration value by controlling the carbon dioxide valve in the carbon dioxide concentration adjustment module, controlling the controlled concentration in the cabinet so that the error between the controlled concentration in the cabinet and the set value does not exceed 0.5%, and maintaining the cell culture environment, placing the cell culture dish on the stage, operating the host computer to select the electrode pin number and electrode pin distance in the electrode device, turning on the electrode drive module, using the position sensor to detect the distance between the electrode device and the stage in real time, comparing the set values, moving the electrode device by controlling the stepping motor, so that the distance between the electrode device and the stage and the set value error does not exceed 1 mm, setting the current pulse electric field parameters by the host computer, the output waveform of the pulse electric field is square or triangular, where the output voltage amplitude is plus minus 100- plus minus 4000V, the pulse width is 0.1-100 mu s, the pulse frequency is 1-10 Hz, and the number of pulse trains is 10-1000, pulse electric field processing time is 1 minute-2 hours, and the cell culture time is 1-48 hours, setting the pulse electric field processing time, driving the stepping motor by main control module to move the electrode device upward and return to the original position.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of cell culture device capable of generating variable pulsed electric field.

TI Carbon dioxide methanation catalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide for producing methane, comprises carrier containing cerium, lanthanum, praseodymium, gadolinium, erbium, terbium, and neodymium, and catalyst component containing nickel.
AU TAKAHASHI E
   YOSHIKAWA K
   KANAEDA M
   NAKAMURA H
AE HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
GA 201980641W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide methanation catalyst comprises a carrier and a catalyst component. The catalyst converts carbon dioxide into methane using hydrogen. The carrier contains cerium, lanthanum, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, ytterbium and lutetium. The catalyst component contains nickel supported by the carrier.
   USE - Carbon dioxide methanation catalyst is used for reducing carbon dioxide for producing methane (claimed) used as fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent carbon dioxide methanation performance even in large temperature range, and can be prepared by simple process, and efficiently converts carbon dioxide at less than 300 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reduction of carbon dioxide, which involves supplying gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the catalyst, reducing carbon dioxide, and producing methane. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1-6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrier contains zirconium, lanthanum, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, europium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, holmium, erbium, thulium, ytterbium and lutetium The carrier contains an oxide. The catalyst contains 0.1-40 parts mass nickel with respect to 100 parts mass active ingredients of the carrier. The catalyst component further contains platinum, rhodium and/or palladium as an additional component. The carbon dioxide methanation catalyst contains 0.1-1 part mass additional component with respect to 100 parts mass active ingredients of carrier. The gas further contains 0.1-95 mol% nitrogen and 0.1-20 mol% oxygen. Preferred Properties: The adsorption amount of carbon monoxide of catalyst is 0.7 cm3/g or more. The average particle diameter of the catalyst component is 1-50 nm. The maximum carbon dioxide conversion ratio of catalyst under condition in which catalyst inlet temperature is less than 300 degrees C and ratio of nitrogen contained in supplied gas is 90 mol% is 80% or more.

PT P
PN WO2018097721-A1; NL2017858-B1; NL2018406-B1; CN110191749-A; EP3544719-A1; US2019374900-A1; CN115069062-A; US2022339575-A1
TI Apparatus for separation and recovery of first gas            i.e. carbon dioxide from gas mixture of first gas and            other gases, different from first gas, by cyclic            adsorption/desorption process using loose particulate            sorbent for gas adsorption.
AU BEAUMONT M
   KASPER P T
   SMITH I
   GUNKEL A
   BEAUMONT M L
AE SKYTREE BV (ISYS-C)
   GIAURA BV (GIAU-Non-standard)
   SKYTREE BV (ISYS-C)
GA 2018436608
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus (A1) (10) for the separation and                optional recovery of a first gas, preferably carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture (preferably air)                containing the first gas and other gases, different                from the first gas, by a cyclic                adsorption/desorption process using a loose                particulate sorbent for gas adsorption, is claimed.                The sorbent is provided in a stack (22) of at least                two adjacent, parallel, spaced-apart layers (24) in                the apparatus (A1). Each layer comprises a flexible                fabric enclosing the sorbent. The fabric is                permeable to the gas mixture but impermeable to the                sorbent. The fabric of each layer is attached to,                and enclosed by, a stiff frame of the layer. The                apparatus (A1) has an inlet (14) and outlet for                gases on axially opposite sides of the apparatus                (A1). The apparatus (A1) is characterized by each                of the layers extending axially within the                apparatus (A1).
   USE - The apparatus is useful for separating and                optional recovering a first gas, preferably carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture (preferably air or a                flue gas) containing the first gas and other                gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) extracting first gas, preferably carbon                dioxide from the gas mixture containing the first                gas and other gases, preferably air or the flue                gas, different from the first gas, by the cyclic                adsorption/desorption, comprising passing the gas                mixture through the apparatus (A1); and
   (2) apparatus (A2) for separating carbon                dioxide from air by the cyclic                adsorption/desorption process using a loose                particulate sorbent for carbon dioxide adsorption,                where the apparatus has an inlet for a first gas                stream and an outlet for a second gas stream and                the inlet can be in communication with an enclosed                or confined space, preferably in a building or in a                vehicle, and the outlet can be in communication                with both an interior of the space and an exterior                of the space, and has a first valve mechanism which                can control the outlet to provide selective                communication of the outlet with either the                interior of the space or the exterior of the                space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of a                sealable enclosure with a rigid vacuum                chamber.
   Apparatus (10)
   Vacuum chamber (12)
   Inlet (14)
   Stack (22)
   Parallel, spaced-apart layers (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The layers are                  provided in at least two laterally-extending,                  axially spaced-apart, parallel stacks of                  axially-extending layers. The axially-extending                  layers also extend vertically within the                  apparatus (A1). The layers in each stack are                  spaced laterally apart from one another, so as to                  form channels for the flow of gas through the                  apparatus (A1), the layers being spaced apart by                  0.3-0.7 (preferably 0.4-4) cm. The frame of each                  layer: is substantially flat and is open at its                  middle, preferably its axial and vertical middle,                  and has a rectangular, preferably square, shape;                  and comprises two attached, identical,                  substantially flat and open, stiff frame members                  which have the shape of the frame. Each frame                  member has many reinforcing ribs extending across                  open portions of the frame member and attached to                  its opposite sides. A pair of the ribs extends                  diagonally across each frame member from the                  comers of the frame member, and the pair of ribs                  are attached to each other where they cross. A                  flat, electrical heating element extends across                  the open middle, preferably the axial and                  vertical middle, of the frame of each of at least                  one layer. The apparatus (A1) further comprises a                  source of heated air to the sorbent in the layers                  for desorbing carbon dioxide from the layers. The                  gas mixture diffuses through each layer initially                  through its fabric, then through its sorbent, and                  then finally through its fabric. The apparatus                  (A1) having axially-extending baffle and that are                  adjacent to, and on laterally opposite sides of,                  the inlet and outlet, respectively of the                  apparatus (A1) and that preferably extend                  vertically from a bottom wall to a top wall of                  the apparatus (A1). The apparatus (A1) having at                  least one laterally- and vertically-extending                  baffle between the inlet and outlet of the                  apparatus (A1) and that preferably extend                  vertically from a bottom wall to a top wall of                  the apparatus (A1). The apparatus (A1) having a                  pair of laterally- and vertically-extending                  baffles between each of two or more of the stacks                  to channel movement of gas, from which the first                  gas, particularly carbon dioxide, has been                  partially removed, laterally within each stack                  and between the stacks. The apparatus (A1)                  contains at least 10, preferably at least 25-40                  layers. Each stack contains at least 10,                  preferably greater than or equal to 15 or more                  layers. The layers form a serpentine flow path                  for gas through the apparatus (A1). The first                  valve mechanism is a single two-way valve. The                  inlet can be in communication with both the                  interior of the space and the exterior of the                  space, and has a second valve mechanism which                  controls the inlet to provide selective                  communication with either the interior of the                  space or the exterior of the space. The second                  valve mechanism is a single two-way valve. The                  inlet can be in communication with only the                  interior of the space. Preferred Process: The                  process further comprises: providing a stream                  essentially comprising the first gas from the                  desorption process to a further process; and a                  chemical conversion process, a process for the                  growth of plants, and/or a process for the                  carbonation of a fluid including a beverage or an                  aquarium.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN114292786-A
TI Device useful for producing hydrogen coupled with hydrogen fuel cells by sludge fermentation based on immobilized cells.
AU DING D
   HU P
AE SUZHOU GUTANLV HYDROGEN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202262100M
AB    NOVELTY - Device for producing hydrogen coupled with hydrogen fuel cells by sludge fermentation based on immobilized cells, is claimed. The activated sludge comprises organic matter, nutrients, microorganisms and moisture. The device comprises a configuration module for configuring a substrate and auxiliary materials, and for combining the Clostridium P21 with an adsorption filler to form immobilized cell particles, where the substrate comprises the activated sludge, the auxiliary material comprises phosphate solution, the Clostridium P21 is a rod-shaped alkalophilic Clostridium, the sludge anaerobic fermentation module is used to utilize Clostridium P21, an alkaline cleaning module for removing carbon dioxide and acid gas in the mixed gas by neutralization, and a adsorption module removes the peculiar odor in the mixed gas through activated carbon adsorption, and outputs the mixed gas to supply power to the hydrogen fuel cell module.
   USE - The device is useful for producing hydrogen coupled with hydrogen fuel cells by sludge fermentation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides hydrogen, carbon dioxide and acid gas with a purity of more than 98%, and increases efficiency of utilization of bacterial species in the system, and yield of hydrogen and the efficiency of power generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device for producing hydrogen coupled with hydrogen fuel cells by sludge fermentation based on immobilized cells, is claimed. The activated sludge comprises organic matter, nutrients, microorganisms and moisture. The device comprises a configuration module for configuring a substrate and auxiliary materials, and for combining the Clostridium P21 with an adsorption filler to form immobilized cell particles, where the substrate comprises the activated sludge, the auxiliary material comprises phosphate solution, the Clostridium P21 is a rod-shaped alkalophilic Clostridium, the sludge anaerobic fermentation module is used to utilize Clostridium P21, ferment through the liquid medium of substrate and auxiliary materials, and set the pH to more than 7 and the fermentation temperature of more than 40degreesC to produce a mixed gas containing hydrogen, when the fermentation residue is periodically and quantitatively outputted intermittently, the immobilized cell particles adsorbed with Clostridium P21 are left in the fermenter, the mixed gas comprises high-purity hydrogen, carbon dioxide and acid gas, a water washing module, receiving the mixed gas, and washing away the suspended particles in the mixed gas, an alkaline cleaning module for removing carbon dioxide and acid gas in the mixed gas by neutralization, and a adsorption module removes the peculiar odor in the mixed gas through activated carbon adsorption, and outputs the mixed gas to supply power to the hydrogen fuel cell module.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for sludge fermentation and hydrogen production coupled with a hydrogen fuel cell based on immobilized cells, using the device, which involves periodically and quantitatively and intermittently, adding liquid culture medium including the substrate and auxiliary materials and the immobilized cell particles adsorbed with Clostridium P21 into the fermenter, setting the pH to be constant to more than 7 and the fermentation temperature to more than 40degreesC to produce a mixed gas containing hydrogen, and to output the fermentation residue from the feeding port regularly and quantitatively and intermittently, where the mixed gas only comprises more than 98% purity, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and acid gas, the Clostridium P21 is a rod-shaped alkalophilic Clostridium, the substrate is carbohydrate, protein, fat and sugar included in the activated sludge for hydrogen production by fermentation, oil, acid and/or organic acid substances, the auxiliary materials comprise phosphate complex solution and nutrients included in the activated sludge, and the nutrients are nitrogen salts, phosphorus salts and/or potassium salts, receiving the mixed gas, washing away the suspended particles in the mixed gas, removing carbon dioxide and acid gas in the mixed gas by neutralization, removing the peculiar odor in the mixed gas through activated carbon adsorption, and outputting the mixed gas to supply power to the hydrogen fuel cell module.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The high-purity hydrogen is hydrogen with a purity of more than 98%, and the sludge is anaerobic fermentation. The modules comprise feeding unit which is located at the upper end of the fermenter, and is used to periodically and quantitatively place the liquid medium including the substrate and auxiliary materials and the immobilized cell particles adsorbed with Clostridium P21 into the fermenter, where the substrate is carbohydrate, protein, fat, sugar, oil, acid and/or organic acid in the activated sludge for hydrogen production by fermentation, and the auxiliary materials comprise activated sludge including nutrients, the nutrients are nitrogen salts, phosphorus salts and/or potassium salts, a deoxygenation unit, located at the bottom of the fermentor, for purging the liquid culture medium with pure inert gas from the bottom of the fermentor to remove residual oxygen in the liquid culture medium, a temperature control unit for putting the liquid culture medium into a fermenter with a stirrer, and setting the fermentation temperature to a temperature of 50-80degreesC, a pH control unit for controlling the pH of the fermentor in the fermentation cycle to be a constant value of 8-12 through the acid solution and the alkaline solution, and separation and output unit which is located at the lower portion of the fermenter, and is used for periodically and quantitatively outputting the fermentation residue from the feeding port, and leaving the immobilized cell particles adsorbed with Clostridium P21 in the fermenter through filtration. The device further comprises a pretreatment module for removing the activated sludge before feeding, heavy metal and/or inorganic salt, where the inorganic salts are silicon oxide, iron oxide, aluminum oxide, calcium oxide and/or salt. The activated sludge further comprises heavy metals and/or inorganic salts for removing heavy metals and/or inorganic salts. The phosphate complex solution/l of the liquid culture medium comprises 1.4 g potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 1.1 g dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 2 g ammonium sulfate, 0.5 g magnesium sulfate, 10 g yeast extract, 10 g morpholine ethanesulfonic acid, 40 mg nitrilotriacetic acid, 20 mg manganese sulfate monohydrate, 16 mg hexahydrate ammonium ferrous sulfate, 4 mg cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 0.04 mg zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.4 mg copper chloride dihydrate, 0.4 mg nickel chloride hexahydrate, 0.4 mg sodium molybdate, 0.4 mg sodium selenate, 0.4 mg tungsten sodium and 0.3 g cysteine. The immobilized cell pellets adsorbed with Clostridium P21 are prepared and used with the liquid culture medium at a ratio of 10 ml/l. The adsorption filler of the immobilized cell particles is activated carbon, sodium alginate, polyvinyl alcohol, polypropylene phthalamide and/or polyvinyl butyral. The adsorption filler is activated carbon.Preferred Method: The method further involves sterilizing the liquid culture medium at a high temperature of more than 100degreesC, where the inert gas is 99.99% high-purity nitrogen. The method involves washing 2 g activated carbon particles with a diameter of 0.8-1 mm using deionized water, after soaking for 24 hours, drying in an oven at 110degreesC for 24 hours, mixing with the Clostridium P21 with a concentration of 1 g/l and 100 ml fermentation bacteria liquid after the temperature of the activated carbon particles is reduced to normal temperature, in an anaerobic incubator, thus the final concentration of the activated carbon particles is 2%, where the concentration of the Clostridium P21 is 1 g/l, immobilizing the mixture of the activated carbon and the Clostridium P21 for 12 hours, and filtering out the immobilized cell particles that are immobilized. The adsorption filler is sodium alginate, and the production steps of the immobilized cell particles involves obtaining 2 g sodium alginate and heating and dissolving in 50 ml water, after the temperature of the sodium alginate solution is reduced to normal temperature, in an anaerobic incubator and 50 ml bacterial species concentration is the Clostridium of 2 g/l P21, dropping the bacterial mixture of the sodium alginate and the Clostridium P21 into a 10% calcium chloride aqueous solution with a syringe, immobilizing for 12 hours, and filtering the immobilized cell particles that are immobilized. The fermentation temperature is 60degreesC and the pH is constant at 10.5.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PN WO2021013640-A1; EP3770145-A1; CN114423729-A; EP4003951-A1; US2022259131-A1; JP2022541622-W
TI Producing acrolein or acrylic acid as target            product involves continuously passing stream of            starting reaction gas mixture consisting of propene,            molecular oxygen, water, molecular nitrogen, carbon            monoxide, and carbon dioxide.
AU AMAKAWA K

   BENDER M

GA 202110647E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing acrolein or acrylic acid as target                product involves continuously passing a stream of a                starting reaction gas mixture consisting of 2-15                vol.% propene, 4-37.5 vol.% molecular oxygen,                0.5-20 vol.% water, 0-20 vol.% molecular nitrogen,                0.5-30 vol.% carbon monoxide, 1-65 vol.% carbon                dioxide, 0-80 vol.% hydrocarbon selected from                methane, ethane, propane, n-butane, isobutane and                ethene, and 0-5 vol.% other compounds including                acrolein or acrylic acid.
   USE - Method for producing acrolein or acrylic acid                as target product continuously from propene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the acrolein or acrylic                acid as target product continuously from propene in                a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner                with improved carbon dioxide balance without                causing/involving significant economic                disadvantages regarding its generation or using,                and enables both high overall propene conversions                and high resulting yields of target product (e.g.                acrylic acid).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing acrolein or acrylic acid as target                product involves continuously passing a stream of a                starting reaction gas mixture consisting of 2-15                vol.% propene, 4-37.5 vol.% molecular oxygen,                0.5-20 vol.% water, 0-20 vol.% molecular nitrogen,                0.5-30 vol.% carbon monoxide, 1-65 vol.% carbon                dioxide, 0-80 vol.% hydrocarbon selected from                methane, ethane, propane, n-butane, isobutane and                ethene, and 0-5 vol.% other compounds including                acrolein or acrylic acid. The molecular oxygen and                the molecular nitrogen are provided in a molar                ratio of oxygen and nitrogen of 0.5 250-500 degrees                C. The fixed catalyst bed comprises shaped catalyst                bodies whose active mass is multimetal oxide                containing molybdenum, bismuth and iron. The                propene conversion comprises 80-99 mol% for                accompanying selectivity of the formation of the                target product acrolein to obtain a stream of a the                target product acrolein containing product gas                mixture. The temperature of the product gas mixture                is optionally reduced. The product gas mixture is                added in the form of molecular oxy gen, inert gas,                or molecular oxygen and inert gas. The stream of                the thus resulting starting reaction gas mixture is                continuously passed in another reaction stage                consisting of 2-15 vol.% acrolein, 1-45 vol.%                molecular oxygen, 2-30 vol.% water, 0-20 vol.%                molecular nitrogen, 0.5-30 vol.% carbon monoxide,                1-65 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0-80 vol.% hydrocarbon                selected from methane, ethane, propane, n-butane,                isobutane and ethene, and 0-5 vol.% other compounds                including propene or acrylic acid. The stream of                the target product acrolein containing product gas                mixture or the stream of the target product acrylic                acid containing product gas mixture is continuously                transported into a separating zone. The residual                gas mixture is continuously conducted out of the                separating zone. The residual gas mixture comprises                0.01-1 vol.% propene, 1-10 vol.% molecular oxygen,                0.5-25 vol.% water, 0-20 vol.% molecular nitrogen,                0.5-40 vol.% carbon monoxide, 1-75 vol.% carbon                dioxide, 0-92 vol.% hydrocarbon selected from                methane, ethane, propane, n-butane, isobutane and                eth- ene, and 0.01-5 vol.% other compounds                including acrolein or acrylic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of the method for producing acrolein or acrylic                acid as target product continuously from                propene.
   Optional steam stream (1)
   Hydrocarbon stream (2)
   Propene feedstock stream (3)
   Molecular oxygen source (4)
   Starting reaction gas mixture (5)
   Reaction stage (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The fixed catalyst bed                  of the former reaction stage comprises catalyst                  bodies whose active mass is multimetal oxide of                  general formula Mo12BiaFebX1cX2dX3eX4fOn.X1=nickel or cobalt;X2=thallium, an alkali metal or an alkaline                  earth metal;X3=zinc, phosphorus, arsenic, boron,                  antimony, tin, cerium, lead or tungsten;X4=silicon, aluminum, titanium and/or                  zirconium;a=0.5-5;b=2-4;c=3-10;d=0.02-2;e=0-5;f=0-10; andn=a number which is determined by valency                  and frequency of the elements other than                  oxygen.

PT P
PN EP4011824-A1; WO2022122523-A1
TI Method for reversible chemical storage of            hydrogen, comprises contacting hydrogen with carbon            dioxide and alcohol in presence of hydrogenation            catalyst to form alkyl formate, and dehydrogenating            into hydrogen and carbon dioxide.
AU SPONHOLZ P
   RYABCHUK P
   AGAPOVA A
   SANG R
   JACKSTELL R
   JUNGE H
   BELLER M
AE APEX ENERGY TETEROW GMBH (APEX-Non-standard)
   APEX ENERGY TETEROW GMBH (APEX-Non-standard)
GA 202279438E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reversible chemical storage of                hydrogen, comprises: (1) contacting hydrogen with                carbon dioxide and a 1-18C alcohol in presence of                hydrogenation catalyst to effect storage of                hydrogen by forming an 1-18C alkyl formate; and (2)                converting 1-18C alkyl formate into hydrogen and                carbon dioxide in presence of water, a base and (a)                a transition-metal hydride catalyst or its                precursor, the transition-metal hydride catalyst                comprising a metal-ion comprising ruthenium,                iridium, manganese, cobalt, molybdenum, iron and                rhodium, at least one hydride ligand coordinated to                the metal-ion, and (i) a tridentate ligand                coordinated to the metal-ion and comprising a                phosphor-nitrogen-phosphor (PNP)-chelating ligand,                a phosphor-nitrogen-nitrogen (PNN)-chelating                ligand, a phosphor-carbon-phosphor (PCP)-chelating                ligand, an nitrogen-carbon-nitrogen (NCN)-chelating                ligand, a phosphor-carbon-nitrogen (PCN)-chelating                ligand.
   USE - The method is useful for method for reversible                chemical storage of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: ensures storage of hydrogen having                less toxicity; and reduces carbon monoxide                formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for reversible chemical storage of                hydrogen, comprises: (1) contacting hydrogen with                carbon dioxide and a 1-18C alcohol in presence of                hydrogenation catalyst to effect storage of                hydrogen by forming an 1-18C alkyl formate; and (2)                dehydrogenating by converting 1-18C alkyl formate                into hydrogen and carbon dioxide in presence of                water, a base and (a) a transition-metal hydride                catalyst or its precursor, the transition-metal                hydride catalyst comprising a metal-ion comprising                ruthenium, iridium, manganese, cobalt, molybdenum,                iron and rhodium, at least one hydride ligand                coordinated to the metal-ion, and (i) a tridentate                ligand coordinated to the metal-ion and comprising                a phosphor-nitrogen-phosphor (PNP)-chelating                ligand, a phosphor-nitrogen-nitrogen                (PNN)-chelating ligand, a phosphor-carbon-phosphor                (PCP)-chelating ligand, an nitrogen-carbon-nitrogen                (NCN)-chelating ligand, a phosphor-carbon-nitrogen                (PCN)-chelating ligand, a                phosphor-phosphor-phosphor (PPP)-chelating ligand                and an nitrogen-nitrogen-nitrogen (NNN)-chelating                ligand, or (ii) a bidentate ligand coordinated to                the metal-ion and selected from a phosphor-phosphor                (PP)-chelating ligand or an nitrogen-nitrogen                (NN)-chelating ligand, or (iii) a tetradentate                phosphor-phosphor-phosphor-phosphor                (PPPP)-chelating ligand coordinated to the                metal-ion, or (b) a heterogeneous transition-metal                catalyst. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                reversible chemical storage system for hydrogen                construed for performing the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method: the                  1-18C alkyl formate is 1-6C alkyl formate and the                  1-18C alcohol is 1-6C alcohol; the metal-ion                  comprises ruthenium, iridium, iron and manganese;                  and the tridentate ligand of the transition-metal                  hydride catalyst is a PNP-chelating ligand of                  formula (I).Q1, Q2 = 2-6C alkylene group or 5-7C                  cycloalkylene group;R1, R2, R3, R4 = 1-6C alkyl group, 3-7C                  cycloalkyl group, 6-14C aryl group, 2-9C                  heteroaryl group or benzyl; andR5 = H, 1-8C alkyl group or 3-7C cycloalkyl                  group.In the method: the bidentate ligand is                  1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane; the                  transition-metal hydride catalyst comprises 8                  components which are e.g.                  bis(2-(diisopropylphosphino)ethyl)amine-carbonyl                  ruthenium chloride complex,                  bis(2-(diphenylphosphino)ethyl)amine-carbonyl                  ruthenium chloride complex,                  bis(2-(diisopropylphosphino)ethyl)amine-irridium                  chloride complex,                  bis(2-(ditert-butylphosphino)ethyl)amine-carbonyl                  ruthenium chloride complex and                  2-(diisopropylphosphino)-N-(2-(diisopropylphosphino)ethyl)-N-methylethanamine-carbonyl                  ruthenium chloride complex; the precursor of the                  transition-metal hydride catalyst is                  bis(2-(diisopropylphosphino)ethyl)amine-dicarbonyl                  manganese bromide complex,                  2-(4,5-dihydro-1H-imidazol-2-yl)pyridine-tricarbonyl                  manganese bromide complex, iron(II)                  tetrafluoroborate hexahydrate                  tris(2-(diphenylphosphino)ethyl)phosphine,                  ruthenium                  dichloride-bis(benzene)-1,2-bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane                  and ruthenium                  dichloride-bis(benzene)-((phenylphosphinediyl)bis(ethane-2,1-diyl))bis(diphenylphosphine);                  the content of transition-metal hydride catalyst                  or precursor is 0.001-0.01 mol.% based on amount                  of the 1-18C alkyl formate; the heterogeneous                  transition-metal catalyst comprises at least one                  of a metal comprising ruthenium, palladium,                  platinum and cobalt; the base comprises potassium                  hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, cesium hydroxide,                  lithium hydroxide and potassium phosphate; the                  content of base is 5-35 mol.% based on amount of                  the 1-18C alkyl formate; the content of water is                  1.5-2.5 equivalents per mol 1-18C alkyl formate;                  the hydrogenation catalyst corresponds to the                  transition-metal hydride catalyst or to the                  heterogeneous transition-metal catalyst Preferred                  Method: In the method, the dehydrogenation                  conditions comprise a reaction temperature is                  20-150 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                demonstrates the necessity of applying a base,                demonstrates the increase of hydrogen evolution                rate by increasing the temperature that do not                support the claims.
