PT P
PN CN113402854-A
TI Method for modifying plastic by capturing carbon dioxide product by using nanofiltration, involves mixing micron-sized magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture, plastic, alkali-soluble polyester, dispersant, and antioxidant, extruding, dissolving, separating, and drying.
AU WANG R
   WANG Z
   CAO S
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B08443
AB    NOVELTY - The method for modifying plastic by capturing carbon dioxide product by using nanofiltration, involves (1) crushing magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, which are recovered products of carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltered seawater using a crusher, using a Raylemill to grind, grinding recovered product magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate by wet grinding, where the size is 1-100 microns, and drying wet-ground magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate to obtain mixture of micron-sized magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, (2) mixing micron-sized magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture obtained in step (1), plastic, alkali-soluble polyester, dispersant, and antioxidant, and extruding in a twin-screw extruder to obtain modified plastic containing alkali-soluble polyester, and (3) dissolving the obtained modified plastic containing alkali-soluble polyester in sodium hydroxide solution to remove alkali-soluble polyester, separating, and drying.
   USE - Method for modifying plastic by capturing carbon dioxide product by using nanofiltration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively controls the void distribution of the modified plastic, and improves the mechanical property of the porous modified plastic.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for modifying plastic by capturing carbon dioxide product by using nanofiltration, involves (1) crushing magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, which are recovered products of carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltered seawater to size of 1-10 mm using a crusher, using a Raylemill to grind to size of 0.2-1 mm, grinding recovered product magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate to size of 0.2-1 mm by wet grinding, where the size is 1-100 microns, and drying wet-ground magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate to obtain mixture of micron-sized magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, (2) mixing 2.4-6.5 parts mass micron-sized magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate mixture obtained in step (1), 81-96 parts mass plastic, 1-10 parts mass alkali-soluble polyester, 0.5-2 parts mass dispersant, and 0.1-0.7 part mass antioxidant, and extruding in a twin-screw extruder to obtain modified plastic containing alkali-soluble polyester, and (3) dissolving the obtained modified plastic containing alkali-soluble polyester in sodium hydroxide solution with concentration of 0.04-1.05 %mass to remove alkali-soluble polyester, separating, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate, which are recovered products of carbon dioxide captured by nanofiltration seawater, are specifically obtained by (A) adding sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate to adjust the pH value of the waste water to 7-9, taking the processed product waste water as reference, continuously adding 0.05-2 %mass basic polyaluminum chloride, anionic polyacrylamide with concentration of 0.5-100 ppm and molecular weight of 10-18 million, 0.05-0.1 %mass sodium methylene dinaphthalene sulfonate, until large flocculant calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate are formed in the product wastewater containing nanofiltration seawater to capture carbon dioxide, reducing the product waste water temperature to 5-10 degrees C, accelerating the sedimentation of calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, discharging the waste water of the precipitated calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate into a concentrating tank through a pipeline, pressing, and drying the calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate by a filter press to obtain block-shaped calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, (B) mechanically cutting the block-shaped calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate obtained in step (A), and crushing into small blocks of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate by a crusher, and (C) conveying the small blocks of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate obtained in the step (C) to a thermal infrared drying and heating cavity by a material transmission system for thermal infrared drying to obtain a finished product. Preferred Composition: In the wet grinding method in step (1), the ball-to-material ratio is (10-16):1. The moisture content of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate obtained by drying is 0.5-5%. Preferred Component: In the step (1), the ball material is alumina and/or zirconia. Preferred Conditions: In the step (1), the ball milling time is 6-10 hours, and the ball milling temperature is less than 60 degrees C. In the step (C), the residence time of the small pieces of magnesium carbonate and calcium carbonate in the thermal infrared drying heating chamber is 0.1-0.5 hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The modified plastic has a porosity of 30-95%, an air permeability of 100-1000 s/100ml, and a pore size of 0.2-0.9 mu m.

PT P
PN CN112107502-A
TI Preparing hydrolyzed oat protein additive used in skin moisturizing cosmetics by pretreating oat bran, mixing with water and enzymes, homogenizing, centrifuging, freeze-drying supernatant and dispersing with sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate.
AU ZENG J
   CHEN Q
   SI W
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2020C9921B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing hydrolyzed oat protein additive involves pretreating oat bran using supercritical carbon dioxide, then mixing with distilled water to form oat bran paste, adding enzymes, adjusting pH value, treating with an auxiliary pulse electromagnetic field under homogenization to obtain hydrolyzed oat protein paste, centrifuging, allowing the supernatant to stand, freeze-drying to obtain hydrolyzed oat protein and then uniformly dispersing with sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hydrolyzed oat protein additive which is used in skin moisturizing cosmetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to control, efficiently obtains small molecular weight oat hydrolyzed protein powder and prepares hydrolyzed oat protein additive with narrow molecular weight distribution, good moisturizing effect and stable quality. The hydrolyzed oat protein additive is easily absorbed by skin when used for moisturizing cosmetics. The sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate is used to assist in improving the moisturizing properties of oat hydrolyzed protein.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing hydrolyzed oat protein additive involves pretreating oat bran using supercritical carbon dioxide, then mixing with distilled water to form oat bran paste, adding enzymes, adjusting pH value, treating with an auxiliary pulse electromagnetic field under homogenization to obtain hydrolyzed oat protein paste, centrifuging, allowing the supernatant to stand, freeze-drying to obtain hydrolyzed oat protein and then uniformly dispersing with sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate, by (a) transferring the oat bran into a pressure vessel, then passing in carbon dioxide to turn the carbon dioxide into a supercritical state, using the supercritical carbon dioxide to process the oat bran and allowing the supercritical carbon dioxide to fully penetrate the oat bran and promote cracking to obtain pretreated oat bran, (b) mixing the obtained pretreated oat bran with distilled water uniformly to form an oat bran paste, then placing in a sealed container, transferring to a mixing equipment, adding enzymes, mixing, then adjusting the pH value, then sending to the homogenizer for homogenization treatment, where the homogenization valve is set with pulse electromagnetic field, and obtaining hydrolyzed oat protein paste through auxiliary pulse electromagnetic field under the homogenization effect, (c) centrifuging the obtained hydrolyzed oat protein paste, then collecting the supernatant, sending to a stabilization chamber for static treatment and then freeze-drying to obtain hydrolyzed oat protein, and (d) dispersing the obtained hydrolyzed oat protein and sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate evenly to obtain product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The temperature and pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide in step (a) exceeds the critical temperature of 31.1 degrees C and exceeds 7.39 MPa, respectively. The treatment time is controlled at 5-15 minutes. In the preparation of oat bran paste in step (b), the mass ratio of pretreated oat bran to distilled water is 1-2:10-20. The temperature of the sealed placement in step (b) is controlled at 1-4 degrees C for 24-28 hours. In step (b), the enzyme is Alcalase (RTM: Subtilisin) which is added in an amount of 0.5-0.8 %mass into the oat bran. In step (b), the pH is adjusted to 8.5-9 using 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution. The temperature of the homogenization treatment in step (b) is controlled at 65-70 degrees C, the pressure is controlled at 10-15 MPa, and the homogenization treatment is 20-35 minutes. The pulse electromagnetic field uses a frequency of 30-50 kHz. The centrifugation in step (c) is performed at rotation speed of 8000-12000 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 10-15 minutes. The temperature of the standing treatment in step (c) is performed at 85-95 degrees C for 20-25 minutes. In the step (d), the mass ratio of hydrolyzed oat protein to sodium pyrrolidone carboxylate is 1-2:3-6.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)

PN CN111715031-A; CN111715031-B
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide absorption medium            involves soaking anion exchange resin in absolute            ethanol, drying, adding to manganese nitrate solution,            performing ion exchange, stirring with glycine and            citric acid, and evaporating.
AU LI L
   LIU X
   CHEN P
   WANG C
   CHEN W
   DI J
AE UNIV JIANGXI NORMAL (UYJX-C)
GA 202098398T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide absorption                medium involves soaking anion exchange resin in                absolute ethanol and saturated sodium chloride                solution, rinsing with deionized water, drying at                40 degrees C or less, adding dried anion exchange                resin to manganese nitrate and cerium nitrate                solution, adjusting pH, stirring for more than 5                hours, adding hydrogen peroxide, leaving still,                filtering, washing solid phase with deionized water                2-3 times, drying at 40 degrees C or less to obtain                solid phase (A), soaking solid phase (A) in sodium                hydroxide solution, leaving still for more than 4                hours, separating solid and liquid, washing solid                phase with deionized water 2-3 times, placing in                ion exchange column, adding 1-aminopropyl-3                methylimidazolium bromide aqueous solution for ion                exchange to obtain solution (B), adding glycine and                citric acid to solution (B), stirring for more than                18 hours at room temperature, evaporating to remove                aqueous phase, and extracting and removing                remaining glycine and citric acid.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide absorption                medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides carbon dioxide absorption                medium with excellent stability, rapid carbon                dioxide absorption speed, and high capture                rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon dioxide absorption                medium involves soaking anion exchange resin in                absolute ethanol and saturated sodium chloride                solution for 5 minutes at room temperature, and                hydrochloric acid-oxalic acid solution for more                than 20 minutes, rinsing with deionized water 2-3                times, drying at 40 degrees C or less, adding dried                anion exchange resin to manganese nitrate and                cerium nitrate solution, adjusting pH to 1-3 with                dilute hydrochloric acid, stirring for more than 5                hours, adding hydrogen peroxide solution, leaving                still for more than 20 hours, filtering, washing                solid phase with deionized water 2-3 times, drying                at 40 degrees C or less to obtain solid phase (A),                soaking solid phase (A) in sodium hydroxide                solution, leaving still for more than 4 hours,                separating solid and liquid, washing solid phase                with deionized water 2-3 times, placing in ion                exchange column, adding 1-aminopropyl-3                methylimidazolium bromide aqueous solution for ion                exchange to obtain solution (B), adding glycine and                citric acid to solution (B), stirring for more than                18 hours at room temperature, evaporating to remove                aqueous phase, and extracting and removing                remaining glycine and citric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The modified titanium                  dioxide is prepared using titanium dioxide                  powder, hydrofluoric acid solution, deionized                  water, 5% hydrogen peroxide solution, 10% aqueous                  ammonia solution, and 1-2 g/100 mL ammonium                  chloride. The hydrofluoric acid solution                  comprises 5% hydrofluoric acid, and balance                  water. The manganese nitrate-cerium nitrate                  solution comprises 30-50 g/500 mL manganese                  nitrate, 14-22 g/500 mL cerium nitrate, and                  balance water. The hydrogen peroxide solution                  comprises 20 %mass hydrogen peroxide, and balance                  water. The amount of hydrogen peroxide is                  (1/10)-(1/9) of mass of manganese nitrate-cerium                  nitrate solution. The sodium hydroxide solution                  comprises 10-16% sodium hydroxide, and balance                  water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The optimized                  preparation method involves using modified                  titanium dioxide to be mixed with solid phase (A)                  before being placed in ion exchange column.                  Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of solid                  phase (A) and modified titanium dioxide is                  10:(2-4). The hydrochloric acid-oxalic acid                  solution comprises 5 %mass hydrochloric acid, 3                  %mass oxalic acid, and balance water. The mass                  ratio of anion exchange resin to manganese                  nitrate-cerium nitrate solution is 1/4. The                  aqueous solution of                  1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide                  comprises 10-20%                  1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, and                  balance water. The mass of the aqueous solution                  of 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide                  added to the ion exchange column is 2-3 times the                  mass of the solid phase in the ion exchange                  column. The amount of glycine is 10-20 g/100 mL                  solution (B), and citric acid is 3-8 g/100 mL                  solution (B).
DC A91 (Ion-exchange resins, polyelectrolytes.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN KR2020068948-A; KR2274373-B1
TI Method for cooling injection mold, involves cooling molten resin in injection mold is cooled by supplying carbon dioxide to capillary tube installed in cooling pipe for predetermined time to cool injection molding product.
AU CHOI J H
AE UNIV GWANGJU IND ACADEMIC COOP (UYGW-Non-standard)
GA 202054379U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves processing (S3) a cooling hole of a predetermined depth toward an inner surface of a cavity on a rear surface of a mold at a position corresponding to an center point of Voronoi polygons. A carbon dioxide (CO2) supply device is provided in a processed cooling hole and automatically receives carbon dioxide according to the set program of the computer. A capillary tube is installed (S4) inside the cooling hole to spray the carbon dioxide toward the inner wall of the cooling hole. A molten resin is injected (S5) through an injection device into a cavity of an injection mold in which a capillary tube is installed. The molten resin in the injection mold is cooled (S6) by supplying carbon dioxide to the capillary tube installed in the cooling pipe for a predetermined time through control of a program which is set to cool the injection mold injected with the molten resin to cool the injection molding product.
   USE - Method for cooling injection mold.
   ADVANTAGE - The cooling pipe installed in the mold is not arbitrarily installed, and the surface of the product is divided into a predetermined area by using centroidal voronoi diagram (CVD), and cooling pipe is arranged in the center of the area to rapidly cool the molten resin inside the mold while minimizing the temperature deviation of the product, thus increasing the productivity of the injection molded product and producing a high quality query product. The cooling time of the mold can be shortened by spraying carbon dioxide to cool the mold and cooling the mold at a cryogenic temperature. The mold cooling is performed while reducing the total temperature deviation based on the temperature sensed by the temperature sensor installed in the mold when cooling the mold, thus further increasing the completion of the injection molded product through uniform cooling of the injection molded product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for cooling injection mold. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for extracting center point position of Voronoi polygon region (S2)
   Step for processing cooling hole of predetermined depth toward inner surface of cavity (S3)
   Step for installing capillary tube inside cooling hole (S4)
   Step for injecting molten resin through injection device (S5)
   Step for cooling molten resin in injection mold (S6)

PN US2019314251-A1
TI Constructing bioactive compositions including liposome or elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide, used to treat skin anomalies comprises e.g. forming benevolent phospholipids solution.
AU RICHARD D A
AE PPP & C INC DBA ROBIN MCGRAW REVELATION (PPPC-Non-standard)
GA 201986362E
AB    NOVELTY - Construction of cosmeceutically bioactive compositions including liposome or elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide and in a continuously operating process comprises forming a solution or mixture of cosmeceutically benevolent phospholipids, where the solution being a hydrophobic or hydrophilic, natural or synthetic ingredient, with an aqueous phase, removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve the activity of the labile biomolecules, loading the solution of the membrane lipid or niosome components and desired hydrophobic bio-actives into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor, and depressurizing the resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates.
   USE - The method is useful for construction of cosmeceutically bioactive compositions (claimed) which are used for the treatment of skin anomalies created as a result of aging skin or chronic environmental. Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides highly optimized chemical formulations, provides a significant permeability barrier to both small molecules and macromolecules that becomes the rate limiting step; allows large scale production of pharmaceutical grade liposomes which are sterile, of a predetermined size, and are substantially free of organic solvents; provides the liposomes and elastic niosomes using low pressure fluids; and does not subject botanicals, proteins and peptides to extreme shear forces or temperatures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Construction of cosmeceutically bioactive compositions including liposome or elastic niosome vesicle epidermal delivery vehicles without use of supercritical carbon dioxide and in a continuously operating process comprises: forming a solution or mixture of cosmeceutically benevolent phospholipids, where the solution being a hydrophobic or hydrophilic, natural or synthetic ingredient, with an aqueous phase, removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve the activity of the labile biomolecules, loading the solution of the membrane lipid or niosome components and desired hydrophobic bio-actives into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor, where the pressure reactor has been previously driven to a pre-determined working temperature, pressurizing the reactor with compressed carbon dioxide until reaching a pre-determined working pressure, and depressurizing the resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates, where the resulting solution contains water soluble cationic or non-ionic surfactants and hydrophilic or hydrophobic bio-actives.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes an amphiphilic liposome where at least 80 wt.% of an active agent is disposed inside the liposomes. The method includes a cosmeceutical composition comprising active agent-loaded amphiphilic liposomes and a vehicle, and using amphiphilic liposomes in vitro, in vivo or ex-vivo transfection of cells. Preferred Components: The lipid is a nonionic amphiphilic lipid, an ionic amphiphilic lipid, or a mixture of a nonionic lipid and an ionic lipid. The membrane lipid is a membrane phospholipid of the phosphate group phosphatidylcholine (PC) amphiphilic molecules, comprising a polar head group and a lipophilic tail, and is esterified with an additional alcohol of choline possessing a zwitterionic isoelectric point and are negatively charged at higher pH values and positively charged at lower pH values. The membrane lipid is a membrane phospholipid of the phosphate group phosphatidylethanolarnine (PE), and is esterified with an additional alcohol of ethanolamine and is a neutral charged zwitterionic. The membrane lipid is a membrane phospholipid of the phosphate group phosphatidylglycerol (PG) with glycerol being anionic in charge. The membrane lipid is a membrane phospholipid of the phosphate group consisting of a ceramide unit with a phosphorylcholine moiety attached, and a sphingolipid analogue of phosphatidylchol, or sphingomyelin (or ceramide phosphorylcholine). The membrane lipid is an amphiphilic liposome where the molar ratio of the mixture of cholesterol and at least one neutral or zwitterionic lipid is 8-0.15. The membrane lipid includes at least one non-ionic amphiphilic lipid is an ester of at least one polyol, the ester being a polyethylene glycol containing 0.5-70 ethylene oxide units, sorbitan, glycerol containing from 1-35 ethylene oxide units, polyglycerol containing 1-18 glycerol units, or a fatty acid containing at least one saturated or unsaturated, linear or branched, 8-22C alkyl chain, where at least one cationic amphiphilic lipid is present at 0.5-65 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the amphiphilic lipid phase, an emulsion further comprises a water-soluble or fat-soluble cosmetic or cosmeceutical active ingredient, a formation of unilamellar vesicles is comprised of one lipid bilayer between 50-250 nm enclosing a large aqueous core, and multilamellar vesicles comprised of at least two concentric lipid bilayers between 1-5 mu m, cholesterol is used to anchor phosphate group PC amphiphilic molecules, the phosphate group PE molecules, and the phosphate group PG molecules, an amphiphilic liposome is comprised of neutral lipids where the neutral lipids are cholesterol or mixtures of cholesterol or at least one neutral or zwitterionic lipid, an amphiphilic liposome includes phosphatidylcholine comprsing 14:0 PC, 16:0 PC, 18:0 PC, 16:0-18:1 PC, 18:1c9 PC, soy bean PC or egg PC, an amphiphilic liposome includes K (neutral) of a mixture of cholesterol and at least one neutral or zwitterionic lipids is less than 0.45, and less than 0.2 and less than 0.1, an amphiphilic liposome includes a mixture of cholesterol and at least one neutral or zwitterionic lipid consisting of cholesterol/phosphatidylcholine, cholesterol/phosphatidylethanolamine or cholesterol/phosphatidylethanolamine/phosphatidylcholine, and an amphiphilic liposome includes a mixture of lipid components with amphiphilic properties, where the mixture of lipid components comprises at least one pH responsive component, and an amphiphilic liposome encapsulates at least one active agent. The liposomes comprise non-encapsulated active agents.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109989794-A; CN109989794-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired power generation system heats primary air in primary air preheater by exhaust steam splitting portion of low pressure turbine working medium and carries coal powder into boiler furnace for combustion.
AU LIU M
   YANG K
   ZHANG X
   YAN J
   XING Q
   CHONG D
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201962011V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a secondary air preheater (16) whose air inlet is connected to an environment, and air outlet is connected to a secondary air inlet of a boiler. An exit of a low pressure turbine (6) heats up in a high temperature regenerator (4), an intermediate temperature regenerator (3) and a low temperature regenerator (2). An exit of low temperature working fluid of a main compressor (1) heats in the low temperature regenerator, the intermediate temperature regenerator and the high temperature regenerator. An outlet splitting low temperature working medium of low temperature regenerator is passed through an intermediate temperature flue gas heat exchanger (13). The primary air is heated in a primary air preheater (15) by an exhaust steam splitting portion of the working medium of low pressure turbine and the coal powder is carried into a boiler furnace for combustion. The secondary air absorbs heat in the secondary air preheater and enters the boiler furnace to assist combustion.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired power generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The portion of the low temperature supercritical carbon dioxide working medium is diverted from the outlet of the low temperature regenerator into a tail portion of the boiler, and the medium temperature flue gas heat exchanger absorbs the heat of the medium temperature flue gas. The portion of the low-temperature supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid is diverted from the main compressor outlet into the boiler tail flue low-temperature flue gas heat exchanger to absorb the low-temperature flue gas heat so as to reduce the boiler exhaust temperature, achieve the cascade utilization of energy, and improve boiler efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An outlet splitting low temperature working medium of main compressor is passed through a low temperature flue gas heat exchanger (14) and returns to an inlet of intermediate temperature regenerator. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an operation method of the supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired power generation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the supercritical carbon dioxide coal-fired power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Main compressor (1)
   Low temperature regenerator (2)
   Intermediate temperature regenerator (3)
   High temperature regenerator (4)
   Low pressure turbine (6)
   Intermediate temperature flue gas heat exchanger (13)
   Low temperature flue gas heat exchanger (14)
   Primary air preheater (15)

TI Method for reducing dust for blasting shock            blisters, involves loading water into water bag,            placing foaming agent in water bag, followed by            utilizing high-temperature and pressure, utilizing air            bubbles and water mist to reduce dust.
AU YANG S
   AI Z
   HOU S
   JIANG C
   ZHANG G
   LI Y
AE POWERCHINA ROAD BRIDGE GROUP CO LTD (CNPW-C)
GA 2019160473
AB    NOVELTY - A dust reducing method involves loading water                into a water bag, the ratio of the volume of the                water, placing a foaming agent in a water bag, and                containing the foaming agent in an amount of                0.2-0.5 g per liter of water, charging carbon                dioxide gas or carbonic acid into the water bag and                sealing the water bag, and the air pressure in the                water bag after sealing, installing the water bag                at the end of the explosive column in the blasthole                and block the blasthole with the gun mud,                detonating explosives with detonators, utilizing                the heat and vibration generated by the explosion                to rapidly decompose the carbonic acid in the water                bag to generate a large amount of carbon dioxide                gas, and utilizing carbon dioxide gas to generate a                large number of bubbles in the water bag, utilizing                the high-temperature and pressure gas generated by                the explosion to burst the water bag, and utilizing                the air bubbles and water mist diffused around the                water bag to reduce dust.
   USE - Method for reducing dust for blasting shock                blisters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A dust reducing method involves loading water                into a water bag, the ratio of the volume of the                water to the volume of the water bag is: 0.6-0.7,                placing a foaming agent in a water bag, and                containing the foaming agent in an amount of                0.2-0.5 g per liter of water, charging carbon                dioxide gas or carbonic acid into the water bag at                25 degrees C and sealing the water bag, and the air                pressure in the water bag after sealing is 0.5-0.6                MP, installing the water bag at the end of the                explosive column in the blasthole and block the                blasthole with the gun mud, detonating explosives                with detonators, utilizing the heat and vibration                generated by the explosion to rapidly decompose the                carbonic acid in the water bag to generate a large                amount of carbon dioxide gas, and utilizing carbon                dioxide gas to generate a large number of bubbles                in the water bag, utilizing the high-temperature                and high-pressure gas generated by the explosion to                burst the water bag, and utilizing the air bubbles                and water mist diffused around the water bag to                reduce dust.

PN CN107903576-A
TI Battery separator comprises e.g. EW-200 glass fiber plain cloth, phenolic resin, coupling agent, heat stabilizer, anti-chemical modifying agent, barium titanate powder and core-shell acrylic acid elastic emulsion.
AU ZHOU D
AE NINGBO PENGHUI MICROPORE TECHNOLOGY CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018300812
AB    NOVELTY - Battery separator comprises 64.2-65 pts. wt. EW-200 glass fiber plain cloth, 29.8-30.2 pts. wt. phenolic resin, 0.1-0.7 pts. wt. coupling agent, 5-15 pts. wt. heat stabilizer, 0.5-5 pts. wt. anti-chemical modifying agent, 4.6-5.8 pts. wt. barium titanate powder, 2-6 pts. wt. flame retardant synergist, 5-15 pts. wt. bromine flame retardant and 3-6 pts. wt. core-shell acrylic acid elastic emulsion.
   USE - Used as battery separator.
   ADVANTAGE - The separator has light weight, high intensity, high temperature resistance, corrosion resistance, and has good flame resistance and toughness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing battery separator, comprising (i) respectively taking each component, (ii) mixing the barium titanate and 95% of ethanol solvent, and ultrasonic for 2 hours, (iii) adding the coupling agent into mixed solution with ultrasonic obtained in the step (ii) and heating to 70 degrees C and heat preserving for 2 hours, heating while stirring, (iv) drying the mixed solution obtained in the step (iii) to obtain the surface-modified barium titanate powder, (v) adding heat stabilizer, anti-chemical modifying agent, flame retardants synergist, bromine flame retardants, core-shell acrylic elastic emulsion and barium titanate powder obtained in the step (iv) into a double-screw extruder, controlling the rotation speed of the screw is 180-600 revolutions/minutes, placing the liquid carbon dioxide into conveying pump, where the carbon dioxide delivery pressure can be controlled at any constant pressure in the range of the critical pressure 7.4-50 MPa by the pressure set by the pressure valve to deliver carbon dioxide, heating the carbon dioxide the critical temperature (at 31 degrees C) or higher temperature to convert it into supercritical carbon dioxide, and then injecting the supercritical carbon dioxide are in the extruder three-and fiveregion, carrying out melting, extruding and crushing to obtain mixture powder, (vi) dissolving mixture powder obtained in the step (v) into the phenolic resin in ethanol solvent, mixing uniformly, (vii) adding the EW-200 glass fiber plain cloth into barium phenolic resin obtained in the step (vi) solvent to obtain the prepreg and (viii) layering the prepreg obtained in the step (vii) and solidifying to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The bromine flame retardant is tetrabromobisphenol A, decabromodiphenylethane, 2,4,6-tribromotriphenyloxy-1,3,5-triazine, ethylene-(double tetrabromophthalimide), brominated epoxy, or (2-ethylhexyl) tetrabromophthalate. The flame retardant synergist is zinc borate, antimony trioxide, antimony pentoxide, sodium antimonate and molybdenum oxide. The chemical modifier is perfluoroalkyl containing acrylic additive. The heat stabilizer is rare-earth heat stabilizer and organic tin heat stabilizer. The double-screw extruder comprises 10 zones, the temperature of the temperature control zone 1-2 is 180-260 degrees C, the temperature of the temperature control zone 3-4 is 180-260 degrees C, and the temperature control zone 5-6 The temperature is 180-260 degrees C, the temperature in the temperature controlled 7-8 zone is 180-260 degrees C, the temperature in the temperature controlled 9-10 zone is 180-260 degrees C, and the pressure in each zone is 2-35MPa. In the step (iii), the supercritical carbon dioxide flow speed is 1-50 ml/minutes. The double-screw extruder is provided with two vacuumizing, starting end of one end of the conveying section, melting section and the other one at the measuring section.

PT P
PN CN107755424-A; CN107755424-B
TI Improving plant extraction efficacy by using carbon dioxide enrichment and endophyte interaction comprises e.g. pre-culturing hyperaccumulator of Sedum alfredii in tap water, choosing seedling and immersing in endophyte bacteria inoculation.
AU LI T
   WU K
   LUO J
   LI J
   SONG Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201820069D
AB    NOVELTY - Improving plant extraction efficacy by using carbon dioxide enrichment and endophyte interaction comprises e.g. pre-culturing hyperaccumulators of Sedum alfredii in tap water, choosing a seedling, immersing in endophytic bacteria inoculation, where the endophytic bacteria complex bacterium includes Rhodococcus, Buttiauxella, Herbaspirillum, and four kinds of endophytes, where endophytes of Rhodococcus and Buttiauxella are derived from roots of hyperaccumulator Sedum alfredii and endogenous bacteria of Stenotrophomonas.
   USE - The method is useful for improving plant extraction efficacy by using carbon dioxide enrichment and endophyte interaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures to increase photosynthesis and biomass, and simultaneously applies endogenous bacteria to promote the absorption and accumulation of heavy metal cadmium in hyperaccumulatory plants, and solves the problems of slow plant growth and small biomass in plant extraction, and enhances super accumulation of the plant's environmental adaptability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving plant extraction efficacy by using carbon dioxide enrichment and endophyte interaction comprises (i) pre-culturing hyperaccumulators of Sedum alfredii in tap water, choosing a seedling with a root length of 1-2 cm, immersing in endophytic bacteria inoculation for 2 hours, where the endophytic bacteria complex bacterium includes Rhodococcus, Buttiauxella, Herbaspirillum, and four kinds of endophytes, where endophytes of Rhodococcus and Buttiauxella are derived from the roots of hyperaccumulator Sedum alfredii, endogenous bacteria of Stenotrophomonas and Herbaspirillum endophyte are derived from stems, and the endogenous bacteria complex bacterium liquid is made up of four endophytic bacteria liquids in a volume ratio of 1:1:1:1 and bacteria content in each bacterium is 109 CFU/m; (ii) transplanting the bacteria-treated seedlings of Sedum alfredii into a black plastic bucket containing a nutrient solution, where the cadmium concentration in nutrient solution is 50 mu M, adding endophyte complexes into the nutrient solution, where the bacterial concentration is 106 CFU/ml, placing the black plastic barrel into the plant growth box for cultivation, culturing a growth chamber and maintaining a carbon dioxide concentration to 800 mu l/l, changing nutrient solution for every 3 days, maintaining the concentration of bacteria in nutrient solution at 106 CFU/ml; and (iii) culturing by using carbon dioxide enrichment for 1 month, harvesting hyperaccumulator plant of Sedum alfredii and analyzing biomass and cadmium extraction content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the pre-culture is carried out by placing hyperaccumulator plant of Sedum alfredii in an artificial climate chamber, pre-incubating with tap water for 1 week, controlling incubation photo-period of 14 hours in light time, maintaining day/night temperature to 25/20 degrees C, where the relative humidity of the climate chamber is maintained to 70% during the day time and 85% at night time. The four kinds of endophyte mycelium is prepared by separately grinding the stems and roots of of hyperaccumulation plant Sedum alfredii, using DF-ACC-Cd solid medium to culture the slurry separately, picking colonies for scribing, purifying the four kinds of endophytes respectively, activating the four endophytes by using lysogeny broth liquid media, culturing each strain at 25-35 degrees C for 1-2 days, centrifuging the obtained bacterial solution at 10,000revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, washing the pellet and centrifuging with sterile phosphate buffer to remove the supernatant, continuously processing for 3 times, where the precipitated part is bacteria, adding sterile water, where the amount of bacteria in bacterial solution is 109 CFU/l, mixing the four bacterial liquids in a volume ratio of 1:1:1:1:1, adjusting the bacterial suspension OD600 to 1-1.2 with 0.85% sterile sodium chloride (NaCl) solution, i.e. endophytic bacteria, where the bacterial concentration is 109 CFU/ml. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the composition of nutrient solution comprises 0.7 mmol/l potassium sulfate, 0.1 mmol/l potassium chloride, 2 mmol/l calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, 0.5 mmol/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.1 mmol/l potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 10 mu ol/l boric acid, 0.5 mmol/l manganese(II) sulfate monohydrate, 0.1 mu mol/l zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.01 mu mol/l ammonium molybdate tetrahydrate, 20 mu mol/l ferric EDTA solution, where the pH of the nutrient solution is adjusted by using sodium hydroxide (NaOH) or hydrogen chloride (HCl). Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the control conditions for the plant growth box includes: humidity is 70%, day and night temperature is 25 degrees C and 20 degrees C, daylight illumination conditions includes: 100 mu mol.m2/second, treating under light for 14 hours a day.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

MC A12-W04A; P43-J

TI Preparing cyclodextrin-metal complex comprises adding hexamethylene tetramine and metal halide into cyclodextrin aqueous solution, stirring and reacting.

   HUANG F
   ZHU H
   OU Z

   SHEN J
   DENG R
AE HUNAN INST ENG (HNIE-C)
GA 202161984Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclodextrin-metal complex comprises adding hexamethylene tetramine and metal halide into cyclodextrin aqueous solution, stirring and reacting.
   USE - The complex is useful to catalyze the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide to prepare cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes raw materials, which are economical and does not changes complex structure after recycling for many times.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) cyclodextrin-metal complex prepared by the above mentioned method; and
   (2) use of complex to catalyze the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxide to prepare cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ions in the metal halide are zinc, calcium, magnesium, ferric ion or ferrous ion, and the halide ions are chlorine, bromine and/or iodine. The cyclodextrin is alpha -cyclodextrin, beta -cyclodextrin or gamma -cyclodextrin. The molar ratio of hexamethylenetetramine, metal halide and cyclodextrin is 1:1-5:0.2-5. In the cyclodextrin aqueous solution, the molar concentration of cyclodextrin is 0.01-0.02 mol/lo. The epoxide is ethylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, cyclohexene oxide, cyclopentene oxide, or styrene oxide. The molar ratio of the cyclodextrin-metal complex and epoxide is 0.005-0.05:1. Preferred Method: The stirring is carried out for 12-24 hours.

PN CN112321564-A
TI Extracting nicotine from waste tobacco involves using supercritical carbon dioxide as extractant for supercritically extracting waste tobacco raw material particles after drying, crushing, granulating, and particle drying.
AU LIU M
   PAN J
   SONG D
   FENG Y
   CAO Y
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 202117904W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting nicotine from waste tobacco, involves using supercritical carbon dioxide as extractant for supercritically extracting waste tobacco raw material particles after drying, crushing, granulating, and particle drying to obtain nicotine extract.
   USE - Method for extracting nicotine from waste tobacco.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes the ethanol solvent brought during granulation, does not allow the solvent to remain in the extract, does not require other operations, and is simple, safe, environmentally-friendly, suitable for industrial and scale production process and more in line with human pursuit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The granulation process involves adding 95% concentration alcohol solution for moistening crushed material, and granulating with 10-20 mesh sieve. Preferred Conditions: The method involves drying before crushing at 90-105 degrees C for 1.5-3 hours. The method involves drying the particles at 45-70 degrees C in constant temperature oven. The method involves performing supercritical extraction at 45-60 degrees C under extraction pressure of 32-40 MPa, and separating at 40-55 degrees C under separation pressure of 6-8 MPa. Preferred Composition: The addition amount of 95% alcohol during granulation is 20-40%.

TI Treating wastewater by treating wastewater with calcium hydroxide, binding formed hydroxides of heavy metals with calcium carbonate, introducing flocculant, separating precipitate, and acidifying clarified portion with carbon dioxide.
AU SHAMUKOV S I
   TIKHONOVA G G
   DESYATSKOVA E L
   TARASOVA A S
AE SREDNYAYA VOLGA LLC (SRED-Non-standard)
GA 202113109E
AB    NOVELTY - A wastewater is treated by treating wastewater with calcium hydroxide and binding the formed hydroxides of heavy metals with calcium carbonate, where the calcium carbonate is introduced in a mass ratio to calcium hydroxide of 1:4-8 in terms of dry matter, introducing flocculant when the pH is 10-11, separating precipitate, and acidifying the clarified portion of the aqueous system with carbon dioxide until the pH is 6.5-8.5.
   USE - Treatment of wastewater.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Natural limestone is used as a source of calcium carbonate. Highly polymerized anionic or cationic flocculants, or mixture in the form of 0.1% aqueous solution, are used as the flocculant. Preferred Process: Flocculant is additionally introduced into the aqueous system and the formed sediment is separated in the case of the presence of magnesium ions in the original waste water, after treatment with carbon dioxide.

PN KR2020134572-A; KR2235012-B1
TI New non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst for producing a cyclic carbonate.
AU YAVUZ C T
   SUBRAMANIAN S
   KIM D Y
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2020C1554C
AB    NOVELTY - Non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst (1) is new.
   USE - New non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst for producing a cyclic carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide can be converted into a cyclic carbonate with excellent selectivity and persistence without additives such as a solvent or a cocatalyst under normal pressure or high pressure conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst of formula (1) is new.
   n=5000-500000; and
   X=halo.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) producing a heterogeneous imidazoline-based organic catalyst; and
   (2) producing a cyclic carbonate which involves reacting epoxide and carbon dioxide using the non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): Heterogeneous imidazoline-based organic catalyst is produced by reacting terephthalaldehyde and ammonium chloride in the presence of a solvent. Preferred Components: Molar ratio of terephthalaldehyde and ammonium chloride is 1:1-3. The solvent is greater than or equal to 1 selected from dimethylformamide, 1,4-dioxane, 1,2-dichlorobenzene, methanol, acetone, chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, and methylene chloride. No co-catalyst is added. The epoxide is greater than or equal to 1 of epiclohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, 4-fluorostyrene oxide, glycidyl 2-methyl ether, propylene oxide, epoxyhexane, 1,2-epoxy-3-phenoxypropane, resorcinol diglycidyl ether, and cyclohexene oxide. Preferred Process: The method includes cooling the mixture to room temperature after the reaction, followed by filtering and drying the non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst. The reaction is carried out at atmospheric pressure and a temperature of 80-120 degrees C. Preferred Parameters: Reaction is performed at 150-155 degrees C for 24-72 hours. The selectivity of cyclic carbonate is greater than or equal to 92%.
   EXAMPLE - The non-uniform imidazoline-based organic catalyst was prepared by dissolving 3.727 mmol terephthalaldehyde and 14.899 mmol ammonium chloride in 15 mL anhydrous dimethylformamide, heating mixture to 150 degrees C while stirring, reacting for 48 hours, waiting the reaction for cooling to room temperature, filtering precipitate on a filter paper, washing in the order of water, methanol, acetone, tetrahydrofuran, and methylene chloride until the filtered solution became transparent. The finally obtained product was dried in a vacuum oven at 100 degrees C to obtain a catalyst in powder form.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN109954508-A; CN109954508-B
TI Complex photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to form methanol and ethanol comprises ternary heterojunction.
AU XIONG W
   DAI W
   LUO S
   LUO X
   YANG L
   ZOU J
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 201959919N
AB    NOVELTY - Complex photocatalyst comprises ternary heterojunction structure comprising layered titanium3C2Tx and silver and silver(+1) cation dispersed on the titanium3C2Tx layered structure 2S, where The Tx in the layered titanium3C2Tx is a capping functional group on the surface of titanium3C2.
   USE - The material is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to form methanol and ethanol (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the comprising mixing titanium aluminum carbide with hydrofluoric acid and etching to obtain layered titanium3C2Tx, mixing the titanium3C2Tx dispersion, silver nitrate solution, and sodium sulfide solution and carrying out photoreduction treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The particle size of the silver and silver-2-S is independently 10-40 nm, the mass ratio of the silver and silver-2S to the layered titanium3C2Tx is 0.05-0.08:1, and the mass of the silver is based on the mass of silver-2-S. The hydrofluoric acid has a mass concentration of 35-45%, where the ratio of the amount of the titanium aluminum carbide to the hydrofluoric acid is 0.8-1.2 g: 8-12 ml. The photoreduction is performed under irradiation of a xenon lamp, and the photoreduction time is 25-35 minutes. The method further comprises post-treating the etched product, and the post-treatment comprises washing, centrifuging and drying in sequence. The centrifugation speed is 7000-9000 rotations/minute (rpm), and the centrifugation time is 4-6 minutes.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AE CHANGZHOU ZHONGTIAN AEROSOL PROD CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)

The cleaning liquid comprises 58.5-78 pts. wt. sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, 45-60 pts. wt. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, 15-20 pts. wt. ethanol and 15-20 pts. wt. deionized water.    USE - Used as cleaning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The cleaning agent is non-toxic, economical, environmentally friendly, harmless and non-flammable, improves safety performance, increases economic benefits and reduces production cost.

The fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether is 12C linear alkyl fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether having 6-8 oxyethylene groups.

PN KR2018128107-A
TI Anaerobically treating sewage by precipitating solid in sewage, decomposing organic matter in sewage into methane and carbon dioxide using anaerobic microorganism, and recovering sulfide in treated sewage.
AU BAE J
   SHIN C
AE UNIV INHA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (INHI-C)
GA 201897894Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method for anaerobically treating sewage, involves (a) precipitating the solid contained in the sewage, (b) decomposing the organic matter contained in the sewage into methane and carbon dioxide using an anaerobic microorganism, and (c) recovering the sulfide contained in the treated sewage.
   USE - The method and apparatus are useful for anaerobically treating sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method treats sewage without bad smell and removes 99% of phosphorus remaining in the treated sewage water together with the sulfide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for anaerobically treating sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The method further involves (d) recovering methane dissolved in the discharged effluent. The method further involves concentrating the sludge precipitated in step (a), and heating and stirring the concentrated sludge to decompose the organic matter of the sludge into by-products and methane gas. The method further involves removing nitrogen in the treated sewage after the step (b) and before the step (c). The step (c) is performed in an enclosed atmosphere to prevent the loss of dissolved methane. The step (c) involves performing a flocculation and sedimentation method or an ion exchange resin method. The flocculation and sedimentation method involves transforming the zeta potential to an unstable state, where the flocculant and the flocculation aid used in the flocculation and sedimentation method are aluminum and a cationic polymer, respectively. The flocculation and sedimentation method involves performing a rapid stirring step, a slow stirring step, and a precipitation step. The ion exchange resin method involves ion-binding using an artificial resin or a natural resin.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01; D04-A01B; D04-A01J; D04-B06; D04-B07B; D04-B07C; D04-B07D; E11-Q02B; E31-F01C; E31-H05; E34-C03; H09-F03; A11-C03; A12-M; A12-M01; A12-M02; A12-W11E; A12-W11J

TI Method for supercritically extracting erucic acid, involves adding broccoli seeds to oven, spreading, drying, crushing, feeding broccoli seed powder to supercritical extraction kettle, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU LUAN L
   HUANG Y
   WU Y
   LIU X
   TAN M
   LI J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2018802059
AB    NOVELTY - The method for supercritically extracting erucic acid, involves (1) adding broccoli seeds to oven, spreading evenly, drying at high temperature, crushing through the sieve, (2) feeding the broccoli seed powder to supercritical extraction kettle, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and controlling separation tank.
   USE - Method for supercritically extracting erucic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The economical method enables supercritical extraction of erucic acid with short time, solvent free residue, high extraction rate, high purity and yield of more than 70%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (1) comprises drying at 90-150 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The step (2) comprises extracting at pressure of 25-40 MPa and temperature of 35-50 degrees C for 3-6 hours, controlling carbon dioxide flow rate at 6-8 BV/hour, controlling pressure of separation tank at 6-8 MPa and temperature of separation tank at 35-45 degrees C. Preferred Properties: The raw material particle size is 20-60 mesh. Preferred Composition: The volume of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle is 20 L and feed amount is 2.0 kg.

PN CN108085138-A; CN108085138-B
TI Processing lavender essential oil comprises e.g. hydrolyzing fresh lavender lysozyme enzyme, inactivating enzyme, extracting by liquid carbon dioxide extraction, separating to obtain the lavender essential oil, and picking fresh lavender.
AU YANG L
AE YANTAI YANSHENG INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (YANT-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG HUAGU SPICE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018433858
AB    NOVELTY - Processing lavender essential oil comprises hydrolyzing the fresh lavender lysozyme enzyme, inactivating enzyme, extracting by liquid carbon dioxide extraction, separating to obtain the lavender essential oil, and picking the fresh lavender and storing in -20 to -5 degrees C in environment.
   USE - The method is useful for processing lavender essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The invention has low extracting temperature, low pressure, prevents high temperature and pressure damage to the essential oil, the extraction, and prolongs the use age of the essential oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of fresh lavender, lysozyme and water is 10-50:0.02-1:50-90, enzymolysis temperature is 27-55 degrees C, the enzymolysis time is 30-60 minutes. The inactivating temperature is 8-120 degrees C and the inactivating time is 5-20 minutes, the sterilizing pressure is 0-5 MPa. The temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide is 15-31.26 degrees C, the pressure is 5-10 MPa, the flow speed is 25-35 l/hour, the extracting time is 0.5-2 hours. The mass ratio of retention agent, lavender essential oil and retention agent in the lavender essential oil is 50-99.5:0.5-50, the retaining agent is proportion of glycol, propanetriol and isopropanol. Preferred Method: The separating comprises a first-stage separating pressure of separating kettle to separate and the secondary separating kettle to separate, where the primary separating kettle is 6-8 MPa, the separation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, and the separation time is 0.5% of the separation pressure 3 hours, separating kettle is 4-6 MPa, the separating temperature is 25-40 degrees C, and the separation time is 0.5-3 hours, separation kettle to separate the back bottom residue is left as a mixture. The transferring mixture to an oil-water separator, adding flocculating agent into the mixture, allowing to stand for 20-150 minutes, the mass ratio of the mixture and the flocculating agent is 99-99.99:0.01-1, performing oil-water separation under the condition of 30-50 degrees C, obtaining lavender wax and water treatment, water treatment filtering to obtain water and residue, the flocculating agent is active carclazyte, polyaluminum chloride, or sodium polyacrylate and polyacrylamide.

PT P
PN CN107857879-A; CN107857879-B
TI Preparation of bisphenol acid-based nonisocyanate            polyurethane involves preparing bisphenol isopropanol            diglycidyl ether from bisphenolic acid, reacting with            carbon dioxide, and reacting obtained compound with            polyamine.
AU FAN H
   MA Z
   TIAN B
   JIN D
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201826416F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of bisphenol acid-based                nonisocyanate polyurethane involves preparing                bisphenol isopropanol diglycidyl ether from                bisphenolic acid, reacting with carbon dioxide,                obtaining bisphenolate based bicyclic carbonate,                and reacting obtained compound with                polyamine.
   USE - Preparation of bisphenol acid-based                nonisocyanate polyurethane used for preparing                bisphenol acid-based nonisocyanate polyurethane                composite coating (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of bisphenol acid-based nonisocyanate                polyurethane composite coating, which involves                mixing bisphenol-based nonisocyanate polyurethane                having weight average molecular weight of 4000-5000                with an epoxy resin, coating and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Bisphenolic acid,                  isopropyl alcohol, catalyst (A) and solvent (A)                  are mixed and reacted at 80-120 degrees C to                  obtain isopropanol bisphenolate. The obtained                  compound is mixed with epichlorohydrin, catalyst                  (B) and solvent (B), reacted at 100-150 degrees C                  to obtain bisphenol acid diglycidyl ether.                  Bisphenol diglycidyl ether, catalyst (C) and                  solvent (C) are added to an autoclave, and carbon                  dioxide gas is introduced to replace air. The                  resultant material is then heated to 100-140                  degrees C, and the pressure in the autoclave is                  adjusted to 4-8 MPa. After the reaction is                  completed, the resultant material is post-treated                  to obtain bisphenolate-based bicyclic carbonate.                  The obtained compound is mixed with polyamine and                  solvent (D), and reacted at 60-100 degrees C to                  obtain bisphenolic acid based non-isocyanate                  polyurethane. Preferred Composition: The mass                  ratio of bisphenol acid and isopropyl alcohol is                  1:0.2-1. The solvent (A) is isopropanol,                  n-butanol and/or isobutanol. The mass ratio of                  diphenolic acid with respect to solvent (A) is                  1:2-5. The catalyst (A) is concentrated sulfuric                  acid, concentrated hydrochloric acid,                  p-toluenesulfonic acid or thionyl chloride. The                  mass ratio of diphenolic acid with respect to                  catalyst (A) is 100:0.5-5. The mass ratio of                  isopropanol bisphenolic acid ester with respect                  to epichlorohydrin is 1:1.5-3. The catalyst (B)                  is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and/or                  barium hydroxide. The mass ratio of isopropanol                  bisphenolate and catalyst (B) is 1:0.25-0.4. The                  solvent (B) is isopropanol, n-butanol and/or                  isobutanol. The mass ratio of isopropyl alcohol                  bisphenolate with respect to solvent (B) is                  1:2-5. The catalyst (C) is tetrabutylammonium                  bromide, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, and/or                  tetrabutylammonium chloride. The mass of catalyst                  (C) is 0.5-2 %mass with respect to content of                  diphenolic acid diglycidyl ether. The solvent (C)                  is tetrahydrofuran, acetone and/or toluene. The                  mass of solvent (C) is 0.5-1.5 times the mass of                  the diphenolic acid diglycidyl ether. The                  polyamine is ethylenediamine,                  hexamethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine,                  triethylenetetramine and/or isophoronediamine.                  The mass ratio of bisphenolate-based bicyclic                  carbonate and polyamines is 1:0.1-0.5. The                  solvent (D) is N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, and/or                  N-methylpyrrolidone. The content of solvent (D)                  is 1.5-3 times the mass of bisphenolate-based                  bicyclic carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The epoxy resin is                  bisphenol A-type epoxy resin or ethylene glycol                  diglycidyl ether. The equivalent ratio of amino                  group in bisphenol-based nonisocyanate                  polyurethane with respect to epoxy group in epoxy                  resin is 0.5-1.5.
DC A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); G02 (Inks, paints, polishes â€“ polymer-based paints and inks are also classified in Section A (C09D, F, G).)

PT P
PN CN107827862-A
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate using nickel-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of conjugated nickel porphyrin microporous polymer material.
AU MAI W
   ZHANG X
   CHEN Z
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201824341J
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using nickel-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of conjugated nickel porphyrin microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at normal temperature and normal pressure for 12-48 hours without solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate with excellent catalytic activity, efficiency and stability under mild reaction condition, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated nickel porphyrin microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of nickel tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (I) in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. The conjugated nickel porphyrin microporous polymer material is replaced with conjugated nickel porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material. The conjugated nickel porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of nickel tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (II) and benzylamine as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The heterogeneous catalyst is used in an amount of 0.2-0.5 mol%. The co-catalyst is used in an amount of 3.6-7.2 mol%.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107827860-A
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate using zinc-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of conjugated zinc porphyrin microporous polymer material.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG X
   ZHAI S
   CAO Y
   XU Z
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201824341L
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using zinc-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of conjugated zinc porphyrin microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at normal temperature and normal pressure for 12-48 hours without solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate with excellent catalytic activity, efficiency and stability under mild reaction condition, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated zinc porphyrin microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of zinc tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (I) in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. The conjugated zinc porphyrin microporous polymer material is replaced with conjugated zinc porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material. The conjugated zinc porphyrin bibenzylamine microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of zinc tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (II) and benzylamine as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The heterogeneous catalyst is used in an amount of 0.2-0.5 mol%. The co-catalyst is used in an amount of 3.6-7.2 mol%.

PN KR2022128880-A
TI Preparing alloy catalyst useful for reducing            carbon dioxide to produce chemical fuel, comprises e.g.            forming mainstream metal thin film on one surface of            substrate, and forming dissimilar metal layer on            surface of mainstream metal thin film.
AU OH J H
   KIM J H
   SONG J
   HONGSEUNGWON
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022C3525R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alloy catalyst comprises (a)                preparing a substrate; (b) forming a mainstream                metal thin film on one surface of the substrate;                (c) forming a dissimilar metal layer on the surface                of the mainstream metal thin film; (d) immersing                the substrate on which the mainstream metal thin                film and the dissimilar metal layer are formed in                an aqueous solution, and applying an anode voltage                to the surface of the dissimilar metal layer to                form a metal oxide; and (e) forming an alloy                nanostructure by flowing a reducing current density                to the metal oxide.
   USE - The alloy catalyst is useful for reducing                carbon dioxide (claimed) to produce chemical                fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces alloy catalyst into                three-dimensional nanostructure easily and quickly                than in the prior art; can control composition of                minor metal elements in the alloy catalyst; can                effectively suppress structural collapse due to                surface diffusion (migration) of mainstream metal                atoms, which is minutely generated as the carbon                dioxide reduction reaction proceeds, so that                maintaining the catalytic properties of alloy                catalyst; and is economical. The alloy catalyst has                high efficiency and stability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the method for preparing alloy catalyst for                reducing carbon dioxide (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), an                  adhesive layer is formed on one surface of the                  substrate, and the mainstream metal thin film is                  formed on the adhesive layer. The dissimilar                  metal layer formed through underpotential                  deposition and/or electrodeposition method. In                  the step (d), an anode voltage of 1.5-3 V                  compared to a reversible hydrogen electrode (RHE)                  is applied for &#8805; 1 minute. The steps                  (d) and (e) are made in the same aqueous                  solution. Preferred Components: The adhesive                  layer includes titanium, tantalum, and/or                  chromium. The adhesive layer has thickness of                  1-10 nm. The mainstream metal thin film has                  thickness of 50-1000 nm. The substrate includes                  metals including iron, copper, zinc and/or                  aluminum, alloys including stainless steel,                  semiconductor containing silicon and/or gallium                  arsenide, polymer plastics containing silicon,                  acrylate, vinyl, and/or carbonate, compound of                  indium and tin oxide, glass, and/or quartz. The                  mainstream metal thin film includes gold, silver,                  palladium and/or zinc. The dissimilar metal                  includes at least one of silver, palladium,                  copper, tin, zinc and iron as metal. The                  dissimilar metal layer is formed in a continuous                  layer structure or a discontinuous island                  structure. The continuous layer has thickness of                  1 &#8491; to 10 nm. The discontinuous island                  has thickness of 50-400 nm. The alloy catalyst                  has reduction current density of -0.1 to 10                  mA/cm2. The reduction current density flows until                  the point at which the electrode potential value                  applied to the metal oxide changes in the                  negative direction. The alloy nanostructure                  comprises 1-15 at.% composition of dissimilar                  metal.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of the carbon                dioxide reduction efficiency comparison of the                alloy nanostructure in which the trace amount to a                small amount of copper is contained, that do not                support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

GA 202124494S
AB    NOVELTY - A load-type gold-platinum alloy electrode preparing method involves taking 5-10 g carbon black or carbon nano tube, adding into 200 ml acetone solution, raising the temperature to 60 degrees C, condensing and refluxing for 4 hours, drying and washing, removing the acetone solution on the surface, adding into 200 ml hydrochloric acid solution, heating to 120 degrees C, condensing and refluxing for 10 hours, drying and washing, removing the surface of the hydrochloric acid solution, adding into the nitric acid solution, heating to 120 degrees C, condensing and refluxing for 10 hours, drying and washing, removing the surface of nitric acid solution, drying for 5-10 hours to obtain the Vulcan XC-72R carbon black with hydrophilicity or carbon nano-tube carrier.

PN CN111960422-A
TI Preparing two-dimensional silicon nanomaterials comprises adding calcium disilicide in tube-type reaction furnace, adding pure carbon dioxide, exhausting air in the furnace, reacting, cooling, washing with distilled water, and drying.
AU SUN L
   XIE J
   LIU T
   HUANG S
   ZHANG L
   WU J
   JIANG R
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2020B7974H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing two-dimensional silicon nanomaterials comprises (a) adding 1-10 g calcium disilicide in tube-type reaction furnace placed flat, where the calcium disilicide is Zintl compound with a layered structure, (b) adding pure carbon dioxide or a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into tubular reactor, then exhausting air in the furnace, and reacting at 300-800 degrees C for 3-10 hours at a heating rate of 5-10 degrees C/minute, (c) cooling to room temperature, washing the product with 10% dilute hydrochloric acid, then washing with distilled water, and drying under vacuum at 70 degrees C to obtain two-dimensional silicon nanosheets.
   USE - The two-dimensional silicon nanomaterial is useful in preparing lithium battery cathode material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can generate calcium carbonate and calcium oxide through the reaction of carbon dioxide and calcium disilicide; used as lithium battery cathode electrode; has good electrochemical performance, and is suitable for industrialization. The silicon nanomaterials: has first lap discharge capacity of 1200 mA.h.g-1, first lap charge capacity of 840 mA.h.g-1, first lap Coulomb efficiency of 70%, has capacity, which does not decrease after 500 cycles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of two-dimensional silicon nanomaterial in preparing lithium battery cathode material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas containing carbon dioxide is a carbon dioxide/nitrogen mixed gas, or carbon dioxide/argon mixed gas. The carbon dioxide content is 10-50%. Preferred Method: The method comprises treating the calcium disilicide with 1-3 M sodium hydroxide. The using method comprises dispersing two-dimensional silicon nanosheets in distilled water, ultrasonically dispersing for half an hour, then coating them with polydopamine by solution polymerization, drying the coated products, heat treating at 800 degrees C for 4 hours under the protection of argon atmosphere for coating the carbon layer to obtain silicon wafer-carbon composite material, then preparing the silicon wafer-carbon composite material, conductive graphite, and sodium carboxymethyl cellulose into slurry at a mass ratio of 8:1:1, then coating on copper foil, and drying for overnight in vacuum at 100 degrees C to prepare battery electrode sheets.
