PN CN111926601-A
TI Controlling fiber film forming process by magnetic field comprises e.g. placing calcium lignosulfonate solution into sealed reactor, making lignin solution to flow through magnetic field, and re-configuring calcium lignosulfonate solution.
AU HU X
   ZHANG Q
AE GUANGZHOU ZHONGCI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8402W
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling fiber film forming process by magnetic field comprises: (i) preparing 8-30 wt.% calcium lignosulfonate solution; (ii) placing calcium lignosulfonate solution into a sealed reactor filled with active agent i.e. sodium stearate, and processing at 70-80 degrees C for 10 minutes to 1 hour under vacuum condition; (iii) increasing magnetic field outside the reactor, making lignin solution to flow through the magnetic field, and branching a micro solution into microscope observation range; (iv) introducing carbon dioxide gas into lignin solution, controlling flow rate of carbon dioxide gas by flow meter, observing carbon dioxide gas flow rate at the moment when the microfilm substrate appears, and maintaining carbon dioxide gas flow rate until the formation is finished; and (v) sending formed fiber film into drying device for drying, evaporating residual solvent in fiber film, shaping nano-fiber film, rinsing by using mineral water, and re-configuring calcium lignosulfonate solution.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling fiber film forming process by magnetic field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is capable of controlling the agglomeration force between the fiber particles through magnetic field; improves the dimensional stability; reduces the phase interface tension; improves the curing reaction performance; has good curing ability under low dosage condition; and broadens the application field of the fiber film.

PN CN111875546-A; CN111875546-B
TI Use of sea urchin-shaped cobalt-based            photocatalyst in converting carbon dioxide to            synthesize benzo-nitrogen heterocycles.
AU ZHENG H
   YANG S
   SHEN Y
   TU X
   HU A
AE UNIV HANGZHOU NORMAL (UYNL-C)
   UNIV HANGZHOU NORMAL (UYNL-C)
GA 2020B1959U
AB    NOVELTY - Use of sea urchin-shaped cobalt-based                photocatalyst in converting carbon dioxide to                synthesize benzo-nitrogen heterocycles, is claimed.                The sea urchin-shaped cobalt-based photocatalyst                includes complex composed of organic ligand L and                cobalt nitrate hexahydrate; the complex is sea                urchin-shaped microsphere structure formed by the                arrangement of multiple of nano-needle complex                crystals, and the main blank of organic ligand L is                p-aminobenzoic acid.
   USE - The sea urchin-shaped cobalt-based                photocatalyst is useful in converting carbon                dioxide to synthesize benzo-nitrogen heterocycle                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: has large specific surface                area, high photocatalytic efficiency, reduced                energy consumption and reduced cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing sea urchin-shaped cobalt-based                photocatalyst, comprising (i) mixing and stirring                organic ligand L with p-toluic acid as main blank,                and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate to form mixed                solution, (ii) heating the mixed solution, cooling                and filtering to obtain primary catalyst product,                and (iii) washing the primary catalyst product with                organic solvent, and vacuum drying to obtain final                product; and
   (2) use method, comprising pouring carbon                dioxide into the solution containing                o-phenylenediamine compounds, performing                carbonylation reaction under the action of sea                urchin-shaped cobalt-based photocatalyst for                forming a ring with o-phenylenediamine compounds to                synthesize benzo-nitrogen heterocycle compound,                where o-phenylenediamine compound is of formula                (I).
   R1, R2 = H, halo, or short-chain aliphatic                hydrocarbon groups.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the molar                  ratio between organic ligand L and cobalt nitrate                  hexahydrate is 0.5-2:1; and the stirring time is                  10-30 minutes. In step (ii), the heating                  temperature is 150-230 degrees C; the heating                  time is 10-20 hours, and cooling temperature is                  20-30 degrees C. In step (iii), the organic                  solvent is dimethylformamide and ethanol; the                  amount of each wash is 5-10 ml of organic solvent                  per mole of primary catalyst product; and washing                  is carried out until the quality of primary                  catalyst product is no longer changes. In step                  (iii), the drying temperature is 70-90 degrees C,                  and the drying time is 10-15 hours. The                  preparation method of organic ligand L comprises                  adding p-aminobenzoic acid into mixed solution                  composed of organic solvents, and stirring to                  obtain final product. The organic solvent in the                  preparation method of organic ligand L is                  dimethylformamide and ethanol; the volume of                  mixed solution added to each mole of                  p-aminobenzoic acid is 5-20 ml; and the stirring                  time is 10-30 minutes.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

GA 201877709Q
AB    NOVELTY - The device (200) has a carbon dioxide sensor (210) for measuring concentration of carbon dioxide gas within a trachea of a patient through an endotracheal tube (ETT) (100). A differential pressure sensor (215) measures pressure of air within the trachea of the patient. A processing unit (205) processes an input received from the carbon dioxide sensor and the differential pressure sensor. A user interface monitors an output data and evaluates a correct position of the ETT within the trachea of the patient. The user interface is communicatively coupled to receive the output data from the processing unit.
   USE - Detachable device for detecting and monitoring a position of an ETT within a trachea of a patient by a user device e.g. laptop, tablet, and smartphone (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device allows a user/physician to easily detect and monitor the position of the ETT on the user device. The device provides a simple connectivity between the user interface and the processing unit through a wireless communication or wired communication.

Several carbon dioxide gas canisters are used for                inflating the space between the inner membrane and                the outer membrane.
   USE - Fully-sealed double-membrane carbon dioxide                air layer insulating air-supported greenhouse for                agricultural environment protection. The full-closed structure effectively                prevents the occurrence of plant diseases and                insect pests. The greenhouse provides good heat                preservation effect, the large space provides no                beam and no column, and the space size is suitable                for planting various crops which is convenient for                the mechanical operation. The materials are                recycled, no construction waste is generated, the                migration is convenient and can restore the                original appearance of the land.

PN CN112940349-A; CN112940349-B
TI Combined toughening agent in epoxy toughening,            comprises mixture of hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic            carbonates and aliphatic cyclic carbonates comprising            glycidyl ether-type cyclic carbonate containing            flexible aliphatic segment.
AU KE J
   WANG H
   LI X
   HUANG Y
   WANG F
   ZHOU J
AE CHINA BLUESTAR CHENGRAND CHEM IND RES (CNCC-C)
   CHINA BLUESTAR CHENGRAND RES CHEM IND (CNCC-C)
GA 2021740808
AB    NOVELTY - A combined toughening agent containing                five-membered cyclic carbonate groups comprises                mixture of hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic carbonates                and aliphatic cyclic carbonates in a mass ratio of                1-10:10. The hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic carbonate                mixture comprises 1,3-dioxolan-2-one-containing                compound (I) or (II), and hydroxy-containing                compound (III). The aliphatic cyclic carbonate is                glycidyl ether-type cyclic carbonate containing                flexible aliphatic segment.
   USE - Combined toughening agent in epoxy toughening                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combined toughening agent has improved                impact strength when incorporated in the epoxy                resin system. The preparation method of toughening                agent is simple, easy, safe, reliable, green and                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A combined toughening agent containing                five-membered cyclic carbonate groups comprises                mixture of hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic carbonates                and aliphatic cyclic carbonates in a mass ratio of                1-10:10. The hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic carbonate                mixture comprises 1,3-dioxolan-2-one-containing                compound of formula (I) or (II), and                hydroxy-containing compound of formula (III). The                aliphatic cyclic carbonate is glycidyl ether-type                cyclic carbonate containing flexible aliphatic                segment.
   R = group of formula (a) or (b).
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the combined toughening agent, which                involves (A) placing the aromatic epoxy resin and                catalyst in a closed reaction kettle, continuously                feeding carbon dioxide under the conditions of                carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-4 MPa and                temperature of 80-150 degrees C, stirring, reacting                for 8-24 hours, adding distilled water to the                airtight reaction kettle, reacting at 50-100                degrees C for 24-48 hours, cleaning the obtained                solid or transparent viscous liquid product, vacuum                drying to obtain hydrolyzed aromatic cyclic                carbonate mixture, (B) placing the glycidyl ether                and catalyst in a closed reaction vessel,                continuously feeding carbon dioxide under the                conditions of a carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-4                MPa and temperature of 80-150 degrees C, stirring,                reacting for 8-60 hours to obtain aliphatic cyclic                carbonate, and (C) mixing hydrolyzed aromatic                cyclic carbonate mixture and aliphatic cyclic                carbonate in a mass ratio of 1-10:10.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is one or                  more of tetrabutylammonium bromide, lithium                  bromide, zinc bromide or benzyltriethylammonium                  chloride. Preferred Properties: The mass ratio of                  the aromatic epoxy resin to catalyst is                  100:(0.5-2). The mass ratio of the aromatic epoxy                  resin to t distilled water is 10:(2-10).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cleaning solvent                  is one or more of water, methanol, ethanol,                  acetone, ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran, carbon                  dichloride or carbon trichloride. The glycidyl                  ether is one or more of ethylene glycol                  diglycidyl ether, butylene glycol diglycidyl                  ether, hexanediol diglycidyl ether, polyethylene                  glycol diglycidyl ether or polypropylene glycol                  diglycidyl ether. Preferred Condition: The vacuum                  drying in process (A) is carried out at a                  temperature of 100-150 degrees C for 24-72 hours.                  Preferred Properties: The polyethylene glycol                  diglycidyl ether has a number average molecular                  weight of 600-2000 g/mol. The polypropylene                  glycol diglycidyl ether has a number average                  molecular weight of 500-2500 g/mol. The mass                  ratio of the glycidyl ether to catalyst is                  100:(0.5-2).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The aromatic epoxy                  resin is one of bisphenol-A epoxy resin and                  bisphenol-F epoxy resin.

PT P
PN CN112823879-A; CN112823879-B
TI Use of cerium-based catalyst in reaction of carbon dioxide and methanol to synthesize dimethylcarbonate.
AU LI G
   ZHENG K
   LI Z
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202157364W
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a cerium-based catalyst in reaction of carbon dioxide and methanol to synthesize dimethylcarbonate, is claimed. The cerium-based catalyst comprises an active component and a carrier, where the active component comprises cerium-oxygen complex MxCe1-xOy (I).
   USE - The cerium-based catalyst is useful in reaction of carbon dioxide and methanol to synthesize dimethylcarbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: preparation method is simple to operation; has a higher specific surface area, a lower pressure drop, excellent mechanical stability and thermal stability; is particularly suitable for continuous reaction operations without using any dehydrating agent; and provides the conversion rate of methanol to 24.3% and the selectivity of dimethyl carbonate to 78.6% under the reaction conditions of 140 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of a cerium-based catalyst in reaction of carbon dioxide and methanol to synthesize dimethyl carbonate, is claimed. The cerium-based catalyst comprises an active component and a carrier, where the active component comprises cerium-oxygen complex of formula MxCe1-xOy (I). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing cerium-based catalyst comprises (i) dissolving the cerium precursor and the M precursor in deionized water to obtain a salt solution A, where in the salt solution A, the total concentration of the cerium salt and the M precursor is 0.01-0.1 mol/liter, the molar ratio of the cerium precursor and the M precursor is 8-9.5:2-0.5, the alkali is dissolved in deionized water to obtain alkali solution B, the concentration of the alkali solution B is 0.6-1 mol/liter; (ii) mixing salt solution A and alkali solution B, stirring at 90-100 degrees C for 4-6 hours, filtering, washing and drying and the molar ratio of the solute in the alkaline solution B to the salt solution A is 3:1; (iii) calcining the dried product at 300-500 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain the active component; (iv) mixing and grinding the obtained active component with deionized water to obtain an active component slurry, coating the active component slurry on the carrier and drying; and (v) calcining the dried product at 300-500 degrees C for 4-6 hours.
   x = 0-2;
   y = 1.9-2; and
   M = manganese, copper, titanium, bismuth, zirconium, iron, magnesium or aluminum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is a honeycomb cordierite ceramic carrier, a homogeneous titanium dioxide carrier, and a nest ceramic carrier. The cerium precursors are cerium nitrate, cerium sulfate, and cerium chloride and the M metal precursors are nitrates, sulfates and chlorides of corresponding metals. In the step (i), the alkali comprises urea, ammonia, sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide and potassium hydroxide. Preferred Method: In the preparation of cerium-based catalyst the drying temperature in step (ii) and step (v) is 80-120 degrees C, and the drying time is 12-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the reaction is 100-180 degrees C, the reaction pressure is 2-3 MPa, the feed molar ratio of methanol/carbon dioxide is 1:1-1:2, and the space velocity of the reaction is 2600-3000 ml/g catalyst/hour.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112675874-A
TI Preparing palladium-copper oxide catalyst for preparing methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation comprises e.g. centrifuging brown green solution to collect precipitate and centrifugally washing precipitate with formaldehyde.
AU FANG F
   SHI H
   ZOU Y
   WEI Z
   LI Y
AE CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CORP 719TH RES (CSHI-C)
GA 202145701P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing palladium-copper oxide catalyst for preparing methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation comprises (i) completely dissolving cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) powder in the de-ionized water to obtain the CTAB aqueous solution; (ii) adding palladium chloride solution and potassium iodide solution into the CTAB aqueous solution, stirring the solution uniformly, transferring into oil bath, stirring and heating; (iii) adding ascorbic acid into the heating solution until the solution is brown; (iv) performing heat preservation of brown solution in the oil bath, taking out and naturally cooling; (v) adding copper chloride solution into the cooling solution, sequentially adding sodium hydroxide solution and hydroxylamine-hydrogen chloride solution, shaking uniformly and allowing to stand at room temperature; and (vi) centrifuging the brown green solution to collect precipitate, and centrifugally washing the precipitate with formaldehyde for many times and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts copper oxide to wrap palladium nano-particles with egg yolk structure YSPd/copper oxide catalyst with more stable catalytic performance and longer life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing palladium-copper oxide catalyst for preparing methanol by carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation comprises (i) completely dissolving cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) powder in the de-ionized water to obtain the CTAB aqueous solution; (ii) adding palladium chloride solution and potassium iodide solution into the CTAB aqueous solution, where the mol ratio of palladium chloride and potassium iodide is 1:5-1:8, stirring the solution uniformly, transferring into 80-100 degrees C of oil bath, stirring and heating for 5-10 minutes; (iii) adding ascorbic acid into the heating solution until the solution is brown; (iv) performing heat preservation of the brown solution in the oil bath for 10-30 minutes, taking out and naturally cooling; (v) adding the copper chloride solution into the cooling solution, where the mol ratio of copper chloride and palladium chloride solution is 10:1, sequentially adding sodium hydroxide solution and hydroxylamine-hydrogen chloride solution, shaking uniformly, allowing to stand at room temperature until the solution color becomes brown green; and (vi) centrifuging the brown green solution to collect the precipitate, and centrifugally washing the precipitate with formaldehyde for many times and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the ratio of the de-ionized water and CTAB powder is 1 ml:4-4 mg. In the step (ii), the molar concentration of palladium chloride solution is 0.01 mmol/l, the molar concentration of potassium iodide solution is 0.1 mmol/l and the volume ratio of the CTAB aqueous solution to palladium chloride solution is 20-30:1. In the step (iii), the molar concentration of the ascorbic acid solution is 0.1 mmol/l. In the step (v), the molar concentration of the copper chloride solution is 0.1 mmol/l, which is diluted for 150-200 times before adding, the molar concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 1 mmol/l and the molar concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution and hydroxylamine-hydrogen chloride solution is 1 mmol/l. In the step (vi), the prepared catalyst with egg yolk structure has a palladium loading amount of 1 wt.%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN112641761-A
TI Stable beta-nicotinamide adenine mononucleotide            slow-release micro-pill useful as slow-release enteric            pellet, comprises blank pill core, beta-nicotinamide            adenine mononucleotide liposome nano-sphere and enteric            coating material.
AU HU Y
   WANG W
   LIN M
AE XIAMEN KINGDOMWAY BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
   XIAMEN KINGDOMWAY GROUP CO (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 202148647Q
AB    NOVELTY - Stable beta -nicotinamide adenine                mononucleotide (NMN) slow-release micro-pill                comprises blank pill core, NMN liposome nano-sphere                and enteric coating material. The pill is prepared                by a vacuum bottom spraying regular flow fluidized                bed coating technology. The NMN liposome                nano-sphere is prepared by supercritical carbon                dioxide fluid technology and comprises core                materials and support wall material. The core                material comprises NMN. The support wall material                comprises mono-fatty acid glycerides and                phospholipids. The NMN liposome nano-sphere is                adsorbed on the surface of the blank pill core and                and the enteric coating material is coated on the                surface of the blank pill core and the NMN liposome                nano-sphere.
   USE - The stable NMN slow-release micro-pill is                useful as slow-release enteric pellet                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The NMN micro-pill has excellent                enteric-coated slow-release performance, good                stabilizing effect, less irritation to the                gastrointestinal tract, and high bioavailability,                so can achieve convenient administration effects,                high drug bioavailability, and good patient                compliance and can accurately achieve slow release                in the intestinal tract.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                preparation method of stable NMN slow-release                micro-pill, comprising (i) mixing the core material                and the support wall material in a solvent to form                a composite solution, introducing supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid into the composite solution,                maintaining for a period of time under the                condition that the temperature and pressure are                sufficient to keep the supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid in a supercritical state to dissolve the                supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in the support                wall material to expand the support wall material,                and quickly releasing the pressure to solidify the                support wall material around the core material to                obtain NMN liposome nano sphere; and (ii) placing                the blank pill core in a vacuum bottom spraying                regular flow fluidized bed, and controlling the                temperature at 30-40 degrees C, adjusting the air                flow of the vacuum bottom spraying regular flow                fluidized bed to make the blank pill core in a                fountain type fluidized state, uniformly mixing the                NMN liposome nano sphere with the enteric coating                material and spraying on the blank pill core for                coating and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The slow-release                  micro-pill comprises 60-90 wt.% blank pill core,                  0.5-15 wt.% NMN liposome nano-sphere and 3.5-30                  wt.% enteric coating material. Preferred                  Components: The NMN liposome nano-sphere                  comprises 1-25 wt.% NMN, 15-50 wt.% mono-fatty                  acid glycerides and 25.5-70 wt.% phospholipids.                  The grain diameter of the stabilized NMN                  slow-release micro-pill is 15-30 meshes, the                  grain diameter of the blank pill core is 15-30                  meshes and the grain diameter of the NMN liposome                  nano sphere is 40-100 nm. The material of the                  blank pill core is sucrose, microcrystalline                  cellulose or starch. The mono-fatty acid                  glyceride is glycerol monostearate, glycerol                  monolaurate or glycerol monooleate. The                  phospholipid is egg yolk lecithin, soybean                  phospholipid, hydrogenated soybean phospholipids,                  or sunflower phospholipids. The enteric coating                  material comprises 70-99 wt.% water-soluble                  polymer and optionally 0.1-20 wt.% plasticizer                  and/or 0.1-10 wt.% anti-sticking agent. The                  water-soluble polymer is methacrylic acid-ethyl                  acrylate copolymer, acrylic polymers or cellulose                  derivative. The plasticizer is triethyl citrate,                  glycerol acetate or phthalate. The anti-sticking                  agent is talc and/or silicon dioxide. Preferred                  Method: In the step (i), mixing the core material                  and the support wall material in a solvent to                  form a composite solution by dissolving the core                  material in water to form an aqueous solution,                  dissolving the support wall material in an                  organic solvent by ultrasonically mixing for                  25-45 minutes to form an oil phase solution, and                  uniformly mixing the aqueous solution and the oil                  phase solution; and the supercritical carbon                  dioxide fluid is maintained at a temperature of                  35-60 degrees C and a pressure of 10-40 MPa for                  10-30 minutes.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B02 (Fused ring heterocyclics.); A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.)
MC B04-B01B; B04-B03E; B04-C02A1; B04-C02B2; B04-C03B; B05-A01B; B05-B01P; B05-B02C; B07-A02; B10-C04B; B10-E04C; B12-M10B; B12-M11B; B14-E10; A11-B05B1; A12-B; A12-V01; A12-W05; A12-W14; A12-W15

PT P
PN CN112227547-A
TI Flame-retardant insulation board comprises polystyrene, carbon dioxide, graphite, micro silicon powder, bromocyclododecane, methyl octabromide, calcium stearate, talc, zinc borate, and industrial alcohol.
AU JIA G
   HUANG D
   ZHANG H
AE JIA G (JIAG-Individual)
GA 202109514Y
AB    NOVELTY - Flame-retardant insulation board comprises 82-89 wt.% polystyrene, 6 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.5-1.5 wt.% graphite, 5-10 wt.% micro silicon powder, 4-10 wt.% bromocyclododecane, 4-10 wt.% methyl octabromide, 0.5-1 wt.% calcium stearate, 1-2 wt.% talc, 0.4-0.8 wt.% zinc borate, and 0.5-1 wt.% industrial alcohol.
   USE - Flame-retardant insulation board.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame-retardant insulation board reduces the thermal conductivity, has the effect of heat preservation and energy saving, has the components of hexabromocyclododecane, methyl octabromo ether, and zinc borate, which is flame retardant, achieves effect of fire prevention, so that the flame retardant insulation board made of the materials can take into account energy saving and fire prevention.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an insulation wall, which comprises comprises insulation boards, the insulation boards are distributed on the same wall surface, and there is a space between adjacent insulation boards, one side of the thermal insulation board is tightly bonded to the wall body by bonding mortar, and the bonding mortar fills the gap between the thermal insulation board and the wall body to form a void-free cavity, and the other side of the thermal insulation board and circumferential edge in the thickness direction is provided with a fireproof coating; and
   (2) a method for manufacturing a flame-retardant thermal insulation board, which involves selecting 82-89 wt.% polystyrene, 6 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.5-1.5 wt.% graphite, 5-10 wt.% micro silicon powder, 4-10 wt.% bromocyclododecane, 4-10 wt.% methyl octabromide, 0.5-1 wt.% calcium stearate, 1-2 wt.% talc, 0.4-0.8 wt.% zinc borate, and 0.5-1 wt.% industrial alcohol, adding prepared raw materials into the first-stage extruder to heat, increase temperature, and mixing to make uniformly plasticized, and injecting carbon dioxide into the first-stage extruder, transporting the molten mixture processed by the first-stage extruder to the second-stage extruder for cooling, and extruding the die when cooled to the viscous mixture, reshaping and cooling down the shape by a leveler, and finally trimming and shaping.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid carbon dioxide stored in the low-pressure storage tank is input into the high-pressure buffer tank through a booster pump to maintain the pressure in the tank, and then the carbon dioxide in the high-pressure buffer tank is input into the first-stage extruder in a quantitative and constant pressure. The thermal insulation wall further comprises a wall body. The adhesive layer is connected to the first surface and/or the second surface of the wall body and is used to bond the flame-retardant insulation board. An insulation slurry layer, connected with the flame-retardant insulation board. The anti-cracking layer is connected with the thermal insulation slurry layer, the finishing layer is connected with the anti-cracking layer. A flame-retardant filler is arranged between the adjacent flame-retardant insulation boards. The two sides of the flame-retardant insulation board are coated with interface slurry. The thermal insulation slurry includes a slurry made of reclaimed rubber powder, inorganic cementing materials, admixtures and polyphenyl particles. The anti-cracking layer includes a glass fiber mesh cloth, an anti-cracking mortar, and an elastic primer which are stacked in sequence. The thermal insulation wall includes an anchor bolt, which connects the adhesive layer and the flame-retardant insulation board to the wall body.

TI Preparing mesoporous zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide solid solution catalyst useful in hydrogenating carbon dioxide to methanol, comprises e.g. preparing template mixed solution of cetrimonium bromide and polyethylene glycol and reacting.
AU SONG F
   JU M
   CAO Y
   LIU Y
   YANG B
   YAN J
   XU Q
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2020A9636B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesoporous zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide solid solution catalyst comprises e.g. (i) mixing Zinc nitrate and zirconium nitrate, dissolving in deionized water to prepare metal nitrate mixed solution, dissolving and mixing, (ii) mixing cetrimonium bromide and polyethylene glycol 2000, dissolving in deionized water to prepare template mixed solution, completely dissolving and mixing, (iii) pouring the metal nitrate mixed solution into the template mixed solution, magnetically stirring to obtain mixed solution of metal nitrate and template agent, (iv) reacting, adding mixed solution of metal nitrate and templating agent and sodium carbonate solution by adopting the mode of co-current addition, adding into the reaction vessel, titrating and aging, (v) cooling and centrifuging the product to obtain solid precipitate, washing and drying, the catalyst precursor, and (vi) adding the catalyst precursor into tube furnace and calcining.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in hydrogenating carbon dioxide to methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has improved pore structure, high carbon dioxide conversion rate and methanol selectivity in carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation reaction; and inhibits the high temperature sintering and agglomeration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing mesoporous zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide solid solution catalyst comprises (i) mixing Zinc nitrate and zirconium nitrate, dissolving in deionized water to prepare metal nitrate mixed solution, dissolving and mixing, (ii) mixing cetrimonium bromide and polyethylene glycol 2000, dissolving in deionized water to prepare template mixed solution, completely dissolving and mixing, (iii) pouring the metal nitrate mixed solution into the template mixed solution, magnetically stirring for 20-30 minutes to promote the thorough mixing of the solution to obtain mixed solution of metal nitrate and template agent, (iv) reacting at 50-90 degrees C, adding mixed solution of metal nitrate and templating agent and sodium carbonate solution by adopting the mode of co-current addition, adding into the reaction vessel, titrating, maintaining the above reaction temperature, magnetically stirring for 0.5-1 hour, stopping stirring, and aging for 3-5 hours at the above reaction temperature, (v) cooling and centrifuging the product to obtain solid precipitate, washing the solid precipitate with deionized water and drying, the catalyst precursor, and (vi) adding the catalyst precursor into tube furnace and calcining to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between zinc and zirconium in the metal nitrate mixed solution is 1:4. In step (ii), the ratio between the total moles of cetrimonium bromide and polyethylene glycol 2000 is 1:2-10, preferably 2:1. In step (iv), the sodium carbonate has concentration of 0.5-1 mol/l. Preferred Method: The step (v) comprises centrifuging at a speed of 8000-12000 revolutions/minute, washing the solid precipitate 3-6 times with deionized water and drying at 90-120 degrees C for 12 hours. In step (vi), the calcining temperature is 450-550 degrees C.

PN CN111793489-A; CN111793489-B
TI In-situ gas generation foaming gel system useful            in carbonate reservoir, comprises air releasing agent,            sustained release agent, foaming agent, polymer,            crosslinking agent, stabilizer, and water.
AU JIN F
   ZHOU S
   LI X
   XU J
   ZHENG Y
   SONG S
   GAO S
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2020A4272W
AB    NOVELTY - In-situ gas generation foaming gel system                comprises 15-20 wt.% air releasing agent, 5-10 wt.%                sustained release agent, 0.1-0.15 wt.% foaming                agent, 0.2-0.5 wt.% polymer, 0.01-0.08 wt.%                crosslinking agent, 0.01-0.02 wt.% stabilizer, and                water (remaining amount).
   USE - The in-situ gas generation foaming gel system                is useful in carbonate reservoir (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The gel system: can react with rocks along the                way, thus being able to migrate into the deep part                of the formation, automatically spreads and block                the channeling channel on the oil-water interface,                thus starts the low permeability layer and expands                the sweep coefficient, and improves the oil                reservoir recovery factor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of in-situ gas generation foaming gel system                in carbonate reservoirs, comprising (i) preparing                in-situ gas generation foaming gel system by adding                the designed amount of air release agent, sustained                release agent and polymer into the mixing water,                and stirring to make it dissolve uniformly, then                add the designed amount of crosslinking agent and                stabilizer in turn, continuing to stir until                uniform, and slowly adding foaming agent, stirring                at low speed until it is uniformly mixed; and (ii)                injecting the system prepared in step (i) into the                carbonate reservoir, the gas release agent react                thermochemically with the reservoir rock to slowly                release carbon dioxide gas, the sustained release                agent in the system can delay gas release the                reaction speed causes the system to move to the                deep part and act on the deep part of the layer,                the carbon dioxide gas produced interacts with the                foaming agent in the system to produce a large                number of bubbles, the polymer and crosslinking                agent in the system react and crosslinking in the                formation and react with carbon dioxide, where foam                compound forms a foam gel, and the carbon dioxide                foam gel migrates to the deep part of the formation                to block the channeling channel, thus starting the                low permeability layer and improving the oil                recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The air releasing                  agent is weak acid including acetic acid and                  citric acid. The sustained release agent is                  sodium acetate, sodium citrate. The foaming agent                  is alpha -olefin sulfonate and sodium dodecyl                  sulfate. The polymer is water soluble polymer,                  and water soluble polymer can be hydrolyzed                  polyacrylamide (HPAM). The cross-linking agent is                  chromium propionate and chromium acetate. The                  stabilizing agent is thiourea.

PN US2020176787-A1; WO2020112810-A1; AU2019388977-A1; CA3121538-A1; SG11202105644-A1; KR2021113193-A; CN113424344-A; EP3888170-A1; JP2022509870-W
TI Method for producing electricity in a molten            carbonate fuel cell, by employing one or more baffles            which reduce unblocked flow cross-section of cathode            gas collection volume by 10 percent or more.
AU GEARY T C
   BARCKHOLTZ T A
   ROSEN J
   DAS S K
   WILLMAN C A
   HILMI A
   YUH C
   MOSHI G
   PANCAKE D S
   A W C
   ABDELKADER H M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
   EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202048921M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing an anode input                stream comprising hydrogen and/or reformable fuel                into an anode gas collection volume, and                introducing a cathode input stream comprising                oxygen and carbon dioxide into a cathode gas                collection volume. The anode gas collection volume                is defined by an anode surface, a first separator                plate, and an anode collector providing support                between the anode surface and the separator plate.                The cathode gas collection volume is defined by a                cathode surface, a second separator plate (350),                and a cathode collector (360) providing support                between the cathode surface and the second                separator plate. The cathode gas collection volume                is further defined by one or more baffles (380) in                contact with the second separator plate. The one or                more baffles reduce an unblocked flow cross-section                of the cathode gas collection volume by 10% or                more.
   USE - Method for producing electricity in a molten                carbonate fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - When operating a molten carbonate fuel cell                under conditions for elevated carbon dioxide                utilization, the baffles increase transference                and/or operating voltage. In contrast to                conventional operating conditions, operating a                molten carbonate fuel cell with transference of                0.95 or less (or 0.97 or less when operating with                increased open area and/or reduced unblocked flow                cross-section) can increase the effective amount of                carbonate ion transport that is achieved, even                though a portion of the current density generated                by the fuel cell is due to transport of ions other                than carbonate ions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The molten carbonate fuel cell is operated at                a transference of 0.97 or less and an average                current density of 60 mA/cm2 or more to generate                electricity, an anode exhaust comprising hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, and a cathode                exhaust comprising 2.0 vol.% or less carbon                dioxide, 10 vol.% or more oxygen, and 1.0 vol.% or                more water. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included                for a molten carbonate fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view and a top view                of a cathode gas collection volume including                multiple baffle structures.
   Bipolar plate (310)
   Electrolyte (342)
   Separator plate (350)
   Cathode collector (360)
   Baffles (380)

PN CN108179046-A
TI Producing hydrogen gas and liquefied natural gas            from coke oven gas, comprises e.g. performing electric            decoking by removing tar, benzene, naphthalene and            other liquid droplets and water particles from raw            material gas.
AU TANG P
AE SICHUAN JIERUI HENGRI NATURAL GAS ENG CO (YTJR-C)
GA 201850350Y
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen gas and liquefied natural                gas (LNG) from coke oven gas, comprises (a)                performing electric decoking by removing most of                tar, benzene, naphthalene and other liquid droplets                and water particles from the raw material gas in                high voltage corona electric field, (b)                pressurizing the coke oven gas in a compression                system, (c) using BOG gas of LNG for cooling the                raw material coke oven gas, further removing tar,                benzene and naphthalene, (d) performing temperature                swing adsorption process (TSA) to remove                macromolecular impurities, (e) performing two-stage                desulfurization by wet method and dry method to                remove sulfide from coke oven gas, (f) converting                carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide and hydrogen,                and (g) pressurizing MDEA solution with a                compressor, to remove carbon dioxide using membrane                separation method for separating hydrogen and                methane, concentrating and purifying the separated                high concentration methane through dehydration,                removing mercury, and liquefying.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen                gas and liquefied natural gas (LNG) from coke oven                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing hydrogen gas and liquefied natural                gas (LNG) from coke oven gas, comprises (a)                performing electric decoking by removing most of                tar, benzene, naphthalene and other liquid droplets                and water particles from the raw material gas in                high voltage corona electric field, (b)                pressurizing the coke oven gas in a compression                system, (c) using BOG gas of LNG (BOG gas refers to                the gas after passive heating of the LNG                gasification) for cooling the raw material coke                oven gas, further removing tar, benzene and                naphthalene, (d) performing temperature swing                adsorption process (TSA) to remove macromolecular                impurities including tar, benzene and naphthalene,                (e) performing two-stage desulfurization by wet                method and dry method to remove sulfide from coke                oven gas, (f) converting carbon monoxide into                carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and (g) pressurizing                methyl diethanolamine (N-methyl-diethanolamine)                (MDEA) solution with a compressor, to remove carbon                dioxide using membrane separation method for                separating hydrogen and methane, concentrating and                purifying the separated high concentration methane                through dehydration, removing mercury, and                liquefying to obtain LNG.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electric decoking                  is carried out using a tubular electric decoking                  device at voltage of 40-60 KV. The method                  comprises cooling the compression system,                  preferably a screw compressor to 40 degrees C by                  a cooler, and pressurizing at 0.5 MPa. The method                  is carried out by exchanging heat of BOG gas and                  coke oven gas to low temperature, reducing the                  temperature of coke oven gas to less than or                  equal to 10 degrees C, and separating heavy                  components including water, naphthalene and                  benzene from coke oven gas. The TSA process is                  carried out by alternately arranging adsorption                  towers for adsorption and regeneration process,                  and performing two-column process to remove                  macromolecules including tar, benzene and                  naphthalene from the coke oven gas. The wet and                  dry two-stage desulfurization process are carried                  out by a sequence manner. The method comprises                  compressing the obtained product with the                  multi-compressor, and pressurizing for subsequent                  processes. The method comprises drying high                  concentration of methane separated by the                  membrane separation method by a regenerated air                  pre-drying tower using two towers alternately                  drying the regeneration operation, removing                  mercury from an active charcoal tower, and                  finally refrigerating to obtain liquefied mixed                  refrigerant for LNG.

PN CN108165413-A
TI Preparing scented wine, comprises e.g. preparing sauce-flavor liquor by taking completely mature red sorghum and crushing, uniformly mixing, adding glucoamylase, adding amylase and preparing agarwood essential oil by extraction process.
AU ZHANG X
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201849141D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing scented wine, comprises (i) preparing sauce-flavor liquor by taking completely mature red sorghum and crushing, adding crushing red sorghum into a fermenter containing distilled water, and uniformly mixing, adding glucoamylase, adding amylase to the fermenter and placing the fermenter in the above step at room temperature for 30 days; and (ii) preparing agarwood essential oil by taking the mature agarwood for pulverization and then placing into an extraction kettle, passing carbon-dioxide gas through a heat exchanger and pumping heat once the critical temperature and pressure required for the process is reached to obtain a supercritical fluid, introducing carbon-dioxide supercritical fluid into an extraction vessel to completely contact with the pulverized agarwood, adding a polar solvent into an extraction kettle, and extracting the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing scented wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, produces product having pulp, alcohol, long aftertaste, good taste, rich aroma without medicinal properties, and suitable for the majority of drinkers to drink.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing scented wine, comprises (i) preparing sauce-flavor liquor by (a1) taking completely mature red sorghum and crushing, (a2) adding crushing red sorghum into a fermenter containing distilled water, and uniformly mixing, (a3) adding glucoamylase to the fermenter, controlling the internal temperature at 55-65 degrees C while controlling the pH at 4-4.5, (a4) adding amylase to the fermenter while controlling the internal temperature to 75-85 degrees C while controlling the pH to 6-7.2 and (a5) placing the fermenter in the above step at room temperature for 30 days; and (ii) preparing agarwood essential oil by (b1) taking the mature agarwood for pulverization and then placing into an extraction kettle, (b2) passing carbon-dioxide gas through a heat exchanger and pumping heat once the critical temperature and pressure required for the process is reached to obtain a supercritical fluid, (b3) introducing carbon-dioxide supercritical fluid into an extraction vessel to completely contact with the pulverized agarwood, (b4) adding a polar solvent into an extraction kettle, and (b5) extracting the mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (b2) the critical temperature of carbon-dioxide is 31.16 degrees C, and the critical pressure is 7.38 MPa. The polar solvent of the step (iv) in the step (ii) is methanol or isopropanol. In the step (iii), the proportion of the agarwood oil is 0.06-0.08%.

TI Oil displacement using carbon dioxide flooding additives used for improving oil recovery comprises injecting carbon dioxide flooding additive in the form of follower or pre-slug into oil during carbon dioxide flooding process.
AU YANG Y
   CAO X

GA 201841329G
AB    NOVELTY - Oil displacement using carbon dioxide flooding additives comprises injecting carbon dioxide flooding additive in the form of follower or pre-slug into oil during carbon dioxide flooding process.
   USE - Method for oil displacement using carbon dioxide flooding additives used for improving oil recovery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes mixed phase effect between carbon dioxide gas flooding additive and crude oil, reduces minimum miscibility pressure of carbon dioxide flooding, and is easy to operate and suitable for high viscosity, high paraffin content and low permeability reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oil displacement using carbon dioxide flooding additives comprises injecting 1-30 wt.% carbon dioxide flooding additive in the form of follower or pre-slug into oil during carbon dioxide flooding process. The carbon dioxide flooding additive is benzoate compound of formula (I), where relative molecular mass is 150.07-290.22.
   n=2-12.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The injection process is done by premixing carbon dioxide to form a homogeneous mixture in the premix tank with the additives; and injecting at speed of 3-5 t/d at amount of 0.01-0.1 PV. The oil displacement method is done by premixing 10-30 wt.% carbon dioxide to form a homogeneous mixture in the premix tank with the additives; injecting at speed of 3-5 t/d at amount of 0.01-0.1 PV to form pre-stage plug layer; and oil-displacing. Preferred Components: The total dosage of carbon dioxide flooding additive is 1-10 wt.%.

PN JP7089311-B1
TI Manufacture of calcium carbonate involves            absorbing carbon dioxide gas in sodium hydroxide            aqueous solution to obtain aqueous sodium carbonate            solution, reacting calcium oxide with sodium hydroxide            solution to obtain lime milk, and adding sodium            carbonate aqueous solution to lime milk.
AU SAKAGUCHI H
   EGUCHI K
   ODA T
   NANRI Y
AE SHIRAISHI KOGYO KAISHA LTD (SHRK-C)
GA 202283868K
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of calcium carbonate involves (a)                performing carbon dioxide absorption step of                absorbing carbon dioxide gas in 13-21% sodium                hydroxide aqueous solution to obtain 15-24% or less                aqueous sodium carbonate solution, (b) performing                hydration step of reacting calcium oxide with less                than 0-6% aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide to                obtain lime milk which is a suspension of calcium                hydroxide having BET specific surface area of 5-40                m2/g, and performing carbonation step by adding the                obtained sodium carbonate aqueous solution to the                lime milk and reacting. In the carbonation step,                the initial concentration of the lime milk is                11-19%. The sodium carbonate aqueous solution is                set to a concentration of 15-24% and reacted at                20-40 degrees C to obtain a spindle-shaped calcite                crystalline calcium carbonate having BET specific                surface area of 4-20 m2/g.
   USE - Manufacture of calcium carbonate. Uses include                but are not limited to as papermaking, coating                pigments, plastics, sealants, rubbers, and                foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables transportation to a                production site as a high-concentration sodium                carbonate, suppresses the amount of carbon dioxide                released into the environment as a whole, and                contributes to the improvement of global                warming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide gas                  that is not used in the carbon dioxide gas                  absorption step is used again in the carbon                  dioxide gas absorption step. Preferred Process:                  The method further involves separating the                  filtrate containing sodium hydroxide and calcium                  carbonate, and washing the calcium carbonate                  obtained in the solid-liquid separation step with                  a washing liquid after the carbonation step. The                  method involves adding high-concentration sodium                  hydroxide aqueous solution to the filtrate                  obtained in the solid-liquid separation step and                  the used washing solution obtained in the washing                  step or concentrating the filtrate and the used                  cleaning solution by heating to obtain an aqueous                  solution containing 13-21% sodium hydroxide, and                  the aqueous solution is used in the carbon                  dioxide gas absorption step. An aqueous solution                  obtained by adjusting the filtrate obtained in                  the solid-liquid separation step and the used                  washing solution obtained in the washing step so                  that the concentration of sodium hydroxide is                  less than 6%. Preferred Composition: In the                  carbonation step, the initial concentration of                  the lime milk is 11-24%. The concentration of the                  sodium carbonate aqueous solution is set to                  15-24%, and the reaction is carried out at 40-80                  degrees C to obtain needle-shaped aragonite                  crystalline calcium carbonate having BET specific                  surface area of 3-10 m2/g.

PN WO2021173549-A1; AU2021226734-A1; CA3170335-A1; CN115210174-A; KR2022146581-A
TI Purifying carbon nanostructured material by            oxidizing unpurified carbon nanostructured material            contaminated with metal catalyst particles and            amorphous carbon structures, exposing to flowing gas            comprising carbon monoxide, and heating.
AU GAILUS D
AE NANOCOMP TECHNOLOGIES INC (NANO-Non-standard)
   NANOCOMP TECHNOLOGIES INC (NANO-Non-standard)
GA 2021A1885R
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon nanostructured material is purified                by (a) obtaining unpurified carbon nanostructured                material being contaminated with metal catalyst                particles and amorphous carbon structures; (b)                oxidizing unpurified carbon nanostructured material                by exposure to carbon dioxide at an elevated                temperature, where oxidizing removes amorphous                carbon structures to form carbon dioxide-treated                carbon nanostructured material and carbon monoxide;                (c) exposing carbon dioxide-treated carbon                nanostructured material to flowing gas comprising                carbon monoxide produced in step (b); (d) raising                temperature of carbon dioxide-treated carbon                nanostructured material and flowing gas from 20                degrees C to 200 degrees C to produce purified                carbon nanostructured material and gaseous stream                comprising volatile metal species; and (e)                transporting gaseous stream away from purified                carbon nanostructured material.
   USE - Purification of purified carbon nanostructured                material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The purification method substantially removes                the extraneous amorphous carbon, as well as                metal-containing residual catalyst particles,                without damaging or destroying the carbon                nanotubes. Benefits of the method further include,                but are not limited to, the possibility of                generating sufficient carbon monoxide needed for                forming the carbonyl formation step in situ during                the oxidation step. This may not only reduce the                overall carbon emissions of the method, but may                also reduce the need for a separate carbon monoxide                feedstock.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Quantity of unpurified                  carbon nanostructured material is obtained from a                  floating catalyst chemical vapor deposition                  process. Temperature of carbon dioxide-treated                  carbon nanostructured material and flowing gas is                  raised to greater than or equal to 150 degrees C.                  The method further comprises step (f) passing the                  gaseous stream to a condenser to condense metal                  species comprising iron pentacarbonyl. Condensed                  iron pentacarbonyl is recycled into the floating                  catalyst chemical vapor deposition process for                  producing another quantity of unpurified carbon                  nanostructured material. Passing the gaseous                  stream to the condenser produces a stream of                  carbon monoxide exiting the condenser. Stream of                  carbon monoxide is added to the flowing gas of                  step (c). Preferred Compositions: Metal catalyst                  particles comprise iron. Purified carbon                  nanostructured material contains less than or                  equal to 10 wt.% metal and less than or equal to                  10 wt.% amorphous carbon. Carbon nanostructured                  material comprises single wall carbon nanotubes                  and/or multiwall carbon nanotubes. Preferred                  Conditions: Oxidation in step (b) occurs at                  150-500 degrees C and at 0.01-100 atmospheres.                  Pressure of flowing gas in step (c) is greater                  than or equal to 50 bar.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent stability, activity, product selectivity and conversion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing catalyst, which involves:
   (A) taking spherical nano silicon dioxide carrier and transition metal precursor solution uniformly to obtain suspension;
   (B) adding reducing agent and alkali solution under heating, reacting, cooling, filtering and drying to obtain copper silicon dioxide intermediate;
   (C) mixing copper silicon dioxide intermediate, organic matter containing heteroatom, the organic matter containing the carbon source and the structural assistant for carrying out polymerization reaction;
   (D) carrying out suction filtration to obtain a solid;
   (E) drying, grinding and heating under inert gas protection to obtain copper iron intermediate;
   (F) mixing the intermediate with alkaline aqueous solution;
   (G) filtering and drying the filtrate to obtain solid; and
   (H) grinding and heating under inert gas protection to obtain the catalyst.

AE DAI Q (DAIQ-Individual)
GA 202075558B

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and energy-efficient production of carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device, which comprises the cell (E1) comprising a cathode (C1) and an anode (A1), a feed device (f1) for the starting material, an educt comprising carbon dioxide feed to the cathode (C1), a discharge device (D1) for discharging the mixture comprising formic acid and/or formate, which is connected to the cell (E1), a reactor (R1) for releasing the product comprising carbon monoxide from the mixture, a feed device (f2) for the mixture comprising formic acid and/or formate, which is connected to the reactor (R1) and is designed to feed the mixture comprising formic acid and/or formate to the reactor (R1), and a discharge device (D2) for discharging the product comprising carbon monoxide from the reactor (R1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The cell (E1) comprises at least one anion exchange membrane in a region of cathode (C1), between the cathode (C1) and anode (A1), and at least one cation exchange membrane or bipolar membrane in a region of anode (A1), between the cathode (C1) and anode (A1). The cathode (C1) comprises a metal, preferably copper, bismuth, lead, mercury, indium, tin, cadmium and/or thallium, more preferably lead and/or bismuth. Preferred Process: The release of carbon monoxide from the mixture is performed by dehydration, preferably by heating and/or by heterocatalytic dehydration. The process further involves converting carbon monoxide into hydrocarbon(s) in the presence of a basic electrolyte comprising iron, nickel and/or nickel-iron in an electrolytic cell (E2).

PN CN110950338-A
TI Carbon dioxide capturing involves crushing and            grinding converter steel slag to make steel slag            powder, putting the steel slag powder in rotating            packed bed, and then adding steel-making wastewater to            rotating packed bed.
AU ZHANG H
   DONG J
   ZUO Q
   WEI C
   CAO C
   LIAO C
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 202028297T
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capturing involves crushing and                grinding converter steel slag to a particle size of                100 mu m to make steel slag powder, putting the                steel slag powder in a rotating packed bed, adding                steel-making wastewater to the rotating packed bed                for leaching reaction. The steel slag powder and                steel-making wastewater are provided in a ratio of                1:50-100 kg/l. The obtained liquid phase is calcium                ion-rich leachate. The leachate is heated at 0-80                degrees C, and then passed into the leachate                converter flue gas with stirring for carbonization                reaction. The obtained material is filtered and                separated, and then washed and dried to make nano                calcium carbonate.
   USE - Method for capturing carbon dioxide in                converter flue gas to co-produce high purity nano                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method captures the carbon dioxide in                converter flue gas to co-produce high purity nano                calcium carbonate in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with high value-added product,                and realizes synergistic treatment effect of                converter slag, cold rolling wastewater and                converter waste gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flowchart of                the method for co-producing                p-nitrophenylphosphorylcholine in converter flue                gas. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The converter steel                  slag composition contains 30-60% calcium oxide,                  17-42% silicon dioxide, 0.8-18% iron (III) oxide,                  0.1-13% magnesium oxide, 3-5% aluminum oxide,                  0-1.2% sulfur trioxide, 1.2-2.8% phosphorus                  pentoxide, and 0-2.5% Calcium carbonate. The                  converter flue gas contains 16-30% carbon                  dioxide, 10-40% carbon monoxide, 36-46% nitrogen,                  and 0-2% nitrogen oxides. The nanometer calcium                  carbonate has a particle size of 950 nm.

The acid adjustment tank (20) for adjusting the pH of the contaminated water introduced from the sedimentation tank. The carbon dioxide gas supply unit (30) is used for for injecting carbon dioxide gas into the acid adjustment tank. The pumping tank (40) is used for storing contaminated water having a pH adjusted in the acid adjusting tank. The treatment tank (50) is used for receiving the contaminated water from the pumping tank to process to remove contaminants contained in the contaminated water and a storage tank (60) storing a pH adjuster containing aluminum sulfate. The carbon dioxide gas is flowed into the lower portion of the acid adjuster, and the pH adjuster of the acid adjuster is flowed into the upper portion. The flocculation tank is used for forming a floc by reacting the contaminants contained in the contaminated water and the flocculant.

PN CN109950494-A; CN109950494-B
TI Preparing silicon-carbon composite material by using molten salt electrolysis comprises e.g. performing calcium chloride-wollastonite-calcium oxide molten salt electrolysis process, introducing carbon dioxide gas and dissolving wollastonite.
AU HU L
   HU M
   LIU P
   WU H
   LV X
   XU J
   DANG J
   YU W
   LI T
   YOU Z
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
   UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 201958260L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon-carbon composite material by using molten salt electrolysis comprises performing calcium chloride-wollastonite-calcium oxide molten salt electrolysis process, introducing carbon dioxide gas, dissolving wollastonite in the molten salt to obtain silicate ions, electrolyzing the silicate ions, reducing the silicate ions at the cathode of the electrolytic cell to obtain a nano-silicon material, generating oxygen ions by using the introduced carbon dioxide gas as a source of carbon, using the oxygen ions in the calcium chloride-based molten salt to capture carbon dioxide, fixing it as a carbonate ion, when the carbonate ion reaches the cathode by diffusion in the molten salt, reducing it to a nano-carbon material at the cathode to obtain a silicon-carbon composite material on the cathode.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon-carbon composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has excellent structural stability, flexibility and lubricity of the carbon material, so that the silicon volume can be changed little during the process of intercalating silicon into lithium ions; prevents the problem of the lithium intercalation in the silicon, where the silicon product expands and pulverizes; has high specific capacity of silicon lithium storage and high conductivity of carbon material; and achieves the complementary advantages.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The molten salt temperature is controlled at 650-900 degrees C by controlling the electrolysis voltage at 1.6-2 V, then the mass ratio of three components in calcium chloride-wollastonite-calcium oxide molten salt comprising calcium chloride, wollastonite and calcium oxide is controlled to 25-40:1.5:0.5, and forming a core-shell structured silicon-carbon composite of carbon nanotube-coated silicon nanowires on the cathode material.

PN WO2019006263-A1; CA3068611-A1; AU2018291000-A1; CN110945086-A; EP3645635-A1; KR2020047515-A; US2020165463-A1; BR112019027591-A2; EP3645635-B1; CN110945086-B; AU2018291000-B2
TI Coating used to convert formaldehyde to carbon            dioxide, and as paint for building interior, comprises            alcohol/aldehyde oxidase and formate oxidase            immobilized on solid particulate support, and latex            binder.
AU ROPER J A
   BALIJEPALLI S
   DOLL P
   BOMMARIUS A S
   JONES C W
   MURDOCK C R
   ROBBINS J M
   NOMURA A

   HOLEWINSKI A
   GADDA G
   BLEGERPARRI S
   DOR P
   ROPERIII J A
   STONE P
   JOHN ANDERSON ROPER I
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   ROHM & HAAS CO (ROHM-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
   UNIV GEORGIA STATE RES FOUND (UYGA-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   ROHM & HAAS CO (ROHM-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
   UNIV GEORGIA STATE RES FOUND (UYGA-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES INST (GTEK-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   ROHM & HAAS CO (ROHM-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
   UNIV GEORGIA STATE RES FOUND (UYGA-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   ROHM & HAAS CO (ROHM-C)
   GEORGIA TECH RES CORP (GTEK-C)
   UNIV GEORGIA STATE RES FOUND (UYGA-C)
GA 2019017223
AB    NOVELTY - Coating comprises: an alcohol/aldehyde oxidase                and a formate oxidase, where both the                alcohol/aldehyde oxidase and the formate oxidase                are immobilized on a solid particulate support; and                a latex binder.
   USE - The coating is useful: for conversion of                formaldehyde to carbon dioxide; and as paint for                building interior (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The coating has a color difference quantified                by CIELAB metric DE of less than 5, preferably less                than 2 as measured by ASTM D2244 compared to a                non-enzyme containing control; provides the                formaldehyde which is abated in the air with an                abatement efficiency as defined by at least 75%                removal of starting levels of formaldehyde in the                air as determined spectrophotometrically by test                method JC/T 1074-2008.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a liquid coating composition for the                formation of the coating, comprising an aldehyde                oxidase and a formate oxidase immobilized on a                solid particulate support, and a liquid latex                binder composition;
   (2) forming a coating comprising coating the                liquid coating composition onto a substrate, and                drying the liquid coating composition to form the                coating; and
   (3) converting atmospheric formaldehyde to                carbon dioxide, comprising contacting the coating                with an atmosphere comprising formaldehyde, and                converting at least a portion of the formaldehyde                to carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The enzyme stabilizer                  comprises a monosaccharide, a disaccharide, or an                  oligosaccharide containing 3-10 monosaccharide                  units, preferably where the enzyme stabilizer is                  include sucrose, trehalose, mannitol, sorbitol                  (preferred), xylose, xylitol, mannose, raffinose,                  lactose, maltose and/or galactose. The                  alcohol/aldehyde oxidase has greater than 95%                  sequence homology with an alcohol/aldehyde                  oxidase of SEQ ID NO: 1 (comprising a fully                  defined sequence of 663 amino acids as given in                  the specification), specifically greater than 99%                  sequence homology with SEQ ID NO: 1. The                  alcohol/aldehyde oxidase is of SEQ ID NO: 1                  having a R241K mutation or a N218D mutation. The                  formate oxidase has SEQ ID NO: 2 (comprising a                  fully defined sequence of 578 amino acids as                  given in the specification). The solid                  particulate support is an inorganic support,                  preferably a porous inorganic support. The                  inorganic particulate support has a particle                  diameter of 0.1-800 (preferably 0.5-100) mu m,                  and a pore diameter of 50 Angstrom to 200 nm,                  preferably 100 Angstrom to 50 nm. The solid                  particulate support comprises silica, preferably                  the silica is a mesocellular foam, porous                  microspheres, porous core-shell particles, porous                  nanoparticles and/or particles with a porous                  silica layer. The alcohol/aldehyde oxidase is of                  SEQ ID NO: 1 having Arg-241-Lys mutation or a                  Asn-218-Asp mutation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The coating further                  comprises an enzyme stabilizer, a plasticizer, a                  rheology modifier, a thickener, a film forming                  agent, a surfactant, a preservative, a biocide, a                  mildewcide, a colorant, a defoaming agent, a                  dispersing agent, a drying retarder, an extending                  agent, a pH adjuster and/or a wax. The atmosphere                  is in a building interior.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The latex binder is an                  acrylic latex, an acrylonitrile-butadiene latex,                  an alkyd latex, an ethylene-vinyl acetate latex,                  a natural rubber latex, a neoprene latex, a                  polyamide latex, a polybutadiene latex, a                  polybutylene latex, a polychloroprene latex, a                  polyester latex, a polyisoprene latex, a                  polypropylene latex, a polyurethane latex, a                  polyvinyl alcohol latex, a polyvinyl butyral                  latex, a polyvinyl chloride latex, a                  polyvinylidene chloride latex, a silicone                  emulsion latex, a styrene-acrylic latex, a                  styrene-acrylonitrile latex, a styrene-butadiene                  rubber latex, a styrene-isoprene latex, a vinyl                  acetate latex and/or vinyl-acryl latex,                  preferably where the latex binder comprises an                  acrylic latex, a styrene-acrylic latex and/or a                  vinyl acetate latex.
   EXAMPLE - Sampling bags were cut open to apply coatings                and heat sealed. The enzyme-loaded silica was mixed                using 1 ml of 0.1 M pH 7 potassium phosphate buffer                and 1 ml of acrylic binder and mixed. 500 mu l                aliquots were spread along the inside of the                sampling bag using a plastic applicator with a 0.1                mm thickness to give coatings.

PN CN109028629-A; CN208431976-U
TI Carbon dioxide refrigerant refrigeration system, has compressor connected to oil separator, and refrigerant circulating pump connected with inlet of flooded evaporator, where outlet of flooded evaporator is located in top part of sprayer.
AU ZHANG X
   PANG C
   WEI J
   ZHANG Z
AE SHANDONG SHENZHOU REFRIGERATION EQUIP CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4994N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a refrigerant circulating loop provided with a compressor (1), an oil separator (2), a condenser (3) and a siphon pipe (5). An outlet of the compressor is connected to the oil separator. A refrigerant outlet of the oil separator is connected to the condenser through a three-way valve pipeline. The condenser is connected to a refrigerant reservoir (6) through the siphon pipe. The refrigerant reservoir is connected to a flooded evaporator (7) through an electronic expansion valve (8). An outside of the flooded evaporator is provided with a refrigerant circulating pump (9). The refrigerant circulating pump is connected with an inlet of the flooded evaporator. An outlet of the flooded evaporator is located in a top part of a sprayer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigerant refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has small mounting space, and increases heat exchange between multiple refrigerant i.e. ammonia, and prevents a system main body from pressure so as to improve safety and stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide refrigerant refrigeration method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic circuit diagram of a carbon dioxide refrigerant refrigeration system.
   Compressor (1)
   Oil separator (2)
   Condenser (3)
   Siphon pipe (5)
   Refrigerant reservoir (6)
   Flooded evaporator (7)
   Electronic expansion valve (8)
   Refrigerant circulating pump (9)
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
