PN CN108452464-A
TI Fire alarm device used in automatic fire alarm system, has carbon dioxide fire extinguisher that is respectively provided with connecting pipes at both ends, and fire detector which is arranged in middle of connecting pipe.
AU ZHANG Y
AE ZHANG Y (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 201869236C
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an alarm device main portion (1) which is provided with a black box (17). The black box is connected with a 360-degree panoramic camera (9). The alarm device is provided with a towel slot (15), and comprises a temperature sensing switch (16). The temperature sensing switch is connected to a controller. The controller is connected to a distribution box switch. The alarm device main portion is provided with a carbon dioxide fire extinguisher (2) in a lower portion of a main cavity. The carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is respectively provided with connecting pipes (3, 4) at both ends. A nozzle (5, 6) is arranged at an end of the connecting pipe. A fire detector (7, 8) is arranged in a middle of the connecting pipe.
   USE - Fire alarm device used in automatic fire alarm system.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire alarm device has good stability, good reliability, convenient operation and use, novel design, strong practicability and easy promotion.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the fire alarm device.
   Alarm device main portion (1)
   Carbon dioxide fire extinguisher (2)
   Connecting pipes (3,4)
   Nozzles (5,6)
   Fire detectors (7,8)
   Specific-degree panoramic camera (9)
   Towel slot (15)
   Temperature sensing switch (16)

PT P
PN IN299133-B
TI Producing hydrogen from coal gas or syngas includes e.g. passing coal gas or syngas through membrane contractor to remove carbon dioxide, passing resultant through reactor and passing enriched gas through set of hydrogen selective membranes.
AU MADHUSOODANA C D
   KUMAR P
   DAGANI S M R
   NAKKA S R K
   LTTAGUNTA N M
   DAMACHARLA M R
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 2018605758
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from coal gas or syngas, comprises: passing the coal gas or syngas through a membrane contractor to remove carbon dioxide in feed gas; passing the resulting gas through water gas shift reactor to produce hydrogen; passing the gas through another set of membrane contractor to remove carbon dioxide to produce hydrogen enriched gas; and passing the enriched gas through a set of hydrogen selective membranes to produce high purity hydrogen gas.
   USE - The method and system are useful for producing hydrogen from coal gas or syngas (claimed), where the hydrogen produced is useful in many industrial applications and energy generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system enhance the efficiency of hydrogen production and hydrogen purification and produce hydrogen with greater than 99% purity using hydrogen selective membranes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system comprising membrane contractors (2) capture acid gases e.g. carbon dioxide and hydrogen from the syngas, water gas shift reactor (4) having catalyst with structured packing for containing carbon monoxide to hydrogen in presence of steam, and hydrogen selective membrane then separates hydrogen with 99% purity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic representation of the system.
   Membrane contractors (2)
   Pre-heater (3)
   Water gas shift reactor (4)
   Water gas shift reactor steam (5)
   Water trap (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The syngas is sent to the membrane contactor for removal of carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, where the carbon dioxide depleted syngas is then sent to pre-heater (3) before passing to the water gas shift reactor steam (5) mixed with the pre-heated syngas to produce hydrogen, the stream from water gas shift reactor is passed to a cooled and through a water trap (7), the unconverted stream is recovered and drained through a drain valve , the dry outlet gas from water-gas shift (WGS) reactor is passed through another membrane contractor to remove the carbon dioxide produced in the WGS reactor, and the gas is then passed through a hydrogen selective membrane then the retentate stream may be recycled or passed through another set of membrane to recover hydrogen and the used sorbent which is rich in carbon dioxide coming from membrane contractors are passed through a heater for regeneration of sorbents and then through a disrober column to separate carbon dioxide stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The membrane contractor has hollow fiber membranes through which amine sorbents are passed and feed gas pass through shell side. The carbon dioxide in the feed gas is absorbed in liquid amine. The carbon dioxide depleted gas is passed through a pre-heated which is maintained at 300 degrees C. The gas shift reactor has structured packing of catalyst coated ceramics and the temperature is maintained at 400 degrees C for converting carbon monoxide to hydrogen in the presence of water vapor. The purity of the hydrogen gas can be enhanced by finally passing the gas through another set of palladium membrane.
   EXAMPLE - Feed gas, comprised of 53.17 vol.% of nitrogen, 15.48 vol.% of hydrogen, 18.66 vol.% of carbon monoxide and 12.69 vol.% of carbon dioxide, which was a simulated gas mixture representing syngas/coal gas produced in a gasifier was passed through a membrane contactor system. The membrane contactor has hollow fiber membranes through which amine sorbents were passed and feed gas was passed though shell side. The carbon dioxide in the feed gas was absorbed by liquid amine and the feed gas coming out from the membrane contactor had 60.18 vol.% of nitrogen, 17.27 vol.% of hydrogen, 21.17 vol.% of carbon monoxide, and 1.38 vol.% of carbon dioxide. This carbon dioxide depleted gas was the passed through a pre-heater which was maintained at 300 degrees C. The measured quantity of stream was mixed with this gas stream and then passed to a water gas shift reactor The water gas shift reactor had structured packing of catalyst coated ceramics. The temperature of reactor was maintained at 400 degrees C for converting carbon monoxide to hydrogen in the presence of water vapor The outlet of the reactor was 51.9 vol.% of nitrogen, 28.74 vol.% of hydrogen, 0.16 vol.% of carbon monoxide, 19.2 vol.% of carbon dioxide. This gas was again passed through another membrane contactor system to remove the produced carbon dioxide, so that gas will have only hydrogen and nitrogen. The composition of this enriched hydrogen gas was 63.78 vol.% of nitrogen, 35.2 vol.% of hydrogen, 0.1 vol.% of carbon monoxide and 0.91 vol.% of carbon dioxide. This gas was then passed through a hydrogen selective membrane e.g. zeolite membranes or palladium coated membranes for purifying hydrogen gas. The permeate gas comprised of 97.85 vol.% of hydrogen, 1.62 vol.% of nitrogen, 0.05 vol.% of carbon monoxide and 0.46 vol.% of carbon dioxide.

AU ZHANG Y
   WANG J

   BAI H
AE XIAN LVTAN RESOURCES RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202207201Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified chitosan-based carbon aerogel involves dissolving chitosan in acetic acid to obtain 1-3 wt.% chitosan solution, adding a cross-linking agent to the obtained chitosan solution, stirring the reaction after the reaction is completed, freezing, drying to obtain a chitosan aerogel, performing gradient heat treatment on the obtained chitosan aerogel in an inert atmosphere to obtain a carbon aerogel with a pore size of 250-400 microm, grafting the quaternary ammonium group to the surface of the obtained carbon aerogel, performing suction filtration, washing, drying to obtain the carbon aerogel grafted with the quaternary ammonium group, introducing carbon monoxide into the obtained quaternary ammonium group-grafted carbon aerogel and performing vacuum drying to obtain a modified chitosan-based carbon aerogel.
   USE - Method for preparing modified chitosan-based carbon aerogel used for carbon dioxide capture system in air (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction conditions, simple technological process, easy control of the reaction process, wide source of raw materials, no unnecessary waste generation, cost-effective and environment protection. The prepared modified chitosan-based carbon aerogel is an efficient and easy-to-regenerate adsorbent, has broad application prospects for carbon dioxide adsorption, and has certain application value for environmental protection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a scanning electron microscope image of the prepared modified chitosan-based carbon aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent is selected from glutaraldehyde, formaldehyde or glyoxal.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary ammonium group is selected from allyltrimethylammonium chloride, bisallylbismethylammonium chloride or dimethyldiallylammonium chloride. Preferred Conditions: The crosslinking agent is adding to the prepared chitosan solution, stirring at 50-70degrees Celsius for 2-4 hours, freezing, and drying at -80degrees Celsius for 24 hours to obtain a chitosan aerogel. The quality of chitosan and the volume ratio of the cross-linking agent are 0.5-4:1 (g/mL). The gradient heat treatment is performed by first pre-treating at 400degrees Celsius for 1 hours at a heating rate of 2degrees Celsius/minutes, and treating at 800-900degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours at a heating rate of 5degrees Celsius/minutes. The carbon aerogel grafted to the obtained quaternary ammonium group is soaking in a 0.5-0.75 M sodium carbonate solution for 4-6 hours.

PN CN113600135-A
TI Preparation of three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material used for carbon dioxide adsorption, involves mixing graphene oxide dispersion with organic amine, hydrothermally reacting mixture to obtain hydrogel, washing hydrogel with water, freezing and drying.
AU WU J
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2021C9029R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material involves: mixing graphene oxide dispersion with organic amine, and hydrothermally reacting to obtain a hydrogel; and washing the hydrogel with water, freezing and drying.
   USE - Preparation method of three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material used in acid gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic amines with relatively high amino content are selected as the crosslinking agent and reducing agent, and the three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material is prepared by a one-step hydrothermal method, which is simple to operate and low in cost. Organic amines are rich in amino groups, which provides adsorption sites for the adsorption of acid gases, with strong selectivity and high adsorption capacity, and the adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide reaches 2.27 mmol/g. The organic amine molecules intercalate between the graphene sheets, which effectively reduces the agglomeration between graphenes. The freeze-drying technology gives the material a multi-level pore structure and increases the specific surface area of the material. The BrunauerEmmettTeller specific surface area reaches 37 m2/g, regeneration performance of the three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material is stable, with 10 adsorption regeneration times, and the regeneration rate reaches 91%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the three-dimensional porous graphene aerogel material prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine and/or polyethyleneimine with a weight average molecular weight of 600-10000. The weight ratio of graphene oxide to organic amine is 1:1-5, preferably 1:3. The concentration of the graphene oxide dispersion liquid is 2-6 , preferably 4mg/mL.Preferred Process: The hydrothermal reaction takes place at 140-180degreesC for 2-12 hours and pH is adjusted to 8 before mixing the graphene oxide dispersion liquid with the organic amine and washed with water until the pH of the supernatant is neutral. The freezing is liquid nitrogen freezing, the drying is vacuum freeze drying. The hydrothermal reaction takes place at at 180 degreesC for 12 hours. The pH adjustment adopts a 0.1M sodium hydroxide solution. The freezing time is 10-30 minutes. The vacuum freeze-drying time is 48-72 hours.

PT P
PN CN112604030-A; WO2022116464-A1
TI Preparing acellular matrix by decellularizing bone tissues and organs and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on product.
AU XU W
AE GUANGDONG MEDICAL INSTR INST (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG ACAD SCI MEDICINE & HEALTH INS (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202138353K
AB    NOVELTY - Method (M1) for preparing acellular matrix involves (a) decellularizing bone tissues and organs and (b) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the product obtained in step (a).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing acellular matrix.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method uses supercritical carbon dioxide as the extractant, which is easier to diffuse into the micropores of bone tissues and organs than liquid, and has good solute capacity, makes the microporosity of the bone tissue more significant, which is conducive to the induction of bone growth after transplantation, does not use toxic chemicals, hence is safe and prepares acellular matrix which has excellent biocompatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) acellular matrix prepared by performing the above-mentioned method, where the acellular matrix has a three-dimensional porous structure;
   (2) bone repair scaffold comprising the acellular matrix and drug-loaded microspheres, where the drug-loaded microspheres are adsorbed on the surface and the pores of the acellular matrix; and
   (3) method (M2) for preparing the bone repair scaffold, involves (a) preparing drug-loaded microspheres and (b) uniformly mixing the drug-loaded microspheres with the acellular matrix so that the drug-loaded microspheres cover the surface of the acellular matrix and fill the pores of the acellular matrix and incubating at 37-55 degrees C until the drug-loaded microspheres adhere to the acellular matrix, where the mass ratio of the drug-loaded microspheres to the acellular matrix is 1:(3-10).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves decellularizing bone tissues and organs with phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer solution containing EDTA, then immersing in tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (tris) buffer solution containing sodium dodecyl sulfate and then washing with a PBS buffer solution, where the bone tissues and organs are chosen from at least one of cancellous bone and cortical bone. The step (b) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 15-20 MPa and 35-40 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The drug-loaded microspheres comprise carrier microspheres and drugs loaded on the carrier microspheres, where the drug comprises bone repair drugs and/or growth factors, and the carrier microsphere comprises degradable polyester and mesoporous bioglass. The drug is chosen from at least one of bone morphogenetic protein-2, bone morphogenetic protein-7, vascular endothelial growth factor, alendronate sodium, naringin and resveratrol, the carrier The drug loading rate of the drug microspheres is 40-80%. The drug-loaded microspheres have drug loading rate of 40-80%. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves mixing the drug with the mesoporous bioglass to obtain a mixed powder, then dispersing the mixed powder in the degradable polyester solution to obtain a blended liquid, dripping the blended liquid to the aqueous solution of the surfactant and then performing the solid-liquid separation to prepare the drug-loaded microspheres.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The degradable polyester is chosen from at least one of polylactic acid, polylactic acid-glycolic acid copolymer, polycaprolactone, poly-3-hydroxyalkanoate, poly(3-hydroxybutyrate), poly(3-hydroxybutyrate)-co-(3-hydroxyvalerate), polytrimethylene carbonate, and polybutylene succinate. The degradable polyester has molecular weight of 1-6 million Da.

TI On-board air quality adjustment system has air filter module which activates air filter to filter outside air entering car air conditioner according to control signal output by central processing module.
AU NI Y
AE MAANSHAN CAINI AUTOMOBILE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MAAN-Non-standard)
GA 202121574C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a central processing module which analyzes the carbon dioxide concentration data in the air in a car, when a car air conditioner is on, and outputs the control signal to a mode control module to enter the air circulation mode, when the carbon dioxide concentration data is lower than the preset threshold. The central processing module outputs the control signal to a mode control module to enter the air external circulation mode, and analyzes the PM2.5 concentration data in the outside air, when the carbon dioxide concentration data is higher than the preset threshold. The mode control module controls the air circulation mode of the car air conditioner to enter the outer circulation mode or the inner circulation mode according to the control signal output by the central processing module. An air filter module activates an air filter to filter the outside air entering the car air conditioner according to the control signal output by the central processing module.
   USE - On-board air quality adjustment system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes smart monitoring of the air in the car, thus ensuring safety and health of driver and passenger. The system has better economic benefit and development prospect.

PN CN111579789-A; CN111579789-B
TI Detecting varicella-zoster virus titer, by inoculating diploid cells into plate, placing cells in carbon dioxide incubator, diluting prepared virus sample, inoculating sample to plate, adding maintenance solution and detecting titer.
AU LI C
   ZHAO H
   LIANG H
   YAN L
   WANG W
   ZHU X
   ZHOU H
   SHEN H
AE CHANGCHUN QIJIAN BIOLOGY PROD CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202084660B
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorescent detection method for detecting varicella-zoster virus titer, involves inoculating diploid cells into 96-well plate, placing the cells in a carbon dioxide incubator, diluting the prepared virus sample, inoculating the sample into the above 96-well plate, adding maintenance solution, placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for culture, obtaining 96-well plate after culture, discarding the culture medium, fixing in the fixative, adding primary antibody for incubation, where the primary antibody is a specific VZV monoclonal antibody, adding fluorescently-labeled secondary antibody to continue the incubation after washing the 96-well plate incubated with the primary antibody, where the secondary antibody is an anti-species antibody, and detecting with a fluorescence quantitative detector to calculate the virus titer of the sample after washing the 96-well plate incubated with the secondary antibody.
   USE - The fluorescent detection method for detecting varicella-zoster virus titer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method detects varicella-zoster virus titer rapidly with high specificity, high sensitivity and high accuracy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for antibody capable of binding to varicella-zoster virus, which comprises antibody light chain variable region sequence comprising 111 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 3) fully defined in the specification and antibody heavy chain variable region sequence comprising 119 amino acid sequence (SEQ ID NO: 4) fully defined in the specification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The diploid cells are diploid cells sensitive to varicella-zoster virus, preferably human embryonic lung diploid cell 2BS cell line, or MRC-5 cell line, or KMB-17 cell line. The specific VZV antibody is directed against VZV and its glycoprotein E (VZV-gE), glycoprotein B (VZV-gB), glycoprotein H (VZV-gH) polyclonal antibodies and monoclonal antibodies. The secondary antibody is a fluorescein-labeled anti-species antibody, including anti-mouse, rats, and anti-rabbit and anti-human anti-species antibodies. The number of inoculated cells is 2-5x 105/ml, culture condition is 35-40 degrees C, carbon dioxide concentration is 4-6%, and culture time is 20-48 hours. The culture conditions are 35-40 degrees C, 4-6% carbon dioxide concentration, and culture for 60-84 hours. The number of cell inoculations is 2-4x 105/ml, the culture condition is 37 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 5%, and the culture time is 24 hours. The culture conditions are 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide concentration, and culture for 72 hours. The dilution is diluted with a diluent, and every time after dilution. The dilution solution comprises phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), 50% sucrose, 10% sodium glutamate, 10% fetal bovine serum, minimum essential medium (MEM), 2% newborn bovine serum, 1% glutamine and 2% sodium bicarbonate. The virus quantity is 50 mu l 2-50 plaque-forming unit (PFU), preferably 5-30 PFU. The maintenance solution comprises MEM, 5% newborn calf serum and 1% glutamine. The fixative agent comprises 90% ethanol and 10% formaldehyde. The fixation agent comprises 100-300 ml fixative per well, and maintained at room temperature for 10-30 minutes. The fixation solution comprises 90% ethanol and 10% formaldehyde. The fixing condition is 15 minutes at room temperature. The incubation conditions are: 37 degrees C or 25 degrees C or room temperature, the incubation time is 0.5-2 hours. The incubation time is 18-24 hours. The incubation temperature is 37 degrees C and the incubation time is 1 hour. The washing liquid is tris(hydroxymethyl)aminomethane (Tris)-buffered saline and Tween-20 (RTM: Polyoxyethylene sorbitol ester) (TBST) washing liquid. The washing liquid comprises PBS, 0.5-2% Tween 20 (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate), and the washing times are 2-5 times. The washing conditions are: PBS, 1% Tween 20 (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate) and the number of washing times is 3 times. The specific VZV monoclonal antibody is a human immunoglobulin (Ig)G monoclonal antibody. The antibody is a human IgG type antibody.

PT P
PN CN111257506-A; CN111257506-B
TI Fuel sulfur content automatic recognition algorithm, has set of instructions for calculating increment integral of Sulfur dioxide, and calculating sulfur-to-carbon ratio of true peak to calculate fuel sulfur content.
AU PENG S
   SU N
   HU J
AE TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
   TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
GA 202053573C
AB    NOVELTY - The algorithm has set of instructions for obtaining data of Sulfur dioxide and Carbon dioxide concentration changes with time within monitoring point time. Time scale is determined. Time series are calculated. Peak time of a suspect peak is calculated. A local maximum value of the Sulfur dioxide concentration in a neighborhood near the given suspect time is calculated. A time point corresponding to the peak is recorded. Starting time of the suspect peak is calculated. The suspect peak is determined as a false peak When there no time satisfies formula. An end time of each suspect peak is calculated. True and false of the suspect peak are determined. Increment integral of Sulfur dioxide and Carbon dioxide concentration for each true peak are calculated. Sulfur-to-carbon ratio of the true peak is calculated to calculate fuel sulfur content.
   USE - Fuel sulfur content automatic recognition algorithm.
   ADVANTAGE - The algorithm enables calculating the sulfur content of fuel oil based on the sulfur-to-carbon ratio of the exhaust gas, automatically identifying the suspected ship that uses fuel oil with excessive sulfur content if the identification result is inaccurate and has no quantitative basis so as to provide basis and support for maritime supervision.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graph view illustrating a relationship between sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide concentration changes with time for fuel sulfur content automatic recognition algorithm.

PN CN110286593-A; CN110286593-B
TI Method for controlling CO2 capture system after            chemical adsorption combustion based on multi-target            predictive control, involves minimizing performance            index, and performing continuous controlling process            according to collecting system.
AU WU X
   JIA D
   SHEN J
   LI Y
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2019840483
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a carbon dioxide                trap rate and a reboiler temperature as controlled                variables in a carbon dioxide capture system after                chemical is adsorbed. A lean liquid stream and the                reboiler steam flow rate are selected. A state                space model of the carbon dioxide capture system is                established. A prediction model is established in a                predictive control. A controller parameter,                prediction time domain sampling time, a control                time domain, a carbon emission weight and a steam                extraction flow weight are set. A terminal                constraint time is reached at each sampling time. A                performance index is minimized. A system barren                solution flow rate is obtained. A continuous                controlling process is performed according to a                collecting system.
   USE - Method for controlling CO2 capture system                after chemical adsorption combustion based on                multi-target predictive control.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables constructing a performance                index with terminal restriction function, and can                comprehensively reflect the system carbon emission                and extraction energy consumption in the design                framework of model predictive control, so that the                system can dynamically and flexibly adjust the                carbon emission reduction function, and realizing                the optimization control of the carbon capture                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system                for controlling a CO2 capture system after chemical                adsorption combustion based on multi-target                predictive control. '(Drawing includes non-English                language text)'

PN CN109965049-A; CN109965049-B
TI Preparing active ingredient by separating and extracting green tea comprises e.g. roughly crushing tea, crushing the tea leaves, mixing crushed tea leaves with pure water, and treating the mixture containing tea leaves and purified water.
AU YANG X
   HE J
   YIN G
   ZHANG X
   ZHENG X
AE BY-HEALTH CO LTD (BYHE-Non-standard)
GA 201960634Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing active ingredient by separating and extracting green tea comprises (1) roughly crushing the tea, and crushing the tea leaves to 5-20 meshes; (2) mixing the crushed tea leaves obtained in the step (1) with pure water, using the pure water in an amount of 10-40% of the tea quality, and placing the mixture containing the tea leaves and purified water under the environment of 110-140 degrees C for 10-90 seconds to obtain mixture A; (3) adding 100-40% by mass of ethanol to the mixture A, and suffocating the mixture A after adding ethanol, so thoroughly mixing the tea powder and the ethanol, and the treatment time is 60-240 seconds to obtain a mixture B; and (4) carrying out supercritical extraction, placing the mixture B in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle, treating with supercritical carbon dioxide at a specific extraction temperature and pressure, after processing, and collecting mixture C to obtain the decaffeinated tea powder.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing active ingredient by separating and extracting green tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high removal efficiency of caffeine, saves the time for decaffeination of supercritical carbon dioxide, and has less loss of tea polyphenols during the process of removing caffeine, and In particular, ingredients including catechins has been retains to a large extent.

PT P
PN CN109926017-A
TI High-performance spherical lithium silicate-based carbon dioxide adsorption particle.
AU QIN C
   MA L
   RAN J
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 201959113U
AB    NOVELTY - High-performance spherical lithium silicate-based carbon dioxide adsorption particle, is claimed. The diameter of the adsorption particle is 0.2-1.5 mm, mass fraction of lithium silicate is 80-90%, and the mass fraction of potassium carbonate is 10-20%.
   USE - The particle is useful for adsorbing carbon dioxide, methane-steam adsorption enhanced reforming and biomass-steam vaporization hydrogen production.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle has uniform particle size, high adsorption capacity, stable cycle absorption-desorption performance, compression resistance and wear resistance. The method utilizes raw material of low cost, is simple and reliable, and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the adsorption particles, comprising (i) taking Massage Erbi, Li2CO3, vapor phase silica, potassium carbonate in ratio of (2-2.2):1:(0.05-0.0.25) and mixing evenly, calcining in air at 700-800 degrees C, cooling after 4-8 hours, (ii) pulverizing and grinding the bulk material formed after cooling into a powdery lithium silicate based adsorbent material, and mixing with the polymer pore former and water, (iii) slowly placing the material prepared in (ii) into a screw extruder to extrude the cylindrical material and perform natural drying, (iv) cutting the obtained cylindrical material into short cylinders, where the height of the short cylinder is similar to the length of the diameter, and placeing in a high-speed rounding machine for rounding into a ball, and naturally drying for 24-48 hours, and (v) calcining the shaped particles for 0.5-2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the pore-forming agent is a polyethylene, a polystyrene-based polymer and is easily pyrolyzed, and the pore-forming agent has a particle diameter of 10-50 mu m. In the step (ii), The amount of the polymer pore-forming agent is 0-30 wt.%, and the amount m of water is determined according to the total mass n of lithium silicate and the pore former, where m:n is 1:4-1:6.

PN CN109351144-A
TI Purifying production system for converter gas, has pressure swing adsorption decarburization device which subjects fine desulfurized gas to remove sulfide and carbon dioxide to obtain decarburized gas.
AU GUO Z
   ZHANG B
AE EAST CHINA ENG SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 201919278N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has temperature change adsorption decontamination device which subjects compressed gas to initially remove oil, dust, water, phosphine and hydrogen fluoride in the compressed gas to obtain a degassing gas. The degassing gas is converted into hydrogen sulfide by the deoxygenation desulfurization device, and the carbonyl sulfide in the degassing gas is hydrolyzed to hydrogen sulfide, and the oxygen in the miscellaneous gas is removed to obtain an oxygen desulfurization gas. The desulfurization gas is removed from the mercaptan and dimethyl disulfide by a fine desulfurization device to obtain a fine desulfurization gas. The fine desulfurized gas is subjected to a pressure swing adsorption decarburization device to remove sulfide and carbon dioxide to obtain decarburized gas. The decarbonized gas is removed by the alcohol amine decarburization device to remove carbon dioxide, mechanical impurities and free liquid to obtain purified gas.
   USE - Purifying production system for converter gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The degassing system of converter gas adopts low-temperature circulation control of degassing gas to control the oxygen content of converter gas below 0.7%, which reduces the temperature rise of reaction, avoids the disproportionation reaction of carbon monoxide, increases system safety and reduces energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a purification production process of a converter gas purification production system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the dust removal system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The purified gas has a dust content of less than 0.1 ppm (m%) and a total sulfur content of less than 0.1 ppm (m%), oxygen content less than 30ppm (v%), carbon dioxide content less than or equal to 20ppm (v%), phosphine, hydrogen fluoride less than 0.1ppm (v%). The carbon monoxide content in the purified gas is greater than 60%.

PN CN109096341-A
TI New complex useful in nanomaterial and catalyst as carbon dioxide reduction cathode catalyst, prepared by e.g. taking solution I containing palladium chloride and solution II containing cucurbituril compound as raw material.
AU CHEN R
   CAO M
   LI H
   CAO R
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 201902454S
AB    NOVELTY - Complex (I) are new. (I) belongs to monoclinic system having I2/c space group and unit cell parameters are: a is 13-13.1 Angstrom , b is 17.5-17.6 Angstrom , c is 22.3-22.4 Angstrom , alpha and gamma is 90 degrees , beta is 94.9-95 degrees and Z is 4.
   USE - (I) are useful in nanomaterial and catalyst as carbon dioxide reduction cathode catalyst (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) reduces the cathode catalyst over-potential by carbon dioxide and improves selectivity and stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Complex of formula L.2(H3O).(PdCl4).xH2O (I) are new. (I) belongs to monoclinic system having I2/c space group and unit cell parameters are: a is 13-13.1 Angstrom , b is 17.5-17.6 Angstrom , c is 22.3-22.4 Angstrom , alpha and gamma is 90 degrees , beta is 94.9-95 degrees and Z is 4.
   x = number of moles of free water per mole of the complex i.e. less than or equal to 6-8; and
   L = cucurbituril compound molecule including five-membered ring compound molecule or six-membered ring compound molecule.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIM are included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) nanomaterial obtained after reduction of the complex (I); and
   (3) catalyst comprising (I) and nano-palladium wrapped in cucurbituril.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises taking solution I containing palladium chloride and solution II containing cucurbituril compound as raw material using solution diffusion method. Preferred Components: The complex is Me10CB(5)-(H2O).2H3O(PdCl4).6H2O (II), where (II) belongs to monoclinic system having I2/c space group and unit cell parameters are: a is 13.03-13.04 Angstrom , b is 17.53-17.54 Angstrom , c is 22.31-22.32 Angstrom , alpha and gamma is 90 degrees , beta is 94.97-94.98 degrees , and Z is 4. The solution diffusion method is H-type diffusion tube and the time is not greater than 3 hours; molar concentration of hydrogen ions and palladium chloride of solution I is 0.025-0.035 mol/l and 0.001-0.017 mol/l respectively and the molar concentration of cucurbituril compound in the solution II is 0.001-0.010 mol/l. Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) adding deionized water and hydrochloric acid to palladium chloride to form transparent aqueous solution of palladium chloride is solution I, (ii) dissolving Me10CB(5) in deionized water under stirring to obtain solution II, and (iii) transferring the solution I and solution II to one side of the reaction device and diffusion. The reduction of complex comprises hydrogenating at 250-350 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and heating complex at 250-350 degrees C for 1-10 hours under hydrogen atmosphere to obtain nano-material, where the composition of the hydrogen atmosphere is 5% hydrogen and 95% nitrogen.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN CN109077186-A; CN109077186-B
TI Use of fresh fish oil after fractionation or combination of above two oils and other oils in piglet feed, which is prepared by degassing, fresh water fish oil into fractionation tank, filling carbon dioxide in fish oil and discharging.
AU LI X
   WEN S
   WEN C
AE GUANGZHOU ZHIRUN OIL & FOODS IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019017188
AB    NOVELTY - Use of fresh fish oil after fractionation or combination of above two oils and other oils is claimed in piglet feed, where amount of grease in feed is 0.5-10%.
   USE - Fresh fish oil is used in piglet feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The fresh fish oil reduces=production of stress reaction syndrome, and promotes growth of piglets.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing fresh fish oil, which involves:
   (A) degassing, fresh water fish oil into fractionation tank, filling carbon dioxide in fish oil and discharging continuously replace gas, where ventilating rate of carbon dioxide is 0.5-2 L/minute per kilogram of grease and ventilation time control, heating fish oil for 18-25 minutes;
   (B) eliminating crystalline memory, heating to 60-70 degrees C, keeping constant temperature for 10-70 minutes;
   (C) cooling at 0.1-0.3 degrees C per minute, reducing temperature to 20-25 degrees C, and keeping for 30-100 minutes;
   (D) restoring temperature to 32-35 degrees C, keeping for 50-100 minutes;
   (E) controlling cooling speed, cooling at 0.5-1 degrees C per 30 minutes, cooling to 18-20 degrees C, keeping for 100-300 minutes; and
   (F) separating crystallized product to obtain fresh fish oil which is fractionation product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The greases are coconut oil and modified deep sea fish oil. The compound oil includes fish oil, modified deep seafish oil, and coconut oil. The fish oil is mixture of freshwater fish oil and fresh water fish oil after fractionation. The modified deep sea fish oil is deep sea fish oil and short chain fatty acid grease ester exchange treatment. The short chain fatty acid oil is coconut oil. Preferred Process: The modified deep sea fish oil is obtained by performing trans esterification of deep sea fish oil and coconut oil. The piglet feed is applied to feed for sow inlactation. The fish oil is fresh fish oil and fractionation mixture in ratio 1-5:0.5-2. The fresh fish oil and fractionation freshwater fish oil is selected from carp, carp, crucian, slag in fish. The modified deep-sea fish oil is processed by taking equal quantity of deep sea fish oil and coconut oil into reaction tank, homogenizing and mixing, dehydrating invacuum degassing, adding 4-18% immobilized lipase to reaction tank, stirring for reaction,reaction time control after finishing reaction at 1-4 hours, and filtering to remove immobilized lipase to obtain ester exchange product to modified deep sea fish oil.    USE - The recombinant microorganism is useful for                preparing useful material chosen from butanol,                isobutanol, hexanol, heptanol, octanol, nanolol,                decanol, tert-butanol, 1-butanol,                2-methyl-1-butanol, 2-methyl-2-butanol isobutanol,                putrescine, L-ornithine, arginine, polynuclear                aromatic hydrocarbons (PHA), polylactate,                polylactate-co-glycolate, polyisobalate,                polyhydroxybutyrate (PHB), 4-hydroxybutyrate,                biodiesel, gasoline, olefin, gamma -iminobutyric                acid, 3-hydroxpyropionic acid, 3-aminopropionic                acid, acrylic acid, 1,3-diaminopropane,                caprolactam, threonine, valine, isoleucine, fumaric                acid, astaxanthin, silibin, lycopene, lutein and                retinol, and C3 compound (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for preparing a useful substance                comprising an C3 compound as an intermediate                product involves (a) cultivating the recombinant                microorganism with a carbon source of formic acid                and carbon dioxide to produce a C3 compound as an                intermediate product, and (b) recovering the                resultant useful material; and
   (2) method for producing a C3 compound                involves (a) culturing the recombinant                microorganism in a source of formic acid and carbon                dioxide to produce a C3 compound, and (b)                recovering the resulting C3 compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is                  given.Preferred Microorganism: The recombinant                  microorganism further comprises a gene encoding                  pyruvate formate lyase or a recombinant vector                  containing the gene. Preferred Biomolecule: The                  gene encoding an enzyme is chosen from                  formate-tetrahydrofolate ligase, methenyl                  tetrahydrofolate cyclohydrolase, and                  methylene-tetrahydrofolate dehydrogenase. The                  gene encoding for the formate-tetrahydrofolate                  ligase comprises nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID                  NO: 7. The gene coding for methenyl                  tetrahydrofolate cyclohydrolase comprises nucleic                  acid sequence of SEQ ID NO: 8 or SEQ ID NO: 19.                  The gene encoding methylene-tetrahydrofolate                  dehydrogenase comprises nucleic acid sequence of                  SEQ ID NO: 9. The host microorganism comprises                  (i) a central carbon assimilation circuit is                  implanted, and (ii) a recombinant microorganism                  where a central carbon assimilation circuit is                  introduced from outside. The central carbon                  assimilation circuit may comprise at least one                  chosen from serine-glyoxylate transaminase,                  malyl-CoA lyase, malate-CoA ligase,                  succinyl-CoA:(S)-malate CoA-transferase,                  phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase, and                  hydroxypyruvate reductase, where the enzyme                  involved in any one or more of the central carbon                  assimilation circuits of the recombinant                  microorganisms is substituted, further                  introduced, or amplified. The recombinant                  microorganisms in which the enzyme is                  substituted, further introduced, or amplified                  chosen from Methylobacterium, Roseobacter,                  Rhodobacter, Chloroflexus, Acetobacterium,                  Mannheimia, and Arabidopsis. The                  serine-glyoxylate transaminase encoded by a                  nucleic acid molecule is chosen from basepair                  sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 6, 13, 16, and 21. The                  malyl-CoA lyase encoded by a nucleic acid                  molecule is chosen from basepair sequences of SEQ                  ID NOs: 5, 14, and 15. The malate-CoA ligase                  encoded by a nucleic acid molecule is chosen from                  basepair sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 3, 4, 11 and                  12. The succinyl-CoA:(S)-malate CoA-transferase                  is encoded by a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID                  NO: 17 or 18. The phosphoenolpyruvate                  carboxykinase is encoded by a nucleic acid                  sequence of SEQ ID NO: 20. The hydroxypyruvate                  reductase is encoded by a nucleic acid sequence                  of SEQ ID NO: 10. The gene encoding for the                  pyrubate formate lyase comprises nucleic acid                  sequence of SEQ ID NO: 22. The gene is                  overexpressed by any one strong promoter chosen                  from trc promoter, tac promoter, T7 promoter, lac                  promoter and trp promoter. The glycine cleavage                  complex is enriched or amplified or further                  introduced. The glycine cleavage complex                  comprises a nucleic acid sequence of SEQ ID NO:                  63. The gene constituting the glycine cleavage                  complex is introduced by introducing a plasmid                  containing the gene constituting the glycine                  cleavage complex, where the intrinsic promoter of                  the gene is replaced with any one the                  above-mentioned strong promoter. The gcvR gene is                  deleted. The gcvR gene comprises nucleic acid                  molecule of SEQ ID NO: 66. The serine diamine is                  enriched or amplified or further introduced. The                  serine diamine comprises a nucleic acid sequence                  of SEQ ID NO: 69. The recombinant microorganism                  is capable of biosynthesis of pyruvic acid,                  glycine or serine assimilated from formic acid                  and carbon dioxide. The host microorganism is                  chosen from Escherichia, Mannheimia, Rhodobacter,                  and Methylobacterium. The recombinant                  microorganism is chosen from Methylobacterium,                  Roseobacter, Rhodobacter, Chloroflexus,                  Acetobacterium, Mannheimia, Escherichia and                  Arabidopsis. Sequences not defined here may be                  found at                  ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Covalent organic framework used in carbon dioxide            gas collector and catalyst composition, comprises            triazine group formed through nitrile trimerization of            imidazole unit, and has porous structure to capture            gas.
AU YOON S H
   PARK K H
   LEE G S
   LEE K Y
   YUNSUNGHO
   HO P K
   LEE G
   LEEKWANGYEOL
AE UNIV KOOKMIN IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UYKO-C)
GA 2018522615
AB    NOVELTY - Covalent organic framework comprises a                triazine group formed through nitrile trimerization                of imidazole unit (I).
   USE - Covalent organic framework used in carbon                dioxide gas collector and catalyst composition (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The covalent organic framework has porous                structure to capture gas, improves selective                adsorption efficiency of carbon dioxide to                nitrogen, prevents exhaust of air, and stably fixes                transition metals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Covalent organic framework comprises a                triazine group formed through nitrile trimerization                of imidazole unit of general formula (I).
   Ra1 and Ra2=hydrogen;
   Rb1, Rb2, Rb3 and Rb4=independently represent                hydrogen, 1-4C alkyl group, a hydroxy group, a                methoxy group, a nitro group, an amine group, a                halogen group, a carboxyl group, an                alkylcarboxylate group or form a benzene ring fused                to parent;
   Rc1, Rc2, Rc3, Rd1, Rd2 and Rd3=independently                represent hydrogen, 1-4C alkyl group, a hydroxyl                group, a methoxy group, a nitro group, an amine                group, a halogen group, a carboxyl group or form a                fused benzene ring to the parent;
   A=C, N, P, O or S;
   p1 and p2=1 or 2;
   q1 and q2=0 or 1; and
   n=0 or 1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing covalent organic framework, which                involves providing monomers of general formula (I)                and then heating the units in an ionic liquid to                perform a nitrile trimerization reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  is selected from iridium, palladium, platinum,                  molybdenum, iron, cobalt, nickel, ruthenium,                  rhodium, rhenium, gold, chromium, silver,                  tungsten, zinc, aluminum or manganese. The                  heating is carried out at 250-500 degrees C. The                  ionic liquid is zinc chloride. The gas is carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The imidazole unit                  also has general formulae (II-III). The organic                  skeleton also comprises a transition metal                  chelated by the imidazole unit of general formula                  (I). The organic skeleton is formed by nitrile                  trimerization of the linker of general formula                  (IV) and the imidazole unit of formula (I). The                  linker is benzene-1,4-dicarbonitrile or                  (1,1'-biphenyl)-4,4'-dicarbonitrile.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107890621-A; CN107890621-B
TI Preparing hexavalent chromium toxic solid waste involves taking pre-treated chromium (IV) containing toxic solid waste, adding sodium bicarbonate powder as activator for grinding in grinding process, protecting under carbon dioxide.
AU LIN Z
   LIU W
   LIU X
   DENG H
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018289239
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hexavalent chromium toxic solid waste involves taking pre-treated chromium (IV) containing toxic solid waste, adding sodium bicarbonate powder as activator for grinding in the grinding process, protecting under carbon dioxide protection to prevent the organic substance impurities remained in the waste material of the explosion, grinding and sieving the waste, putting the sieved powder into the reactor, heating at 180-300 degrees C and reacting for 1.5-3 hours, water injection cooling in the reactor, adding sodium carbonate as mineralizer stirring mixing uniformly, reacting for 3-10 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing hexavalent chromium toxic solid waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare hexavalent chromium toxic solid waste in cost effective manner which greatly improves the processing efficiency of the waste slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hexavalent chromium toxic solid waste involves taking pre-treated chromium (IV) containing toxic solid waste, adding sodium bicarbonate powder as activator for grinding in the grinding process, protecting under carbon dioxide protection to prevent the organic substance impurities remained in the waste material of the explosion, grinding and sieving the waste, putting the sieved powder into the reactor, heating at 180-300 degrees C and reacting for 1.5-3 hours, water injection cooling in the reactor, adding sodium carbonate as mineralizer stirring mixing uniformly, reacting for 3-10 hours at 70-200 degrees C, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging and separating to obtain desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The toxic hexavalent chromium pretreatment comprises the chromium (VI) containing toxic solid waste after simple sorting, performing primary crushing process. The toxic hexavalent chromium (VI) containing toxic solid waste is industrial waste residue of magnesium hydroxide and calcium carbonate as the main component. The flow rate of carbon dioxide is 20-40 L/minute. The toxic hexavalent chromium containing harmless treatment method for solid waste, where rate of the temperature rise to 15-25 degrees C per minute, the speed of the cooling is 5-10 degrees C/minute. The preserving temperature of the reaction is 120-150 degrees C, toxic hexavalent chromium is added, followed by the addition of activator at 200-230 degrees C and mineralizer. Preferred Composition: The device for grinding is ball mill and hole diameter of the sieve is 200-500 meshes. The mass ratio of the sodium bicarbonate as activator and solid waste is 0.2-1:1. The mass ratio of sodium carbonate used as mineralizer and solid waste is 0.2-0.7:1. The mass ratio of injection water solid waste material and water is 1:1 to 5.

PN CN107653191-A
TI Culturing layer of Nannochloropsis oceanica by sterilizing closed reactor, preparing medium, installing glow sticks in reactor, inoculating Nannochloropsis oceanica, introducing carbon dioxide and culturing Nannochloropsis oceanica.
AU LIU X
   WU P
   SHEN F
   FENG L
AE DONGTAI CIBAINIAN BIOLOGICAL ENG CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2018114705
AB    NOVELTY - Method of culturing layer of Nannochloropsis oceanica involves sterilizing a closed reactor, preparing medium in the closed reactor, evenly installing several glow sticks in the reactor, inoculating Nannochloropsis oceanica in the medium, evenly stirring the contents, installing top-down multi-layer film in the sealed reactor, coating each layer of thin film coated with N.oceanica, evenly introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor and culturing N.oceanica.
   USE - The method is useful for culturing layer of N.oceanica.
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves energy, ensures that the algae species in the medium can get enough weak light due to glow stick, utilizes improved culture medium and generates heat which is suitable for the propagation of the N.oceanica.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of culturing layer of Nannochloropsis oceanica involves (a) sterilizing a closed reactor under high-temperature, (b) preparing medium in the closed reactor and controlling the reactor temperature at 23-24 degrees C, (c) evenly installing several glow sticks in the reactor, (d) inoculating Nannochloropsis oceanica in the medium, evenly stirring the contents, installing top-down multi-layer film in the sealed reactor and coating each layer of thin film coated with N.oceanica, where the N.oceanica is layered through several films placed in a sealed reactor and (e) evenly introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor 8-10 times, preferably once in every 10-20 minutes and culturing for 22-24 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (a), sterilizing at 200-300 degrees C while cleaning with hypochlorite for 10-15 minutes. In step (b), the medium comprises 100 pts. wt. water, 30-40 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 10-20 pts. wt. 4-5 mol/l malic acid, 10-20 pts. wt. bran, 20-30 pts. wt. soybean meal, 10-20 pts. wt. aluminum nitride, 10-20 pts. wt. calcium carbide and 5-10 pts. wt. 2-3 mol/l hydrochloric acid. In step (d), evenly stirring the contents for 10-20 minutes. In step (e), introducing 0.3 l/minute carbon dioxide.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing sodium sesquicarbonate comprises:                preparing a soda solution and carbonizing it with                gaseous carbon dioxide with a concentration of                25-75% at a temperature of 60-95&#176; C;                separating the precipitate of sodium                sesquicarbonate; and drying. The soda solution is                prepared by dissolving soda ash in a recycled                mother liquor containing 2.5-5.5 wt.% sodium                hydrogen carbonate, 17.5-21.5 wt.% disodium                carbonate, and 73-80 wt.% water, undergoing                carbonization to the degree of 20-35% with gases                containing 25-75% carbon dioxide at 25-70&#176;                C, crystallizing at 25-70&#176; C, and washing                the filtered wet sodium sesquicarbonate with a 25%                solution of sodium sesquicarbonate at their ratio                (0.5-1): 1.

PN TW626939-U
TI Carbon dioxide exhaust monitoring device, has air exhaust device for discharging air in area to be monitored when drive circuit is activated, and suction device for sucking air outside area when circuit is not activated.
AU YEH P Y
   YEH Y T
   LI C H
   LU C L
AE LU C L (LUCL-Individual)
GA 2022914107
AB    NOVELTY - This device uses a carbon dioxide sensor to convert the concentration of carbon dioxide in the air in the area to be monitored into an analog signal, and then through an A/D converter, the analog signal is converted into a digital signal and sent to the single chip, through the single chip internal design program. When the converted digital signal exceeds the set upper limit, the single chip will output a high-state voltage signal to the drive circuit to start the exhaust and suction device. When the converted digital signal is lower than the set lower limit, the single chip will output a low-state voltage signal to the drive circuit to tum off the exhaust and suction device.

PT P
PN JP2022067075-A
TI Manufacture of carboxylic acid used as raw material for e.g. industrial products, involves reacting alkene-compound with formic acid and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen in presence of quaternary ammonium salt.
AU OKADA M
   OKU T
   CUI Z Z
AE NIPPON SHOKUBAI CO LTD (JAPC-C)
   NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202263100T
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a carboxylic acid (I) involves reacting alkene-compound (II) with formic acid and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of a quaternary ammonium salt (III).
   USE - Manufacture of carboxylic acid (claimed) used as raw material for industrial products and daily necessities.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of carboxylic acid, and suppressed usage of compound that is easily deactivated in the air or a highly toxic compound as a reaction accelerator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of a carboxylic acid of formula (I) involves reacting alkene-compound of formula (II) with formic acid and/or carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the presence of a quaternary ammonium salt of formula (III).
   R1,R2 = organic groups having H or 1-24C, in which R1 and R2 is optionally linked;
   R3-R6 = organic groups;and
   X = halo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of the compound (III) is 20 mol or less with respect to 1 mol of the metal atom contained in the metal complex.
   EXAMPLE - 0.46 g Cyclohexene, 0.93 g formic acid, 0.19 g p-toluenesulfonic acid monohydrate, 0.15 g tetramethylammonium iodide as a reaction accelerator was added to 5.93 g acetic acid and pre-dissolved. The obtained mixed solution and 0.19 g bis(triphenylphosphine)rhodium carbonyl chloride as a catalyst were added to the pressure resistant reactor and sealed. The sealed pressure-resistant reactor was heated to 180 degrees C in an aluminum block type electric furnace, and the reaction was carried out for 2.5 hours. After cooling, the contents in the pressure-resistant reactor were recovered, and a gas chromatography (GC) apparatus equipped with a flame ionization detector was used to perform qualitative and quantitative analysis of the reaction products contained in the recovered product. The internal standard method was adopted for the quantitative analysis by gas chromatography (GC). As a result of qualitative analysis by GC, by-products such as acetoxycyclohexane, cyclohexane, and iodocyclohexane were detected along with the main product cyclohexanecarboxylic acid, and had cyclohexene conversion rate of 90.2 mol.

PN CN114160208-A
TI Preparing palladium(II)-based metal-organic frameworks catalyst useful for catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises soaking aluminum-based metal-organic framework and palladium(II) bis(acetylacetonate) in polar solvent, hydrothermally reducing the soaked system, and drying.
AU SHI H
   WANG X
   SU H
   LI Y
   WEI Z
   CHEN H
AE CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CORP 719TH RES (CSHI-C)
GA 202242104P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing palladium(II)-based metal-organic frameworks (Pd/MOF) catalyst for catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises (1) soaking aluminum-based MOF (metal-organic frameworks) and palladium(II) bis(acetylacetonate) in polar solvent for 24 hours, (2) hydrothermally reducing the soaked system in sealed container at 130-160degrees Celsius for 4-6 hours, and (3) drying the sample in vacuum oven at 70-80degrees Celsius for 24 hours to obtain final product
   USE - The method is useful for catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: does not require reduction pretreatment before the reaction; has large specific surface area, small particle size of Pd particles, and large number of exposed active centers; and solves the technical problems that the existing MOF catalysts are easy to deform and collapse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between Al-based MOF and palladium(II) bis(acetylacetonate) is 1:0.01-0.04, preferably 1:0.03. In step (1), the polar solvent is chloroform. The detergents for the white solids are deionized water and dimethylformamide.Preferred Method: In step (ii), the soaked system is hydrothermally reduced in a sealed container at 140-150degrees C for 5 hours. The preparation of aluminum-based MOF, comprises (i) adding equimolar amounts of aluminum chloride and H2BDC (1,4-benzene dicarboxylic acid), (ii) transferring the mixture to stainless steel hydrothermal kettle lined with polytetrafluoroethylene, and placing in oven at 140-150degrees Celsius for 3 days to obtain white solid substance, and (iii) washing the white solid material and centrifuging.

AE URALVAGONZAVOD DZERZ SCI PROD CORP STOCK (URAL-Non-standard)
GA 2022263557
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the field of foundry                production. The method for curing the liquid-glass                mixture in the manufacture of molds and rods                includes filling the equipment with a liquid-glass                mixture and purging it with carbon dioxide to form                carbonic acid and sodium hydro-carbonate. Before                purging with carbon dioxide, a salt is applied to                the surface of the liquid-glass mixture, the cation                of which is a metal with a positive electrode                potential, and the anion is an acid residue greater                in strength than carbonic acid, with a molecular                ratio of liquid glass and salt 2:1. The application                of the mentioned salt reduces the content of                low-melting sodium hydro-carbonate formed during                purging with carbon dioxide, which lowers the                strength characteristics of the liquid-glass                mixture.
   USE - Foundry production.
   ADVANTAGE - Stable strength of the liquid-glass mixture is                provided.

TI Treating hydrocarbon-containing formation involves injecting aqueous solution that contains alkali nitrite, ammonium halide, and foaming agent into formation, and injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into formation.
AU AL-TAQ A A
   ALYOUSIF Z
   ALSWAIE O
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021E0395H
AB    NOVELTY - Treating hydrocarbon-containing formation involves injecting an aqueous solution that contains an alkali nitrite, an ammonium halide, and a foaming agent into the formation, and then injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into the formation. The portion of the carbon dioxide is allowed to dissolve in the aqueous solutions to cause the alkali nitrite and ammonium halide to react and generate nitrogen gas, and then allowed a foam to form by the interaction of the nitrogen gas with the foaming agent.
   USE - Method for treating hydrocarbon-containing formation for enhanced oil recovery in gas-swept zones.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat hydrocarbon-containing formation, which improves oil recovery by gas injection, the injection profile of a well may be modified, particularly where the well contains strata having varying permeabilities, and enhances oil recovery in gas-swept zones.

   YANG Y

   ZHANG X

GA 2021B8515P
AB    NOVELTY - Directionally culturing and enriching high-value microalgae biomass comprises: (i) ultrasonically crushing sludge to obtain sludge extract; (ii) inoculating microalgae in sludge extract, adding exogenous plant hormone in sludge mixed solution obtained by inoculating microalgae, and culturing for 5-8 days; and (iii) introducing carbon dioxide, and culturing to microalgae stable period.
   USE - The method is useful for directionally culturing and enriching high-value microalgae biomass, which is useful for biofertilizer (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improved yield of microalgae value-added biomass.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) biofertilizer, comprising microalgae;
   (2) preparing the biofertilizer, comprising: concentrating microalgae; or culturing microalgae under carbon dioxide hot air drying treatment; and
   (3) device for directionally culturing and enriching high-value microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: An initial inoculating density of the microalgae is 1-5x106 cells/mL based on volume of the sludge extract. The exogenous plant hormone is composed of indole-3-acetic acid and indole butyric acid in a mass ratio of 1:1. A concentration of the exogenous plant hormone is 0.1-10 mg/L based on volume of the sludge mixed solution.Preferred Method: The ultrasonic process is intermittent ultrasonic process i.e. intermittent ultrasonic for 4 seconds, and stopping 4 seconds; where the intermittent ultrasonic is carried out at less than 50degrees Celsius for 20 minutes under intermittent ultrasonic frequency of 15-25 kHz. A volume percentage content of the carbon dioxide is 10-60%. A ventilation amount of the carbon dioxide is 1 vvm. A ventilation way of the carbon dioxide is intermittent aeration. The intermittent aeration process is carried out by ventilation for 4 hours, and stopping 3 hours. A diameter of bubbles generated by the intermittent aeration is 1-10 mum. The culturing process is carried out at 20-30degrees Celsius under an illumination intensity of more than 2000 Lux and a light-dark ratio of 0.8-1.5:1.

PN CN113479913-A
TI Producing potassium bicarbonate from ammonia and co-producing ammonium bicarbonate to achieve carbon neutrality, using production process of ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate to change concentrated ammonia water that absorbs carbon dioxide in shift gas to potassium hydroxide aqueous solution.
AU TIAN Y
   ZHUO Y
   LIU C
   ZHUO Z
AE SHAANXI TONGZE HUMAN SETTLEMENT ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C08425
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing potassium bicarbonate from synthetic ammonia and co-producing ammonium bicarbonate to achieve carbon neutrality, involves using combined production process of synthetic ammonia and ammonium bicarbonate to change the concentrated ammonia water that absorbs carbon dioxide in the shift gas to a mass fraction of 10-20% potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, controlling reaction temperature to 20-30degreesC, controlling pressure to 0.7 MPa or 1.4 MPa, and generating potassium bicarbonate product after reaction.
   USE - Method for producing potassium bicarbonate from synthetic ammonia and co-producing ammonium bicarbonate to achieve carbon neutrality.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the product cost by 50%, is conducive to recovery and fixation of carbon dioxide, and effectively realizes the carbon neutralization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The potassium hydroxide aqueous solution with a mass fraction of 10-20% used in the method is prepared by a method of dissolving solid potassium hydroxide, preferably by commercial 48% potassium hydroxide solution dilution method. The method involves: carrying out a potassium chloride aqueous solution electrolysis workshop with synthetic ammonia process, and 10-20 %mass potassium hydroxide aqueous solution is obtained as intermediate product of electrolysis workshop; and using diaphragm method for electrolyzing potassium chloride aqueous solution to obtain 10-11 %mass potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, or using ion-exchange membrane method for electrolyzing potassium chloride aqueous solution to obtain 30-32 %mass potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, and adjusting to obtain potassium hydroxide aqueous solution with concentration of 10-20 %mass. The method involves reducing pressure of decarbonization in process of synthetic ammonia co-production of urea to 0.7 MPa or 1.4 MPa, using 10-20 %mass potassium hydroxide aqueous solution for absorption, and preparing potassium bicarbonate product after saturation.

PN US11059720-B1; WO2021154788-A1; US2021229991-A1
TI Dry reforming involves providing methane feed and carbon dioxide feed to react in dry reforming reaction over red mud catalyst support composition comprising caustic red mud waste material produced from alumina extraction process , Dry reforming involves providing methane feed and carbon dioxide feed to react in dry reforming reaction over red mud catalyst support composition comprising caustic red mud waste material produced from alumina extraction process.
AU FADHEL B A
   ALSOLAMI B H
   BAMAGAIN R
   ALSOLAMI B
   FADHEL B
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202179574P
AB    NOVELTY - Method of dry reforming with red mud catalyst support composition involves providing a methane feed and carbon dioxide feed to react in a dry reforming reaction over the red mud catalyst support composition at a temperature of 500-1000degrees Celsius and pressure of 5-20 bar to produce synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The red mud catalyst support composition comprises caustic red mud waste material produced from an alumina extraction process from bauxite ore. The methane conversion rate is at least 4% for at least 6 hours.
   USE - Dry reforming.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The red mud catalyst support composition further comprises at least one added catalytic metal. At least one added catalytic metal is periodic table group 3-12 metal.Preferred Composition: The caustic red mud waste material comprises more than 1 wt.% catalytic metal. The red mud catalyst support composition comprises 20-30 wt.% aluminum oxide, 5-10 wt.% calcium oxide, 15-25 wt.% iron oxide, 5-15 wt.% sodium oxide, 15-25 wt.% silicon dioxide, and 5-10 wt.% titanium dioxide.Preferred Condition: The dry reforming reaction is carried out at a temperature of 700-750degrees Celsius and pressure of 14 bar.Preferred Properties: The red mud catalyst support composition preferably has a methane conversion rate of at least 10% for at least 6 hours. The majority of particles of the red mud catalyst support composition have a particle size of less than 70 microm.Preferred Process: The method involves producing at least 5 mol% hydrogen from the dry reforming reaction.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The methane feed and carbon dioxide feed has a gas hourly space velocity of 1000-10000 hour-1. The molar ratio of the methane feed to carbon dioxide feed is 1:1-1:1.75.

PN DE102019007112-A1
TI Producing carbohydrates by providing dried water kefir grains, providing liquid saturated with microalgae, merging water kefir grains together with liquid saturated with microalgae, and drying microalgae-containing kefir grains.
AU GRUBER B
AE GRUBER B (GRUB-Individual)
GA 2021384929
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing carbohydrates involves (a) providing dried water kefir grains, (b) providing a liquid saturated with microalgae, (c) merging the water kefir grains together with the liquid saturated with microalgae in such a way that the water kefir grains soak up the liquid saturated with microalgae, (d) removing the microalgae enriched water kefir grains, and (e) drying the microalgae-containing water kefir grains.
   USE - The process is useful for producing carbohydrates.
   ADVANTAGE - The process removes water kefir grains enriched with microalgae from cultivation liquid in a simple manner, which saves costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for arrangement for performing the method, which has container designed to hold liquid saturated with microalgae, a light source which is arranged above the container, a carbon dioxide source, and a pump with a line for supplying carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide source to the liquid saturated with microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method further involves supplying carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide source or air and light from a light source or sunlight to accelerate the growth of the microalgae. The method further involves (f) crushing the dried microalgae containing water kefir grains. The method further involves adding carbon dioxide, which is produced during the cultivation of the water kefir. The method further involves providing a gas vent which generates the smallest possible gas bubbles. The step (a) involves filling the dried water kefir grains into a mesh bag, and inserting the mesh bag with water kefir grains into the liquid saturated with microalgae.

PT P
PN CN111450820-A
TI Preparing chromium oxide supported titanium dioxide photocatalyst used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, by preparing titanium dioxide nanosheets, mixing with potassium chromate, and subjecting to visible light deposition.
AU DONG J
   ZHU X
   BAO J
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202075204F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing chromium oxide supported titanium dioxide photocatalyst involves (a) preparing titanium dioxide nanosheets by hydrothermal method, (b) dispersing titanium dioxide nanosheets in water to obtain a titanium dioxide suspension, mixing the potassium chromate aqueous solution with the titanium dioxide suspension, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain a chromium precursor solution, and (c) subjecting the chromium precursor solution to visible light deposition, washing, freezing and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing chromium oxide supported titanium dioxide photocatalyst, which is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has rate of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane of 0.42 mu mol/hour and selectivity of methane of as high as 98%, and is prepared by simple method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of preparing titanium dioxide nanosheets involves heating and reacting tetrabutyl titanate and hydrofluoric acid at 160-200 degrees C for 36-50 hours to obtain a reaction product, washing the reaction product with absolute ethanol and deionized water, drying at 60-80 degrees C to obtain the nanosheets. The step (b) involves dispersing the titanium dioxide nanosheets in water, stirring for 20-30 minutes, ultrasonically treating for 3-5 minutes, dripping the potassium chromate aqueous solution into the titanium dioxide suspension, stirring for 20-30 minutes, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-60 seconds to obtain a chromium precursor solution. The step (c) involves subjecting to visible light at a vacuum degree of -0.1 MPa under a 300 W xenon lamp with wavelength of 400-460 nm by using a cut-off filter as radiation source, top-illuminating for 3-4 hours at a distance of light source of 12-15 cm away from catalyst, light during the deposition process, stirring the chromium precursor solution and maintaining the temperature at 12-16 degrees C by circulating water. The step of washing involves washing with deionized water after centrifugation at less than or equal to 1000 rpm for 30-60 seconds. The step (c) involves freezing using a liquid nitrogen and drying by vacuum drying at -35 to -50 degrees C for 20-24 hours. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the potassium chromate aqueous solution is 0.03-0.05 mol/l. The volume ratio of the potassium chromate aqueous solution and the titanium dioxide suspension is 0.25-1%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E32 (Compounds of Ti, Zr, Hf, Cu, Ag, Au, Zn, Cd, Hg, Ga, In, Te, Ge, Sn, Pb, As, Sb, Bi.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN111234257-A; CN111234257-B
TI New Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid used in catalyzing cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds.
AU DAI W
   MAO P
   LI B
   ZHANG S
   YANG L
   LUO S
   LUO X
   ZOU J
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 2020518782
AB    NOVELTY - A Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid having repeating structural unit (I), is new.
   USE - Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid used in catalyzing cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid has excellent catalytic activity and dispersion uniformity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid having repeating structural unit of formula (I), is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of Schiff base zinc complex-functionalized polyionic liquid, which involves mixing Schiff base of formula (II), 1,4-bis(bromomethyl)benzene and amide solvent under protective atmosphere, polymerizing to obtain a Schiff base-functionalized polyionic liquid, mixing resulting product, zinc bromide, and an alcohol solvent, and performing addition reaction; and
   (2) use of ionic liquid in catalyzing the cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the Schiff base to 1,4-bis(bromomethyl)benzene is 1:(1-1.5). The mass ratio of the Schiff base functionalized polyionic liquid and zinc bromide is 1:(0.45-0.5). The Schiff base is prepared by mixing isoniazid, 4-pyridinecarbaldehyde and a solvent. The ratio of the amount of the epoxy compound substance to the mass of the polyionic liquid functionalized by the Schiff base zinc complex is 1 mmol:(2.1-4.2) mg.
