PN CN111211306-A
TI MXene-carbon-porous silicon material useful: as cathode material of lithium ion battery; and as lithium metal battery current collector in lithium metal battery, comprises silicon-magnesium alloy, carbon dioxide and MXene.
AU FENG J
   AN Y
   TIAN Y
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202050235T
AB    NOVELTY - MXene-carbon-porous silicon material comprises silicon-magnesium alloy (Mg2Si), carbon dioxide and MXene.
   USE - The carbon-porous silicon material is useful: as cathode material of lithium ion battery; and as lithium metal battery current collector in lithium metal battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has excellent electrical properties, uniform carbon coating and controllable thickness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - MXene-carbon-porous silicon material comprises silicon-magnesium alloy (Mg2Si), carbon dioxide and MXene, where the structure of MXene-carbon-porous silicon material is carbon coated on the outer surface of porous silicon, and the composite of carbon and porous silicon is embedded in the interlayer spacing of Mxene. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing MXene-carbon-porous silicon material comprising mixing carbon-coated porous silicon with MXene to obtain MXene-carbon-porous silicon material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon-porous silicon material is prepared by reacting silicon-magnesium alloy as precursor with carbon dioxide gas to obtain product, and pickling the product. The temperature at which the precursor reacts with carbon dioxide is 400-1500 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.1-24 hours. The temperature at which the precursor reacts with carbon dioxide is 600-1500 degrees C. Preferred Components: The Mxene is titanium carbide powder(Ti3C2), titanium carbide (Ti2C), tantalum carbide (Ta4C3), titanium niobium carbide (TiNbC), vanadium carbide (V0.5Cr0.5) 3C2, vanadium carbide, niobium carbide powder (Nb2C), niobium carbide (Nb4C3) and/or titanium carbide (Ti3CN). The pickling reagents used for pickling are hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid, acetic acid, oxalic acid and/or citric acid. The molar ratio between silicon-magnesium alloy and carbon dioxide is higher than 1:1.1, and the molar ratio between product silicon and MXene is 0.5:1-10:1.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN CN110776589-A; CN110776589-B
TI New quaternary ammonium-based polyionic compound            used as liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide            thickener for shale gas production and oil and gas            production.
AU WANG Y
   ZANG X
   WU J
   LIU Z
   ZHENG L
   HAN X
   WU L
   ZHOU Y
   WANG N
AE UNIV NORTHWEST (UYXB-C)
   SHAANXI YANCHANG PETROLEUM GROUP CO LTD (SXYP-C)
GA 2020160694
AB    NOVELTY - A quaternary ammonium-based polyionic compound                (I), is new.
   USE - New quaternary ammonium-based polyionic                compound used as liquid or supercritical carbon                dioxide thickener for shale gas production and oil                and gas production (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) has good sand-carrying and                seam-forming properties with no water-sensitive and                water blocking damage, and saves the water                resources. The anions and cations of compound (I)                are ester-soluble groups which can be dissolved at                lower temperature and pressure in the presence of                co-solvent to achieve good mutual solubility in                carbon dioxide and to effectively increase the                viscosity of compound (I).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A quaternary ammonium-based polyionic compound                of formula (I), is new.
   R1-R3 = 1-30C alkyl;and
   R4-R8 = SO3- or H.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The sulfonating agent                  is chlorosulfonic acid or sulfur trioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of                  compound (I) involves (A) reacting vinyl benzoate                  as raw material, 1,4-dioxane as solvent and                  2,2-azobisisobutyronitrile as initiator under                  nitrogen atmosphere, heating, magnetically                  stirring to obtain a crude polyvinyl benzoate                  solution, (B) dripping the crude polyvinyl                  benzoate solution into poor solvent,                  precipitating polyvinyl benzoate to obtain a                  white solid precipitate, (C) drying the white                  solid to obtain polyvinyl benzoate, (D)                  dissolving polyvinyl benzoate and sulfonating                  agent in dichloromethane under cold water bath                  environment, adding sulfonating agent to the                  polyvinyl benzoate solution, condensing,                  refluxing to obtain a sulfonated product, (E)                  washing the sulfonated product, purifying, (F)                  condensing the washed and purified sulfonated                  product and quaternary ammonium salt, heating in                  acetone solvent and drying. Preferred Conditions:                  The reaction in step (A) is carried out at 60-100                  degrees C for 1-5 hours. The reaction in step (D)                  is carried out at 15-35 degrees C for 1-4 hours.                  The heating in step (F) is carried out at 45-55                  degrees C for 4-6 hours. Preferred Composition:                  The initiator is added in an amount of 2-5 %mass                  based on mass of vinyl benzoate. The vinyl                  benzoate has concentration of 35-80 wt.%. The                  molar ratio of polyvinyl benzoate to sulfonating                  agent in step (D) is 1:1-2. The molar ratio of                  sulfonated product to quaternary ammonium salt in                  step (F) is 1:1. Preferred Component: The poor                  solvent comprises methanol, ethanol or                  water.

General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

TI Rapid separation and preparation of artemisinin by dehydrating southernwood, soaking in hydrogen peroxide, washing, drying, crushing, granulating, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, analyzing and separating, and crystallizing.
AU MA F
   SHI X
   MA S
   LIANG X
AE TIANMA ANHUI TRADITIONAL CHINESE MEDICIN (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020084126
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid separation and preparation of artemisinin comprises selecting southernwood, dehydrating in dehydrating device, soaking in hydrogen peroxide, taking out, repeatedly washing with clean water for 2-4 times, dehydrating, drying, crushing for 3-5 times, granulating to granularity of 10-15 mesh, storing at 15-20 degrees C, adding into extracting kettle, producing supercritical carbon dioxide using heating device and pressing device, pumping into extracting kettle, filling entrainer, extracting at 25-40 MPa at 45-75 degrees C for 1-2 hours, adjusting pressure of extracting kettle, discharging carbon dioxide by separating device, analyzing and separating artemisinin, concentrating to obtain crude artemisinin, adding ethanol solvent, and purifying and crystallizing.
   USE - The method is for rapid separation and preparation of artemisinin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly reduces extraction time and improves preparation efficiency.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Separating is done at 5-15 degrees C at 1-10 MPa. Preferred Components: The entrainer having purity of more than 96% is ethanol, diethyl ether, methanol, sunflower seed oil and/or peanut oil, preferably diethyl ether.

PN CN110075864-A
TI Palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocatalyst useful for producing methane by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprises a palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocrystal, and an inert carrier comprising e.g. activated carbon.
AU LUO L
   PENG Y
   WANG M
   LI H
   ZENG J
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 201971374U
AB    NOVELTY - Palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocatalyst comprises a palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocrystal as an active ingredient with an average size of 6-10 nm.
   USE - The nanocatalyst useful for producing methane by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanocatalyst improves the catalytic activity and stability of the catalyst during the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methane, provides selectivity to methane to 100% and conversion frequency to 4.4 times and 11 times than the commercial ruthenium/carbon and palladium/carbon catalysts, and applies onto the oxygen circulating process of space exploration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the nanocatalyst, comprising (i) providing palladium nanoparticle with an average size of 5-7 nm; (ii) utilizing triiron dodecacarbonyl for forming iron oxide onto the surface of palladium nanoparticle to obtain precursor nanocrystal; and (iii) calcining the precursor nanocrystal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The nanocatalyst further comprises an inert carrier, where the palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocrystal is supported onto the inert carrier. Preferred Components: The inert carrier is activated carbon, silicon dioxide, titanium dioxide and/or silicon carbide. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises taking palladium acetylacetonate as a palladium source, and reacting at less than 100 degrees C in the presence of morpholine borane and oleylamine to obtain palladium nanoparticle with an average size of 5-7 nm. The step (ii) comprises mixing palladium nanoparticle with triiron dodecacarbonyl in the presence of oleylamine, heating to 280-310 degrees C under a protective atmosphere, reacting, and forming iron oxide onto the surface of palladium nanoparticle to obtain precursor nanocrystal. The step (iii) comprises introducing the precursor nanocrystal and inert carrier material into an organic solvent before performing calcination treatment to obtain a precursor substance loaded with precursor nanocrystal, and calcining the precursor substance loaded with precursor nanocrystal at 500-600 degrees C in a mixed atmosphere of hydrogen and nitrogen to obtain palladium-iron intermetallic complex nanocatalyst.

   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares size controllable simple and uniform shape single layer graphene nano-plate by one-step hydrothermal method, can generate formic acid yield of 87% without byproduct, uses easily available raw material, has simple process flow, can reduce production cost, and is good for industrial application in a large scale, and uses electrolyte of normal common salt solution, without adding any organic matter. The graphene nano-plate has carbon dioxide catalytic reduction performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for graphene nano-plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The graphene oxide powder is obtained by providing 1 g natural graphite, adding 0.74 g sodium nitrate and 34 ml 98% sulfuric acid in ice water bath, stirring, adding 5 g potassium permanganate, keeping the temperature below 20 degrees C, then transferring to 35 degrees C water bath, stirring for 3 hours, adding 250 ml de-ionized water, adding 4 ml 30 wt.% hydrogen peroxide and ending the reaction, firstly centrifugally washing the obtained bright yellow suspension with 5% hydrochloric acid solution, so as to remove the remaining sulfuric acid, then washing many times with de-ionized water until the pH is neutral, washing graphene solution, freeze drying or vacuum drying to obtain the solid powder. The specific step comprises providing the dried graphene oxide powder, adding concentrated nitric acid with mass fraction of 65%, adding graphene oxide powder concentrated nitric acid with volume of 1/10 to 1/20, ultrasonically stirring until even disperse solution, then placing in a hydrothermal reaction kettle, reacting for 4-10 hours at 100-200 degrees C, cooling the reaction kettle to room temperature, using rotary evaporation, centrifuging and dialyzing, adding distilled water to wash repeatedly, collecting and drying to obtain controllable size, uniform shape graphene nano-plate. Preferred Components: The mass percentage of the concentrated nitric acid is 65%. The dosage of graphene oxide powder is 400 mg; the dosage of the concentrated nitric acid is 20-40 ml. The reaction temperature is 140-180 degrees C, preferably 160 degrees C and the time is 6 hours. The size of the graphene nano-plate is 30-180 nm, the thickness is 1 nm; the oxidation degree g value reaches 2.01.

TI New carbon monoxide dehydrogenase having increased oxygen tolerance or enzymatic activity, useful for producing carbon dioxide, and in device or filter for removing carbon monoxide.
AU KIM Y H
   KIM S M
   LEE J H
   KIMYONGHWAN
AE UNIST ULSAN NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (ULSA-C)
GA 2022163794
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon monoxide dehydrogenase (CO dehydrogenase) having increased oxygen tolerance or enzymatic activity, is new.
   USE - The CO dehydrogenase is useful for producing carbon dioxide, and in a device or a filter for removing carbon monoxide (all claimed) from cigarette combustion, cigarette filters, air purifiers, suction filters or gas boilers.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO dehydrogenase has increased oxygen tolerance or enzymatic activity even in presence of oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a polynucleotide encoding the CO dehydrogenase;
   (2) a vector expressing the CO dehydrogenase;
   (3) a microorganism expressing the CO dehydrogenase;
   (4) a method for producing CO dehydrogenase;
   (5) a method for removing carbon monoxide involves contacting carbon monoxide with the CO dehydrogenase; and
   (6) a method for producing carbon dioxide involves contacting carbon monoxide with the CO dehydrogenase.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation (Claimed): The CO dehydrogenase is produced by culturing a microorganism expressing a polynucleotide encoding the CO dehydrogenase. Preferred Biomolecules: The CO dehydrogenase has modified amino acid at position 82, 559, 565, 578, 580, 586, 593, 597 and 610 in a CO dehydrogenase, where the modification is at least one chosen from deletion, addition and substitution. In the CO dehydrogenase, amino acid at position 82 is leucine, at position 559 is alanine, at position 565 is valine, at position 578 is threonine, at position 580 is isoleucine, at position 586 is isoleucine, at position 593 is threonine, at position 597 is threonine and at position 610 is valine. The modification is a substitution, where substituted amino acid is tryptophan, tyrosine, serine, histidine, aspartic acid, glutamic acid, asparagine, alanine, threonine, glutamine, leucine and valine. The carbon monoxide dehydrogenase is a protein encoded by a polynucleotide containing nucleotide sequences of SEQ ID NOs: 24-43. Preferred Property: The oxygen tolerance is 1-200 times increased as compared to wild type. The enzyme activity is 1-200 times increased compared to the wild type. Sequences not defined here may be found at ftp://ftp.wipo.int/pub/published_pct_sequences/publication.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of creation of a phylogenetic diagram of carbon monoxide dehydrogenase, that do not support the claims.

TI Preparing modified acid-activated montmorillonite composite film based on polyaniline intercalation in separation and purification of carbon dioxide, involves using montmorillonite, inorganic acid, aniline, ammonium persulfate.
AU LI J
   WANG J
   ZHANG X
   WANG Y
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202202477W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing modified acid-activated montmorillonite composite film based on polyaniline intercalation involves using montmorillonite, inorganic acid, aniline, ammonium persulfate as raw material, through acid activation, cation exchange, subjecting to in-situ polymerization process to prepare polyaniline intercalation modified acid-activated montmorillonite nanosheet, adding polyaniline intercalation modified acid-activated montmorillonite nanosheet composite material into the polyvinyl amine solution to prepare casting solution, coating on the surface of the microporous supporting filter membrane to obtain mixed matrix composite film, the thickness of the wet coating of the film is 10-200 microns.
   USE - Method for preparing modified acid-activated montmorillonite composite film based on polyaniline intercalation used in separation and purification of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to operate, green and environment-friendly, and provides mixed matrix composite film with high carbon dioxide flux and selectivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The method involves adding montmorillonite to 1-10 mole asterisk L-1 hydrochloric acid solution, refluxing for 1-24 hours at the temperature of 50-100?oC, the mass ratio of dry mass of montmorillonite and inorganic acid solution is 1:(1-30), cooling the obtained acid-activated montmorillonite at room temperature, by centrifuging for 10-120 minutes in the rotating speed range of 8000-12000 rpm, separating out from the solution, washing the lower layer precipitate in the centrifugal tube for 2-5 times by de-ionized water, putting in 60-100 drying in the vacuum oven for 12-48 hours to obtain the white grey powder, namely acid-activated montmorillonite, named as product, preparing the polyaniline intercalation modified acid activated montmorillonite, dispersing the product A in the de-ionized water, where the mass ratio of the product A and the de-ionized water is (1-10):(100-1000), using the ultrasonic dispersion machine at the ultrasonic frequency of 30-60 kiloHertz, ultrasonic temperature is in the range of 15-60degrees Celsius for 60-480 minutes, to form a stable dispersion, obtaining solution B, adding aniline solution into the acid solution with hydrogen ion concentration of 1-10 mole asterisk L-1, ensuring the mass ratio of aniline and acid solution is (0.1-10):(50-200), slowly adding into the solution B in the time of 2-30 minutes, the temperature is 0-5degrees Celsius, mechanically stirring for 30-240 minutes at the rotating speed of 100-500 r/minutes to obtain solution C, then dissolving ammonium persulfate in the acid solution with hydrogen ion concentration of 1-10 mole asterisk L-1, where the mass ratio of ammonium persulfate and acid solution is (1-25):(50-200), slowly adding to the solution C in the time of 10-30 minutes to obtain solution D, the solution D at the temperature of 0-5degrees Celsius, rotating speed in the range of 50-300rpm continuously mechanically stirring for 4-48 hours for in-situ polymerization of aniline in acid-activated montmorillonite layer, obtaining the deep green precipitate, namely the crude product of aniline intercalation modified acid activated montmorillonite, named as product E. The product E is pumped by vacuum pump with power of 300-600 W, orderly using 1-3 mole asterisk L-1 acid solution, using de-ionized water to wash for 2-5 times, removing ammonium persulfate and other impurities. The obtained solid nano-composite material is dried for 12-24 hours in vacuum oven at 60-100degrees Celsius to obtain the dark green powder, namely the polyaniline intercalation modified acid-activated montmorillonite composite material, named as product F, preparing polyaniline intercalation modified acid-activated montmorillonite composite film, adding the product F into polyvinyl amine solution with concentration of 1-10 wt.%, preparing into uniform casting solution, where the product F accounts for 0.1-15 wt.% mass percentage content of the polyethylene amine, named as solution G, the product G is coated on the surface of the microporous filtering film by solution casting method, controlled the wet coating thickness is 10-200 microns, the mixed matrix composite film in the relative humidity is 40-100%, the temperature is 25-80degrees Celsius of constant temperature and constant humidity drying box for drying for 8-24 hours. The montmorillonite is from commercially bought sodium-base montmorillonite. The mixed matrix composite film is used for carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation, the film is fixed in a circular film tank, the effective film area is 1-12.56 cm2, the upstream side feeding pressure range of the film is 0.1-1.5 megaPascal, the pressure of the downstream side is 0.1 megaPascal, the gas permeation test temperature is 10-80degrees Celsius, controlling the flow rate of the feeding gas is 20-100 sccm, the downstream side of the film cavity adopts hydrogen as the purge gas purge, the purge gas flow rate is controlled at 20-50 sccm, using gas chromatography to detect the downstream side component. The gas performance test is carried out in the humidifying state, the feeding side gas comprises a feeding gas and a blowing gas, the humidity is adjusted by the humidifier to ensure the relative humidity of the feeding gas reaches 40-100%. Preferred Components: The inorganic acids used in the activation of the montmorillonite includes hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, concentrated sulfuric acid, nitric acid, or mixed acid of hydrochloric acid and nitric acid, where the mol ratio of hydrochloric acid and nitric acid in the mixed acid is (1-3):(0.3-1). The microporous filtering film is a supporting film, the interception molecular weight is 5000-50000 and the material is polysulfone, poly (ether sulfones) fluoride, polytetrafluoroethylene, cellulose acetate.

TI Method of liquefying natural gas, involves feeding nitrogen through expander to cooling circuit and heat exchanger with specific temperature, after which throttling and returning to cooling circuit, while exhaust carbon dioxide is compress.
AU LUNKOVA L G
   RAZUMOV I K
   GULKOV A N
   LAPSHIN V D
AE UNIV FAR EASTERN FEDERAL (UFAR-C)
GA 202166284Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves drying and purification of impurities, further direction to a heat exchanger. The cooling circuit of which is a closed nitrogen cycle, followed by obtaining liquefied natural gas and throttle. The nitrogen is compressed in a chain containing sequentially installed pairs, each of which includes a compressor and an air cooler, after which the nitrogen flow is cooled in the second heat exchanger, the cooling circuit of which is a closed cycle of carbon dioxide. The nitrogen is fed through the expander to the cooling circuit a heat exchanger with a specific temperature, while the exhaust carbon dioxide is compressed and cooled, after which throttled and returned to the cooling circuit of the second heat exchanger.
   USE - Method of liquefying natural gas.

TI Preparation of raw materials for phosgene            synthesis involves dividing the electrolytic cell into            anode compartment and cathode compartment by            electrochemical diaphragm, and generating chlorine and            carbon dioxide on anode and cathode.

AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical preparation of raw materials                for phosgene synthesis involves dividing the                electrolytic cell into an anode compartment and a                cathode compartment by an electrochemical                diaphragm, where the anode compartment electrolyte                is an aqueous solution containing chloride ions.                The cathode compartment electrolyte is an organic                composite electrolyte in which carbon dioxide is                dissolved. The chlorine is generated on the anode                and carbon monoxide is generated on the cathode                during the electrolysis reaction. The obtained                chlorine and carbon monoxide are used to synthesize                phosgene. The aqueous solution containing chloride                ions is aqueous hydrochloric acid solution, aqueous                sodium chloride solution or potassium chloride                solution.
   USE - Method for electrochemical preparation of raw                materials for phosgene synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to electrically reduce                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide in a diaphragm                electrolytic cell, and simultaneously produce                chlorine gas.

The cathode is gold, silver,                  copper, zinc electrodes, or alloy of the                  foregoing metals. The organic composite                  electrolyte contains organic solvent, organic                  supporting electrolyte, organic additive and                  water. The organic solvent is butyl acetate or                  propylene carbonate. The organic supporting                  electrolyte is quaternary ammonium salt, or                  choline chloride. The organic additive is                  metalloporphyrin compounds, metal phthalocyanine                  compounds, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  tricarbonyl-2,4'-dipyridine metal halide,                  imidazole ionic liquid, diethyl carbonate, or                  acetonitrile.

TI Preparing ruthenium-based carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst used in catalysis, by mixing ionic liquid and reducing agent solution, dripping ruthenium ion salt solution, depositing nano-ruthenium particles and vacuum drying.
AU LI C
   GUO X
   CHEN H
   ZHANG S
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 2020787606
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing ruthenium-based carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation catalyst, involves selecting different kinds of ionic liquid, uniformly mixing ionic liquid and reducing agent solution, dripping salt solution containing ruthenium ion to mixed solution, heating and stirring for reflux period of time under protection of inert gas to obtain stable nano-ruthenium particles with different particle size, cooling to certain temperature after the reaction is completed, ultrasonically stirring and depositing different ionic liquid stable nano-ruthenium particles to different carriers, and vacuum drying for certain period to obtain the catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalysis application.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent metal dispersion, reaction activity and stability under low temperature conditions, reduces the temperature of methanation reaction, and improves the catalytic performance of low-temperature carbon dioxide methanation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ruthenium ion salt solution comprises ruthenium chloride, ruthenium nitrate and/or ruthenium iodide solution. The inert gas comprises nitrogen, helium and/or argon. The deposited carrier comprises aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, ceric dioxide, silicon dioxide, zirconium dioxide and other oxide. Preferred Composition: The concentration of ruthenium ion salt solution is 0.01-0.05 mol/l. The ratio of amount of ionic liquid to and ruthenium element is 1-20. Preferred Conditions: The method involves performing chemical reduction reaction at 40-100 degrees C for 0.5-6 hours under stirring reflux period, cooling to 10-40 degrees C, and vacuum drying for 2-12 hours. Preferred Property: The particle size of nano-ruthenium is 1-20 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid comprises imidazole and quaternary ammonium salt, such as 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hydroxide, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium hexafluorophosphate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium trifluoromethanesulfonate and/or nitrosonium tetrafluoroborate. The chemical reducing agent comprises sodium borohydride and/or hydrazine hydrate, and the concentration of chemical agent is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. The washing solution comprises ethanol, water and/or methanol.

   USE - New alkyl-substituted coumarin-containing compound used as carbon dioxide/photodimerization dual response polymer for preparing multi-regulated molecular size screening system.
   ADVANTAGE - The alkyl-substituted coumarin-containing compound contains aminoalkyl substituted coumarin derivative which has both carbon dioxide stimulus responsiveness and photodimerization activity. The coumarin compounds in compound (I) can undergo photocyclization addition reaction when irradiated with lambda more than 300 nm light, and undergo a photolysis reaction when irradiated with lambda less than 260 nm light.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An alkyl-substituted coumarin-containing compound of formula (I), is new.
   R1, R2 = H, methyl or group of formula (a);
   n = 0 or 1;
   R3 = 2-10 C straight chain;and
   R4 = 2C, 4C, 6C,8C or 10C straight chain.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of the compound (I) involves (1) dissolving hydroxycoumarin and chlorohydrin compound in dimethylformamide, transferring to three-necked bottle, adding potassium carbonate, passing nitrogen gas for 30 minutes, reacting at 60-100 degrees C for 5-10 hours, cooling to room temperature, slowly adding the reaction liquid in drops to ice water for precipitation, leaving still for 24 hours, filtering out the precipitate, recrystallizing from ethyl acetate, vacuum drying, (2) dissolving the product of the step (1) and potassium hydroxide in acetone, transferring to three-necked flask, warming to 40-56 degrees C, passing nitrogen gas for 30 minutes, adding epichlorohydrin into three-necked flask, reacting for 10-30 hours, cooling, suction filtering, performing rotary evaporation to remove the solvent, dissolving the product in chloroform, washing three times with ultrapure water, performing rotary evaporation to remove the solvent, recrystallizing of ethanol to obtain coumarin derivatives with epoxy groups, (3) adding diamine compound solution dissolved in ethanol to a three-necked flask, condensing and refluxing, heating to 40-78 degrees C, adding the ethanol solution of coumarin derivatives with epoxy groups into three-necked flask, reacting for 2-10 hours, performing rotary evaporation to remove the solvent, dissolving in ultrapure water, extracting with ethyl acetate, washing three times with saturated brine, and performing rotary evaporation.

PN CN110564578-A
TI Alcohol-free dark beer comprises beer has less alcohol content, chromaticity, bitterness, carbon dioxide, and less ester.
AU DONG J
   YIN H
   XING L
   CHANG Z
   QIAN Z
   HU X
   LIU X
   YANG M
   WANG J
   ZHANG W
AE TSINGTAO BREWERY CO LTD (TSIN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6998Y
AB    NOVELTY - Alcohol-free (0% volume) dark beer comprises beer has an alcohol content of less than 0.05% vol, chromaticity of 100-150 EBC, bitterness of 15-40 EBC, original wort concentration of 5.5-8.5 degrees P, carbon dioxide content of 0.50-0.60%, total alcohol content of greater than or equal to 15 mg/l, and total ester content of greater than or equal to 5 mg/l.
   USE - Used as 0.0% volume alcohol-free dark beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can solve the problems of current non-alcoholic beer lacks the alcohol ester flavor of traditional beer, and beer has light taste and prominent acidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing beer comprising preheating the dark beer degassing, dealcoholizing, cooling, filling with carbon dioxide, carrying out post-modifying process, stabilizing, and filling in order to prepare 0.0% volume alcohol-free dark beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The degassing is specifically carried out by degassing the dark beer preheated to 40-43 degrees C with a vacuum degree of 80-90 mbar until the carbon dioxide in the dark beer is completely discharged. The dealcoholizing comprises vacuum distilling the dark beer after removing carbon dioxide at 40-43 degrees C with a vacuum degree of 80-90 mbar until the ethanol content decreases to less than 0.05%. The post-modifying includes adding natural beer flavor and natural sweet component to the de-alcoholized dark beer, where the added amount of natural beer flavor is 0.05-0.2%, preferably 0.1 wt.%. The added amount of natural sweet component is 0.5-3 g/100 ml, preferably 1 g/100 ml based on the amount of sugar. The natural sweet component comprises fructose syrup, maltose syrup, sucrose syrup, glucose syrup, and sugar. The manner of adding the natural beer flavor and natural sweetness component is fed line. The method comprises taking barley malt, malted malt, wheat malt, burnt malt, black malt coated with rice, corn or syrup as auxiliary material, crushing, gelatinizing, saccharifying, filtering, boiling, gyrating, cooling and oxygenating, adding yeast, fermenting, and filtering to obtain dark beer. The method specifically comprises crushing and mixing barley malt, malted malt, wheat malt, burnt malt, black malt coated with rice, corn or syrup as auxiliary material with the material-water ratio of 1:3-1:4, saccharifying, where rice or corn is used as an auxiliary material, pulverizing the auxiliary material, and then pulping at 80-90 degrees C to be gelatinized, mashing with malt after the protein is stopped, filtering the mashed mash at 76 plus minus 1 degrees C to obtain clear wort, boiling, adding hops or hop products during the boiling process, performing swirling precipitation after boiling, cooling to 8-10 degrees C, oxygenating and adding yeast for fermentation to obtain beer fermentation broth, and filtering the beer fermentation broth with diatomite filtration method to obtain dark beer.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Preparing highly dispersed nanoprecipitated silica            involves placing sodium silicate solution in a constant            temperature water bath, adding D-Sodium gluconate in            water bath, stirring mixture, introducing carbon            dioxide to system.

GA 201886696T

TI Producing value product in genetically modified microorganism with reduced alcohol dehydrogenase activity involves converting carbon monoxide into reduction equivalent and carbon dioxide and fixing the latter in Calvin-Benson-Bassham cycle.
AU HUO Y
   ROHLIN L E U
   RABIZADEH S
AE EASEL BIOTECHNOLOGIES LLC (EASE-Non-standard)
GA 2018829523
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a value product in a genetically modified microorganism comprises converting carbon monoxide into a reduction equivalent and carbon dioxide in the microorganism, and fixing the carbon dioxide in a Calvin-Benson-Bassham cycle (CBB) in the microorganism to produce the value product, where the microorganism is genetically modified to have reduced or abrogated activity for at least one of aldehyde dehydrogenase and alcohol dehydrogenase.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a value product in a genetically modified microorganism, where the value product is an aldehyde, an alcohol, a polyhydroxyalkanoate (claimed) and/or a carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes genetically modified microorganism that has reduced or abrogated activity for at least one of aldehyde dehydrogenase and alcohol dehydrogenase, and provides increased yield of the value product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microorganism is an aerobic microorganism and belongs to the genus Ralstonia. Preferred Method: The aerobic microorganism is cultivated under aerobic conditions or is genetically modified to grow under anaerobic conditions. The method further comprises recovering the value product from the microorganism. The value product is recovered in the gas phase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microorganism comprises a second genetic modification to convert carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide and to generate the reduction equivalents during the conversion of the carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide. The second genetic modification comprises a recombinant gene that encodes an enzyme having carbon monoxide reductase activity. The second genetic modification further comprises a recombinant gene that encodes an enzyme having hydrogenase activity. The microorganism comprises a third genetic modification to have at least one recombinant gene in the CBB cycle. The microorganism comprises a recombinant gene in the CBB cycle encoding a protein having phosphoribulokinase activity or phosphoketolase activity.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon monoxide is a component of syngas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN108219921-A
TI Producing edible grade silkworm pupa oil by using silkworm cocoons comprises hydraulically cold pressing, spiral cold pressing and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction by supercritical extracting crude oil and adding cosolvent.
AU DONG S
AE HAIYAN LINGTE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAIY-Non-standard)
GA 2018533468
AB    NOVELTY - Producing edible grade silkworm pupa oil by using silkworm cocoons as raw materials comprise hydraulically cold pressing, spiral cold pressing and performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction; the extraction of unsaturated fatty acid from raw oil comprises adding the crude oil in supercritical carbon dioxide device, performing supercritical extraction, placing the cosolvent containing 5-10% ethyl acetate and 0.01-0.02% ofloxacin into ethanol solution, collecting raffinate to obtain edible grade silkworm pupa oil.
   USE - The silkworm pupa oil is useful for preventing and treating hyperlipemia and promoting liver regeneration, preventing and treating non-alcoholic fatty liver.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has high automation, can extract high-purity unsaturated fatty acid quickly from the silkworm pupa. The extracted unsaturated fatty acid has natural fragrance, no organic solvent residue and high alpha -linolenic acid content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The supercritical extraction conditions are as follows: the extraction temperature is 35-40 degrees C, the extracting pressure is 30-40 MPa, the extracting time is 0.6-1.0 hour, separating kettle I pressure is 10-12 MPa, the temperature is 40-45 degrees C, separating kettle II pressure is 5-7 MPa, the temperature is 15-20 degrees C. The ofloxacin composition comprises 80-90 wt.% levofloxacin and 10-20 wt.% dexovafloxacin. The preparation step of the crude oil comprises hydraulically and cold pressing by placing the silkworm pupa into the liquid oil squeezing device, pressurizing oil at room temperature to obtain primary oil and oil residue. The pressure is 40-60 kg/cm2. The preparation step of the crude oil further comprises a spiral cold squeezing: crushing the oil cake, placing it into the screw press, extruding to obtain the second cold pressed oil. The preparation step of the crude oil further comprises: combining the recuperation of crude oil and a second cold pressed oil, filtering, placing the filtrate in vacuum condition, charging nitrogen, statically placing to obtain crude oil. The nitrogen filling and the allowing to stand time is 5-8 days.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic; Hepatotropic.

TI Fully automated neutralization treatment system useful for alkaline sewage produced by concrete mixing station, comprises carbon dioxide storage tank; carbon dioxide vaporizer with inlet connected to outlet of carbon dioxide storage tank.
AU NI K
AE WENZHOU SENTAI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 2020084277
AB    NOVELTY - Fully automated neutralization treatment system comprises carbon dioxide storage tank (1); carbon dioxide vaporizer (2) with inlet connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide storage tank; carbon dioxide spray tower (4) with inlet connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide vaporizer; first-level shower plate (4-1) and a second-level shower plate (4-2) are sequentially arranged in carbon dioxide spray tower from top to bottom; an alkaline sewage pond whose inlet is connected to the outlet of carbon dioxide spray tower; electronic pH monitor and a stirrer are connected in the alkaline sewage tank; the outlets of the alkaline sewage pond are respectively connected with the upper part of the first-level shower plate and the second-level shower plate of the carbon dioxide spray tower; the connection methods are all pipeline connections, where carbon dioxide electronic valve is provided on a connection pipeline between carbon dioxide vaporizer and carbon dioxide spray tower.
   USE - The fully automated neutralization treatment system is useful for alkaline sewage produced by concrete mixing station (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fully automated neutralization treatment system: can avoids the disadvantages caused by the neutralization of mineral acids e.g. hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid, and realizes full automation at the same time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fully automated neutralization treatment system comprises carbon dioxide storage tank (1); carbon dioxide vaporizer (2) with inlet connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide storage tank; carbon dioxide spray tower (4) with inlet connected to an outlet of the carbon dioxide vaporizer; first-level shower plate (4-1) and a second-level shower plate (4-2) are sequentially arranged in the carbon dioxide spray tower from top to bottom; an alkaline sewage pond whose inlet is connected to the outlet of the carbon dioxide spray tower; electronic pH monitor and a stirrer are connected in the alkaline sewage tank; the outlets of the alkaline sewage pond are respectively connected with the upper part of the first-level shower plate and the second-level shower plate of the carbon dioxide spray tower; the connection methods are all pipeline connections, where a carbon dioxide electronic valve is provided on a connection pipeline between the carbon dioxide vaporizer and the carbon dioxide spray tower, electronic liquid valves are connected on the remaining pipes; an inlet pump is provided on an outlet pipe of the alkaline sewage pond, a return water pump is provided on an outlet pipe of the carbon dioxide spray tower; the system also includes an electronic control system, the pH electronic monitor transmits a signal to an electronic control system, the electronic control system automatically controls the opening and closing of the carbon dioxide electronic valve, the water inlet pump and the water return pump. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for fully automatic neutralization treatment of alkaline sewage produced by concrete mixing station by using system comprising gasifying the liquid carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide storage tank by carbon dioxide vaporizer, entering the carbon dioxide spray tower; passing the alkaline sewage produced by the concrete mixing station in the alkaline sewage tank to the top of the first-level shower plate and the second-level shower plate of the carbon dioxide spray tower respectively, neutralizing with the carbon dioxide entering the carbon dioxide spray tower, returning the neutralized sewage treatment liquid to the alkaline sewage tank; during the neutralization process, the pH electronic monitor monitors the pH of the sewage in the alkaline sewage tank in real time, and transmits the monitoring result to the electronic control system through an electrical signal; when the pH is greater than 7.5, the electronic control system automatically controlling the carbon dioxide electronic valve, the water inlet pump and the return water pump to be in an open state; when the pH is less than or equal to 7.5, the electronic control system automatically controls the carbon dioxide electronic valve, the inlet pump and the return pump to be in a closed state, and completing neutralization treatment of the alkaline sewage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the fully automated neutralization treatment system.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Carbon dioxide vaporizer (2)
   Carbon dioxide spray tower (4)
   First-level shower plate (4-1)
   Second-level shower plate (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first-level shower plate and the second-level shower plate are both provided with non-blocking sprinklers; the number of the non-blocking sprinklers is four. Preferred Method: In the method, the volume flow of the carbon dioxide gas obtained after the gasification is 20-1000 l/minutes. In the method, the volume flow rate of the outflow of alkaline sewage produced by the mixed soil mixing station in the alkaline sewage pond (5) is 5-50 m3/hour. In the method, the volume flow rate of the reflux of the sewage treatment liquid obtained by the neutralization is 5-50 m3/hour.

After                the supercritical carbon dioxide is heated by the                supercritical carbon dioxide geothermal heat                exchanger, it is heated by the supercritical carbon                dioxide solar collector to drive the supercritical                carbon dioxide generator set to generate                electricity.    USE - Method for performing supercritical carbon                dioxide power generation and long-distance steam                transportation.

The steam-water heat                exchanger for industrial hot water that uses                long-distance transportation of steam for heat                exchange and steam-water heat exchanger for                domestic hot water in the industrial park. After                the supercritical carbon dioxide is heated by the                supercritical carbon dioxide geothermal heat                exchanger, it is heated by the supercritical carbon                dioxide solar collector to drive the supercritical                carbon dioxide generator set to generate                electricity. The hot water load used in industrial                parks has a relatively large jump and has the                characteristics of intermittent use of steam. The                steam in the long-distance steam transmission                pipeline is controlled to enter the steam-water                heat exchanger for industrial hot water for                steam-water heat exchange, followed by controlling                all the supercritical carbon dioxide after heat                exchange by the supercritical carbon dioxide                geothermal heat exchanger, entering the                supercritical carbon dioxide solar collector,                reheating, driving the supercritical carbon dioxide                generator set power generation. The heat is                exchanged between supercritical carbon dioxide and                steam, and after reheating this portion of                supercritical carbon dioxide, it merges with the                supercritical carbon dioxide after being reheated                by the supercritical carbon dioxide solar collector                to drive the supercritical carbon dioxide generator                set to generate electricity.

The liquid metal                  alloy is heated to 200degrees Celsius. The                  purity of the CO2 gas is 99.999%. The liquid                  metal in-situ composite material is uniformly                  stirred by magnetic stirring, ultrasonic stirring                  or centrifugal stirring.

PT P
PN US2021347652-A1
TI Removing rare earth elements from coal ash involves introducing supercritical carbon dioxide to coal ash to get first mixture, introducing first acid to first mixture to get second mixture, and removing first composition from second mixture.
AU HUANG Z
   FAN M
AE UNIV WYOMING (UYWO-C)
GA 2021C73674
AB    NOVELTY - Removing rare earth elements from coal ash involves introducing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) to the coal ash to form a first mixture. A first acid is introduced to the first mixture to form second mixture. A first composition from the second mixture is removed comprising one or more rare earth elements.
   USE - Method for removing rare earth elements from coal ash, used in high technology products such as permanent magnets, lasers, health care devices, catalysts, rechargeable batteries, military products, and various other devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to remove rare earth elements from coal ash, where rare earth elements has electromagnetic, metallurgical, and optical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Process: The method further involves introducing the first mixture to separation conditions prior to the introducing an acid to the first mixture; introducing the first composition to a second acid to form a third mixture; adjusting a pH of the third mixture to form a precipitate comprising the one or more rare earth elements; removing a portion of the first acid from the second mixture; and reintroducing the first acid to the first composition. Preferred Conditions: The first mixture is formed at a temperature of 30degreesC to 200degreesC, a pressure of 1000 psi to 4000 psi, or combination. The first acid is used in the form of a 0.1-10 wt.% solution. The method of removing a rare earth element from a coal-derived product such as coal ash particularly involves disposing the coal-derived product in a reactor, the coal-derived product comprising the rare earth element and a metal, the metal comprising Al, Fe, or a combination; contacting the coal-derived product with supercritical CO2 and an acid to form a mixture; removing a portion of the metal from the mixture to form a first composition having a reduced content of the metal; and precipitating a second composition from the first composition, the second composition comprising the rare earth element from the mixture. The first acid, second acid, and third acid are the same or different. Preferred Components: The first acid is selected from inorganic acid, organic acid, or a combination. The inorganic acid is seelcted from hydrochloric acid (HCl), sulfuric acid (H2SO4), nitric acid (HNO3), or combinations. The first acid comprises organic acid with 1-25C carboxylic acid. The organic acid is seelcted from oxalic acid, citric acid, formic acid, lactic acid, acetic acid, uric acid, malic acid, tartaric acid, or combinations. The second acid is oxalic acid, citric acid, or both. The first portion and second portion of the first component comprises one or more rare earth elements.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN CN113248379-A
TI Preparing ethyl methyl carbonate comprises reacting carbon dioxide, methanol, ethanol continuously in pipeline reactor in presence of catalyst to obtain reaction mixture, rectifying and separating product and side product diethyl carbonate.
AU HE R
   PENG L
   ZHANG Y
AE CHONGQING WEIERYI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2021A0200Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing ethyl methyl carbonate comprises (i) adding carbon dioxide, methanol, ethanol into the pipeline reactor, performing continuous reaction in the presence of a catalyst to obtain the reaction mixture; and (ii) adding the reaction mixture into the rectifying tower for rectifying and separating the product methyl ethyl carbonate and side product diethyl carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethyl methyl carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides 80-90% high comprehensive selectivity of ethyl methyl carbonate and produces ethyl methyl carbonate with electronic grade purity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the diagram of the preparing ethyl methyl carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst in the step (i) comprises alumina, zirconium phosphate, cerium oxide and nickel at weight ratio of 0.4-0.6:0.2-0.3: 0.15-0.2:0.1-0.2, preferably 0.5:0.2:0.2:0.1or 0.4:0.3:0.2:0.1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises mixing the methanol and ethanol at mass ratio of 0.9-1.2:1.1-1.5 and pumping into the pipeline reactor. The pressure of the pipeline in the step (i) is 3-5 Mpa, and the reaction temperature is 90-150 degrees C. The step (i) further comprises returning the un-reacted carbon dioxide into the pipeline reactor after being purified from the pipeline reactor to continue to participate in the reaction. The rectification comprises (a) introducing the reaction mixture into the first rectifying tower for rectifying and introducing the first rectifying tower bottom extract into the second rectifying tower and rectifying, where the tower top distillate is methyl ethyl carbonate and the tower bottom collecting stream is diethyl carbonate; (b) introducing the first rectifying tower top product into the third rectifying tower for rectifying, whete the tower top collecting component is dimethyl carbonate, methanol, ethanol and water and the tower bottom collecting stream is methyl ethyl carbonate, dimethyl carbonate and water; or (c) returning the extracted component at the top of the third rectifying tower into the pipeline continuous reactor to continue to participate in the reaction. The first rectifying tower, the tower plate number is 40-60, the tower top temperature is 72-76 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 108-112 degrees C, the reflux ratio is R3-6, the second rectifying tower, the tower plate number is 50-70, the tower top temperature is 106.9 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 124-130 degrees C, the reflux ratio is R4-6, the third rectifying tower, the tower plate number is 30-50, the tower top temperature is 68-72 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 96-100 degrees C and the reflux ratio is R2-5. The first rectifying tower, the tower plate number is 50, the tower top temperature is 74 plus minus 1 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 109 plus minus 1 degrees C, the reflux ratio R is 4, the second rectifying tower, the tower plate number is 60, the tower top temperature is 106 plus minus 1 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 127 plus minus 1 degrees C, the reflux ratio R= 5 and the third rectifying tower plate number is 40, the tower top temperature is 70 plus minus 1 degrees C, the tower kettle temperature is 98 plus minus 1 degrees C and the reflux ratio R is 3. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the added carbon dioxide into the mixed alcohol of methanol and ethanol in the step (i) is 3-5:2-3.

GA 2021863260

   USE - Method for preparing oxygen-doped carbon nitride composite photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) to convert high value-added fuel (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly improves light utilization, CO2 affinity and carrier transfer, and has a good application in the conversion of photocatalytic reduction of CO2 to high value-added fuel.

The drying conditions are vacuum drying at 60 degrees C for 12 hours. The composite catalyst is denoted as O CN x, where x represents the molar ratio of dicyandiamide to 1,3,5-trichlorotriazine, x=0.1-0.5, and the O-CN-x is red, has a spherical structure, and is uniformly dispersed. Preferred Components: The high value-added fuel is carbon monoxide (CO) and methane (CH4).

PN CN111482200-A
TI Preparation method of composite photocatalyst, by            preparing zinc-benzimidazole (BIM) by wet chemical            method, performing surface defect treatment to obtain            defective zinc-BIM-histidine, and hybridizing by            graphene quantum dots.
AU WEI D
   GAN Y
   YAN W
   ZHANG J
   XIAO X
   LIU Y
AE XI'AN MODERN CHEM RES INST (CHNI-C)
GA 2020754101
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of graphene quantum dots                (GQDs)/zinc-benzimidazole (BIM)-histidine composite                photocatalyst, involves (a) preparing zinc-BIM by                wet chemical method, (b) performing surface defect                treatment on the zinc-BIM prepared in step (a), to                obtain defective zinc-BIM-histidine, and (c) using                GQDs to hybridize the defect zinc-BIM-histidine to                obtain the photocatalyst.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of                GQDs/BIM-histidine composite photocatalyst, which                is used in the photocatalytic conversion of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes easily available raw                materials, and has simple preparation, can easily                control the preparation conditions. The prepared                photocatalyst is a green and pollution-free                high-performance catalyst, which can efficiently                convert the greenhouse gas carbon dioxide into                fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the preparing                  zinc-BIM by wet chemical method includes adding a                  zinc salt and benzimidazole to dimethylformamide,                  and stirring at room temperature to obtain the                  reaction product, washing the reaction product                  with deionized water, centrifuging, and drying to                  obtain zinc-BIM. In step (b), the surface defect                  treatment on zinc-BIM-histidine includes:                  weighing the zinc-BIM prepared in step (a) into                  deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing,                  transferring to the reaction vessel, adding                  polyvinylpyrrolidone and L-histidine, putting it                  into an oil bath, stirring, heating, reacting,                  lowering the temperature to room temperature,                  then washing with absolute ethanol and water, and                  freeze-drying to obtain defective                  zinc-BIM-histidine. In step (c), the using GQDs                  to hybridize defect zinc-BIM-histidine includes                  weighing the defective zinc-BIM-histidine                  prepared in step (b) into anhydrous n-hexane,                  ultrasonically dispersing, then stirring at room                  temperature, while dripping GQDs aqueous                  solution, reacting, washing with absolute ethanol                  and deionized water, centrifuging, and                  freeze-drying to obtain the defective                  GQDs/zinc-BIM-histidine photocatalyst. Preferred                  Conditions: In step (a), the stirring time is                  12-36 hours. In step (b), the heating of the oil                  bath is performed at 90-150 degrees C for 15-30                  hours. The ultrasonic dispersion time is 5-60                  minutes and the stirring time is 1-5 hours.                  Preferred Composition: In step (a), the ratio of                  the zinc salt to the amount of benzimidazole is                  (0.250-0.550 g): (1.200-1.500 g). In step (b),                  the proportions of zinc-BIM, deionized water,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, and L-histidine are                  0.050-0.200 g, 20-100 ml, 0.100-0.500 g, and                  0.300-0.800 g. The dosage ratio of defective                  zinc-BIM-histidine and anhydrous n-hexane is                  (10-50 mg):(20-100 ml). The amount of GQDs                  aqueous solution added is 5-20 mu L, and the                  concentration of GQDs aqueous solution is 100-250                  mg/ml. Preferred Components: The zinc salt is                  zinc nitrate hexahydrate.

PN US2019210904-A1; US10793458-B2
TI Treating wastewater containing ammonia nitrogen            nitrogen involves treating the wastewater to create a            gas and without the addition of chemicals that are not            inherently present in the wastewater and converting            substantially all ammonia.
AU ORENTLICHER M
   SIMON M M
AE BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
   BION ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGIES INC (BION-Non-standard)
GA 201960297J
AB    NOVELTY - Treating wastewater containing ammonia                nitrogen involves treating the wastewater at a                temperature of at least 60 degrees C to create a                gas and, without the addition of chemicals that are                not inherently present in the wastewater,                converting substantially all ammonia in the                wastewater to gaseous form in the gas, cooling the                gas and mixing the gas containing gaseous ammonia                with carbon dioxide and water vapor forming                dissolved ammonium carbonate and ammonium                bicarbonate in a liquid condensate, where each of                the ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor are                derived from the wastewater, crystallizing the                concentrated dissolved ammonium carbonate and                ammonium bicarbonate condensate.
   USE - Method for treating wastewater containing                ammonia nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves concentrating the dissolved ammonium                  carbonate and ammonium bicarbonate. The                  concentration involves taking place after the                  cooling step and before the crystallizing step.                  Treating wastewater involves stripping the                  wastewater of ammonia, drying the wastewater                  using a dryer and removing ammonia with the water                  vapor and filtering the wastewater using an ultra                  filter to remove solids of greater than 0.5                  micron in size. Preferred Components: The                  dissolved ammonium carbonate and ammonium                  bicarbonate are concentrated to a saturation                  concentration in the liquid condensate using                  reverse osmosis operating at a temperature                  between 35 and 50 degrees C. Substantially all of                  the gaseous ammonia in the gas is converted into                  ammonium carbonate and ammonia bicarbonate using                  the carbon dioxide in the gas without the                  addition of carbon dioxide not present in a                  digestate. The wastewater is a digested dairy                  manure or other livestock manure. The solid                  crystals of ammonium bicarbonate are grown in a                  crystallizer to create a solid ammonium salt.                  Preferred Conditions: The treating wastewater is                  carried out at a temperature of at least 80                  degrees C. dissolved ammonium carbonate and                  ammonium bicarbonate concentration in the                  concentrated reverse osmosis effluent is at least                  about 50 times the concentration of dissolved                  ammonium bicarbonate in the digestate.

TI Gastroretentive pharmaceutical composition includes active ingredient effective in stomach, auxiliary substance, and component capable of forming carbon dioxide upon contact with gastric juices.
AU LISAL J
   JAMAEINOVA J
   VYBIRALOVA Z
   TRNKOVA J
   REZAC J
   SVOBODA Z
AE PRO.MED.CS PRAHA AS (PROM-Non-standard)
   PRO.MED.CS PRAHA AS (PROM-Non-standard)
GA 201951495T
AB    NOVELTY - A gastroretentive pharmaceutical composition comprises active ingredient(s) effective in stomach, and auxiliary substance(s) capable of swelling the composition to density below 1 g/cm3 at pH 1.5-3.5, auxiliary substance(s) ensuring sustained release of the active ingredient, and component(s) capable of forming carbon dioxide upon contact with gastric juices.
   USE - Gastroretentive pharmaceutical composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The formulation enables to prolong the drug residence time in stomach where the active pharmaceutical ingredient is slowly released from the dosage form and a portion of it locally acts on the mucosa.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the preparation of the pharmaceutical composition comprising wet granulation and/or dry homogenization of the active ingredient with the auxiliary substances and components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The component capable of forming carbon dioxide upon contact with gastric juices comprises 2-15 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% carbonates or hydrogen carbonates of alkali metals and alkaline earth metals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The active ingredient effective in stomach is rebamipide. The auxiliary substance capable of swelling the pharmaceutical composition to density below 1 g/cm3 at pH 1.5-3.5 is crospovidone, which is preferably present at 5-15 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The auxiliary substances capable of ensuring sustained release of the active ingredient include cellulose and cellulose ethers such as hydroxypropylcellulose, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, sodium carboxymethylcellulose, or ethylcellulose; alginic acid and alginates, such as propylene glycol alginate or sodium alginate; polymethacrylate polymers; guar gum; or xanthan gum. The auxiliary substance capable of ensuring sustained release of the active ingredient comprises 10-50 wt.%, preferably 15-35 wt.% alginate such as propylene glycol alginate, and/or 3-15 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% cellulose ether. The component is capable of adhering to the stomach mucose, preferably mucoadhesive component is alginate such as propylene glycol alginate. The composition comprises 20-50 wt.%, preferably 30-40 wt.% active ingredient(s) effective in stomach, preferably rebamipide, cellulose ether(s) such as hydroxypropylcellulose, hydroxypropyl methylcellulose or ethylcellulose; alginate(s) such as propylene glycol alginate, sodium alginate, or alginic acid; carbon dioxide-forming component such as sodium hydrogen carbonate, sodium carbonate, calcium hydrogen carbonate or calcium carbonate; crospovidone; lubricant(s) such as magnesium stearate, calcium stearate, stearic acid, sodium laurylsulfate, filler/diluent and/or disintegrant such as saccharide alcohols (mannitol, sorbitol or xylitol), lactose, starch, cellulose, sodium cross-carmelose or sodium carboxymethyl starch. Preferred Method: The pharmaceutical composition is prepared by wet granulation of the active ingredient, preferably rebamipide, with a filler/diluent and crospovidone, drying, and homogenization of the obtained granules with cellulose ether, alginate, crospovidone, carbon dioxide-forming component and lubricant. The homogenization is carried out in three steps, in the following order: homogenization of the granulate with cellulose ether, homogenization of the thus obtained homogenizate with alginate and carbon-dioxide-forming component, and homogenization of the thus obtained homogenizate with the lubricant.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration is oral (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - A tablet composition was prepared from 32.43% rebamipide, 6.49% hydroxypropyl methylcellulose, 10.81% mannitol, 9.95% crospovidone, 6.49% sodium bicarbonate, 1.08% magnesium stearate, and 32.76% alginate propylene glycol.

PN CN109880135-A
TI Preparation of cellulose membrane material involves mixing cellulose, base and solvent, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting, defoaming, pouring on support layer, placing in regeneration bath of e.g. aliphatic alcohol, washing and drying.
AU XIE H
   JIN L
   ZHENG Q
   ZHANG L
   XU Q
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2019549429
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cellulose membrane material involves mixing cellulose, an organic base and an organic solvent, introducing carbon dioxide at pressure of 0.1-15 MPa into the mixture, reacting at 30-100 degrees C for 0.1-24 hours to form a uniform phase cellulose solution, defoaming by centrifugation, pouring on a flat support layer, slowly smoothening by a doctor blade, placing the support layer in a regeneration bath of water, an aqueous acid salt solution or 1-3C lower aliphatic alcohol, washing, regenerating and drying.
   USE - Preparation of cellulose membrane material.
   ADVANTAGE - The cellulose membrane material having excellent transparency and mechanical properties can be formed economically and efficiently.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of the aqueous acid-base salt solution is 1-20 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic base is a compound chosen from compounds of formulae (I)-(X). The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyridinone, tetramethyl urea, tetraethyl urea, N,N-dimethylimidazolidinone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylacetamide, pyrrolidone, 2-azacyclohexane, epsilon -caprolactam, N,N-dimethylpropenylurea, sulfolane and/or piperylene sulfone. The cellulose solution contains cation (I)-(X) and anion of formulae (XI) and (XII).R1 = 1-6C alkyl;R21-R23 = methyl;n = 1 or 2;m = 1-6;R,R2-R5 = H, methyl or ethyl;andR11 = H or 1-6C alkyl.Preferred Property: The smoothened solution layer has thickness of 5-1000 mu m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cellulose is microcrystalline cellulose or alpha -cellulose and plant cellulose obtained from cotton pulp, wood pulp, bamboo pulp, crop straw or crop seed husk, and has structural formula (A), and has polymerization degree of more than 50 and less than 100. The support layer is a glass plate, a stainless steel plate or an engineering plastic. Preferred Composition: The raw materials contain 0.1-20 wt.% cellulose, 0.5-50 wt.% organic base and remaining organic solvent.

PN CN109772384-A
TI Hydroxyphosphorus lime loaded platinum catalyst catalyzing the decomposition of formaldehyde to non-toxic carbon dioxide and water at room temperature comprises hydroxyapatite as catalyst carrier and platinum nanoparticles as active center.
AU XU Z
   HUANG G
   YAN C
AE UNIV JIANGHAN (UYJJ-C)
GA 201947025T
AB    NOVELTY - Hydroxyphosphorus lime loaded platinum catalyst comprises hydroxyapatite as catalyst carrier and platinum nanoparticles as active center, where the mass of platinum in catalyst is 0.2-0.4%.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in catalyzing the decomposition of formaldehyde to non-toxic carbon dioxide and water at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of hydroxyphosphorus lime loaded platinum catalyst comprising dispersing the hydroxyphosphorus lime in water, obtaining the dispersion liquid, adding platinum precursor into the dispersion liquid to obtain mixed solution, adding reducing agent into the mixed solution for reducing the platinum precursor to obtain a mixture, and purifying the mixture to obtain the catalyst containing platinum with mass of 0.2-0.4 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The platinum precursor is chloroplatinic acid, platinum element in the chloroplatinic acid accounts for 0.2-0.4% by mass of the catalyst. The reducing agent is a mixture of sodium borohydride and sodium hydroxide. Calcium salt is calcium nitrate, calcium acetate and calcium chloride; phosphate is disodium hydrogen phosphate, trisodium phosphate and dipotassium phosphate; the alkali is sodium hydroxide solution, potassium hydroxide solution and mixture solution of potassium hydroxide and sodium hydroxide. The modifier is sodium citrate and hexamethylene tetramine. Calcium salt and phosphate mol ratio is 4-5:1; the weight ratio of the modifier and calcium salt is 1:45-50. Preferred Method: In the process, dispersing hydroxyphosphorus lime in water and stirring for 0.2-0.3 hours to obtain dispersion liquid, immersing the platinum precursor in the dispersion liquid for 0.25-0.5 hours to obtain the mixed solution. The hydroxyphosphorus lime is prepared by dispersing the calcium salt and modifier in water to obtain dispersion liquid I, dissolving phosphate in the alkali solution to obtain solution II, mixing dispersion liquid I and the solution II uniformly, performing hydro-thermal treatment at 150-200 degrees C, then calcining obtained precursor at 600-800 degrees C to obtain hydroxyphosphorus lime material.

PN KR2019028937-A
TI Removing metal cations e.g. sodium in concentrated water by performing electrodialysis of concentrated water, mixing with ammonia solution, injecting carbon dioxide gas to precipitate sodium, and removing sodium hydroxide.
AU HWAN B J
   SEUNGWOO L
   CHUN C S
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 2019277231
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing metal cations (sodium, calcium and magnesium) in concentrated water from a seawater desalination plant, involves performing electrodialysis of the concentrated water to separate a concentrated water containing monovalent cations containing sodium and a concentrated water containing divalent cations comprising calcium and magnesium, mixing the concentrated water with ammonia solution, injecting carbon dioxide gas to precipitate sodium in the concentrated water, and separating and removing the sodium hydroxide.
   USE - The method is useful for removing metal cations (e.g. sodium, calcium and magnesium) in concentrated water from seawater desalination plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and drastically reduces total dissolved solids (TDS) of the concentrated water by precipitating sodium, calcium and magnesium in the concentrated water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the sodium removal step, the concentration of the metal ion in the concentrated water is 10000 ppm or less. The method involves recovering the ammonia from aqueous solution of ammonium chloride formed together with sodium hydrogencarbonate in the sodium removal step. The method involves (i) separating magnesium precipitate from the concentrated water after precipitating the magnesium into a solid state by raising a pH of the concentrated water comprising divalent cations separated in the pretreatment step to a first range, (ii) adjusting the pH of the concentrated water from which magnesium is separated to a second range higher than the first range to precipitate calcium in a solid state and then separating the calcium precipitate from the concentrated water, (iii) reacting the magnesium precipitate with carbon dioxide to precipitate magnesium carbonate, and (iv) precipitating calcium carbonate by reacting the calcium precipitate with carbon dioxide. The method involves using concentrated water separated by solid-liquid separation after precipitating sodium as raw water for desalination process, or reusing sodium hydroxide obtained through electrolysis as a pH regulator.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)
