PN CN109402400-A
TI Recovering chromium from a chromium-containing            sludge under alkaline condition involves mixing            chromium-containing sludge with sodium carbonate            solution, and then performing leaching by heating and            pressurizing carbon dioxide.
AU ZHOU B
   LI L
   GAO M
   WANG H
   BAI L
   ZHAO B
AE CHENGDE BRANCH HBIS CO LTD (HBIS-C)
GA 201922636L
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering chromium from a chromium-containing                sludge under alkaline condition involves mixing                chromium-containing sludge with sodium carbonate                solution, performing leaching by heating and                pressurizing carbon dioxide, and separating solid                and liquid completing leaching to obtain a                chromium-containing solution and a leaching                slag.
   USE - Method for recovering chromium from a                chromium-containing sludge under alkaline                condition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recover chromium from a                chromium-containing sludge under alkaline                condition, which has mild leaching process                condition, significant economic and environmental                benefit, realizes resource recovering and harmless                treatment of chromium element in                chromium-containing sludge, reaches chromium                recovery rate to more than 95%, increases added                value of recovered chromium, reduces energy                consumption, does not discharge wastewater in                production process, and good application                prospect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The                  chromium-containing sludge contains 10-25 wt.%                  chromium and 5-20 wt.% calcium. Preferred                  Conditions: The solid-liquid ratio of                  chromium-containing sludge and sodium carbonate                  solution is 1:(1-5), and mass ratio of                  chromium-containing sludge and sodium carbonate                  in sodium carbonate solution is (0.25-2):1. The                  leaching is carried out at a temperature of                  90-150 degrees C and pressure of 0.5-1.5MPa for                  1-4 hours. The leaching is completed, and                  reaction slurry is diluted with water or dilute                  alkali solution, and then solid-liquid separation                  is performed to obtain a leaching slag and a                  chromium-containing solution, and when chromium                  concentration in the obtained chromium-containing                  solution is greater than or equal to 10g/L then                  chromium-containing solution is crystallized to                  obtain sodium chromate crystals and a                  crystallized liquid. The crystallization liquid                  is carbonized and then returned for leaching the                  chromium-containing sludge. The leaching reaction                  is carried out in an autoclave.

PN CN109096092-A
TI Preparing stable isotope 13C labeled pyruvic acid comprises e.g. condensing 13C-labeled acetaldehyde with thiol, reacting with n-butyllithium and 13C labeled carbon dioxide and deprotecting 13C-labeled dithioketal propionic acid.
AU QIU J
   LI H
   JIANG Y
   CAI Y
   FANG H
AE SHANGHAI CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (SHNR-C)
GA 201902460Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stable isotope 13C labeled pyruvic acid comprises (i) condensing 13C-labeled acetaldehyde as a raw material with thiol to obtain intermediate 13C-labeled thioacetal, (ii) reacting intermediate 13C-labeled thioacetal with n-butyllithium to obtain intermediate 13C-labeled dithioacetal alkyllithium solution, (iii) reacting intermediate 13C-labeled dithioacetal alkyllithium solution with 13C-labeled carbon dioxide to obtain intermediate 13C labeled dithioketal propionic acid, and (iv) deprotecting 13C-labeled dithioketal propionic acid to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing stable isotope 13C-labeled pyruvic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes cheap and easily available raw materials; and has short four-step synthetic conditions, mild reaction conditions, more than 99% chemical purity, more than 99% atom 13C abundance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acetaldehyde is acetaldehyde, (1-13C)-acetaldehyde, (2-13C)-acetaldehyde or (1,2-13C2)-acetaldehyde; thiol is 1,2-ethanedithiol, 1,3-propanedithiol, 1,4-butanedithiol, 1,5-pentanedithiol, methanethiol or ethanethiol; reaction solvent is dichloromethane, ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran or diethyl ether; and acid is hydrogen chloride gas, boron trifluoride diethyl ether solution, zinc trifluoroacetate, lithium bromide or lithium tetrafluoroborate. The molar ratio between thiol and acetaldehyde is 1:1-10:1. The organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, diethyl ether, 1,4-dioxane, toluene or benzene. The molar ratio between 13C-labeled dithioacetal and n-butyllithium is 1:6-1:1. The solvent is methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, water, acetone, tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, dimethyl sulfoxide and/or N,N-dimethylformamide; catalyst is ferric chloride hexahydrate, mercury dichloride, elemental iodine, zinc bromide, cerium trichloride, hydrated iron sulfate or tetrabutylammonium bromide; and the molar ratio between catalyst and 13C-labeled dithioketal propionic acid is 1:10-1:1; and 13C-labeled pyruvic acid is (1-13C)-pyruvate, (2-13C)-pyruvate, (3-13C)-pyruvate, (1,2-13C2)-pyruvate, (1,3-13C2)-pyruvate, (2,3-13C2)-pyruvate or (1,2,3-13C3)-pyruvate. Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises adding reaction solvent, thiol and acetaldehyde into the reaction vessel and stirring for 4-12 hours under acid catalysis at 0-60 degrees C. The step (ii) comprises adding 13C-labeled thioacetal into organic solvent and n-butyllithium and stirring at -40 to 0 degrees C for 1-10 hours. The step (iii) comprises reacting 13C-labeled dithioacetal alkyl lithium solution into the carbon dioxide at -40 to 0 degrees C for 1-8 hours, heating at room temperature, stirring overnight, adjusting the pH to 9-11, separating the solution by silica gel column chromatography, where the volume ratio between eluent ethyl acetate and petroleum ether is 1:1-1:8. The step (iv) comprises adding solvent and catalyst into the reaction vessel, further adding 13C-labeled dithioketal propionic acid to control the reaction temperature to 0-50 degrees C, stirring for 4-24 hours and purifying.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.)

PN CN109019694-A; CN109019694-B
TI Preparing micro-nano structured spherical manganese carbonate comprises dissolving, allowing to react, introducing carbon dioxide, cooling, separating, washing and drying.
AU HAI C
   XI G
   ZHOU Y
   SHEN Y
   ZENG J
   LI X
   REN X
   ZHANG L
   SUN Y
   DONG S
   QI G
AE QINGHAI INST SALT LAKES CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAQH-C)
GA 2018A52139
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micro-nano structured spherical manganese carbonate comprises (i) dissolving water-soluble manganese salt and aqueous ammonia in mass ratio of 0.25-0.5 g:1 ml in alcohol solvent to obtain reaction mixture where ratio of water-soluble manganese salt and alcohol component in alcohol solvent is 0.04-0.2 g:1 ml; (ii) allowing reaction mixture to react at 60-80 degrees C for 2-6 hours and introducing carbon dioxide to obtain reaction product; and (iii) cooling reaction product, separating solid and liquid, washing obtained solid phase and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing micro-nano structured spherical manganese carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and does not cause any pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol component is ethylene glycol. The volume fraction of alcohol component is not less than 20%. The water-soluble manganese salt is manganese chloride, manganese sulfate or manganese acetate. The size of micro-nano structure spherical manganese carbonate is 0.2-2 mu m. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), introducing carbon dioxide at rate of 0.5-2 l/minute. In the step (iii), washing solid phase three times with de-ionized water and washing once with absolute ethanol and drying washed solid phase vacuum drying oven at 70-90 degrees C for at least 8 hours.

PN CN108543533-A; CN108543533-B
TI Platinum-loaded titania/hydroxyapatite shell structure composite photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction, comprises titania photocatalyst nanorod core of preset length and diameter, and hydroxyapatite shell layer loaded with platinum.
AU ZHONG R
   CHANG Z
   WANG X
   FAN Y
   WANG J
   ZHANG L
   LI D
   CHONG R
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 201875915T
AB    NOVELTY - A platinum-loaded titania/hydroxyapatite shell structure composite photocatalyst comprises a composite core of titania photocatalyst nanorod and a shell layer of hydroxyapatite loaded with platinum. The nanorod has a length of 150-500 nm and a diameter of 50-80 nm. The shell layer has a thickness of 2-10 nm.
   USE - Platinum-loaded titania/hydroxyapatite shell structure composite photocatalyst for carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst comprises an airtight interface, uniform shell layer, and controlled thickness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composite photocatalyst, which involves preparing a titania/calcium hydroxide intermediate, directly phosphating to form hydroxyapatite shell layer, and supporting platinum on the shell layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The titania nanorods are added to a calcium hydroxide solution, heated and evaporated under protection of argon gas, and calcium hydroxide is gradually deposited on the surface of nanorods. Phosphating involves adding the intermediate to diammonium hydrogen phosphate solution, adjusting pH to 8-12, and hydrothermally reacting at 100-180 degrees C for 4-12 hours. Platinum is deposited by photodeposition.

PT P
PN CN110270314-A; CN110270314-B
TI Preparing naphthalimide-based zinc-based metal            organic framework useful for separating carbon            dioxide/nitrogen in a flue gas, comprises dissolving a            zinc salt and a ligand, heating the reaction,            separating and activating.
AU LI Z
   XIANG S
   ZHANG Z
   TAO J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 201982596D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing naphthalimide-based zinc-based metal                organic framework comprises dissolving a zinc salt                and a ligand, heating the reaction, separating, and                activating after soaking.
   USE - The framework is useful for separating carbon                dioxide/nitrogen in a flue gas (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The zinc salt                  comprises zinc nitrate hexahydrate, heptahydrate                  zinc sulfate or dihydrate zinc acetate. The                  ligand comprises H2NDI and H2BDC, where the H2NDI                  comprises a double pyrazolyl of naphthalene                  diimide ligand, and the solvent comprises N, N                  -formamide and alcohol. The alcohol comprises                  methanol and/or ethanol, the small molecule                  organic solvent comprises methanol, ethanol,                  acetone and dichloromethane. The molar ratio of                  the zinc salt to the H2NDI and the H2BDC is 1:                  (0.2-0.6): (0.8-1.2). The molar ratio of the zinc                  salt to the N,N-dimethylformamide and the alcohol                  is 1: (510-530): (240-250). The molecular formula                  is (Zn2(H2NDI)(BDC).2MeOH.2.5H2O)n, where H2NDI                  is a naphthyldiimide ligand containing a                  bispyrazolyl group, and BDC is a deprotonated                  phthalic acid. The molar ratio of the zinc salt                  to the H2NDI and the BDC is 1: (0.2-0.6). The                  three-dimensional porous structure with double                  interpenetration, the unit cell parameters are                  alpha =90 degrees , beta =122.425(5) degrees and                  gamma =90 degrees . The BET specific surface area                  is 313.06 m2.g-1, and the pore diameter is mainly                  distributed.    USE - The method is useful for coupling dehydrating                for improving carbon dioxide hydrogenation                conversion rate.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation reaction is carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation to methanol, carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation to dimethyl ether or carbon dioxide                  hydrogenation reverse water gas shift reaction.                  The carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction is                  another reaction that generates water. The metal                  structure is a through-hole metal foam made of                  copper, nickel, aluminum, silver, iron or their                  alloys, or a 3D structure formed by sintering                  micron fibers of these metals and their alloys.                  The dehydrating agent includes inorganic metal                  alkoxides, silicon alkoxides, concentrated                  sulfuric acid, alkali metal oxides, aluminum                  oxide, silica gel, calcium carbide, inorganic                  acid anhydrides, organic ketals, acetals,                  nitriles, organic acid anhydrides, cyclic ethers,                  and mixtures of the aforementioned dehydrating                  agents or mixtures of dehydrating agents and                  inert components. The metal structured                  catalyst includes two parts (i) macroscopic metal                  structure with a microscopic through-hole                  structure, and (ii) micro-nano catalyst particles                  embedded in the cavity of the structure or a                  catalyst film attached and fixed on the surface                  of the structure. The dehydrating agent meets the                  following conditions (a) there is a liquid or                  gaseous state in the temperature range of room                  temperature to 300&#176;C, (b) can react with                  water, and a large reaction equilibrium constant                  can be obtained in the temperature range of room                  temperature -300&#176;C, Keq &#8805; 100,                  (c) reacting product with water is not a strong                  acid or a strong base, and is easily separated                  from methanol, dimethyl ether or carbon monoxide,                  (d) under the conditions of methanol synthesis,                  dimethyl ether synthesis, and reverse water gas                  shift, that is, room temperature -300&#176;C,                  and Not easy to react with carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen, (e) is easy to obtain and the supply is                  large, and (f) value of the coupling reaction                  itself increases, that is, the value of the                  dehydration product is higher than that of the                  dehydrating agent itself.

AB    NOVELTY - Production of titanium carbide-plated diamond comprises sintering surface plated diamon in carbon-containing mixed gas atmosphere. The mixed gas contains 5-15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide, 5-15 pts. wt. hydrogen and 25-45 pts. wt. methane.

   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve durability of bonding tool.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The sintering is carried out at 550-750 degrees C for 90-180 minutes. The production of titanium carbide-plated diamond comprises weighing titanium dioxide, magnesium chloride and aluminum powder, and mixing uniformly to get coating powder; mixing anhydrous ethanol, butanol and glycerol uniformly to prepare binder; weigh diamond and binder, adding to container, stirring, adding coating powder, stirring to get mixed raw materials, and setting aside; and placing mixed raw materials in a vacuum chamber, heating to 750-850 degrees C under vacuum conditions, keeping for 50-100 minutes, filling vacuum evaporation chamber with argon gas to cool and cooling.

An oxygen content detector (7), a fan and a programmable logic controller (PLC) control unit (9) are set near the flexible pipe portion of the gas carbon dioxide pipeline and the flexible pipe portion of the liquid carbon dioxide pipeline. The PLC control unit is respectively and electrically connected with the oxygen content detector and the fan. The outlet pipeline of the fan is far away from the flexible pipe portion of the gas carbon dioxide pipeline and the flexible pipe portion of the liquid carbon dioxide pipeline.
   USE - Device for avoiding accident caused by leaked gas when filling liquid carbon dioxide tank car.
   ADVANTAGE - The air containing a large amount of carbon dioxide is discharged to the filling area far away from the carbon dioxide tank car storage tank through the outlet pipeline of the fan, so that the carbon dioxide concentration of the air in the filling area is reduced and the oxygen concentration of the air in the filling area is improved.

The                carbon dioxide in the waste ion liquid is conveyed                to the solution-carbon dioxide mixing device                through a carbon dioxide discharge end. The system                uses lower regeneration temperature and effectively                uses the low grade waste heat, and can be widely                applied to the places with low grade heat energy                and with air dehumidifying cooling adjusting                requirement.

PT P
PN DE102017222030-A1; WO2019110443-A1; EP3720815-A1; EP3720815-B1
TI Providing carbon dioxide for synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprises supplying carbon dioxide-containing flue gas to low-pressure absorber, in which carbon dioxide from second starting gas mixture is absorbed in solvent.
AU MAKHYNYA Y
   EL HAWARY T
   EL-HAWARY T
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP IND SOLUTIONS AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
GA 201950231B
AB    NOVELTY - Providing carbon dioxide for synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprises utilizing a carbon dioxide-containing flue gas as a first starting gas mixture, supplying first starting gas mixture to a low-pressure absorber, in which carbon dioxide from the first starting gas mixture is absorbed in a liquid solvent based on an ammonia/water mixture, utilizing a carbon dioxide-containing syngas as a second starting gas mixture, and supplying second starting gas mixture to a high-pressure absorber, in which carbon dioxide from the second starting gas mixture is absorbed in a liquid solvent at an increased pressure. The same carbon dioxide-depleted liquid solvent is used for the absorption step in the low-pressure absorber and the absorption step in the high-pressure absorber, where solvent is supplied to the high-pressure absorber and the low-pressure absorber in at least two parallel sub-flows.
   USE - The method is useful for providing carbon dioxide for synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has lower specific energy consumption and reduced capital costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Providing carbon dioxide for synthesizing urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprises utilizing a carbon dioxide-containing flue gas as a first starting gas mixture, supplying first starting gas mixture to a low-pressure absorber, in which carbon dioxide from the first starting gas mixture is absorbed in a liquid solvent based on an ammonia/water mixture; utilizing a carbon dioxide-containing syngas as a second starting gas mixture, and supplying second starting gas mixture to a high-pressure absorber, in which carbon dioxide from the second starting gas mixture is absorbed in a liquid solvent at an increased pressure. The same carbon dioxide-depleted liquid solvent is used for the absorption step in the low-pressure absorber and the absorption step in the high-pressure absorber, where solvent is supplied to the high-pressure absorber and the low-pressure absorber in at least two parallel sub-flows. A first carbon dioxide-enriched solvent flow exiting the low-pressure absorber and a second carbon dioxide-enriched solvent flow exiting the high-pressure absorber are then combined in order to form a common solvent flow. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for providing carbon dioxide for the synthesis of urea from ammonia and carbon dioxide, comprising at least one low-pressure absorber, a device for supplying a first starting gas mixture comprising a flue gas containing carbon dioxide to this low-pressure absorber, a device for supplying a liquid solvent based on an ammonia-water mixture to this low-pressure absorber, a high-pressure absorber, a device for supplying a second starting gas mixture comprising a carbon dioxide-containing synthesis gas to this high-pressure absorber, a device for supplying a liquid solvent based on an ammonia-water mixture to this high-pressure absorber, at least one exit conduit on the low pressure absorber for discharging carbon dioxide loaded solvent from the low pressure absorber, at least one exit conduit on the high pressure absorber for discharging carbon dioxide loaded solvent from the high pressure absorber and at least one desorption device suitable for expelling carbon dioxide at elevated pressure from the carbon dioxide-laden solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A first carbon dioxide-enriched solvent stream leaving the low pressure absorber and a second carbon dioxide-enriched solvent stream leaving the high-pressure absorber are subsequently combined to form a common solvent stream. Carbon dioxide is desorbed in a desorption device from a first carbon dioxide-enriched solvent stream emerging from the low-pressure absorber, from a second carbon dioxide-enriched solvent stream emerging from the high-pressure absorber, or from a combined common solvent stream comprising low-pressure absorber and high-pressure absorber. The carbon dioxide-enriched solvent is compressed to a pressure above the urea synthesis pressure, and the carbon dioxide is desorbed from the solvent in a desorption apparatus at a pressure above the urea synthesis pressure and then the desorbed carbon dioxide is fed to a urea synthesis unit. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide-enriched solvent is desorbed at elevated temperature from the solvent, preferably at a temperature of 150 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C. The solvent enriched with carbon dioxide from the flue gas is first brought to an elevated pressure from the low pressure absorber and then combined with the solvent enriched with carbon dioxide from the synthesis gas from the high pressure absorber. The second carbon dioxide-enriched solvent stream leaving the high-pressure absorber is subsequently cooled and then released, where gases absorbed in the solvent which are different from carbon dioxide, preferably nitrogen and/or hydrogen, are outgassed and separated from the solvent. The combined solvent stream of high-pressure absorber and low-pressure absorber is brought to a higher pressure after combining, where the pressure increase is carried out by a pump in the liquid state. Carbon dioxide depleted solvent from the desorption apparatus is at least partially recycled to the low pressure absorber and/or at least partially to the high pressure absorber. The combined solvent stream from the low-pressure absorber and the high-pressure absorber, before it is supplied to the desorption, is divided into at least two partial streams, where the first partial stream is fed to a first heat exchanger and the second partial stream is fed to a second heat exchanger. The two partial flows are reunited after flowing through the two heat exchangers and then fed to the desorption device. A dilute aqueous ammonia solution having an ammonia content of 5-30 wt.%, preferably 15-20 wt.% is used as a solvent for the absorption of carbon dioxide in the two absorbers. The flue gas is heated or cooled prior to the step of absorbing in the low pressure absorber. The flue gas is filtered or wet-washed prior to the step of absorbing in the low pressure absorber to remove fly ash or soot particles. The flue gas purified of carbon dioxide is removed from the top region of the low-pressure absorber and/or a purified synthesis gas is removed from the top region of the high-pressure absorber.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises means for supplying a gas mixture from a separator to the low-pressure absorber. The low pressure absorber and the high pressure absorber are arranged in a common apparatus comprising a low pressure zone and a high pressure zone. The device comprises at least one first turbine arranged in the conduit path of a conduit for depleted solvent discharged from the desorption apparatus between desorption apparatus and the high pressure absorber to vent the depleted solvent and recover stored mechanical energy. The output line of the high-pressure absorber leads into a common line for carbon dioxide-laden solvent, which leads to desorption. At least one device, preferably at least one pump is arranged in the line path from the low-pressure absorber to the desorption device to increase the pressure of the loaded solvent. At least one device, preferably a pump is arranged downstream of the junction of the outlet line of the low pressure absorber in the output line of the high pressure absorber in the common line to the desorption, to increase the pressure of the loaded solvent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN JP2021178269-A
TI Gas recovery installation for recovering carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide, has regeneration apparatus that is configured to diffuse carbon dioxide from process liquid by pressure with respect to process liquid which absorbed carbon dioxide in absorber.

AE TAKUMA KK (TAKU-C)

GA 2021D0805D

A regeneration apparatus (20) is configured to diffuse a carbon dioxide from the process liquid by the pressure with respect to the process liquid which absorbed the carbon dioxide in the absorber, and for stripping using a stripping gas. A first collection gas discharge pipe and second collection gas discharge pipe are provided for emitting outside the carbon dioxide diffused from the process liquid. The state emits outside the carbon dioxide diffused from the process liquid with the pressure through collection discharge pipe. A switch unit switches the state which emits outside the carbon dioxide diffused from the process liquid by the stripping through second collection discharge pipe.
   USE - Gas recovery installation for recovering carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide such as biogas and combustion exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation improves cooling efficiency and heating efficiency by installing the heat-transfer jacket, and improves the diffusion efficiency. The recovery efficiency of carbon dioxide can be improved in combination with the stripping effect of the stripping gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The regeneration device includes a decompression tower that decompresses the treatment liquid that has absorbed the carbon dioxide to dissipate the carbon dioxide. A treatment liquid supply unit is configured for supplying the treatment liquid from which carbon dioxide is released from the decompression tower to the stripping tower. The liquid temperature adjusting unit is configured for adjusting the temperature of the treated liquid. The liquid temperature adjusting unit is provided with low-temperature treatment for adjusting the treatment liquid in the absorber. A heat exchanger (30) adjusts the temperature of the process liquid by temperature control of the stripping gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas recovery method for recovering carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon dioxide, involves allowing the treatment liquid to absorb carbon dioxide contained in the carbon dioxide-containing gas, and releasing carbon dioxide from the treatment liquid by reducing the pressure of the treatment liquid that has absorbed carbon dioxide and stripping using a stripping gas.

PN US2019329222-A1; TW657859-B1; WO2019211714-A1; CN110404399-A; TW201945074-A
TI Regenerating carbon dioxide absorbent by bringing used carbon dioxide absorbent in contact with carbon-containing dielectric material to form dielectric energy-susceptible combination and dielectric-heating to remove carbon dioxide.
AU CHEN C
   WANG C
   SHEN J
   CHEN Z
   WANG Z
AE UNIV NAT CHENG KUNG (UNCK-C)
   CHEN Z (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201991649T
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide absorbent is regenerated by (a) bringing used carbon dioxide absorbent in contact with carbon-containing dielectric material to form dielectric energy-susceptible combination; and (b) subjecting dielectric energy-susceptible combination to a dielectric heating to remove carbon dioxide from used carbon dioxide absorbent for regeneration.
   USE - Regeneration of carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - Dielectric heating, such as a microwave heating, is a relatively efficient heating treatment. As a result, the decomposition reaction for the used carbon dioxide absorbent is achieved in a relatively short time such that regeneration efficiency of the carbon dioxide absorbent can be improved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Dielectric heating is microwave heating. The method further includes dissolving regenerated carbon dioxide absorbent with a solvent to form a solution. The method further includes removing solvent from solution to purify the regenerated carbon dioxide absorbent. Step (a) is implemented by disposing the used carbon dioxide absorbent on the carbon-containing dielectric material. Used carbon dioxide absorbent is laid in a form of a layer having an average thickness of 0.2-0.5 cm. Preferred Compositions: Carbon-containing dielectric material is in a form of a sheet or powders. Carbon-containing dielectric material is selected from electroconductive carbon black sheet and/or silicon carbide sheet. Carbon-containing dielectric material is selected from electroconductive carbon black powders and/or silicon carbide powders. Used carbon dioxide absorbent is in an amount of 0.05-3 g/cm2 of the carbon-containing dielectric material. Weight ratio of used carbon dioxide absorbent to carbon-containing dielectric material is 10:1-5:1. Preferred Parameters: Microwave heating is implemented at a power ranging from 800 watts to 2000 watts for 10-80 s.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN KR2022101941-A
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent for generating            high-density thermal energy induced by carbon dioxide,            is prepared by dispersing nanoporous material in carbon            dioxide chemical absorbent, where carbon dioxide is            injected into colloidal material.
AU KANG Y T
   KIM S
   CHOI H
   LEE J
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202293836A
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprises colloidal                material prepared by dispersing nanoporous material                in carbon dioxide chemical absorbent. The carbon                dioxide in colloids is collected and thermal energy                is generated, when carbon dioxide is injected into                colloid material. The separated carbon dioxide is                recovered and re-injected when heat of temperature                is applied. The colloid comprises 5-7 wt.%                nanoporous material having pore size of 4-10 nm.                The nanoporous material includes metal-organic                framework, covalent organic framework or mesoporous                silica. The carbon dioxide chemical absorbent                includes monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, methyl                diethanolamine, aminoethyl ethanolamine,                2-amino-2-methyl-1-proponol, diethylenetriamine,                piperazine, sodium hydroxide or potassium                carbonate. The nanoporous material includes                (3-aminopropyl)trimethoxysilane,                (3-(2-aminoethylamino)propyl)trimethoxysilane,                3-(2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethylamino)propyl-trimethoxysilane                and/or polyetherimide.
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is useful for                generating high-density thermal energy induced by                carbon dioxide.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing methanol, involves (a)                capturing carbon dioxide by means of a carbon                dioxide sorbent and forming a loaded carbon dioxide                sorbent, (b) passing the loaded carbon dioxide                sorbent to a carbon dioxide stripping unit (A), (c)                providing a methanol synthesis gas comprising                hydrogen and carbon dioxide, (d) passing the                methanol synthesis gas from step (c) to a methanol                synthesis and forming a raw methanol product, and                (e) purifying the raw methanol product in a                distillation unit (B) comprising at least one                distillation column, obtaining a hot overhead                stream from at least one distillation column, where                the hot overhead stream provides heat to heat                exchanger (C) and at least part of the heat is                provided to at least one, carbon dioxide stripping                unit (A) for stripping carbon dioxide from loaded                carbon dioxide sorbent, and condensing stream to                liquid methanol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing methanol in                plant with carbon dioxide import from carbon                capture (claimed), preferably green methanol plant,                where the methanol is crude methanol and                distillation of methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces energy consumption,                provides reutilization, saves energy, recycles                energy, minimizes waste, reduces overall energy                consumption of green plant (re-use of distillation                energy to stabilizer column and carbon dioxide                reboiler in carbon capture unit) and reduces                electrical import for heater/boiler (or reduced                fuel consumption in steam generation).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                comprising at least one carbon dioxide stripping                unit (A), at least one methanol distillation unit                (B), at least one overhead heat exchanger unit (C)                fluidly connected to at least one heat exchanger                unit (D), where units A, B, C and D are arranged                such that a raw methanol product is purified in a                distillation unit (B) comprising at least one                distillation column, obtaining a hot overhead                stream from the at least one distillation column,                where the hot overhead stream provides heat to heat                exchanger (C) and at least portion of heat is                provided to at least one, carbon dioxide stripping                unit (A) for stripping carbon dioxide from loaded                carbon dioxide sorbent, and condensing stream to                liquid methanol.

The carbon dioxide in step (a) originates from                  flue gas and/or synthesis gas. Preferred                  Composition: The heat provided to at least one                  carbon dioxide stripping unit (A) is                  approximately 20-100%, preferably 45-100% energy                  requirements in stripping unit. Preferred                  Condition: The heat provided to carbon dioxide                  stripping unit (A) is additionally provided by                  steam. The portion or all hot overhead stream is                  supplied to district heating. The portion of hot                  overhead stream is supplied to stabilizing                  reboiler. Preferred Device: In system, the units                  (A), (B), (C) and (D) are arranged such that                  portion or all overhead heat stream is supplied                  to district heating. In system, the units (A),                  (B), (C) and (D) are arranged such that portion                  of overhead heat stream is supplied to the                  stabilizing reboiler.

PN JP6408082-B1; WO2019012873-A1; JP2019013906-A; CN110290850-A; CA3064029-A1; US2020001233-A1; IN202047004953-A; US10933371-B2; IN372331-B; CN110290850-B; CA3064029-C
TI Recovering and concentrating carbon dioxide gas by            sorbing carbon dioxide while contacting with mixed gas            containing carbon dioxide and evaporative cooling,            introducing water vapor into honeycomb which sorbs            carbon dioxide, and desorbing.
AU OKANO H
   HIROSHI O
AE SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
   NISHIBU GIKEN KK (NISH-Non-standard)
   SEIBU GIKEN KK (SEIB-Non-standard)
GA 201880746L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas is recovered and                concentrated by performing accommodation rotation                of honeycomb rotor (12) which carries                water-insoluble carbon-dioxide sorption material                particles which have sorption capability of carbon                dioxide gas into the casing which has sorption zone                (13) and desorption zone (14) and is sealed;                sorbing carbon dioxide while contacting with mixed                gas containing carbon dioxide and evaporative                cooling in sorption zone in the state with which                honeycomb is wet; introducing water vapor into                honeycomb which sorbs the carbon dioxide in the                desorption zone; and desorbing highly concentrated                carbon dioxide.
   USE - Recovery and concentration of a carbon dioxide                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The sorption heat which arises by sorption of                a carbon dioxide is removed with evaporative                cooling of the water on the surface of a honeycomb,                temperature rise of a honeycomb or source gas is                suppressed, and carbon dioxide gas can be sorbed                highly efficient. Durability of an amine sorption                material with low heat resistance can be improved.                The preprocessor of source gas is simple, a low dew                point dehumidification device is unnecessary, and                initial cost and operation cost are held down.                Regeneration effect of a honeycomb rotor and                service life can be improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                carbon-dioxide-gas recovery and concentration                apparatus comprising water-filling screen purge                zone provided at either or both of sorption zone                and desorption zone boundary with respect to                rotation direction of rotor comprising                water-insoluble sorption material which has the                adsorption capability of carbon dioxide gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                carbon-dioxide recovery and concentration                apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Carbon-dioxide sorption honeycomb rotor                (12)
   Sorption zone (13)
   Desorption zone (14)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Carbon dioxide is made                  to adsorb by contacting with mixed gas containing                  carbon dioxide of less than or equal to 100%                  relative humidity, and carrying out evaporative                  cooling in sorption zone in the state with which                  the honeycomb is wet and allowing water vapor                  pass in the desorption zone. The apparatus                  recovers wastewater from water-filling screen                  purge zone, and water-supplies to water-filling                  screen purge zone and/or steam generation tank                  again. Raw material gas is pretreated with water                  scrubber and desulfurization equipment, before                  throwing into the honeycomb rotor, by preheating                  with cold heat generated by waste heat                  utilization type absorption refrigerator or                  adsorption type freezer, and cooling and                  dehumidifying at not greater than 0 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: Water-insoluble                  carbon-dioxide sorption material is an amine ion                  exchange resin. Carbon-dioxide sorption material                  is a solid adsorbent which supports the amine.                  Rotor is a honey-comb rotor in which honeycomb                  has base material made of porous paper containing                  plastics fiber of an inorganic fiber main body                  supporting solid amine microparticles. Rotor is                  adhered with adsorbent particles obtained by                  attaching granular ion exchange resin or amine                  carbon-dioxide absorber to the front and back of                  sheet material of metal foil or synthetic resin                  sheet through heat-resistant and water-resistant                  adhesive agent, where rotor is a rotor in which                  the sheet is corrugated and further wound or                  laminated. Preferred Properties: Particle                  diameter of carbon-dioxide sorption material                  particle supported on the honeycomb rotor is less                  than or equal to 1 mm. Preferred Device: The                  apparatus has a pre-dry zone by an air blow                  provided with respect to rotation direction of                  rotor behind the water-filling screen purge zone                  between the sorption zone and the desorption                  zone.

AU LIU A
   LIU G
   YU J
AE PINGLUO XIANGMEI CHEM IND CO LTD (PING-Non-standard)

GA 202096900K
AB    NOVELTY - Reaction kettle comprises kettle body (1), and kettle cover (2) fixed on top of kettle body. A stirring mechanism is arranged inside kettle body. A first cooling medium heat exchange tube (3) is arranged in kettle body. A liquid carbon dioxide gasification tube (4) is arranged below first cooling medium heat exchange tube. The first cooling medium heat exchange tube and the liquid carbon dioxide gasification tube are both in coil structure. The inlet end of liquid carbon dioxide gasification pipe is connected with liquid carbon dioxide inlet (5). The outlet end of liquid carbon dioxide gasification pipe is connected with gas carbon dioxide outlet. The gas carbon dioxide outlet is connected with gas carbon dioxide inlet through connecting pipe. The gas carbon dioxide inlet is connected to bottom of kettle body. The gas carbon dioxide pipe is connected. The gas outlet of gas carbon dioxide pipe is located at bottom of kettle body.
   USE - The reaction kettle is used for cyanamide production.
   ADVANTAGE - The kettle has no additional heating needed to gasify liquid carbon dioxide, which saves energy and reduces circulating cooling water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of reaction kettle.
   Kettle body (1)
   Kettle cover (2)

   Liquid carbon dioxide gasification tube (4)

TI Method for measuring diffusion coefficient of            carbon dioxide in crude oil, involves determining            carbon dioxide diffusion coefficient, and obtaining            universal gas constant and balanced interface            concentration of carbon dioxide gas.
AU WANG H

   LANG D
   LUO M
   PAN W
   WANG R

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC PETROLEUM EXPLORATION & PRODN (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 2018925685
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling a sealed container                with crude oil at constant temperature. The sealed                container is filled with carbon dioxide gas at                constant temperature. Pressure of primary carbon                dioxide gas is measured at initial time period when                the primary carbon dioxide gas reaches equilibrium                pressure. A relationship between diffusion time t                and the pressure is determined by using expansion                coefficient. Carbon dioxide diffusion coefficient                is determined to obtain a compressibility factor of                the carbon dioxide gas. A universal gas constant                and balanced interface concentration of the carbon                dioxide gas is obtained at a rock core                section.
   USE - Method for measuring diffusion coefficient of                carbon dioxide in crude oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing account carbon                dioxide into crude oil crude oil volume expansion,                accurately measuring diffusion coefficient of                carbon dioxide in crude oil.

PT P
PN KR2022025315-A
TI Reaction device for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into liquid fuel, consists of inner peripheral surface of reaction space for storing catalyst, in which carbon dioxide and hydrogen come into contact with catalyst, react and convert into liquid fuel and water vapor.
AU CHOI C K
AE CHOI C K (CHOI-Individual)
GA 2022345616
AB    NOVELTY - A reaction device consists of an inner peripheral surface of a reaction space for storing a catalyst, comprising a heat medium conduit, a thermal balance tank and a cooler. Since the liquid phase and the gas phase coexist, the evaporation solution that maintains the isothermal section by latent heat is filled until the heating medium oil coil is sufficiently submerged in the solution. Then, the heat medium oil pipe and heat medium oil coil are connected as a pipe with a circulation pump interposed therebetween, and the heat medium flow path is filled inside the connected pipe line, and the filled heat medium oil is circulated by the circulation pump at high speed. When the upper cooler is operated while applying power to the electric heater at the bottom of the thermal balancing tank, the heat supplied to the lower heater and the heat absorbed by the upper cooler are balanced, thereby maintaining a constant pressure and temperature.
   USE - Reaction device used for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into liquid fuel and synthetic fuel gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The reaction device can economically and effectively convert carbon dioxide back into fuel by reacting with hydrogen without introducing carbon dioxide into the global environment. The temperature of the catalyst inside the reaction space is always maintained at the most suitable temperature for the reaction, so the temperature of the reaction transferred by carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the liquid fuel is controlled while simultaneously reacting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A reaction device consists of an inner peripheral surface of a reaction space for storing a catalyst, comprising a heat medium conduit, a thermal balance tank which forms an airtight space by placing an electric heater at the lower portion and a cooler having a constant amount of cooling heat at the upper portion, and discharges all of the non-condensed gas in the space. Since the liquid phase and the gas phase coexist, the evaporation solution that maintains the isothermal section by latent heat is filled until the heating medium oil coil is sufficiently submerged in the solution. Then, the heat medium oil pipe and heat medium oil coil are connected as a pipe with a circulation pump interposed therebetween, and the heat medium flow path is filled inside the connected pipe line, and the filled heat medium oil is circulated by the circulation pump at high speed. When the upper cooler is operated while applying power to the electric heater at the bottom of the thermal balancing tank, the heat supplied to the lower heater and the heat absorbed by the upper cooler are balanced, thereby maintaining a constant pressure and temperature. The maintained temperature is maintained at a temperature suitable for the reaction. When the raw material gas is passed through the heating medium oil pipe, the heat medium oil is made in a coil shape so that the heat of the heating medium oil can be sufficiently conducted to the raw material gas, and the raw material gas is passed through the coil type pipe line. When it flows into the reaction space filled with catalyst, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, which are raw material gases, come into contact with the catalyst at the most suitable temperature for the reaction to occur and react, so that it is converted into liquid fuel and water vapor, and heat of reaction is generated simultaneously. The generated reaction heat is absorbed into the circulating heat medium flow path to raise the internal temperature of the thermal balancing tank, thereby increasing the pressure, so that the power supplied to the heater decreases as much as the reaction heat. By controlling the reaction temperature and measuring the heat of reaction simultaneously, the reactor is provided for effectively carrying out the reaction of converting carbon dioxide back into fuel without inducing carbon dioxide to the global environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The device consists of a cooling source which is refrigerant compressor and an air-cooled condenser, and comprising static pressure expansion valve and a compressor suction refrigerant temperature controller to maintain a constant discharge pressure even when the atmospheric temperature changes, so that the refrigerant flow rate flowing into the heat medium oil coil is kept constant and Inlet temperature into the cooling coil. The static pressure expansion valve controls the flow rate flowing into the evaporator so that the outlet temperature maintains the same value and the suction refrigerant gas temperature that maintains the refrigerant vapor sucked into the refrigerant compressor in an overheated state at a constant temperature controller. The cooling device that always has a constant amount of cooling heat despite changes in the temperature of the atmosphere. The thermal equilibrium tank consists of an electric heater at the bottom of an airtight container, and a cooler at the top, When all of the non-condensable gas inside is removed and the evaporation solution is filled with the heating medium oil coil sufficiently immersed in the solution, and heating and cooling are performed simultaneously, the pressure is controlled according to the amount of power supplied to the heater. The temperature rises in proportion to the pressure. So, if the rising temperature is maintained at the best reaction temperature, when the reaction does not occur, the reaction vessel, catalyst, and the thermal equilibrium tank that allows the raw material gas to rise to a temperature suitable for the reaction, and when the reaction occurs, absorbs and cools the reaction heat and measures the amount of heat generated by the reaction, so that the reaction effect of the catalyst can be known by the measured reaction heat. In the reaction apparatus, by adjusting the ratio of the diameters of the cross-sectional circles of the reaction space catalyst layer and heat medium oil layer, the temperature rise caused by the heat of reaction is alleviated. Because it prevents the occurrence of local hot spots and prevents the catalyst from being damaged, the reactor effectively carries out the reaction of converting carbon dioxide back into fuel without leaving the earth environment. The reaction device configured to control the temperature raised by the reaction heat generated due to the reaction by the ratio of the diameter of the cross-sectional circle of the reaction space layer to the diameter of the cross-sectional circle of the heat medium oil layer.

TI Cracking hydrocarbons comprises producing heated outlet gas in combustion chamber from combustion of hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas and generating mixture of heated outlet gas with hydrocarbon feed.
AU FEMANDES D P
   BUSS R
   CHEN D H
   FERNANDES D P
AE UNIV LAMAR (UYLA-Non-standard)
GA 2022183963
AB    NOVELTY - Cracking (M1) hydrocarbons, comprises: producing a heated outlet gas in a combustion chamber from combustion of a hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with an oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas, where the hydrocarbon fuel source is burned in the combustion chamber to produce the heated outlet gas comprising carbon dioxide and water; generating a mixture of the heated outlet gas with a hydrocarbon feed and steam in a cracking system; heating the mixture in the cracking system to produce an ethylene and/or propylene containing stream and a separate water/carbon dioxide stream; separating the water from the carbon dioxide in the water/carbon dioxide stream; and recycling at least a portion of the separated carbon dioxide back to the combustion chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for cracking hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides current steam cracking furnaces all emit carbon dioxide and nitric oxide to the atmosphere; recycles carbon dioxide furnaces are operated at the same pressure of cracking which takes place in the coils of the cracker; utilizes pure oxygen as the oxidizer in furnaces results in no nitrogen oxides emission and the net carbon dioxide exported is of high-purity pipeline grade; circulates carbon dioxide concept to ethane/propane cracking furnaces to produce ethylene/propylene reduces carbon dioxide emissions; integrates furnace exhaust heat for preheating, steam generation, or power generation (running a turbine) reduces energy consumption of the cracking system; utilizes minimum natural gas (NG) consumption, the cracking system will have the lowest carbon footprint; circulates carbon dioxide concept can be extended to steam boiler to generate steam with carbon dioxide production for enhanced oil recovery or other carbon dioxide utilization purposes (e.g. urea, baking soda, or polycarbonate); and facilities-wide carbon dioxide pipeline network to deliver carbon dioxide as working gas for major combustion units furnaces, boilers, and heaters without greenhouse and nitrogen oxides emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (a) system (S1) for cracking hydrocarbons, comprising a combustion chamber configured to produce a heated outlet gas from combustion of a hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with an oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas, where the heated outlet gas includes carbon dioxide and water, a cracking system configured to heat a mixture of the heated outlet gas with a hydrocarbon feed and steam to produce an ethylene and/or propylene containing stream and a separate water/carbon dioxide stream, a separator system configured to separate the water from the carbon dioxide in the water/carbon dioxide stream and a recycling stream exiting the separator system, the recycling stream configured to recycle at least a portion of the separated carbon dioxide back to the combustion chamber;
   (b) heat treating (M2) hydrocarbons, comprising providing a hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with an oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas to a boiler, where the hydrocarbon fuel source is burned in the boiler to produce a heated outlet gas comprising carbon dioxide and water, separating the water from the carbon dioxide in the heated outlet gas and recycling at least a portion of the separated carbon dioxide back to the boiler; and
   (c) system (S2) for cracking hydrocarbons, comprising: a boiler configured to produce a heated outlet gas from combustion of a hydrocarbon fuel source mixed with an oxidant source and carbon dioxide gas, where the heated outlet gas includes carbon dioxide and water; a separator system configured to separate the water from the carbon dioxide in the heated outlet gas; and a recycling stream exiting the separator system, the recycling stream configured to recycle at least a portion of the separated carbon dioxide back to the boiler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises: heating the recycled portion of the separated carbon dioxide stream with heat from the water/carbon dioxide stream exiting the cracking system; heating at least a portion of hydrocarbon feed with heat from the water/carbon dioxide stream exiting the cracking system; and providing the water separated from the water/carbon dioxide stream to at least one heat exchangers to generate steam. The heat for the heat exchangers is provided from the water/carbon dioxide stream exiting the cracking system. The generated steam is used to generate electricity. The method (M2) further comprises: heating the recycled portion of the separated carbon dioxide stream with heat from the heated outlet gas exiting the boiler; heating at least a portion of the oxidant source with heat from the heated outlet gas exiting the boiler; providing the water separated from the water/carbon dioxide stream back to the boiler, and heating the water with heat generated in the boiler to produce high pressure steam; and superheating the high pressure steam with heat from the heated outlet gas exiting the boiler.Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon fuel includes natural gas. The system further comprises a heat exchanger coupled to the recycling stream, the heat exchanger being configured to heat at least a portion of hydrocarbon feed with heat from the water/carbon dioxide stream exiting the cracking system. The system (S1) further comprises heat exchangers configured to generate steam coupled to cracking system. The separator system includes mol sieve beds configured to remove water from the water/carbon dioxide stream at elevated temperatures. The hydrocarbon fuel source includes natural gas.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

AU KHAN P A

   HAN P A
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 201874180W
AB    NOVELTY - Co-producing methanol, ammonia and urea from                hydrocarbon feedstock, comprises: (a) from first                methanol process, having first reforming step (20)                and first methanol conversion step obtaining first                effluent of methanol and first gas effluent of                hydrogen, nitrogen and unconverted carbon oxides,                and from second methanol processes, having second                reforming step and second methanol conversion step                obtaining second effluent having methanol and                second gas effluent of hydrogen, nitrogen and                unconverted carbon oxides; (b) producing ammonia                synthesis gas from first and/or second gas effluent                and withdrawing ammonia synthesis gas having                hydrogen:nitrogen molar ratio of 3:1; (c)                catalytically converting nitrogen and hydrogen of                ammonia synthesis gas and withdrawing effluent                having ammonia and purge-gas stream of hydrogen,                nitrogen and/or methane; and (d) reacting part of                effluent having ammonia with part of flue gas                having carbon dioxide from one of first and second                reforming steps to produce urea.
   USE - The process is useful for co-producing                methanol, ammonia and urea from a hydrocarbon                feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides the co-production of                methanol and ammonia together with urea production                from a hydrocarbon feed without venting to the                atmosphere carbon dioxide captured from the                methanol or ammonia synthesis gas and without using                expensive air separation units and water gas shift;                provides the carbon dioxide removal from flue gas                from reforming section to convert partially or                fully all ammonia into urea; is sequential and                once-through (single pass) process for the                co-production of methanol and ammonia without water                gas shift and carbon dioxide removal and without                air separation unit used in the reforming section                of the plant; allows the optimized use of two                parallel methanol production lines; utilizes the                first and second methanol steps that minimize any                flaring of synthesis gas to optimize the energy                consumption, thus minimizing the operational                expenditure and/or maximizing the use of highly                expensive reforming section by utilizing the                margins in compressor design and voluntarily                different deactivation cycle in the first and                second methanol step; is environmentally friendly                due no emissions to the surroundings of the carbon                dioxide captured from the methanol and ammonia                synthesis gas and all carbon monoxide (and carbon                dioxide) produced for methanol synthesis; is                applicable for any plant capacity including large                plants producing more than 2000 MTPD ammonia and                methanol for 3000, 5000 MTPD or even more; and                utilizes the single reactor rather than two                conventional boiling water reactors, thus                significantly reducing plant costs and the                operating pressure in the methanol synthesis stage                can be kept as high as 120 bars or even higher that                are significant savings in terms of equipment size                and overall investment costs as methanol synthesis                favored at high pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Co-producing methanol, ammonia and urea from a                hydrocarbon feedstock, comprises: (a) from a first                methanol process, comprising a first reforming step                (20) and first methanol conversion step obtaining a                first effluent comprising methanol and a first gas                effluent comprising hydrogen, nitrogen and                unconverted carbon oxides, and from a second                methanol processes, comprising a second reforming                step and a second methanol conversion step                obtaining a second effluent comprising methanol and                a second gas effluent comprising hydrogen, nitrogen                and unconverted carbon oxides; (b) producing an                ammonia synthesis gas from the first and/or second                gas effluent in a common catalytic methanation                stage and withdrawing the ammonia synthesis gas                preferably having a hydrogen:nitrogen molar ratio                of 3:1; (c) catalytically converting the nitrogen                and hydrogen of the ammonia synthesis gas in a                common ammonia synthesis stage and withdrawing an                effluent comprising ammonia and a purge-gas stream                comprising hydrogen, nitrogen and/or methane; and                (d) reacting at least part of the effluent                comprising ammonia with at least part of a flue gas                comprising carbon dioxide from at least one of the                first and second reforming steps to produce urea.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a plant                comprising a first and a second parallel methanol                process lines, at least one common methanation                reactor and at least one common ammonia synthesis                step.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic block diagram of                the process.
   Natural gas (1)
   Steam (2)
   Air (3)
   Methanol synthesis gas (4)
   First reforming step (20)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The plant further                  comprises an interconnecting synthesis line                  allowing fluid communication between the first                  and second methanol process line; a urea                  production section; a formaldehyde production                  section; and a UFC-85 production section. The                  plant is arranged to carry out the process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In each of the first and                  second methanol processes the following steps are                  carried out producing a methanol synthesis gas                  (4) containing hydrogen, carbon oxides and                  nitrogen by steam (2) reforming the hydrocarbon                  feedstock in a reforming section comprising a                  primary and a secondary reforming stage, and                  catalytically converting the carbon oxides and                  hydrogen of the methanol synthesis gas in a                  once-through methanol synthesis stage and                  withdrawing an effluent comprising methanol and a                  gas effluent comprising nitrogen, hydrogen and                  unconverted carbon oxides. The secondary                  reforming stage is an air (3)-blown secondary                  reforming stage. The hydrocarbon feedstock is                  natural gas (1) or substitute natural gas or a                  gas comprising greater than 80% methane. The                  methanol conversion of the first and second                  once-through methanol synthesis stage of the                  first and the second methanol processes are                  controlled at least by controlling the quantity                  of syngas from the first and/or second reforming                  step fed to the first and/or second methanol                  production stage. The methanol synthesis is                  conducted by passing the synthesis gas through at                  least one boiling water reactor and subsequently                  through at least one adiabatic fixed bed reactor.                  The methanol synthesis is conducted by passing                  the synthesis gas through at least one boiling                  water reactor and subsequently through at least                  one gas cooled reactor. The boiling water reactor                  is in the form of a single reactor of the                  condensing-methanol type which comprises within a                  common shell a fixed bed of methanol catalyst                  particles and cooling means adapted to indirectly                  cooling the methanol synthesis gas with a cooling                  agent. The process further comprises cooling the                  synthesis gas withdrawn from each methanol                  reactor to condense methanol and passing the gas                  through a separator, withdrawing a bottom                  fraction from the separator containing the raw                  methanol, withdrawing an overhead fraction                  containing synthesis gas which is passed to the                  subsequent methanol reactor, and forming a single                  liquid effluent containing methanol by combining                  the bottom fractions of the separators of each                  reactor containing the raw methanol. The process                  further comprises withdrawing a hydrogen-rich                  stream from the purge-gas stream comprising                  hydrogen, nitrogen and methane and adding this                  stream to step (a). The process further comprises                  withdrawing an off gas from the purge-gas stream                  comprising hydrogen, nitrogen and methane and                  adding this stream to step (a) as fuel. The                  process comprises further parallel methanol                  processes. At least a part of the methanol from                  the first and/or second methanol process is                  converted into formaldehyde. The formaldehyde is                  absorbed into aqueous urea solution based on at                  least part of the urea to produce urea                  formaldehyde concentrate (UFC-85). The UFC-85 is                  added to the urea as a coating.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Ensuring supply of electricity and carbon dioxide at field work site comprises providing electricity using methanol or alcohol-water generators, and purifying mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to high-purity gas and liquefying.
AU CHEN Y
   LIU A
   HAN D
   SHI H
   WANG J
   FAN H

   YAN J
   SHEN G

AE SHANGHAI SUISHAN IND CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   STATE GRID SHANGHAI COMPREHENSIVE ENERGY (SGCC-C)
   SUISHAN NINGBO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUIS-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI HERONG ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202194552F
AB    NOVELTY - Ensuring supply of electricity and carbon dioxide at field work site comprises providing electricity using methanol or alcohol-water generators, where the exhaust gas produced by the generator in generation of electricity is a mixture of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, oxygen, and moisture, and the carbon dioxide content is about 13%, using carbon dioxide adsorbent based on pressure swing adsorption method, purifying the mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to high-purity carbon dioxide gas and liquefy it under pressure; where the carbon dioxide content is 70-90%; and the temperature is 10-20 degrees C, liquefying carbon dioxide in mixed gas under pressure of 3-6.5 MPa, sending liquid carbon dioxide to storage tank for standby, purifying carbon dioxide gas and liquefy the purified product gas using non-condensable gases mainly nitrogen and oxygen, which are directly discharged to the outside; and outputting surplus power required power from the generator.
   USE - The method is useful for ensuring field power and carbon dioxide supply in field operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes sources, easily available raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for ensuring field power and carbon dioxide supply in field operation
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide adsorbent is 13X or other modified adsorbent with balanced adsorption characteristic. The workflow comprises (i) fully charging battery pack in advance to provide initial power for control and power management module; (ii) adding methanol, injecting water, generator starts, outputting power under action of the control and power management module, and dischargiung mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, (iii) dividing generator output into two ways: one way outputs electricity, which is charged and discharged under the action of the control and power management module, and provides electricity to the inside and outside; the other way outputs the mixed gas containing carbon dioxide to send the carbon dioxide purification device to control and power Extract the carbon dioxide mixture with higher purity under the action of the management module; (iv) charging generator is first fully with the battery pack, and accepting role of control and power management module to output power to satisfy the power demand of the device itself, introducing surplus power to other electrical loads on site; (v) extracting higher-purity carbon dioxide mixed gas by carbon dioxide purification device under the action of the control and power management module is send to the pressurization/refrigeration liquefaction module, completing liquefaction, and sending it to the carbon dioxide storage device for standby to satisfy the on-site carbon dioxide demand the non-condensable gases of the pressurization/refrigeration liquefaction module include nitrogen, oxygen, moisture, etc., drain directly.

PN KR2021133414-A; KR2410689-B1
TI Carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system comprises fuel cell unit for receiving hydrogen and natural gas from liquid hydrogen storage unit to generate electricity and carbon dioxide collecting unit collects carbon dioxide.
AU CHANG D
   PARK H J
   JE S H
AE KOREA HYDRO & NUCLEAR POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2021C5273K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system comprises fuel cell unit (20) for receiving hydrogen and natural gas from a liquid hydrogen storage unit (10) to generate electricity. A carbon dioxide collecting unit (30) collects carbon dioxide generated from the cell unit. The collecting unit receives cooling heat of liquid hydrogen discharged from the storage unit and liquefies the carbon dioxide to collect carbon dioxide. A water electrolysis unit produces hydrogen by receiving electric power produced by a renewable energy generation unit. The fuel cell is provided with multiple unit fuel cells that are supplied with natural gas and hydrogen.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide generated from a fuel cell is captured by using the cooling heat of liquid hydrogen. The energy consumption required for the carbon dioxide liquefaction process is reduced by utilizing liquid hydrogen cooling and heat, and the energy efficiency of the entire system is greatly improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system involves:
   (A) receiving natural gas and hydrogen and sending carbon dioxide generated as fuel cell unit generates electric power to the carbon dioxide capture unit;
   (B) supplying liquid hydrogen from the liquid hydrogen storage unit to the carbon dioxide collecting unit; and
   (C) capturing the carbon dioxide by using the cooling heat of the liquid hydrogen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system (The drawing include non-English language text)
   Liquid hydrogen storage unit (10)
   Fuel cell unit (20)
   Carbon dioxide collecting unit (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method of operating the carbon dioxide capture type energy generation system involves generating electricity using a renewable energy generation system; receiving the generated power and producing hydrogen from a water electrolysis system; receiving the hydrogen and storing it as liquid hydrogen, where the producing of hydrogen is when the electric power produced by the renewable energy system exceeds a preset reference value by supplying the electric power to the water electrolysis system to generate hydrogen. The stored liquefied hydrogen is stored in the liquefied hydrogen storage unit, when the external power demand exceeds a preset reference value after supplying the liquefied hydrogen stored in the liquefied hydrogen storage unit to the carbon dioxide collecting unit. The method further involves vaporizing and supplying to the fuel cell.

PN KR2020079686-A; KR2155236-B1
TI Carbon dioxide absorbent comprising diamine compound useful for separating carbon dioxide.
AU YI H J
   LEE W
   KIM J
   LLEHEEWON
   NA J G
AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 202062394N
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprising diamine compound (I).
   USE - The carbon dioxide absorbent is useful for separating carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorbent has high carbon dioxide absorption rate, low stripping temperature, low renewable energy and high stability, does not form precipitate by reacting with carbon dioxide, can be used by diluting water at high concentration by dissolving in water even when combined with carbon dioxide while reducing total energy required compared to conventional water-based absorbents, and exhibits excellent reproducibility and high economic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide absorbent comprising diamine compound of formula (I).
   n = 0-10;
   R1, R2 = 1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, 1-30C heterocycloalkyl, 6-30C aryl or 1-30C heteroaryl (all are optionally substituted), or amine group (-NR4R5);
   R4, R5 = optionally substituted 1-30C alkyl or 6-30C aryl, or H; and
   R3 = 1-30C alkyl, 3-30C cycloalkyl, 1-30C heterocycloalkyl, 6-30C aryl or 1-30C heteroaryl (all are optionally substituted), or H.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separating carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide absorbent containing (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide absorbent is aqueous solution. The aqueous solution comprises 20-80 wt.% (I). Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) absorbing carbon dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide by using aqueous solution containing (I) as carbon dioxide absorber, and (b) removing the carbon dioxide absorbed by the carbon dioxide absorbent. The step (a) is carried out at 20-60 degrees C under pressure of 1-20 atm. In step (a), the ratio (L/G) of the carbon dioxide absorbent to the gas mixture containing carbon dioxide is 2-7 L/Nm3. The step (b) is carried out at 80-100 degrees C when removing carbon dioxide under pressure of 1 atm. The method further comprises performing re-use step of performing steps (a) and (b) using carbon dioxide-absorbed and regenerated carbon dioxide absorber after step (b). The reuse step is repeated multiple times.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R1-R3 = methyl, ethyl, n-propyl, iso-propyl, n-butyl, iso-butyl, or t-butyl.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
