PN CN111363197-A
TI Supercritical fluid recovery of thermosetting fiber composite material by crushing waste products made of thermosetting fiber composite materials into granules, adding to supercritical fluid reactor, and then adding carbon dioxide to react.
AU YIN A
   WANG H
   ZHANG M
   JIN X
   CAO F
   XU W
   DONG L
   JIA J
   YANG C
AE THERMAL POWER GENERATION TECHNOLOGY RES (THER-Non-standard)
GA 202063637G
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical fluid recovery of thermosetting fiber composite material involves: (A) crushing waste products made of thermosetting fiber composite materials into granules to obtain thermosetting fiber composite material particles; (B) adding the thermosetting fiber composite material particles obtained in step (A) to a supercritical fluid reactor; (C) opening the vent on the top of the reactor, and inputting carbon dioxide from a lower portion of the reactor until the reactor is full of carbon dioxide; (D) adding ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol to the reactor, and soaking the thermosetting fiber composite material particles in the reactor in ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol; (E) increasing the temperature of the particles in the reactor to 280-350 degrees C, increasing the pressure in the reactor to 7-10 MPa, heat preserving for 60-100 minutes, swelling the epoxy resin and unsaturated polyester resin in the thermosetting fiber composite material particles.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical fluid recovery of thermosetting fiber composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can significantly increase the recovery rate of fibers, preserve fiber performance and reduce resource consumption; has no secondary pollution, nearly zero emissions in the whole system and low reaction conditions; and is easy to control the reaction. The solid, liquid, and gas products collected after reaction can be reused to realize the harmless treatment of wastes and the high-value reuse of materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical fluid recovery of thermosetting fiber composite material involves: (A) crushing waste products made of thermosetting fiber composite materials into granules to obtain thermosetting fiber composite material particles; (B) adding the thermosetting fiber composite material particles obtained in step (A) to a supercritical fluid reactor; (C) opening the vent on the top of the reactor, and inputting carbon dioxide from a lower portion of the reactor until the reactor is full of carbon dioxide; (D) adding ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol to the reactor, and soaking the thermosetting fiber composite material particles in the supercritical fluid reactor in ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol; (E) increasing the temperature of the particles in the reactor to 280-350 degrees C, increasing the pressure in the reactor to 7-10 MPa, heat preserving for 60-100 minutes, swelling the epoxy resin and unsaturated polyester resin in the thermosetting fiber composite particles under the combined action of supercritical carbon dioxide and ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol, and then decomposing the resin in the particles; (F) separating and recovering carbon dioxide and gaseous and liquid organic matter in the reactor, where the gaseous and liquid organic matter includes benzene and its derivative, and phenol and its derivative; and (G) purging down from an upper portion of the reactor, removing the glass fiber and/or carbon fiber adhering to the inner wall of the reactor, discharging the glass fibers and/or carbon fibers from the lower portion of the reactor and recovering.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (F) further involves recycling the heat of ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol and gaseous and liquid organic matter in the reactor with a heat exchanger for heating the thermosetting fiber composite particles, carbon dioxide, ethanol, n-propanol, or n-butanol to enter the reactor. Preferred Conditions: The feeding time of carbon dioxide from the lower portion of the reactor in step (C) is 20-50 seconds, preferably 30 seconds. The temperature of the thermosetting fiber composite material particles in the reactor in step (E) is increased to 290C-330 degrees C, preferably 300 degrees C. The pressure in the reactor is increased to 7.5-9.5 MPa, preferably 8 MPa, and the heat preserving time is 70-90 minutes, preferably 80 minutes. Preferred Components: The weight of the thermosetting fiber composite material particles in step (A) is less than 5 g, preferably less than 2 g. The amount of the thermosetting fiber composite material particles added to the supercritical fluid reactor in step (B) is not greater than 40% of the internal volume of the reactor. In step (D), the amount of ethanol, n-propanol or n-butanol added to the reactor is not greater than 40% of the internal volume of the reactor.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); 
MC A05-A01C; A05-D01; A07-A03B; A07-A03D; A08-R03A; A11-B09C; A12-S08B; A12-S08C; F03-D; F03-E02

The dust absorption tank is provided with a second exhaust pipe, one side of the second exhaust pipe is provided with a gas drying tank; and one side of the gas drying tank is provided with a second air inlet pipe.    ADVANTAGE - The device can produce final carbon dioxide product with high purity, and reduce the emission by collecting the air carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises a bonnet (1), a first air inlet pipe (2), an air pump (3), a first exhaust pipe (4), a filter (5); the bonnet is set with the first air inlet pipe, and one side of the first gas inlet pipe is provided with the air pump. The one side of the air pump is provided with the first exhaust pipe, one side of the first exhaust pipe is provided with the filter; and one side of the filter is provided with a side dust absorbing tank. The dust absorption tank is provided with a second exhaust pipe, one side of the second exhaust pipe is provided with a gas drying tank; and one side of the gas drying tank is provided with a second air inlet pipe. The one side of the second air inlet pipe is provided with a storage tank, one side of the air storage tank is provided with a waste gas pipe, and the exhaust pipe is provided with a gas detector. The one side of the gas detector is provided with an electromagnetic valve switch, the lower part of the exhaust pipe 0620is provided with a carbon dioxide outlet pipe, and the carbon dioxide outlet pipe is provided with a flow meter.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device useful for capturing carbon dioxide in cement production.
   Bonnet (1)
   First air inlet pipe (2)

   Filter (5)

The air pump connected with the first exhaust pipe through a pipe, and the first exhaust pipe and the filter are connected by a bolt. The first exhaust pipe is welded with the dust absorbing pot, and the dust absorption tank is welded with the second exhaust pipe. The second exhaust pipe is connected through a pipe to the gas drying tank, and the gas drying tank is connected through pipe with the second air inlet pipe. The second air inlet pipe connected with the gas storage tank through the pipe fitting, and the gas storage tank is welded with the exhaust pipe. PN CN108178125-A; CN108178125-B
TI Preparing two-phase absorbent useful for removing carbon dioxide from a bio hydrogen ethane gas, comprises mixing chain based absorbent ethylenediamine and an organic solvent, heating and recycling.
AU CHENG J
   ZHU J
   LIU J
   CEN K
   WANG Z
   YANG W
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2018503705
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing two-phase absorbent comprises e.g. (i) mixing chain based absorbent ethylenediamine and an organic solvent N, N-dimethylacetamide; (ii) spraying ethylenediamine and N, N-dimethylacetamide absorber liquid two-phase absorbent into decarbonization reactor and reacting; (iii) filtering solid precipitate of ethylenediamine-aminoformate, heating at 110-120 degrees C, removing gas phase product formed, recovering droplets formed by the condensation of ethylenediamine vapor in the gas phase product, collecting carbon dioxide gas and sending into gas storage tank; and (iv) sending the liquid N,N-dimethylacetamide solvent separated by the solid-liquid two-phase mixture into the step (i) for recycling, mixing liquid phase product N,N-dimethylacetamide formed by the thermal decomposition of the solid precipitate with the condensed droplet N,N-dimethylacetamide recovered by the condensing device, and sending into the step (i) for recycling.
   USE - The two-phase absorbent is useful for removing carbon dioxide from a bio hydrogen ethane gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves rate of carbon dioxide removal by the two-phase absorbent at 15-29% higher than that of the traditional alcohol amine aqueous solution ethylenediamine -water, shortens absorption equilibrium time by 8-20%, and increases carbon dioxide absorption capacity by 10-21%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing two-phase absorbent comprises (i) mixing chain based absorbent ethylenediamine and an organic solvent N, N-dimethylacetamide at mass ratio 1:50-1: 30 by mechanical stirring at room temperature for 5-15 minutes to obtain a homogeneous mixed liquid two-phase ethylenediamine and N, N-dimethylacetamide absorber; (ii) spraying ethylenediamine and N, N-dimethylacetamide absorber liquid two-phase absorbent into decarbonization reactor using high-pressure pump, and spraying atomized droplets downwards through nozzle to perform countercurrent mixing with bottom-up biohydrogen gas, dissolving carbon dioxide in the biohydrogen gas in the organic solvent N,N-dimethylacetamide, and reacting chemically with the chain amine absorbent ethylenediamine to form a solid precipitate of ethylenediamine-aminoformate; (iii) filtering solid precipitate of ethylenediamine-aminoformate at the bottom of the spray decarbonization reactor from the liquid N,N-dimethylacetamide solvent by a suction filtration device, transferring solid precipitate obtained by suction filtration into the regeneration tower, heating at 110-120 degrees C, removing gas phase product formed by the thermal decomposition of N,N-dimethylacetamide-aminoformate by a condensing device, recovering droplets formed by the condensation of ethylenediamine vapor in the gas phase product, collecting carbon dioxide gas having a purity of greater than or equal to 99% discharged from the condensing device and sending into gas storage tank; and (iv) sending the liquid N,N-dimethylacetamide solvent separated by the solid-liquid two-phase mixture into the step (i) for recycling, mixing liquid phase product N,N-dimethylacetamide formed by the thermal decomposition of the solid precipitate with the condensed droplet N,N-dimethylacetamide recovered by the condensing device, and sending into the step (i) for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The theoretical absorption carbon dioxide capacity of ethylenediamine (EDA) in step (i) is 1 mol carbon dioxide /mole. The biological hydrogen alkane gas in step (ii) is a mixed gas produced by two-phase fermentation of biomass waste comprising 5-21 wt.% hydrogen, 35-63 wt.% methane, 30-50 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.2-1 wt.% hydrogen sulfide and 0.2-1 wt.% water.

PN JP6906111-B1; WO2021149281-A1; WO2021149284-A1; JP2020539303-X; JP2020542346-X
TI Device for fixing carbon-dioxide, has diluted portion that dilutes concentration of sodium hydroxide such that calcium carbonate is produced when reagent supply portion supplies calcium chloride to carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid after dilution.
AU SORIMACHI K
   KENJI S
AE SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
   SHINKO IND CO LTD (KOBM-C)
   KENJI S (KENJ-Individual)
   SHINKO SANGYO CO LTD (SHIA-C)
   SORIMACHI K (SORI-Individual)
GA 202187384D
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid preparation portions and carbon-dioxide fixing portion which are included. The carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion makes the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid and the carbon-dioxide containing gas contact and produces the sodium hydrogencarbonate and sodium carbonate. The diluted portion dilutes the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid after a contact with the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid and the carbon-dioxide containing gas to 0.2 N or less. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide is a concentration which does not add up a portion for a precipitation generation, and the neutralization consumed amount. A calcium carbonate is produced when the reagent supply portion supplies calcium chloride to the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid after the dilution.
   USE - Device for fixing carbon-dioxide for energy circulating-type electric power generating apparatus (claimed) used for thermal power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The new fixing device of a carbon dioxide is provided. The carbon dioxide is fixed in a more stable state. The amount of calcium carbonate synthesized by the contact is reduced. The concentration is reduced and generation of harmful gas is suppressed. Formation of a calcium hydroxide is suppressed and more carbon dioxides are fixed. The fixing device is very useful in a process of combustion exhaust gas containing a carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid preparation portions contain a seawater supply portion and the electrolysis portion. The seawater supply portion supplies seawater to the electrolysis portion. The seawater contains sodium chloride and calcium chloride. The electrolysis portion prepares the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride by electrolyzing the seawater. The carbon-dioxide fixing portion contains a gas supply portion, diluted portion, and carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion and a reagent supply portion. The gas supply portion supplies carbon-di-oxide containing gas to the carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion. The carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid preparation portions, a concentration adjustment portion, a reagent supply portion, and carbon-dioxide fixing portion are included. The carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid preparation portions contain a seawater supply portion and the electrolysis portion. The seawater supply portion supplies seawater to the electrolysis portion. The seawater contains sodium chloride and calcium chloride below 0.05 mol/L. The electrolysis portion prepares the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid containing sodium hydroxide and calcium chloride by electrolyzing the seawater. The concentration adjustment portion dilutes the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the carbondioxide fixing-reagent liquid to 0.2 N or less, and the concentration of the sodium hydroxide is a concentration which does not add up a portion for a precipitation generation, and the neutralization consumed amount. The reagent supply portion makes the concentration of calcium chloride in the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid 0.05 mol/L or more by supplying calcium chloride to the carbondioxide fixing-reagent liquid after the dilution. The carbon-dioxide fixing portion contains a gas supply portion and a carbondioxide-fixation reaction portion. The gas supply portion supplies carbondioxide containing gas to the carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion. The carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion makes the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid and the carbon-dioxide containing gas after supply of the calcium chloride contact, and fixes the carbon dioxide. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an energy circulating-type electric power generating apparatus has an electric power generation portion and carbon-dioxide fixing portion which are included. The first electric power generation portion contains a combustion portion, a generator, and a gas exhaustion portion. The combustion portion burns a fuel and generates a heat energy. The first electric power generation portion converts the heat energy into a driving force, and drives and electricpower-generates the generator. The gas exhaustion portion exhausts the carbon-dioxide containing gas which arises by combustion. The electrolysis portion of the carbon-dioxide fixing device electrolyzes the seawater using electricity electric-power-generated by the electric power generation portion, and prepares the reagent solution. The gas supply portion of the carbon-dioxide fixing device supplies the carbon-dioxide containing gas that the gas exhaustion portion exhausts to the carbon-dioxide-fixation reaction portion of the carbon-dioxide fixing device;
   (2) a carbon-dioxide fixing method involves diluting the concentration of sodium hydroxide in the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid after a contact with the carbon-dioxide fixing-reagent liquid and the carbon-dioxide containing gas to 0.2 N or less. The concentration of the sodium hydroxide is a concentration which does not add up a portion for a precipitation generation, and the neutralization consumed amount. A calcium carbonate is produced when the reagent supply process supplies calcium chloride to the carbondioxide fixing-reagent liquid after the dilution; and
   (3) an energy circulating-type electric power generation method involves performing the electric power generation process includes a further more nearly second electric power generation process. The second electric power generation process is an electric power generation process by a hydrogen fuel battery. The hydrogen fuel battery is electric-power-generated using the hydrogen generated by electrolysis of the seawater by the electrolysis process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view showing the carbon dioxide fixing device. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Reaction chamber (11)
   Electrolysis chamber (12)
   Liquid extraction portion (50)
   Filter (51)

AE KRONES AG (KROE-C)

   USE - The plant is useful for carrying out alcoholic fermentation and treatment of carbon dioxide produced during alcoholic fermentation (claimed).

PT P
PN CN113011640-A; CN113011640-B
TI Method for quickly calculating urban aviation carbon dioxide emissions, involves obtaining total aviation carbon dioxide emission, adding total aviation carbon dioxide emission in airport of city to obtain aviation carbon dioxide emission.
AU LV C
   CAI B
   XU S
   CAO L
AE MIN ECOLOGICAL ENVIRONMENT ENVIRONMENTAL (ECOL-Non-standard)
GA 202174338N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a matching function relationship between aircraft type parameters and flight information based on a pre-established flight information database and aircraft type parameter database. The total carbon dioxide emissions of the departure airport and total carbon dioxide emissions of the landing airport of all flight information in a certain airport in the city are classified and aggregated according to pre-set accounting period within accounting period. The total emissions from the departure airport and the total emissions from the landing airport are obtained and added together to obtain the total aviation carbon dioxide emissions in a certain airport in the city. The above process are repeated to obtain the total aviation carbon dioxide emissions in each airport of the city in the accounting period and the total aviation carbon dioxide emissions is added in each airport of the city to obtain the total aviation carbon dioxide emissions of the city.
   USE - Method for quickly calculating urban aviation carbon dioxide emissions.
   ADVANTAGE - The accounting method provides the space distribution of the cross-regional aviation carbon dioxide emission and accumulates and accounts the urban aviation carbon dioxide emission from bottom to top, thus improving the accuracy and integrity of the urban aviation carbon dioxide emission accounting and enriching the greenhouse gas emission evaluation system of urban transportation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for rapidly accounting for carbon dioxide emissions from urban aviation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for quickly calculating urban aviation carbon dioxide emissions. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Carbon capture-gas denitration purifying process            combining absorption and rectification by using hollow            fiber hollow fibrous membrane contactor for absorbing            carbon dioxide mixed gas and passing to multi-stage            washing tower bottom.
AU ZHANG L
   XU M
   WU J
   SHEN J
   YANG T
AE HANGZHOU KUAIKAI HI TECH CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 2018667410
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon capture-gas denitration purifying                process combining absorption and rectification                involves using hollow fiber hollow fibrous membrane                contactor for absorbing carbon dioxide mixed gas in                power plant flue gas, passing carbon dioxide mixed                gas to multi-stage washing tower bottom, providing                lye in multi-stage washing tower, lye transporting                and recycling, discharging carbon dioxide mixed gas                to cooler by lye, and exhausting through                multi-stage washing tower upper portion to obtain                carbon dioxide gas; passing carbon dioxide gas in                multi-stage washing tower into cooler, directly                discharging condensed water from condenser bottom,                passing cooled carbon dioxide gas into compressor,                adjusting compressor, and entering carbon dioxide                gas into compressor; and continuously passing                compressed carbon dioxide gas into pre-cooler,                discharging condensed water from cooler bottom,                entering cooled carbon dioxide gas in absorber,                disposing absorbent bed in absorber, and passing                carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - Method for carbon capture-gas denitration                purifying process combining absorption and                rectification.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy saving, has no additional                equipment required, produces high quality and high                purity carbon dioxide, removes and recycles sulfur                dioxide and nitrogen dioxide gas in multi-stage                washing tower, and removes low-boiling impurities                including nitrogen gas and high boiling points and                difficult to remove nitrogen oxide and oxygen gas                in rectification column.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon capture-gas denitration purifying                process combining absorption and rectification                involves:
   (A) using hollow fiber hollow fibrous membrane                contactor for absorbing carbon dioxide mixed gas in                flue gas of power plant, passing carbon dioxide                mixed gas to multi-stage washing tower bottom,                providing lye in multi-stage washing tower, lye                transporting and recycling by circulation pump,                discharging carbon dioxide mixed gas to cooler                through lye, and exhausting through upper portion                of multi-stage washing tower to obtain carbon                dioxide gas;
   (B) passing carbon dioxide gas in multi-stage                washing tower into cooler, directly discharging                condensed water from bottom of condenser, passing                cooled carbon dioxide gas into compressor,                adjusting compressor to boost compressor to 2.5-3.0                MPa, and entering carbon dioxide gas into                compressor;
   (C) continuously passing compressed carbon                dioxide gas into pre-cooler, discharging condensed                water from cooler bottom, entering cooled carbon                dioxide gas in absorber, disposing absorbent bed in                absorber, passing carbon dioxide gas from top to                bottom through absorbent bed, absorbing gas                impurities, and entering absorbed carbon dioxide                gas into dryer;
   (D) entering carbon dioxide gas from dryer                into liquefier, requiring liquefier with                refrigeration system for providing refrigerant,                cooling refrigerant to carbon dioxide gas                liquefaction temperature, entering from middle of                rectifying tower, ascending steam in lower section                of rectification column along rectification column,                and descending liquid condensed in upper column of                rectification column along rectification column;                and
   (E) vapor-liquid two-phase countercurrent                contacting, discharging gas impurities from                rectification column by overhead vent valve,                passing carbon dioxide liquid in tower kettle                reboiler into pressure reducing valve, and passing                decompressed liquid to finished storage tank for                storage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The operation temperature                  of multi-stage washing tower is 20-30oC. The                  operation temperature of cooler is 10-20 degrees                  C and 5-10 degrees C. The operation pressure of                  compressor is 2.5-2.8 MPa. The operation                  temperature of liquefier is - 20 to - 25 degrees                  C. The refrigeration system uses ammonia or                  Freon-cascade refrigeration. The operation                  pressure of rectification tower is 2.0-2.3 MPa.                  The operation temperature of tower top condenser                  is -?25 to - 28 degrees C. The operation                  temperature of tower kettle reboiler is - 15 to -                  18 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN114811249-A
TI Anti-dumping device for paving gas pipeline, has            mounting seat whose upper end is provided with fixing            frame, gas pipeline main body provided between mounting            seat and fixing frame, and installation seat whose            outer end is connected with rotating baffle flow            mechanism.
AU SHAO G
   PAN E
   HU B
   YE L
   LIN L
   YE J
   CHEN C
AE WENZHOU YONGLIANG MACHINERY TECHNOLOGY (WENZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1410M
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a mounting seat (100) whose                upper end is provided with a fixing frame (200). A                gas pipeline main body (300) is provided between                the mounting seat and the fixing frame. An outer                end of the installation seat is connected with a                rotating baffle flow mechanism (400). Left and                right ends of a top overflow inserting mechanism                (500) are provided with left and right two ends of                the mounting base. Multiple magnetic repulsion                force mechanisms (600) are installed at an outer                end surface of the fixed frame.
   USE - Anti-dumping device for paving gas pipeline                used in residents and industrial enterprises. Uses                include but are not limited to natural gas,                artificial gas, liquefied petroleum gas, marsh gas                and coal gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The rotating baffle mechanism drives the                rotating cover body to rotate through the blowing                of the wind, and the wind generated by rotation                disperses the wind blown by the wind such that the                blowing force is reduced, the mounting seat is not                easy to be affected by the blowing, and influenced                by the rotating vibration. The carbon dioxide                solution in the overflow inserting mechanism is                shaken, the carbon dioxide is caused to overflow,                the piston and the inserting rod are pushed to                overflow and the extruding inserting rod is                inserted in the ground, thus enhancing the                reinforcing stability, and hence enhancing the                anti-dumping effect. The magnetic repulsion force                mechanism counteracts blowing force by the magnetic                repulsive force between the magnetic iron strip and                magnetic iron sheet, thus avoiding the fuel gas                conveying work of the gas pipeline main body to                influence, and reducing economic loss.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                anti-dumping device for paving gas pipeline.

   300Gas pipeline main body
   400Rotating baffle mechanism
   500Top inserting and fixing mechanism
   600Magnetic repulsion force mechanism

TI Oxygen generating antibacterial sticker composition prepared by mixing porous inorganic material based on oxygen generating agent.
AU CHEOL B J
AE CHEOL B J (CHEO-Individual)
GA 202283993D
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen generating antibacterial sticker composition is prepared by mixing 10-30 pts. wt. porous inorganic material based on 100 pts. wt. oxygen generating agent. The binder is further mixed in an amount of 1-5 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. oxygen generating agent, and 2-8 pts. wt. antibacterial agent composition is further mixed based on 100 pts. wt. oxygen generating agent. The oxygen generating agent is potassium superoxide, sodium peroxide and/or calcium peroxide. The porous inorganic material is zeolite, alumina, silica and/or diatomite. The antibacterial agent composition comprises a mixture of cyclosilicate and phyllosilicate, and illite, silver, zinc, and/or platinum, and titanium dioxide. The binder is mixed with a cured resin and rosin in a weight ratio of 1:9-4:6, where the cured resin is urethane acrylate, epoxy acrylate and/or polyester acrylate, and comprises 10-30 pts. wt. reactive monomer diluent based on 100 pts. wt..
   USE - Used as oxygen generating antibacterial sticker composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen generating antibacterial sticker composition has convenient use of generating oxygen by the surrounding moisture and carbon dioxide; is easily attached to a selected and required position of the mask and stably releases oxygen for a long time by moisture and carbon dioxide contained in breathing, so it has the advantage of eliminating the occurrence of headaches due to lack of oxygen and speeding up recovery of physical strength, and oxygen generating material in the form of a sticker does not melt due to moisture, so it is used cleanly while increasing the oxygen generating efficiency, and the physical strength consumed by exercise, is quickly recovered by the clean oxygen supply; and prevents bacteria or infectious pathogens from propagating in a humid mask by providing an antibacterial function while prolonging the use time as oxygen is gradually and stably generated.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) system for manufacturing oxygen generating antibacterial sticker; and
   (2) manufacturing oxygen generating antibacterial sticker.

PN CA3081971-A1; WO2021243462-A1; CA3166916-A1
TI Production of synthesis gas e.g. carbon monoxide            for manufacturing e.g. methanol, involves reducing            stream comprising carbon source and excess of hydrogen            on contact with oxy-flame obtained by combustion of            hydrogen in presence of stream comprising oxygen.
AU LABRECQUE R
   SCHULZ R
   VIENNEAU M
   LAROCQUE G
AE HYDRO-QUEBEC (QUGO-C)
GA 2021E3516R
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthesis gas comprising carbon                monoxide and hydrogen involves reducing a stream                (S1) comprising a carbon source and an excess of                hydrogen on contact with an oxy-flame. The hydrogen                comes from a reducing current, a portion (P1) of                which is found in the flow (S1) and a portion (P2)                is used to generate the oxyflame by combustion of                the hydrogen in the presence of a stream (S2)                comprising oxygen (1) comes from an oxidizing                current. The stream (S1) and the stream (S2) are at                a distance from each other such that the oxy-flame                supports the reaction between the carbon source and                the hydrogen.
   USE - Production of synthesis gas e.g. carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen for                manufacturing chemicals and fuels e.g. methanol and                synthetic hydrocarbons (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is robust, versatile and simple,                and reduces production costs in addition to the                beneficial environmental effect of recycling carbon                sources such as carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) reactor for producing synthesis gas, which                comprises a reaction chamber (10) in which the                synthesis gas is produced by reduction reaction, at                least an unit (U1) for feeding the reaction chamber                with a reducing current comprising hydrogen, the                portion (P1) of the reducing current to generate                the oxy-flame in the combustion chamber, by                combustion of hydrogen in the presence of the                stream (S2), at least one unit (U2) for supplying                the reaction chamber with an oxidizing stream (3)                forming the stream (S2); and
   (2) system, which comprises the reactor                coupled to a synthesis gas cooling device to form a                cooled synthesis gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the                reactor.
   Oxygen (1)
   Oxidizing stream (3)
   Tube (4)
   Opening (7)
   Reaction chamber (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The unit (U1) and the                  (U2) are tubes. The reactor comprises (U2)                  comprising tubes allowing the injection of the                  oxidizing current into the reaction chamber, and                  units (U1) comprising openings allowing the                  injection of the reducing current into the                  reaction chamber. Each opening (7) is defined by                  an annular space delimited by the outer diameter                  of a tube of the tubes and extending                  perpendicularly from the outer wall of the tube.                  The reactor further comprises a reducing current                  distribution chamber separated from the reaction                  chamber by a separation wall, and a distribution                  chamber separated from the reaction chamber by a                  partition wall. The distribution chamber and                  separation wall are traversed by the tubes, the                  annular space extending perpendicularly from the                  outer wall of each tube also passing through the                  partition wall. The reducing current is hydrogen                  which is fed into the reaction chamber by the                  unit (U1) comprising tube (T1), the oxidizing                  current is oxygen, which is fed into the reaction                  chamber by the unit (U2) comprising a tube (T2).                  The carbon source is supplied by an independent                  current which is injected into the reaction                  chamber through at least one opening located in a                  wall of the reaction chamber. The opening is                  formed by a tube (T3) concentric with the tubes                  (T1) and (T2). The tube (T2) forming the inner                  tube, the tube (T1) forming an intermediate tube                  and the tube (T3) forming an outer tube. The                  opening is formed by an annular space in a lower                  wall of the reaction chamber, the annular space                  having an internal diameter defined by an                  external diameter of the tube (T1). The                  distribution chamber serves to supply the                  independent current comprising the carbon source                  and crossed by the tube (T1) and the tube (T2).                  The cooling device is a direct contact cooler.                  The system further comprises an apparatus for                  condensing the cooled syngas to recover water,                  and equipment for recycling at least a portion of                  the recovered water to the cooling device. The                  condensing apparatus is a cooler-condenser.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reduction reaction                  is carried out in absence of solid catalyst. The                  oxy-flame generates ionic species and free                  radicals which promote the conversion of the                  carbon source into carbon monoxide. The carbon                  source comprises carbon dioxide, at least one                  type of oxygenated molecule of formula: CaHbOc                  (I) and/or one or more hydrocarbons, preferably                  carbon dioxide. The reducing current comprises                  hydrogen. The oxidizing current is oxygen. The                  oxidizing stream comprises oxygen and carbon                  dioxide. The carbon source is supplied by an                  independent current. The independent stream                  comprises carbon dioxide and at least one type of                  oxygenated molecule (I). The oxygen is obtained                  from an electrolysis reaction of water. The                  hydrogen is obtained from a reaction of                  electrolysis of water. The carbon source comes                  from a gas mixture resulting from a process for                  gasification or pyrolysis of biomass. The carbon                  source comprises carbon dioxide. The synthesis                  gas further comprises carbon dioxide. At least a                  portion of the recovered water is recycled to the                  cooling step. The reduction reaction is carried                  out in the absence of solid catalyst.a = 3.5-5;b = 5.5-8.5;andc = 1.5-3.5.Preferred Process: The reduction reaction                  involves performing a reverse reaction of gas to                  water or reverse water gas shift. The method                  further involves cooling the synthesis gas to                  form a cooled synthesis gas, and condensing the                  cooled synthesis gas and recovering water.                  Preferred Conditions: The reduction reaction is                  carried out at a temperature of at least 1000                  degrees C, preferably at most 2200 degrees C. The                  stream (S1) and the stream (S2) are at a distance                  from each other of 0.1-100 mm, preferably 0.6-30                  mm. Preferred Composition: The reduction reaction                  is carried out using hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  in molar ratio of 2-7, oxygen and carbon dioxide                  in molar ratio of 0.35-0.9 and oxygen and                  hydrogen in molar ratio of 0.1-0.3. The synthesis                  gas comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide in                  molar ratio of at least 1.8, preferably 1.8-3.0.                  The molar ratio of difference of hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide and sum of carbon monoxide and                  carbon dioxide is 2 or more. The molar ratio of                  hydrogen and sum of carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide (2CO+3CO2) is more than 1.

TI Fuel less water pump for pumping out ground water from bore well, comprises installing condenser unit on lower temperature side and evaporator unit, and temperature side thermodynamic working fluid moves condenser unit to evaporator unit.
AU PUROHIT D
AE PUROHIT D (PURO-Individual)
GA 2021D9785G
AB    NOVELTY - Fuel less water pump, comprises installing condenser unit on lower temperature side. The evaporator unit is installed on higher temperature side. The thermodynamic working fluid is moved from the condenser unit to the evaporator unit on basis of temperature gradient. The bore-well structure is incorporated a casing pipe, discharging pipe and a flexible air tight moveable separator to at least one evaporator unit for pumping out ground water, where the thermodynamic working fluid used is carbon dioxide. The fuel less water pump operates without external electric power supply.
   USE - Fuel less water pump for pumping out the ground water from bore well.
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel less water pump maintains safety working pressure and fluid velocity in closed loop of power generator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of harvesting or pumping out ground water from bore-well using fuel less water pump, which involves installing condenser unit (102) in groundwater when surface water is hotter than the groundwater and vice versa and evaporator unit (104), installing in groundwater when surface water is colder than the groundwater and vice versa, supplying thermodynamic working fluid (114) from condenser unit (102) to evaporator unit, connecting a bore-well structure (106) incorporating a casing pipe (108), discharging pipe (110) and a flexible air tight moveable separator (112) to evaporator unit (104) for pumping out ground water (116), operating the fuel less water pump with a thermodynamic working fluid having a boiling point lower than 20 degrees C, where the thermodynamic working fluid used is carbon dioxide, and operating the fuel less water pump (100) without external electric power supply.

PN WO2021217261-A1; US2021340681-A1
TI Apparatus for electrochemically generating metal hydroxide, oxygen and hydrogen, has supply unit that supplies acidic solution from anode region to second container to effect dissolution of solid metal silicate material.
AU RAU G H
   TAHMASEBI S
   MEZEI A V
   BRERETON C M H
   KELLAND M J
AE PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
   PLANETARY HYDROGEN INC (PLAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2652T
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus comprises an electrolytic container (10) that includes an electrolytic solution including a metal salt which undergo electrolysis when a direct current is applied. An ion-exchange membrane is disposed in the electrolytic container between an anode (12) and a cathode (14) to define a cathode region (15) and an anode region (13). A second container (20) is disposed externally to the electrolytic container to hold a quantity of a solid metal silicate material. A supply unit supplies acidic solution from the anode region to the second container to effect dissolution of the solid metal silicate material and to generate a metal salt solution. The solid metal silicate material, the acidic solution, and the electrolytic solution are selected so that the metal in the solid silicate material and the metal in the metal salt are the same, and the metal salt solution and the electrolytic solution contain the metal salt.
   USE - Apparatus for electrochemically generating metal hydroxide, oxygen and hydrogen used as fuel, energy storer and chemical feedstock in industrial chemistry and manufacturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal hydroxide, oxygen and hydrogen are electrochemically generated in the presence of metal silicates in a cost effective manner. The metal silicate material is dissolved in the acidic solution to generate the metal salt solution, thus increasing the availability and lowering the cost of the metal salts, and hence reducing the cost and improving the efficiency of the electrochemical system. The apparatus is capable of generating hydrogen, an oxidative gas and a metal hydoxide for sequestering gaseous carbon dioxide or other acid gasses in an economical and efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method of generating hydrogen, an oxidative gas and a metal hydroxide for sequestering gaseous carbon dioxide or other acid gases.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of the apparatus with a 2-chambered electrochemical container, or electrolyzer.
   Electrolytic container (10)
   Anode (12)
   Anode region (13)
   Cathode (14)
   Cathode region (15)
   Second container (20)

PN CN113441187-A
TI Preparing copper-based metal organic framework/imidazole framework structure catalyst useful in selective catalytic reduction denitrification, comprises dissolving pretreated copper-metal-organic framework, and 2-methylimidazole in ultrapure water, centrifuging and drying.
AU DU J
   MA J
   LV R
   ZHANG L
   WU Z
   WANG T
   WANG C
   MA G
   YANG L
   LUO H
   QIN Y
AE SHANGHAI SHENTAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 2021B5945Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based metal organic framework/imidazole framework structure catalyst comprises (1) performing functional pretreatment on copper-metal-organic framework (Cu-MOF) powder to obtain pretreated Cu-MOF, (2) dissolving pretreated Cu-MOF, cobalt nitrate and doped metal nitrate in ultrapure water and mixing to obtain solution C, and dissolving pretreated Cu-MOF and 2-methylimidazole in ultrapure water and mixing to obtain solution D, (3) mixing the solution C and the solution D, ultrasonically stirring, and washing, centrifuging and drying the product to obtain Me-zeolitic imidazolate framework-67 (ZIF-67)/Cu-MOF powder, and (4) allowing Me-ZIF-67/Cu-MOF powder to stand under a preset micro-reducing atmosphere.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in selective catalytic reduction denitrification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has a larger specific surface area and a high porosity, and due to the adjustability of the pore structure, the functional diversity of organic ligands and a large number of vacant metal sites can achieve good denitration performance through metal doping.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a copper-based metal organic framework/imidazole framework structure catalyst, prepared by the above method, where the catalyst is a core-shell structure with copper-based metal organic framework as a core and an imidazole skeleton structure as a shell layer; and
   (2) used method of the copper-based metal organic framework/imidazole framework structure catalyst in selective catalytic reduction denitrification, comprising feeding the flue gas to be treated into the selective catalytic denitrification device, using carbon monoxide in the flue gas as a reducing agent, under the action of a copper-based metal organic framework/imidazole framework structure catalyst, making carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxides in the flue gas to undergo selective catalytic reduction reaction, reducing nitrogen oxides to nitrogen, oxidizing carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, and outputting nitrogen and carbon dioxide from the outlet of the denitration device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the solution C, the mass ratio of the pretreated Cu-MOF, cobalt nitrate and doped metal nitrate is 2:1:1. In the solution D, the mass ratio of the pretreated Cu-MOF and 2-methylimidazole is 1:1. The metal Me doped in the doped metal nitrate includes vanadium, aluminum, iron, cerium, or zinc.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the preparation of the Cu-MOF powder includes (a) dissolving copper nitrate trihydrate in ultrapure water to obtain solution A, and dissolving 1,3,5 benzoic acid in anhydrous ethanol solution to obtain solution B, (b) mixing the solution A and the solution B, reacting in an autoclave at 100-200degrees Celsius for 1-12 hours, and centrifugally drying the reaction product to obtain the Cu-MOF powder, where the mass ratio of the copper nitrate trihydrate and the 1,3,5 benzoic acid is 2:0.1-2:2; and the functional pretreatment includes dissolving Cu-MOF powder and polyvinylpyrrolidone in methanol, stirring for 1-12 hours, centrifuging, purifying, and drying to obtain the pretreated Cu-MOF. In the step (4), the step of placing the Me-ZIF-67/Cu-MOF powder in a preset micro-reducing atmosphere environment includes placing Me-ZIF-67/Cu-MOF powder in a slightly reducing atmosphere at 1-500degrees Celsius for 1-20 hours, where the slightly reducing atmosphere is a mixed atmosphere of 1 vol.% carbon monoxide and 99 vol.% nitrogen. The temperature of the selective catalytic reduction reaction is 150-300degrees Celsius, and the gas space velocity is 3000-5000/hour.

AU MUGISHIMA T
AE HONDA MOTOR CO LTD (HOND-C)
GA 202135249K
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogen-producing unit comprises formic-acid accommodating portion which accommodates a formic-acid solution. The catalyst accommodating portion which accommodates a catalyst solution. The supply path which has a detachable coupling with respect to a hydrogen utilization apparatus, and connects hydrogen utilization apparatus and formic-acid accommodating portion. The unit main body in which formic-acid accommodating portion. The dripping apparatus which drips the catalyst solution in catalyst accommodating portion in formic-acid accommodating portion.
   USE - Hydrogen-producing unit for generating hydrogen and carbon dioxide by reforming reaction of the formic acid in presence of catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy capacity of hydrogen gas per volume is low, it is necessary to use a high pressure tank for a hydrogen tank so that sufficient quantity of hydrogen gas can be stored.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for following:

   (2) a recycling method which recycle hydrogen-producing unit, which involves formic-acid solution and the catalyst solution remain in formic-acid accommodating portion of a used hydrogen-producing unit, concentrating catalyst solution within formic-acid accommodating portion by removing water and a formic acid from the residue in formic-acid accommodating portion, taking out catalyst solution which remains in the formic-acid accommodating portion, replenishing the removed catalyst solution or catalyst solution in catalyst accommodating portion, and replenishing formic-acid solution in formic-acid accommodating portion.

PN CN112321921-A; CN112321921-B
TI Rubber composition useful for automobile air            conditioner pipe comprises hydrogenated nitrile rubber,            poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide), anti-aging agent,            heat-resistant agent, carbon black, penetrating barrier            agent, and damping agent.
AU NIE J
   WANG H
   WANG M
   LIN L
   PENG J
AE QINGDAO SUNSONG CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202115108J
AB    NOVELTY - Rubber composition comprises 80-90 pts. wt.                hydrogenated nitrile rubber, 10-20 pts. wt. poly                (p-phenylene terephthalamide), 2-4 pts. wt.                anti-aging agent, 7-12 pts. wt. heat-resistant                agent, 20-40 pts. wt. carbon black, 102-202 pts.                wt. penetrating barrier agent, 10-30 pts. wt.                damping agent, 10-15 pts. wt. peroxide                cross-linking agent, and 3-6 pts. wt. auxiliary                crosslinking agent. The permeation barrier includes                graphene and DK2 polymer grade organoclay.
   USE - The rubber composition is useful for                automobile air conditioner pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The rubber composition: has a heat resistance                up to 180 degrees C, excellent heat resistance                life, resistance to carbon dioxide permeability.                The rubber hose made of the rubber composition can                meet the use of carbon dioxide refrigerant air                conditioning pipes demand.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the rubber composition for automobile air                conditioner pipe comprising (i) combining the                hydrogenated nitrile rubber, poly (pphenylene                terephthalamide), damping agent, graphene according                to the proportion in the mixed solution of dimethyl                formamide and xylene, high speed cutting and                stirring in a sealed container, and condensing,                solvent separation, drying obtains the blend of                hydrogenated nitrile rubber/poly (p-phenylene                terephthalamide), (ii) adding the mixture obtained                in the step (i) and the heat-resistant agent,                anti-aging agent, carbon black, DK2 polymer-grade                organic clay into the internal mixer for                pressurizing and mixing, discharging the mixed                rubber material to the open mill, and placing for                future use, and (iii) adding the product obtained                in the step (ii), the auxiliary cross-linking agent                and peroxide cross-linking agent into the internal                mixer for mixing, discharging the mixed rubber                material to the open mill for returning glue and                mixing, and pressing into the adhesive tape                suitable for processing on the tablet                machine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The acrylonitrile                  content in the hydrogenated nitrile rubber is                  42-49%, and the saturation is 99%. The                  polynononyl terephthalamide is poly                  1,9-nonylidene terephthalamide. The anti-aging                  agent is anti-aging agent MC. The heat-resistant                  agent includes cerium oxide and lithium                  carbonate. The mass ratio of the cerium oxide and                  lithium carbonate is 2:5-2:10. The mass ratio of                  graphene and DK2 polymer-grade organoclay in the                  permeation barrier agent is 1:50-1:100. The                  carbon black is carbon black N115 and/or carbon                  black N234. The damping agent is KY-1330. The                  auxiliary crosslinking agent is ethylene glycol                  dimethacrylate. The peroxide crosslinking agent                  is TMCH. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  volume ratio of the dimethyl formamide to the                  xylene in the mixed solution is 30:70, the                  temperature of the high speed shearing and                  stirring process is 120-130 degrees C, the                  rotating speed of the stirrer is 3000                  rotations/minute (rpm), stirring for 4-6 hours.                  In the step (ii), pressurizing and mixing until                  the temperature reaches 180-190 degrees C, the                  mixed rubber material is discharged to the open                  mill upper and lower sheet, parking for 16-24                  hours. In the step (iii), mixing until the                  temperature reaches 100-105 degrees C,                  discharging the mixed rubber material to the open                  mill to return rubber and mixing for 2-3 minutes,                  then pressing into adhesive tape suitable for                  processing on the tablet machine to obtain the                  rubber composition for automobile air                  conditioning tube.

The adsorption                thorns extend to circle of elastic metal arc strip. The                one end of connecting metal rope far away from                elastic metal arc strip is fixedly connected with                inner bag ball. An outer end of inner bag ball                is fixedly connected with uniformly distributed                activated carbon adsorption columns.

The                efficiency of oil stain dissolution is greatly                accelerated. The elastic metal arc strip is                promoted to expand into an arc under the repulsive                action of the magnetic ball, and mutually combined                with the other elastic metal arc strips to form a                variety of different sizes of magnetic-attracting                rings. The adsorption effect of the particles                containing in the sewage is improved.

TI Fan self-fire extinguishing system, has The liquid storage tank filled with evaporation liquid, power generation groove connected with sliding plate, and power generating groove whose inner wall upper unit is fixed with piezoelectric ring.
AU YANG C
AE YANG C (YANG-Individual)
   HAITIAN FIREFIGHTING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAFI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C5857L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a box body (1) whose top unit is connected with a fan (2). An inner wall of the box body is formed with a circular cavity (5). A right lower unit at an inner wall of the circular cavity is fixed with a liquid storage tank (3). The liquid storage tank is filled with evaporation liquid. An inner wall upper unit of the liquid storage tank is fixedly connected with a baffle plate (4). An inner wall of a power generation groove (6) is connected with a sliding plate. An inner wall upper unit of the power generating groove is fixed with a piezoelectric ring (7). An inner wall lower unit of a discharging groove is formed with multiple spraying ports. The piezoelectric ring is connected with an electromagnetic valve.
   USE - Fan self-fire extinguishing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system sprays gaseous evaporation liquid into a power generating tank in a pulsed manner when fire occurs in a van, and internal temperature rises and squeezes the piezoelectric ring by the sliding plate in a continuous manner so as to generate current on the piezoelectric ring in a continuous manner. Flows copper oxide powder in a cavity into the discharging groove so as to uniformly sprinkle the copper oxide powder from the spraying ports under action of centrifugal force generated by rotation of the fan, so that the copper oxide is reacted with carbon monoxide to generate carbon dioxide to increase concentration of the carbon dioxide in the van, thus suppressing the fire in the van in an effective and rapid manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a fan self-fire extinguishing system in partial section.
   Box body (1)
   Fan (2)
   Liquid storage tank (3)
   Baffle plate (4)
   Circular cavity (5)
   Power generation groove (6)
   Piezoelectric ring (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - The piezoelectric ring is made of piezoelectric ceramic material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Blades of the fan are made of magnetic material.

By combining oral administration with special fumigation for external use, it can significantly improve the efficacy of nasal comfort tablets and reduce the burden and side effects of oral drugs on the liver.

   (2) a method for using a nasal comfort tablets, which involves placing nasal comfort tablet and water into the distiller, and performing distillation operation to obtain a medical liquid vapor.

A medicine storage cavity is arranged in the distillation flask, and the temperature sensor is arranged in the medicine storage cavity and is electrically connected to the heating device. The drainage assembly includes a drainage cap and a drainage column, the drainage cap is connected with the distillation flask, and the drainage column is in communication with the drainage cap. The sealing assembly includes a sealing cover and a stirring column, the sealing cover is connected with the distillation flask, and the stirring column is arranged on the sealing cover. Preferred Process: The method for using nasal comfort tablets involves placing nasal comfort tablets and water into the medicine storage cavity, covering with the sealing cap, and controlling the stirring column to stir, so that the nasal comfort tablets are dissolved to obtain a medical solution. The heating device is controlled to heat the liquid medicine to 80-100 degrees C by the temperature sensor, so that the liquid medicine is vaporized to obtain liquid medicine vapor. The drainage cover is covered so that the medical liquid vapor enters the drainage column from the drainage cover, and the drainage column is inserted into the nostril so that the medical liquid vapor enters the nostril from the drainage column.

TI Hierarchical porous flower-like carbon nitride assembled nano-sheet used as photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide is obtained by layering and orderly assembling flower-like carbon nitride porous nanosheets.
AU XIANG Q
   LI F
   ZHANG H
   LIAO Y
   JIN L
   LI J
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 202039633G
AB    NOVELTY - Hierarchical porous flower-like carbon nitride assembled nano-sheet is obtained by layering and orderly assembling porous nanosheets. The flower-like carbon nitride has flower body size of 4-10 mu m, nanosheet thickness of 20-60 nm, specific surface area of 90-140 m2/g, pore volume of 0.4-1 m3/g, and pore size of 1-6 nm.
   USE - Hierarchical porous flower-like carbon nitride assembled nano-sheet used as photocatalyst for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-sheets enhance photocatalytic activity because hierarchical porous structure enhances light collection efficiency, increases specific surface area of material providing more active sites for redox reaction on the surface, and forms unique transmission channel that promotes diffusion of reactant molecules and carriers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the nano-sheet comprising mixing by stirring melamine in dimethyl sulfoxide until solution is clear and transparent to obtain 1-4x 104 mg/L melamine solution, mixing by stirring cyanuric acid in dimethyl sulfoxide until solution is clear and transparent to obtain 1-4x 104 mg/L cyanuric acid, mixing obtained solutions together to obtain melamine and cyanuric acid molar ratio of 1:0.1-2, stirring for 5-30 minutes until white precipitate forms to obtain suspension, centrifuging, washing residue, and drying to obtain cyanuric acid-melamine complex precursor; and dispersing cyanuric acid-melamine complex precursor in ethanol to obtain dispersion with concentration of 2-5x 104 mg/L, reacting mixture by stirring in water bath at 20-80 degrees C for 1-5 hours, centrifuging resulting suspension, washing residue, drying, heating in muffle furnace at 2-5 degrees C/ minute rate from room temperature to 550 degrees C, keeping at 550 degrees C for 2-8 hours, and cooling to room temperature to obtain nano-sheets.

TI Catalyst system used for producing cyclic carbonates, comprises pre-catalyst chosen from bismuth-containing compound and silicon-containing compound, and metal oxide support containing magnesium oxide, and titanium oxide.
AU PIROMCHART T
   DELIA V
AE PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN PUBLIC CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
   PTT EXPLORATION & PRODN CO LTD (PTTE-Non-standard)
GA 202023684C
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst system comprises a pre-catalyst chosen from a bismuth-containing compound (I) and a silicon-containing compound (II), and a metal oxide support chosen from magnesium oxide, silicon oxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, and titanium oxide.
   USE - Catalyst system used for producing cyclic carbonates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst system is inexpensive, and has excellent catalytic activity even under mild conditions using pure and impure carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst system comprises a pre-catalyst chosen from bismuth-containing compound of formula: BiXz (I) and silicon-containing compound of formula (II), and a metal oxide support chosen from magnesium oxide, silicon oxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, and titanium oxide.
   X,Y' = halide;
   z = 1-3;
   m = 1-4;
   R1-R3 = 1-4C alkyl or 1-4C alkoxy;
   R4-R6 = 1-4C alkyl or aryl;and
   or R4+R5+R6 = heteroaryl.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) use of catalyst system for producing cyclic carbonates;
   (2) preparation of the catalyst system, which involves refluxing a silane compound of formula (III) with an amine-based compound in an organic solvent at a temperature of 100-150 degrees C for 12-72 hours, where the molar ratio between the silane compound and the amine-based compound is (5:1)-(1:5) to obtain the compound (I), refluxing the mixture containing the compound (I) in an organic solvent and the metal oxide support suspended in the organic solvent at a temperature of 100-200 degrees C for 5-50 hours, where the concentration of compound (I) is 5-15 (v/v)% and the concentration of metal oxide is 10-20 (w/v)%, and contacting the pre-catalyst with the metal oxide for 1-5 hours, where the concentration of the pre-catalyst is 1-10 wt.% of the metal oxide; and
   (3) manufacture of cyclic carbonates, which involves reacting epoxide-based compounds with carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalyst system.
   A = halide;
   n = 1-4;and
   R11-R13 = R1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precatalyst comprises bismuth chloride. The metal oxide support comprises silicon dioxide. Preferred Process: The contact of precatalyst with the metal oxide is performed using a dipping method, an impregnation method, or a solid state reaction method.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is chosen from 1-methyl-3-(3-trimethoxysilyl)propyl-1H-imidazol-3-ium and N,N,N-trimethyl-3-(trimethoxysilyl)propan-1-aminium. The amine-based compound is an imidazole-based compound, preferably N-methylimidazole. The organic solvent is an aromatic hydrocarbon solvent, preferably toluene. The amine-based compound is of formula (IV).q = 1-4;R14-R16 = R1;R17,R18 = R4;andor R17+R18 = heteroaryl.Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the silane compound and the amine-based compound is (2:1)-(1:2).
   DEFINITION - Preferred Definitions:
   X = chloride;
   Y',A = chloride, bromide or iodide;
   R1-R3 = methoxy;
   R4-R6 = methyl;
   R4+R5+R6 = heteroaryl of formula (V);
   R8 = methyl;and

TI Methanation catalyst for manufacturing methane            comprises iron group metallic-element containing            microparticles which consist of ceria microparticles,            iron group metal, or its oxide.
AU FUKUI M
   IMAGAWA H
AE TOYOTA CHUO KENKYUSHO KK (TOYW-C)
   TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 202019318Y
AB    NOVELTY - A methanation catalyst comprises iron group                metallic-element containing microparticles which                consist of ceria microparticles, iron group metal,                or its oxide. The content of iron group metallic                element is 15-80 mass% measured with X-ray                fluorescence analysis with respect to total amount                of cerium (Ce) and iron group metallic element, and                content of iron group metallic element measured                with X-ray fluorescence analysis. The standard                deviation of content of iron group metallic element                is less than or equal to 4 measured with energy                dispersion-type X-ray spectroscopy analysis at                measurement area of 1 mu mx 1 mu m depth of 20                points extracted at random in methanation catalyst,                and sodium (Na) content is less than or equal to 1                at.%.
   USE - A methanation catalyst for manufacturing                methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methanation catalyst has high catalyst                activity and manufactures methane from carbon                dioxide and hydrogen at high yield even at low                temperature of less than or equal to 250 degrees                C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacturing methanation catalyst which                involves producing coprecipitate which adds                precipitant containing sodium (Na) to precursor                solution containing cerium ion and iron group metal                ion, washing coprecipitate until Na content is less                than or equal to 1 at.%, and transforming cerium                compound and iron group metallic compound to iron                group metallic-element containing microparticles                which consist of ceria microparticles, iron group                metal, or its oxide;
   (2) manufacturing methanation catalyst which                involves producing coprecipitate which adds                precipitant which does not contain Na to precursor                solution containing cerium ion and iron group metal                ion, and contains cerium compound and iron group                metallic compound, and transforming cerium compound                and iron group metallic compound to iron group                metallic-element containing microparticles which                consist of ceria microparticles, iron group metal,                or its oxide; and
   (3) manufacturing methane which involves                making mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to                contact the methanation catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The average particle                  diameter of iron group metallic-element                  containing microparticles is 0.5-10 nm. Preferred                  Components: The precipitant containing Na is                  greater than or equal to 1 of sodium hydroxide                  and sodium carbonate. The precipitant which does                  not contain Na is ammonium                  hydrogencarbonate.

PN CN110818491-A
TI Preparing wheat foliar fertilizer involves adding ice water to yam, crushing, adding complex enzymes, performing enzymatic hydrolysis, passing carbon dioxide, continue to incubate, filtering and removing slag, concentrating yam concentrate.
AU LI N
   PU Y
   DING H
   LI Z
   GONG Y
   GUO X
   TIAN R
AE SHANDONG CENT CROP GERMPLASM RESOURCES (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020181418
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing wheat foliar fertilizer involves adding 3-4 times mass of ice water to yam, crushing, and adding complex enzymes. The enzymatic hydrolysis is performed at 30-35 degrees C for 1.5-2.0 hours, passing carbon dioxide, continue to incubate for 0.5-2.0 hours, and filtering and removing slag. The yam concentrate is obtained, and concentrated under vacuum at 60-70 degrees C to a solid content of 30-40% to obtain yam concentrate. The carnauba wax and alginate is added to yam concentrate, stir to make mixed solution. The humic acid, ternary compound fertilizer, snail mucus extract, sophorolipid and glycerol are mixed, add water and stir to obtain mixture. The mixture is added to mixed solution, heat up by program temperature, and keep stirring at 300-350 rpm during the heat preservation. The mass ratio of the mixture to mixed liquid is 2-3:10. The colloid mill is ground 4-5 times, then homogenized by high-pressure homogenizer 2-3 times to obtain homogeneous solution, and dried to obtain product.
   USE - Method for preparing wheat foliar fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare wheat foliar fertilizer increase yield per mu, and improve wheat grain fullness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for applying wheat foliar fertilizer, which involves:
   (A) spraying wheat foliar fertilizer 3 times in the whole growth period of wheat;
   (B) using 3 kg per mu each time;
   (C) adding 25 kg water to spray the wheat leaves, the first time is february 5-february 12, second time in march 20th-march 27th, the third time: April 15-April 22;
   (D) performing the spraying of fertilizer before 8 o'clock or after 6 p.m, fertilizer must be sprayed on the leaves, green stalks or wheat ears; and
   (E) spraying again in case of rain 24 hours after spraying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Composition: The added amount of the compound enzyme accounts for 0.8-1.2% of the weight of the yam. The complex enzyme is composed of pullulanase and beer yeast in a mass ratio of 1: 3. The mass ratio of the yam concentrate, carnauba wax and alginate is 1: 0.2: 0.1. The mass ratio of humic acid, ternary compound fertilizer, snail mucus extract, sophorolipid and glycerol is 25-35: 42-48: 12-15: 7-8: 5-6. The mass ratio of water and humic acid added is 2-3: 1. Preferred Conditions: The permeation amount of carbon dioxide is 1.0-1.2 g/cm3 for the density of the foam slurry. The program temperature rise and heat preservation are specifically: temperature rise to 70-80 degrees C, heat preservation for 5 minutes, and then temperature rise to 95-100 degrees C, heat preservation for 2.5 minutes. The working parameters of the high-pressure homogenizer are a pressure of 20-25 megapascal (MPa) and a temperature of 60-70 degrees C.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).)
