TI Heterometallic titanium (IV), crystalline and            porous metal organic framework solid for solar fuel            generation, photoactivated degradation, carbon dioxide            photoreduction, comprises tricarboxylic ligand as an            organic portion and divalent metal.
AU CARLOS M G
   JAVIER C G
   NATALIA M P
   MARTI GASTALDO C
   CASTELLS GIL J
   MUNOZ PADIAL N
   MUNOZ P N
   CASTELLS G J
   MARTI G C
AE UNIV VALENCIA (UYPV-C)
   UNIV GRANADA (UYRA-C)
   UNIV VALENCIA (UYPV-C)
   UNIV GRANADA (UYRA-C)
   UNIV GRANADA (UYRA-C)
   UNIV VALENCIA (UYPV-C)
GA 2019997666
AB    NOVELTY - Heterometallic titanium (IV), crystalline and                porous metal organic framework solid comprises                tricarboxylic ligand L as an organic portion of the                metal organic framework (MOF), and titanium (IV)                with at least one divalent metal M (II)(1-5) in the                structural unit and inorganic portion of the MOF,                where the titanium (IV) and the divalent metal are                homogeneously distributed at the atomic level, and                the solid MOF also includes at least one polar                solvent molecule S.
   USE - Heterometallic titanium (IV), crystalline and                porous metal organic framework solid for solar fuel                generation, photoactivated degradation, carbon                dioxide photoreduction, heterogeneous catalysis, as                a component of an electronic component and as a                porous or photoactive coating for the control of                contaminants (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heterometallic titanium (IV), crystalline                and porous metal organic framework solid has                photocatalytic activity with visible light,                chemical stability in aqueous medium with pH in the                ranges of 1-13, 1-10 or 2-12, porosity with a                surface area (BET) equal to or greater than 1000                m2/g and photocatalytic activity with UV                radiation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for synthesizing crystalline and porous                heterometallic Ti(IV) MOF solid, which involves                mixing polar solvent, S, a precursor of Ti (IV), at                least one salt of a divalent metal of formula MX2                or MY, a tricarboxylic ligand L, where the                stoichiometric ratio between the divalent metal                salt and the ligand is between 1:1 and 1:6, and,                optionally, an inorganic acid in a molar ratio                between 5-500 gram/mol equivalents of MX2 or MY                salt, and then, heating the reaction mixture to                give the solid MOF, where the Ti (IV) and the                divalent metal together form a metallic cluster                interconnected with the tricarboxylic ligand L,                where X is selected from F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, NO3-,                ClO4-, BF4-, SCN-, OH-, CH3COO- or C5H7O2- and Y is                selected from SO4(2-) or CO3(2-).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic portion                  includes less than or equal to 50 wt.% titanium                  and the remainder up to 100% divalent metal (s),                  preferably, 15-50 wt.% titanium. The divalent                  metal is selected from Mg2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+,                  Ti2+, V2+, Cr2+, Mn2+, Fe2+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cu2+,                  Zn2+ or Cd2+. The MOF is represented by formula                  (Ti(IV)M(II)3(O)3L4)S. The Ti (IV) precursor is                  selected from an organometallic Ti (IV) precursor                  such as a Ti(IV) alkoxide, preferably Ti(IV)                  isopropoxide, Ti(IV) methoxide, Ti ethoxide(IV),                  Ti(IV) n-proproxide, Ti(IV) n-butoxide,                  triethanolamine-Ti(IV) isopropoxide, Ti(IV)                  tert.-butoxide, Ti(IV)oxo di-acetylacetonate, Ti                  (IV) tetrachloride, bis (cyclopentadienyl)-Ti                  (IV) dichloride, cyclopentadienyl-Ti(IV)                  trichloride or Ti(IV)oxosulfate, an air-stable                  Ti(IV)polynuclear compound such as a                  Ti(IV)hexanuclear complex, or a heterometallic                  Ti(IV) MOF solid, preferably the Ti (IV)                  heteromethalic MOF solid of formula                  (Ti(IV)M(II)3(O)3L4)S. The inorganic acid is                  selected from hydrochloric acid, formic acid,                  acetic acid, propanoic acid, benzoic acid and                  derivatives. Preferred Conditions: The Ti (IV)                  and the divalent metal M (II)(1-5) together forms                  a metallic cluster interconnected with the                  tricarboxylic ligand L to give a                  three-dimensional crystalline and porous                  structure, where the tricarboxylic ligand L is                  selected from aryl-C6 tricarboxylic acid, an                  aryl-C3N3 tricarboxylic acid or a derivative, an                  (aryl-C'6)3-aryl-C6 tricarboxylic acicd or                  (arl-C'6)3-aryl-C3N3 tricarboxylic acid. The                  titanium (IV) and divalent metal are present in                  molar ratio of 50:50 and 99:1. The solid MOF is                  (Cu(II)(3-2z)Ti(IV)(z)(L)2)S, where z is 0 and                  less than 1.5. Preferred Process: The method for                  obtaining a MOF solid of formula                  (Ti(IV)M(II)3(O)3L4)S in a one-pot synthesis and                  by direct reaction involves mixing a polar                  solvent S, a Ti(IV) precursor, at least one salt                  of a divalent metal of formula MX2 or MY, a                  tricarboxylic ligand L and an inorganic acid, and                  then heating the reaction mixture at a                  temperature greater than 80 degrees C for a                  period equal to or greater than 24 hours, and                  then cooling to give the MOF solid of formula                  (Ti(IV)M(II)3(O)3L4)S, where Ti (IV) and divalent                  metal are reacted in ratio between 50:50 and                  99:1. The method for obtaining a solid MOF of                  formula (Cu(II)(3-2z)Ti(IV)(z)(L)2) S involves                  mixing polar solvent S, a precursor of Ti (IV),                  at least one salt of a divalent metal of formula                  CuX2 or CuY and a tricarboxylic ligand L, and                  then heating the reaction mixture at a                  temperature greater than 100 degrees C for a                  period of time equal to or greater than 24 hours,                  and then cooling to give the product. The method                  for obtaining solid MOF of formula (Ti (IV)                  (3-4w)M(II)wO(L)2X(3-w))S involves mixing a polar                  solvent S, a Ti (IV) precursor, at least one salt                  of a divalent metal of formula MX2 or MY and a                  tricarboxylic ligand L, and then heating the                  reaction mixture to a temperature below 100                  degrees C and then cooling to give the solid MOF                  of formula                  (Ti(IV)(3-4w)M(II)wO(L)2X(3-w))S.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polar solvent S is                  selected from N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone,                  methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, n-propanol, water                  and mixtures. The tricarboxylic ligand L is                  selected from comppunds of formulae                  (I-IV)..R1=COOH or group of formula (IV);R2=-H, -(CH2)(0-5)-CH3, -NH2, -OH, -NO2,                  -COOH or halogen; andn=1-3.The tricarboxylic ligand L is preferably                  1,3,5-benzene-tricarboxylic acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).)

PN CN110327426-A
TI Preparing capsule comprises e.g. cleaning and drying stiff silkworm, rhizoma typhonii, rhizoma arisaematis, Angelica dahurica, rhizoma gastrodiae, radix sileris, Notopterygium root and Panax notoginseng, cutting, crushing and centrifuging.
AU HUA Q
   ZHOU G
   ZHAO X
   WANG N
   NIU J
   ZHANG L
   YUE X
AE HEBEI AOXING GROUP PHARM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201986902P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing capsule comprises e.g. cleaning and drying stiff silkworm, rhizoma typhonii, rhizoma arisaematis, Angelica dahurica, rhizoma gastrodiae, radix sileris, Notopterygium root and Panax notoginseng, cutting, crushing the medicinal materials, adding medicinal powder into high speed centrifuge and centrifuging, extracting medicinal material using carbon dioxide using supercritical fluid as a medium for essential substances, concentrating the medicine essential substances, low temperature drying, concentrating essential substances and medicinal materials, grinding and sieving, collecting fine particles for ultra-micro crushing, sieving to obtain ultra-micro powder, processing rhizoma typhonii and rhizoma arisaematis concentrate fine particles before mixing, taking each medicine spare particles concentrate in hollow mixer for mixing, mixing, filling into capsules or adding different adhesives for wet granulation.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing capsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the efficacy of capsule; increases ultrafine powder content; reduces gap of drug particles in the capsule; enhances particle flow in the capsule; and can reduce the number of patients taking medicine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing capsule comprises (1) cleaning and drying stiff silkworm, rhizoma typhonii, rhizoma arisaematis, Angelica dahurica, rhizoma gastrodiae, radix sileris, Notopterygium root and Panax notoginseng, (2) cutting, crushing the medicinal materials, adding medicinal powder into high speed centrifuge and centrifuging, extracting medicinal material using carbon dioxide using supercritical fluid as a medium for essential substances, concentrating the medicine essential substances, (3) low temperature drying, concentrating essential substances and medicinal materials, grinding and sieving by 50-100-mesh sieve, collecting fine particles for ultra-micro crushing, sieving by 300-400-mesh sieve to obtain ultra-micro powder, processing rhizoma typhonii and rhizoma arisaematis concentrate fine particles before mixing, (4) taking each medicine spare particles concentrate in hollow mixer for mixing, (5) mixing, filling into capsules or adding different adhesives for wet granulation, adding lactose, microcrystalline cellulose and the like for dry granulation after filling capsule, (6) inspecting capsule and packaging to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (3), separately processing rhizoma typhonii and rhizoma arisaematis before adding to the medicinal material. In the step (2), separately heating and concentrating in a heating and concentrating device, and heating and concentrating medicinal material under vacuum, and the heating temperature is 15-40 degrees C, internal air pressure of the heating and concentrating device is less than or equal to -0.09 Mpa, and the concentration time is 8-15 hours. Preferred Components: The binder is povidone k30 or hydroxypropyl methylcellulose.

PN CN110169451-A
TI Probiotic active fragrance used for private household healthcare, includes passion fruit juice, passion fruit peel polysaccharide, honey freeze-dried powder, xanthan gum, pectin, defatted low lactose milk and mixed probiotics.
AU GU Y
AE GU Y (GUYY-Individual)
GA 201975560D
AB    NOVELTY - Probiotic active fragrance comprises 50-65 wt.% passion fruit juice, 1-4 wt.% passion fruit peel polysaccharide, 1-4 wt.% honey freeze-dried powder, 0.08-0.16 wt.% xanthan gum, 0.1-0.2 wt.% pectin, 23.12-30.56 wt.% defatted low lactose milk, 1-4 wt.% mixed probiotics, 5-10 wt.% flavored concentrated juice, 0.08-0.16 wt.% sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 0.08-0.16 wt.% sodium alginate and 0.1-0.2 wt.% propylene glycol alginate.
   USE - Probiotic active fragrance.
   ADVANTAGE - The product contains original flavor and nutrition of passion fruit; has anti-tumor, anti-inflammatory, antilipemic and anti-oxidative biological activities; helps regulate human intestinal flora through probiotics; and has good healthcare effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of probiotic active fragrance comprising:
   (A) washing passion fruit peel, drying, cutting into strips, sterilizing by UV irradiation with wavelength of 200-275 nm for 5-10 minutes, crushing and sieving to particle diameter of 1-4 mm;
   (B) extracting ground passion fruit peel by supercritical carbon dioxide, placing ground passion fruit peel in extraction kettle, soaking in carbon dioxide liquid for 1-4 hours, extracting at pressure of 18-23 MPa, separating with pressure value of 5-8 MPa, heating at 45-48 degrees C for 1-4 hours and separating passion fruit peel polysaccharide in extract;
   (C) mixing milk with balsamic juice and defatted low-lactose milk at 40-70 degrees C;
   (D) adding mixed probiotics to mixed milk, subjecting mixed milk with probiotics to multi-stage fermentation for 3-6 days to obtain mixed probiotic solution; and
   (E) adding passion fruit peel polysaccharide to mixed probiotics stock solution, adding flavored concentrated juice and honey freeze-dried powder, adding pectin, xanthan gum, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, sodium alginate and propylene glycol alginate, filling, sealing and packaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The mixed probiotics are Bifidobacterium, Lactobacillus rhamnosus, Lactobacillus reuteri and Lactobacillus bulgaricus at ratio of 2:1:2:3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flavored concentrated juice is apple juice and/or lemon juice. Preferred Method: The multi-stage fermentation consists of two portions, the front portion and thick portion where front portion is fermented at 14-16 degrees C for 1-3 days to proliferate probiotics, and fermented at 35-45 degrees C for 1-3 days.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic; Antiinflammatory; Antilipemic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN109877484-A; CN109877484-B
TI No-clean and residue-free solder paste includes tin-bismuth alloy, tin-bismuth-silver alloy, tin-bismuth-silver alloy, thixotropic agent, active agent, solvents, sodium polyepoxysuccinate and polycarbonate polyol.
AU LIU M
   WU G
   LIAN H
   LI Y
AE DONGGUAN YONGAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201955007Q
AB    NOVELTY - No-clean and residue-free solder paste comprises 86-91 pts. wt. solder powder alloy and 9-14 pts. wt. flux. The solder powder alloy is composed of tin-bismuth alloy, tin-bismuth-silver alloy, tin-bismuth-silver alloy, tin-bismuth-silver-copper alloy, tin-silver-copper alloy, tin-lead alloy, tin-lead-bismuth alloy, tin-lead-silver alloy and/or lead-tin-silver alloy. The flux is made of 5-8 pts. wt. thixotropic agent, 5-12 pts. wt. active agent, 20-30 pts. wt. solvent 1, 15-35 pts. wt. solvent 2, 10-25 pts. wt. solvent 3, 10-25 pts. wt. ether solvent, 1-2 pts. wt. sodium polyepoxysuccinate and 2-4 pts. wt. polycarbonate polyol.
   USE - No-clean and residue-free solder paste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of no-clean and residue-free solder paste comprising:
   (A) adding carbon dioxide to reaction vessel, adding propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin and dipropylene glycol after the pressure reaches 1.5 MPa, heating at 60 degrees C, continuously adding carbon dioxide until the pressure in the reactor reaches 2.5 MPa, adding liquid double metal cyanide catalyst, reacting for 30 hours at constant temperature and removing unreacted propylene oxide and epichlorohydrin under vacuum to obtain polycarbonate polyol;
   (B) weighing raw materials of flux, adding ether solvent, heating at 130-140 degrees C, adding solvent 1, solvent 2 and solvent 3, stirring until completely melted, adding thixotropic agent and stirring until completely melted;
   (C) cooling to 115-125 degrees C, adding active agent and sodium polysuccinate, stirring until completely melted, cooling to obtain paste flux and storing in cold storage at 0-10 degrees C; and
   (D) weighing solder powder alloy and flux, pouring into the vacuum mixer, stirring at low speed for 5-10 minutes, stirring nitrogen gas at medium speed for 20-30 minutes to mix the tin powder and flux thoroughly and vacuuming at medium speed for 5-7 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thixotropic agent is mixture of modified hydrogenated castor oil and polyamide. The active agent is succinic acid, glutaric acid, adipic acid, sebacic acid, ethanolamine, and/or diethanolamine. The solvent 1 is trimethylolpropane and/or tetradecane. The solvent 2 is isobornylcyclohexanol, cetyl alcohol, 1,8-octanediol, and/or 1,2-octanediol. The solvent 3 is tetraethylene glycol and/or dodecanol. The ether solvent is diethylene glycol diethyl ether or diethylene glycol methyl ether. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, and dipropylene glycol is 20:2:1.
DC M26 (Non-ferrous alloys - including alloy production and composition (C22C).); M23 (Soldering; welding - including brazing, flame cutting and scarfing, cutting and welding rods, soldering and unsoldering apparatus and solder compositions (B23K).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P55 (Soldering, welding metal (B23K).)

PN WO2019102206-A1; GB2570203-A; GB2570203-B
TI Accessory for straining fluid in fluid circuit piping of heating/cooling system, has collar that is mountable around casing of pipeline strainer to permanent magnet in vicinity of screen collector located within casing pipeline strainer.
AU COWLEY R
   WILKINSON D
   COWLEY R A
   WILKINSON D L
AE VEXO INT UK LTD (VEXO-Non-standard)
GA 201948424A
AB    NOVELTY - The accessory (501) comprises casing for connection to a fluid piping inflow conduit and to a fluid piping outflow conduit. The casing defines an interior chamber within which a screen collector is beatable, a fluid inlet port and a fluid outlet port. The casing defines a fluid flow path between the fluid inlet port and the fluid outlet port that extends through the interior chamber. The accessory comprises a collar (502) that supports permanent magnet (503) arrangement. The collar mountable around the casing of the pipeline strainer to position of permanent magnet in the vicinity of a screen collector located within the casing of the pipeline strainer.
   USE - Accessory for straining fluid in fluid circuit piping of heating/cooling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The capturing particles from the system water improves the operational efficiency of the system to achieve the original design efficiency. The risk of component damage and system failure, which minimizes energy and maintenance costs and reduces carbon dioxide (C02) emissions, when the performance efficiency of a heating or cooling system reduces due to the effects of corrosion contamination, both the amount of energy required to operate the system and the amount of carbon dioxide (C02) emitted by the system increases. The capturing of magnetic particles is smaller in size than the particles captured mechanically, which minimizes the quantity of unwanted particles in the flowing fluid, which provides cleaner fluid flow. The ability to move one or more adjustable permanent magnets into a position effectively minimizes the rest internal diameter of the collar, which enables the collar to be mounted to a pipeline strainer in a tensioned condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for treatment of fluid in a fluid circuit of a heating or a cooling system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of accessory for a pipeline strainer.
   Accessory (501)
   Collar (502)
   Permanent magnet (503)
   External surface (506)
   Obverse face (507)

TI Metal material deep linear cutting processing device used in machining industry, has cooling gas spray gun whose outlet is aligned with metal cutting wire that is cut from top to bottom to move metal material to be processed.
AU YU H
   LUO K
   XIONG H
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2019464908
AB    NOVELTY - The processing device has a carbon dioxide cooling gas storage tank (1) whose outlet is connected to a cooling gas conduit (4). The cooling gas conduit is divided into two paths, and is provided with a first cooling gas injection switch (10) for connecting the cooling gas and the cooling gas inlet of a lubricating fluid spray gun (11). A cooling gas spray gun (16) is provided with a second cooling gas injection switch (15). The cooling gas and the outlet of the lubricating fluid spray gun are aligned with a metal cutting wire (14) and the upper surface of a metal material (12) to be processed. The outlet of the cooling gas spray gun is aligned with the metal cutting wire after the cutting is completed. The metal cutting wire is cut from the top to the bottom to move the metal material to be processed. The metal material to be processed is a steel material, an aluminum alloy material, a copper alloy material or a titanium alloy material.
   USE - Metal material deep linear cutting processing device used in machining industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The use amount of the lubricating liquid in the wire cutting process is reduced, the service life of the wire is improved, and the working delay caused by the wire breakage is reduced, thus effectively improving the machining efficiency of the metal material and protecting the worker's body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a metal material deep linear cutting processing method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the metal material deep linear cutting processing device.

TI Rotating packed bed for facilitating mass transfer            between sorbent and carbon dioxide, has first packing            material for receiving sorbent from central chamber,            and second packing material for receiving sorbent from            first material.
AU STRAND A
   EIMER D A
   VIK A
   TORVANGER O
   YING J
AE FJELL BIODRY AS (FJEL-Non-standard)
   COMPACT CARBON CAPTURE AS (COMP-Non-standard)

GA 201928173B
AB    NOVELTY - The bed (22) has a central chamber for                receiving a flow of sorbent (24) that is selected                as liquid. A flow path is formed for supplying the                sorbent between the central chamber and a region                for mass transfer between gas and the sorbent. The                flow of sorbent through the region for mass                transfer is in cross-flow with the flow of gas                through the region for mass transfer. A first                packing material receives sorbent from the central                chamber. A second packing material receives sorbent                from the first packing material, where sorbent flow                resistance of the second packing material is less                than the sorbent flow resistance of the first                packing material.
   USE - RPB for facilitating mass transfer between                sorbent and gas i.e. dirty flue gas such as high                concentration carbon dioxide, for a gas capture                system used on an offshore platform.
   ADVANTAGE - The RPB maintains tangential gas flow velocity                to be smaller than the inner radius of the flow                annulus, so that the conversion of tangential                velocity to static pressure can be reduced, where                the vane profile is varied to compensate. The RPB                maintains active mass transport area to be greater                than the actual surface area of the packing if the                packing stimulates the formation of droplets and                liquid film as the liquid moved from inner                perimeter of the RPB towards a periphery, where an                effective contact area between the gas and sorbent                is increased without causing early flooding to                reduce the need for servicing the RPB system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a rotating packed bed (RPB) system for                providing mass transfer between sorbent and                gas

PT P
PN CN108991333-A
TI Separating and clarifying juice comprises            selecting goji fruit, washing, swelling cleaned goji            fruit with clean water, pulping, enzymatic hydrolysis,            decomposing, centrifuging, separating, clarifying juice            sterilizing and separating.
AU CHU L
   ZHAO Y
   MA Y
   ZHU F
   HE F
   GE B
   DING C
AE CHINA COOP JINAN FRUIT RES INST (CHCO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A3802X
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and clarifying juice comprises (1)                selecting goji fruit and washing, (2) swelling                cleaned goji fruit with clean water, pulping to                make a slurry, inducting carbon dioxide to protect,                (3) heating pulp and maintaining, cooling, adding                fruit pulp enzyme for enzymatic hydrolysis, adding                endoprotease, decomposing protein into small                molecular amino acid, (4) centrifuging pulp                obtained in the step (3), separating the juice from                the fruit residue, collecting the juice to obtain                juice, (5) adding pectinase and amylase into the                juice, performing secondary ultrasonic enzymolysis                under ultrasonic condition, performing the enzyme                process, (6) clarifying juice after inactivating                enzyme, subjecting membrane ultrafiltration to                obtained supernatant, (7) ultrafiltration                concentrating, sterilizing the goji clear juice to                obtain clear juice and (8) subjecting goji fruit                powder through superfine crushing, separating fruit                residue after drying.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                clarifying juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is stable and is no feculent                phenomena stored for a long time. The juice has                clear appearance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and clarifying juice comprises (1)                selecting goji fruit as raw material and washing                with water, (2) swelling cleaned the goji fruit                with clean water, pulping to make a slurry,                inducting carbon dioxide to protect in the pulping                process, (3) heating the obtained pulp to 80-95                degrees C and maintaining for 1-3 minutes, cooling                to 40-50 degrees C, adding fruit pulp enzyme for                enzymatic hydrolysis for 0.5-2 hours, adding                endoprotease, decomposing the protein into small                molecular amino acid, where the enzymatic                hydrolysis time is 0.5-2 hours, improving juice,                (4) centrifuging pulp obtained in the step (3),                separating the juice from the fruit residue,                collecting the juice to obtain juice, (5) adding                pectinase and amylase into the juice, performing                secondary ultrasonic enzymolysis under ultrasonic                condition, performing the enzyme process, (6)                clarifying juice after inactivating enzyme,                subjecting membrane ultrafiltration to obtained                supernatant, (7) ultrafiltration concentrating,                sterilizing the goji clear juice to obtain clear                juice and (8) subjecting goji fruit powder through                superfine crushing, separating fruit residue after                drying
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (1),                  selecting non-rotten, without plant diseases and                  insect pests and impurity goji, goji surface dust                  is washed with clean water, cleaning time is 1-4                  minutes and the washing time is 2-4 times. In the                  step (2), the swelling and rehydrating water is                  water in a ratio of mass ratio of strontium to                  water is 1:(2-5), the heated to 75-90 degrees C                  to maintain for 40-60 minutes, the swelling and                  rehydration sufficiently soften the mash to                  facilitate beating, and the water-soluble                  functional components e.g. polysaccharides in the                  mash are fully leached. In the step (2), the flow                  rate of carbon dioxide in the process of pulping                  is 200-240 ml/minutes. In the step (3), the                  adding amount of mashed fruit enzyme is mashed                  fruit weight of 0.001-0.005 %, the adding amount                  of the protease is 0.001-0.002 % of the pulp                  weight. In the step (4), the pulp is centrifuged                  and centrifuged for centrifugation at a                  centrifugal speed of 3000-5000                  revolutions/minutes, and the centrifugation time                  is 20-40 minutes and the supernatant is collected                  as a mash and the lower pomace is used. In the                  step (v), the adding amount of the pectase is                  0.20-0.40 % of goji fruit, the adding amount of                  amylase is 0.10-0.20 % of goji fruit, the                  inactivating treatment is to heat the digested                  juice after secondary digestion to 85-95 degrees                  C for 30-120 seconds. In the step (5), the                  ultrasonic power is 400-1200w, the temperature is                  30-50 degrees C and the time is 20-30 minutes. In                  the step (6), the membrane filtration uses film                  material is sulfonated polysulfone membrane, the                  cut-off molecular weight is 2000-3000, the                  macromolecule substance of ultrafiltration to                  remove residue, the ultrafiltration temperature                  is 35-45 degrees C, inlet pressure is 2.0-2.5bar                  and outlet pressure is 1.5-2.0bar. In the step                  (8), the drying is hot wind drying, the drying                  temperature is 55-70 degrees C, the drying time                  is 4-6 hours, after drying, the powder is ground                  by ultrafine powder.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108892104-A; CN108892104-B
TI Use of hydrogen fluoride process device by selecting superheated steam for hydrofluoride reaction, applying superheated steam, purging, injecting anhydrous calcium sulfate, breaking fluorite, rectifiying and recovering.
AU YANG S
AE YANG S (YANG-Individual)
GA 201897183X
AB    NOVELTY - Use of hydrogen fluoride process device comprises selecting superheated steam for hydrofluoride reaction, applying superheated steam, purging granular layer moving bed filter, injecting anhydrous calcium sulfate into riser, and passing superheated steam into U-shaped bed body; breaking fluorite, and sending to dissemination tube; separating solid phase particles, transporting through riser through vapor chamber, and returning to reaction chamber through return pipe; transporting solid phase particles through riser in vapor chamber of isostatic chamber; passing superheated steam through filter materials; introducing hydrofluoric gas, washing and condesning; sending into secondary condenser; injecting rude hydrofluoric acid solution into liquid receiving tray, heating, and rectifiying; vaporizing sulfur dioxide, silicon tetrafluoride, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide, and condensing; and recovering hexafluorosilicic acid, hydrogen fluoride and sulfuric acid liquid.
   USE - Method for using hydrogen fluoride process device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Use of hydrogen fluoride process device comprises selecting 300-450 degrees C and 0.1 MPa superheated steam as heat medium and transport medium for hydrofluoride reaction, applying superheated steam to circulating fluidized bed reactor, purging granular layer moving bed filter, injecting anhydrous calcium sulfate particles into riser, and passing superheated steam through cyclone separator into U-shaped bed body; breaking fluorite into 3-5 mm particles, and sending to dissemination tube through screw conveyor; separating solid phase particles by more than 75 mu m by cyclone separator, transporting through riser through vapor chamber, and returning to reaction chamber through return pipe; transporting solid phase particles through riser in vapor chamber of isostatic chamber, and returning to reaction chamber through return pipe to continue the reaction; passing superheated steam through filter materials; introducing hydrofluoric gas at 160 degrees C, washing and condesning; sending into secondary condenser; injecting rude hydrofluoric acid solution into liquid receiving tray, heating, removing sulfuric acid and water, and rectifiying; vaporizing sulfur dioxide, silicon tetrafluoride, carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulfide to form ascending steam, and condensing; and recovering hexafluorosilicic acid, hydrogen fluoride and concentrated sulfuric acid liquid.

PN CN108558333-A
TI Producing flameproof aerated bricks using phosphogypsum and fly ash comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, milling to obtain raw material, placing in kiln to obtain clinker and performing water dissolution.
AU CHEN X
   LIU L
   WANG J
   XIE H
   LI Z
   CHEN J
   ZHUANG C
   JIN K
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 2018779936
AB    NOVELTY - Producing flameproof aerated bricks using phosphogypsum and fly ash comprises e.g. mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, milling to obtain raw material, placing into the roasting kiln to obtain clinker, subjecting clinker to water dissolution, carrying out solid-liquid separation, subjecting precipitate obtained by the solid-liquid separation for flotation, separating sulfide, roasting sulfide, catalytically reacting flue gas generated by roasting with vanadium pentoxide, absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, in liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation adding carbon dioxide, drying precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide, adding aluminum hydroxide, cement, lime, kaolinite, quartz, ammonium persulfate, calcium silicate, polyvinyl alcohol powder, attapulgite and rubber powder into the mixer, mixing uniformly, crushing, grinding to obtain mixed material, adding water, and stirring to obtain slurry.
   USE - The method is useful for producing flameproof aerated bricks using phosphogypsum and fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing flameproof aerated bricks using phosphogypsum and fly ash comprises (i) mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier, milling to obtain raw material, placing into the roasting kiln to obtain clinker, subjecting clinker to water dissolution, and carrying out solid-liquid separation, (ii) subjecting precipitate obtained by the solid-liquid separation for flotation, separating sulfide, roasting sulfide, catalytically reacting flue gas generated by roasting with vanadium pentoxide, and absorbing using concentrated sulfuric acid to obtain sulfuric acid, (iii) in liquid obtained by solid-liquid separation adding carbon dioxide, and drying precipitate to obtain aluminum hydroxide, and (iv) adding aluminum hydroxide, cement, lime, kaolinite, quartz, ammonium persulfate, calcium silicate, polyvinyl alcohol powder, attapulgite and rubber powder into the mixer, mixing uniformly, crushing, grinding to obtain mixed material, adding water, stirring to obtain slurry, pouring slurry into the mold to form brick blank, placing brick blank into the steaming kettle, and steaming to obtain flameproof aerated brick.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the additive is sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or sodium hydroxide, the modifier is anthracite, carbon or coal gangue, and the kiln is industrial rotary kiln, industrial tunnel kiln or industrial vertical kiln. In the step (i), the mass ratio of phosphogypsum and the fly ash is 1.4:1-1.5 (pts. wt.), the addition ratio of the additive is 1:1, according to mol ratio of sodium oxide, aluminum oxide, and ferric oxide, the amount of modifier is 14-26% weight of total raw meal, the roasting temperature is 1060-1360 degrees C, the roasting time is 1.5-4 hours, the water-solids ratio during water dissolution is 4-7:1. In the step (ii), roasting at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours in oxygen-enriched environment under presence of 40-45% sulfide. In the step (iii), drying at 100-120 degrees C until water content is less than 0.5%. In the step (iv), adding 18-25 pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide, 20-25 pts. wt. cement, 15-20 pts. wt. lime, 11-16 pts. wt. kaolinite, 7-10 pts. wt. quartz, 2-3 pts. wt. ammonium persulfate, 15-20 pts. wt. calcium silicate, 1-2 pts. wt. polyvinyl alcohol powder, 7-8 pts. wt. attapulgite and 3-5 pts. wt. rubber powder. In the step (iv), grinding to 80-100 meshes sieve, the mass ratio of the mixture to water is 1:1-1.2, the steaming temperature is 180-190 degrees C preferably 185 degrees C and time is 10-15 hours preferably 12 hours.

PN KR1864304-B1
TI Fire detection method used in apartment house, involves determining whether fire occurs in each household by using smoke density, and carbon dioxide density and fine dust concentration produced by automatic ventilation system.
AU LEE G H
   JEON J Y
   KIM G T
AE KOREA LAND & HOUSING CORP (KOLA-Non-standard)
GA 201846093Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving the sensing value of a fire sensor (200) for each household, carbon dioxide (CO2) density and fine dust concentration produced by a system (100). The smoke density is produced using the sensing value of fire sensor. The determination is made whether the fire occurs in each household using smoke density, CO2 density and fine dust concentration. The detection is made whether the smoke density exceeds predetermined first smoke density threshold for predetermined first time and the CO2 density and fine dust concentration exceeds the predetermined threshold, when the fire occurrence is determined. If the rate of increase of fine dust concentration exceeds the predetermined rate of increase of fine dust concentration compared with time the smoke concentration exceeds the second smoke concentration threshold value which is smaller than first smoke concentration threshold value and the second time period is longer than the first time period.
   USE - Fire detection method used in apartment house.
   ADVANTAGE - The fault alarm is minimized by using the CO2 density and the fine dust concentration calculated on the automatic ventilation system during the fire detection. Thereby the reliability of the automatic fire detection system is improved by detecting the fire using various heterogeneous sensing data.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The detection is made that the smoke concentration exceeds the second smoke concentration threshold value for a second predetermined time or more. The rate of increase of CO2 concentration is relative to time when the smoke concentration exceeds the second smoke concentration threshold value exceeds the preset first increase rate of the CO2 concentration. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fire detecting system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block diagram of the automatic system for detecting fire. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Automatic ventilation system (100)
   Fire dust sensor (120)
   Fire sensor (200)
   Smoke sensor (210)
   Automatic fire detecting device (300)

PN CN108070551-A
TI Separating and culturing human primary liver cell, by processing liver tissue, digesting, centrifuging, detecting cell viability, culturing, identifying cell morphology, and performing enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay and PCR detection.
AU 
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201842376S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for separating and culturing human primary liver cell, involves cutting fresh liver tissue under aseptic condition, sealing, maintaining at 4 degrees C, transporting to the laboratory, placing the tissue in a pre-cooled sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer containing double antibodies, washing for several times to remove blood and connective tissue, performing acupuncture lavage, digesting the cut tissue with granule-bound starch synthase (GBSS) mixed enzyme solution, centrifuging, removing supernatant, taking cell filtrate for detection of cell viability by Trypan Blue staining, after cell count, resuspending in complete culture medium, inoculating the coated flask, placing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide environment, performing identification of cell morphology, performing enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA), and performing real-time (RT)-PCR detection.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and culturing human primary liver cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates and cultures human primary liver cell simply with high cell number and high survival rate, and provides reliable cell resource for experiments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating and culturing human primary liver cell, involves (a) cutting fresh liver tissue under aseptic condition, sealing, maintaining at 4 degrees C and transporting to the laboratory, (b) placing the tissue in a pre-cooled sterile phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) buffer containing double antibodies and washing for several times to remove blood and connective tissue, (c) performing acupuncture lavage with preheated PBS buffer, (d) performing acupuncture lavage with preheated Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS) buffer, (e) performing acupuncture lavage with preheated granule-bound starch synthase (GBSS) mixed enzyme solution, (f) digesting the cut tissue with GBSS mixed enzyme solution, centrifuging, and removing supernatant, (g) taking cell filtrate for detection of cell viability by Trypan Blue staining, (h) after cell count, resuspending in complete culture medium, inoculating the coated flask, and placing at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide environment, (i) performing identification of cell morphology, (j) performing enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA), and (k) performing real-time (RT)-PCR detection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The concentration and pH of the PBS buffer is 0.01 M and 7.2-7.4. The HBSS buffer comprises 1-5% bovine seruym albumin (BSA). The pH of the HBSS buffer is 7.2-7.4. The GBSS mixed enzyme solution (pH 7.2-7.4) contains 0.1-0.5% collagenase IV and 0.1-0.5% neutral protease solution. Preferred Medium: The complete culture medium is Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium, high glucose (H-DMEM) containing 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 u/ml penicillin, 100 u/ml streptomycin, and 4-6 mu g/ml insulin, where the pH of the H-DMEM is 7.4. Preferred Method: The lavage time and volume for GBSS mixed enzyme solution is 10-30 minutes, and 40-60 ml, respectively. The step (h) involves coating the flask with 3-4 mu g/cm2 polylysine and culturing at 37 degrees C in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator.

PN CN107814379-A
TI Preparation of low-defect graphene ribbon sponge using carbon nanotube sponge, involves immersing carbon nanotube sponge in oxidizing mixed solution to obtain graphene sponge, mixing graphene sponge and hydrazine hydrate.
AU PENG Q
   LI Y
   HE X
   QIN Y
   ZHAO X
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 201823905S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-defect graphene ribbon sponge using carbon nanotube sponge, involves reacting hydrogen/argon carrier gas with carbon source and catalyst to form carbon nanotube sponge, adding potassium permanganate to mixed acid of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid to form strong oxidizing mixed solution, immersing carbon nanotube sponge in oxidizing mixed solution to obtain graphene sponge, placing graphene sponge in hydrochloric acid solution, carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying, mixing graphene sponge and hydrazine hydrate to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing low-defect graphene ribbon sponge using carbon nanotube sponge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of low-defect graphene ribbon sponge with high dispersion and mechanical properties, excellent compression performance and high specific surface area.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing low-defect graphene ribbon sponge using carbon nanotube sponge, involves (I) dissolving catalyst ferrocene in liquid carbon source 1,2-dichlorobenzene to obtain catalyst/carbon source solution, injecting the catalyst/carbon source solution into preheating zone of the tube furnace, reacting the hydrogen/argon carrier gas with the carbon source and catalyst into the reaction zone of tube furnace to form carbon nanotube sponge, (II) adding potassium permanganate to mixed acid of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid, ultrasonically dispersing to form strong oxidizing mixed solution, (3) immersing the carbon nanotube sponge in strong oxidizing mixed solution, leaving still at room temperature, vacuuming, removing the carbon nanotube sponge filled with the solution, leaving still to obtain graphene sponge, (4) adding double oxygen droplets into ice water, cooling the graphene sponge to room temperature until bubbles are not generated, then placing the graphene sponge in hydrochloric acid solution, removing impurities, using deionized water to replace hydrochloric acid until the pH range reaches 5-8, using anhydrous ethanol to replace deionized water, (5) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying of graphene ribbon sponge, (6) mixing graphene sponge and hydrazine hydrate in reaction vessel to obtain chemically reduced low defect graphene sponge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of low-defect graphene ribbon sponge is carried out by (1) ultrasonically dissolving the catalyst ferrocene the liquid carbon source 1,2-dichlorobenzene to obtain catalyst/carbon source solution, dissolving 0.1-40 g ferrocene powder in 10-500 ml of 1,2-dichlorobenzene, injecting catalyst/carbon source solution into the preheating zone of the tube furnace using a precision syringe pump, passing the hydrogen/argon carrier gas to reaction zone containing carbon source and catalyst, growing and stacking to form carbon nanotube sponges, preheating at 150-450 degrees C, heating at 700-1000 degrees C in high temperature reaction zone, (2) weighing potassium permanganate, stirring at 100-900 rpm, adding potassium permanganate to mixed acid of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid to obtain mixture (A), stirring the mixture (A) for 10-90 minutes, ultrasonically dispersing at 10-100 kHz for 1-24 hours to form strong oxidizing solution, (3) immersing the carbon nanotube sponge in strong oxidizing solution, leaving still at room temperature for 1-24 hours, vacuuming, removing air, removing the carbon nanotube sponge, heating at 30-90 degrees C for 1-24 hours, vacuuming at -0.1--0.5 MPa, (4) placing 1-30 ml hydrogen peroxide in 100-400 ml ice water, cooling the obtained graphene sponge to room temperature, until no bubble is generated, immersing the graphene sponge in concentration of 1-30 wt.% hydrochloric acid for 1-24 hours, subsequently using deionized water to replace the hydrochloric acid until the pH value reaches 5-8, replacing deionized water with anhydrous ethanol for 1-32 hours, maintaining hydrogen peroxide concentration at 30 wt.%, (5) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical drying to obtain graphene sponge, (6) sealing 0.02-10 g graphene ribbon sponge with 5-40 ml hydrazine hydrate, and carrying out reduction reaction at 50-150 degrees C for 3-50 hours to obtain chemically reduced low defect graphene sponge. The step (4) comprises adding 1-20 ml hydrogen peroxide to 100-300 ml ice water, cooling the graphene sponge to room temperature, until air bubbles are not generated, immersing the graphene sponge in 1-20% concentration of hydrochloric acid for 1-20 hours, subsequently using deionized water to replace the hydrochloric acid until the pH reaches 7-8, replacing the deionized water with anhydrous ethanol for 1-24 hours. The step (5) comprises freeze-drying the graphene ribbon sponge. Preferred Composition: The total volume ratio of potassium permanganate to mixed acid of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid is (0.2-60 g):1 L. The volume ratio of sulfuric acid and phosphoric acid is (2-10):1. The volume ratio of hydrogen and argon is 1:1-1:4. The volume ratio of 10-98 %mass sulfuric acid and 10-85 %mass phosphoric acid is (5-10):1. The concentration of potassium permanganate in the mixture (A) is 5-50 g/L. Preferred Conditions: The preheat zone temperature is maintained at 200-400 degrees C. The high temperature reaction zone is maintained at 800-1000 degrees C. The carrier gas flow rate is maintained at 400-2000 mL/minute. The vacuuming process is carried out at -0.1--0.2 MPa. The step (6) comprises carrying out reduction reaction of graphene ribbon sponge at 70-100 degrees C for 5-24 hours.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN107815351-A
TI Preparation method of natural spice for use on            paper towel, involves drying fresh wild Chrysanthemum            and honeysuckle, using steam fixation, air drying,            crushing, adding solvent to crushed material, heating,            stirring, and evaporating.
AU XU Y
AE ANHUI BILUN LIFE PAPER CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201823884L
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of natural spice                involves weighing fresh wild Chrysanthemum and                honeysuckle, placing in oven, drying, using steam                fixation, air drying, crushing, adding solvent to                the crushed material, heating, stirring and                evaporating for 15-20 minutes, adding 4-6 %mass                coconut oil, refrigerating the mixture mixed with                coconut oil, taking out, placing in a distillation                device, vacuum-distilling, subjecting the distilled                liquid to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction                after 2 times of low temperature distilling.
   USE - Preparation method of natural spice for use on                paper towel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of natural                spice with certain health effects for the skin and                excellent fresh and refreshing effect, which                prevents insect bites in summer and seasonal skin                sensitivity problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation method of natural spice                involves (1) weighing fresh wild Chrysanthemum and                honeysuckle according to mass ratio of (7-8):(2-3),                flatly placing in oven, drying at 30-35 degrees C                for 18-20 hours, using steam fixation for 30-40                seconds, air drying to moisture content of 12-15%,                and crushing to 20-30 mesh, (2) adding 10-12 %mass                solvent to the crushed material, heating at 40-50                degrees C, where the solvent comprises 2-3 wt.%                carboxymethyl cellulose, 3.5-4.5 wt.% sodium                bicarbonate, 4-5 wt.% sodium chloride, 20-25 wt.%                water, and balance amount of ethanol, stirring and                evaporating for 15-20 minutes, and adding 4-6 %mass                coconut oil, and (3) refrigerating the mixture                mixed with coconut oil at 0-5 degrees C for 5-8                hours, taking out at 30-35 degrees C, placing in a                distillation device, vacuum-distilling, subjecting                the distilled liquid to supercritical carbon                dioxide extraction at 15-18 MPa and 45-55 degrees C                for 60-70 minutes after 2 times of low temperature                distilling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature during                  the steam fixation in step (1) is 120-130 degrees                  C. The airflow drying speed in the step (1) is                  2-3 cubic meter/hour. In the step (3), the                  process conditions for the low temperature                  distillation is 0.3-0.6 Pa vacuum degree, 40-50                  degrees C distilling temperature, and 3-5 degrees                  C condensation temperature. The supercritical                  carbon dioxide extraction in step (3) is                  performed at 16 MPa and 50 degrees C for 65                  minutes.

PN CN107711398-A
TI Cultivating Narcissus bulbs comprises e.g. washing Narcissus bulbs using potassium permanganate solution, spraying ethanol, spraying treatment agent, processing in the darkroom and planting in greenhouse soil.
AU JU G
AE BENGBU HONGRUI GARDEN CO LTD (BENG-Non-standard)
GA 201817809B
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Narcissus bulbs comprises (i) washing Narcissus bulbs using potassium permanganate solution, mixing with organic fertilizer, immersing in water and incubating; (ii) spraying ethanol and cutting bulb using sterilized blade; (iii) spraying treatment agent to intercept culture stem block and wound, processing culture block in the darkroom using flash lamp strobe and irradiating infrared light (iv) mixing organic fertilizer and soil in the glass container, wetting, sealing and introducing carbon dioxide and ozone; and (v) planting the sprout tuber in greenhouse soil.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating Narcissus bulbs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating Narcissus bulbs comprises (i) washing Narcissus bulbs using potassium permanganate solution for 3-4 minutes, mixing with organic fertilizer at 4-7 degrees C for 13-16 days, immersing in water and incubating in a thermo-tank for germination to obtain germinated bulb; (ii) cleaning the bulb by spraying 75% ethanol, cutting bulb using sterilized blade to small blocks, where the each bud is of two scales and taking 2.2-2.5 cm upper end of the lower stem as culture block and cleaning the wound using 75% ethanol to obtain the intercept culture stem block; (iii) spraying treatment agent to intercept culture stem block and wound, processing culture block in the darkroom, using flash lamp strobe of intensity 920-950 lux for 16-20 minutes of frequency 15 times/minute and processing using irradiating infrared light of intensity 240-270 lux for 1-2h, (iv) mixing organic fertilizer and soil in a mass ratio of 1:3 in the glass container, adding the processed culture tuber into the soil ,wetting using the treatment agent, sealing, introducing carbon dioxide and ozone into culture at 25-28 degrees C, culturing using fluorescent lamp of intensity 340-380 lux for 20-24 days and increasing the illumination intensity to 710-730 lux for 12-15 days to obtain the sprout tuber; and (v) planting the sprout tuber in greenhouse soil for culturing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the organic fertilizer comprises 20-30 pts. wt. soybean meal, 14-18 pts. wt. maize bran, 11-14 pts. wt. apple residue, 8-10 pts. wt. furfural residue, 6-9 pts. wt. orange peel, 5-8 pts. wt.lemon peel, 4-7 pts. wt. hawthorn leaves, 3-5 pts. wt. bitter melon and 1-3 pts. wt. kelp powder. In the step (iii), the treatment agent comprises 100- 120 pts. wt. water, 7-9 pts. wt. saffron extract, 4-6 pts. wt. Dendrobium extract, 2-4 pts. wt. zinc gluconate, 1-2 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate and 0.4-0.6 pts. wt. colchicine, where the saffron extract and the Dendrobium extract are obtained by adding 4-6 times of water, boiling and concentrating to 1/5 of the original volume. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the germination treatment temperature is 26-29 degrees C for 9-11 hours and the mass concentration of potassium permanganate solution is 0.3-0.5 %. In the step (iv), the intake of carbon dioxide is 4-6 % of container volume, and the ozone is 1-2% of container volume.

TI Removal of carbon dioxide involves supplying            carbon dioxide absorbent to one of lumen side and shell            side of membrane contactor, supplying vapor to other of            lumen side and shell side, and removing carbon dioxide            collected in absorbent.
AU SONG H J
   JI L
   KANG J H
   PARK H
   KANG D W
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 202284536D
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide involves (i)                supplying a carbon dioxide absorbent (C1) that                captures carbon dioxide to one of a lumen side and                a shell side of a membrane contactor, (ii)                supplying vapor to the other of the lumen side and                the shell side, and (iii) removing the carbon                dioxide collected in the absorbent (C1) from the                lumen side and the shell side with the vapor to                prepare a mixed vapor (V1) containing the removed                carbon dioxide and the vapor.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is energy-saving, minimizes the                loss of the absorbent while removing carbon dioxide                from the absorbent by using the membrane contactor                and vapor, and lowers the content of renewable                energy for producing carbon dioxide. The device has                reduced size and can be used at high                temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide removal device, which comprises an                absorbent storage tank for storing and supplying                the absorbent (C1), a vapor generator for                generating and supplying the vapor, and membrane                contactor for removing the carbon dioxide collected                in the absorbent (C1).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: The membrane contactor                  comprises a ceramic hollow fiber membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The vapor is water                  vapor. Preferred Process: The method further                  involves contacting an exhaust gas with a carbon                  dioxide absorbent (C1') to prepare the absorbent                  (C1) before step (i), and cooling the vapor (V1)                  to separate carbon dioxide from the vapor (V1)                  after step (iii).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The removal device                  further comprises a chiller for cooling the vapor                  (V1) to separate the carbon dioxide stripped from                  the vapor (V1), and discharging mixed gas (G1)                  containing the separated carbon dioxide, an                  analyzer for analyzing the carbon dioxide of the                  gas (G1), a vent line for discharging the gas                  (G1) from the chiller to the outside, a pump for                  supplying the absorbent (C1) from the absorbent                  storage tank to the membrane contactor, an                  absorbent treatment tower for producing the                  absorbent (C1) in which the removal device                  contacts the exhaust gas with the absorbent (C1')                  to collect carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas,                  an inert gas supply tank for supplying an inert                  gas to the vapor generator to adjust the pressure                  of the vapor, and a gas flow regulator for                  regulating the pressure of the inert gas                  discharged from the inert gas supply tank.                  Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the                  absorbent storage tank is 30-95 degrees C. The                  temperature of the vapor generator is 95-120                  degrees C. The temperature of the chiller is -20                  degrees C to 30 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The absorbent (C1)                  comprises an amine, an amino acid or its salt,                  ammonium hydroxide, ammonia and/or an ionic                  liquid, preferably piperazine of formula (I) or                  its derivative, hydrazine, monoethanolamine                  (MEA), methyldiethanolamine (MDEA),                  2-amino-2-mMethyl-1-propanol (AMP),                  2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol (AHPD),                  2-amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol (AMPD),                  2-aminomethyl-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol,                  2-amino-1,1,1-ethanetriol, ethylene diamine,                  diethylene triamine, triethylene tetraamine,                  tetraethylene pentamine, diethanolamine,                  triethanolamine, glycine, alanine, beta -alanine,                  valine, cysteine, proline, leucine, isoleucine,                  methionine, tryptophan, phenylalanine, serine,                  threonine, tyrosine, asparagine, glutamine,                  lysine, arginine, histidine, aspartic acid,                  glutamic acid and/or gamma -aminobutyric                  acid.R1,R2 = H, straight 1-9C alkyl or branched                  3-9C alkyl.

TI Power generation system for removing and discharging carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas, has carbon dioxide liquefaction unit that liquefies captured carbon dioxide through heat exchange with liquefied gas supplied from storage tank.

TI Vehicle, has charge port for supplying electric            power of external power supply to battery, carbon            dioxide collection apparatus for collecting carbon            dioxide, and lid for covering charge port and carbon            dioxide extraction opening.

AB    NOVELTY - The vehicle has a charge port (4) for                supplying electric power of an external power                supply to a battery (2). A carbon dioxide                collection apparatus (1) collects carbon dioxide. A                carbon dioxide extraction opening (3) for receiving                the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide                collection apparatus. A lid (5) covers the charge                port and the carbon dioxide extraction opening. An                integrated connection port is provided with a cable                integrated hose that is connected to a charge cable                (41). The charge cable is connected to the charge                port and the carbon dioxide extraction                opening.
   USE - Vehicle i.e. car (from drawings).
   ADVANTAGE - The charge port and the carbon extraction                opening are covered with the lid when charging work                and a carbon dioxide extraction operation are                performed, so that opening/closing movement of the                lid can be suppressed at connection time of of the                chare cable, the charge port and the carbon dioxide                extraction opening, thus reducing burden of an                operator during working and improving operator`s                convenience.

PT P
PN WO2019075206-A1; CN111386146-A; US2020318897-A1
TI Separating carbon dioxide from mixture of carbon            dioxide or component e.g. argon and hydrogen, comprises            e.g. cooling mixture to obtain partially condensed            stream, feeding stream into phase separator, and            splitting formed liquid stream.
AU XU J
AE XU J (XUJJ-Individual)
   XU J (XUJJ-Individual)
GA 201936374Q
AB    NOVELTY - Separating carbon dioxide from a mixture                comprising carbon dioxide and/or at least one                component consisting of hydrogen, nitrogen, argon,                carbon monoxide or methane, where the mixture has a                pressure of greater than 10 (preferably 60-300)                bar, comprises (1) cooling the mixture to obtain a                partially condensed stream, (2) feeding the stream                into a phase separator to produce a carbon                dioxide-depleted gas stream and a carbon                dioxide-rich liquid stream, (3) splitting the                liquid stream from the phase separator into at                least two liquid substreams, (4) heating at least                one of the liquid substreams to form at least one                two-phase substream, and (5) feeding the substream                and the remaining liquid substream(s) into a                distillation column to produce a liquid comprising                substantially carbon dioxide and an overhead vapor                comprising substantially the component, where the                substream with a higher temperature is fed to a                lower location of the column than that of the                substream with a lower temperature.
   USE - The process is useful for separating carbon                dioxide from a mixture comprising carbon dioxide                and/or at least one component consisting of                hydrogen, nitrogen, argon, carbon monoxide or                methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is economical; eliminates or                reduces booster compressor for the same level of                carbon dioxide capture, as are the losses                associated with the pressure reduction of the                liquid carbon dioxide from the phase separator, and                that the hydrogen gas that is separated by the                membrane unit does not need to be cooled and then                heated, thus eliminating the need for heat                exchanger area of those molecules, and the                associated temperature (&#916; T) and pressure                (&#916; p) losses; and is more efficient and                simpler process to reduce the cost of carbon                dioxide rejection or capture from coal gasifier gas                or mixtures of carbon dioxide with lower boiling                components e.g. hydrogen, helium, nitrogen, carbon                monoxide and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a process (p2) for separating carbon dioxide                from a mixture comprising carbon dioxide and at                least one component comprising hydrogen, nitrogen,                argon, carbon monoxide and/or methane, where the                mixture has a pressure of greater than 10                (preferably 60-300) bar, comprising (1a) cooling                the mixture by a single loop refrigeration system                with a mixed refrigerant to obtain a partially                condensed stream, (2a) feeding the partially                condensed stream into a phase separator to produce                a carbon dioxide-depleted gas stream and a carbon                dioxide-rich liquid stream, and (3a) feeding the                carbon dioxide-rich liquid stream into a                distillation column to produce a liquid comprising                substantially carbon dioxide and an overhead vapor                comprising substantially the at least one                component; and
   a process (p3) for separating carbon dioxide                from a mixture comprising carbon dioxide and at                least one of the components consisting of hydrogen,                nitrogen, argon, carbon monoxide and/or methane,                where the mixture has a pressure of greater than 10                (preferably 60-300) bar comprising (1aa) cooling                the mixture to obtain a partially condensed stream,                (2aa) feeding the partially condensed stream into a                phase separator to produce a carbon                dioxide-depleted gas stream and a carbon                dioxide-rich liquid stream, (3aa) feeding the                carbon dioxide-rich liquid stream into a                distillation column to produce an overhead vapor                stream and a liquid comprising substantially carbon                dioxide, (4aa) heating and vaporizing the liquid                comprising substantially carbon dioxide to obtain a                heated and vaporized carbon dioxide gas, (5aa)                further heating the heated and vaporized carbon                dioxide gas to obtain a superheated carbon dioxide                gas, and (6aa) expanding the superheated carbon                dioxide in an expander for power generation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (p1) further                  comprises: cooling the overhead vapor to a                  temperature of &#8804; 15 K above carbon                  dioxide freeze out temperature and partially                  condensing the overhead vapor to form a second                  liquid and a vapor, and feeding the second liquid                  to the top of the distillation column; and                  feeding at least a portion of the overhead vapor                  to a Dephlegmator to obtain a third liquid and a                  vapor, and feeding at least a portion of the                  third liquid to the top of the distillation                  column. In the process (p1), a single loop                  refrigeration system with a mixed refrigerant is                  used to provide at least a portion of                  refrigeration for the cooling of step (1), at                  least a portion of the liquid from the                  distillation column is heated, vaporized, further                  heated, and expanded for power generation. The                  process (p1) further comprises vaporizing at                  least two pressures the liquid comprising                  substantially carbon dioxide to provide at least                  a portion of the refrigeration for the cooling of                  the mixture. In the step (1a), the mixture is                  cooled by the single loop refrigeration system                  with the mixed refrigerant using a refrigeration                  process comprising (a1) compressing a vapor                  refrigerant comprising at least two components in                  a refrigerant compressor to obtain a compressed                  refrigerant, (b1) cooling the compressed                  refrigerant by an external coolant to obtain a                  partially condensed refrigerant, (c1) further                  cooling, condensing, and subcooling the partially                  condensed refrigerant in a heat exchanger to                  obtain a subcooled refrigerant, (d1) reducing the                  pressure of the subcooled refrigerant to obtain a                  reduced pressure refrigerant, (e1) heating and                  vaporizing the reduced pressure refrigerant in                  the heat exchanger of step (c1) to obtain the                  vapor refrigerant and to provide refrigeration                  for the cooling of the mixture and the cooling,                  condensing, and subcooling of the partially                  condensed refrigerant, and (f1) feeding the vapor                  refrigerant to the refrigerant compressor,                  thereby completing the cycle, where the                  temperature of the reduced pressure refrigerant                  from step (d1) is &#8804; 10K lower than the                  temperature of the subcooled refrigerant from                  step (c1). The process (p2) further comprises                  applying compression heat generated in the last                  stage of the refrigerant compressor to the carbon                  dioxide-depleted gas stream and expanding the                  heated carbon dioxide-depleted gas stream in an                  expander for power generation. In the process                  (p3), during off-peak electricity demand hours,                  at least a portion of the liquid comprising                  substantially carbon dioxide from the                  distillation column is stored, while during peak                  electricity demand hours, at least a portion of                  the stored and instantaneously generated liquid                  comprising substantially carbon dioxide are                  heated, vaporized, further heated, and expanded                  in an expander for power generation. The liquid                  comprising substantially carbon dioxide is                  vaporized and/or further heated by exhaust gas of                  a gas turbine and/or the effluent of a water-gas                  shift reactor. The process (p3) further comprises                  applying a gas mixture to a membrane separator to                  obtain the mixture comprising carbon dioxide and                  the component, prior to step (1aa), where the                  membrane separator comprises a membrane                  selectively permeable to hydrogen but is less                  permeable to carbon dioxide. The mixture is                  obtained from gasification of a carbonaceous                  material, and at least a portion of the overhead                  vapor stream from the distillation column is                  heated and then mixed with the mixture obtained                  from the gasification of the carbonaceous                  material to be fed to a water-gas shift reactor                  to convert at least some of the carbon monoxide                  into hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the water-gas                  shift reactor, and the reactor effluent is cooled                  and then dried before being cooled in step (1aa).                  The mixture is obtained from gasification of a                  carbonaceous material, and at least a portion of                  the overhead vapor stream from the distillation                  column is heated and then added to the mixture to                  be cooled in step (1aa). Preferred Components:                  The mixture comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen,                  carbon monoxide and small amounts of inert gas                  components, e.g. nitrogen and argon, preferably,                  the mixture is obtained from gasification of a                  carbonaceous material. The pressure of the                  distillation column is lower than the pressure of                  the carbon dioxide-rich liquid stream coming out                  of the phase separator, and the liquid substream                  to be heated to a higher temperature is let down                  in pressure to a level higher than that of the                  distillation column before it is heated, where at                  least a portion of the carbon dioxide-depleted                  gas stream from the phase separator, after being                  heated, is expanded to a lower pressure to                  generate work, optionally the work provides                  refrigeration for the cooling of the mixture. The                  mixed refrigerant comprises at least one of                  ethane and ethylene, and at least one of                  propylene, propane, butanes and butenes. The                  mixed refrigerant comprises non-2C components                  from a single source, preferably, the mixed                  refrigerant comprises industrial liquefied                  petroleum gas or industrial butanes. The                  refrigerant compressor is a multi-stage                  compressor with a compression ratio in the last                  stage of greater than 2.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
