PN WO2022085789-A1
TI Recovery of carbon dioxide involves absorbing carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide adsorbing/desorbing liquid agent comprising amino-substituted cyclic compound, such that reaction product is precipitated in liquid agent, and desorbing carbon dioxide by heat-treating liquid agent.
AU SEIJI Y
   HIROKI M
   TETSUYA S
   YU F
   JUN H
   GEN K
   SOICHI K
   KAZUSHI A
AE UNIV TOKYO METROPOLITAN PUBLIC CORP (UYTM-C)
GA 202257127D
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of carbon dioxide involves (i-1) absorbing carbon dioxide using a carbon dioxide adsorbing/desorbing liquid agent comprising an amino-substituted cyclic compound (I), such that a reaction product of the compound (I) and carbon dioxide is precipitated in the liquid agent, and (ii-1) desorbing carbon dioxide from the liquid agent by heat-treating the liquid agent.
   USE - Recovery of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently recovers carbon dioxide using liquid having sufficient carbon dioxide adsorbing/desorbing ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of carbon dioxide involves (i-1) absorbing carbon dioxide using a carbon dioxide adsorbing/desorbing liquid agent comprising an amino-substituted cyclic compound (I), such that a reaction product of the compound of formula (I) and carbon dioxide is precipitated in the liquid agent, and (ii-1) desorbing carbon dioxide from the liquid agent by heat-treating the liquid agent.
   m = 0 or 1;
   R1,R2 = alkyl, alkoxy, carboxy, alkoxycarbonyl, formyl, alkylcarbonyl, alkylthio, sulfo, alkoxysulfonyl, nitro, hydroxy, thio, cyano or halo (in which alkyl is optionally substituted);
   p1,p2 = 1 or 2;
   q1 = 0-22 (when m is 0) or 1-10 (when m is 1);
   p1+q1 = 12 or less (when q1 is 0-11) or 11 or less (q1 is 0-10);and
   q2 = 0-10.
   (i) When q1 is 2 or more, two or more R1 are optionally substituted alkyl, and are bonded to form a ring; (ii) when q2 is 2 or more, two or more R2 are optionally substituted alkyl, and are bonded to form a ring; and (iii) two amino with p1 are not arranged at meta positions, when m is 0 and p1 is 2. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for absorbing carbon dioxide; and
   (2) method for desorbing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (ii-1) is performed in the coexistence of a base catalyst. Alternately, the recovery of carbon dioxide involves (i-2) absorbing carbon dioxide using carbon dioxide adsorbing/desorbing liquid agent comprising compound (I), and (ii-2) desorbing carbon dioxide from the liquid agent by heat-treating the liquid agent in the presence of base catalyst. The processes (i-1) and (ii-1) or processes (i-2) and (ii-2) are repeated two or more times. Preferred Compound: The compound (I) is preferably chosen from compounds of formulae (I-1)-(I-4).
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN KR2022135934-A
TI New strain comprising gene i.e. glycine            synthase-reductase pathway (GSRP) are introduced into            carbon dioxide absorbing microorganisms, useful as            composition in kit for removing carbon dioxide having            e.g. hydrogen consumption rate.
AU CHOBYUNGKWAN
   SONG Y
   SHIN J O
   JIN S R
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2022C8081P
AB    NOVELTY - Strain comprising gene including glycine                synthase-reductase pathway (GSRP) are introduced                into carbon dioxide absorbing microorganisms in                which the GSRP does not exist, is new.
   USE - The strain is useful as composition in kit for                removing carbon dioxide having hydrogen consumption                rate, carbon dioxide consumption rate, acetate                production rate and cell growth rate (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The strain indicates higher level than carbon                dioxide absorbing microorganism, and increases                carbon dioxide absorption rate compared to                conventional carbon dioxide absorbing                microorganisms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   producing strain; and
   removing carbon dioxide, comprising culturing                the strain under conditions in which carbon dioxide                is present.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: (claimed). Preferred                  Components: The gene including the GSRP is                  derived from the Clostridium drakeiSL1T strain,                  where the gene includes a glycine cleavage (gcv)                  T gene, a gcvH gene, a gcvPA gene, a gcvPB gene,                  grdX gene, thioredoxin reductase (trx0B gene,                  trxA gene, grdE gene, grdA gene, grdB gene, grdC                  gene and/or grdD gene. The gcvT gene has a fully                  defined sequence of 1107 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO:                  1), as given in the specification, and the gcvH                  gene has a fully defined sequence of 384                  nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 2), as given in the                  specification. The gcvPA gene has a fully defined                  sequence of 1341 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 3), as                  given in the specification. The gcvPB gene has a                  fully defined sequence of 1461 nucleotides (SEQ                  ID NO: 4), as given in the specification. The                  grdX gene has a fully defined sequence of 378                  nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 5), as given in the                  specification, and the trxB gene has a fully                  defined sequence of 921 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO:                  6), as given in the specification, and the trxA                  gene has a fully defined sequence of 333 (SEQ ID                  NO: 7), as given in the specification. The grdE                  gene has a fully defined sequence of 1287                  nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 8), as given in the                  specification. The grdA gene has a fully defined                  sequence of 965 nucleotides (SEQ ID NO: 9), as                  given in the specification. The carbon dioxide                  absorbing microorganism in which the GSRP does                  not exist is Eubacterium limosumstrain. Preferred                  Method: The method is carried out by introducing                  a gene including GSRP into carbon dioxide                  absorbing microorganisms in which GSRP does not                  exist.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details of culture and                genetic analysis of SL1T strain that do not support                claims.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Adsorbent material used for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises complex formed by co-precipitation route using metallic nitrates, and base, where adsorption capacity depends on calcination and recalcination of complex.
AU PONTES BITTENCOURT QUITETE
   DO COUTO FRAGA A
AE PETROBRAS PETROLEO BRASIL SA (PETB-C)
GA 202180544Y
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent material comprises a complex formed by co-precipitation using metallic nitrates, more specifically, nickel and aluminum, and base. The adsorption capacity depends on the calcination and recalcination condition of the complex. The complex when calcined at 500 degrees C has carbon dioxide (CO2) adsorption capacity of 1.12mm CO2/g at a temperature of 25 degrees C and a pressure of 1 bar. The complex has a BET surface area in the range of 0.4 less than ABET less than 80m2/g for nickel aluminate (NiAlO3) samples calcined at temperatures of 1100 degrees C, 900 degrees C and 500 degrees C; and complex has a basicity evaluated at 1.53 mmol CO2/g, in NiAlO3 compounds calcined at 500 degrees C.
   USE - Adsorbent material used for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent material enables the adsorption and desorption cycle of CO2, where CO2 adsorption capacity does not have a direct relationship with the basicity of the presence of complex, has has BET area equal to 94.4 m2/g and optimal capacity for adsorption of dioxide carbon of 1.25 mm CO2/g at a temperature of 25 degrees C and pressure of 1 bar, and adsorbent material is a calcined product of aluminates and/or oxides, more specifically perovskite oxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method or obtaining an adsorbent material, involves carrying out co-precipitation using metal nitrates (Ni, Al) and 14.5 wt.% ammonium hydroxide solution as precipitating agent at a pH of (9-11); washing and drying the samples for overnight in an oven at 120 degrees C obtained after aging for 17 hours; calcinating the samples generated under different conditions of 500 degrees C for 4 hours, 500 degrees C for 4 hours, followed by calcination at 900 degrees C for 5 hours or at 1100 degrees C for 5 hours with static atmosphere; calcinating the samples generated that is NiAlO3 at 1100 degrees C, 500 degrees C, and 900 degrees C, where NiAlO3 samples calcined at 500 degrees C showed excellent adsorption results; and
   (2) method for capturing carbon dioxide using an adsorbent material comprising aluminate and oxides of perovskite type, involves contacting a fluid containing CO2 with an adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide, under conditions that affect the adsorption of CO2 in the calcined aluminate and/or oxide adsorbent, in particular in the perovskite oxide, in which the capture took place through the use of a thermogravimetric scale from Mettler Toledo (TGA/SDTA 851E) at 25-100 degrees C /10 degrees C/minute nitrogen or argon; 100 degrees C-60 minute/nitrogen or argon; 100 degrees C-25 degrees C-10 degrees C/minute/nitrogen or argon; 25 degrees C-150 minute-CO2; and 25 degrees C 150 minute argon or nitrogen.

The textural properties get influenced by the capture of carbon dioxide. The compounds NiAlO3, calcined at 500 degrees C and oCe0.4Ni0.6AlO3, calcined at 500 degrees C showed better adsorption results than reference materials (coal) and MCM-41-AMP. The presence of crystalline phases of NiO and Al2O3 is correlated to the ability to store oxygen and act in oxidation-reduction cycles. The cerium is added to the nickel-aluminum complex. The complex of nickel (Ni), cerium (Ce) and aluminum (Al) is calcined at 500 degrees C, has an adsorption capacity of CO2a1.25 mm CO2/g at a temperature of 25 degrees C and pressure of 1 bar. TI Surface fitting-based method for calibrating            carbon dioxide gas sensor, involves taking candidate            fitting surface model corresponding to minimum sum of            absolute values as calibration fitting surface model of            gas sensor to be calibrated.
AU LV J
   MAO K
   QIU J
   CHI K
AE HANGZHOU MULTIIR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 202167961V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring (S1) first                acquisition voltages of each initial gas sensor.                The normalization processing is performed (S2) on                each first acquisition voltage of the initial gas                sensor to obtain first processing data                corresponding to each first acquisition voltage.                The surface fitting is performed (S3). The second                acquisition voltages of the gas sensor to be                calibrated are acquired (S4). The normalization                processing is performed (S5) on each second                acquisition voltage of the gas sensor to be                calibrated. The second processed data and the                ambient temperature corresponding to the second                processed data are inputted (S6) into each                candidate fitting surface model to obtain an output                value corresponding to each candidate fitting                surface model. The candidate fitting surface model                corresponding to the minimum sum of absolute values                is taken (S7) as the calibration fitting surface                model of the gas sensor to be calibrated.
   USE - Surface fitting-based method for calibrating                carbon dioxide gas sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the calibration of the gas                sensor to be calibrated only by acquiring candidate                fitting surface models by adopting a large amount                of data and then acquiring a small amount of second                acquisition voltage of a newly generated gas                sensor, so that each newly generated gas sensor is                not required to independently establish a data                model according to a large amount of sample data of                the gas sensor to be calibrated, and reduces the                time, labor and material resources required by                calibration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a surface                fitting-based system for calibrating carbon dioxide                gas sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a                surface fitting-based method for calibrating carbon                dioxide gas sensor. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
   Step for acquiring first acquisition voltages                of each initial gas sensor (S1)
   Step for performing normalization processing                on each first acquisition voltage of the initial                gas sensor to obtain first processing data                corresponding to each first acquisition voltage                (S2)
   Step for performing surface fitting according                to the first processing data, the ambient                temperature and the carbon dioxide concentration of                each initial gas sensor to obtain a candidate                fitting surface model corresponding to each initial                gas sensor (S3)
   Step for acquiring multiple second                acquisition voltages of the gas sensor to be                calibrated (S4)
   Step for performing normalization processing                on each second acquisition voltage of the gas                sensor to be calibrated to obtain second processing                data corresponding to each second acquisition                voltage (S5)
   Step for inputting the second processed data                and the ambient temperature corresponding to the                second processed data into each candidate fitting                surface model to obtain an output value                corresponding to each candidate fitting surface                model (S6)
   Step for taking the candidate fitting surface                model corresponding to the minimum sum of absolute                values as the calibration fitting surface model of                the gas sensor to be calibrated (S7)

PN CN111875483-A
TI Extracting lignan compounds from Schisandra            comprises implementing the method in a supercritical            carbon dioxide extraction device assisted by magnetic            stirring, which specifically includes turning on a            low-temperature thermostat.
AU WANG Z
   BI J
   LI Q
   CHANG S
   YANG Q
   YAN X
AE UNIV CHANGCHUN TECHNOLOGY (UYGL-C)
GA 2020B1963F
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lignan compounds from Schisandra                comprises implementing the method in a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device                assisted by magnetic stirring, which specifically                includes turning on a low-temperature thermostat                and setting the temperature to 3-8 degrees C and                opening the high-purity air valve and high-purity                carbon dioxide valve when the set temperature is                reached. The high-purity air and CO2 is injected to                fill the system with gas, and at the same time, the                Schisandra sample powder is weighed in the                extraction kettle, and absolute ethanol is added,                the seal is tightened, and then the extraction                kettle is put into a heat-collecting thermostatic                magnetic stirrer to preheat. The inlet valve and                outlet valve is opened, CO2 is let in to exhaust                the air in the extraction kettle, then the outlet                valve is closed to check the air tightness of the                device, the booster pump and the heat-collecting                thermostatic magnetic stirrer is turned on at the                same time.
   USE - Method for extracting lignan compounds from                Schisandra.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to extract lignan compounds                from Schisandra, which improve the extraction                efficiency, and enhances the solubility and                selectivity of supercritical CO2 during the                extraction process and reduces the extraction                pressure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting lignan compounds from Schisandra                comprises implementing the method in a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device                assisted by magnetic stirring, which specifically                includes turning on a low-temperature thermostat                and setting the temperature to 3-8 degrees C and                opening the high-purity air valve and high-purity                carbon dioxide valve when the set temperature is                reached. The high-purity air and CO2 is injected to                fill the system with gas, and at the same time, the                Schisandra sample powder is weighed in the                extraction kettle, and absolute ethanol is added,                the seal is tightened, and then the extraction                kettle is put into a heat-collecting thermostatic                magnetic stirrer to preheat. The inlet valve and                outlet valve is opened, CO2 is let in to exhaust                the air in the extraction kettle, then the outlet                valve is closed to check the air tightness of the                device, the booster pump and the heat-collecting                thermostatic magnetic stirrer is turned on at the                same time, the speed is set to 25-100r/min, when                the pressure in the extraction kettle reaches                3-12MPa, pressurizing is stopped, and extracted at                35-80 degrees C 15-180min. The extraction kettle is                cooled to room temperature, the extraction kettle                outlet valve, and the extraction kettle is opened,                and the extraction kettle is flushed with ethanol.                The solid-liquid mixture is transferred to a                centrifuge tube, centrifuged to deposit the                Schisandra sample powder on the bottom of the                centrifuge tube, discarded, and all the supernatant                is taken out for use. The supernatant is                concentrated under reduced pressure to form an                extract, and the extract is vacuum dried and                pulverized to form a powder to obtain a Schisandra                lignan compound extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mass (g) of the                  schisandra sample powder, and the volume (mL) of                  absolute ethanol is 1:2. The pressure in the                  extraction kettle is 9 MPa. Preferred Components:                  The lignan compounds in the schisandra are                  schisandrin A, gomixin D, schisandrin B,                  schisandrin, schisandrin A, anwuzhisu,                  schisandrin B and schisandrin C.

PT P
PN EP3572140-A2; EP3572140-A3; JP2019205997-A; EP3572140-B1
TI Carbon dioxide conversion system used for environment e.g. deep space vehicles or in-situ resource utilization in Mars atmosphere comprises gas-liquid contactor-separator, electrochemical conversion cell, and cleaned ionic liquid storage.
AU KAMIRE R
   YATES S
AE HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
   HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
   HONEYWELL INT INC (HONE-C)
GA 2019992689
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide (CO2) conversion system (100) comprises a first gas-liquid contactor-separator downstream of the environment (140); an electrochemical conversion cell (130) downstream of the first gas-liquid contactor-separator; and a cleaned ionic liquid storage (105) intermediate the first gas-liquid contactor-separator and the electrochemical conversion cell.
   USE - The carbon dioxide conversion system is used for an environment, which is ambient gas or closed to ambient gas (claimed). The environment is e.g. deep space vehicles, spacecraft used in long-duration missions, specifically, space stations and spacecraft and habitats used in and beyond low earth orbit, aircraft or submarines. The system is used for In-situ Resource Utilization (ISRU) if the CO2 source is the Mars atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The system removes CO2 from a supply gas and converts it into oxygen. It operates independent of gravity, uses ionic liquids (ILs), which act as liquid CO2 sorbents, where in contrast to solid sorbents, liquid CO2 sorbents can be more easily exchanged in space, while continuous flow processing has the potential to afford a more robust system and a lower overall mass. Select ILs have high capacities for CO2, chemical and electrochemical stabilities, compatibilities with humidity in the gas stream,and negligible vapor pressures. The capacity of IL for both CO2 and water allows for dual function CO2 and humidity removal, while the chemical stability of both 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate and 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate makes repeated absorption/desorption, temperature cycling, and electrochemical cycling possible. The lack of vapor pressure makes ILs particularly suited to the closed environments in space where any risk of air contamination or loss of liquids by evaporation outside the environment must be avoided. If the desired target of ISRU is life support, CO2 can be electrolyzed to CO and O2. If the target is fuel generation for propulsion, CO2 and water can be electrolyzed to CO, H2, and/or methane (CH4) and O2. CO and H2 can be further converted to CH4 at a second catalyst, or electrochemically generated CH4 could be utilized directly. The O2 product can be stored for use when burning the fuel. The system also enables water electrolysis to H2 and O2 in the same cell as CO2 transformation. Water electrolysis in the same electrolysis cell(s) can eliminate the need for the current O2 generation cell stack. The system may significantly decrease the amount of O2 (and/or water) that will be required to be brought on each space mission. This saves both space on the vessel and launch weight, and may require less power than alternative CO2 conversion technologies for recovery of the same amount of O2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic diagram of carbon dioxide conversion system.
   Carbon dioxide conversion system (100)
   Scrubber-separator (102)
   Cleaned ionic liquid storage (105)
   Electrochemical conversion cell (130)
   Environment (140)

PN CN109704382-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises e.g. washing, pulverizing, classifying the limestone raw materials, calcining, recovering exhaust gas, aging, adding polyethylene glycol and stirring.
AU HE F
   WANG Y
   HA Y
   ZHONG S
   ZHANG H
AE SICHUAN LONGMANGLIN CHEM CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201942321K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises (i) washing, pulverizing and classifying the limestone raw materials to obtain uniform size limestone particles, (ii) calcining the limestone particles at 1000-1300 degrees C to obtain calcium oxide, (iii) recovering exhaust gas generated during calcination and obtaining purified carbon dioxide gas by using a membrane separation technology, (iv) according to the ratio of calcium oxide and deionized water 1:5-1:10, stirring calcium oxide and deionized water and mixing for digestion for 10-14 hours, to obtain a calcium hydroxide suspension, (v) aging treatment of calcium hydroxide suspension 1-2 hours, (vi) adding polyethylene glycol as a crystal form controlling agent to the calcium hydroxide suspension after aging, where the amount of the crystal form controlling agent is 2-5 g/l, and the solution is uniformly mixed by stirring, and the stirring speed is 1000-1300 revolutions/minute for 15-30 minutes to obtain a mixed solution A.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium carbonate whiskers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, realizes controlling temperature, pressure, pH, crystal form controlling agent concentration and carbon dioxide concentration, ensures quality of the product, reduces air pollution and production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing calcium carbonate whiskers, comprises (i) washing, pulverizing and classifying the limestone raw materials to obtain uniform size limestone particles, (ii) calcining the limestone particles at 1000-1300 degrees C to obtain calcium oxide, (iii) recovering exhaust gas generated during calcination and obtaining purified carbon dioxide gas by using a membrane separation technology, (iv) according to the ratio of calcium oxide and deionized water 1:5-1:10, stirring calcium oxide and deionized water and mixing for digestion for 10-14 hours, and the stirring speed is 1000-1300 revolutions/minute to obtain a calcium hydroxide suspension, (v) aging treatment of calcium hydroxide suspension 1-2 hours, (vi) adding polyethylene glycol as a crystal form controlling agent to the calcium hydroxide suspension after aging, where the amount of the crystal form controlling agent is 2-5 g/l, and the solution is uniformly mixed by stirring, and the stirring speed is 1000-1300 revolutions/minute for 15-30 minutes to obtain a mixed solution A, (vii) under the stirring condition, introducing the prepared carbon dioxide gas into the bottom of the mixed solution A, the carbon dioxide in an amount of 60-100 ml/min, adjusting the pH to 9-11, and controlling the temperature at 80-90 degrees C, where the pressure is 0.1-0.3 MPa, the stirring speed is 1000-1300 revolutions/minute, and stirring the mixture until the calcium carbonate whisker is completely precipitated to obtain the calcium carbonate whisker semi-finished product, and (viii) separating the semi-finished product of calcium carbonate whisker, washing and drying to obtain a finished product of calcium carbonate whisker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the crushing of the limestone may be jaw crusher, a rotary crusher, a roller crusher or a impact crusher. In the step (ii), a rotary kiln is used as a calcination vessel, and anthracite is a source of heat. In the step (iii), the outlet port of the rotary kiln is connected to the intake port of the carbon dioxide purification unit. In the step (vi), the polyethylene glycol is chemically pure. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), heating deionized water to 80-90 degrees C before the calcium oxide digestion. In the step (viii), the drying temperature is 80-90 degrees C.

TI Atmospheric carbon dioxide extractor assembly            comprises airflow chamber containing adsorbent            composite metal-organic framework, and source of            electromagnetic radiation associated with the airflow            chamber, where in operation composite metal-organic            framework adsorbs carbon dioxide.
AU GILLESPIE R
AE SOUTHERN GREEN GAS LTD (SGRE-Non-standard)
GA 2022A85529
AB    NOVELTY - Atmospheric carbon dioxide extractor assembly                (10) comprises an airflow chamber (12) containing                an adsorbent composite metal-organic framework (14)                produced by additive manufacturing, a source of                electromagnetic radiation (16) associated with the                airflow chamber, where in operation the composite                metal-organic framework adsorbs carbon dioxide from                air flowing through the airflow chamber and the                adsorbed carbon dioxide is, under the influence of                electromagnetic radiation generated by the source                of electromagnetic radiation, desorbed from the                composite metal-organic framework for extraction of                the desorbed carbon dioxide from the air. The                source of electromagnetic radiation is in the form                of a microwave device which emits the                electromagnetic radiation for desorption of the                adsorbed carbon dioxide from the composite                metal-organic framework. The microwave device emits                the electromagnetic radiation at a wavelength                spectrum including infrared radiation, and                microwave radiation.
   USE - Atmospheric carbon dioxide extractor                assembly.
   ADVANTAGE - The atmospheric carbon dioxide extractor                assembly utilizes electromagnetic radiation or                resistive heating in effectively desorbing the                adsorbed carbon dioxide from the composite                metal-organic framework, and lends itself to                powering solely by renewable energy sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method of extracting carbon dioxide from                air;
   a method for fabricating an atmospheric carbon                dioxide extractor;
   an atmospheric carbon dioxide extractor                cartridge; and
   a method of constructing the atmospheric                carbon dioxide extractor cartridge.

TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide and air combined cycle solar power generation system, has compressor whose outlet is connected with low temperature inlet of regenerator, and air turbine whose outlet is connected with inlet of heat collector.

AU XUE F
   SU J
   LIU Q
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 2020772361
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises e.g. spray tower (1), centrifugal dehydrator (3), screw feeding device (19) and alkaline material grinding device (21). The bottom end of the spray tower is a cone-shaped structure, the middle of the bottom end of the spray tower is fixedly connected with a first pipe (2) is fixedly connected with A solenoid valve, the bottom end of the first pipe is fixedly connected with a centrifugal dehydrator. A second pipe is fixedly connected to the right side of the centrifugal dehydrator and a first water pump is fixedly connected to the second pipe. The end of the second pipe is far away from the centrifugal dehydrator is fixedly connected with the right bottom end of the spray tower. A motor is fixedly connected to the lower end of the left outer wall of the spray tower, the power output end of the motor is fixedly connected to a rotating shaft and the right end of the rotating shaft penetrates into inside the spray tower and the right end of the rotating shaft.
   USE - The device is useful for treating calcium-based alkali residue by using pure carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device: has reasonable structure; provides good treatment effect on calcium-based alkali residue; and production method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Device comprises spray tower (1), centrifugal dehydrator (3), screw feeding device (19) and alkaline material grinding device (21). The bottom end of the spray tower is a cone-shaped structure, the middle of the bottom end of the spray tower is fixedly connected with a first pipe (2) is fixedly connected with A solenoid valve, the bottom end of the first pipe is fixedly connected with a centrifugal dehydrator. A second pipe is fixedly connected to the right side of the centrifugal dehydrator and a first water pump is fixedly connected to the second pipe. The end of the second pipe is far away from the centrifugal dehydrator is fixedly connected with the right bottom end of the spray tower. A motor is fixedly connected to the lower end of the left outer wall of the spray tower, the power output end of the motor is fixedly connected to a rotating shaft and the right end of the rotating shaft penetrates into inside the spray tower and the right end of the rotating shaft is rotatably connected with the right inner wall of the spray tower and the outer wall of the rotating shaft is uniformly fixed and connected with a multiple of blades. The lower part of the left outer wall of the spray tower is located at one end of the motor is fixedly connected to the third pipe and the other end of the third pipe penetrates the spray tower upper end of the left outer wall enters into the spray tower and the other end of the third pipe is fixedly connected with a water outlet pipe and the bottom end of the water outlet pipe is fixedly connected with a spray head. A second water pump is fixedly connected to the third pipe and a mist eliminator is fixedly connected to the inner top of the spray tower. A carbon dioxide input pipe is fixedly connected through the top middle of the spray tower and the bottom end of the carbon dioxide input pipe is located below the spray head and the bottom end of the carbon dioxide input pipe is fixedly connected with an air outlet pipe and the bottom end of the air outlet pipe is fixedly connected with an air outlet nozzle. The bottom end of the alkaline material grinding device is fixedly connected to the feed port of the screw feeding device through a fifth pipe. The discharge port of the screw feeding device is fixedly connected to the upper end of the right outer wall of the spray tower through a fourth pipe. A carbon dioxide concentration detector and a water inlet pipe are fixedly connected to the middle of the right outer wall of the spray tower from top to bottom. A pH value detection device is fixedly connected to the bottom end of the left outer wall of the spray tower. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for treating calcium-based alkali slag using pure carbon dioxide, comprising (i) adding the calcium-based alkali slag into the alkaline material grinding device for grinding, passing the screw feeding device through the fifth pipe and the screw feeding device through the fourth pipe and transporting the ground calcium-based alkali slag particles into the spray tower; (ii) opening the valve on the water inlet pipe, injecting water into the spray tower after the calcium-based alkali slag particles and water are injected into the spray tower, turning on the motor and the motor drives the shaft to rotate, the rotating shaft drives the blades to rotate, stirring the calcium-based alkali slag particles and water using the rotating blades to form a calcium-based alkali slag slurry, turning on the second water pump and the second water pump and transporting the calcium base slag slurry into the outlet pipe; (iii) spraying from the spray head, falling the calcium-based alkali slag slurry finally into the lower part of the spray tower, stirring and pumping continuously to form a circulation; (iv) transporting the carbon dioxide gas into the spray tower through the carbon dioxide input pipeline, passing the carbon dioxide gas into the gas outlet pipe and spraying evenly from the multiple gas outlet nozzles; (v) spraying the uniformly sprayed carbon dioxide gas is in full contact with the calcium-based alkali slag lye from the shower head and reacting the carbon dioxide gas with the calcium-based alkali slag, where the chemical equation of the reaction given as specified and removing other gases from the gas discharge pipe after removing moisture through the demister; (vi) using the carbon dioxide concentration detector to detect the concentration of carbon dioxide gas, and the pH value detection device to detect the pH value of the calcium base slag slurry, when the pH value reaches the required value, stopping the input of carbon dioxide, stopping the work of the motor and the second water pump, opening the solenoid valve, passing the calcium base slag slurry into the centrifugal dehydrator from the first pipe, the centrifugal dehydrator separates the calcium-based alkali slag slurry from slag and water, pumping the water into the spray tower by the first water pump through the second pipe, using continuously; and (vii) discharging the reaction product calcium carbonate through the slag discharge pipe and injecting the spray tower continuously to water and calcium-based alkali residue particles for the next round of calcium-based alkali residue treatment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of device.

PT P
PN KR2020129647-A; KR2232149-B1
TI Membrane-based separation of carbon dioxide involves forming carbon dioxide-lean and rich retention and permeate stream, performing gas/liquid separation of cooled permeate stream, and recovering high-purity carbon dioxide liquid stream.
AU KIM J
   HOON L S
AE UNIV HANYANG IUCF-HYU (UHYG-C)
GA 2020B5023M
AB    NOVELTY - Membrane-based separation and capturing of carbon dioxide in a gas mixture involves providing a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture generated by combustion of a fuel and an oxygen-containing gas in a gas turbine, separating carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture and recirculating to the gas turbine, cooling the compressed gas mixture, transferring the cooled gas mixture to a separation membrane module to form a carbon dioxide-lean retention stream (RS1) and a carbon dioxide-rich permeate stream (PS1), compressing the permeate stream (PS1) and transferring to the heat exchanger and cooling by heat exchange, transferring the cooled permeate stream (PS1) to a distillation column and performing gas/liquid separation as a bottom stream as a high-purity carbon dioxide liquid stream, and separating the flow into a low-purity carbon dioxide-containing gaseous mixture, and recovering the high-purity carbon dioxide liquid stream.
   USE - Membrane-based separation and capturing of carbon dioxide in gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces inefficiency and the amount of electric energy required for the operation of the entire process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Membrane-based separation and capturing of carbon dioxide in a gas mixture involves (a) providing a carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture generated by combustion of a fuel and an oxygen-containing gas in a gas turbine and discharging from a gas turbine, (b) separating a portion of the carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture and recirculating to the gas turbine while compressing the remaining unrecirculated, (c) transferring the compressed gas mixture to a heat exchanger for cooling, (d) transferring the cooled gas mixture to a separation membrane module having selective permeability to carbon dioxide to form a carbon dioxide-lean retention stream (RS1) and a carbon dioxide-rich permeate stream (PS1), (e) compressing the permeate stream (PS1) and transferring to the heat exchanger in a pressurized state and cooling by heat exchange, (f) transferring the cooled permeate stream (PS1) to a distillation column and performing gas/liquid separation as a bottom stream as a high-purity carbon dioxide liquid stream, and separating the flow into a low-purity carbon dioxide-containing gaseous mixture, and (g) recovering the high-purity carbon dioxide liquid stream. The carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture discharged from the gas turbine in step (a) comprises less than 5 mol% carbon dioxide when some of the carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is not recycled in step (b). On the other hand, when a portion of the carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is recycled, the carbon dioxide concentration is increased by 0.1-20 mol% compared to the case of not recycled, and the cooling required for the process is performed through heat exchange in the heat exchanger. The separation membrane-based process supply cooling heat according to regasification of liquefied natural gas (LNG).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of gas mixture recycled to gas turbine/gas mixture not recycled is 0.2-1. The carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture discharged from the gas turbine preferably comprises 5.5-8 mol% carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide-rich permeate stream (PS1) comprises 20-70 mol%, preferably 20-55 mol% carbon dioxide, and the carbon dioxide-rich permeate stream (PS2) comprises 50-90 mol% carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide-lean retention stream (RS1) comprises 1-10 mol% carbon dioxide.

   GUO X
   BU J
   PENG P
   HAN H
   YANG Y
   DONG M
   TU H
   LI L
   LIAO M
   GU Y
   YI R
   SHI Y
   XIA L
   CHEN H
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWESTERN PETROLEUM (UYSW-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU SAILU PETROLEUM TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 201992479J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dense oil and gas reservoir using supercritical carbon dioxide thickener comprises e.g. (i) adding perfluorooctylethanol and p-toluenesulfonyl chloride to the three-necked flask with the molar ratio of 1:1.1-1.2, adding pyridine and stirring uniformly, keeping the temperature at 0-20 degrees C, stirring uniformly, reacting for 3 hours, adding filter paper and dispersing by ultrasonic wave for 1 hour, removing unreacted p-toluenesulfonyl chloride and filter paper, removing the pyridine by repeating the washing with 15% dilute hydrochloric acid to obtain white solid, washing for multiple times with distilled water and drying under vacuum at 40 degrees C for 3 hours, (ii) adding (N-tert-butoxycarbonyl)serine and p-toluene perfluorooctane sulfonate with the molar ratio of 2.1-2.2:1 to three-necked flask, adding potassium carbonate and N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring uniformly, heating up to 40 degrees C for 4 hours, washing with distilled water for multiple times, and vacuum drying.
   USE - The thickener is useful for fracturing and chemical flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - The thickener: increases carbon dioxide viscosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing dense oil and gas reservoir using supercritical carbon dioxide thickener comprises (i) adding perfluorooctylethanol and rho -toluenesulfonyl chloride to the three-necked flask with the molar ratio of 1:1.1-1.2, adding pyridine and stirring uniformly, keeping the temperature at 0-20 degrees C, stirring uniformly, reacting for 3 hours, adding filter paper and dispersing by ultrasonic wave for 1 hour, removing unreacted rho -toluenesulfonyl chloride and filter paper, removing the pyridine by repeating the washing with 15% dilute hydrochloric acid to obtain white solid, washing for multiple times with distilled water and drying under vacuum at 40 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain intermediate product of p-toluene perfluorooctyl sulfonate, (ii) adding (N-tert-butoxycarbonyl)serine and p-toluene perfluorooctane sulfonate with the molar ratio of 2.1-2.2:1 to three-necked flask, adding potassium carbonate and N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring uniformly, heating up to 40 degrees C for 4 hours, washing with distilled water for multiple times, vacuum drying to obtain intermediate product of tert-butyl 1,3-bis(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,9,9,10,10,10-heptadecafluorodecyloxy)propan-2-ylcarbamate, (iii) adding the mixed solution of dichloromethane and trifluoroacetic acid to the three-necked flask with the ratio of 2:1, adding tert-butyl 1,3-bis(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,9,9,10,10,10-heptadecafluorodecyloxy)propan-2-ylcarbamate to the above mixed solution, reacting the mixture at constant temperature of 45 degrees C for 2 hours, adding dichloromethane for 3 times to obtain organic layer, drying the organic layer by anhydrous sodium sulfate and adding 1-6 isocyanate, the molar ratio of 1,6-hexanediisocyanate and tert-butyl 1,3-bis(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,9,9,10,10,10-heptadecafluorodecyloxy)propan-2-ylcarbamate is 1:2.05-2.10, carrying out the reaction at 50 degrees C for 2 hours, washing the crude product for multiple times with mixed solvent of ethyl acetate and benzene, filtering and drying in vacuum to obtain final product of 1,1'-(hexane-1,6-diyl)bis(3-(1,3-bis(3,3,4,4,5,5,6,6,7,7,8,8,9,9,10,10,10-heptadecafluorodecyloxy)propan-2-yl)urea). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of supercritical carbon dioxide thickener for preparing dense oil and gas reservoir, where the above 1, 2, and 3 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide thickeners are respectively added to the high temperature and high pressure closed system in which the supporting device is installed, introducing 99 wt.% , 98 wt.%, 97 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide into the closed system through the injection valve, rotating the rotor, adjusting the system pressure and temperature values, and completely stirring to completely dissolve the thickener, i.e. complete the preparation of the mixed system, turning on the rheometer, setting the pressure value to 28 MPa, and the temperature at 60 degrees C, the viscosity of the mixed system is increased by 96-163 times compared with the viscosity of the unthickened supercritical carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

PN CN115031228-A
TI Method for determining air leakage amount of            oxygen-enriched combustion boiler, involves detecting            obtained carbon dioxide concentration, condensed water            vapour amount, discharging smoke amount and recycling            smoke amount.
AU LI Y
   ZHAO Z
   LI J
   SUN Y
   CHENG T
AE STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER RES INST CO (SGCC-C)
GA 2022B9694B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining smoke exhaust                amount of base coal according to the carbon dioxide                concentration obtained by detecting the obtained                carbon dioxide concentrations according to a                received base coal parameter and the smoke                re-circulation extraction point. The flue gas                recirculation extraction points of the target                device are detected to obtain the obtained water                vapor concentration. The condensed water vapor                amount corresponding to unit quality received by                the received coal quality received from the                received carbon dioxide is obtained. Oxygen                injection quantity of the corresponding base coal                is received. Corresponding total air leakage volume                and oxygen injection amount of a corresponding                target device are obtained. Corresponding carbon                dioxide amount and amount of water vapor, condensed                vapor, discharging smoke amount and recycled smoke                amount are determined.
   USE - Method for determining air leakage amount of                an oxygen-enriched combustion boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables making determination of the                air leakage amount of the target device in the                oxygen-enriched combustion boiler become feasible,                and making the needed device number less when                determining the air-leakage amount of target                device, so as to effectively reduce the cost of                determining air-flowage amount in the combustion                boiler in an efficient manner. The method allows a                user to collect primary air-injection oxygen                parameter and secondary-air injection oxygen                parameter when determining recirculation smoke                amount, drying air injection oxygen parameters and                smoke-recirculation extraction point to detect the                obtained oxygen concentration, thus effectively                reducing the number and scale of measuring                instrument needed by determining unit quality                received corresponding to the base coal-circulating                flue gas amount, and hence reducing the cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) an                oxygen-enriched combustion boiler air leakage                amount determining device; (2) a computer device;                (3) a computer readable medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for determining air leakage amount of an                oxygen-enriched combustion boiler. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).

PN CN114966856-A
TI Method for carbon sequestration site selection            based on multi-band seismic data, involves making            injected carbon dioxide free phase storage under high            density under sealing condition of fracture zone side            compact carbonate rock.
AU SHAN X
   ZHENG W
   ZHANG W
   ZHANG J
   TIAN F
AE INST GEOLOGY & GEOPHYSICS CHINESE ACAD S (CAGG-C)
GA 2022B5477J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining original seismic                data and logging data of known well site. A                three-dimensional variance attribute body is                calculated. A deposition stable area is defined. A                virtual well location is selected based on the                deposition stable area. A stable stratum wave                impedance data body is obtained through virtual                well wave impedance interpolation. A difference                between the stable strata wave impedance data body                and a wave impedance inversion data body is                determined. A solution reservoir interpretation                model is obtained. A migration space and a storage                space are analyzed after carbon dioxide injection.                A closed structure with a certain capacity and good                carbon dioxide migration environment is searched.                An injected carbon dioxide free phase storage under                high density under a fracture zone side compact                carbonate rock and a mud top part layer sealing                condition is selected.
   USE - Method for selecting a carbon sequestration                site based on multi-band seismic data. Used in oil                and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving the seismic data                effective frequency band, seismic data for                underground geologic detail characterization                capability, and improving the accuracy of the                solution reservoir interpretation model. The method                allows analyzing and depositing the stable region                wave impedance and fault, thus improving the                precision of the model, and solving the problem                that the fracture zone near the development of the                broken solution distribution is complex, difficulty                of accurately locating and identifying the space                geometrical structure of carbon sealing storage                tank, so as to assist the problem of carbon dioxide                sequestration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (1) a carbon sequestration site                selection system based on multi-band seismic data;                (2) an electronic device; and (3) a computer                readable storage medium.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flow diagram of a                method for selecting a carbon sequestration site.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

TI Zinc-based metal organic framework used as absorbing material in carbon dioxide capture or natural gas purification, comprises two types of alpha and beta types, compound is a ligand, carboxylic acid groups in the ligand are all involved.
AU ZHANG G
   GUO R
   LU Z
AE UNIV HOHAI (UYHO-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Zinc-based metal organic framework comprises two types of alpha type and beta type. The compound (I) is a ligand. (CN113583250A-001), where R represents any one of methyl, methoxy, amino, hydroxyl or trifluoromethyl. PT P
PN CN113149811-A
TI Adjustment of methanol synthesis involves reacting raw material gas to obtain methanol and exhaust gas, where raw material gas and exhaust gas contain hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and has setting adjustment coefficient to preset target value, automatically clearing and recalculating.
AU LU L
   NI A
   ZHANG J
   LIU H
   ZHANG Y
   JIANG X
   FENG C
   LEI C
AE CHINA SHENGHUA COAL TO LIQUID & CHEM CO (SHGR-C)
   GUONENG YULIN CHEM CO LTD (GUON-Non-standard)
GA 202191019A
AB    NOVELTY - Adjustment of methanol synthesis involves reacting raw material gas under the conditions of methanol synthesis to obtain methanol and exhaust gas, where the raw material gas and exhaust gas contain hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where the setting adjustment coefficient (B) satisfying specific equation, when adjustment coefficient is equal to target value within 2-5 hours, it will be automatically cleared and recalculated, and when adjustment coefficient is not equal to the target value, recording the adjustment coefficient (B), automatically clearing it, and recalculating.
   USE - Adjustment of methanol synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adjustment of methanol synthesis to ensure stable operation, and effectively improve the methanol selection rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Adjustment of methanol synthesis involves reacting raw material gas under the conditions of methanol synthesis to obtain methanol and exhaust gas, where the raw material gas and exhaust gas contain hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where the setting adjustment coefficient (B) satisfying the equation: B = (( Sigma Qh- Sigma Q'h)-( Sigma Qc- Sigma Q'c))/(( Sigma Qco- Sigma Q'co)-( Sigma Qc- Sigma Q'c)) (I), where Sigma Qh is the cumulative flow of hydrogen in the feed gas, Sigma Q'h is the cumulative flow of hydrogen in the exhaust gas, Sigma Qc is the cumulative flow of carbon dioxide in the raw gas, Sigma Q'c is the cumulative flow of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, Sigma Qco is the cumulative flow of carbon monoxide in the raw gas, and Sigma Q'co is the cumulative flow of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas, when adjustment coefficient is equal to target value within 2-5 hours, it will be automatically cleared and recalculated, and when adjustment coefficient is not equal to the target value, recording the adjustment coefficient (B), automatically clearing it, and recalculating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: When adjustment coefficient is not equal to the target value, recording the adjustment coefficient (B), automatically clearing it, and recalculating using the ratio of the sum of the adjustment coefficients (B1-Bn) to the number of adjustment coefficients (n), where n is 2-10, preferably 3-5. When adjustment coefficient is greater than the target value, the cumulative flow of carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas or the raw gas increases. When adjustment coefficient is less than the target value, the cumulative flow of carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas or the raw gas is reduced. The target value is 2.03-2.15, preferably 2.05-2.1. The cumulative flow rate of hydrogen in the raw material gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Qh = Sigma (Qh) = Sigma (Qx Ch) (II), where Qh is the real-time flow rate of hydrogen in the raw material gas, Q is the real-time flow rate of the raw material gas, and Ch is the real-time component content of hydrogen in the raw material gas. The cumulative flow rate of carbon dioxide in the raw material gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Qc = Sigma (Qc) = Sigma (Qx Cc) (III), where Qc is the real-time flow rate of carbon dioxide in the raw material gas, Q is the real-time flow rate of the raw material gas, and Cc is the real-time component content of carbon dioxide in the raw material gas. The cumulative flow rate of carbon monoxide in the raw material gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Qco = Sigma (Qco) = Sigma (Qx Cco) (IV), where Qco is the real-time flow rate of carbon monoxide in the raw material gas, Q is the real-time flow rate of the raw material gas, and Cco is the real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the raw material gas. The cumulative flow of carbon monoxide in the raw material gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Qco = Sigma (Qco) = Sigma (Qx Ccox alpha 1) (V), where alpha 1 is the correction factor of 0.95-1.05. The correction factor satisfies the equation: alpha 1 = Co/Cco (VI), where Co is the standard real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas, and Cco is the real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas. The cumulative flow of hydrogen in the raw material gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Qh = Sigma (Qh) = Sigma (Qx Chx alpha 2) (V), where alpha 1 is the correction factor of 0.95-1.05. The correction factor satisfies the equation: alpha 2 = (Ch+Cco-C standard,Co)/Ch (VII), where Ch is the real-time component content of hydrogen in the feed gas. The cumulative flow rate of hydrogen in the exhaust gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Q'h = Sigma (Q'h) = Sigma (Q'x Ch) (VIII), where Q'h is the real-time flow rate of hydrogen in the exhaust gas, Q' is the real-time flow rate of the exhaust gas, and Ch is the real-time component content of hydrogen in the exhaust gas. The cumulative flow rate of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Q'c = Sigma (Q'c) = Sigma (Q'x Cc) (III), where Q'c is the real-time flow rate of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas, Q' is the real-time flow rate of the exhaust gas, and Cc is the real-time component content of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas. The cumulative flow rate of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas satisfies the equation: Sigma Q'co = Sigma (Q'co) = Sigma (Q'x Cco) (IV), where Q'co is the real-time flow rate of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas, Q is the real-time flow rate of the exhaust gas, and Cco is the real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas.

PN CN110589857-A
TI Producing alkali using waste salt comprises pre-treating concentrated waste salt, dissolving in ammonium bicarbonate solution, passing carbon dioxide, heating, filtering, cooling, treating mother liquor with adsorbent and recycling.
AU LIU J
   ZHANG Y
   YAN S
   SHEN J
   GU S
   MA X
   CHEN D
AE UNIV CHANGZHOU (UNCZ-C)
GA 2019A7612E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing alkali using waste salt comprises (i) pre-treating the collected concentrated waste salt and dissolving in ammonium bicarbonate solution to obtain a saturated saline solution, transferring it into a high-pressure reaction kettle to absorb ammonia, performing cooling, continuously introducing carbon dioxide under heat preservation of water bath for carbonation, heating and maintaining the temperature in water bath when the reaction kettle temperature begins to drop, cooling and discharging the material, filtering and washing sodium bicarbonate filter cake many times to reduce the sodium chloride content, drying, weighing and measuring the purity and (ii) heating the filtrate to evaporate ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding the waste salt, heating, filtering when it is hot, cooling the filtrate to crystallize out ammonium chloride crystals, drying, weighing, measuring for purity, treating the mother liquor with an adsorbent and recycling.
   USE - The method is useful in producing alkali.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can be realized to obtain high purity soda and ammonium chloride products by exploring different reaction conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing alkali using waste salt comprises (i) pre-treating the collected concentrated waste salt under room temperature and dissolving in ammonium bicarbonate solution to obtain a saturated saline solution, transferring it into a high-pressure reaction kettle to absorb ammonia, performing cooling after absorption, continuously introducing carbon dioxide under heat preservation of water bath for carbonation, heating and maintaining the temperature in water bath when the reaction kettle temperature begins to drop, completing the reaction, cooling and discharging the material, filtering and washing sodium bicarbonate filter cake many times to reduce the sodium chloride content, drying, weighing and measuring the purity and (ii) heating the filtrate to evaporate ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding the waste salt until the filtrate is saturated, heating the filtrate, filtering when it is hot, cooling the filtrate to crystallize out ammonium chloride crystals, drying, weighing, measuring for purity, treating the mother liquor with an adsorbent and recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (i) comprises pre treating the waste salt to reduce the organic content, where the mass concentration of sodium chloride is 60-85%, the molar ratio of ammonia gas to sodium chloride in the waste salt is (1.0-2.0):1 and the mass concentration of the saline solution is 30-50%. The feeding amount of ammonium bicarbonate is 10-18 wt.% the mass of waste salt. The adsorbent is activated carbon, macroporous sulfonic acid resin, acrylic ester-dimethyl acrylate copolymer or porphyrin cross-linked chitosan. The amount of adsorbent is 5-30 wt.% the mass of waste salt. Preferred Method: The heating temperature of water bath is 50-80 degrees C and the carbonation process is carried out at a pressure of 1-5 MPa.

AB    NOVELTY - System (S1) comprises: (a) an additive composition comprising at least one carbon dioxide-generating compound, and at least one clay; and (b) a shampoo composition, where the additive composition and the shampoo composition are combined to form a cleansing composition at or near the time of use.
   USE - The system is useful for cleansing keratin fibers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system have acceptable properties of stability and sensoriality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) cleansing (M1) keratin fibers, comprising mixing an additive composition comprising at least one carbon dioxide-generating compound and at least one clay with shampoo composition to form a cleansing composition, applying the cleansing composition to the keratin fibers, and optionally rinsing the cleansing composition from the keratin fibers;
   (2) system (S2) comprising an additive composition comprising sodium bicarbonate and magnesium aluminum silicate and/or kaolin, and shampoo composition comprising at least one cleansing surfactant;
   (3) cleansing (M2) keratin fibers, comprising mixing an additive composition comprising sodium bicarbonate and magnesium aluminum silicate and/or kaolin with a shampoo composition comprising at least one cleansing surfactant to form a cleansing composition, at or near the time of use, applying the cleansing composition to the keratin fibers, and optionally rinsing the cleansing composition from the keratin fibers;
   (4) a kit comprising a first compartment comprising an additive composition comprising at least one carbon dioxide-generating compound and the clay, a second compartment comprising the shampoo composition, where the additive composition and the shampoo composition are in mutually separate compartments; and
   (5) a multi-chamber container comprising a first and second separate chambers, where the first chamber comprises additive composition comprising at least one carbon dioxide-generating compound and clay, and the second chamber comprises shampoo composition, where the container is configured so that the additive composition is dispensed from the first chamber and the shampoo composition is dispensed from the second chamber substantially simultaneously to form a cleansing composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-generating compound is carbonate or bicarbonate salts of alkaline metals or alkaline earth metals. The carbonate or bicarbonate salts of alkaline metals or alkaline earth metals are sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium bicarbonate, magnesium bicarbonate and/or calcium bicarbonate. The clay is silicate-based clay including aluminum silicate, calcium silicate, magnesium aluminum silicate, magnesium silicate, magnesium trisilicate, sodium magnesium silicate, zirconium silicate, attapulgite, bentonite, fuller's earth, hectorite, kaolin, lithium magnesium silicate, lithium magnesium sodium silicate, montmorillonite and/or pyrophyllite, zeolite. The additive composition comprises: 10-40 wt.% of carbon dioxide-generating compound; and 60-90 wt.% of clay. The shampoo composition comprises at least one surfactant including anionic, non-ionic and/or amphoteric surfactants. The system (S1) comprises: the additive composition and shampoo composition comprising at least one cleansing surfactant including anionic, non-ionic and/or amphoteric surfactants, and optionally at least one conditioning agent, where the additive composition and the shampoo composition are combined to form a cleansing composition at or near the time of use at a ratio of additive composition:shampoo composition of 1:7 to 1:1. The carbon dioxide-generating compound in the additive composition is carbonate or bicarbonate salts of alkaline metals or alkaline earth metals. The additive composition comprises 10-40 wt.% sodium bicarbonate, and 60-90 wt.% magnesium aluminum silicate and/or kaolin, where the shampoo composition further comprises at least one conditioning agent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2019090679-A; KR2424981-B1
TI Volatile organic compound processing system for            use in e.g. diesel engine of e.g. crude oil carrier,            has oil storage tank for storing oil, and heat            exchanger for cooling and delivering compressed            volatile organic compound to mixer.
AU HOON L J
   HAMJINKI
   JANG Y H
   HO L I
   KWON S H
   IL L
   SUNG S K
   ANTAEGI
AE KOREA SHIPBUILDING & OFFSHORE ENG CO LTD (HHIH-C)
   HYUNDAI HEAVY IND CO LTD (HHIH-C)
GA 201969961X
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has an oil storage tank (OT)                for storing oil. A gas fuel storage tank (GT)                stores gas fuel. A mixer (27) mixes volatile                organic compound (VOC), which is generated in the                oil storage tank with the gaseous fuel. The mixer                supplies mixture to an engine that is mounted on a                ship. A carbon dioxide absorber (133) removed                carbon dioxide from the VOC and delivers the                removed carbon dioxide to the mixer. A VOC                compressor (11) compresses carbon dioxide-removed                VOC. A heat exchanger (16) cools and delivers the                compressed VOC to the mixer. A gas-liquid separator                (17) gas-liquid separates the cooled VOC. A VOC                storage tank (15) stores the separated liquid VOC.                The mixer mixes the gaseous VOC, which is separated                from the gas-liquid separator with the gaseous                fuel. A VOC discharge line (13) is connected to the                gas-liquid separator in the oil storage.
   USE - VOC processing system for use in an engine of                a ship e.g. oil tanker, transmission line, and                crude oil carrier utilized for transporting crude                oil from a production site to a consumer. Uses                include but are not limited to a main engine                (ME)-liquid gas injection, an X-dual-fuel engine, a                gas injection engine, and a diesel engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively prevents environmental                pollution by suppressing emission of VOC to the                atmosphere by utilizing the VOC as fuel of the                engine. The system maximizes processing efficiency                and energy consumption efficiency of the VOC by                changing a processing structure according to the                generation timing of the VOC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a VOC processing system.
   Gas fuel storage tank (GT)
   Oil storage tank (OT)
   VOC processing system (1)
   VOC compressor (11)
   VOC discharge line (13)
   VOC storage tank (15)
   Heat exchanger (16)
   Gas-liquid separator (17)
   Mixer (27)
   Carbon dioxide absorber (133)

PT P
PN CN107829107-A; CN107829107-B
TI Preparing graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves coating electrochemical exfoliated graphene paste on conductive support.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG H
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 201824313U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst involves coating electrochemical exfoliated graphene paste on a conductive support and placing in a porous insulating polymer bag as an anode, and the cathode is a pure conductive support and electrode is obtained. The electrode is electrolyzed in an electrolytic cell containing a metal source, then after the electrolysis, the solid is separated and washed, then graphene loaded with monodisperse metal atoms, and the electrolyte is recovered and used.
   USE - Method for preparing graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, and is coated on gas diffusion electrode as working electrode, saturated potassium bicarbonate in 0.1 M carbon dioxide solution for electrolysis, used in platinum sheet to electrode, saturated calomel and reference electrode, constant potential scanning, and gas phase chromatography detection for generating gas product (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst, which has high efficiency and good material stability, improves the Faraday efficiency of the electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, high selectivity of product, effectively reduces the electro-catalytic carbon dioxide reduction needed for over-potential.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst involves coating electrochemical exfoliated graphene paste on a conductive support and placing in a porous insulating polymer bag as an anode, and the cathode is a pure conductive support and electrode is obtained. The electrode is electrolyzed in an electrolytic cell containing a metal source, then after the electrolysis, the solid is separated and washed, then graphene loaded with monodisperse metal atoms, and the electrolyte is recovered and used. The monodisperse metal atom/graphene solid obtained is mixed with the heteroatom source material and metal source, then subjecting the mixture to a high-temperature inert atmosphere for heat treatment. The heat-treated graphene/carbon nanotubes support monodisperse metal atoms are washed with acid to remove the unstable atoms and obtaining graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom-composite catalyst as desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metal source contains iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, or zinc. The heteroatom comprise nitrogen source, phosphorus source, sulfur source, or boron source, where nitrogen source comprise melamine, amino acid, dicyandiamide, urea, polyethyleneimine, or aliphatic unsaturated amine, sulfur source comprise thiourea, ammonium thiocyanate, or dibenzyl disulfide, phosphorus source comprise phytic acid or phosphoric acid, and boron source comprise boric acid, or aminophenylboronic acid hemisulfate. The heteroatom-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes comprises nitrogen-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, sulfur-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, phosphorus-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, boron-doped graphene/carbon nanometers tubes, nitrogen-sulfur co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, nitrogen-phosphorus co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, nitrogen-boron co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, phospho-co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, thio-boron co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, boron-phosphorus-codoped graphene/carbon nanotubes, phosphor-nitrogen-phosphorus co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, nitrogen-nitrogen-boron co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, sulfur-phosphorus boron co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes, and nitrophosphorus-boron co-doped graphene/carbon nanotubes. The conductive support comprise titanium alloy, nickel foil, or titanium foil. The organic electrolyte is a metal-containing imidazole ionic liquid and mixture of metal salt and imidazole ionic liquid. The metal in the metal-containing imidazole ionic liquid is iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, or zinc. The imidazole ionic liquid include 1-butyl 3-methylimidazolium chloride or 1-vinyl 3-methylimidazolium chloride. Preferred Conditions: The graphene/carbon nanotube-supported monodisperse metal atom composite catalyst is prepared by pressing the graphene material on both surfaces of the conductive support body and filled into porous insulating polymer bag, used as positive electrode, and negative electrode is a pure conductive support. The anode and cathode electrodes form an array, where the distance between the two electrodes is 5-30 mm. The average pore size of the polymer bags is 0.1-25 mu m. The electrolytic cell comprises an electrode array, which has multiple electrodes, and multiple electrolytic cells, which together form an electrolytic cell array, where each electrolytic cell is independently powered by single power source or multiple electrolytic cells and are connected in parallel and powered by single power source. The power supply is a direct current (DC) power supply or variable frequency alternative current (AC) power supply. The DC voltage between cathode and anode is controlled at 4-8 Volt, electrolysis is performed for 2-72 hours, exchange voltage direction is maintained for 30 seconds to 6 hours. The electrolyte is an aqueous or organic electrolyte and the temperature of the electrolyte is controlled at 25-70 degrees C. The monodisperse metal atom/graphene solid and heteroatom material are mixed in the weight ratio of 1:1 to 1:100, then subjected to liquid ultrasonic dispersion mixing or ball milling mixing. The liquid-phase ultrasonic dispersion mixing is performed under ultrasonic frequency of 5-35 kilohertz, power of 100-500 Watt, and ultrasonic dispersion mixing is performed for 2-6 hours at 15-40 degrees C. The ball milling is performed in stainless steel, zirconia, or agate ball mills. The diameter of the ball beads is 0.5-1.5 cm, volume of the ball mill tank is 100-250 ml, and the ball ratio is maintained to 10-100:1. The ball milling speed is set to 200-580 revolutions/minute, for 5-20 hours and ball mill uses dry ball or wet ball mill. The wet ball mill uses water and ethanol as dispersion. The heat treatment is performed at 600-1000 degrees C for 1-5 hours under the inert gas of argon or nitrogen. Preferred Composition: The aqueous electrolytic solution comprises mixed aqueous solution of soluble metal chloride and hydrochloric acid, mixed aqueous solution of soluble metal sulfate and sulfuric acid, or mixed aqueous solution of soluble metal nitrate and nitric acid. The catalyst comprise monodisperse metal atoms supported on/in the graphene/carbon nanotubes, 0.001-5.0 wt.% supported monodisperse metal atom content, and 1-20 wt.% heteroatom doping content.
