PT P
PN CN107737585-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent, involves reacting 2,5,8,11-quadronacin borate esters, 1,3,6,8-tetrabromoanthracene, triphenylphosphine, cesium fluoride, aluminum nitrate and N,N-dimethylformamide.
AU HUANG X
   LIN M
AE HUANG X (HUAN-Individual)
GA 2018183862
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of carbon dioxide absorbent, involves adding 2,5,8,11-quadronacin borate esters, 1,3,6,8-tetrabromoanthracene, triphenylphosphine, cesium fluoride and aluminum nitrate to three-necked flask and mixing to obtain a mixture, adding N,N-dimethylformamide and toluene to three-necked flask and mixing to obtain a mixture, adding 2-aminoterephthalic acid to three-necked flask and stirring to obtain pre-reaction solution, ultrasonically reacting pre-reaction solution, heating under nitrogen environment and heat preserving.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent with increased absorption capacity and enhanced chemical stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon dioxide absorbent, involves (1) adding 20-22 pts. wt. 2,5,8,11-quadronacin borate esters, 13-15 pts. wt. 1,3,6,8-tetrabromoanthracene, 3-5 pts. wt. triphenylphosphine, 10-15 pts. wt. cesium fluoride and 40-50 pts. wt. aluminum nitrate to three-necked flask and mixing to obtain a mixture, (2) adding N,N-dimethylformamide and toluene to three-necked flask and mixing to obtain a mixture, adding 2-aminoterephthalic acid to three-necked flask and stirring to obtain pre-reaction solution, (3) placing the three-necked flask comprising pre-reaction solution in an ultrasonic oscillation apparatus, heating under nitrogen environment and heat preserving, (4) separating the filter residue by filtration, washing the filter residue with chloroform, acetone and deionized water 3-5 times respectively, adding the washed residue into cable extractor and carrying out extraction using tetrahydrofuran to obtain extracted product, (5) mixing the extracted product and L-asparagine solution, and transferring the mixture into fermenter, adding yeast powder and sealing the fermenter, (6) after the above fermentation is completed, taking out the fermentation product and filtering, separating the fermentation filtrate and obtain fermented filter residue, rinsing the fermented filter residue 3-5 times with absolute ethanol and deionized water, respectively, freeze-drying and washing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (2) comprises adding N,N-dimethylformamide 2-3 times the mass of mixture comprising 2,5,8,11-quadronacin borate esters, 1,3,6,8-tetrabromoanthracene, triphenylphosphine, cesium fluoride and aluminum nitrate, mixing for 30-35 minutes and the amount of 2-aminoterephthalic acid is 10-15 %mass of the mixture and stirring for 30-40 minutes. The step (3) comprises ultrasonically reacting pre-reaction solution at 150-200 W with ultrasonic frequency of 25-30 kHz for 30-40 minutes and heating at 70-80 degrees C for 24-30 hours. The step (5) comprises carrying out sealed fermentation at 37-40 degrees C for 5-7 days. The step (6) comprises freeze-drying at -15--10 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The step (5) comprises using 18 g/L L-asparagine solution. The mass ratio of extracted product and L-asparagine solution is 1:5. The amount of yeast powder is 10 %mass of the fermentation mixture.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN CN114130410-A
TI New bismuth iron titanium oxyhalide photocatalytic material useful for degrading organic pollutant rhodamine B, prepared by mixing bismuth compound, titanium dioxide, ferrous oxide and bismuth oxyhalide nanosheets, adding molten salt, carrying out calcination in muffle furnace, grinding, washing.
AU GU Y
   ZHANG Q
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 202238909D
AB    NOVELTY - Bismuth iron titanium oxyhalide photocatalytic material (I) is new.
   USE - The bismuth iron titanium oxyhalide photocatalytic material is useful for degrading organic pollutant rhodamine B (claimed), photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, easy operation, strong ability to degrade pollutants under illumination; no secondary pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Bismuth iron titanium oxyhalide photocatalytic material of formula (Bi7Ti2Fe2O17R1) (I) is new.
   R1 = Br or I.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (i) adding bismuth nitrate pentahydrate to ethylene glycol, stirring with constant temperature magnetic stirrer to make fully dissolved to obtain solution A, (ii) dissolving potassium halide in ultrapure water to obtain solution B, (iii) adding solution B dropwise into solution A, performing magnetic stirring at room temperature for 1 hour, centrifugal washing and drying cleaned sample in an oven to obtain bismuth oxyhalide nanosheets, (iv) mixing bismuth compound, titanium dioxide, ferrous oxide and bismuth oxyhalide nanosheets in molar ratio of 3:2:1:1, adding molten salt for grinding, and carrying out calcination in muffle furnace at 973-1023K and (v) taking out the calcined sample and grinding, repeatedly washing with deionized water to remove the molten salt, and drying cleaned sample in oven to obtain final product.Preferred Method: In step (iii), the drying temperature is 80degrees Celsius, and rotational speed of the centrifugal washing is 6000-8000 revolution/minute. In step (iv) when the potassium halide is potassium bromide, the calcination temperature in the muffle furnace in step (iv) is 1023K, and the duration is 6 hours, when the potassium halide is potassium iodide, the calcination temperature in the muffle furnace is 973K, and the duration is 12 hours.Preferred Components: The concentration of solution A in step (i) is 0.1 mol/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium halide is potassium bromide or potassium iodide. The bismuth compound is bismuth trioxide or bismuth oxide. The molten salt is potassium chloride, sodium chloride and/or cesium chloride; the addition amount of molten salt is 100-120 wt.%.

PN WO2022027100-A1
TI Controlling the carbon output from an alumina            refinery comprises capturing water vapor from a flash            train in the refinery, compressing the vapor, and            utilizing a portion of energy in the vapor to heat a            process stream within the refinery.
AU CHATFIELD R
AE ALCOA AUSTRALIA LTD (ALUM-C)
GA 202221646F
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling the carbon output from an alumina                refinery, comprises the steps of: capturing water                vapor from a flash train in the refinery;                compressing the captured water vapor; and utilizing                at least a portion of the energy in the captured                water vapor to heat a process stream within the                alumina refinery.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling the                carbon output from an alumina refinery                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the energy efficiently as                possible to reduce costs and carbon dioxide                emissions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The water vapor is                  captured from a flash vessel in the flash train,                  where the flash train comprises many flash                  vessels. The method comprises the additional step                  of feeding the heated process stream to a flash                  train, where the step of feeding the heated                  process stream to a flash train comprises feeding                  at least a portion of the heated process stream                  to the flash train. The step of heating a process                  stream comprises passing the process stream                  through at least one heater in a train of                  heaters, where the train of heaters precedes the                  flash train, and where the flash train is a heat                  recovery flash train, where the flash train                  comprises at least two flash vessels. The water                  vapor is sourced from the final and/or                  penultimate flash vessel in the flash train, the                  process stream is from the final and/or                  penultimate heater in the train, and the train of                  heaters comprises at least one heater. The flash                  train forms part of the digestion circuit, the                  heat interchange circuit, the evaporation                  circuit, the precipitation circuit or a                  calcination heat recovery system in the alumina                  refinery. The method further comprises the steps                  of heating a fluid stream with calciner flue gas                  to provide a heated fluid stream, and passing the                  heated fluid stream to a flash train and forming                  water vapor. The method comprises the further                  step of exporting excess compressed water vapor                  out of the flash train, where the water vapor                  from the flash train is less than 80&#176; C.                  The step of compressing the captured water vapor                  is repeated to attain the desired water vapor                  condensing temperature, where the step of                  compressing the captured water vapor is performed                  by mechanical vapor recompression. There are                  provided many mechanical vapor compressors in                  series where the compressed stream from the first                  compressor in the series is passed to the second                  compressor for further compression, and the                  captured water vapor is compressed in an                  electrically powered compressor where the                  electricity has a carbon intensity of less than                  0.5.t.carbon dioxide e/MWhr, where the                  electricity is generated from a renewable power                  source, i.e. zero-carbon power source e.g.                  hydrogen or nuclear, and the electricity is                  stored in a battery after generation and prior to                  use. The method comprises the further step of                  removing particulate matter from the captured                  water vapor prior to the step of compressing the                  captured water vapor.

PN CN113307516-A
TI Preparing sulfoaluminate cement clinker, involves weighing aluminum section waste slag, calcium raw material and correction material, mixing uniformly, grinding, calcinating obtained cement raw material, and quenching.
AU YU L
   YANG Z
   YANG Y
AE UNIV TONGJI (UYTJ-C)
GA 2021A2640P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sulfoaluminate cement clinker involves weighing an aluminum section waste slag, calcium raw material and correction material, mixing uniformly, grinding to obtain cement raw material. The raw cement material is calcined, and quenched to obtain sulfoaluminate cement clinker.
   USE - Method for preparing sulfoaluminate cement clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables completely replacing bauxite to realize the resource utilization of aluminum section waste slag, and effectively curing harmful elements in aluminum section waste slag without using any pretreatment method, in simple, less energy consumption and eco-friendly manner without carbon dioxide emissions during the calcination process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The main chemical components of the aluminum section waste residue comprise aluminum oxide, sulfur trioxide, calcium oxide, sodium oxide, silicon oxide, ferric oxide and phosphorus pentoxide, potassium oxide, chromium oxide, and fluorine element. The calcium raw materials are selected from carbide slag, lithium slag, or nickel slag. The correction material is selected from phosphogypsum or desulfurized gypsum. Preferred Process: The cement raw material is obtained by mixing aluminum section waste slag, calcium raw material and correction material, grinding to a 0.08 mm square hole sieve with a sieve residue of 8-10%, and 0.20 mm square hole sieve with a sieve residue of less than 1%. Preferred Condition: The calcining temperature is 1100-1200 degrees C and the calcining time is 0.5-1.0 hour. The quenching speed is 40-55 degrees C/minute, quenching temperature is 60-120 degrees C, and the quenching time is 20-30 minutes. The mass percentage ratio of aluminum section waste slag: calcium raw material: calibration material is 35-65%: 30 -65%: 5-10%.

PT P
PN CN113120975-A
TI Recycling anode material of waste ternary lithium battery comprises e.g. calcining anode material of ternary lithium battery and reducing agent, subjecting to water immersion treatment, and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU ZHOU L
   SHI X
   WANG N
   CAO Y
AE BEIJING BAILI GERUI RESOURCES TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202184061F
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling anode material of a waste ternary lithium battery comprises calcining the anode material of the ternary lithium battery and a reducing agent, subjecting the calcined product to water immersion treatment, and introducing carbon dioxide simultaneously, performing solid-liquid separation, obtaining a solution rich in lithium bicarbonate and a water leaching residue, heating the solution rich in lithium bicarbonate, and separating by solid-liquid to obtain lithium carbonate and filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling anode material of waste ternary lithium battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and reduces recycling costs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is ternary lithium battery anode material and/or carbon. The weight of the reducing agent accounts for 10-40 wt.% of anode material of the ternary lithium battery and the reducing agent. The mass ratio of the nickel, cobalt, manganese hydroxide and lithium carbonate is 5-20:1. Preferred Method: The calcining is performed at a temperature of 400-650 degrees C for 1-3 hours, and under a protective atmosphere of carbon dioxide. In the water immersion treatment, the material-to-liquid ratio is 1:5-10, the temperature is 20-75 degrees C, and the time is 1-5 hours and the carbon dioxide inlet rate is 0.5-2 l/minute. The heating is performed at 60-80 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The method comprises the carbon dioxide generated by the heating is returned to the water leaching treatment for recycling, and returning the filtrate to the water leaching treatment as a leaching agent for recycling. The water leaching residue is subjected to acid leaching treatment, and then solid-liquid separation is carried out to obtain the acid leaching residue containing waste graphite and the acid leaching liquid. In the acid leaching treatment, the material-to-liquid ratio is 1:5-10, the temperature is 50-90 degrees C, the time is 2-5 hours, and the acid concentration is 30-100 g/l. The method comprises adjusting the pH of the acid leaching solution to 3-7, filtering, obtaining the purified liquid and sedimentation residue, adjusting the pH of the purified liquid to 8-11, filtering to obtain nickel, cobalt, manganese hydroxide and precipitated liquid, and returning the precipitated liquid to the purified liquid for recycling. The method further includes calcining the nickel cobalt manganese hydroxide and lithium carbonate at 500-900 degrees C for 2-8 hours to obtain a regenerated anode material.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The saccharide is monosaccharide or disaccharide. The mixed gas includes carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and the molar ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:2-2:1. The mixed gas has space velocity of 10-100 NLx gcat-1x h-1. Preferred Method: The contact is carried out at 500-1000 degrees C.

PN CN110845302-A
TI Preparing ethylene glycol by adding polycarbonate electrolyte residual liquid to water and catalyst for reaction, where polycarbonate electrolyte residual liquid comprises ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, bromoalkyl compound and ammonia.
AU WANG Y
   YANG Z
   SHEN G
   ZOU M
AE UNIV SHENYANG TECHNOLOGY (UYTE-C)
GA 202019957J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing ethylene glycol involves taking polycarbonate electrolyte (PEC) residual liquid as raw material, adding water and catalyst for reaction to obtain ethylene glycol, where the PEC residual liquid comprise PEC, ethylene carbonate, ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, triethylene glycol or high alcohol and alcohol ether, 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide with out complete reaction, bromoalkyl compound and ammonia.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing ethylene glycol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of kettle residue which is difficult to handle, and is suitable for industrial production of PEC for preparing ethylene glycol and plasticizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The reaction is carried out at 80-160 degrees C for 2-8 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of water and PEC residual liquid is (0.4-1):1. The mass ratio of catalyst and PEC residual liquid is (0.005-1):1. The catalyst comprises mixtures (A) and (B), and additive agent (C), in a mass ratio of (0-1):(1-3):(0-2), where the compound A and additive agent (C) cannot be zero at the same time. Preferred Method: The mixture (A) in the catalyst is prepared by taking a mixed acid composed of dodecanoic acid, myristic acid, hexadecanoic acid, and octadecanoic acid with castor oil and oleic acid as raw material, neutralizing and saponifying with 30% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution at 65-85 degrees C for 1-3 hours, adding a transparent agent comprising aqueous solution of sodium methoxide or sodium ethoxide, where the effect of which makes the catalyst grain size fine and nano-sized which greatly improves the transparency of catalyst product, and stirring to obtain mixture (A). The catalyst is prepared by adding mixture (A) to mixture (B) having 30-40 %mass and mixing in a mass ratio of 1:1 at 65-85 degrees C, pre-saponifying for 0.5-2.5 hours to obtain a mixture, adding 10 %mass additive agent (C) and water to the solution of mixtures (A) and (B), and reacting at 20-50 degrees C for 8-30 hours to obtain strong alkali composite metal nanosoap. Preferred Components: The catalyst is PEC residual liquid. The mixture (B) is strong base type composite metal nanosoap which is chosen from sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, alkali metal hydroxide, alkaline earth metal hydroxide, alkali metal carbonate, alkaline earth metal carbonate or sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate and/or potassium carbonate. The additive agent (C) is PEC residual liquid.

TI ARTIFICIAL SNOW PRODUCTION METHOD FOR AGRICULTURE NEEDS.
AU SYROVATKA V I
   ZHDANOVA N V
   OBUKHOV A D
AE FEDERAL SCI AGRO-ENG CENT VIM (SCAG-Non-standard)
GA 201990007A
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to agriculture, in particular, to method of producing artificial snow. Method involves microwave energy treatment of turbulent air flow, polarization of chemical elements of air and drops of water from its moisture, mixing of air, water and carbon dioxide and jet discharge of the mixture under pressure into atmosphere. Turbulent water-air flow of mixture is imparted to rotary motion in gap between truncated cones and is ejected into atmosphere by centrifugal method, converting in atmosphere air-air mixture flow into hollow thin-walled cone from air mixture with formation of minute drops nuclei of snowflakes at 8001,000 mm from end of conical nozzle.
   USE - Agriculture.
   ADVANTAGE - Use of invention will make it possible to increase intensity of process of artificial snow formation.

PN JP2019151770-A
TI New cyclic polycarbonate compound for forming            molded product including industrial components and            biomedical materials.
AU EMA T
   MAEDA C
   NOZAKI K
AE UNIV OKAYAMA (UYOK-C)
   NOZAKI K (NOZA-Individual)
   SUMITOMO SEIKA CHEM CO LTD (SEIT-C)
GA 2019793887
AB    NOVELTY - A cyclic polycarbonate compound (I), is                new.
   USE - Cyclic polycarbonate compound for forming                molded product including industrial components and                biomedical materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclic carbonate compound has excellent                thermal stability, is environmentally-friendly, and                can be prepared efficiently, economically and                stably with high yield. The decomposition of the                molecular terminal due to heat can be suppressed.                The porphyrin complex effectively catalyzes                copolymerization of epoxide and carbon dioxide with                high selectivity and activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A cyclic polycarbonate compound of formula                (I), is new.
   R1-R4 = alkyl, aryl or H;
   or R1+R2+R3+R4 = ring;
   m+n = 5 or more;and
   n/m = 1 or less.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                manufacture of polycarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The aluminum porphyrin                  complex compound catalyst is preferably a                  compound of formula (V).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The polycarbonate                  compound is prepared by copolymerizing an epoxide                  and carbon dioxide in the presence of an aluminum                  porphyrin complex compound catalyst of formula                  (III).at least one of the A1-A8 = substituent of                  formula: -D'-E+.X- (i), and other is H or alkyl,                  and A1,A3,A5 and A7 are preferably group (i), and                  A2,A4,A6 and A8 are preferably group (i) or                  H;X = anion;D' = 1-20C divalent organic group;andE+ = 3-60C quaternary ammonium group or                  quaternary phosphonium group.Preferred Component: The cyclic                  polycarbonate compound is preferably a compound                  of formula (II).x = 1-4, preferably 2.
   Preferred Definitions:
   n = 1.
   EXAMPLE - Carbon dioxide at pressure of 2 MPa and 0.5 mu                mol aluminum porphyrin complex of formula (A) were                added to a metal-made autoclave, which was                preheated at 150 degrees C for 2 hours and cooled.                20 mmol Cyclohexene oxide was added under nitrogen                atmosphere, heated at 120 degrees C and cooled. The                unreacted substances were distilled off under                reduced pressure. Methylene chloride was added to                the reaction mixture, and 0.2 ml 1 M hydrochloric                acid and 0.15 mmol phenanthrene were added, and                polycyclohexene carbonate was produced. The                obtained compound exhibited bimodal peaks (a) and                (b), and had number average molecular weight of 33                kg/mol at peak (a) and 16 kg/mol at peak (b), and                molecular weight distribution of 1.02 at peak (a)                and 1.03 at peak (b).
   R = O(CH2)6N+Bu3Cl-.

PT P
PN WO2018047079-A1; CA3073643-A1
TI Balancing an imbalanced redox flow battery having            partially oxidized negative electrolyte, comprises            measuring the extent of oxidation of the negative            electrolyte, and adding reducing agent to the positive            electrolyte.
AU CUNNINGHAM S
   MALLINSON S
AE REDT LTD DUBLIN IRELAND (REDT-Non-standard)
GA 201821667K
AB    NOVELTY - Balancing an imbalanced redox flow battery                having partially oxidized negative electrolyte,                comprises: (a) measuring the extent of oxidation of                the negative electrolyte; and (b) adding reducing                agent to the positive electrolyte to reduce the                vanadium5+/ (vanadium dioxide)+ ions to                vanadium4+/(vanadium oxide)2+ ions, the reducing                agent combining carbon, oxygen and hydrogen in such                ratios that the products of the reaction include                vanadium5+/ (vanadium dioxide)+ ions to                vanadium4+/(vanadium oxide)2+ ions, water and                carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for balancing an                imbalanced redox flow battery having partially                oxidized negative electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, reaction                  products in addition to water and carbon dioxide                  are produced. To remedy the imbalance in the                  redox flow battery having oxidized negative                  electrolyte, by performing: fully discharging the                  redox flow battery from a state of full charge,                  or a partially discharged state, to the ions in                  the electrolytes i.e. in the negative reservoir                  is vanadium3+ ions, and in the positive                  reservoir, 40% of the ions will be vanadium4+                  ions, with 60% being vanadium5+ ions; mixing of                  the discharged positive and negative electrolytes                  by opening the normally closed crossover valves                  and using a positive pump to pump electrolyte                  from the positive reservoir to the negative                  reservoir and vice versa; and pre-charging of the                  unbalanced electrolytes, which can be any one of                  sets of ions i.e. (i) the first set are 20% which                  undergo no change in pre charging, the 20%                  vanadium3+ ions in the negative electrolyte are                  in the state which they should be at the end of                  the pre-charge, together with a corresponding 20%                  of vanadium4+ ions in the positive reservoir, the                  latter represent only a quarter of the total of                  80% of vanadium4+ ions in the positive reservoir                  after mixing, (ii) provision of a first amount of                  charge, in equal and opposite measure to the two                  electrolytes in the two reservoirs, addition of                  electrons to ions in the negative electrolyte and                  removal of electrons from ions in the positive                  reservoir, this converts the 20% vanadium3+ ions                  in the positive reservoir to vanadium4+ ions and                  20% of vanadium4+ ions in the negative                  electrolyte to vanadium3+ ions, and the actual                  pre-charge occurs at a steady current, the                  imbalance is the 60% of vanadium4+ ions in the                  negative electrolyte, these re pre-charged to                  vanadium3+ ions by provision of a second amount                  of charge and indeed partial normal charging of                  the positive electrolyte, and (iii) provision of                  this second amount of charge converting all the                  negative electrolyte ions to vanadium3+ ions, the                  second charge also converts 60% of the vanadium4+                  ions in the positive electrolyte to vanadium5+                  ions, at the end of this charge, the voltage at                  the reference cell steps up again, corresponding                  to the negative electrolyte reaching its                  pre-charge state, with the negative electrolyte                  stepping down, corresponding with the voltage                  difference stepping up. As the amount of                  imbalance increases, the two pre-charge voltage                  steps, a first step and a second step, move away                  from the balanced electrolytes voltage step, and                  the extent of the movement is proportional to be                  extent of imbalance. A final step for remedying                  an imbalance is achieved by the addition of a                  calculated quantity of oxalic acid to the                  positive electrolyte, slowly in four equal                  portions, to return it to its normal, balanced                  pre-charge state. The addition of oxalic acid or                  an equivalent is in small portions, to avoid                  excessive effervescence and spillage of the                  electrolyte. The percentage imbalance can be                  measured by: measuring the time (t1) to the first                  step; measuring the time (t2) to the second step;                  calculating the time (T) to the mid-point between                  the steps; calculating the time (t) from the                  first step to the mid-point; and calculating the                  percentage imbalance as the percentage (t) is of                  (T), ad by using equations of the specification.                  Preferred Components: The reducing agent is any                  one of a carboxylic acid, a precursor of                  carboxylic acid or intermediate product of a                  carboxylic acid, where the carboxylic acid                  includes oxalic acid, ethanoic acid or methanoic                  acid, the precursor of carboxylic acid includes                  ethylene glycol or glycerine, and the                  intermediate product of the carboxylic acid                  includes glyoxal, glycolaldehyde, glycolic acid                  or glyoxylic acid. The step (a) comprises: mixing                  the two electrolytes having fully discharged the                  battery first, and then pre-charging it noting                  the time taken to the pre-charge voltage step of                  the negative tank electrolyte and then the                  pre-charge voltage step of the positive tank                  electrolyte. In the method: the rate of charge is                  held constant, and is adjusted to remain constant                  if it is not, and the percentage state of                  oxidation of the negative electrolyte is given by                  the ratio of the difference in the times divided                  by their sums; during the pre-charging step, the                  electrolytes are pumped, charging voltage is                  applied and electrical charge is absorbed by the                  redox flow battery; during pre-charging the                  voltage across a reference cell, to which a                  sample of the electrolytes is pumped, remains                  close to zero until close to the end of charging;                  and once the pre-charging has finished, with all                  vanadium4+ ions in the positive electrolyte and                  all vanadium3+ ions in the negative electrolyte,                  the vanadium4+ ions are progressively charged to                  vanadium5+ ions and the vanadium3+ ions to                  vanadium2+ ions without further step change in                  reference cell voltage or open circuit voltage                  across the stack, until full charge is reached, a                  further step change in reference cell voltage                  occurs.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

AU KAZARYAN V P
   KHVOSTOVA V YU
   OVODOV S O
   SHILOV E M
   SVINTSOV M V
AE GAZPROM VNIIGAZ NATURAL GASES & GAS TECH (GZPM-C)
GA 201821182N
AB    NOVELTY - Method of supporting production reservoir of the gas well into the well implies pumping a binding composition, which is a mixture of reagents, containing 60-80 wt % of modified tetraethoxysilane and 20-40 wt % of aqueous acid catalyst solution. It is forced into the reservoir by a gaseous agent selected from group of gases: nitrogen, exhaust gases of internal combustion engine, carbon dioxide. At that, gaseous agent is fed to the well at a pressure exceeding reservoir pressure at least by 1.0 MPa. After that well is kept for process settling for at least two days.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduced water saturation of reservoir bottom-hole zone, enhanced gas well deliverability with single treatment of reservoir.

PT P
PN CN107540004-A; CN107540004-B
TI Recovery of battery grade lithium carbonate from lithium iron phosphate battery cathode powder material involves boiling lithium iron phosphate battery cathode powder, filtering, purifying, adding ammonium carbonate to second material.
AU XU M
   LIU C
   ZHANG H
AE HEFEI GUOXUAN HIGH TECH POWER SOURCE CO (HEFG-C)
   HEFEI GUOXUAN HIGH TECH POWER SOURCE CO (HEFG-C)
GA 2018035356
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of battery grade lithium carbonate from lithium iron phosphate battery cathode powder material comprises boiling lithium iron phosphate battery cathode powder at high temperature and pressure, and filtering to obtain first material; purifying first material to obtain second material; adding ammonium carbonate to second material, charging carbon dioxide, performing high-temperature and high-pressure reaction, and filtering to obtain third material; and washing third material.
   USE - Method for recovering battery grade lithium carbonate from lithium iron phosphate battery cathode powder material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short flow, high recovery rate, and strong social value and economic benefit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic acid is sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid or phosphoric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first material is prepared by mixing waste lithium iron phosphate battery anode powder and water at mass ratio of 1:3-5, adding sodium hydroxide and waste iron phosphate lithium battery anode powder material at ratio of 8-15:100, carrying out high temperature reaction for 2-5 hours at 120-150 degrees C, stirring at 300-600 revolutions/minute, and filtering. The second material is prepared by adding inorganic acid to adjust pH value to 4.5-6, adding flocculating agent, filtering, and removing aluminum. The third material is prepared adding ammonium carbonate in second material, filling 0.3% carbon dioxide, carrying out high temperature reaction for 2-3 hours at 120-150 degrees C, stirring at 300-500 revolutions/minute, and filtering. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of flocculant and aluminum element is 1-5:1000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The flocculating agent is polyacrylamide, polyacrylic acid or methacrylic acid.

   ZHANG C

AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202283330K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing dimethyl ether by carbon dioxide                hydrogenation involves adding carbon dioxide and                hydrogen in the methanol synthesis reactor equipped                with a catalyst, carrying out carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reaction for preparing                water-containing crude methanol. The crude dimethyl                ether is prepared from crude methanol containing                water by dehydration reaction of methanol in                dimethyl ether synthesis reactor containing                catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing dimethyl ether by carbon                dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method not only converts the waste carbon                dioxide into dimethyl ether liquid, but also                provides a new way for industrial production of                dimethyl ether. In addition, the method                comprehensively utilizes internal energy of the                process system, further improves the energy                efficiency of carbon dioxide hydrogenation dimethyl                ether process and reduces the discharge of carbon                dioxide. The carbon dioxide processing amount is                4.4 ton per hour, the yield of dimethyl ether is                2.30 ton/hour, the energy utilization rate of                carbon dioxide preparing dimethyl ether process is                69.75 %, net carbon dioxide emission reduction rate                is 79.60 %.

PN WO2020099877-A1; EP3880631-A1; US2022002197-A1
TI Cement additive used in blended cement composition and cement admixture for manufacturing cement such as Portland cement, comprises precipitated calcium carbonate formed from mineral sequestration of carbon dioxide.
AU IMBABI M S
   GLASSER F
   MCDONALD L J
AE CARBON CAPTURE MACHINE UK LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON CAPTURE MACHINE UK LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON CAPTURE MACHINE UK LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202044032T
AB    NOVELTY - Cement additive comprises a precipitated calcium carbonate formed from mineral sequestration of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The additive is used in a blended cement composition, and cement admixture for manufacturing cement such as Portland cement (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The additive enables control and uniformly enhances reaction between cement hydration products and ensures internal calcium carbonate reaction. The cement has high compressive strength of substantially up to 55 MPa. The composition enhances the sustainability of cement production. The composition exhibits synergistic effect of enhanced reactive calcium carbonate and the small particle effect which it acts as a filler. The composition reduces emissions of carbon dioxide to the atmosphere by capturing carbon dioxide and then using it to reduce the amount of cement needed per unit volume. The calcium carbonate polymorph is controlled to suit the requirements. The precipitated calcium carbonate has controlled size and shape of particles and the particle size distributions to afford high quality and high performance as compared with other cement additive.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a cement admixture comprising a precipitated calcium carbonate additive;
   (2) a cement comprising a precipitated calcium carbonate additive;
   (3) a method of changing the mineralogy of a hydrated cement, involving introducing amorphous calcium carbonate, a reacting cement and amorphous calcium carbonate resulting in the formation of calcium carbonate silicate (hydrates) including scawtite and/or tilleyite, spontaneously, and performing internal reaction, and/or introducing nano-crystalline calcium carbonate, and performing a reaction between the cement and the nano-crystalline calcium carbonate;
   (4) a method of making reactive and amorphous calcium carbonate, involves capturing gaseous emissions from one or more cement plants and, in subsequent stages, from the gaseous emissions converting carbon dioxide to carbonate and bicarbonate ions in aqueous solution and precipitating calcium, magnesium carbonates and carbonate hydrates; and
   (5) a method of manufacturing cement, comprising forming a cement admixture having an additive comprising a precipitated calcium carbonate, and adding water to form a slurry.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive further comprises silica. The precipitated calcium carbonate is provided in substantially 1-25 wt.%, preferably 12.5 wt.% within the blended cement composition, or within a blended cement composition in which the admixture is used. Preferred Components: The calcium carbonate is calcite. The calcium carbonate is a mixture of amorphous and calcite solids. The silica is configured to react with cement. The cement is Portland cement. The calcium carbonate is used to reduce the cement content of cement products. Preferred Properties: The particle size of the calcium carbonate is substantially 0.8-1.5 mu m. The particle size of the calcium carbonate is nano-sized. Preferred Conditions: The calcium carbonate is precipitated under controlled conditions.

PN CN111019309-A
TI Modified polylactic acid foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. carboxy-terminated polylactic acid, polylactic acid, polyamide, porous grapheme, chain extender, coupling agent, plasticizer, and dispersant.
AU DING Y
   DING H
   LIN Q
   LV F
   YANG W
   DING X
AE FUJIAN ANDAFU NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202033938A
AB    NOVELTY - Modified polylactic acid foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprises 45-75 pts. wt. carboxy-terminated polylactic acid, 20-50 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 25-45 pts. wt. polyamide, 1-5 pts. wt. porous grapheme, 8-16 pts. wt. chain extender, 5-12 pts. wt. coupling agent, 1-8 pts. wt. plasticizer, 8-15 pts. wt. dispersant, 0.5-2.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer, and 1.2-3.8 pts. wt. antioxidant.
   USE - Used as modified polylactic acid foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has good sound insulation performance, light stability, cold and heat resistance, improves strength after foaming, increases plasticity of resin molecule, and makes more flexible and easy to process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing modified polylactic acid foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprising carboxy-terminated polylactic acid, polylactic acid, polyamide, porous graphene, chain extender, coupling agent, plasticizer, dispersant, light stabilizer and antioxidant, melt polyamide at 210-220 degrees C and cooling to 150-160 degrees C, adding carboxy-terminated polylactic acid and coupling agent, thermally insulating and stirring for 6-10 minutes to get mixture, adding polylactic acid, chain extender, plasticizer, dispersant, porous graphene to mixture in sequence, heating to 135-140 degrees C under nitrogen environment, reacting for 1-2 hours, cooling to 70-80 degrees C, adding light stabilizer and antioxidant, thermally insulating and stirring for 0.5-1 hours to obtain modified polylactic acid base, placing obtained modified polylactic acid base material in sealed pressure-resistant mold, heating to 130-135 degrees C, and introducing supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into mold to control pressure in the mold at 15.5-18.5 MPa for 3-6 hours, cooling to 100-110 degrees C and quickly release the pressure to obtain modified polylactic acid foamed material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The modified polylactic acid foam material based on supercritical carbon dioxide comprises 60 pts. wt. carboxy-terminated polylactic acid, 40 pts. wt. polylactic acid, 35 pts. wt. polyamide, 4 pts. wt. porous grapheme, 8 pts. wt. chain extender, 6 pts. wt. coupling agent, 4 pts. wt. plasticizer, 8 pts. wt. dispersant, 1.5 pts. wt. light stabilizer, and 2.4 pts. wt. antioxidant. Preferred Method: The polylactic acid is L-polylactic acid, D-polylactic acid, and/or external polylactic acid, and the molecular weight of the polylactic acid is 8.5x 104 to 5.5x 105 g/mol. The molecular weight of carboxy-terminated polylactic acid is 8x 104 to 6x 105 g/mol, and the proportion of carboxy groups in the terminal groups accounts for 70-80% of the total terminal group molar ratio. Preferred Components: The polyamide is a polyamide obtaining by ring-opening polymerization of a lactam having 6-14 carbon atoms. The plasticizer is epoxy soybean oil, tributyl citrate, acetyl tributyl citrate, and/or maltitol. The chain extender is triethanolamine, dimethylethanolamine, and/or t-BuDEAt, AND the coupling agent is a silane coupling agent or a titanate coupling agent. The light stabilizer is benzotriazole and benzophenone, the antioxidant is isooctyl 3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenylpropionate. The dispersant is aqueous solutions of methanol, ethanol, propanol, butanol and/or pentanol, and the mass fraction of alcohol in the aqueous solution is 55%.

PN CN110734422-A; WO2021083022-A1
TI Producing propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate by adding chlorine and hydrogen chloride remover, hypochlorous acid and propylene, chloropropanol and calcium hydroxide, propylene oxide and carbon dioxide to reaction unit.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
   WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 202011292A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate by (a) adding chlorine and hydrogen chloride remover to a first reaction unit, reacting with water and chlorine gas to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, and reacting hydrogen chloride and hydrogen chloride remover to obtain carbon dioxide and chloride, (b) adding hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid and propylene or ethylene into a second reaction unit, and reacting hypochlorous acid with propylene or ethylene to produce chloropropanol or chloroethanol, (c) adding chloropropanol or chloroethanol and calcium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide to a third reaction unit, reacting to form propylene oxide or ethylene oxide and calcium chloride or aqueous solution of sodium chloride, and (d) adding propylene oxide or ethylene oxide and carbon dioxide to a fourth reaction unit to obtain the target product.
   USE - The method is used for producing propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate by simple and economical process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing propylene carbonate or ethylene carbonate involves (a) adding chlorine and hydrogen chloride remover to a first reaction unit, reacting with water and chlorine gas to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, and reacting hydrogen chloride and hydrogen chloride remover to generate carbon dioxide and chloride, where the hydrogen chloride remover used is preferably calcium carbonate and sodium bicarbonate, (b)adding the hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid from the first reaction unit and propylene or ethylene into second reaction unit, and reacting the hypochlorous acid with propylene or ethylene to produce chloropropanol or chloroethanol, (c) adding the chloropropanol or chloroethanol from the second reaction unit, and calcium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide into third reaction unit, and reacting to form propylene oxide or ethylene oxide and calcium chloride or aqueous solution of sodium chloride, and (d) adding propylene oxide or ethylene oxide from third reaction unit and carbon dioxide from first reaction unit to fourth reaction unit to obtain target product.

TI Carbon dioxide methane reforming catalyst            comprises cobalt, magnesium oxide, noble metal and            aluminum oxide.
AU CHEN L
   CHEN P
   HUANG Q
   ZHANG D
   LIU W
   YANG T
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201883722E
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide methane reforming catalyst                comprises 8-18 wt.% cobalt, 5-15 wt.% magnesium                oxide, 0.002-0.5 wt.% noble metal and aluminum                oxide (balance amount), and the noble metal is                platinum, palladium, ruthenium, rhodium and/or                iridium.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide methane reforming                catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent synergistic effect,                increased catalytic activity by more than 30%                compared to the single metal cobalt-based or noble                metal-based aerogel catalyst, and excellent carbon                dioxide oxygenation methane reforming activity and                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing carbon dioxide methane reforming catalyst                comprising (i) taking inorganic salts of cobalt,                magnesium and aluminum, preparing                cobalt-magnesium-aluminum hydrogel by sol-gel                method, and washing with deionized water and                absolute ethanol to obtain                cobalt-magnesium-aluminum alcohol gel, the                inorganic salts of cobalt, magnesium and aluminum                are nitrates sulfate and a chloride, (ii) taking                inorganic salts of noble metals and absolute                ethanol, preparing solution of noble metal in                ethanol, adjusting the quality of precious metals                in ethanol solution according to the mass                percentage of cobalt in cobalt-magnesium-aluminum                gel, the inorganic salt of noble metals is noble                metal chloride, chlorates; (iii) mixing the cobalt                magnesium-aluminum alcohol gel obtained in the step                (i) with the noble metal-containing ethanol                solution obtained in the step (ii), and                ultrasonically dispersing for 10-30 minutes, (iv)                transferring the ultrasonically dispersed alcohol                gel and the noble metal-containing ethanol solution                mixture into the autoclave, supercritical drying at                260 degrees C with a pressure of 8-10 MPa for 2-4                hours, and then maintaining the temperature,                evaporating the ethanol at a rate of 8-15                ml/minutes to dry the product to obtain                cobalt-precious metal-magnesium-aluminum aerogel                powder, and (v) calcining obtained cobalt-precious                metal-magnesium-aluminum aerogel powder at 600-850                degrees C for 2-6 hours in an air atmosphere,                carrying out reduction at 750-800 degrees C for 2-3                hours in a hydrogen/inert gas mixture with a                hydrogen volume ratio of 50-100% to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The relationship                  between the noble metal and the mass percentage                  of cobalt is: precious metal = 0.0017 (cobalt%)                  2-0.072 (cobalt%) + 0.75. The inert gas nitrogen,                  argon and/or helium.

PN CN108659161-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide responsive polymer hollow microsphere involves transferring monomer I to dropping funnel, and dissolving initiator in deionized water to obtain solution II, and continuously introducing nitrogen gas to solution II.
AU FENG Y
   MU M
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2018836297
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide responsive polymer hollow microsphere involves transferring 1-5 pts. wt. monomer I to a dropping funnel, and dissolving 0.05-0.5 pts. wt. initiator in deionized water to obtain solution II, and continuously introducing nitrogen gas to solution II at normal temperature to remove oxygen and further dissolving 0.05-0.4 pts. wt. emulsifier in pure water to obtain emulsifier solution. The monomer I and solution II are simultaneously added dropwise to emulsifier solution under nitrogen protection at 40-80 degrees C, and the reaction is stirred for 1-7 hours and after the reaction is finished, slag is filtered to obtain seed microsphere emulsion. The monomer I is selected from styrene, methyl methacrylate, t-butyl methacrylate, butyl acrylate, or p-hydroxystyrene. 1-6 pts. wt. compound A, 0-0.6 pts. wt. styrene, and 0.01-0.5 pts. wt. crosslinking agent are mixed with mixed monomer III, and 0.02-0.1 pts. wt. initiator are dissolved in deionized water to obtain solution IV.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide responsive polymer hollow microsphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon dioxide responsive polymer hollow microsphere, which has environment-friendly response mode, microsphere shape is controllable, and carbon dioxide stimulation response is controllable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The solution IV is mixed with monomer III and are simultaneously added dropwise into the seed microsphere emulsion under nitrogen protection at 40-80 degrees C, and the reaction is stirred for 1-15 hours, then the reaction is filtered to obtain core-shell microsphere emulsion. The core-shell microsphere emulsion is reacted with an excess of organic solvent, then subjected to stirring and etching, during which the organic solvent is replaced for multiple times, and after solid etching for 30-40 hours to obtain solid by solid-liquid separation and obtaining the desired product. The compound A is represented by I. The compound A is represented by formula (I).
   R1=CH3, CH2CH3 or CH2CH2CH3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The emulsifier is selected from polyvinylpyrrolidone, Tween (RTM: Polysorbate surfactant) 60, Tween (RTM: Polysorbate surfactant) 80, Tween (RTM: Polysorbate surfactant) 85, sodium lauryl sulfate, or sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate. The crosslinking agent is selected from divinylbenzene, ethylene glycol diacrylate, or N,N-methylenebisacrylamide. The initiators are selected from water-soluble initiators such as ammonium persulfate, potassium persulfate, azodicyanovaleric acid, azobisisobutylphosphonium hydrochloride, azo, or diimidazolidinyl propane dihydrochloride. The organic solvent used in etching is selected from tetrahydrofuran, chloroform, ethyl acetate, toluene, petroleum ether or acetone. Preferred Conditions: The organic solvent used for etching is 5-15 times the core-shell microsphere emulsion to be etched. The monomer I and solution II are simultaneously added dropwise to emulsifier solution and continuously passed through the nitrogen gas for 0.5-1 hours, where oxygen is removed by nitrogen. The organic solvent is replaced in every 6 hours. The amount of the deionized water added with initiator and with emulsifier corresponds to the soluble solute added to it.

AB    NOVELTY - Fixation of carbon dioxide involves contacting                (i) solution containing sodium hydroxide and gas                containing carbon dioxide, diluting (ii) 0.01-2 N                sodium hydroxide in solution and adding (iii)                calcium chloride to solution. The mixed solution                comprises 0.05-0.5 mol/L calcium chloride.
   USE - Fixation of carbon dioxide contained in                combustion exhaust gas, during manufacturing                process (claimed).

Preferred Process: The fixation of carbon dioxide                  is performed by feeding gas into the solution and                  the mixed solution in at least one of step (i)                  and contact step (iii) and cooling liquid mixture                  after step (iii). Preferred Condition: The                  fixation of carbon dioxide is carried out at                  temperature of the solution and the mixed                  solution is 70 degrees C or more in at least one                  of step (i) and step (iii).

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a low-reduced iron production unit (700) for producing low reduced iron by receiving powdered iron ore. A high-purity carbon dioxide separator separates and discharges carbon dioxide from exhaust gas generated from the production unit. A blast furnace (600) is provided for producing molten iron. A heater (1100) is used for heating the gas from which hydrogen is removed from a hydrogen separator (1000) and supplying the gas to the blast furnace. A carbon dioxide decomposer (1300) decomposes some of the carbon dioxide discharged from the separators and supplies the decomposed carbon dioxide to the manufacturing unit, where a sintered ore manufacturing unit is connected to a rear end of the furnace.
   USE - Carbon dioxide emission reduction type molten iron manufacturing apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus replaces existing carbon fuel, and reduces overall carbon dioxide emissions, and increases utilization efficiency.

TI Producing synthetic fuel from hydrogen and carbon            dioxide by extracting hydrogen molecules from hydrogen            compounds in hydrogen feedstock, extracting carbon            dioxide molecules from dilute gaseous mixture, and            producing synthetic fuel.
AU HEIDEL K
   MURPHY J
   CORLESS A
   HOLMES G
   MCCAHILL J
   STUKAS A
   RITCHIE J
   NOLD K
AE CARBON ENG LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON ENG LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON ENG LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
   CARBON ENG LTD (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 201851469B
AB    NOVELTY - Synthetic fuel is produced (I) from hydrogen                and carbon dioxide by (a) extracting hydrogen                molecules from hydrogen compounds in hydrogen                feedstock to produce hydrogen-containing feed                stream; (b) extracting carbon dioxide molecules                from dilute gaseous mixture in carbon dioxide                feedstock to produce carbon dioxide containing feed                stream; and (c) processing hydrogen and carbon                dioxide containing feed streams to produce                synthetic fuel, where a portion of material used in                greater than or equal to 1 steps of extracting                hydrogen molecules, extracting carbon dioxide                molecules, and processing hydrogen and carbon                dioxide containing feed streams is obtained from                material produced in another one of the steps of                these steps.
   USE - Production of synthetic fuel from hydrogen and                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - A cost-effective, operationally efficient, and                operationally flexible overall system can be                improved by having one subsystem use energy and/or                fluids produced by another subsystem, rather than                obtaining the energy and/or fluids from an external                source. Furthermore, the system can potentially                reduce the carbon intensity of the produced synfuel                compared to conventional fossil fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                (100) for producing synthetic fuel from hydrogen                and carbon dioxide comprising hydrogen production                subsystem (103) to extract hydrogen molecules from                hydrogen compounds in hydrogen feedstock to produce                hydrogen containing feed stream, carbon dioxide                capture subsystem (101) to extract carbon dioxide                molecules from dilute gaseous mixture in carbon                dioxide feedstock to produce carbon dioxide                containing feed stream, and synthetic fuel                production subsystem (102) to process hydrogen and                carbon dioxide containing feed streams to produce                synthetic fuel, where subsystems are physically                coupled to another one of the subsystems by a                material transfer coupling for transferring a                portion of material produced in one subsystem to                another one of the subsystems for use.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a system for producing synthetic fuel from                hydrogen and carbon dioxide including carbon                dioxide capture subsystem, hydrogen production                sub-system, and synthetic fuel production                subsystem.
   System for producing synthetic fuel from                hydrogen and carbon dioxide (100)
   Carbon dioxide capture subsystem (101)
   Synthetic fuel production subsystem                (102)
   Hydrogen production subsystem (103)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: Hydrogen feedstock is                  water. Dilute gaseous mixture is air. Produced                  material includes water produced during of steps                  (b) or (c), and a portion of produced water is                  used for a portion of hydrogen feedstock.                  Extracting carbon dioxide molecules comprises                  contacting dilute gaseous mixture with carbon                  dioxide capture solution, precipitating a portion                  of captured carbon dioxide into calcium carbonate                  (CaCO3) solids, calcining CaCO3 solids to produce                  calciner product gas stream, and extracting water                  from calciner product gas stream to produce a                  portion of produced water. A portion of produced                  water is steam. SGR reactant feed streams                  comprises carbon dioxide reactant feed stream,                  hydrogen reactant feed stream, CH4 reactant feed                  stream, water reactant feed stream, or                  Fischer-Tropsch light end hydrocarbon reactant                  feed stream, where carbon dioxide reactant feed                  stream includes a portion of carbon dioxide                  containing feed stream, and hydrogen reactant                  feed stream comprises a portion of hydrogen                  containing feed stream. Preferred Processes: Step                  of processing hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  containing feed streams comprises combining and                  heating hydrogen and carbon dioxide containing                  feed streams, producing syngas stream, and                  extracting water from syngas stream to produce a                  portion of produced water. Step of extracting                  carbon dioxide molecules comprises contacting                  dilute gaseous mixture with carbon dioxide                  capture solution, precipitating a portion of                  captured carbon dioxide into CaCO3 solids,                  calcining CaCO3 solids to produce calciner                  product gas stream, and further comprising                  feeding a portion of calciner product gas stream                  to solid oxide electrolyzer cell used in the step                  of extracting hydrogen molecules. Extracting                  carbon dioxide molecules further comprises a                  slaker, produced material includes water produced                  during processing of hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  containing feed streams and a portion of water                  produced is used by the slaker. Produced material                  includes oxygen molecules produced during                  extracting hydrogen molecules, and the method (I)                  further includes combusting fuel using a portion                  of produced oxygen molecules during extracting                  carbon dioxide molecules and/or processing                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide containing feed                  streams. Combusting a portion of produced oxygen                  molecules and fuel produces heat for producing                  calciner product gas stream during extracting                  carbon dioxide molecules. Combusting a portion of                  produced oxygen molecules and fuel produces heat                  for producing syngas stream during processing                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide containing feed                  streams. The method (I) further includes                  regenerating carbon dioxide rich aqueous capture                  solution during extracting carbon dioxide                  molecules using a portion of produced oxygen                  molecules and fuel. Produced material includes                  fuel produced during processing hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide containing feed streams, and the                  method (I) further includes combusting a portion                  of produced fuel during extracting carbon dioxide                  molecules and/or processing hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide containing feed streams. The method (I)                  further includes regenerating carbon dioxide rich                  aqueous capture solution during extracting carbon                  dioxide molecules using a portion of produced                  fuel. A portion of energy for extracting hydrogen                  molecules, extracting carbon dioxide molecules,                  and processing the hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  containing feed streams is provided by an                  electricity source. Extracting carbon dioxide                  molecules comprises operating calciner to produce                  carbon dioxide containing feed stream, where                  processing hydrogen and carbon dioxide containing                  feed streams comprises operating syngas                  generation reactor (SGR) unit at a pressure                  selected to enable the SGR unit to receive carbon                  dioxide containing feed stream from the calciner                  without being substantially cooled and compressed                  between the calciner and the SGR unit. The method                  (I) further includes feeding carbon dioxide                  containing feed stream and reactant feed streams                  into the SGR unit, the reactant feed streams                  comprising hydrogen reactant feed stream, methane                  (CH4) reactant feed stream, water reactant feed                  stream, or Fischer-Tropsch light end hydrocarbon                  reactant feed stream. The method (I) further                  includes operating the SGR unit to produce syngas                  product stream by reverse water gas shift (RWGS)                  reaction, steam methane reforming (SMR) reaction,                  and direct methane reforming (DMR) reaction. The                  method (I) further includes treating syngas                  product stream to produce recycle streams that                  provide reactant to the SGR unit. The method (I)                  further comprises electrically heating recycle                  streams and/or reactant feed streams. The method                  (I) further includes heating SGR unit with                  thermal energy produced by electricity. The                  method (I) further includes heating the SGR unit                  with thermal energy produced by combusting an                  oxidant and a fuel comprising hydrogen from                  hydrogen-containing feed stream, natural gas, or                  Fischer-Tropsch light end hydrocarbon. Extracting                  carbon dioxide molecules comprises heating the                  calciner with thermal energy produced by                  combusting an oxidant and a fuel comprising                  hydrogen from hydrogen-containing stream, natural                  gas, or Fischer-Tropsch light end hydrocarbons.                  Extracting hydrogen molecules further comprises                  producing oxygen containing stream, a portion of                  which is used as the oxidant by one or both of                  the SGR unit and the calciner. The method (I)                  further includes heating CaCO3 material stream                  used in extracting carbon dioxide molecules with                  thermal energy from syngas product stream from                  SGR unit. The method (I) further includes                  directly contacting CaCO3 material stream with                  syngas product stream and operating SGR in RWGS                  mode, SMR mode, and/or DMR mode. The method (I)                  further includes heating the calciner with                  thermal energy produced by electricity.                  Extracting carbon dioxide molecules further                  comprises calcining CaCO3 material in a fluidized                  bed reactor vessel of the calciner, and                  discharging a hot CaO solids stream from the                  calciner. The method (I) further includes                  pre-heating CaCO3 material before entry into the                  calciner with thermal energy from a calciner                  product gas stream. The method (I) further                  includes extracting water from calciner product                  gas stream, boiling extracted water to produce                  steam, and fluidizing in fluidized bed reactor                  vessel with the steam. The method (I) further                  includes preheating SGR reactant feed streams                  before feeding to the SGR unit, with thermal                  energy from a syngas product stream discharged                  from the SGR unit. The method (I) further                  includes combusting oxidant and a fuel in an SGR                  burner of the SGR unit and producing a hot burner                  exhaust stream, then heating oxidant feed stream                  of SGR burner and/or water reactant feed stream                  to SGR unit, using thermal energy from the hot                  burner exhaust stream. Preferred Conditions: SGR                  unit is operated at a pressure of 1-10 bar, and                  received carbon dioxide containing feed stream                  has a temperature of 850-900 degrees C.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling carbon dioxide involves introducing a mixed gas containing carbon dioxide into a carbon dioxide separation/recovery unit (B) for separating carbon dioxide, mixing hydrogen with the separated carbon dioxide, introducing the mixed gas into a space (E1) of a separation membrane reactor (E) having the space (E1) on the non-permeate side and a space (E2) on the permeate side filled with a catalyst for promoting the reaction, and a water vapor separation membrane partitioned by a tube (E3) for reacting the carbon dioxide and hydrogen, passing and separating the water vapor which is a reaction byproduct in the space (E1) through the space (E2), and allowing the sweep gas to flow through the space (E2) for flowing out the water vapor. The sweep gas is carbon dioxide separated by the unit (B) or a mixed gas from which carbon dioxide is separated.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient recycling of carbon dioxide, by simple and economical process.

   Hydrogen tank (C)
   Separation membrane reactor (E)
   Spaces (E1,E2)
   Tube (E3)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is separated by pressure swing method. Method for recycling carbon dioxide further involves supplying the sweep gas flowing out of the space (E2) to a tube which circulates the byproduct gas of an iron mill. Preferred Component: The sweep gas is a gas dehumidified in the front stage and/or the back stage of the unit (B), preferably the mixed gas from which carbon dioxide is separated.

PT P
PN CN109486660-A
TI Carbon dioxide absorption tower photobioreactor            comprises carbon dioxide intake unit and reaction unit,            where reaction unit is carbon dioxide absorption tower            with gas distributor installed at bottom, and reactor            also includes feed unit.
AU CHENG J
   CEN K
   LIU J
   ZHOU J
   WANG Z
   YANG W
   ZHOU Z
   ZHANG Y
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201927137C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorption tower                photobioreactor comprises a carbon dioxide intake                unit and a reaction unit, where the reaction unit                is a carbon dioxide absorption tower with a gas                distributor installed at the bottom, and the                reactor also includes a sodium bicarbonate feed                unit, sodium bicarbonate discharge unit, carbon                dioxide recovery unit, and microalgae culture                photobioreactor unit (6). The carbon dioxide intake                unit comprises a carbon dioxide gas source (1),                dryer (2-1), pressure control valve (3) and check                valve (4) arranged in sequence, and is connected to                the gas distributor (7) at the bottom of the carbon                dioxide absorption tower through a pipeline. The                sodium carbonate feed unit comprises a sodium                carbonate dissolution tank (9), a pressure pump                (10) and an on-off valve (11) arranged in sequence,                which are connected to the top of the carbon                dioxide absorption tower (8) through a pipeline.                The discharging unit comprises a product outlet                switching valve.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorption tower                photobioreactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide absorption tower                photobioreactor overcomes the technical problems of                short carbon dioxide residence time and low                utilization rate of traditional runway pool                aerators, and is economically feasible, which can                improve the utilization of carbon dioxide and                promote the growth of microalgae.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for cultivating microalgae using the carbon dioxide                absorption tower photobioreactor, which                involves:
   (A) preparing a sodium carbonate solution                having a concentration of 200 mM/L in a sodium                carbonate dissolution tank, and injecting a carbon                dioxide absorption tower from the top through a                pressure pump, and controlling a volume ratio of                the sodium carbonate solution in the tower to                0.4-0.6;
   (B) introducing the dry carbon dioxide gas                into the gas distributor at the bottom of the                carbon dioxide absorption tower, and fully reacting                by bubbling in a sodium carbonate solution, such                that the reaction temperature is 30-50 degrees C,                adjusting the intake pressure to control the intake                air volume of carbon dioxide, so that the pressure                inside the tower is maintained at 0.3-0.5                megaapscal, discharging the unreacted carbon                dioxide gas from the top of the tower and recycled                through the carbon dioxide recovery system;
   (C) controlling the reaction time in the                control tower to 60-120 minutes, so that the                reaction product at the bottom of the column has                molar ratio of Sodium bicarbonate/sodium carbonate                to 7-9;
   (D) sending the reaction product to the                microalgae culture photobioreactor system, and                inoculating the algae solution, such that the                initial absorbance OD is 0.3-0.5, light intensity                is 8,000-12,000 lux, ambient temperature is 20-35                degrees C, and the solution pH is 7-10; and
   (E) adjusting the carbon dioxide intake amount                and the sodium carbonate feed amount according to                the processing capacity of the microalgae culture                photobioreactor system, maintaining the                HCO3-concentration in the carbon dioxide absorption                tower reaction product at 200-400 mM/L to promote                the growth of the microalgae.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon                dioxide absorption tower photobioreactor.
   Carbon dioxide gas source (1)
   Dryer (2-1)
   Pressure control valve (3)
   Check valve (4)
   Microalgae culture photobioreactor unit                (6)
   Gas distributor (7)
   Carbon dioxide absorption tower (8)
   Sodium carbonate dissolution tank (9)
   Pressure pump (10)
   On-off valve (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The dryer is                  internally filled with a color changing silica                  gel. The gas distributor has a pore size of less                  than or equal to 1 mm and a porosity of greater                  than or equal to 0.8. The raw materials used to                  prepare the sodium carbonate solution are                  chemically pure sodium carbonate, a natural                  alkali rich in sodium carbonate, or a microalgae                  aging liquid containing sodium carbonate. The                  microalgae cultured in the microalgae culture                  photobioreactor system is green algae,                  cyanobacteria or diatom.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
