PT P
PN US2020141638-A1; US11125495-B2
TI Process equipment for preparing enhanced oil recovery, comprises heat exchanger configured to receive carbon dioxide recycle stream and cool carbon dioxide recycle stream to produce cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream.
AU PRIM E
AE PILOT ENERGY SOLUTIONS LLC (PILO-Non-standard)
GA 2020374525
AB    NOVELTY - Process equipment comprises heat exchanger configured to receive a carbon dioxide recycle stream and cool the carbon dioxide recycle stream to produce a cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream, where the cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 1-8C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of water, a multi-stage column configured to receive the cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream and produce a top effluent stream and a bottom effluent stream, a single stage separator configured to receive the top effluent stream and produce a vapor stream and a liquid stream, where the vapor stream is transferred to the heat exchanger to produce a heated vapor stream. The heated vapor stream comprises carbon dioxide and 1-4C hydrocarbons, and an expander configured to receive the bottom effluent stream and produce an expanded bottom effluent stream, where the expanded bottom effluent stream comprises 3-8C hydrocarbons.
   USE - Process equipment used for preparing enhanced oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using an equipment, which involves:
   (A) receiving by a dehydrator, a feed stream comprising water, carbon dioxide, and 1-8C hydrocarbons, dehydrating by the dehydrator, the feed stream to produce a carbon dioxide recycle stream, where the carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises carbon dioxide, 1-8C hydrocarbons, and is substantially free of water;
   (B) cooling by a heat exchanger, the carbon dioxide recycle stream to produce a cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream, separating by a multi-stage separator, the cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream into a top effluent stream and a bottom effluent stream, separating, by a single-stage separator, the top effluent stream into a vapor stream and a liquid stream;
   (C) heating by the heat exchanger, the vapor stream to produce a heated vapor stream, where the heated vapor stream comprises carbon dioxide and 1-4C hydrocarbons; and
   (D) expanding by an expander, the bottom effluent stream to produce an expanded bottom effluent stream, where the expanded bottom effluent stream comprises 3-8C hydrocarbons.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrator comprises a molecular sieve. The multi-stage separator comprises a distillation column. The carbon dioxide recycle stream, the heated vapor stream, and the bottom effluent stream are not subjected to cryogenic conditions. The carbon dioxide recycle stream, the heated vapor stream, and the bottom effluent stream are not processed by membranes. The carbon dioxide recycle stream, the heated vapor stream, and the bottom effluent stream are not subjected to carbon dioxide recovery solvents. The feed stream is received from a subterranean hydrocarbon formation. The heated vapor stream is injected into a subterranean hydrocarbon formation. The cooled carbon dioxide recycle stream comprises vapor. The bottom effluent stream comprises liquid. The method involves receiving a feed stream, and dehydrating the feed stream to produce the carbon dioxide recycle stream. The carbon dioxide recycle stream, the heated vapor stream, and the bottom effluent stream are not subjected to carbon dioxide recovery solvents.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR1872851-B1
TI Hybrid carbon dioxide sampling system, has reboiler for supplying heat to perform absorbent regeneration, first line for supplying air passing compressor to reboiler, and second line for supplying vapor ejected in reboiler to turbine.
AU CHO H H
   KIM S H
   CHOI S M
   PARK S
   SOHN H S
   KIM S
AE UNIV YONSEI IND ACADEMIC COOP FOUND (UYIA-C)
GA 201853718S
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a gas-turbine installation part (10) provided with a compressor (11) for condensing air. A turbine (13) ejects compressed air from the compressor. A combustor (12) combusts fuel and discharges combustion gas. A carbon dioxide collecting device (20) is provided with a filtering portion (21) for removing particulate in exhaust gas. An absorptive tower (22) collects carbon dioxide by reacting the carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide absorbent. A heat (23) absorbs the carbon dioxide. A stripper (24) reproduces and separates the carbon dioxide absorbent. A reboiler (25) supplies necessary heat to perform absorbent regeneration. A first line (40) supplies air passing the compressor to the reboiler. A second line supplies vapor ejected in the reboiler to the turbine.
   USE - Hybrid carbon dioxide sampling system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system separates the carbon dioxide sampled in the carbon dioxide collecting device, and reproduces the carbon dioxide absorbent without using energy from a heat recovery steam generator so as to solve environment problem, reduce energy cost and improve energy efficiency. The system supplies the carbon dioxide reproduced in the carbon dioxide collecting device to the gas-turbine installation part so as to reduce temperature of necessary working fluid flowing from high-temperature parts e.g. combustion chamber, vane blade and casing, amount of compressed air in the compressor according to mixing ratio, thus improving system efficiency. The system circulates the carbon dioxide when performing carbon dioxide depositing process in a combined cycle thermal power plant, so that amount of the carbon dioxide included with exhaust gas can be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram of a hybrid carbon dioxide sampling system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Gas-turbine installation part (10)
   Compressor (11)
   Combustor (12)
   Turbine (13)
   Carbon dioxide collecting device (20)
   Filtering portion (21)
   Absorptive tower (22)
   Heat (23)
   Stripper (24)
   Reboiler (25)
   Line (40)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN107974844-A
TI Dyeing natural fiber e.g. cotton by adding natural plant dye, stabilizer and liquid carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, heating, adding natural fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, and dyeing.
AU CHU P
   XU Y
AE JIANGSU NEW REBA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201835120Y
AB    NOVELTY - Dyeing natural fiber includes adding natural plant dye, stabilizer and liquid carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, heating, keeping the temperature, adding natural fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, dyeing, controlling the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device to release pressure, recovering carbon dioxide and natural plant dye, controlling the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device to release pressure, cooling to room temperature, reducing to atmospheric pressure to obtain dyed natural fiber, acetone cleaning and washing with water.
   USE - Dyeing natural fiber to obtain dyed natural fiber i.e. cotton, hemp, silk, fur or yarn, fabric, or garment made from natural fiber (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes natural dye dyeing and printing of natural fiber, does not need pretreatment, has good environmental protection and good color fixing effect, and saves water resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dyeing natural fiber comprises:
   (A) adding natural plant dye, stabilizer and liquid carbon dioxide into supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, heating at 105-125 degrees C, keeping the temperature for 20-30 minutes, adding natural fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, and dyeing at 130-150 degrees C and 18-25 MPa for 90-110 minutes;
   (B) controlling the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device to release pressure, and recovering carbon dioxide and natural plant dye at 25-35 degrees C and 4-6 MPa;
   (C) controlling the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device to release pressure, cooling to room temperature, and reducing to atmospheric pressure to obtain dyed natural fiber;
   (D) acetone cleaning, washing with water, drying, adding into supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing device, adding natural plant dye and liquid carbon dioxide, and dyeing at 90-120 degrees C and 18-25 MPa for 70-120 minutes; and
   (E) repeating steps (B) and (C), and obtaining product i.e. dyed natural fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The natural plant dye is rose dye, violet dye, saffron dye, purple dye and/or turmeric dye. The natural plant dye after extraction has a granularity of 80-100 mesh. The stabilizer is dimethyl formamide, ethylenediamine and/or diethylenetriamine. It is added at 15-20 g/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The mass ratio of natural plant dye and natural fiber is 1:5-1:10, preferably 1:7.
DC A83 (Clothing, footwear.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)
MC A08-E03; A08-M01A; A09-D03; A12-S05N; A12-S05P; F03-F31; F03-F33

PN WO2019129652-A1
TI Carbon dioxide source for growing plant comprises absorbent material acting as a bearer, and carbonate material used for supplying carbon dioxide within the absorbent material, and emitting gaseous carbon dioxide into the soil of a plant.
AU UGUR ERINC G
   SEZER M
   MERT S
   KAYMAKCI O
AE ARCELIK AS (ARCK-C)
GA 2019578597
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide source comprises an absorbent material acting as a bearer, and carbonate material used for supplying carbon dioxide (CO2) within the absorbent material, and emitting gaseous CO2 into the soil of a plant into which it is embedded as a result of carbonate material reacting with an acidic solution.
   USE - A carbon dioxide source for growing plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is emitted by using methods which are low cost, controllable, without risks and using natural resources, and producing carbon dioxide gas in natural conditions without using carbon dioxide bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide source comprises an absorbent material with a gel mesh structure and carbonate material entrapped within the gel mesh structure, formed by mixing a water-soluble carbonate material into a synthesis solution during synthesizing of the absorbent material. The carbon dioxide source comprises greater than or equal to 1 carbonate salt consisting of carbonate and/or bicarbonate salts. The carbon dioxide source comprises greater than or equal to 1 absorbent material selected from superabsorbent polymers, hydrogels and zeolites. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide source is formed by dissolving a carbonate material in a nutrient solution, and impregnating together with water into ready-made absorbent material granules, powder or particles.

TI Online monitoring of dye concentration and automatically controlling circulating pump flow comprises e.g. collecting detected dye concentration data and transmitting to computer automatic analysis control unit by dye concentration detector unit, and adjusting frequency of circulating pump.
AU GUO K

   DU J

   LIN D

   LIU C

   YANG W
   WANG J
AE QINGDAO JIFA GROUP CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202268374W
AB    NOVELTY - Online monitoring of dye concentration and automatically controlling circulating pump flow comprises (i) turning on the booster pump and pressurizes carbon dioxide to a predetermined amount, heating, rising carbon dioxide, and pressure rises to 25-30 MPa to reach the pressure and temperature required by the process conditions, automatically adjusting the circulating pump to run at a lower power, adjusting opening of circulating pump to the maximum power and flow for the fastest dissolution of dyes, (ii) using detector in the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing circulation system to detect the concentration of dyes in the system in real time, and (iii) collecting detected dye concentration data and transmitting to the computer automatic analysis control unit by the dye concentration detector unit, and automatically adjusting the frequency of the circulating pump according to the received circulating pump control instruction the circulating pump flow control unit.
   USE - The method is useful for online monitoring of dye concentration and automatically controlling circulating pump flow.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the dyeing quality, and reduces the circulating pump loss and energy loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Online monitoring of dye concentration and automatically controlling circulating pump flow comprises (i) turning on the booster pump and pressurizes the carbon dioxide to a predetermined amount at specific temperature, heating, rising carbon dioxide to 120-130degrees Celsius, and pressure rises to 25-30 MPa to reach the pressure and temperature required by the process conditions, automatically adjusting the circulating pump to run at a lower power according to preset rules by computer automatic analysis and control unit before reaching the pressure and temperature required by the process conditions, adjusting the opening of the circulating pump to the maximum power and flow for the fastest dissolution of dyes after reaching the pressure and temperature required by the process conditions, (ii) using the detector in the supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing circulation system to detect the concentration of dyes in the system in real time, and (iii) collecting the detected dye concentration data and transmitting to the computer automatic analysis control unit by the dye concentration detector unit, comparing and calculating the received dye concentration data with its pre-stored process by the computer automatic analysis control unit, generating the corresponding circulating pump control command and sending to the circulating pump flow control unit, and automatically adjusting the frequency of the circulating pump according to the received circulating pump control instruction the circulating pump flow control unit. The system comprises supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous dyeing system and dye concentration online monitoring system. The supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous dyeing system includes a circulating pump, a valve, a dye kettle, a dyeing kettle, and a pipeline. The dye concentration online monitoring system includes a dye concentration detector unit, a computer automatic analysis control unit, and a circulating pump flow control unit. The circulating pump flow control unit of the dye concentration online monitoring system is connected with the circulating pump in the supercritical carbon monoxide Anhydrous dyeing system, and the dye concentration detector unit is connected with the dyeing kettle inlet in the supercritical carbon monoxide anhydrous dyeing system.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for computer-readable storage medium, comprising a set of instructions for online monitoring of dye concentration and automatically controlling circulating pump flow.

TI Producing biomethane comprises separating feed            stream containing methane and impurities, separating            first retentate stream, feeding first permeate stream            to high recovery module, compressing, separating and            forming feed stream.
AU MITARITEN M J
AE AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
GA 201964602V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomethane, comprises: separating a                feed stream containing methane and impurities at a                first purification stage comprising at least one                membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane                into a first retentate stream and a first permeate                stream; separating the first retentate stream at a                second purification stage comprising at least one                membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane                into a second retentate stream and a second                permeate stream, the second retentate stream being                biomethane; feeding the first permeate stream to a                high recovery module comprising a second compressor                and a third purification stage comprising at least                one membrane in parallel or in series; compressing                the first permeate stream at the secondary                compressor; separating the compressed first                permeate stream at the third purification stage                into a third retentate stream and a third permeate                stream; and the feed stream is formed by combining                the third retentate stream with the combined                stream.
   USE - The method is useful for producing                biomethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high recovery of                biomethane from biogas using gas separation                membranes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) installation for producing biomethane                containing at least 94% methane, comprising a                source of a biogas, a main compressor, an inlet of                which is in fluid communicating with the source, a                first purification stage comprising at least one                membrane selective for carbon dioxide over methane,                an inlet of the first purification stage being in                fluid communication with an outlet of the main                compressor, a second purification stage comprising                at least one membrane selective for carbon dioxide                over methane, an inlet of the second purification                stage being in fluid communication with a retentate                outlet of the first purification stage, and a high                recovery module comprising a third purification                stage comprising one or more membranes in parallel                or in series that are selective for carbon dioxide                over methane and a secondary compressor in fluid                communication between a permeate outlet of the                first purification stage and an inlet of the third                purification stage, where a retentate outlet of the                third purification stage is in fluid communication                with the first purification stage inlet so as to                allow a retentate stream from the third                purification stage to be recycled to the first                purification stage; and
   (2) increasing a recovery of methane in                biomethane product gas produced by an existing                installation for producing biomethane comprising a                source of a biogas, a main compressor, a first                purification stage comprising at least one membrane                selective for carbon dioxide over methane, a second                purification stage comprising at least one membrane                selective for carbon dioxide over methane, an inlet                of the main compressor being in fluid communication                downstream of the source, an inlet of the first                purification stage being in fluid communication                downstream of an outlet of the main compressor, an                inlet of the second purification stage being in                fluid communication downstream of a retentate                outlet of the first purification stage, a permeate                outlet of the second purification stage being in                fluid communication with the inlet of the main                compressor, the method comprising the steps of                providing a high recovery module comprising a                secondary compressor and a third purification stage                comprising at least one membrane in series or in                parallel that are selective for carbon dioxide over                methane, placing an inlet of the secondary                compressor in downstream flow communication with a                permeate outlet of the first purification stage,                placing an inlet of the third purification stage in                downstream flow communication with an outlet of the                secondary compressor and placing a retentate outlet                of the third purification stage in fluid                communication with the inlet of the first                purification stage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises a step of feeding the second permeate                  stream and a raw biogas stream to a main                  compressor and compressing them so as to form a                  combined stream, the raw biogas stream comprising                  40-75% methane and impurities of greater than 0%                  and up to 10% nitrogen, greater than 0% and up to                  3% oxygen, 20-55% carbon dioxide, greater than 0                  ppm and up to 5000 ppm hydrogen sulfide, 0-100                  ppm siloxanes, greater than 0 ppm and up to                  1000-2000 ppm of C6+hydrocarbons and volatile                  organic compounds (VOCs), and greater than 0%                  water. The first retentate stream comprises at                  least 60% methane, the first permeate stream                  comprises less than 20% methane, the second                  retentate stream comprises at least 94% methane,                  the second permeate stream comprises less than                  80% methane, the third retentate stream comprises                  at least 40% methane. In the method, an amount of                  water is removed from the combined stream at a                  dehydration unit or through cooling after                  compression prior to combination with the third                  retentate stream. The method further comprises:                  the steps of feeding the second permeate stream                  and a raw biogas stream to a main compressor and                  compressing them so as to form a combined stream                  and forming the feed stream is formed by                  combining the third retentate stream with the                  combined stream, where the second permeate stream                  comprises at least 40% methane, at least 20%                  carbon dioxide, less than 1000 ppm hydrogen                  sulfide, less than 1000 ppm C6+hydrocarbons, VOCs                  and siloxane, and less than 0.05% water and the                  first permeate stream comprises 5-15% methane,                  greater than 0 ppm but up to 10000 ppm of                  hydrogen sulfide, greater than 0% but up to 3%                  water, and up to 95% carbon dioxide; the steps of                  feeding the combined stream to an                  adsorption-based purification unit where amounts                  of C6+hydrocarbons present in the combined stream                  are removed by the adsorption-based purification                  unit, the adsorption-based purification unit                  comprising an adsorbent bed, the adsorption-based                  purification unit being selected from the group                  consisting of a non-regenerative media unit, a                  pressure swing adsorption unit, a temperature                  swing adsorption unit, and a pressure-temperature                  swing adsorption unit and optionally, when the                  adsorption-based purification unit is a pressure                  swing adsorption unit, a temperature swing                  adsorption unit or a pressure-temperature swing                  adsorption unit, regenerating the adsorption bed                  with the third permeate stream; the steps of                  compressing the second permeate stream and a raw                  biogas stream at a main compressor so as to form                  a combined stream and forming the feed stream by                  combining the third retentate stream with the                  combined stream, where the raw biogas stream has                  a methane concentration of 40-75% and at least                  98% of methane in the raw biogas stream is                  recovered in the second retentate stream, the                  step of removing amounts of hydrogen sulfide from                  the second retentate stream with a hydrogen                  sulfide scavenger media so as to achieve a                  hydrogen sulfide concentration in the second                  retentate stream of less than 4 ppm of hydrogen                  sulfide. The method, where after water removal at                  the dehydration unit and prior to combination                  with the third retentate stream, hydrogen sulfide                  levels in the combined stream are reduced to less                  than 4 ppm using hydrogen sulfide scavenger                  media. The third purification stage comprises a                  first group of at least one membrane that is in                  series with a second group of at least one                  membranes, the first permeate stream is separated                  by the first group into an intermediate retentate                  stream and an intermediate permeate stream, the                  intermediate retentate stream is fed to the                  second group where it is separated into the third                  permeate stream and the third permeate stream.                  The at least one membrane of the first                  purification stage is selective for hydrogen                  sulfide, C6+hydrocarbons, carbon dioxide,                  siloxane and water over methane. The membrane of                  the second purification stage is selectivity for                  methane over carbon dioxide. The method, where a                  pressure differential over the membrane between                  the first permeate stream and the feed stream is                  less than 50 psig. The second permeate stream                  comprises at least 40% methane, at least 20%                  carbon dioxide, less than 1000 ppm hydrogen                  sulfide, less than 1000 ppm of C6+ hydrocarbons                  and siloxane, and less than 0.05% water. The                  first permeate stream comprises 5-15% methane,                  greater than 0 ppm but up to 10000 ppm of                  hydrogen sulfide, greater than 0% but up to 3%                  water, and up to 95% carbon dioxide. The membrane                  of the third stage is the same as the at least                  one membrane of the first stage. The membrane of                  the third stage is the same as the at least one                  membrane of the second stage. The product gas is                  comprised of at least 94% methane, below 3%                  carbon dioxide, below 150 ppm of hydrogen                  sulfide, below 100 ppm of VOC's, and below 0.01%                  water. The installation, where a permeate outlet                  of the second purification stage is in fluid                  communication with the main compressor inlet so                  as to allow a permeate stream from the second                  purification stage to be recycled to the main                  compressor. The installation further comprises a                  dehydration unit in fluid communication between                  the main compressor outlet and the first                  purification stage inlet that is adapted and                  configured to remove amounts of water from a                  combination stream received from the compressor                  outlet prior to the combination stream being fed                  to the first purification stage inlet. The                  dehydration unit is a condenser or dehydration                  media. The membrane of the first purification                  stage is in the form of flat films or many hollow                  fibers. The high recovery module further                  comprises a fourth purification stage comprising                  at least one membrane selective for carbon                  dioxide over methane, an inlet of the fourth                  purification being in downstream flow                  communication with a retentate outlet of the                  third purification stage, a permeate outlet of                  the second purification stage is in fluid                  communication with the main compressor inlet so                  as to allow a permeate stream from the second                  purification stage to be recycled to the main                  compressor and a retentate outlet of the fourth                  purification stage is in fluid communication with                  the first purification stage inlet so as to allow                  a retentate stream from the fourth purification                  stage to be recycled to the first purification                  stage.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The membrane of the                  first purification stage comprises a porous                  polymeric substrate having at least one                  separation layer, the substrate comprising                  polyimides, poly sulfones, polyether ether                  ketones and/or mixtures. The substrate is a                  polyether ether ketone. The separation layer is                  made of benzene-1,4-dicarbaldehyde repeating                  units of formula (Ia). The separation layer is                  made of a copolymer or block polymer of                  tetramethylene oxide, propylene oxide, and/or                  ethylene oxide.The membrane of the second                  purification stage is comprised of a material                  comprising cellulose acetate, a polysulfone, a                  polyimide and/or mixtures.

TI Preparing metal-organic frameworks-74-nickel cobalt material, comprises e.g. reacting 5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphine nickel(II) solution with terephthalic acid, cetrimonium bromide, methylimidazole, cooling and post processing.
AU SHI Y
   WU Z
   NIU D
   CHU Q
   LI X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201942386P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal-organic frameworks-74-nickel cobalt material, comprises e.g. dissolving 5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphine nickel(II), 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-methoxyphenyl)porphyrinato)cobalt(II)) and terephthalic acid in a dimethylformamide ethanol system, then ultrasonically stirring, adding alkyl polyglycoside, cetrimonium bromide and methylimidazole to mixed solution, and then ultrasonically homogenizing, continuously heating, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, pouring out supernatant, then taking out the purple crystals, purifying using dimethylformamide, then removing and soaking in methanol, during which time the methanol is replaced three times, then soaking in anhydrous ethanol, then drying by supercritical carbon dioxide method, exchanging the carbon dioxide, during this process, discharging carbon dioxide, venting, sealing the chamber, heating and then venting, heating and slowly venting the gas chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing metal-organic frameworks-74-nickel cobalt material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has excellent cobalt selective catalytic reduction denitration performance, denitration catalytic efficiency up to 99% at 175-300 degrees C and synthetic production rate up to 96%; and exhibits excellent crystallinity (near 1000 m2/g specific surface area) and uniform pore distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing metal-organic frameworks-74-nickel cobalt material, comprises (i) dissolving 5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphine nickel(II), 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-methoxyphenyl)porphyrinato)cobalt(II)) and terephthalic acid in a dimethylformamide (DMF) ethanol system in a volume ratio of 1.09:10.83-14.37:3.68, then ultrasonically stirring until uniformly mixed, where molar ratio of 5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphine nickel(II) to 5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-methoxyphenyl)porphyrinato)cobalt(II)) is 3.43:12.01-11.26.2.57, and the molar ratios of the two metal salts to terephthalic acid is 5.42:1.09-13.68:1.09, adding alkyl polyglycoside, cetrimonium bromide and methylimidazole to mixed solution, and then ultrasonically homogenizing, where the molar ratio of alkyl polyglycoside to cetrimonium bromide is 0.34:3.43-2.57:0.69, the molar ratio of alkyl polyglycoside to methylimidazole is 0.26:7.64-12.01:0.29, and the molar ratio of alkyl polyglycoside to 5,10,15,20-tetraphenyl-21H,23H-porphine nickel(II) is 0.52:3.43-2.67:8.77, then continuously heating in an oven at 80-140 degrees C for 18-30 hours, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging at a speed of 8000-10000 revolutions/minute for 10-20 minute, pouring out supernatant, taking out purple crystals, 3-30 times purifying using 30-75 ml of DMF, and removing and soaking in methanol for 6-8 days, during which time the methanol is replaced three times, and (ii) soaking the obtained material in anhydrous ethanol for 2-4 days, replacing the ethanol for every 24 hours, then drying at 60-80 degrees C by supercritical carbon dioxide (SCD) method, exchanging the carbon dioxide for every 8-12 hours, during this process, discharging carbon dioxide under positive pressure for every 2 hours for 5-8 minutes, maintaining the exhaust rate of carbon dioxide below the filling rate to maintain the dry state of the entire drying chamber, venting, sealing the chamber, then heating to the exhaust rate of carbon dioxide and maintaining the venting rate below the filling rate to maintain complete drying chamber, venting, heating to 40-60 degrees C and slowly venting the gas chamber after 15 hours.

PT P
PN CN108715438-A; CN108715438-B
TI Mineralization of carbon dioxide and simultaneous decomposition of water involves reacting water, iodine and sulfur dioxide, concentrating hydrogen iodide phase and sulfuric acid phase, and processing hydrogen iodide to produce hydrogen.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG Z
   ZHOU Z
   LIU J
   ZHU J
   HUANG Z
   YANG W
   CHENG J
   NI M
   CEN K
   ZHOU J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201888183P
AB    NOVELTY - Mineralization of carbon dioxide and simultaneous decomposition of water involves (1) feeding water, excess iodine and sulfur dioxide to a Bunsen reaction device, stirring, reacting to obtain aqueous hydrogen iodide phase and sulfuric acid phase solution, (2) concentrating sulfuric acid phase solution to obtain sulfuric acid product, (3) subjecting hydrogen iodide phase to electrodialysis, obtaining concentrated hydrogen iodide solution, (4) reacting concentrated hydrogen iodide solution and magnesium silicate natural minerals to obtain magnesium iodide, water and silica, and a mixed liquid of iodine-entrained concentrated hydrogen iodide solution, (5) filtering mixed liquid to obtain by-product silica, distilling filtrate, obtaining iodine and magnesium iodide-water crystals, (6) hydrolyzing and carbonizing the crystals to obtain hydrogen iodide steam and magnesium carbonate product, (7) thermally decomposing the hydrogen iodide steam to obtain iodine and hydrogen product.
   USE - Mineralization of carbon dioxide and simultaneous decomposition of water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method combines carbon dioxide mineralization technology with thermochemical sulfur iodine open circulation to decompose water to produce hydrogen, under mild reaction conditions, and produces hydrogen and sulfuric acid with high added value, and high theoretical thermal efficiency due to the organic combination of hydrogen production cycle and mineralization technology, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Mineralization of carbon dioxide and simultaneous decomposition of water involves (1) feeding water, excess iodine and sulfur dioxide to a Bunsen reaction device, in a ratio of 14-16:1.5-9:1, in which excess iodine ensures liquid-liquid phase separation, stirring the reaction solution at a uniform rate, self-exothermic reacting at 20-120 degrees C and 1-2 atm to obtain upper and lower layers of aqueous hydrogen iodide phase and sulfuric acid phase solution, (2) subjecting sulfuric acid phase solution to multi-stage concentration treatment at 120-260 degrees C, 0.08-1.3 atm and under adiabatic conditions, to obtain sulfuric acid product, (3) subjecting hydrogen iodide phase to electrodialysis, obtaining concentrated hydrogen iodide solution on cathode side of electrodialysis cell, returning the diluted hydrogen iodide solution obtained on anode side and iodine, to the Bunsen reaction device for reuse, (4) carrying out spontaneous exothermic reaction of concentrated hydrogen iodide solution and magnesium silicate natural minerals, at 20-90 degrees C, to obtain magnesium iodide, water and silica, and a mixed liquid of iodine-entrained concentrated hydrogen iodide solution, (5) filtering mixed liquid to obtain by-product silica, subjecting filtrate to distillation, obtaining iodine and magnesium iodide-water crystals of formula: MgI2-nH2O, returning iodine to the Bunsen reaction device for reuse, (6) placing the crystals in a fixed bed or fluidized bed reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, nitrogen and water vapor to carry out hydrolysis and carbonation reaction, controlling reaction temperature at 180-350 degrees C, obtaining hydrogen iodide steam and magnesium carbonate product, (7) introducing the hydrogen iodide steam to hydrogen iodide catalytic decomposition reactor for thermal decomposition at 300-500 degrees C to obtain iodine and hydrogen product, and returning iodine to the Bunsen reaction device for reuse. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for device for mineralization of carbon dioxide and simultaneous decomposition of water to produce hydrogen and carbon dioxide, which comprises a Bunsen reaction device connected to liquid phase separation device which is respectively connected to hydrogen iodide concentration device and sulfuric acid concentration device, hydrogen iodide concentration device respectively connected to Bunsen reaction device and magnesium iodide production reactor, magnesium iodide production reactor connected to magnesium iodide distillation device, magnesium iodide distillation device respectively connected to Bunsen reaction device and magnesium iodide hydrolysis carbonation reactor, and magnesium iodide hydrolysis carbonation reactor connected to hydrogen iodide catalytic decomposition reactor, and hydrogen iodide catalytic decomposition reactor connected to Bunsen reaction device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnesium silicate natural mineral is serpentine or olivine.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN113104842-A; CN113104842-B
TI Preparing functionalized graphene useful in            preparing lithium ion battery comprises sealing flake            graphite powder, intercalation aid and peeling aid in            reaction mill equipment, passing carbon dioxide gas,            milling, sieving, and drying.
AU QU Y
   CAO Y
   WANG J
AE HEFEI GUOXUAN HIGH TECH POWER SOURCE CO (HEFG-C)
GA 202181830U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing functionalized graphene comprises                adding flake graphite powder, intercalation aid and                peeling aid into a reaction mill equipment and                sealing, passing carbon dioxide gas into the                reaction mill equipment and performing reaction                milling, taking out the sample, sieving, removing                impurities, and drying to obtain functionalized                graphene.
   USE - The functionalized graphene is useful in                preparing lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method saves preparation cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                functionalized graphene prepared by above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flake graphite                  powder has purity of not less than 99.98% and                  particle size of not less than 200 mesh. The                  intercalation aid comprises quaternary ammonium                  salts, organic amine salts, ferric chloride,                  ethylene carbonate, acetic acid, and/or organic                  amides. The peeling aid comprises potassium                  permanganate, ammonium perchlorate, ammonium                  nitrate, mineral oil emulsion intercalation                  agent, and/or polysiloxane. The mass ratio                  between flake graphite powder, intercalation aid                  and peeling aid is 100:1-5:0.5-1. The ratio of                  flake graphite powder and carbon dioxide gas is                  1:30-50 (g:ml). The reaction mill equipment is                  filled with reaction mill media. The reaction                  mill medium occupies 1/4 to 1/2 of the volume of                  the reaction mill equipment. The reaction mill                  medium is composed of a first ball and a second                  ball. The ratio of the number of the first ball                  to the second ball is 1:5-10. The particle size                  of the first rolling ball is 2-5 mm, and the                  particle size of the second rolling ball is 0.5-1                  mm. The functionalized graphene has CO/C-O-C                  functional groups content of not less than 5%,                  carboxylic acid functional groups content of not                  less than 16%, and the specific surface area of                  not less than 760 m2/g. Preferred Method: The                  rotating speed of the reaction mill is 500-1200                  revolutions/minute and the time is 5-9 hours. The                  step of removing impurities specifically                  includes: sieving the sample to obtain a product,                  and washing the product with deionized water                  until neutral. The drying adopts freeze-drying,                  specifically freeze-drying includes freeze-drying                  at -20 to -10 degrees C for 2-5 hours, and                  gradually heating at 15-25 degrees C.

PN KR2021051163-A; KR2260722-B1
TI Carbonate mineralization method comprises mixing water with waste asbestos slate powder, adding hydrochloric acid, performing mineral carbonation reaction and recovering calcium carbonate generated by mineral carbonation reaction.
AU WOOK K D
   IMBYUNGHYUN
AE UNIV KONGJU NAT IND ACAD COOP GRO (UYKN-C)
GA 202149517W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonate mineralization method comprises mixing water with waste asbestos slate powder to obtain waste asbestos slate slurry, adding hydrochloric acid to the waste asbestos slate slurry to obtain a calcium eluate, performing a mineral carbonation reaction by adding carbon dioxide to the calcium eluate and recovering calcium carbonate generated by the mineral carbonation reaction.
   USE - Used as carbonate mineralization method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: establishes optimal carbonate mineralization condition that can efficiently fix carbon dioxide using harmless asbestos slate; and promotes environmental protection and public health.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentration of waste asbestos slate slurry was adjusted to 8-10 g/l, preferably 10 g/l (1%) by mixing water with the dry waste asbestos slate powder that has been detoxified. The pH of waste asbestos slate slurry was adjusted to neutral using hydrochloric acid and reacted for 30-60 minutes to obtain calcium eluate. The eluate was mixed with hydrochloric acid and complex organic acid of phosphoric acid, tartaric acid and propionic acid (in a weight ratio of 1:1:1-2:2:2), and the pH was adjusted to 7 by adding 10-30 wt.% hydrochloric acid. The waste asbestos slate slurry was heated at 100 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, water was added and heated at 100 degrees C for 20-30 minutes.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 201875461W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous membrane involves                synthesizing hierarchically structured porous                support by coating particles has pores smaller than                than porous support on porous support, and                synthesizing membrane in which zeolitic imidazolate                frameworks (ZIFs) are formed inside hierarchically                structured porous support by performing counter                diffusion.
   USE - Method for preparing porous membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare porous membrane                that increases physical stability, and ensures                higher hydrogen/carbon dioxide separation                factors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for separating hydrogen from mixed gas of hydrogen                and carbon dioxide or syngas, which involves using                porous membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The zinc ion or cobalt                  ion are absorbed on porous support by immersing                  the hierarchically structured porous support                  synthesized in zinc salt solution or cobalt salt                  solution to form zinc ion or cobalt ion imidazole                  crystals inside the porous support by immersing                  the porous support adsorbed with zinc ion or                  cobalt ion in imidazole or imidazole derivative                  solution. The pores is smaller than porous                  support are formed on one side of the                  hierarchically structured porous support. The                  zinc salt solution, the cobalt salt solution, or                  imidazole or imidazole derivative solution is                  dissolved in one solvent which is selected from                  methanol, ethanol, propanol, iso-propanol,                  tert-butanol, n-butanol, methoxyethanol,                  ethoxyethanol, dimethylacetateamide,                  dimethylformamide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP),                  formic acid, nitromethane, acetic acid and                  distilled water. The zeolitic imidazolate                  frameworks are formed inside hierarchically                  structured porous support is formed by coating                  ceramic particles on one side of ceramic support.                  Preferred Condition: The pore size of porous                  support is 50-1000 nanometer. The pore size of                  particles having pores smaller than porous                  support is 2-50 nanometer. The thickness of                  porous membrane is 0.1-500 micrometer. The                  separation is performed at 100-500 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: The porous support is                  selected from alpha-alumina, gamma-alumina,                  silica, glass, mullite, zirconia, titania,                  yttria, ceria, vanadia, silicone, stainless                  steel, calcium oxide, phosphor oxide. The zinc                  salt is selected from zinc nitrate, zinc acetate,                  zinc chloride, zinc sulfate, zinc bromide and                  zinc iodide. The cobalt salt is selected from                  cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, cobalt chloride,                  cobalt sulfate, cobalt bromide and cobalt iodide.                  The imidazole or imidazole derivative is selected                  from benzimidazole, 2-methylimidazole,                  4-methylimidazole, 2-methylbenzimidazole,                  2-nitroimidazole, 5-nitrobenzimidazole and                  5-chlorobenzimidazole.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN107987050-A
TI Preparation of cyclic carbonate using magnesium-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in presence of conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyridine/conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material.
AU ZHANG X
   WANG N
   CHEN Z
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 2018366450
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of cyclic carbonate using magnesium-based catalyst involves reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in the presence of conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyridine/conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material as heterogeneous catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at normal temperature and normal pressure for 12-48 hours without solvent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, economical, rapid and safe preparation of cyclic carbonate with excellent catalytic activity and stability under mild reaction condition, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyridine microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of magnesium tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (I) and 2,2-bipyridine as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. The conjugated magnesium porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of magnesium tetraphenylporphyrin of formula (II) and pyrrole as monomer in the presence of anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxide is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The heterogeneous catalyst is used in an amount of 0.2-0.5 mol%. The co-catalyst is used in an amount of 3.6-7.2 mol%.

PN WO2022106313-A1
TI Synthesizing methanol by providing syngas feedstream comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides, providing catalytic composition comprising methanol synthesis catalyst, putting into contact syngas feedstream with catalytic composition under reaction conditions, and recovering methanol.
AU GRASSO G
   CURULLA-FERRE D
   STEWART J
AE TOTALENERGIES ONETECH (PETF-C)
GA 202273370T
AB    NOVELTY - Process for synthesizing methanol, involves (a) providing a syngas feedstream (3) comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides, (b) providing a first catalytic composition (9) that comprises at least one first methanol synthesis catalyst, (c) putting into contact the syngas feedstream with the first catalytic composition under first reaction conditions comprising first temperature conditions, to provide a first gaseous effluent (15) and optionally a first liquid effluent, (d) providing a second catalytic composition (17) that comprises at least one second methanol synthesis catalyst, (e) putting into contact at least a part of the first gaseous effluent with the second catalytic composition under second reaction conditions comprising second temperature conditions, to provide a second gaseous effluent (23) and a second liquid effluent (21), and (f) recovering methanol from one or more chosen from the first gaseous effluent and the second gaseous effluent.
   USE - The process is used for synthesizing methanol from syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process convert a carbon dioxide-rich syngas feed stream into methanol without being subject to deactivation of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for synthesizing methanol, involves (a) providing a syngas feedstream (3) comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides, where the carbon oxides are chosen from carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide provided that the molar ratio of carbon dioxide over carbon monoxide in the mixture is less than or equal to 1, and that the syngas feedstream comprises at least 12 mol.% carbon dioxide based on the total molar content of the syngas feedstream, (b) providing a first catalytic composition (9) that comprises at least one first methanol synthesis catalyst, (c) putting into contact the syngas feedstream with the first catalytic composition under first reaction conditions comprising first temperature conditions, to provide a first gaseous effluent (15) and optionally a first liquid effluent, (d) providing a second catalytic composition (17) that comprises at least one second methanol synthesis catalyst, where the second methanol synthesis catalyst is the same or different from the one first methanol synthesis catalyst, (e) putting into contact at least a part of the first gaseous effluent with the second catalytic composition under second reaction conditions comprising second temperature conditions, to provide a second gaseous effluent (23) and a second liquid effluent (21), (f) recovering methanol from one or more chosen from the first gaseous effluent and the second gaseous effluent, where the first temperature conditions are at least 270 degrees C and where the second temperature conditions are lower than the first temperature conditions and second temperature conditions are 180-250 degrees C. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use of at least one first catalytic composition comprising at least one first methanol synthesis catalyst in a process for methanol synthesis, the composition is performed in an installation comprising at least two reactors in series where a first reactor comprises the at least one first catalytic composition, and at least one subsequent reactor placed downstream of the first reactor comprises at least one second catalytic composition comprising at least one second methanol synthesis catalyst, where at least one second methanol synthesis catalyst is the same or different from one first methanol synthesis catalyst, the use comprises providing a syngas feedstream comprising hydrogen and carbon oxides to the first reactor to contact the syngas feedstream with the at least first catalytic composition, the carbon oxides are chosen from carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide provided that the molar ratio of carbon dioxide over carbon monoxide in the mixture is less than or equal to 1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first methanol synthesis catalyst and/or the second methanol synthesis catalyst is or comprises a copper zinc oxide catalyst, where the copper zinc oxide catalyst is prepared by co-precipitation. The first methanol synthesis catalyst and/or the second methanol synthesis catalyst is or comprises an indium oxide catalyst, where the indium oxide catalyst comprises a promoter comprising at least one metal. The indium oxide catalyst comprises a promoter comprising at least one metal, where the metal is chosen from ruthenium, rhodium, gold, iridium, palladium, silver, osmium, platinum, copper, nickel, cobalt and/or their combinations. The first methanol synthesis catalyst and/or the second methanol synthesis catalyst is or comprises one or more chosen from an indium oxide catalyst, copper-zinc oxide, copper-zinc oxide/aluminum oxide, copper-zinc oxide-gallium oxide/silicon dioxide, copper-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide/zirconium oxide, zinc oxide, silver/zinc oxide, silver/ferrous oxide, silver/titanium dioxide, silver/zirconium oxide, silver/lanthanum oxide, silver/zinc-ferrous oxide, ferrous oxide, silver/ferrous oxide, cerium oxide, titanium oxide, zirconium oxide, lanthanum oxide, zinc/ferrous oxide, preferably copper-zinc oxide-aluminum oxide/zirconium oxide. The subsequent reactor comprising the second catalytic composition forms at least one subsequent catalytic bed, and at least one reverse water-gas shift catalyst is introduced at the end of the one or more subsequent catalytic beds, where the reverse water-gas shift catalysts are chosen from ferrous oxide, zinc-nickel-ferrous oxide, barium-ferrous oxide-aluminum oxide-nickel oxide, copper-manganese spinel oxide, lanthanum(2-x)calcium(x)copper oxide, oxide supported copper, copper oxide/zirconium oxide, cerium oxide/copper oxide, oxide supported silver, copper-cerium oxide-lanthanum oxide. The first methanol synthesis catalyst and the second methanol synthesis catalyst are the same.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves subjecting first gaseous effluent to a separation step before being put into contact with the second catalytic composition in step (e) to remove at least a part of methanol and water, recycling at least a part of the first gaseous effluent to be put into contact with said first catalytic composition again, and at least a part of the second gaseous effluent to be put into contact with the second catalytic composition again. Preferred Conditions: The first temperature conditions comprise a temperature of 270-350 degrees C. The first temperature conditions the first temperature conditions are at least 275 degrees C, preferably at least 300 degrees C. Preferred Components: The syngas feedstream comprises more than 15 mol.%, preferably 20 mol.% or more carbon dioxide. The carbon oxides present in the syngas feedstream is a mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, where the mixture has a molar ratio of carbon dioxide over carbon monoxide less than or equal to 2, preferably less than or equal to 2.5, or less than or equal to 3, preferably less than or equal to 5. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide in the syngas feedstream is at least 2. The first gaseous effluent comprises at least 1 mol.% methanol based on the total molar content of the first gaseous effluent.

PN CN114459019-A
TI Carbon emission reduction technology using carbon            dioxide with high concentration for circulating sealing            coal, comprises e.g. supplying coal for boiler by coal            supply system, and collecting high-concentration carbon            dioxide.
AU CHEN W
   HE T
   WU D
   ZHOU Z
   TU D
   NING X
AE FUJIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION DESIGN (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202268230C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon emission reduction technology using                carbon dioxide with high concentration involves                providing a boiler cover with a sealing cover, the                sealing cover is filled with carbon dioxide with                high concentration; using the air separation device                to purify the O2 in the air and sending into the                mixed air distribution system to mix with the high-                concentration CO2 mixed gas after mixing the mixed                gas sending to the boiler by the mixed air                distribution system; supplying coal for the boiler                by the coal supply system under the action of the                sealing cover separating the gas outside the O2 and                CO2 into the boiler so that the mixed gas auxiliary                coal powder is fully combusted improving the CO2                concentration of the boiler tail; collecting the                high-concentration CO2 generated by the boiler                combustion by the CO2 collecting device.
   USE - Carbon emission reduction technology using                high-concentration carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The outer cover of the boiler is provided with                a sealing cover for isolating the air from entering                the boiler, and the sealing cover is filled with                carbon monoxide with high concentration, so that                the gas entering into the boiler from the boiler                gap is CO2, which further improves the CO2                concentration generated by the combustion of                boiler. The sealing cover also has heat                preservation effect to the boiler improving the CO                2 concentration and improving the boiler heat                efficiency. The internal circulation system ensures                that CO2 generated by combustion is finally                collected in the carbon dioxide collecting device.                The CO2 collecting device provides CO2 with high                pressure viscosity liquid, which is not compressed                into the supercritical state of high-concentration                CO2 gas through the internal circulation air pump                for unidirectional conveying CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon emission reduction technology using                carbon dioxide with high concentration involves                providing a boiler cover with a sealing cover, the                sealing cover is filled with carbon dioxide with                high concentration; using the air separation device                to purify the O2 in the air and sending into the                mixed air distribution system to mix with the high-                concentration CO2 mixed gas after mixing the mixed                gas sending to the boiler by the mixed air                distribution system; supplying coal for the boiler                by the coal supply system under the action of the                sealing cover separating the gas outside the O2 and                CO2 into the boiler so that the mixed gas auxiliary                coal powder is fully combusted improving the CO2                concentration of the boiler tail; collecting the                high-concentration CO2 generated by the boiler                combustion by the CO2 collecting device and                directly compressing the collected                high-concentration CO2 with concentration not less                than 95 % to the supercritical state 18 %-42 % of                high-concentration CO2 is compressed into high                pressure viscosity liquid 58 %-82 % of high-                concentration CO2 is not compressed supercritical                state of high-concentration CO2 gas through the                internal circulation system for recycling; not                compressing into the supercritical state of 58 %-82                % of high concentration CO2 gas 57.2 %-81. 5 % of                CO2, the loop of the internal circulation system is                sent into the mixed air distribution system and the                air separation device purified O2 mixed again into                the boiler for combustion-supporting the coal                powder; not compressing into the supercritical                state of 58 %-82 % of CO2 gas with high                concentration of 0.5 %-0.8 % through the B loop of                the inner circulation system is unidirectionally                sent into the sealing cover; connecting the CO2 in                the sealing cover with the mixed air distribution                system for the CO2 in the sealing cover by means of                the mixed air distribution system one-way discharge                to the CO2 collecting device of the internal                circulation system C loop; pumping the                supercritical CO2 obtained after compression into                the underground terrane for sealing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The A loop of the                  inner circulating system is provided with an                  inner circulating air pump A for unidirectional                  conveying CO2. The B loop of the internal                  circulation system is provided with an internal                  circulation air pump B for conveying CO2 in                  single direction. The C loop of the inner                  circulating system is provided with an inner                  circulating air pump C for unidirectional                  conveying CO2. The sealing cover is kept at                  0.05-0.1 atmospheric pressure.

PN CN112708524-A
TI Preparing Changbai Mountain red fruit sparkling            wine useful in fruit wine preparation field, comprises            e.g. preparing Changbai Mountain red fruit fermentation            broth, filtering mixed liquid, adding beer, stirring,            and filling.
AU GUO Q
   SHAO C
   WANG Y
   GUO C
AE JILIN CHUCAI AGRIC PROD DEV CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 202148541G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Changbai Mountain red fruit                sparkling wine comprises e.g. washing the mature                Changbai Mountain red fruits, preparing Changbai                Mountain red fruit fermentation broth, adding                purified water into sugar pot, heating, adding                sweetener to dissolve, adding acidity regulator and                stirring evenly, continuously heating, adding pure                water, cooling, adding the red fruit fermentation                broth and mixing well to obtain a mixed solution,                filtering the mixed liquid, adding into the mixing                tank, adding beer, stirring, adding pure water to                adjusting the refractive index of the mixed liquid                to 4-4.5%, qualifying the refractive index,                cooling, filtering to obtain the liquor, spraying                the wine into the carbon dioxide mixer, mixing with                the carbon dioxide input from the carbon dioxide                storage tank, and filling.
   USE - The wine is useful in the field of fruit wine                preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The wine is dark red color, clear and                transparent liquid, tastes sweet and sour,                refreshing and soft, and has strong and harmonious                red fruit aroma and mellow aroma.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Changbai Mountain red fruit                sparkling wine comprises (a) washing the mature                Changbai Mountain red fruits with water to obtain                clean Changbai Mountain red fruits, (b) taking                120-240 pts. wt. clean Changbai Mountain red fruit,                100-200 pts. wt. edible alcohol, 20-40 pts. wt.                purified water, 25-50 pts. wt. erythritol and                0.03-0.06 pts. wt. complex enzymes and complex                bacterial mixture, (c) adding the edible alcohol                into the fermenter with a jacket, adding the                complex enzyme and complex bacterial mixture and                50% erythritol under stirring conditions, closing                the fermenter lid, steaming the jacket to make the                temperature in the fermenter reach 50-55 degrees C,                maintaining for 3-4 days, during this period,                stirring for 2 minutes for every 5-6 minutes,                adding the remaining erythritol into the fermenter,                stirring evenly, steaming the jacket to make the                temperature in the fermenter reach 50-55 degrees C                and maintaining for 4-5 days, and filtering to                obtain the filtrate, which is Changbai Mountain red                fruit fermentation broth, (d) taking 150-300 pts.                wt. purified water, 0.04-0.06 pts. wt. sweetener,                0.05-0.30 pts. wt. acidity regulator, and 40-80                pts. wt. red fruit fermentation broth, (e) adding                40-60% purified water into the sugar pot, heating                to 45-50 degrees C, adding sweetener to dissolve,                adding acidity regulator and stirring evenly,                continuously heating to 85-90 degrees C and                maintaining for 20-25 minutes, adding the remaining                pure water, cooling to 40-45 degrees C, adding the                red fruit fermentation broth and mixing well to                obtain a mixed solution, (f) filtering the mixed                liquid for the first time, adding into the mixing                tank, adding beer, stirring for 15-20 minutes,                adding pure water to adjusting the refractive index                of the mixed liquid to 4-4.5%, qualifying the                refractive index, cooling to 4 to -3 degrees C,                filtering again while cooling to obtain the liquor,                where the beer content in the liquor is 0.1-0.3%,                and (g) spraying the wine into the carbon dioxide                mixer from bottom to top in an atomized state                through the nozzle on the wine inlet pipe of the                carbon dioxide mixer, mixing with the carbon                dioxide input from the carbon dioxide storage tank,                controlling the input pressure of the liquor at                0.8-1 MPa, where the carbon dioxide pressure does                not exceed 0.5 MPa, mixing and filling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The complex enzyme is                  formed by mixing cellulase and protease in a mass                  ratio of 1:2. The complex bacteria is a mixture                  of microzyme, Lactobacillus bulgaricus and lactic                  acid bacteria in a mass ratio of 2:1:1. In the                  step (d), the sweetening agent is white                  granulated sugar.

PN CN112251235-A; CN112251235-B
TI Preparing steel slag-based curing agent used for heavy metal cadmium contaminated soil, involves pulverizing desulfurized gypsum and steel slag using ball mill, and uniformly mixing pulverized gypsum and steel slag.
AU WANG Q
   LI M
   YANG J
AE UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY ELECTRONIC (UYLG-C)
   UNIV ANHUI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYLG-C)
GA 202111103C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing steel slag-based curing agent involves: (S1) pulverizing desulfurized gypsum with water content of less than 2% and a specific surface area of greater than 800 m2/kg and steel slag with a specific surface area of 400-550 m2/kg in a mass ratio of (4-5):50 by a ball mill; and (S2) uniformly mixing the pulverized gypsum and steel slag.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing steel slag-based curing agent for heavy metal cadmium contaminated soil (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables simple preparation of steel slag-based curing agent using wide sources of easy-to-obtain raw materials; adopts all industrial waste slag materials; and is environmentally-friendly, cost-effective and easy to realize.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a system for treating cadmium contaminated soil, which comprises a processing unit located on a crawler excavator and a film coating unit located on bulldozer. The processing unit includes an excavator main control chamber, an excavator main operating platform, and excavator arms (A,B). The crawler excavator is provided with a cargo transporting carriage, and is provided with a recorder for hydraulic conveying and intelligent control. A carbon dioxide storage tank, a curing agent storage tank and a water storage tank are provided in freight car. The arm (A) is provided with a retractable soil rod. One end of the rod is connected with the arm (A), and the other end is provided with a soil breaking cone, which is provided with a curing agent nozzle. The soil rod is provided with rotating rods (A,B). Both ends of the rotating rod (A) are provided with sprinkler heads. Both ends of the rotating rod (B) are provided with stirrer connecting rods distributed in an array. Both ends of the stirrer connecting rod are equipped with soil breaking stirrer. The soil rod s provided with a rotation controller, and is provided with a curing agent channel rod. The arm (B) is provided with a supporting rod provided with a carbon dioxide channel rod, which is provided with carbon dioxide nozzles distributed in an array. One end of the supporting rod is connected with the arm (B), and the other end is equipped with a sensor probe for detecting moisture content and pH value. The carbon dioxide storage tank, the curing agent storage tank and the water storage tank are provided with a carbon dioxide delivery pipe, a curing agent delivery pipe and a water delivery pipe. The carbon dioxide delivery pipe, the curing agent delivery pipe and the water delivery pipe is provided to pass through the recorder. The coating unit includes a compactor control chamber, a compactor and a film paving device; and
   (2) treating cadmium contaminated soil, which involves (S10) loading the curing agent into the curing agent storage tank, (S20) controlling the ground breaking mixer to break and crush the cadmium contaminated soil by using excavator wall, (S30) spraying steel slag and desulfurization gypsum mixed solidifying agent powder and water into the soil and evenly mixing by using the soil breaking stirrer, and adjusting the curing agent and water content in real time through the sensor probe to ensure the moisture content and the maintenance humidity of the soil, (S40) rolling the treated heavy metal cadmium contaminated soil through the compactor, and spreading the film through the film paving device, (S50) injecting carbon dioxide gas through the carbon dioxide nozzle to carbonize and solidify the compacted contaminated soil, and (S60) repeating the above steps (S10)-(S50) until all the construction points are repaired.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: One end of the carbon dioxide delivery pipe is connected with the carbon dioxide storage tank, and the other end is connected with the carbon dioxide channel rod. One end of the curing agent delivery pipe is in communication with the curing agent storage tank, and the other end is in communication with the curing agent channel rod. One end of the water delivery pipe is connected with the water storage tank, and the other end is connected with the sprinkler head.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The specific surface area of the desulfurized gypsum is greater than 800 m2/kg. The specific surface area of the steel slag is 400-550 m2/kg.

The shell layer outlet feed gas heat exchanger is connected with the wet purified gas cooler is connected with the inlet. The bottom outlet of the regeneration tower is connected with the barren and pregnant solution heat exchanger, low pressure barren solution pump. The top outlet of the regeneration tower top cooler and a regeneration tower, a regeneration tower reflux tank and the regeneration tower reflux pump is connected and then connected to the regeneration tower top inlet.
   USE - Used as removing carbon dioxide from ethane gas device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device deeply removes the carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen sulfide and mercaptans, carbonyl sulfide and organic sulfur impurities, greatly reduces the sodium hydroxide consumption of the downstream device and improves the purity of the ethane product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing carbon dioxide from ethane gas, comprising (i) absorbing solution flow ethane gas with the raw material gas heat exchanger to 30-45 degrees C and entering the absorption tower, adding countercurrent contact with the solution into the absorption tower, discharging absorb carbon dioxide, sulfureted hydrogen, thiol, carbonyl sulfur impurity of ethane gas from the absorption tower, carrying out the raw material gas heat exchanger and wet purifying gas cooler heat exchange and cooling to 40 degrees C and entering the wet gas separator into the dehydrating device, (ii) absorbing the carbon dioxide, sulfureted hydrogen and solution rich thiol, removing carbonyl sulfur impurities, adding drawn from the bottom of the absorption tower into the flash tank, controlling the operation pressure of the flash tank is between 0.4-0.6MPa, analyzing large amount of ethane solution absorption and a small amount of impurity in a flash tank, adding the regenerated barren liquor flash and counter-current contact into a flash tower, adding solution flashed in solution after flash through the liquid filter and barren and pregnant solution heat exchanger into the regeneration tower, heating to 80-105 degrees C, controlling the regeneration tower operation pressure is between 0.1-0.3MPa, where the reboiler and from the regeneration tower to generate 115-130 degrees C of steam counter-current contact, removing a lot of impurities analysis solution absorption and a small amount of ethane gas, cooling the solution regeneration of the barren and pregnant solution heat exchanger to -65 to 95 degrees oC and entering the low pressure barren solution pump pressurizing and conveyed to barren solution cooler, cooling to 45-55 degrees C with barren liquor circulating pump pressurizing conveying to an absorption tower and finishing solution regeneration process, (iii) removing a lot of impurities from regeneration tower and a small amount of ethane gas, cooling to 40 degrees C after the regeneration tower reflux tank separates the free solution regeneration tower top cooler, adding solution regeneration tower reflux pump returns into the regeneration tower for recycling, discharging regeneration gas after the tower 20 adsorbing the sulfide impurity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the removing carbon dioxide from ethane gas device (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU LEE Y B
   LEE J
AE KOREA EAST WEST POWER CO LTD (KEPC-C)
   C & G HI-TECH CO LTD (CGHI-Non-standard)
GA 2021B88424
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a reactor (02) that includes a packed tower (03) and a bubble reactor (05) for generating sodium bicarbonate by reacting an aqueous sodium carbonate solution with carbon dioxide gas. A mixing type mixer (08) generates fine bubbles from carbon dioxide gas and supplies a fluid produced by mixing with an aqueous sodium carbonate solution to a gas separator (12). The gas separator separates liquid and gas from the fluid supplied from the mixing type mixer and supplies to the bubble reactor. A dehydrator (10) separates sodium bicarbonate in a slurry state generated in the reactor by centrifugation. A dryer (11) dries the sodium bicarbonate separated in the dehydrator. The mixing type mixer is a rotating collision type.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing integrated sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus and the method combining the packed tower and the bubble reactor maximize the carbon dioxide gas utilization rate by making the packed tower and the bubble reactor integrated. The sodium carbonate aqueous solution is reacted with carbon dioxide gas to produce sodium bicarbonate, reaction time is shortened and facility cost is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacturing sodium bicarbonate.
