PN CN111841568-A; CN111841568-B
TI Preparing cuprous oxide supported palladium composite photocatalytic material, comprises e.g. adding sodium hydroxide solution into copper chloride solution, adding ascorbic acid solution and potassium tetrachloropalladate solution.
AU NIU L
   HAN D
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 2020B2716T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cuprous oxide supported palladium composite photocatalytic material, comprises adding sodium hydroxide solution into copper chloride solution, stirring for 30 minutes, adding ascorbic acid solution and continue stirring for 3 hours, adding potassium tetrachloropalladate solution and continue stirring and reacting for 10-60 minutes, where the whole reaction process is carried out under 55 degrees C water bath conditions, collecting the final product by suction filtration, and vacuum drying to obtain final product, where the concentration of the potassium tetrachloropalladate solution is 15.52 mmol/l, and the amount of the potassium tetrachloropalladate solution is 0.36-5.4 pts. wt..
   USE - The photocatalytic material is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic material has photocatalytic reduction efficiency of carbon dioxide which is more than 3 times that of single cuprous oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) cuprous oxide supported palladium composite photocatalytic material prepared by above method; and
   (2) use of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprising dispersing the cuprous oxide supported palladium composite photocatalytic material in a container containing sodium sulfite solution, capping, loading into the photocatalytic reduction reaction system and sealing, sealing, removing oxygen by vacuum pump, and then passing carbon dioxide through the sealing rubber plug of the reaction system, and placing it under a xenon lamp to illuminate after the reaction system is stable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method utilizes 0.01 mol/l copper chloride solution, 2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, and 0.6 mol/l ascorbic acid solution. The method utilizes 100 pts. wt. copper chloride solution, 10 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide solution, and 10 pts. wt. ascorbic acid solution. The amount of the potassium tetrachloropalladate solution is 3.6 pts. wt.. Preferred Method: The temperature of the vacuum drying is 50-60 degrees C. The oxygen is removed by a vacuum pump until the vacuum degree of the reaction system is -0.1 MPa, and then carbon dioxide is introduced to the reaction system vacuum degree of -0.03 to -0.05 MPa. The carbon dioxide is introduced until the vacuum degree of the reaction system is -0.04 MPa, and placed under a 100-500 W xenon lamp to illuminate after 30 minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Method of producing beer or a beer-like beverage, involves (a) producing or supplying a beer intermediate, (b) storing the beer intermediate in a container, (c) transporting the container containing the beer intermediate to a processing station where the beer intermediate is discharged from the container for further processing, and (d) mixing carbon dioxide with the discharged beer intermediate, where, before step (b), at least one of alcohol, malt syrup, wheat syrup, hop and sugar is added to the beer intermediate such that the stored beer intermediate forms a beer concentrate, and water is mixed with the discharged beer concentrate to obtain ready-to-consume beer or a ready-to-consume beer-like beverage.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a beer concentrate which is manufactured by producing or supplying a beer intermediate having a carbon dioxide concentration of lower than 3 g/kg, preferably lower than 1 g/kg, and adding at least one of alcohol, malt syrup, wheat syrup, hop and sugar to the beer intermediate to form the beer concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The processing station comprises a tapping cock, where the carbon dioxide and water are mixed with the discharged beer concentrate. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide and water are mixed with the beer concentrate at the same time by mixing carbonated water with the beer concentrate. Preferred Components: The produced or supplied beer intermediate has a carbon dioxide concentration of lower than 3 g/kg, preferably lower than 1 g/kg. The beer intermediate contains no alcohol or 5 vol.% or low alcohol before mixing one of alcohol, malt syrup, wheat syrup, hop and sugar. The sugar comprises 25-100% glucose. The water is mixed with the discharged beer concentrate at a ratio of (1:3)-(1:25). The beer intermediate contains alcohol. The alcohol is mixed with the discharged beer concentrate.

AB    NOVELTY - The turbo machine (105) has, in fluid flow series, an inlet (201), an inducerless radial impeller (20) having blades (211,212) and a fully vaneless diffuser (203). The inlet is radially flared to induce a radial component in flow prior to an entry to the impeller. The hub hade angle of the impeller at the entry thereto ranges from 50-70 degrees. The each of the blades is a backswept blade and blades have a blade exit angle ranging from -50 to -70 degrees.
   USE - Turbomachine for compressing supercritical carbon dioxide in closed, indirect-heated Brayton cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The impeller is inducerless and the impeller can produce only a centrifugal pressure rise in the carbon dioxide working fluid, which reduces any time period in which the flow is subcritical, which may occur as the fluid accelerates through the impeller, and further, the radial impeller will continue to operate stably with little or no pressure drop should it enter stall. The hub hade angle yihub is 60 degrees to strike a balance between amount of flow turning and reducing risk of separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for operating turbo machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the compressor

PN JP2020023884-A; WO2020031480-A1; US2021164485-A1; JP7012616-B2; US11319967-B2
TI Centrifugation multistage compressor has intermediate diaphragm which is provided with bypass flow path that supplies portion of carbon dioxide to intermediate stage labyrinth that seals between discharge flow path and suction flow path.
AU NISHIDA K
   NARUSE T
   HIRADATE S
   KOHEI N
   TOMOHIRO N
   KIYOTAKA H
   HIRADATE K
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI IND PROD LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI IND PROD LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI IND PROD LTD (HITA-C)
GA 202012842X
AB    NOVELTY - The compressor has a rotating shaft on which multiple impellers are installed. A discharge flow path is arranged between the first-stage impeller and the last-stage impeller (43), and once discharges carbon dioxide to exterior of machine. A suction flow path is arranged between the first stage impeller and the last stage impeller, and injects the discharged carbon dioxide into the machine. An intermediate diaphragm (6) is installed between the discharge flow path and the suction flow path. An intermediate stage labyrinth (7) is made of aluminum and seals between the discharge flow path and the suction flow path. The intermediate diaphragm is provided with a bypass flow path (8) that supplies a portion of the carbon dioxide flowing in the discharge flow path to the intermediate stage labyrinth.
   USE - Centrifugation multistage compressor.
   ADVANTAGE - The centrifugal multistage compressor suppresses the corrosion such as carbonic acid corrosion of the intermediate stage labyrinth. The intermediate stage labyrinth is installed for reducing the leak flow rate which flows into the clearance gap produced between the impeller and the intermediate diaphragm. The damage to the impeller is suppressed, and the excessive vibration fault of the compressor is prevented. The decrease in performance of the compressor is suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the centrifugal multistage compressor.
   Intermediate diaphragm (6)
   Intermediate stage labyrinth (7)
   Bypass flow path (8)
   Through hole (9)
   Impeller (43)

AE PENN STATE RES FOUND (PSRF-C)
GA 201937291U
AB    NOVELTY - The electrochemical device has an                electrochemical cell comprising a cathode (14), an                anode (18), and a membrane. A portion of the                cathode is separated from a portion of the anode by                the membrane. The cathode comprises a gas-diffusion                electrode. The anode comprises one of a                liquid-electrolyte style electrode and a                gas-diffusion electrode. The membrane is a bipolar                membrane which is configured to maintain a flux of                protons to the cathode and maintain a flux of                hydroxide ions to the anode. The electrochemical                cell is configured to receive carbon dioxide gas                and water and output reduction products of carbon                dioxide at the cathode and oxygen or other oxidized                products of a depolarizer at the anode.

   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical device operates with a                stable electrolyte pH, even under long-term                operation. The configuration minimizes undesired                crossover of reduced chemical products. The                introduction of contaminants into the electrolyte                is minimized or eliminated. The electrochemical                device facilitates stable operation of the                electrochemical cell at high current density and                high faradic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of reducing product crossover in an electrochemical                cell of an electrochemical device.

PT P
PN CN108607603-A; CN108607603-B
TI Schiff base-modified nanosilver-based catalyst used in reaction of carbon dioxide and alkyne comprises carrier i.e. silicon dioxide with Schiff base-functionalized surface, and active component i.e. zero-valent and/or monovalent silver.
AU HUANG Y
   WU Z
   YANG X
   LIU Q
   FAN S
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201880472A
AB    NOVELTY - Schiff base-modified nanosilver-based catalyst comprises carrier and active component. The carrier is silicon dioxide having surface functionalized with Schiff base. The active component is zero-valent and/or positive monovalent silver at 0.1-10.0 mass% of the catalyst.
   USE - Schiff base-modified nanosilver-based catalyst used in the reaction of carbon dioxide and alkyne to produce carboxylic acid or acetylenic acid product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can efficiently catalyze the reaction of carbon dioxide with an alkyne under mild conditions, and has easy preparation, high activity, high stability, phenylpropiolic acid yield of up to 99%, and phenylpropiolic acid initial turnover number of as high as 720.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of Schiff base-modified nanosilver-based catalyst comprising at 20-80 degrees C, adding 0.1-10 vol.% aldehyde solution to amino-containing silane solution under stirring condition, continuously stirring for 2-24 hours, filtering the generated white precipitates, washing, drying at 60-120 degrees C for 2-12 hours, obtaining Schiff base-modified silicon dioxide carrier, uniformly dispersing the Schiff base-modified silicon dioxide carrier in organic solvent, adding 0.012-0.240 mmol/ml silver precursor solution, ultrasonically dispersing, filtering, washing and drying; and
   (2) application of Schiff base-modified nanosilver-based catalyst in the carboxylation of carbon dioxide and alkyne to produce carboxylic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The silver precursor is soluble salt solution, preferably silver nitrate, silver lactate, and/or silver hexafluorophosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The amino-containing silane is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, and/or N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The aldehyde is formaldehyde solution, acetaldehyde and/or propionaldehyde. The mass ratio of aldehyde to amino-containing silane is 1:1-1:5. The alkyne is phenylacetylene, fluorophenylacetylene, 1-acetylene naphthalene, 1-hexyne, 3,3-dimethyl-1-butyne, 4-methoxyphenylacetylene, 3-ethynylthiophene, and/or 1-chloro-4-ethynylbenzene. The mass ratio of catalyst and alkyne is 1:2-1:20. The pressure of carbon dioxide in the reaction atmosphere is 1 atm. The stirring and reaction step is conducted at 40-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain acetylenic acid product. Anhydrous polar organic solvent and alkali are added during the reaction. The alkali is potassium carbonate, sodium carbonate, cesium carbonate, cesium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, triethylamine, and/or potassium tert-butoxide. The mol ratio of alkali and alkyne is 1.2:1-2.0:1. The polar organic solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide (anhydrous), acetonitrile (anhydrous), dimethyl sulfoxide (anhydrous), cyclic propylene carbonate (anhydrous), and/or 1,4-dioxane (anhydrous).
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); E17 (Other aliphatics.)

PN CN108253650-A; CN108253650-B; WO2019141019-A1; US2021164700-A1; US11255579-B2
TI Control method for transcritical carbon dioxide composite heat pump system, involves cooling carbon dioxide (CO2) main road supercooling section and making self-evaporation heat absorption to reach state point.
AU CAO F
   WANG J
   YIN X
   LI M
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201855277C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting an outlet of CO2 main circuit compressor (1) to inlet of a CO2 main circuit (9) of the air-cooled-air-cooled recombiner (2). The outlet of a supercooled-evaporation recombiner is imported from the CO2 main road supercooling section. The CO2 working fluid is compressed from the state point a through the CO2 main road compressor to reach the state point b, and is made to enter CO2 main road of the -cooled-air-cooled recombiner, and the circulating water in the waterway is heated. The heat is exchanged with the CO2 main road supercooling section. The CO2 main road supercooling section is cooled and the self-evaporation heat absorption is made to reach the state point and finally returns to the inlet of the CO2 auxiliary compressor.
   USE - Control method for transcritical carbon dioxide composite heat pump system.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat pump water heater system can be operated stably and reliably under wide load and temperature condition for a long time. The power consumption is reduced and the starting current of the compressor is reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the transcritical carbon dioxide composite heat pump system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   CO2 main circuit compressor (1)
   Supercooled-evaporation recombiner (2)
   Cooling-evaporation compound (3)
   CO2 main circuit expansion valve (4)
   Evaporator (5)
   CO2 main circuit (9)

A first light generating detection unit (31) generates the excitation light irradiated to first optical chemical sensor, and detects fluorescence from first optical chemical sensor. A second light generating detection unit generates the excitation light irradiated to second optical chemical sensor, and detects fluorescence from second optical chemical sensor. A first measurement unit (32) measures carbon dioxide level based on a signal from the first light generation detection unit, and a second measurement unit measures pH based on a signal from the second light generation detection unit. A control apparatus (50) records the data measured in first and second measurement unit. The first and second optical chemical sensors are provided in the outer surface of glass sphere.

PT P
PN WO2018096996-A1; JP2018552522-X; JP7058011-B2
TI Complex used for oral ingestion for preparing beverages, ice confections or ice creams, comprises carbon dioxide hydrate for oral ingestion having specific maximum carbon dioxide generation rate.
AU TSUJI A
   ANO N
   KABESU M
   EGUCHI T
   YAMAMOTO Y
   MUROMACHI SANEHIRO
   MIZUTANI T
   YAMASAKI Y
   INOUE Y
   SHIMOJYO N
   ENOMOTO Y
AE KIRIN KK (KIRI-C)
   UNIV TSUKUBA (UTSK-C)
   KIRIN KK (KIRI-C)
   UNIV TSUKUBA (UTSK-C)
   NIPPON EKITAN CORP (NIEK-Non-standard)
GA 2018428277
AB    NOVELTY - Complex comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) hydrate for oral ingestion, where maximum CO2 generation rate is adjusted to 8 ml/second or less. The maximum CO2 generation rate is calculated by (i) collecting an amount of CO2 hydrate comprising 0.3-0.36 g CO2, and (ii) heating the separated CO2 hydrate in an atmosphere of atmospheric pressure at room temperature of 37 degrees C and humidity of 70%, (iii) adding CO2 hydrate to 10 ml water having a liquid temperature of 37 degrees C, (iv) measuring weight change for at least 30 seconds or more every 5 seconds from the addition to calculate the amount of CO2 generated every 5 seconds, (v) repeating the step of calculating the CO2 generation rate every 5 seconds from the amount of its generation three times, and (vi) setting maximum value of the CO2 generation rate obtained every 5 seconds as the maximum CO2 generation rate of the CO2 hydrate.
   USE - The complex is useful for oral ingestion, which is used for preparing beverages containing CO2 hydrate, ice confections or ice creams (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The complex reduces in risk of carbon dioxide intoxication when added to a food or beverage, and provides unique texture and comfortable feel to drink.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) method for producing the above-mentioned complex; and
   (2) method for regulating CO2 sustained release of the complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: A coating film of ice on the CO2 hydrate mass is formed so that the maximum CO2 generation rate is 8 ml/second or less. The coating film of ice is an ice film having a thickness of 0.6-50 mm. The CO2 hydrate comprises 0.3-0.36 g CO2. The CO2 content is 3-28 wt.%. The coating film of ice comprises 0.5 wt.% or less thickener, and/or 20 wt.% or less sweetening component.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

AU PANG L

AE JIANGYIN JIALI MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B01193
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of liquid metal in-situ composite                graphene thermal interface material involves (s1)                preparing a gallium-based liquid metal alloy, (s2)                adding metal powder to the liquid metal alloy to                prepare liquid metal heat conducting paste, and                (s3) introducing carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide                gas mixed with inert gas in liquid metal                heat-conducting paste to react. The liquid metal                alloy comprises 50-95 wt.% gallium, 0-50 wt.%                indium and 0-30 wt.% tin.
   USE - Preparation of liquid metal in-situ composite                graphene thermal interface material (claimed) used                as effective thermal bonding agent and diffusion                barrier for metal-gallium oxide interface in liquid                metal matrix.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem of low thermal                conductivity and bad stability of the composite                material, realizing the purpose of improving the                thermal conductive and stability of composite                material. Because the graphene has high heat                conductivity coefficient, so can improve the                thermal conductor and stability after compounding                with the liquid metal, compared with the                traditional mechanical stirring mode, the method of                carbon dioxide decomposition can make the material                dispersed more uniformly. The method can solve the                problem that the powder with fine particles added                in molten metal (LM) generates agglomeration                problem, each material interface constructs the                electron heat transmission network of                particle-film-liquid metal, and reducing the                traditional phonon-electron interface                consumption.

PN WO2022129974-A1
TI Selective separation of hydrogen sulfide relative            to carbon dioxide from gas mixture involves contacting            gas mixture with absorbent solution comprising            absorbent compound in solvent, and separating the            solution into liquid phases.
AU DE MEYER F
   CADOURS R
   POULAIN B
   WEISS C
   CLOAREC E
AE TOTALENERGIES ONETECH (PETF-C)
GA 202283836S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting in contact the gas                mixture with an absorbent solution comprising an                absorbent compound in a solvent. The absorbent                compound is present in the absorbent solution at a                content of 50 percent by mass relative to the mass                of the absorbent solution to obtain a gas mixture                depleted in hydrogen sulfide, and an absorbent                solution loaded with hydrogen sulfide. The                absorbent solution loaded with hydrogen sulfide is                separated into a first, absorbent compound-rich                liquid phase, and a second, solvent-rich liquid                phase. The second liquid phase is regenerated to                collect a hydrogen sulfide stream and a regenerated                liquid phase.
   USE - Method for selective separation of hydrogen                sulfide relative to carbon dioxide from gas mixture                comprising hydrogen sulfide and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables selective separation of                hydrogen sulfide relative to carbon dioxide from a                gas mixture comprising at least one hydrocarbon,                preferably natural gas. The method removes                contaminants and impurities from the gas mixture.                The absorbent solution comprising a demixing amine                selectively absorbs hydrogen sulfides and/or                mercaptans. The process is carried out in a simple                and cost-effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram                illustrating an installation used for the                implementation of the method.
   Absorption column (1)
   Flash tank (5)
   Heat exchanger (7)
   Second liquid phase collecting line                (11)
   Lean solution collecting Line (14)

TI Catalyst useful for preparing propylene and synthesis gas by hydrogen dehydrogenation of propane by carbon dioxide oxidation, comprises silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, platinum, tin, metal oxides of cerium oxide, manganese dioxide, zirconium dioxide, zinc oxide and titanium dioxide.
AU GE H
   SONG Y
   HU R
   WANG L
   YANG G
   REN X
   LIU Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2021C8367X
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises carrier, active component, and auxiliary agent. The carrier is silicon dioxide (SiO2) or aluminum oxide (Al2O3). The active component is platinum (Pt) and tin (Sn). The auxiliary agent is metal oxides of cerium oxide (CeO2), manganese dioxide (MnO2), zirconium dioxide (ZrO2), zinc oxide (ZnO) and titanium dioxide (TiO2). The mass of the catalyst is 100%. The loading amount of Pt is 0.1-1% and Sn is 0.5-5%. The loading amount of the metal element in the auxiliary agent is 0.1-5. The catalyst is prepared by: dissolving the precursor of the auxiliary agent is in absolute ethyl alcohol using impregnation method, mixing the obtained solution with the carrier powder, allowing to stand for 6-12 hours at room temperature, drying for 4-10 hours at 60-110degreesC, 500-650degreesC for 3-6 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, tableting, granulating, sieving by 40-60 meshes sieve.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing propylene and synthesis gas by hydrogen dehydrogenation of propane by carbon dioxide oxidation.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has higher conversion rate of propane and carbon dioxide, propylene yield and stability for the hydrogenation of propane by carbon dioxide to produce propylene and synthesis gas, and produces hydrogen and carbon monoxide richly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The auxiliary agent is CeO2, CeO2-MnO2, CeO2-ZnO or ZrO2-ZnO. The mole ratio of the metal oxides in the auxiliary agent is less than or equal to 10%. The precursor of the active ingredient Pt is chloroplatinic acid hexahydrate (H2PtCl66H2O), and the precursor of the active ingredient Sn is stannous chloride dihydrate (SnCl22H2O). tThe precursors of additives CeO2, MnO2, ZrO2, and ZnO are metal nitrates. The precursor of the additive TiO2 is titanium tetraisopropoxide. The reaction gas is a mixed gas of carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen and propane in a volume ratio of 1-2:1-1.5:1.Preferred Method: The method for preparing propylene and synthesis gas by using the catalyst to catalyze the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to oxidize propane by hydrogen dehydrogenation, where filling 40- 60-mesh catalyst into the quartz tube of the micro fixed bed reactor, introducing mixed gas of hydrogen and argon gas into the reactor, raising the temperature from room temperature to 250-550degrees Celsius, after reducing for 0.5-3 hours, switching to argon gas, continuously raising the temperature to 500-650degrees Celsius, switching into reaction gas, the reaction gas is carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen, the volume ratio of the propane is 0.5-5.0:0.5-3:1 of the mixed gas, the pressure is 0.05-0.15 MPa to react. The 40-60 mesh catalyst is filled into the quartz tube of the micro fixed-bed reactor, the mixed gas of hydrogen and argon is passed into the reactor, and the temperature is raised from room temperature to 400-500degreesC, after reduction for 1 to 2 hours, switch to argon gas, continue to increase the temperature to 580-630degreesC, switch to reaction gas, and performing the reaction at a pressure of 0.1-0.15 MPa.

AU ZHOU R
   LIAO C
   WU C

   ZHOU Z
AE TIANBAO ANIMAL NUTRITION TECHNOLOGY CO (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021458030
AB    NOVELTY - Evironmentally friendly and clean production process of D,L-alanine comprises preparing a hydantoin precursor mixture, mixing ammonia-containing hydrogen cyanide mixed gas stream with carbon dioxide to prepare a crude cyanide mixed gas, and contacting crude cyanide mixed gas and reacting with acetaldehyde to generate hydantoin precursor Body mixture, mixing hydantoin precursor mixture and reacting with ammonia and carbon dioxide in a water system for the synthesis of 5-methylhydantoin and stripping obtained reaction material liquid is stripped to obtain an aqueous solution of 5-methylhydantoin, mixing 5-methylhydantoin aqueous solution with alkali, and undergoing high temperature and high pressure hydrolysis reaction, removing carbon dioxide and ammonia in reaction system and recovering to obtain D,L-alanine acid salt solution, hydrochlorizing D,L-alanine, acidifying D,L-alanine salt solution, crystallizing, filtering and drying to obtain D,L-alanine solution.
   USE - The method is useful for environmentally friendly and clean production process of D,L-alanine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts crude hydrocyanic acid and replaces sodiumpotassium cyanide; eliminates the step of neutralizing hydrocyanic acid and sodium/potassium hydroxide, saving the use of alkali; avoids the additional by-product salt produced by the participation of alkaline cyanide in the reaction; reduces the processing pressure of the subsequent product and inorganic salt separation step; and improves the product separation efficiency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of carbondioxide and ammonia is 0.8-2.2:1 in the crude cyanide mixed gas, and the content of hydrogen cyanide is 7-15 wt.%. The added amount of acetaldehyde satisfies the molar ratio of acetaldehyde and hydrogen cyanide of 1:1.0-1.1. The hydantoin precursor mixture comprises 2-hydroxy-propionitrile, 2-amino-propionitrile, 2-methylcarboxyamino-propionitrile, and 5-methylhydantoin. The molar ratio of the hydantoin precursor mixture alkalid on total acetaldehyde, carbon dioxide and ammonia is 1:0.8-2.5:1.2-3.2. The alkali is sodium hydroxide,potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate, potassiumcarbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassiumbicarbonate, calcium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide and/or barium hydroxide. The molar ratio of the alkali and 5-methylhydantoin is 1-2:1. Preferred Method: The contact reaction of the crude cyanide mixed gas and acetaldehyde is controlled at 20-65 degrees C. The high-temperature and high-pressure hydrolysis reaction are carrie dout at 170-180 degrees C, under pressure 0.5-6.0 Mpa. The acidification treatment is carbon dioxide gas or acidic resin treatment.

PN CN112479169-A
TI Argon recovery method for removing carbon monoxide by distillation, involves removing small amount of water and carbon dioxide through drying tower, and outputting remaining gas to cryogenic system, and separating oxygen to obtain argon.
AU ZHU D
AE SHANGHAI LIANFENG ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202130714Y
AB    NOVELTY - The argon recovery method involves injecting argon containing carbon monoxide into the rectification tower, and injecting oxygen and carbon dioxide with a content of 8:2. The temperature of the distillation tower is adjusted to -192-200 degrees C, the melting point of argon and the boiling point of carbon monoxide is reached. The argon that has reached the melting point is mixed with the remaining oxygen and the increased carbon dioxide to separate into the drying tower. The small amount of water and carbon dioxide is removed through the drying tower, and the remaining gas is outputted to the cryogenic system, the temperature of the cryogenic system is adjusted to -190 degrees C, and oxygen is separated to obtain argon.
   USE - Argon recovery method for removing carbon monoxide by distillation.
   ADVANTAGE - The compressor containing liquid nitrogen is continuously increased in the pressure in the rectification tower, such that the argon gas is rapidly expanded, and external work is performed to achieve the purpose of lowering the temperature. The oxygen is separated in the cryogenic system to reduce the temperature of oxygen to the boiling point and the melting point of argon, thus separating oxygen and argon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart illustrating a method for recovering argon gas from carbon monoxide removal by a rectification method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN112390946-A; CN112390946-B
TI Preparing non-isocyanate polyurethane, comprises e.g. mixing trifunctional aziridine polymer, double-end allyl compound, macroporous cation exchange resin and catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide and then adding water into product solid.
AU QIU Y
   RAO C
   CHEN G
AE FUJIAN HUAXIALAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2021212292
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing non-isocyanate polyurethane, comprises (i) mixing trifunctional aziridine polymer, double-end allyl compound, macroporous cation exchange resin and catalyst, introducing carbon dioxide under vacuum, heating at 65-85 degrees C for 2-5 hours, cooling to 30-40 degrees C to obtain first reactant, where macroporous cation exchange resin is a hydrogen type macroporous cation exchange resin containing hydrogen ions, (ii) adding difunctional aziridine polymer, double-end allyl compound, 2,3-epoxysuccinic acid and solvent into first reactant, introducing carbon dioxide under vacuum, heating at 70-95 degrees C, and cooling to 30-40 degrees C for 2-5 hours to obtain second reactant, (iii) filtering macroporous cation exchange resin in the second reactant, adjusting the system pH to 7-9, adding water to the product solid content 45-55%, raising temperature to 60 degrees C and performing vacuum distillation until the solvent content is less than 0.5%, and then adding water into the product solid content of 45-55%.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing non-isocyanate polyurethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes carbon dioxide which is non-toxic; rich resource and low cost; the aziridine polymer has low volatility, it is friendly to the worker and environment; has excellent polyurethane performance and is safe and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for non-isocyanate polyurethane obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i): adding A part of trifunctional aziridine polymer, B part of double-end allyl compound, C part of macroporous cation exchange resin, part D of carbon dioxide and M part of catalyst (calculated by moles), (ii) adding E parts of difunctional aziridine polymer, F parts of double-end allyl compound, G parts of 2,3-epoxysuccinic acid, H parts of carbon dioxide and N parts of solvent, where B is 3asteriskA; 0.01asterisk(A+E) less than or equal to C less than or equal to 0.03asterisk(A+E); 10asteriskA less than or equal to D less than or equal to 15asteriskA; 8asteriskA less than or equal to E less than or equal to 18asteriskA; F is equal to E; 0.1asteriskA less than or equal to G less than or equal to 0.25asteriskA; 16asteriskA less than or equal to H less than or equal to 24asteriskA; 0.01asteriskA less than or equal to M less than or equal to 0.05asteriskA; 30asteriskA less than or equal to N less than or equal to 80asteriskA. Preferred Components: The structural formula of the trifunctional aziridine polymer is of formula (I). The structural formula of the bifunctional aziridine polymer is formula (II). The two-terminal allyl compound is of formulae (III) or (IV). The catalyst is bromo 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium iodide. The solvent is acetone. The reagent for adjusting the pH of the system in the step (iii) is triethylamine, trimethylamine or diethanolamine.R4, R5 = methyl or ethyl;R6 and R7 = hydrogen or methyl; andn, m = 20-100.

PN WO2020011748-A1; CA3104818-A1; AU2019300085-A1; CN112384646-A; KR2021030935-A; BR112021000479-A2; EP3821058-A1; US2021214849-A1; JP2021524544-W; IN202017052868-A; VN79075-A; UZ2100066-B
TI Generating synthesis gas containing e.g. mixtures            of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, by            feeding steam and compressed air to cathode and anode,            respectively, of electrolysis unit and expanding            oxygen-rich gas leaving the anode.
AU HANSEN J B
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202006555H
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, or mixtures of hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                and carbon dioxide is generated by feeding steam                and compressed air to cathode and anode,                respectively, of electrolysis unit or of 1st of a                series of electrolysis units, where electrolysis                unit or units is/are operated under an elevated gas                pressure; and expanding oxygen-rich gas leaving the                anode down to approximately ambient pressure using                a gas expander.
   USE - Generation of synthesis gas containing                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, or mixtures of hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide by                electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - As the operating mechanism of an SOEC is                transfer of oxygen ions through the electrolyte                membrane and recombination to molecular oxygen on                the anode side, the dominant portion or a                significant portion of the mass flow, which enters                the SOEC stacks, leaves on the anode side in the                case of steam or carbon dioxide electrolysis,                respectively. The expander will recover more energy                than invested in compressing the dilution air or in                generating the dilution steam. The method avoids                any use of an air separation unit (cryogenic,                pressure swing adsorption, etc.) by taking                advantage of the ability of being operated in an                endothermal mode, and it provides the necessary                nitrogen by burning the hydrogen produced by steam                electrolysis by air.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Devices: Electrolysis units are                  solid oxide electrolysis cell (SOEC) stacks                  operating in a thermoneutral mode. Preferred                  Component: Synthesis gas is selected from                  methanol synthesis gas, methane synthesis gas,                  ammonia synthesis gas, and dimethyl ether (DME)                  synthesis gas.

PN WO2019098227-A1; JP2021014408-A
TI Producing hydrocarbon compound used as hydrocarbon fuel, by feeding gas containing oxygen into water, irradiating, mixing water with alcohol and liquid substance, and subjecting to impact in carbon-dioxide-containing atmosphere.
AU IMANAKA T
   TAKEMOTO T
AE SI ENERGY CO LTD (SIEN-Non-standard)
   SI ENERGY LTD (SIEN-Non-standard)
GA 201945885V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a hydrocarbon compound, involves (a) feeding gas containing oxygen into water, irradiating the oxygen in the water with light in the presence of an optical catalyst to generate active oxygen and prepare active-oxygen-containing water, and (b) mixing (i) the active-oxygen-containing water, (ii) an alcohol having 1-4C and (iii) a liquid substance containing a hydrocarbon compound, and subjecting the mixture to impact in a carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere, where the amount of hydrocarbon compound in the mixture subjected to impact represents an in increase over the amount of hydrocarbon compound in the liquid substance.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrocarbon compound, which is used as hydrocarbon fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy and simple to perform, produces hydrocarbon compound in a cost-effective manner, and solves energy problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for producing the hydrocarbon compound, comprising a reaction tank for reacting the above-mentioned components (i)-(iii) in the carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere, and a supply line having a nozzle arranged at the tip of the reaction tank, where the above-mentioned components (i)-(iii) from the nozzle are discharged into the reaction tank filled with a carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere, and the supply line is configured to apply an impact force by discharging together and colliding with the wall of the reaction vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves supplying a gas containing oxygen into water in the form of nanobubbles. The step (b) involves premixing the above-mentioned components (i)-(iii) before subjecting to the impact force. The impact force is applied by discharging the above-mentioned components (i)-(iii) together from the nozzle and causing them to collide with a wall surface of the reaction vessel. The method further involves (c) separating the mixture to which the impact force is applied into an aqueous phase and a phase comprising a hydrocarbon compound. Preferred Properties: The active-oxygen-containing water has a dissolved oxygen concentration of greater than or equal to 10 ppm. The average carbon number of the hydrocarbon compound contained in the mixture to which the impact force is applied is less than plus minus 20% with respect to the average carbon number of the hydrocarbon compound contained in the liquid substance. Preferred Components: The water used in step (a) is pure water. The liquid substance comprises at least one chosen from saturated hydrocarbon having 5-27C, light oil, kerosene, gasoline and jet fuel. Preferred Device: The supply line further comprises a premixer for premixing the above-mentioned components (i)-(iii) before discharging from the nozzle. The device further comprises a separation tank that separates the mixture subjected to impact force extracted from the reaction tank. The device further comprises an active-oxygen-containing water production unit for producing active-oxygen-containing water and supplying it to a supply line, a water tank comprising water and a supply unit for supplying gas containing oxygen to water, where the water supplied with the gas containing oxygen is extracted from the water tank and the active-oxygen-containing water production unit comprises a light irradiation unit that irradiates the oxygen in water to generate active oxygen. The supply unit supplies the gas containing oxygen into water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PT P
PN CN107902680-A
TI Refining industrial grade lithium carbonate for producing battery-grade lithium carbonate comprises e.g. formulating industrial grade lithium carbonate, water into slurry and adding organic acid to convert slurry into soluble clear liquid.
AU LIU J
   MA C
   ZHAO M
   LI Q
AE TIANYUAN LITHIUM MATERIALS HEBEI CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018312717
AB    NOVELTY - Refining industrial grade lithium carbonate comprises e.g. (a) formulating industrial grade lithium carbonate and water into a slurry, adding organic acid to convert slurry into a soluble clear liquid, transferring soluble clear liquid to hydrogenation reactor and stirring at 100-300 rotations/minute; (b) introducing carbon dioxide into hydrogenation reactor and monitoring pH in hydrogenation reactor and adding urea to hydrogenation reactor to slowly release carbon dioxide gas and precipitating after reaction into next process; and (c) filtering precipitate, washing, drying and roasting.
   USE - The method is useful for refining industrial grade lithium carbonate for producing battery-grade lithium carbonate (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Refining industrial grade lithium carbonate comprises (a) formulating industrial grade lithium carbonate and water into a slurry, adding organic acid to convert slurry into a soluble clear liquid, transferring soluble clear liquid to hydrogenation reactor at 20-25 degrees C and stirring in a hydrogenation reactor at a motor speed of 100-300 rotations/minute; (b) introducing carbon dioxide into hydrogenation reactor and monitoring pH in hydrogenation reactor and adding urea to hydrogenation reactor, adjusting pH to 7-12, controlling temperature between 80-100 degrees C so that urea slowly releases carbon dioxide gas and precipitating after reaction into next process; and (c) filtering precipitate after the reaction, washing, drying and roasting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), mixing industrial-grade lithium carbonate and water into slurry which is a primary grade or grade deionized water, and amount of deionized water is 25-45 times of the industrial grade lithium carbonate addition amount. In the step (c) washing lithium carbonate precipitate with deionized water multiple times. Preferred Composition: The organic acid in the step (a) is formic acid and adding organic acid by means of a dropper or separatory funnel at a rate of 10-25 ml/minutes. The soluble clear liquid in step (a) is lithium formate and adjusting pH using lithium hydroxide aqueous solution. In the step (b) urea is an aqueous solution of analytically pure urea. In the step (b), supernatant lithium hydroxide to adjust pH, and lithium hydroxide is analytically pure lithium hydroxide. In the step (b) lithium carbonate precipitated is obtained by suction filtration or centrifugation.

PN CN107741406-A; CN107741406-B
TI Method for simultaneous detection of carbon and sulfur contents in solid minerals and biomass fuels, involves combusting sample, supplying solids in products with oxygen, adsorbing followed by utilizing spectrum absorption detector.
AU TAN T
   LI G
   WANG G
   SHEN J
   LI W
   CHEN J
   LIU Z
   CHEN F
   QIN T
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 201818281A
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon and sulfur contents in solid minerals and biomass fuels simultaneous detection method involves fully combusting sample to be tested to obtain combustion products, supplying solids in the combustion products with oxygen, adsorbing particles by glass wool and utilizing water absorbent to absorb the water vapor in the combustion products. Carbon dioxide infrared spectrum absorption detector and sulfur dioxide infrared spectrum absorption detector are utilized to independently measure the concentration of carbon dioxide and the concentration of sulfur dioxide in the combustion products.
   USE - Method for simultaneous detection of carbon and sulfur contents in solid minerals and biomass fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simultaneous detection of carbon and sulfur contents with high detection speed and high accuracy, in simple, easy and cost effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon and sulfur contents in solid minerals and biomass fuels simultaneous detection method involves fully combusting sample to be tested to obtain combustion products, supplying solids in the combustion products with oxygen, adsorbing particles by glass wool and utilizing water absorbent to absorb the water vapor in the combustion products. Carbon dioxide infrared spectrum absorption detector and sulfur dioxide infrared spectrum absorption detector are utilized to independently measure the concentration of carbon dioxide and the concentration of sulfur dioxide in the combustion products by measuring the change of energy, so as to achieve carbon and total carbon in the sample to be tested.

PN CN111514857-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g.            carrying out hydrothermal reaction for sodium ion            intercalation, mixing clay and saturated sodium            chloride solution, placing in hydrothermal reactor,            heating, filtering and washing.
AU SONG Y
   LING Z
   ZHANG Y
   SHI C
   LI G
   CHEN M
   CHAI F
   ZHAO J
   YANG L
   LI Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2020787611
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises                e.g. (1) carrying out hydrothermal reaction for                sodium ion intercalation, mixing clay and saturated                sodium chloride solution according to a mass-volume                ratio of 3 g/1 l and placing in a hydrothermal                reactor, and heating at 110 degrees C for 12-48                hours, at a constant temperature after cooling to                room temperature, filtering and washing reaction                product with de-ionized water, and washing with                absolute ethanol to obtain sodium ion intercalated                clay, (2) hydrothermal reaction for lithium ion                intercalation, mixing clay after sodium ion                intercalation with a lithium chloride solution with                a concentration of 0.5-2.5 mol/l according to the                mass-volume ratio of 3 g/1 l and placing in                hydrothermal reaction, heating at 110 degrees C for                12-48 hours, after constant temperature is over,                the reaction product will be filtering and washing                with de-ionized water, and washing with absolute                ethanol to obtain sodium ion and lithium ion                intercalated clay.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: is environmentally friendly,                safe and easy to operate, has rich reserves and low                prices in nature, and helps reduce production                costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises                (1) carrying out hydrothermal reaction for sodium                ion intercalation, mixing clay and saturated sodium                chloride solution according to a mass-volume ratio                of 3 g/1 l and placing in a hydrothermal reactor,                and heating at 110 degrees C for 12-48 hours, at a                constant temperature after cooling to room                temperature, filtering and washing reaction product                with de-ionized water, and washing with absolute                ethanol to obtain sodium ion intercalated clay, (2)                hydrothermal reaction for lithium ion                intercalation, mixing clay after sodium ion                intercalation with a lithium chloride solution with                a concentration of 0.5-2.5 mol/l according to the                mass-volume ratio of 3 g/1 l and placing in                hydrothermal reaction, heating at 110 degrees C for                12-48 hours, after constant temperature is over,                the reaction product will be filtering and washing                with de-ionized water, and washing with absolute                ethanol to obtain sodium ion and lithium ion                intercalated clay, (3) ultrasonic assisted liquid                phase stripping, mixing sodium ion and obtained                lithium ion intercalated clay with de-ionized                water, and the volume ratio of the de-ionized water                to lithium chloride solution is 4:5, ultrasonically                treating clay nanoplatelets for 0.5-2 hours in an                ice bath to exfoliate clay nano platelets to obtain                a mixture of the clay nano-platelet dispersion,                unstripped clay particles and impurities, (4)                centrifugal sieving, centrifuging mixture obtained                after ultrasonic stripping at 500                revolutions/minutes for 1 hour to removing                unstripped clay particles and impurities to obtain                a stable dispersion of clay nano platelets, and (5)                loading amine by dipping and freeze-drying, adding                amine to stable dispersion and stirring for 0.5-1.5                hours to fully mixing clay nano flake with the                amine, stirring, freeze drying with liquid nitrogen                for 48 hours, a two-dimensional nano sheet                composite material is obtained as a high-efficiency                adsorbent for carbon dioxide adsorption. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon                dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The clay is                  vermiculite, montmorillonite, kaolin and/or                  illite. In the step (5), the amines are                  tetraethylenepentamine, triethylenetetramine,                  diethanolamine, polyethyleneimine,                  tripropylamine, triisopropanolamine, formamide,                  p-phenylenediamine, and/or phthalic acid imides.                  The mass of the supported amine is 0.2-50 wt.%                  clay nano platelets in the stable dispersion.                  Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide adsorbent is                  placing into a fixed bed reactor, vacuum degassed                  at 150 degrees C for 12 hours, and performing                  carbon dioxide adsorption at a pressure range of                  0-50 bar and at 25-75 degrees C. The absolute                  pressure of the vacuum degassing is 0.013                  bar.

PN CN110144228-A
TI Clean coking production technology by using pure oxygen to participate in heating process of coke oven, discharging coke oven flue gas, desulfurizing, and fully contacting carbon dioxide in coke oven flue gas with alkaline absorption liquid.
AU WANG M
   WU S
AE ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULTING (CMEG-C)
GA 201975501C
AB    NOVELTY - Clean coking production technology involves using pure oxygen to participate in heating process of coke oven in coke oven heating system, discharging coke oven flue gas, collecting flue and total flue, entering coke oven flue gas pre-treatment system, desulfurizing, and recovering waste heat; fully contacting carbon dioxide in coke oven flue gas with alkaline absorption liquid, absorbing, producing carbonates, condensing and recovering water vapor in coke oven flue gas, using carbonate substance, or crystallizing and purifying carbonate product; high temperature carbonizing coal material, entering coal gas collection and purification system, cooling coke oven gas, purifying, removing impurities, and getting corresponding tar, ammonia, benzene products and by-products; and separating hydrogen in coke oven gas, sending separated hydrogen to subsequent hydrogen storage and external supply system, sending coke oven gas to coke oven heating system, and supplying remaining coke oven gas.
   USE - The method is used for clean coking production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces or avoids generation of atmospheric pollutants in coking production process, produces clean energy products, improves resource utilization level of coking production waste gas, reduces greenhouse gas emissions during coking production process, and constructs coking green production process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Clean coking production technology involves:
   (A) using pure oxygen as combustion-supporting gas to participate in heating process of coke oven in coke oven heating system, making main component of coke oven flue gas generated after combustion of coke oven heating system is carbon dioxide, water and sulfur dioxide, discharging coke oven flue gas from the coke oven heating system, collecting flue and total flue, entering coke oven flue gas pre-treatment system, desulfurizing coke oven flue gas, and recovering waste heat;
   (B) entering pre-treated coke oven flue gas into carbon dioxide absorption system, fully contacting carbon dioxide in coke oven flue gas with alkaline absorption liquid, absorbing, producing carbonates, achieving carbon dioxide reduction in coking production process, condensing and recovering water vapor in coke oven flue gas by solution, using carbonate substance as medium for coke oven flue gas desulfurization, or crystallizing and purifying carbonate product;
   (C) high temperature carbonizing coal material in carbonization chamber to generate coke oven gas, entering coal gas collection and purification system, cooling coke oven gas, purifying, removing impurities, getting corresponding tar, ammonia, benzene products and by-products, and entering purified coke oven gas into coal gas hydrogen extraction system; and
   (D) separating hydrogen in coke oven gas, sending separated hydrogen to subsequent hydrogen storage and external supply system for external supply or export, sending coke oven gas to coke oven heating system to use as gas, and supplying remaining coke oven gas as fuel.
   The coke oven gas after hydrogen extraction is mainly hydrocarbon, and has calorific value of more than 28000 kJ/m3. The coke oven heating system uses coke oven gas containing CH4, CO and other hydrocarbons produced by coke oven after purification and hydrogen extraction as fuel. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for clean coking production system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline absorption liquid is calcium hydroxide.

PN CN109006675-A
TI Hatching method for breeding eggs by placing sterilized and cleaned eggs in incubator, setting temperature and humidity, controlling carbon dioxide concentration, and monitoring hatching condition.
AU WAN X
AE ZHAOQING HI-TECH ZONE TIANMU NEW ENERGY (ZHAO-Non-standard)
GA 2018A4576B
AB    NOVELTY - Hatching method for breeding eggs, involves (a) placing sterilized and cleaned eggs in a multifunctional incubator, and (b) on the first 1-3 days after hatching, setting the temperature of the incubator to 38.3 degrees , setting to 38 degrees on the 4-7 days, 37.8 degrees on the 8-12 days, 37.6 degrees on the 13-16 days and 37.4 degrees on the 17-19 days and setting humidity on the first 1-19 days to 55-75%, controlling the concentration of carbon dioxide in the incubator at less than 0.3%, monitoring the hatching condition in the incubator by using a mobile phone, controlling the starting and stopping of the heater, humidifier, oxygen machine and egg turning motor in the incubator according to the incubating condition, adjusting the temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, 10 hours before hatching, reducing oxygen in the incubator, and controlling the oxygen ratio at 20-21%.
   USE - The hatching method is useful for breeding eggs.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses the mobile phone to observe the incubation conditions such as hatching environment index inside the incubator in real time, adjusts the temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide in the incubator through the mobile phone to ensure the hatching needs, and has improved breeding efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Hatching method for breeding eggs, involves (a) placing sterilized and cleaned eggs in a multifunctional incubator, where the incubator comprises a chassis, a heater arranged on both sides of the chassis, a fan arranged in front of the heater, a humidifier arranged on the bottom plate of the box body, a temperature sensor arranged at the bottom of the left side wall of the box body, a humidity sensor arranged at the bottom of the right side wall of the box body, a hatching tray arranged in the chassis, where the hatching tray is composed of an incubation tray fixing frame and a rotating support, an egg turning motor and a state recorder arranged on the hatching tray fixing frame, an oxygen machine arranged at the top of the chassis, where the oxygen machine delivers oxygen into the chassis through oxygen conveying pipe, and a controller arranged on the right side of the chassis wall, where the egg turning motor is connected to the controller and the controller and the mobile phone are connected via WiFi or 4G connection, and (b) on the first 1-3 days after hatching, setting the temperature of the incubator to 38.3 degrees , setting to 38 degrees on the 4-7 days, 37.8 degrees on the 8-12 days, 37.6 degrees on the 13-16 days and 37.4 degrees on the 17-19 days and setting humidity on the first 1-19 days to 55-75%, controlling the concentration of carbon dioxide in the incubator at less than 0.3%, monitoring the hatching condition in the incubator by using a mobile phone, controlling the starting and stopping of the heater, humidifier, oxygen machine and egg turning motor in the incubator according to the incubating condition, adjusting the temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration, 10 hours before hatching, reducing oxygen in the incubator, and controlling the oxygen ratio at 20-21%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves, on the first 1-10 days, every day 6:00-18:00, turning the eggs every 3 hours, turning eggs every 4 hours from 18:00 to 6:00 every day, on the first 11-19 days, turning eggs once every two hours, and when turning the eggs, turning the eggs at an angle of 45 degrees to the left or right.

PN CN112299434-A
TI Amino-functionalized MCM-41 molecular sieve raw powder or molecular sieve used for gas adsorption of carbon dioxide has specified Fourier infrared spectrum.
AU WU K
   REN X
   PEI Q
   JIA Z
   YANG G
   LIU Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 2021138422
AB    NOVELTY - Amino-functionalized MCM-41 molecular sieve raw powder or molecular sieve has 1564-1574 per centimeter absorption peak Fourier infrared spectrum, preferably 1569 per centimeter.
   USE - The amino-functionalized MCM-41 molecular sieve raw powder or molecular sieve is used for gas adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The molecular sieve has thermal stability of MCM molecular sieve and surface characteristics brought by organic components.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The MCM-41 molecular sieve is prepared by mixing organic template, water, pore expander, amino modifier, organic solvent, silicon source and alkali to obtain colloidal mixture, performing hydrothermal crystallization on colloidal mixture to obtain product, filtering, washing and drying. Preferred Components: The molecular sieve raw powder or molecular sieve comprises reaction products of organic template, water, pore expander, amino modifier, organic solvent, silicon source and/or alkali. The organic template comprises cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethylammonium chloride and/or cetyltriethylammonium bromide. The molar ratio of pore expander to organic template is 3.5:1-1:1. The amino modifier is 3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, 3-(phenylamino) propyltrimethoxysilane and/or 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The silicon source is silica, tetraethyl orthosilicate, sodium silicate and/or silica sol. The organic solvent is ethanol. The molar ratio of amino modifier to silicon source is 0.8:1-0.3:1. The mass ratio of organic solvent to amino modifier is 1:2-2:1, preferably 1:1. The alkali source is sodium hydroxide, tetramethylammonium hydroxide and/or ammonia water, preferably sodium hydroxide.

PN CN108714289-A; CN108714289-B
TI Treating cyaniding tailings involves electolyzing cyaniding tailings washed ore slurry, oxidizing cyanide in cyanidation slag washed ore slurry into carbon dioxide and nitrogen in electrolytic process and stirring washed ore slurry.
AU SONG Y
   CHEN Y
   ZHAO L
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
   UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 201886242H
AB    NOVELTY - Treating cyaniding tailings involves electolyzing cyaniding tailings washed ore slurry, oxidizing cyanide in cyanidation slag washed ore slurry into carbon dioxide and nitrogen in electrolytic process and stirring cyaniding tailings washed ore slurry continuously.
   USE - Method for treating cyaniding tailings.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to treat cyaniding tailings that has removal rate, and in simple manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The sodium chloride is added in cyaniding tailings water washing ore slurry. The added sodium chloride is dissolved in cyanide tailings washed slurry and chloride ion is formed. The chlorine is dissolved in cyanide tailings washed ore slurry to generate hypochlorous acid. The generated hypochlorous acid is oxidized cyanide tailings washed ore slurry into carbon dioxide and nitrogen. The cyanide tailings washed slurry is subjected to sedimentation treatment. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of cyaniding tailings washing ore pulp and water is 1:4-6. The electrolytic voltage is 4-10 volts. The electrolysis time is 2-4 hours. The distance between cathode and anode is 8-12 mm. The electrodes is connected in series in same electrolytic cell to electrolyze,. Preferred Components: The electrode is titanium plate or stainless steel plate.

TI Carbon dioxide thickening fracturing system comprises output system, fracturing fluid treatment system and liquid carbon dioxide treatment system, where fracturing fluid treatment system includes fracturing fluid thickening system.
AU ZHENG Y

AE YAN'AN SHUANGFENG GROUP CO LTD (YANA-Non-standard)
GA 2021E63639
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide thickening fracturing system comprises output system, a fracturing fluid treatment system and a liquid carbon dioxide treatment system. The fracturing fluid treatment system comprises liquid carbon dioxide thickener stock treatment system and a fracturing fluid thickening system. The liquid carbon dioxide thickener stock treatment system includes liquid carbon dioxide thickener stock tank, a carbon dioxide thickener solution configuration tank, a water storage tank, a sand mixing truck, a liquid high-pressure pump truck, and a fracturing fluid wellhead injection head. The fracturing system further comprises normal temperature and high-pressure pipeline and low-pressure normal temperature pipeline.
   USE - Carbon dioxide thickening fracturing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes liquid state carbon dioxide thickening for building seam, enables effective reduction in influence of liquid on reservoir, improves flowback rate to the greatest extent, ensures reservoir reformation effect, and improves single well production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide thickening fracturing process method.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
